meanings.englishDefinition: The act of assisting another's progress over a wall or other obstacle by forming a step for one of their feet with one's hands.
meanings.koreanDefinition: (장애물을 넘을 수 있게) 발을 받쳐주기, 도움
meanings.example: He gave her a leg up over the fence.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그녀가 울타리를 넘을 수 있게 발을 받쳐 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A boost, a sudden improvement; an advantage, an edge.
meanings.example: This new contract will give our company a leg up on the competition.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 새로운 계약은 우리 회사가 경쟁에서 유리한 고지를 점하게 해줄 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To walk a horse on a hard surface regularly.
meanings.example: The trainer legs up the horse daily to strengthen its joints.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조련사는 말의 관절을 튼튼하게 하기 위해 매일 말을 걷게 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To don fancy clothing; to dress up particularly in flattering or specially altered garments.
meanings.example: She loved to gussy up for special occasions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 특별한 날에 멋지게 차려입는 것을 좋아했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish completely, especially food or liquor.
meanings.example: They polished off the last of the cake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 케이크의 마지막 조각까지 다 먹어치웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove by polishing (rubbing with a fine abrasive).
meanings.example: The surface scratches were polished off with a soft cloth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 표면의 긁힌 자국들은 부드러운 천으로 닦아서 제거했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To power down, to switch off, to deactivate (an appliance, light, etc.).
meanings.example: Turn off the machine and unplug it when you leave.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나갈 때 기계를 끄고 플러그를 뽑으세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To repulse, disgust, or discourage (someone).
meanings.example: Cigarette smoking really turns me off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 담배 연기는 정말 나를 정떨어지게 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a road; to exit.
meanings.example: Turn off at the next exit so we can have lunch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 점심을 먹을 수 있게 다음 출구에서 빠지세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rotate a tap or valve so as to interrupt the outflow of liquid or gas.
meanings.example: Remember to turn the tap off once you've finished.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다 쓰면 수도꼭지 잠그는 것을 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dismiss; to fire.
meanings.example: The manager decided to turn off the incompetent employee.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매니저는 무능한 직원을 해고하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To switch off (an appliance, light, etc.).
meanings.example: Turn off the TV and go to bed.
meanings.exampleKorean: TV 끄고 자거라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To disgust or repel.
meanings.example: His bad breath really turns me off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 입냄새는 정말 정떨어지게 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To power up, to put into operation, to activate (an appliance, light, etc.).
meanings.example: Please turn the lights on so I can see what I'm reading.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 읽고 있는 게 보일 수 있게 불 좀 켜 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sexually arouse.
meanings.example: The way he looks at her really turns her on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 그녀를 쳐다보는 방식은 정말 그녀를 흥분시킨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rebel against; to suddenly attack.
meanings.example: Suddenly all his friends turned on him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 갑자기 그의 모든 친구들이 그에게 등을 돌렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To introduce (someone to something), and especially to fill them with enthusiasm (about it).
meanings.example: My brother turned me on to jazz.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 형이 나에게 재즈를 처음 소개해 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To depend upon; to pivot around, to have as a central subject.
meanings.example: The argument turned on the question of whether or not jobs would be lost.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 논쟁은 일자리를 잃을 것인지 말 것인지의 문제에 달려 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To switch on (an appliance, light, etc.).
meanings.example: Turn on the radio; I want to hear the news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 라디오 좀 켜 봐. 뉴스 듣고 싶어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sexually arouse.
meanings.example: That kind of music really turns him on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저런 종류의 음악은 정말 그를 흥분시킨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To border on; to be adjacent to.
meanings.example: His land abuts on the road.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 땅은 도로와 접해 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take action according to or in response to something.
meanings.example: The police acted on a tip-off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 제보에 따라 조치를 취했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have an effect on.
meanings.example: This drug acts on the central nervous system.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 약은 중추 신경계에 작용한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something because of another thing that has happened; to have an effect on something.
meanings.example: The board decided to act on the consultant's recommendations.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이사회는 컨설턴트의 권고에 따라 행동하기로(조치를 취하기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To calculate the sum of.
meanings.example: Add up all the prices to get the total.
meanings.exampleKorean: 총액을 구하기 위해 모든 가격을 합산하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make sense; to be consistent and plausible.
meanings.example: His story just doesn't add up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 이야기는 앞뒤가 맞지 않는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To result in; to signify.
meanings.example: It all adds up to a lot of hard work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 모든 것은 결국 많은 노고를 의미한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To calculate the total of several numbers.
meanings.example: The figures don't add up correctly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수치들의 합계가 맞지 않는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To seem reasonable or logical.
meanings.example: His story just doesn't add up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 이야기는 도무지 앞뒤가 맞지 않는다 (말이 안 된다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To reply impertinently; to talk back.
meanings.example: Don't answer back when I'm talking to you!
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 말할 때 말대꾸하지 마라!

meanings.englishDefinition: To reply rudely to someone in authority.
meanings.example: Don't answer back when I'm speaking to you!
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 말할 때 말대꾸하지 마!

meanings.englishDefinition: To inquire about the health or well-being of someone.
meanings.example: He asked after your mother.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 너의 어머니 안부를 물으셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ask for news about someone's health or life.
meanings.example: He asked after you when I saw him yesterday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어제 그를 만났을 때 그가 네 안부를 묻더라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To place inside.
meanings.example: Put in the sugar before you stir.
meanings.exampleKorean: 젓기 전에 설탕을 넣으세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To devote (time or effort) to something.
meanings.example: I've put in a lot of hours on this project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 이 프로젝트에 많은 시간을 들였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To submit or tender (an application, etc.).
meanings.example: He put in an application for the job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그 일자리에 지원서를 제출했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To extinguish (a fire, light, etc.).
meanings.example: It took firefighters three hours to put out the blaze.
meanings.exampleKorean: 소방관들이 불을 끄는 데 3시간이 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce or publish.
meanings.example: The company puts out a new model every year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 매년 신모델을 내놓는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To annoy or inconvenience someone.
meanings.example: I hope it doesn't put you out if I stay another night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 하룻밤 더 묵는 게 당신을 번거롭게 하지 않았으면 좋겠네요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To postpone; to delay.
meanings.example: Don't put off until tomorrow what you can do today.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 할 수 있는 일을 내일로 미루지 마라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discourage or repel.
meanings.example: The smell of the place really put me off my food.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그곳의 냄새 때문에 정말 밥맛이 떨어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dress oneself in.
meanings.example: Put on your coat; it's cold outside.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코트 입으렴. 밖이 춥단다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stage or present (a play, show, etc.).
meanings.example: The local dramatic society is putting on a play.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지역 극단에서 연극 한 편을 무대에 올린다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pretend or feign.
meanings.example: He's not really angry; he's just putting it on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 진짜 화난 게 아니라 그냥 그런 척하는 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To save (money) for future use.
meanings.example: I try to put by a little money each month for my vacation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 휴가를 위해 매달 조금씩 돈을 저축하려고 노력한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cautiously try to influence or find out about a situation.
meanings.example: We've put out feelers to see if they'd be interested in a merger.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 그들이 합병에 관심이 있는지 알아보려고 슬쩍 떠보았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To store in its usual place.
meanings.koreanDefinition: (다 쓰고 난 물건을) 치우다, 제자리에 두다
meanings.example: Please put your toys away when you've finished playing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다 놀고 나면 장난감 좀 치우렴.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consume (large amounts of food or drink).
meanings.example: He can really put away the pizza!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 피자를 정말 잘 먹어치운다!

meanings.englishDefinition: To confine to a prison or mental hospital.
meanings.example: The judge put him away for ten years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 판사는 그에게 10년형을 선고해 수감시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To trick or deceive someone.
meanings.example: You can't put one over on me that easily!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그렇게 쉽게 나를 속일 순 없을걸!

meanings.englishDefinition: To propose or suggest.
meanings.example: He put forth a new theory of gravity.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 새로운 중력 이론을 제시했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To exert (effort or energy).
meanings.example: We must put forth our best efforts to succeed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 성공하기 위해 우리는 최선의 노력을 다해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pose (a question) to someone.
meanings.example: I'll put that question to the manager.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 질문을 매니저에게 던져 보겠습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To end something; to settle or conclude.
meanings.example: We need to put these rumors to rest once and for all.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 이 루머들을 완전히 잠재워야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To expel or eject someone forcibly.
meanings.example: He was kicked out of the club for fighting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 싸움질 때문에 클럽에서 쫓겨났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk nonsense; to speak about something without knowing the facts.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 허풍을 떨다, 잘 알지도 못하면서 지껄이다
meanings.example: He's just talking through his hat; ignore him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그냥 헛소리하는 거야. 무시해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discuss or consider (an idea, etc.) informally.
meanings.example: We've been kicking some ideas around for the new project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 새 프로젝트에 대한 몇 가지 아이디어를 논의해 오고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat someone badly.
meanings.example: Don't let them kick you around like that.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들이 너를 그렇게 함부로 대하게 두지 마라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start or commence (especially a game or event).
meanings.example: The music festival kicks off this Friday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 음악 축제가 이번 주 금요일에 시작된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become angry or start a fight.
meanings.example: He kicked off when he found out the truth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 진실을 알고 나서 화를 내기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to take effect or operate.
meanings.example: The medicine should kick in after about 20 minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 약효는 약 20분 후에 나타날 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To contribute (money or help).
meanings.example: We all kicked in ten dollars for the gift.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 선물을 위해 각자 10달러씩 냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To support or prop up.
meanings.example: These columns hold up the roof.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 기둥들이 지붕을 지탱하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To delay or hinder.
meanings.example: Sorry I'm late; I was held up in traffic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 늦어서 미안해요. 차가 막혀서 늦었어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rob someone at gunpoint.
meanings.example: Two masked men tried to hold up the bank.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가면을 쓴 두 남자가 은행을 털려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wait for a short time.
meanings.example: Hold on a minute, I'll be right back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잠깐만 기다려 주세요, 금방 올게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To grip or grasp something tightly.
meanings.example: Hold on tight, the road is bumpy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 길이 울퉁불퉁하니 꽉 잡으세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wait for a short period.
meanings.example: Hang on, I'm almost ready.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잠깐만, 거의 다 됐어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To depend on something.
meanings.example: The future of the project hangs on this decision.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프로젝트의 미래가 이 결정에 달려 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To offer or extend (something).
meanings.example: He held out his hand to greet her.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그녀와 인사하려고 손을 내밀었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To resist or survive in a difficult situation.
meanings.example: The soldiers held out for three weeks against the enemy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 병사들은 적에 맞서 3주 동안 버텼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To delay or postpone doing something.
meanings.example: Let's hold off on the decision until next week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 결정은 다음 주까지 미룹시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep someone or something at a distance.
meanings.example: The police held off the crowd.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰이 군중을 저지했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin a war or armed conflict.
meanings.example: The two countries went to war over a border dispute.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 나라는 국경 분쟁으로 전쟁을 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to get people to say good things about one.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 칭찬을 유도하다, 칭찬을 듣고 싶어 하다
meanings.example: Stop fishing for compliments; you know you look great.
meanings.exampleKorean: 칭찬 유도 그만해. 너 멋져 보이는 거 알잖아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consider or reflect on something.
meanings.example: I need some time to think about your proposal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신의 제안에 대해 생각할 시간이 필요합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hide or take refuge in a place.
meanings.example: The gang holed up in an abandoned warehouse.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 일당은 버려진 창고에 숨어 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To restrain or repress (an emotion, etc.).
meanings.example: She struggled to hold back her tears.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 눈물을 참으려고 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent from progressing.
meanings.example: Lack of education is holding him back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 교육 부족이 그의 발목을 잡고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To postpone or delay.
meanings.example: The matter was held over until the next meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 문제는 다음 회의 때까지 연기되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use a secret or mistake to threaten or control someone.
meanings.example: He's always holding my past mistakes over my head.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 내 과거 실수를 가지고 나를 압박한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To appear or arrive.
meanings.example: He didn't show up for the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 회의에 나타나지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To expose or embarrass someone.
meanings.example: He tried to show me up in front of my friends.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 친구들 앞에서 나를 무안하게 만들려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To display proudly or ostentatiously.
meanings.example: She loves showing off her new car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 새 차를 자랑하는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To refuse or reject.
meanings.example: She turned down his marriage proposal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그의 청혼을 거절했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decrease the volume, heat, or intensity of.
meanings.example: Please turn down the music; it's too loud.
meanings.exampleKorean: 음악 소리 좀 줄여 주세요. 너무 커요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase the volume, heat, or intensity of.
meanings.example: Can you turn up the TV?
meanings.exampleKorean: TV 소리 좀 키워 줄래?

meanings.englishDefinition: To appear or be found, especially unexpectedly.
meanings.example: Don't worry, your keys will turn up eventually.
meanings.exampleKorean: 걱정 마, 열쇠는 결국 나타날 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To flip or capsize.
meanings.example: Turn over the pancake once it's brown.
meanings.exampleKorean: 팬케이크가 갈색이 되면 뒤집으세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To transfer ownership or control.
meanings.example: He turned the business over to his son.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 사업을 아들에게 넘겨주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To transform or change into.
meanings.example: The caterpillar turned into a butterfly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 애벌레가 나비로 변했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go to bed.
meanings.example: I'm tired; I think I'll turn in now.
meanings.exampleKorean: 졸려요. 이제 잠자리에 들어야겠어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To submit or hand in.
meanings.example: Don't forget to turn in your homework.
meanings.exampleKorean: 숙제 제출하는 거 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start behaving in a better way.
meanings.example: He promised to turn over a new leaf and stop smoking.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 새사람이 되어 담배를 끊겠다고 약속했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To result or end up.
meanings.example: Everything turned out fine in the end.
meanings.exampleKorean: 결국 모든 것이 잘 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To extinguish a light.
meanings.example: Please turn out the lights when you leave.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나갈 때 불 좀 꺼 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce or manufacture.
meanings.example: The factory turns out 500 cars a week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 공장은 일주일에 500대의 차를 생산한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reject or despise something as being not good enough.
meanings.example: He turned up his nose at the salary they offered.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그들이 제안한 연봉에 콧방귀를 뀌었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become hostile or opposed to someone or something.
meanings.example: The public is turning against the new law.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대중이 새로운 법에 등을 돌리고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to describe the horror or disgust that a dead person would feel about some current situation.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 지하에 계신 분이 통곡할 일이다, 기절초풍할 노릇이다
meanings.example: My grandfather would turn in his grave if he saw what has become of his business.
meanings.exampleKorean: 할아버지가 자신의 사업이 어떻게 되었는지 보신다면 무덤에서 벌떡 일어나실 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To maintain a safe distance.
meanings.example: Please stand back from the edge of the platform.
meanings.exampleKorean: 승강장 끝에서 뒤로 물러나 주십시오.

meanings.englishDefinition: To support or remain loyal to someone.
meanings.example: His wife stood by him throughout the trial.
meanings.exampleKorean: 재판 내내 그의 아내는 그의 곁을 지켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be ready or waiting to help.
meanings.example: Ambulances were standing by in case of an accident.
meanings.exampleKorean: 만약의 사고에 대비해 구급차들이 대기하고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remain passive while something happens.
meanings.example: How can you stand by and watch him being bullied?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 괴롭힘당하는 걸 어떻게 그냥 보고만 있을 수 있니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To act as a substitute for someone.
meanings.example: I'll be standing in for the manager while she's on vacation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매니저가 휴가 간 동안 제가 그녀를 대신할 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be noticeable or prominent.
meanings.example: Her bright red hair made her stand out in the crowd.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 밝은 빨간 머리는 그녀를 군중 속에서 눈에 띄게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rise to one's feet.
meanings.example: Please stand up when the judge enters the room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 판사가 입실할 때 일어서 주십시오.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail to keep a date or appointment with someone.
meanings.example: He stood me up on our first date.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 우리의 첫 데이트에서 나를 바람맞혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remain valid or effective.
meanings.example: Will this evidence stand up in court?
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 증거가 법정에서 유효할까요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop doing something, especially work.
meanings.example: What time do you knock off work today?
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 몇 시에 퇴근하니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce a price.
meanings.example: They knocked off ten dollars because of the scratch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스크래치 때문에 그들이 10달러를 깎아 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce something quickly or easily.
meanings.example: He knocked off a few sketches in his lunch hour.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 점심시간에 스케치 몇 점을 뚝딱 그려냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To surprise, impress, or excite someone greatly.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 깜짝 놀라게 하다, 깊은 감명을 주다
meanings.example: The new graphics will knock your socks off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 그래픽을 보면 정말 깜짝 놀랄 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone unconscious.
meanings.example: The boxer knocked out his opponent in the second round.
meanings.exampleKorean: 복서가 2라운드에 상대 선수를 KO시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eliminate from a competition.
meanings.example: France was knocked out of the World Cup in the first round.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프랑스는 월드컵 1라운드에서 탈락했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something stop working.
meanings.example: The storm knocked out the power supply.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭풍으로 전력 공급이 중단되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to tell someone to stop doing something annoying.
meanings.example: Knock it off! I'm trying to study.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그만 좀 해! 공부하려고 하잖아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause to fall over.
meanings.example: I accidentally knocked over a glass of water.
meanings.exampleKorean: 실수로 물잔을 넘어뜨렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rob (especially a bank or store).
meanings.example: They were planning to knock over a jewelry store.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 보석 가게를 털 계획을 세우고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause lasting annoyance or resentment.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 눈엣가시 같다, 계속 신경 쓰이다, 불쾌하다
meanings.example: It really sticks in my craw that he never even thanked me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 나에게 고맙다는 말조차 안 한 것이 계속 마음속에 걸린다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat someone violently or roughly.
meanings.example: He used to knock her about when he was drunk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 술에 취하면 그녀를 때리곤 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wander or travel about aimlessly.
meanings.example: He spent a year knocking about Europe after college.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 대학 졸업 후 1년 동안 유럽을 이리저리 돌아다녔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To encourage or urge someone to do something, usually something foolish or risky.
meanings.example: His friends egged him on to jump into the cold water.
meanings.exampleKorean: 친구들은 그에게 차가운 물속으로 뛰어들라고 부추겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To praise or promote something or someone.
meanings.example: The DJ started to big up the new artist.
meanings.exampleKorean: DJ는 신인 아티스트를 치켜세우기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase one's muscle mass through exercise.
meanings.example: He's been bigging up at the gym lately.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 최근 체육관에서 근육을 키우고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To carry someone or something away secretly or mysteriously.
meanings.example: The paintings were spirited away under cover of darkness.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 그림들은 어둠을 틈타 감쪽같이 사라졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage to find time or space for something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: (일정 등을) 짬을 내다, 억지로 끼워 넣다
meanings.example: I'll try to squeeze in a quick meeting before lunch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 점심시간 전에 짧은 회의를 할 짬을 내 보겠습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force a competitor out of a market or position.
meanings.example: Small shops are being squeezed out by the big supermarkets.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대형 슈퍼마켓들 때문에 작은 가게들이 밀려나고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To assemble or build something from parts.
meanings.example: It took me all afternoon to put together the new bookshelf.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 책장을 조립하는 데 오후 내내 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To create or organize (a plan, team, etc.).
meanings.example: We've put together a talented team for the project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 그 프로젝트를 위해 재능 있는 팀을 꾸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To erase or remove by rubbing (e.g., pencil marks).
meanings.example: You can rub out the pencil marks with an eraser.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지우개로 연필 자국을 지울 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To murder or assassinate (slang).
meanings.example: The mob boss ordered his men to rub out the witness.
meanings.exampleKorean: 갱단 두목은 부하들에게 목격자를 없애 버리라고 명령했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a strong desire or craving for someone or something.
meanings.example: I'm longing for a quiet day at the beach.
meanings.exampleKorean: 해변에서의 조용한 하루가 간절히 그립다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use something as a guide or reference for another action.
meanings.example: The dancers key their movements off the beat of the drum.
meanings.exampleKorean: 무용수들은 드럼 비트에 맞춰 동작을 수행한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cheer for or support a team or person.
meanings.example: Which team are you rooting for?
meanings.exampleKorean: 어느 팀을 응원하니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To search for something by moving things around.
meanings.example: She rooted about in her bag for her keys.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 열쇠를 찾으려고 가방 속을 뒤졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something simpler or easier to understand, often in a way that reduces its quality.
meanings.example: The news program has been dumbed down to attract more viewers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 뉴스 프로그램은 더 많은 시청자를 끌기 위해 수준이 낮아졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin to feel love for someone.
meanings.example: They fell in love while working on the project together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 프로젝트를 함께 작업하며 사랑에 빠졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take off all of one's clothes (slang).
meanings.example: It was so hot that I just wanted to get my kit off and jump in the pool.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너무 더워서 옷을 다 벗고 수영장에 뛰어들고 싶었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To exercise, especially at a gym.
meanings.example: I try to work out at least three times a week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 적어도 일주일에 세 번은 운동하려고 노력한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To solve a problem or calculate something.
meanings.example: I'm still trying to work out the cost of the trip.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 아직 여행 경비를 계산해 보려 노력 중이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To result in success or have a good outcome.
meanings.example: Don't worry; I'm sure everything will work out fine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 걱정 마. 모든 게 잘 풀릴 거라고 확신해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To understand someone's character.
meanings.example: I can't work him out; he's very mysterious.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그를 도저히 이해할 수가 없어. 그는 아주 신비로워.

meanings.englishDefinition: To devise or develop a plan.
meanings.example: We need to work out a strategy for the next year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 내년도 전략을 세워야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To promote or speak highly of something or someone.
meanings.example: He's been talking up his new book everywhere.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 어디에서나 자신의 새 책을 홍보하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start a machine or engine.
meanings.example: Wait a minute while I fire up the computer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 컴퓨터 시동 걸 동안 잠깐만 기다려 줘.

meanings.englishDefinition: To excite or motivate someone.
meanings.example: The coach's speech really fired up the team.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코치의 연설이 팀을 정말 고무시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clarify or help explain a situation.
meanings.example: Recent research has shed light on the causes of the disease.
meanings.exampleKorean: 최근의 연구는 그 질병의 원인을 밝혀냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reveal one's previously hidden identity, especially as LGBTQ+.
meanings.example: He decided to come out of the closet to his family.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 가족에게 자신의 정체성을 밝히기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay in one place or remain expectant until something happens.
meanings.example: I'm waiting for the bus.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 버스를 기다리고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To serve someone, especially in a restaurant.
meanings.example: She waits on customers in a busy cafe.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 바쁜 카페에서 손님들 시중을 든다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wait for a result or decision.
meanings.example: We're still waiting on the results of the test.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 아직 테스트 결과를 기다리고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop talking.
meanings.example: Shut up and listen to me!
meanings.exampleKorean: 입 다물고 내 말 좀 들어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To confine or enclose.
meanings.example: The dog was shut up in the laundry room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 개는 세탁실에 갇혀 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove (clothing, etc.).
meanings.example: Take off your shoes before you come in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 들어오기 전에 신발을 벗으세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin flight (of an aircraft).
meanings.example: The plane took off exactly on time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비행기는 정시에 정확히 이륙했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become successful very quickly.
meanings.example: Her singing career really took off after the talent show.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 가수 경력은 장기 자랑 대회 이후 급격히 성공 가도에 올랐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To depart or leave, especially suddenly.
meanings.example: He took off without saying goodbye.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 작별 인사도 없이 가 버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deduct or reduce (a price, amount).
meanings.example: The store took off 20% for the sale.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 가게는 세일을 위해 20%를 깎아 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide with all the necessary clothes or equipment.
meanings.example: They were kitted out with the latest climbing gear.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 최신 등반 장비를 갖추고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rise from bed after sleeping.
meanings.example: I usually get up at 7 o'clock.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 보통 7시에 일어난다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move from a sitting or lying position to a standing position.
meanings.example: He got up to answer the door.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 문을 열어주러 일어났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To organize or arrange (an event, etc.).
meanings.example: We're getting up a petition against the new road.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 새 도로 건설에 반대하는 탄원서를 준비하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dress in a certain way, especially in a costume.
meanings.example: She was all got up as a pirate for the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 파티를 위해 해적 복장을 완벽하게 차려입었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize (Australian/New Zealand slang).
meanings.example: He got up me for making a mess.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 내가 어질러놓은 것에 대해 나에게 화를 냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat something very quickly and greedily.
meanings.example: He wolfed down his breakfast and ran for the bus.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아침밥을 게걸스럽게 먹고 버스를 타러 뛰어갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To explode or fire (of a bomb, gun, etc.).
meanings.example: The bomb went off in a busy shopping center.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭탄이 붐비는 쇼핑센터에서 터졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin to ring or make a loud noise (of an alarm).
meanings.example: My alarm clock didn't go off this morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 아침에 내 알람 시계가 울리지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become spoiled or rotten (of food).
meanings.example: This milk has gone off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 우유는 상했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop liking something or someone.
meanings.example: I've gone off coffee lately; I prefer tea now.
meanings.exampleKorean: 요즘 커피가 싫어졌어. 이제 차가 더 좋아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place.
meanings.example: He's gone off to work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 일하러 나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a bus, train, plane, etc.
meanings.example: I get off at the next stop.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저는 다음 정거장에서 내립니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To escape punishment or receive a light sentence.
meanings.example: He was lucky to get off with just a fine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 벌금만 내고 풀려나다니 운이 좋았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish work.
meanings.example: What time do you get off today?
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 몇 시에 퇴근하니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To experience sexual pleasure or orgasm.
meanings.example: He found it hard to get off without his favorite toys.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 가장 좋아하는 장난감 없이는 쾌감을 느끼기 어려워했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove a piece of clothing.
meanings.example: Get your boots off before you come in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 들어오기 전에 장화 좀 벗어라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To annoy or irritate someone intensely (vulgar slang).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 매우 화나게 하다, 짜증 나게 하다
meanings.example: His constant complaining really pisses me off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 끊임없는 불평은 정말 나를 화나게 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go away (used as an imperative; vulgar slang).
meanings.example: Piss off and leave me alone!
meanings.exampleKorean: 꺼져! 나 좀 혼자 내버려 둬!

meanings.englishDefinition: A sarcastic way of saying 'I'm not listening to you'.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 내 손바닥에 대고 말해 (네 말 안 들어)
meanings.example: I don't want to hear your excuses anymore. Talk to the hand!
meanings.exampleKorean: 네 변명 더 이상 듣고 싶지 않아. 내 손바닥에 대고 말해!

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something more exciting, attractive, or sexually appealing.
meanings.example: They tried to sex up the advertising campaign to attract younger customers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 젊은 고객들을 끌기 위해 광고 캠페인을 더 자극적으로 만들려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To present information in a more sensational or exciting way, especially to gain support.
meanings.example: The government was accused of sexing up the intelligence report.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 정보 보고서를 자극적으로 조작했다는 비난을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To look at or examine something in more detail.
meanings.example: We need to drill down into the data to find the cause of the problem.
meanings.exampleKorean: 문제의 원인을 찾기 위해 데이터를 심층 분석해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ignore or disregard someone or something.
meanings.example: I can't believe she blew me off for her new boyfriend.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 새 남자친구 때문에 나를 무시하다니 믿을 수 없어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To expel gas from the anus; to fart.
meanings.example: Who blew off in the elevator?
meanings.exampleKorean: 누가 엘리베이터에서 방귀 뀌었어?

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat a large amount of food greedily.
meanings.example: We pigged out on pizza and ice cream last night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 어젯밤에 피자와 아이스크림을 엄청나게 먹어치웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To share a task or work together, especially when it's messy or difficult.
meanings.example: If we all muck in, we can finish the cleaning in an hour.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 모두 힘을 합치면 한 시간 안에 청소를 끝낼 수 있어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin a new hobby or activity.
meanings.example: He decided to take up golf in his retirement.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 은퇴 후에 골프를 시작하기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To occupy space or time.
meanings.example: That grand piano takes up half the living room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 그랜드 피아노가 거실의 절반을 차지한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shorten a garment.
meanings.example: These pants are too long; I'll need to take them up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 바지는 너무 길어서 기장을 좀 올려야겠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept an offer or challenge.
meanings.example: I'd like to take up your offer of a lift.
meanings.exampleKorean: 태워다 주시겠다는 제안을 받아들이고 싶습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consider an idea or plan casually, without being serious.
meanings.example: She's toying with the idea of moving to Japan.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 일본으로 이사할까 하는 생각을 가볍게 해보고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To play with something idly.
meanings.example: He sat toying with his glass while he waited.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 기다리는 동안 잔을 만지작거리며 앉아 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause something to start burning.
meanings.example: The rebels set the government building on fire.
meanings.exampleKorean: 반군들이 정부 청사에 불을 질렀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To excite or inspire someone greatly.
meanings.example: Her performance set the audience on fire.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 공연은 관객들을 열광시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force a pitcher to be replaced by hitting them well.
meanings.example: The home team knocked the starting pitcher out of the box in the second inning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 홈 팀은 2회에 선발 투수를 강판시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To confine someone by locking a door.
meanings.example: The students were locked in the classroom.
meanings.exampleKorean: 학생들은 교실에 갇혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fix or commit to (a price, rate, etc.).
meanings.example: It's a good time to lock in a low mortgage rate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 낮은 주택담보대출 금리를 고정하기에 좋은 시기다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent someone from entering by locking the door.
meanings.example: I accidentally locked myself out of the house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 실수로 집 문을 잠그고 나와 버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent employees from working during a labor dispute.
meanings.example: The management decided to lock out the workers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경영진은 직장 폐쇄를 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk enthusiastically about a specialized subject.

meanings.exampleKorean: 새 시스템이 어떻게 돌아가는지 파악하는 데 시간이 좀 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To masturbate (slang, especially of a male).

meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 오후 내내 야동을 보며 자위했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste time or behave aimlessly.

meanings.exampleKorean: 그만 빈둥거리고 일 좀 해!

meanings.englishDefinition: To attack or go after someone or something.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be sexually unfaithful.

meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그가 비서와 바람을 피우고 있었다는 것을 알아차렸다.

meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그 논쟁에서 언니 편을 들기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move from a lying to a sitting position.

meanings.exampleKorean: 환자는 마침내 앉을 수 있을 정도로 회복되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a sexual encounter with someone (slang).

meanings.englishDefinition: To understand or solve something after thinking about it.

meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 결혼식 총비용을 계산해 봐야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize, annoy, or punish someone repeatedly and unfairly.

meanings.englishDefinition: To vomit.

meanings.exampleKorean: 그 조사는 몇 가지 흥미로운 새 사실들을 밝혀냈다.

meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 무거운 쇼핑백들 때문에 움직이기 힘들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move to a lower level; to sink or fall.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be recorded or remembered in a certain way.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop working (of a computer or system).

meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 연설은 청중들에게 아주 좋은 반응을 얻었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To perform oral sex on someone (slang).

meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 파티 후에 그녀에게 구강성교를 해주고 싶어 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To support or help someone.

meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 가족은 그만두겠다는 나의 결정을 지지해 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a copy of computer data.

meanings.exampleKorean: 파일을 정기적으로 백업하는 것을 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move a vehicle backwards.

meanings.exampleKorean: 그 스타 투수는 상대 팀 최고의 선수를 아웃시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To declare one's sexual orientation publicly.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wash dishes and cutlery after a meal.

meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 밤은 네가 설거지할 차례야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make neat or tidy by trimming.

meanings.exampleKorean: 정원의 울타리를 깔끔하게 다듬어야겠다.

meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 길고양이를 거두어주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reprimand someone loudly or harshly.

meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님은 그가 또 늦자 호되게 야단치셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place or vehicle.
meanings.example: Get out of the car now!
meanings.exampleKorean: 당장 차에서 내려!

meanings.englishDefinition: To become known (of a secret).

meanings.exampleKorean: 이 소식이 알려지면 문제가 생길 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce or publish something.

meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 금요일까지 보고서를 내놓기를 희망한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lower one's body; to duck.

meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 나에게 딱 봐도 장물인 노트북을 팔려고 했다.

meanings.exampleKorean: 토론은 발표에 이어서 진행될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To play truant; to skip school or work without permission (slang).
meanings.example: They decided to bunk off school and go to the cinema.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 학교를 빼먹고 영화관에 가기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To engage in sexual activity, especially with a new partner (slang).

meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 파티에서 누구랑 눈이 맞았는지 봤니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To go away; to leave (mild slang).

meanings.exampleKorean: 장난 그만치고 숙제 끝내라.

meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 허리케인이 닥치기 전에 덧창을 판자로 단단히 고정했다.

meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 다가올 불황에 대비해 단단히 준비하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remain brave and determined in a difficult situation.

meanings.exampleKorean: 최근의 상실에도 불구하고 그녀는 잘 견뎌내고 있다.

meanings.exampleKorean: 선장은 폭풍을 피하기 위해 해안가 쪽으로 다가가기로 결정했다.

meanings.exampleKorean: 배는 암석이 많은 해안에서 멀어져야 했다.

meanings.exampleKorean: 적함이 나타나자 전투 배치를 알리는 북소리가 울렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail (of a negotiation, relationship, etc.).

meanings.exampleKorean: 두 회사 간의 협상이 결렬되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend time in a relaxed way, especially when waiting for something.

meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 카드놀이를 하며 오후 시간을 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To choose not to participate in something.
meanings.example: Employees can opt out of the company pension scheme.
meanings.exampleKorean: 직원들은 회사 연금 제도에 참여하지 않기로 선택할 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To order a player to leave the field (in sports).
meanings.example: The referee sent off the captain for a dangerous tackle.
meanings.exampleKorean: 심판은 위험한 태클을 한 주장을 퇴장시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mail or dispatch something.
meanings.example: I sent off the application yesterday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 어제 지원서를 발송했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To see someone depart and say goodbye.
meanings.example: The whole family went to the airport to send him off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 온 가족이 그를 배웅하러 공항에 갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid talking about the main topic directly.
meanings.example: Stop beating around the bush and tell me what you really want.
meanings.exampleKorean: 말 그만 돌리고 진짜 원하는 게 뭔지 말해 봐.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop trying or doing something; to surrender.
meanings.example: Don't give up on your dreams.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신의 꿈을 포기하지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop a habit (e.g., smoking).
meanings.example: He decided to give up smoking for his health.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 건강을 위해 담배를 끊기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very heavily armed with many weapons.
meanings.example: The rebels were armed to the teeth with modern weapons.
meanings.exampleKorean: 반군들은 현대식 무기로 완전 무장하고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take dangerous or unnecessary risks.
meanings.example: Investing all your money in one stock is playing with fire.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 돈을 한 주식에 투자하는 것은 위험한 짓이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deceive someone by giving them something of lower quality.
meanings.example: They tried to fob me off with a cheap imitation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 나에게 값싼 모조품을 속여서 팔려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid answering a question by giving an unsatisfactory answer.
meanings.example: He tried to fob off my questions about the budget.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 예산에 대한 나의 질문을 얼버무리려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To admit that one has done something wrong or embarrassing.
meanings.example: Nobody would own up to breaking the window.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아무도 창문을 깬 것을 자백하려 하지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall over suddenly, especially from exhaustion or illness.
meanings.example: He keeled over in the heat and had to be taken to hospital.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 더위 때문에 갑자기 쓰러졌고 병원으로 이송되어야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To watch or monitor someone or something carefully.
meanings.example: Could you keep an eye on my bag while I go to the bathroom?
meanings.exampleKorean: 화장실 가는 동안 제 가방 좀 봐주실 수 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To remember or consider something.
meanings.example: Please bear in mind that the office will be closed tomorrow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내일은 사무실이 휴무라는 점을 명심해 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decide not to do something because of fear.
meanings.example: He was going to ask her out, but he chickened out at the last minute.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그녀에게 데이트 신청을 하려 했지만, 마지막 순간에 겁을 먹고 포기했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suffer from excessive longing, regret, or envy.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 몹시 질투하다, 부러워 죽다, 애간장을 태우다
meanings.example: Look at my new car and eat your heart out!
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 새 차 좀 봐, 부러워 죽겠지!

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue doing something.
meanings.example: She kept on working despite the noise.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 소음에도 불구하고 계속 일했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To destroy completely; to cause to disappear.
meanings.example: The cold weather killed off all the flowers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 추운 날씨가 꽃들을 모두 죽여 버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be disqualified from a game after committing too many fouls.
meanings.example: Their star player fouled out in the fourth quarter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들의 스타 플레이어가 4쿼터에 5반칙으로 퇴장당했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hit someone so that they fall to the ground.
meanings.example: He was knocked down by a bus.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 버스에 치였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To demolish a building.
meanings.example: They are going to knock down the old library.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 낡은 도서관을 철거할 예정이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce a price.
meanings.example: The dealer knocked down the price to $5,000.
meanings.exampleKorean: 딜러는 가격을 5,000달러로 인하했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To die (a polite or euphemistic way to say die).
meanings.example: Her grandmother passed away peacefully in her sleep.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 할머니는 주무시는 동안 평온하게 세상을 떠나셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To focus more closely on something (with a camera or computer screen).
meanings.example: Can you zoom in on the map?
meanings.exampleKorean: 지도를 확대해 주시겠어요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To adjust a camera lens or screen to show a wider area.
meanings.example: Zoom out so we can see the whole building.
meanings.exampleKorean: 건물 전체를 볼 수 있게 축소해 보세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To interrupt a conversation rudely.
meanings.example: I'm sorry to butt in, but I couldn't help overhearing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 참견해서 죄송합니다만, 우연히 엿듣게 되어서요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone pregnant (slang).
meanings.example: He's terrified that he's knocked her up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그녀를 임신시켰을까 봐 겁에 질려 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare something quickly, especially food.
meanings.example: I can knock up a quick meal for us.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리를 위해 식사를 뚝딱 준비할 수 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wake someone up by knocking on their door.
meanings.example: Can you knock me up at 7 o'clock tomorrow?
meanings.exampleKorean: 내일 7시에 나 좀 깨워 줄래?

meanings.englishDefinition: To start working hard or focusing on a task.
meanings.example: If you want to pass the exam, you need to buckle down and study.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시험에 합격하고 싶다면 마음 잡고 공부해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce something in large quantities and quickly, often mechanically.
meanings.example: The factory cranks out thousands of toys every day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 공장은 매일 수천 개의 장난감을 쏟아낸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop sleeping.
meanings.example: I woke up early this morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 오늘 아침 일찍 일어났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become aware of a situation.
meanings.example: You need to wake up and realize what's happening around you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너는 정신 차리고 네 주변에서 무슨 일이 일어나는지 알아야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent someone or something from harming you.
meanings.example: I take vitamin C to ward off colds.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 감기를 막기 위해 비타민 C를 먹는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare for exercise or an activity by doing gentle movements.
meanings.example: It's important to warm up before you start running.
meanings.exampleKorean: 달리기 시작하기 전에 몸을 푸는 것이 중요하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become friendlier or more enthusiastic.
meanings.example: The audience took a while to warm up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 청중들이 활기를 띠는 데 시간이 좀 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something warm.
meanings.example: Shall I warm up some soup for you?
meanings.exampleKorean: 수프 좀 데워 줄까?

meanings.englishDefinition: To invent a story or excuse.
meanings.example: He made up a story about his car breaking down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 차가 고장 났다는 이야기를 지어냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To form or constitute something.
meanings.example: Women make up 50% of the company's employees.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여성이 그 회사 직원의 50%를 구성한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become friends again after an argument.
meanings.example: They argued but they made up soon after.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 다퉜지만 곧 화해했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To apply cosmetics to the face.
meanings.example: She takes a long time to make up in the morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 아침에 화장하는 데 시간이 오래 걸린다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To compensate for something lost or missed.
meanings.example: I'll make up the time I missed next week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 빠진 시간을 다음 주에 보충하겠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To release strong emotions or energy, especially by doing something active.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 울분을 토하다, 스트레스를 풀다, 열을 식히다
meanings.example: I go jogging after work to blow off steam.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 스트레스를 풀기 위해 퇴근 후 조깅을 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide more details to make a plan or idea more complete.
meanings.example: You need to flesh out your argument with more examples.
meanings.exampleKorean: 더 많은 예시를 들어 당신의 주장을 구체화해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To succeed in doing something difficult.
meanings.example: I didn't think they could pull off such a big deal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그들이 그렇게 큰 거래를 성사시킬 줄 몰랐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drive a vehicle to the side of the road and stop.
meanings.example: We pulled off at a rest area to have lunch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 점심을 먹기 위해 휴게소에 차를 세웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To protrude upwards.
meanings.example: His hair was sticking up at the back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 뒷머리가 위로 삐져나와 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rob someone using a weapon.
meanings.example: The gang tried to stick up the local liquor store.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 패거리는 동네 술집을 털려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put an end to something; to stop or prevent.
meanings.example: The sudden rain put the kibosh on our picnic plans.
meanings.exampleKorean: 갑작스러운 비가 우리의 소풍 계획을 망쳐 놓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove by washing.
meanings.example: Will this stain wash out?
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 얼룩이 씻겨 나갈까요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To cancel an event because of rain.
meanings.example: The game was washed out after only three innings.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경기는 단 3이닝 만에 비로 취소되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become very thin and weak, usually because of illness.
meanings.example: He's wasting away because he won't eat anything.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아무것도 먹으려 하지 않아 몸이 축나고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To protect or take care of someone or something.
meanings.example: The mother bird watches over her chicks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어미 새가 새끼들을 보살핀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To outdo or be superior to someone.
meanings.example: He's always trying to one-up his brother.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 형보다 한 수 앞서려 노력한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave very quickly.
meanings.example: I must dash off now, or I'll be late.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지금 급히 가야 해요, 안 그러면 늦겠어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To write something very quickly.
meanings.example: She dashed off a quick note to her mother.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 어머니께 짧은 메모를 뚝딱 써 내려갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend time in a place without any particular purpose.
meanings.example: There were some teenagers hanging around the corner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 십 대 몇 명이 길모퉁이에서 서성거리고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to persuade people who already agree with you.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 이미 동조하는 사람에게 설득하다, 공자 앞에서 문자 쓰다
meanings.example: I know you agree with me, so I'm just preaching to the choir.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신이 내 의견에 동의한다는 걸 아니까, 제가 하는 말은 이미 다 아는 소리겠네요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To defend or support someone or something.
meanings.example: You must learn to stick up for yourself.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너 자신을 옹호하는 법을 배워야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To relax completely.
meanings.example: I'm just going to chill out at home this weekend.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이번 주말에는 그냥 집에서 푹 쉴 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To calm down after being angry or excited.
meanings.example: Chill out! There's no need to get so upset.
meanings.exampleKorean: 진정해! 그렇게 화낼 필요 없어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually disappear or lose effectiveness.
meanings.example: The effects of the painkiller are starting to wear off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 진통제 효과가 서서히 나타나기 시작한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become thin or damaged through long use.
meanings.example: I've worn out these shoes in just six months.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 신발은 신은 지 6개월 만에 다 해졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone extremely tired.
meanings.example: The long journey wore me out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 긴 여정이 나를 매우 지치게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave badly or cause trouble.
meanings.example: The kids started acting up because they were bored.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들이 지루해서 말썽을 부리기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To malfunction or work incorrectly (of a machine or body part).
meanings.koreanDefinition: (기계 등이) 말을 안 듣다, 오작동하다, (병이) 도지다
meanings.example: My old knee injury is acting up again.
meanings.exampleKorean: 예전 무릎 부상이 다시 도지고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a liquid weaker by adding water.
meanings.example: The juice was too strong, so I watered it down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주스가 너무 진해서 물을 좀 탔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make an idea or opinion less strong or offensive.
meanings.example: The government was forced to water down the new law.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 새로운 법안을 완화할 수밖에 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attribute something to a particular cause.
meanings.example: Let's chalk it up to experience and move on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그건 경험 때문이라고 치고 넘어갑시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sleep in a tent outside.
meanings.example: We decided to camp out under the stars.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 별빛 아래서 야영하기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay in one place for a long time, especially while waiting.
meanings.example: Fans camped out for days to get tickets for the concert.
meanings.exampleKorean: 팬들은 콘서트 티켓을 구하기 위해 며칠 동안 노숙하며 기다렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To identify or explain exactly what is wrong or different.
meanings.example: Something is wrong, but I can't quite put my finger on it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 뭔가 잘못됐는데, 그게 뭔지 딱 집어 말할 수가 없네.

meanings.englishDefinition: To say or repeat something quickly and easily.
meanings.example: She rattled off the names of all the presidents.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 모든 대통령의 이름을 줄줄 읊었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove people or things that are not wanted or useful.
meanings.example: The initial interviews were used to weed out unsuitable candidates.
meanings.exampleKorean: 첫 면접은 부적격한 후보자들을 걸러내기 위해 사용되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce something in large quantities quickly and often carelessly.
meanings.example: The studio churns out two or three low-budget films a month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 스튜디오는 한 달에 두세 편의 저예산 영화를 찍어낸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue doing something or using something.
meanings.example: If you stick with it, you'll eventually see results.
meanings.exampleKorean: 계속 노력하다 보면 결국 결과를 보게 될 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay close to someone.
meanings.example: Stick with me and you'll be fine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 곁에 있어, 그러면 괜찮을 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finalize or confirm the details of something.
meanings.example: We need to nail down a date for the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 회의 날짜를 확정해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force someone to make a decision or give a clear answer.
meanings.example: It's hard to pin him down to a specific time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그에게서 구체적인 시간에 대한 확답을 얻어내기 어렵다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To identify something clearly.
meanings.example: The police are trying to pin down the exact cause of the accident.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 사고의 정확한 원인을 규명하려 노력하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decide not to do something you had agreed to do.
meanings.example: He agreed to help, but he backed out at the last minute.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 돕기로 했지만 마지막 순간에 빠져 버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To need to go home or go to bed (often at midnight).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 집에 갈 시간이다, (밤늦어) 자야 할 시간이다
meanings.example: It's almost midnight; I'd better leave before I turn into a pumpkin.
meanings.exampleKorean: 거의 자정이네. 더 늦기 전에(호박으로 변하기 전에) 가야겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To guess the truth from the information available.
meanings.example: I saw them together and put two and two together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들이 함께 있는 걸 보고 정황을 미루어 짐작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go somewhere with someone, especially when not invited.
meanings.example: Do you mind if I tag along?
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 따라가도 될까?

meanings.englishDefinition: To tell a bold or obvious lie without shame.
meanings.example: He was lying through his teeth when he said he didn't know her.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀를 모른다고 말할 때 그는 새빨간 거짓말을 하고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To explode or be destroyed by an explosion.
meanings.example: The building blew up after the gas leak.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가스 누출 후 건물이 폭발했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become suddenly very angry.
meanings.example: He blew up at me for no reason.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아무 이유 없이 나에게 화를 냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To inflate something (e.g., a balloon).
meanings.example: Can you help me blow up these balloons?
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 풍선들 부는 것 좀 도와줄래?

meanings.englishDefinition: To enlarge a photograph.
meanings.example: I'd like to have this photo blown up and framed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 사진을 확대해서 액자에 끼우고 싶다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enclose or surround with a fence.
meanings.example: We need to fence in the garden to keep the deer out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사슴이 못 들어오게 정원에 울타리를 쳐야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To restrict someone's freedom.
meanings.example: He felt fenced in by the rules and regulations.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 규칙과 규제들에 갇힌 기분이 들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce or do something quickly and without much effort.
meanings.example: She tossed off the article in less than an hour.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 한 시간도 안 되어 기사를 뚝딱 써냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To masturbate (slang, especially of a male).
meanings.example: He spent most of his time tossing off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 대부분의 시간을 자위하며 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a huge amount of something.
meanings.example: He's got money coming out of his ears.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 돈이 아주 많아 주체할 수 없을 정도다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To persuade someone to support you or agree with you.
meanings.example: It took a long time to win her over to our side.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀를 우리 편으로 설득하는 데 오랜 시간이 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend time repairing or improving something.
meanings.example: I'm still working on my novel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 아직 소설 집필 작업을 하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to persuade someone to do something.
meanings.example: My parents are working on me to go to law school.
meanings.exampleKorean: 부모님은 내가 법대에 가도록 나를 설득하고 계신다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish or complete something.
meanings.example: Let's wrap up this meeting and go to lunch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 회의를 마무리하고 점심 먹으러 갑시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cover something in paper or cloth (e.g., a gift).
meanings.example: Have you wrapped up all the Christmas presents?
meanings.exampleKorean: 크리스마스 선물 다 포장했니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To put on warm clothes.
meanings.example: Wrap up warm; it's freezing outside.
meanings.exampleKorean: 든든히 껴입어라. 밖은 매우 춥다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To record something on paper.
meanings.example: Write down your phone number, please.
meanings.exampleKorean: 전화번호 좀 적어 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start a professional practice, especially as a lawyer or doctor.
meanings.example: After passing the bar, he decided to hang out his shingle in his hometown.
meanings.exampleKorean: 변호사 시험에 합격한 후, 그는 고향에서 개업하기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become weak and dry and eventually die (of plants or people).
meanings.example: Without reform, the organization will simply wither away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 개혁 없이는 그 조직은 그냥 사라져 버릴 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very close to a particular state or quality.
meanings.example: His behavior verges on the ridiculous.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 행동은 거의 말도 안 되는 수준에 가깝다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deal with a situation as it develops, rather than following a plan.
meanings.example: I don't have a strict itinerary; I'll just play it by ear.
meanings.exampleKorean: 엄격한 여행 일정은 없어요. 그냥 상황 봐서 하려고요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try as hard as possible to make a good impression.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 최선을 다하다, 좋은 인상을 주려 애쓰다
meanings.example: You need to put your best foot forward at the interview.
meanings.exampleKorean: 면접에서 좋은 인상을 주기 위해 최선을 다해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To defeat a proposal or motion by voting against it.
meanings.example: The committee voted down the proposal to increase taxes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 위원회는 증세 제안을 투표로 부결시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter a place or vehicle.
meanings.example: Get in the car, we're leaving.
meanings.exampleKorean: 차에 타, 우리 출발해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrive at a place.
meanings.example: What time does your flight get in?
meanings.exampleKorean: 네 비행기는 몇 시에 도착하니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To be elected.
meanings.example: Do you think the Labor party will get in?
meanings.exampleKorean: 노동당이 당선될 것 같니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat a large amount of carbohydrates before a race or exercise.
meanings.example: Marathon runners often carb up the night before a race.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마라톤 선수들은 종종 경기 전날 밤에 탄수화물을 많이 섭취한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give someone control of or responsibility for something.
meanings.example: The captain handed over command to his deputy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선장은 부관에게 지휘권을 인계했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give something to someone with one's hand.
meanings.example: He handed over the money to the shopkeeper.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 점원에게 돈을 건네주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get to the point without wasting time.
meanings.example: I don't have much time, so let's cut to the chase.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시간이 많지 않으니 바로 본론으로 들어갑시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn the yards of a ship so as to catch the wind on the opposite side.
meanings.example: The crew had to brace about as the wind changed direction.
meanings.exampleKorean: 바람의 방향이 바뀌자 선원들은 돛대를 반대편으로 돌려야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To brace the yards of a ship so as to catch the wind at a slant.
meanings.example: We braced by to make the most of the side wind.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 측면 바람을 최대한 활용하기 위해 돛대를 비스듬히 고정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn the yards of a ship so that they are more nearly at right angles to the keel.
meanings.example: As the wind moved aft, the sailors braced in the yards.
meanings.exampleKorean: 바람이 뒤쪽으로 이동하자 선원들은 돛대를 선체와 직각으로 돌렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reprimand or scold someone severely.
meanings.example: The teacher told him off for talking during the test.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님은 시험 중에 말을 했다고 그를 야단치셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept and follow a law, rule, or decision.
meanings.example: Competitors must abide by the judge's decision.
meanings.exampleKorean: 참가자들은 심판의 결정에 따라야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually disappear or become thinner through constant friction or use.
meanings.example: The stone steps had been worn away by centuries of use.
meanings.exampleKorean: 돌계단은 수 세기 동안의 사용으로 닳아 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take further action after a previous action.
meanings.example: The doctor will follow up with you after the lab results come in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 검사 결과가 나오면 의사가 당신과 추가 상담을 할 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To die; to stop working (of a machine).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 숨을 거두다, (기계 등이) 수명을 다하다, 멈추다
meanings.example: My old lawnmower finally gave up the ghost yesterday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 낡은 잔디 깎는 기계가 어제 마침내 수명을 다해 멈췄다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a feeling of resentment or a desire to harm someone.
meanings.example: The boss really has it in for me ever since I pointed out his mistake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 그의 실수를 지적한 이후로 사장은 나에게 정말 앙심을 품고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try extremely hard to please or help someone.
meanings.example: She bent over backwards to make sure the guests were comfortable.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 손님들이 편안하게 지낼 수 있도록 필사적으로 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hear news or rumors from someone else.
meanings.example: I heard through the grapevine that they are getting a divorce.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들이 이혼한다는 소문을 풍문으로 들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reveal a secret or tell someone something.
meanings.example: Don't let on that I told you about the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 파티에 대해 말해줬다는 거 입 밖에 내지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very sexually attracted to someone (informal).
meanings.example: She's clearly got the hots for her new trainer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 확실히 그녀의 새 트레이너에게 푹 빠져 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be permanent and impossible to change.
meanings.example: The plans aren't set in stone yet, so we can still make changes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 계획이 아직 확정된 건 아니라서, 여전히 변경할 수 있어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be overlooked or neglected within a system.
meanings.example: In a large school, some students can easily fall through the cracks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 큰 학교에서는 일부 학생들이 쉽게 소외될 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To laugh loudly and suddenly.
meanings.example: He told a joke that made everyone crack up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 농담을 해서 모두가 빵 터졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a mental breakdown.
meanings.example: Under all that pressure, he finally cracked up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 모든 압박 속에서 그는 결국 정신적으로 무너졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put something in order; to finish or settle something.
meanings.example: We need to square away a few more details before the event.
meanings.exampleKorean: 행사 전에 몇 가지 세부 사항을 더 마무리해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To direct all one's attention towards a particular thing.
meanings.example: The investigation zeroed in on the main suspect.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수사는 주 용의자에게 초점을 맞췄다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay or give something, especially reluctantly.
meanings.example: I had to fork over $50 for the repair.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수리비로 50달러나 내야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To relax and do very little.
meanings.example: After a hard week, I just want to kick back and watch a movie.
meanings.exampleKorean: 힘든 한 주를 보낸 뒤라 그냥 편히 쉬면서 영화나 보고 싶다.

meanings.englishDefinition: An illegal payment made in return for a favor or service.
meanings.example: He was accused of taking kickbacks from contractors.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 계약업체들로부터 리베이트를 받은 혐의로 기소되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Praise or credit for an achievement.
meanings.example: You deserve a pat on the back for all your hard work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그동안 열심히 노력한 것에 대해 칭찬받을 만하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A very mild punishment for a crime or mistake.
meanings.example: The judge gave him a mere slap on the wrist for his first offense.
meanings.exampleKorean: 판사는 그의 초범에 대해 단지 솜방망이 처벌을 내렸을 뿐이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rely or depend on someone or something.
meanings.example: You can always count on me for help.
meanings.exampleKorean: 도움이 필요하면 언제든 나를 믿어도 돼.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start behaving in a better way.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 마음을 잡다, 행동을 똑바로 하다, 새사람이 되다
meanings.example: He's decided to clean up his act and find a real job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 마음을 잡고 제대로 된 직장을 구하기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To overcharge someone or cheat them.
meanings.example: We were ripped off by that taxi driver.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 그 택시 기사에게 바가지를 썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To copy or steal an idea or work.
meanings.example: That movie is a total rip-off of an old Japanese film.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 영화는 옛날 일본 영화를 완전히 표절한 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To admit defeat or stop trying.
meanings.example: After trying to fix the car for hours, I finally threw in the towel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몇 시간 동안 차를 고치려 애쓰다 결국 포기했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drive faster or hurry up.
meanings.example: We're going to be late! Step on it!
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 늦겠어! 좀 밟아 봐(빨리 가)!

meanings.englishDefinition: To pass by or end without causing serious trouble.
meanings.example: I hope the scandal will soon blow over.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 스캔들이 곧 잠잠해지기를 바란다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To board a bus, train, plane, etc.
meanings.example: I got on the wrong bus by mistake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 실수로 버스를 잘못 탔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be successful or make progress.
meanings.example: He's getting on well in his new job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 새 직장에서 잘해나가고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become old (informal).
meanings.example: He's getting on in years now.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 이제 나이가 좀 들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a good relationship with someone.
meanings.example: I get on well with my neighbors.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 이웃들과 잘 지낸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue doing something.
meanings.example: Stop talking and get on with your work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그만 떠들고 하던 일이나 계속해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find someone or something after a thorough search.
meanings.example: I finally managed to track down a copy of that rare book.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 마침내 그 희귀 서적 한 권을 찾아냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be deeply affected or upset by something; to consider something seriously.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 진지하게 받아들이다, 마음에 새기다, (비판 등에) 상처받다
meanings.example: Don't take his criticism too much to heart.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 비판을 너무 마음에 담아두지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be sold very cheaply.
meanings.example: The house went for a song because it needed so much work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 집은 수리할 곳이 너무 많아서 헐값에 팔렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spoil or ruin something through clumsiness or a mistake (slang).
meanings.example: I really cocked up that interview.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나 그 면접 완전히 망쳤어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter a building by force, usually to steal something.
meanings.example: Someone tried to break in while we were away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리가 없는 동안 누군가 침입하려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wear new shoes until they become comfortable.
meanings.example: It takes a few days to break in new leather boots.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 가죽 부츠를 길들이는 데는 며칠이 걸린다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To interrupt a conversation.
meanings.example: As she was talking, he broke in with a question.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 말하고 있을 때 그가 질문하며 끼어들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start suddenly (of war, fire, or disease).
meanings.example: Fighting has broken out on the border.
meanings.exampleKorean: 국경 지대에서 전투가 발생했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To escape from prison.
meanings.example: Three prisoners managed to break out last night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 죄수 세 명이 어젯밤에 탈옥했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To develop a skin rash or pimples.
meanings.example: I break out in a rash if I eat strawberries.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 딸기를 먹으면 발진이 돋는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a way that is likely to cause problems or trouble.
meanings.example: Driving that fast on a rainy night is just asking for it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비 오는 밤에 그렇게 빨리 운전하는 건 정말 사고를 자초하는 일이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spoil someone's pleasure or plans.
meanings.example: I don't want to rain on your parade, but the concert has been cancelled.
meanings.exampleKorean: 찬물을 끼얹고 싶지는 않지만, 그 콘서트 취소됐어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To understand something after a period of time.
meanings.example: It took me a while to catch on to what they were doing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들이 무엇을 하고 있는지 알아채는 데 시간이 좀 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become popular.
meanings.example: I don't think this new fashion will ever catch on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 새로운 패션이 유행할 것 같지는 않다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A phrase used to wish a dead person eternal rest.
meanings.example: May he rest in peace.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 평화롭게 잠들기를 바랍니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat someone badly because one is feeling angry or frustrated about something else.
meanings.example: I know you've had a bad day, but don't take it out on me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 힘든 하루를 보낸 건 알겠지만 나한테 화풀이하지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join a conversation by providing one's own opinion.
meanings.example: "I agree," Dave chimed in.
meanings.exampleKorean: "저도 동의해요," 데이브가 맞장구를 쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To help someone out of a difficult situation, especially a financial one.
meanings.example: The government had to bail out the failing banks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 파산 위기의 은행들을 구제해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To jump out of an aircraft using a parachute.
meanings.example: The pilot was forced to bail out over the sea.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조련사는 바다 위에서 낙하산으로 탈출해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove water from a boat with a bucket.
meanings.example: We spent hours bailing out the boat after the storm.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭풍이 지난 후 우리는 몇 시간 동안 배의 물을 퍼냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something stronger or more effective.
meanings.example: The company decided to beef up its security system.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 보안 시스템을 강화하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To realize something or start behaving more sensibly (slang).
meanings.example: You need to cop on and start taking your studies seriously.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너 정신 차리고 공부 좀 진지하게 시작해야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something with a lot of enthusiasm, energy, or by spending a lot of money.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 신나게 하다, (돈·에너지를) 아낌없이 쏟아붓다, 아주 공들여 하다
meanings.example: They really went to town on the wedding decorations.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 결혼식 장식에 정말 아낌없이 공을 들였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use all one's energy or resources to achieve something.
meanings.example: The team went all out to win the championship.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 팀은 우승을 위해 전력을 다했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To study a particular subject as one's main focus at university.
meanings.example: She decided to major in Economics.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 경제학을 전공하기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do everything possible to make something successful.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 온갖 수단을 다 동원하다, 전력을 다하다
meanings.example: The hospital pulled out all the stops to save the child's life.
meanings.exampleKorean: 병원은 아이의 생명을 구하기 위해 온갖 수단을 다 동원했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To maintain a public impression of prosperity or well-being despite financial or personal difficulties.
meanings.example: Even though they were struggling with debt, they tried to keep up appearances.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 빚 때문에 힘들었지만 체면을 유지하려 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gather people or animals together.
meanings.example: The police rounded up several suspects for questioning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 심문을 위해 용의자 여러 명을 연행(소집)했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase a number to the nearest whole number.
meanings.example: Let's round up the total to $50.
meanings.exampleKorean: 총액을 50달러로 올림합시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clog or make something messy with a sticky substance.
meanings.example: Old oil can gunk up the engine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오래된 기름은 엔진을 막히게 할 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decrease a number to the nearest whole number.
meanings.example: The price was rounded down to $20.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가격은 20달러로 내림 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To meet and spend time with people.
meanings.example: We should get together for coffee sometime.
meanings.exampleKorean: 언제 커피 한잔하러 만납시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay money, especially when it is owed or required (informal).
meanings.example: He finally ponied up the money he owed me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 마침내 나에게 빚진 돈을 갚았다(내놓았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To make an offensive gesture with the middle finger.
meanings.example: He flipped me off when I honked at him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 경적을 울리자 그는 나에게 손가락 욕을 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lose one's temper suddenly and unexpectedly.
meanings.example: He tends to fly off the handle over small things.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 사소한 일에도 버럭 화를 내는 경향이 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep something under control or secret.
meanings.example: The government is trying to keep a lid on inflation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 인플레이션을 억제하려 노력하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a mistake or bungle something (informal).
meanings.example: I really goofed up my chances of getting that job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나 그 직장을 구할 기회를 완전히 망쳐 버렸어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take a risk or put oneself in a vulnerable position by saying or doing something bold.
meanings.example: I'm going to go out on a limb and say that this will be a huge success.
meanings.exampleKorean: 위험을 무릅쓰고 말하자면, 이건 대성공일 거예요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gain control or influence over someone.
meanings.example: Once she gets her claws into him, he'll never be able to leave.
meanings.exampleKorean: 일단 그녀가 그를 손에 넣으면, 그는 절대 떠나지 못할 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To repel an attacker or enemy.
meanings.example: The soldiers managed to beat off the attack.
meanings.exampleKorean: 병사들은 공격을 물리치는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To masturbate (slang, especially of a male).
meanings.example: He spent all night beating off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 밤새 자위하며 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To admit to something; to confess (informal).
meanings.example: Come on, fess up! Who ate the last cookie?
meanings.exampleKorean: 어서, 솔직히 말해! 마지막 쿠키 누가 먹었어?

meanings.englishDefinition: To disable a section of code by turning it into a comment.
meanings.example: You should comment out that old function before testing the new one.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 함수를 테스트하기 전에 예전 함수는 주석 처리해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to have all the same things as one's neighbors to avoid looking inferior.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 이웃에 뒤지지 않으려 애쓰다, 남에게 과시하며 경쟁하다
meanings.example: He's always buying the latest gadgets just to keep up with the Joneses.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 이웃에게 뒤처지지 않으려고 항상 최신 기기들을 사곤 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To establish or start something (a business, organization, etc.).
meanings.example: She decided to set up her own design company.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 자신의 디자인 회사를 설립하기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrange or prepare something.
meanings.example: Can you help me set up the stage for the play?
meanings.exampleKorean: 연극 무대 설치하는 것 좀 도와줄래?

meanings.englishDefinition: To trick someone into being blamed for something they didn't do.
meanings.example: I didn't steal the money! I was set up!
meanings.exampleKorean: 난 돈을 훔치지 않았어! 누명을 쓴 거야!

meanings.englishDefinition: To rest in a horizontal position.
meanings.example: I'm going to lie down for a few minutes before dinner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저녁 먹기 전에 몇 분만 좀 누워 있어야겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be already taken, reserved, or in a relationship.
meanings.example: Sorry, this chair is already spoken for.
meanings.exampleKorean: 죄송합니다, 이 의자는 이미 주인이 있어요.
