meanings.englishDefinition: To represent someone's views or interests.
meanings.example: I can't speak for the whole team, but I'm happy with the decision.
meanings.exampleKorean: 팀 전체를 대변할 순 없지만, 저는 그 결정에 만족합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To express one's own opinions rather than having someone else do it.
meanings.example: The results of the test speak for themselves.
meanings.exampleKorean: 테스트 결과가 모든 걸 말해준다. (자명하다)

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing something new or different from one's usual activities.
meanings.example: The company is planning to branch out into the European market.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 유럽 시장으로 진출할 계획이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce to the most essential point.
meanings.example: The problem boils down to a lack of communication.
meanings.exampleKorean: 문제는 결국 소통의 부재로 귀결된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be the most important or fundamental part of a situation.
meanings.example: In the end, it all comes down to money.
meanings.exampleKorean: 결국, 모든 것은 돈에 달려 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something with a quick movement of the finger.
meanings.example: He flicked a piece of lint off his jacket.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 재킷에서 보풀 하나를 털어냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to deal with or understand a difficult problem or situation.
meanings.example: It's time we came to grips with the reality of the situation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이제 우리가 상황의 실체를 정면으로 마주할 때다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually accept a sad or difficult situation.
meanings.example: He's still coming to terms with his father's death.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 여전히 아버지의 죽음을 받아들이려 애쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk to someone in a friendly way because you are sexually attracted to them.
meanings.example: He spent all night trying to chat her up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 밤새 그녀에게 작업을 걸려고 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone or something different from or better than others.
meanings.example: Her talent set her apart from the other students.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 재능은 그녀를 다른 학생들과 구별되게(돋보이게) 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become fully engaged or interested in something.
meanings.example: I'm looking for a project I can really sink my teeth into.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 정말 몰두할 수 있는 프로젝트를 찾고 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize or reprimand someone for a mistake.
meanings.example: The manager took him to task for his poor performance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매니저는 그의 부진한 실적에 대해 그를 호되게 꾸짖었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To improve one's behavior or performance.
meanings.example: You need to shape up if you want to keep your job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 직장을 계속 다니고 싶으면 행동 똑바로 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To develop in a particular way.
meanings.example: The vacation is shaping up to be a lot of fun.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이번 휴가는 정말 재미있게 흘러가고 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be overlooked or forgotten.
meanings.example: A few small details slipped through the cracks during the move.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이사하는 동안 몇 가지 작은 세부 사항들이 누락되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shake hands to confirm an agreement.
meanings.example: It's a deal! Let's shake on it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 거래 성립입니다! 악수로 확정하죠.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get rid of or abolish something.
meanings.example: The company decided to do away with formal dress codes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 공식적인 복장 규정을 없애기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat something very quickly and hungrily.
meanings.example: He scarfed down his lunch in five minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 5분 만에 점심을 뚝딱 해치웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be extremely important or special to someone.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~에게 무엇보다 소중하다, 세상을 다 준 것과 같다
meanings.example: Her granddaughter means the world to her.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀에게 손녀는 세상 무엇보다 소중한 존재다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something in a reverse or unconventional way.
meanings.example: He always tries to go in the out door instead of following the rules.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 규칙을 따르는 대신 항상 거꾸로(엉뚱하게) 행동하려 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a mistake or spoil something (informal).
meanings.example: I really screwed up the presentation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나 발표 정말 완전히 망쳤어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To improve one's knowledge of something already learned but partly forgotten.
meanings.example: I need to brush up on my French before the trip.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여행 가기 전에 프랑스어 공부를 다시 좀 해야겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start dealing with bad or illegal behavior in a more severe way.
meanings.example: The police are cracking down on speeding.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰이 속도 위반을 엄중히 단속하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly realize something.
meanings.example: It finally dawned on me that I had left my keys at home.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 집에 열쇠를 두고 왔다는 사실이 마침내 생각났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide money or information, especially reluctantly (informal).
meanings.example: The insurance company finally coughed up the money.
meanings.exampleKorean: 보험사는 마침내 마지못해 돈을 내놓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fill something completely to the top.
meanings.example: He topped off the gas tank before the long drive.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 장거리 주행 전에 연료 탱크를 가득 채웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish something in a memorable way; added to that (often of something bad).
meanings.example: It rained all day, and to top it off, we missed the last bus.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하루 종일 비가 왔는데, 설상가상으로 마지막 버스까지 놓쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To not participate in an activity.
meanings.example: I think I'll sit this dance out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이번 춤은 좀 빠질게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To monitor or keep a careful watch on someone or something.
meanings.example: The manager keeps close tabs on all his employees.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매니저는 모든 직원을 면밀히 감시(체크)한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly appear in large numbers, especially when not wanted.
meanings.example: Whenever there's money involved, relatives start coming out of the woodwork.
meanings.exampleKorean: 돈이 얽히면 친척들이 갑자기 여기저기서 나타나기 시작한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enjoy oneself in a noisy and excited way.
meanings.example: They were whooping it up at the party last night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 어젯밤 파티에서 신나게 떠들며 놀았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hide or cover something completely so that it cannot be seen.
meanings.example: The clouds blotted out the sun.
meanings.exampleKorean: 구름이 태양을 완전히 가렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to forget an unpleasant memory.
meanings.example: He tried to blot out the memory of the accident.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 사고의 기억을 지우려 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin again from the beginning.
meanings.example: We made a mistake, so we'll have to start over.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리가 실수를 해서 처음부터 다시 시작해야 할 것 같아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act in an exaggerated or theatrical way to get attention.
meanings.example: The actor really hammed it up in the final scene.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 배우는 마지막 장면에서 정말 과장되게 연기했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To interrupt a conversation or activity.
meanings.example: Sorry to cut in, but there's a phone call for you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 끼어들어 죄송합니다만, 전화 왔어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drive suddenly in front of another vehicle.
meanings.example: A car cut in on me without signaling.
meanings.exampleKorean: 차 한 대가 신호도 없이 내 앞에 갑자기 끼어들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make small improvements or repairs to something.
meanings.example: I need to touch up the paint on the door.
meanings.exampleKorean: 문 도색을 좀 손질해야겠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To complete a form or document by providing information.
meanings.example: Please fill in your name and address here.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여기에 성함과 주소를 기입해 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do someone else's work for a short time.
meanings.example: I'm filling in for Joe while he's on vacation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조가 휴가 간 동안 내가 그를 대신하고 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give someone more information about something.
meanings.example: Can you fill me in on what happened at the meeting?
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의에서 무슨 일이 있었는지 자세히 좀 알려줄래?

meanings.englishDefinition: A phrase used to signal the start of a performance or activity.
meanings.example: We have a special guest tonight. Take it away, Mike!
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 특별한 손님을 모셨습니다. 마이크 씨, 시작해 주세요!

meanings.englishDefinition: To finally agree to something that you had been opposing.
meanings.example: The government refused to give in to the terrorists' demands.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 테러리스트들의 요구에 굴복하기를 거부했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reach the same level or quality as someone else.
meanings.example: Go on ahead, I'll catch up with you later.
meanings.exampleKorean: 먼저 가, 나중에 따라잡을게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk to someone to find out what has happened since you last met.
meanings.example: We spent the whole afternoon catching up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 오후 내내 밀린 이야기를 나누며 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give someone a very special and ceremonial welcome.
meanings.example: The city rolled out the red carpet for the visiting dignitaries.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 도시는 방문한 고위 인사들을 위해 대대적인 환영 행사를 열었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To falsely accuse someone of a crime by creating fake evidence.
meanings.example: He claimed that the police had framed him up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 경찰이 자신에게 누명을 씌웠다고 주장했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize someone repeatedly, especially about something they haven't done.
meanings.example: My mom is always getting on my case about my messy room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 엄마는 내 방이 지저분하다고 항상 나를 들볶으신다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fold something carefully and completely.
meanings.example: She folded up the clothes and put them away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 옷을 잘 접어서 치워 두었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail or close down (of a business).
meanings.example: The small company folded up after the big competitor moved in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 작은 회사는 거대 경쟁 업체가 들어온 뒤 문을 닫았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To investigate or examine the facts about a situation.
meanings.example: The police are looking into the cause of the accident.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰이 사고 원인을 조사하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To escape punishment for a crime or bad action.
meanings.example: He thought he could get away with cheating on the test.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 시험에서 부정행위를 하고도 무사히 넘어갈 수 있을 거라 생각했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find oneself in a difficult or dangerous situation, often due to one's own actions.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 곤경에 처하다, 사고를 치다, 문제를 일으키다
meanings.example: If you keep skipping class, you're going to get into trouble.
meanings.exampleKorean: 계속 수업을 빼먹으면 곤경에 처하게 될 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To kill someone (slang).
meanings.example: They threatened to do him in if he went to the police.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 그가 경찰에 가면 그를 해치우겠다고 협박했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone extremely tired.
meanings.example: That long hike really did me in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 긴 하이킹이 정말 나를 녹초로 만들었어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take care of someone or something.
meanings.example: Could you look after my cat while I'm away?
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 없는 동안 내 고양이 좀 돌봐줄래?

meanings.englishDefinition: To fasten one's seat belt in a vehicle.
meanings.example: Please buckle up; we're about to leave.
meanings.exampleKorean: 안전벨트를 매 주세요. 곧 출발합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To succeed in doing something difficult.
meanings.example: It was a difficult role, but she carried it off brilliantly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어려운 역할이었지만 그녀는 훌륭하게 해냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay a lot of money for something, especially reluctantly.
meanings.example: I had to shell out $200 for a new tire.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 타이어를 사느라 200달러나 들여야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lose consciousness temporarily.
meanings.example: He blacked out for a few seconds after the fall.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 넘어진 후 몇 초 동안 의식을 잃었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a power failure.
meanings.example: The whole city blacked out during the storm.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭풍우 동안 도시 전체가 정전되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discuss something in detail to reach an agreement or solution.
meanings.example: They spent hours trying to hash out the details of the contract.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 계약의 세부 사항을 논의하며 몇 시간을 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become less serious or worried.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 기운을 내다, 너무 심각하게 생각하지 않다
meanings.example: Lighten up! It was only a joke.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기운 내! 그냥 농담이었어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To solve a problem or deal with a confusing situation.
meanings.example: I need to straighten out this misunderstanding with my boss.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상사와의 이 오해를 바로잡아야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find or meet someone or something by chance.
meanings.example: I came across an old photo of my parents.
meanings.exampleKorean: 부모님의 옛날 사진을 우연히 발견했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give a particular impression to other people.
meanings.example: He comes across as a very confident person.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 매우 자신감 있는 사람처럼 보인다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish a task or deal with the last remaining parts of something.
meanings.example: The army stayed behind to mop up the remaining enemy forces.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군대는 잔존 적군을 소탕하기 위해 뒤에 남았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ask someone for something, especially money (slang).
meanings.example: He hit me up for twenty dollars.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 나에게 20달러를 빌려달라고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something quickly and without much care.
meanings.example: I can knock together a quick salad for lunch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 점심으로 샐러드 정도는 금방 뚝딱 만들 수 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay too much money for something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 바가지를 쓰다, 엄청나게 비싼 값을 치르다
meanings.example: We had to pay through the nose for a hotel room during the festival.
meanings.exampleKorean: 축제 기간이라 호텔 방값을 엄청나게 비싸게 치러야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To risk everything in an all-out effort to achieve success.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 전부 아니면 전무라는 식으로 덤비다, 모든 것을 걸다
meanings.example: With two minutes left in the game, the team decided to go for broke.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경기 종료 2분을 남기고 팀은 모든 것을 걸고 총공격하기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept a job or responsibility.
meanings.example: I can't take on any more work at the moment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지금으로선 일을 더 떠맡을 수가 없어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hire an employee.
meanings.example: The company is taking on new staff for the summer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 여름 시즌을 위해 새로운 직원을 고용하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fight or compete against someone.
meanings.example: The champion is ready to take on any challenger.
meanings.exampleKorean: 챔피언은 어떤 도전자와도 맞설 준비가 되어 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid dealing with a difficult subject directly.
meanings.example: Everyone was tiptoeing around the issue of his resignation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모두가 그의 사임 문제를 언급하기를 조심스럽게 피하고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become more cheerful or lively.
meanings.example: He perked up when I told him the good news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 좋은 소식을 전해주자 그는 기운을 차렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To vomit (old-fashioned slang).
meanings.example: He drank so much that he had to cast up his accounts.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 너무 많이 마셔서 토해야만 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very strict or firm about something.
meanings.example: I had to put my foot down and refuse to lend him any more money.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 단호하게 더 이상 돈을 빌려줄 수 없다고 거절해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn around and go back in the direction you came from.
meanings.example: We realized we were lost and had to double back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 길을 잃었다는 걸 깨닫고 왔던 길을 되돌아가야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To delay the progress of something.
meanings.example: The bad weather set our project back by two weeks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 악천후로 인해 우리 프로젝트가 2주 정도 지연되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cost someone a particular amount of money (informal).
meanings.example: This car set me back thirty thousand dollars.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 차를 사는 데 3만 달러가 들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To distract someone or confuse them.
meanings.example: The sudden noise threw me off my speech.
meanings.exampleKorean: 갑작스러운 소음이 내 연설을 방해했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get rid of something unwanted (e.g., a cold or habit).
meanings.example: I can't seem to throw off this cold.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 감기가 잘 떨어지질 않네.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very stingy or to try to get small amounts of money in a petty way.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 푼돈을 아끼다, 사소한 이득을 챙기려 애쓰다
meanings.example: He's always angling for farthings instead of looking at the big picture.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 큰 그림을 보는 대신 항상 사소한 이득만 챙기려 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste time or behave in a silly way (British slang).
meanings.example: Stop arsing about and do some work!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그만 빈둥거리고 일 좀 해!

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize someone unfairly.
meanings.example: He's always running down his coworkers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 동료들을 깎아내린다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lose power or stop working (of a battery or machine).
meanings.example: The battery in my watch has run down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 시계 배터리가 다 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find or track something down.
meanings.example: The detective managed to run down the suspect.
meanings.exampleKorean: 형사는 용의자를 추적해 찾아냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give someone secret information or a warning.
meanings.example: Someone tipped off the police about the robbery.
meanings.exampleKorean: 누군가 경찰에 강도 사건을 제보했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To damage something by hitting it.
meanings.example: He banged up his car in the parking lot.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 주차장에서 차를 망가뜨렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put someone in prison (British slang).
meanings.example: He was banged up for three years for theft.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 절도죄로 3년 동안 감옥에 갇혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pass a ball to a teammate (in sports like rugby or football).
meanings.example: The quarterback handed off the ball to the running back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 쿼터백이 러닝백에게 공을 넘겨주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To delegate or transfer a task to someone else.
meanings.example: I'll hand off this project to the marketing team.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 프로젝트를 마케팅 팀에 인계하겠습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To distribute or give something out, especially in large amounts.
meanings.example: He's always dishing out advice, even when it's not wanted.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 원하지 않을 때도 항상 조언을 남발한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend time doing nothing useful; to idle.
meanings.example: Stop lousing around and help me clean the house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빈둥거리지 말고 집 청소하는 것 좀 도와줘.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bind something securely with rope or string.
meanings.example: He tied up the bundle of sticks with a piece of twine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 노끈으로 나뭇가지 묶음을 꽁꽁 묶었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish or complete the final details of something.
meanings.example: We need to tie up a few loose ends before the project is finished.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프로젝트를 끝내기 전에 몇 가지 미비한 사항들을 마무리해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be busy or occupied.
meanings.example: I'm tied up in meetings all afternoon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 오후 내내 회의 때문에 꼼짝 못 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a position of power or responsibility.
meanings.example: The chairman decided to stand down after ten years in the role.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의장은 10년 동안의 임기를 마치고 물러나기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop being on alert or ready for action (of soldiers, etc.).
meanings.example: The troops were told to stand down after the peace treaty was signed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 평화 협정이 체결된 후 군대는 경계 태세를 해제하라는 명령을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To show sexual interest in someone (slang).
meanings.example: He tried to hit on her at the bar, but she wasn't interested.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 바에서 그녀에게 작업을 걸려 했지만, 그녀는 관심이 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly think of a good idea.
meanings.example: She finally hit on a solution to the problem.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 마침내 그 문제에 대한 해결책을 떠올렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become firmly attached to something.
meanings.example: The baby latched onto her mother's breast.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아기가 엄마의 젖을 꽉 물었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To develop a strong interest in something or someone.
meanings.example: He latched onto the idea of starting his own business.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 사업을 시작하겠다는 생각에 푹 빠졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish a supply of something.
meanings.example: We've used up all the milk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 우유를 다 써버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To signal the beginning of a new period or fashion.
meanings.example: The invention of the internet ushered in a new era of communication.
meanings.exampleKorean: 인터넷의 발명은 통신의 새로운 시대를 열었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To receive a very mild punishment for a crime or mistake.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 가벼운 처벌만 받다, 관대한 처분을 받다
meanings.example: Considering the damage he caused, he got off very lightly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 일으킨 손해를 생각하면, 그는 아주 가벼운 처벌만 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eventually arrive at a certain place or situation.
meanings.example: We got lost and ended up in a completely different city.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 길을 잃어서 결국 완전히 다른 도시에 도착했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay for something, especially as a treat for someone else.
meanings.example: The boss offered to spring for lunch for the whole team.
meanings.exampleKorean: 팀 전체 점심은 사장이 사기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid doing something that one should do because it is difficult or unpleasant (slang).
meanings.example: He promised to help, but he copped out at the last minute.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 돕기로 약속했지만 마지막 순간에 꽁무니를 뺐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually become smaller, quieter, or less successful.
meanings.example: His voice tailed off as he saw her angry expression.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 화난 표정을 보자 그의 목소리가 서서히 잦아들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fade away or become quieter.
meanings.example: The music tailed away into the distance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 음악 소리가 먼 곳으로 서서히 사라져 갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something or someone from a place.
meanings.example: The waiter took away the dirty dishes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 웨이터가 더러운 접시들을 치웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To buy food at a restaurant and eat it somewhere else.
meanings.example: Two coffees to take away, please.
meanings.exampleKorean: 커피 두 잔 포장해 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To expect something always to happen or exist in a particular way and not think about any possible problems.
meanings.example: We shouldn't take our health for granted.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 건강을 당연하게 여겨서는 안 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To develop in a particular way or be successful.
meanings.example: The plan didn't pan out as we had hoped.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 계획은 우리가 바랐던 대로 잘 풀리지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat someone with extreme tact and care.
meanings.example: He's very sensitive, so you have to handle him with kid gloves.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 매우 민감하니까 아주 조심스럽게 대해야 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To resemble an older relative in appearance or character.
meanings.example: She takes after her mother with those blue eyes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 저 푸른 눈이 엄마를 닮았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To admit that what one said was wrong.
meanings.example: I'm sorry I called you lazy. I take it back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 게으르다고 말해서 미안해. 그 말 취소할게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return something to a shop.
meanings.example: The shirt was too small, so I took it back to the store.
meanings.exampleKorean: 셔츠가 너무 작아서 가게에 가서 반품했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remind someone of a time in the past.
meanings.example: That song always takes me back to my high school days.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 노래는 항상 내 고등학교 시절을 생각나게 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take control of something (a business, a country, etc.).
meanings.example: The large corporation took over several smaller companies.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대기업이 여러 중소기업을 인수했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take responsibility for a task that someone else was doing.
meanings.example: He'll take over as manager when she retires.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 은퇴하면 그가 매니저 직을 인계받을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discuss something thoroughly, especially before making a decision.
meanings.example: We need to talk over the plans before we start.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시작하기 전에 그 계획에 대해 상세히 논의해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discuss a subject without addressing it directly.
meanings.example: Stop talking around the issue and tell me what you think.
meanings.exampleKorean: 말 돌리지 말고 네 생각이 뭔지 말해 봐.

meanings.englishDefinition: To persuade someone to agree with you.
meanings.example: It took hours, but we finally talked him around to our way of thinking.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몇 시간이 걸렸지만, 결국 그를 우리 생각 쪽으로 설득해 냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become or make someone less excited or angry.
meanings.example: Take a deep breath and try to calm down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 심호흡하고 진정하도록 해 봐.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act or speak prematurely or without sufficient preparation.
meanings.example: Don't go off half-cocked before you have all the facts.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 사실을 파악하기도 전에 성급하게 행동하지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To follow a wrong line of thought or action.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 번지수를 잘못 찾다, 짚어다리를 짚다, 헛다리 짚다
meanings.example: If you think I'm the one who stole it, you're barking up the wrong tree.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 그걸 훔친 사람이라고 생각한다면, 넌 헛다리 짚은 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail to happen or be completed (of a plan or arrangement).
meanings.example: The house sale fell through because the buyer couldn't get a loan.
meanings.exampleKorean: 구매자가 대출을 받지 못해 주택 매매가 무산되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ask about someone's health or well-being.
meanings.example: He inquired after my mother's health.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 우리 어머니의 안부를 물었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join with someone to work together on a task.
meanings.example: We decided to team up with a local firm to develop the software.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 그 소프트웨어를 개발하기 위해 지역 업체와 협력하기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move very quickly.
meanings.example: The cars were tearing along the motorway.
meanings.exampleKorean: 차들이 고속도로를 맹렬히 달리고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something by pulling it quickly.
meanings.example: I tore away the old wallpaper.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 낡은 벽지를 뜯어냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force oneself to leave a place or stop doing something.
meanings.example: I found the book so interesting I couldn't tear myself away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 책이 너무 재미있어서 손을 뗄 수가 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage to support oneself with very little money or food.
meanings.example: They managed to eke out a living on a small farm.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 작은 농장에서 간신히 생계를 꾸려나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a supply of something last longer by using it sparingly.
meanings.example: We had to eke out our remaining food until the rescuers arrived.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 구조대원이 도착할 때까지 남은 음식을 조금씩 아껴 써야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a hotel after paying the bill.
meanings.example: We have to check out of the hotel by noon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 정오까지 호텔에서 체크아웃해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To look at or examine something to see if you like it.
meanings.example: Hey, check out that new sports car!
meanings.exampleKorean: 야, 저 새 스포츠카 좀 봐!

meanings.englishDefinition: To prove to be true or correct.
meanings.example: His story doesn't seem to check out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 이야기는 사실인 것 같지 않다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone or something look cleaner or more attractive.
meanings.example: I need to spruce myself up before the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 파티 전에 몸단장을 좀 해야겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something from a place.
meanings.example: I need to take some money out of the ATM.
meanings.exampleKorean: ATM에서 돈을 좀 찾아야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go somewhere with someone you have invited.
meanings.example: He's taking her out to dinner tonight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 오늘 밤 그녀를 데리고 저녁 먹으러 갈 예정이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To kill or destroy someone or something.
meanings.example: The air strike took out the enemy's radar system.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공습으로 적의 레이더 시스템이 파괴되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be contrary to one's natural character or beliefs.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 본성에 반하다, 성미에 맞지 않다, 결을 거스르다
meanings.example: It goes against the grain for me to lie to my friends.
meanings.exampleKorean: 친구들에게 거짓말을 하는 건 내 성미에 맞지 않는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep someone's spirits high.
meanings.example: The good news buoyed up her spirits.
meanings.exampleKorean: 좋은 소식이 그녀의 기운을 북돋웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To happen or appear unexpectedly.
meanings.example: A few problems cropped up at the last minute.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마지막 순간에 몇 가지 문제들이 불쑥 발생했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a way that is intended to impress others; to show off.
meanings.example: They really put on the dog for their daughter's wedding.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 딸의 결혼식을 위해 정말 허세를 많이 부렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remain in a place after others have left.
meanings.example: She stayed behind after the meeting to talk to the boss.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 상사와 이야기하기 위해 회의가 끝난 뒤에 남았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To watch a place continuously and secretly, usually for legal reasons.
meanings.example: The police staked out the warehouse for three days.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 3일 동안 그 창고를 잠복 감시했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To state your opinion or position very clearly.
meanings.example: He staked out his position on the issue in the morning paper.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 조간신문을 통해 그 문제에 대한 자신의 입장을 분명히 밝혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail to hit a ball in baseball, or to be out after three strikes.
meanings.example: The batter struck out on three pitches.
meanings.exampleKorean: 타자는 3구 만에 삼진 아웃당했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing something new or independent.
meanings.example: He decided to strike out on his own and start a business.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 독립해서 사업을 시작하기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To end or finish something.
meanings.example: We should wind up the meeting and go to lunch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이제 회의를 마무리하고 점심 먹으러 갑시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find oneself in a particular situation or place, often unexpectedly.
meanings.example: He winded up in hospital after the accident.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 사고 후에 결국 병원 신세를 지게 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To annoy someone or make them angry.
meanings.example: Don't listen to him, he's just trying to wind you up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 말 듣지 마, 그냥 널 화나게 하려는 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make so much noise that another sound cannot be heard.
meanings.koreanDefinition: (소음이 다른 소리를) 들리지 않게 하다, 덮어버리다
meanings.example: The sound of the music drowned out her voice.
meanings.exampleKorean: 음악 소리 때문에 그녀의 목소리가 들리지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak for a long time about something in an opinionated way.
meanings.example: She was holding forth on the benefits of a vegan diet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 채식의 장점에 대해 열변을 토하고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A fielding position in cricket, far from the batsman on the off side.
meanings.example: The batsman hit the ball straight to long off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 타자가 롱 오프 쪽으로 공을 바로 쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bend over because of pain or laughter.
meanings.example: He was doubled up with laughter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 배를 잡고 몹시 웃었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To share a room or bed meant for one person.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 함께 쓰다, 두 명이 한 곳을 쓰다
meanings.example: The students had to double up in the small dorm rooms.
meanings.exampleKorean: 학생들은 좁은 기숙사 방에서 두 명씩 함께 지내야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To watch carefully for something to appear.
meanings.example: Keep an eye out for the postman.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우체부 아저씨 오시는지 잘 살펴봐.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue doing something until it is finished, even if it is difficult or boring.
meanings.example: I know the job is hard, but try to stick it out for a few months.
meanings.exampleKorean: 일이 힘들다는 건 알지만, 몇 달만 참고 버텨 봐.

meanings.englishDefinition: To demolish a building.
meanings.example: They are going to pull down the old cinema.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 낡은 영화관을 철거할 예정이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To earn a particular amount of money.
meanings.example: He's pulling down a six-figure salary now.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 지금 6자리 숫자의 연봉을 벌고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To throw a pitch intentionally wide of the strike zone to prevent a steal.
meanings.example: The catcher called for a pitch out to catch the runner stealing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 포수는 도루하는 주자를 잡기 위해 피치 아웃을 요청했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pitch carefully to a dangerous batter by throwing balls outside the strike zone.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 유인구로 승부하다, 어려운 공을 던져 피하다
meanings.example: They decided to pitch around the home run hitter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 홈런 타자를 피해 유인구로 승부하기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be introduced or accompanied by music.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 음악과 함께 시작하다, (선수 등을) 안으로 들여보내다
meanings.example: The team was played in by the school band.
meanings.exampleKorean: 팀은 학교 밴드의 연주와 함께 경기장에 입장했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To exploit someone's feelings or weaknesses.
meanings.example: The advertisement plays on people's fears of getting old.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 광고는 사람들의 노화에 대한 공포를 자극한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop doing business for the day or permanently.
meanings.example: Many small shops have had to put up the shutters due to the recession.
meanings.exampleKorean: 불황 때문에 많은 작은 상점들이 영업을 중단해야만 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To regret something that has already happened and cannot be changed.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 이미 엎질러진 물이다, 이미 끝난 일을 후회하다
meanings.example: It's no use crying over spilt milk; we just have to move on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이미 엎질러진 물이니 후회해봐야 소용없어. 그냥 앞으로 나아가야지.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place suddenly.
meanings.example: He ran off without saying goodbye.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 작별 인사도 없이 급히 떠나 버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce copies of something on a machine.
meanings.example: Can you run off 50 copies of this memo?
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 메모 50부만 복사해 줄래?

meanings.englishDefinition: To be out by hitting a fly ball that is caught.
meanings.example: The batter flew out to center field.
meanings.exampleKorean: 타자는 중견수 플라이 아웃으로 물러났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish or use up all of something.
meanings.example: We've run out of milk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우유가 다 떨어졌어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come to an end (of a contract or period of time).
meanings.example: My passport runs out next month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 여권이 다음 달에 만료된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To allow a debt or bill to increase quickly.
meanings.example: He ran up a huge credit card bill.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 신용카드 빚을 엄청나게 졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something quickly, especially clothes.
meanings.example: She ran up a dress for the party in one evening.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 하룻저녁 만에 파티용 드레스를 뚝딱 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To record the time you leave work by putting a card into a special machine.
meanings.example: I usually punch out at 5:30.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 보통 5시 30분에 퇴근한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hit someone until they are unconscious.
meanings.example: He punched out the guy who was bothering him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신을 괴롭히던 남자를 때려눕혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give a brief summary of the main points.
meanings.example: To sum up, the project was a great success.
meanings.exampleKorean: 요약하자면, 그 프로젝트는 대성공이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare or find something quickly, especially food.
meanings.example: I'll see if I can rustle up some sandwiches.
meanings.exampleKorean: 샌드위치를 좀 뚝딱 만들 수 있는지 볼게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To collapse inwards (of a roof or wall).
meanings.example: The roof of the mine caved in, trapping several workers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 광산의 지붕이 무너져 내려 광부 몇 명이 갇혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop resisting and agree to something.
meanings.example: The company finally caved in to the workers' demands.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 마침내 노동자들의 요구에 굴복했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To respond or get back to someone (slang).
meanings.example: I'll holla back at you tomorrow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내일 다시 연락할게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have sexual intercourse.
meanings.example: They have been sleeping together for several months.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 몇 달째 동침하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To withdraw from an agreement or situation.
meanings.example: The company decided to pull out of the deal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 거래에서 빠지기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move a vehicle away from the side of the road or out of a parking space.
meanings.example: A car pulled out in front of me without signaling.
meanings.exampleKorean: 차 한 대가 신호도 없이 내 앞에서 쑥 끼어들었다 (차를 뺐다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To get a head start or a competitive advantage.
meanings.example: The scholarship gave him a leg up in his academic career.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 장학금은 그의 학문적 경력에 큰 도움이 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To achieve or record a success or score.
meanings.example: The team chalked up their fifth win in a row.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 팀은 5연승을 기록했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a decision.
meanings.example: I haven't made up my mind yet about which car to buy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어떤 차를 살지 아직 결정하지 못했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat something as if it is not important or not a problem.
meanings.example: He shrugged off the criticism and continued with his work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 비판을 대수롭지 않게 넘기고 일을 계속했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce the amount or quantity of something.
meanings.example: I'm trying to cut down on the amount of sugar I eat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 설탕 섭취량을 줄이려고 노력 중이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cut through the trunk of a tree so that it falls.
meanings.example: They had to cut down the old oak tree.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 낡은 오크 나무를 베어내야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find out the true cause or reason for something.
meanings.example: The police are determined to get to the bottom of the mystery.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 그 미스터리의 진상을 규명하기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To distribute things to a group of people.
meanings.example: The teacher handed out the exam papers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님은 시험지를 나누어 주셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To end a relationship suddenly (slang).
meanings.example: She decided to break one off with him after the argument.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 말다툼 끝에 그와 헤어지기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To save money or time for a particular purpose.
meanings.example: I try to set aside some money every month for emergencies.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 매달 비상금을 조금씩 따로 챙겨두려 노력한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ignore or forget something for a period of time.
meanings.example: Let's set aside our differences and work together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리의 차이점은 제쳐놓고 함께 협력합시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone leave a job or position against their will.
meanings.example: He was forced out of his job after the scandal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 스캔들 이후 직장에서 억지로 물러나야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave someone in a difficult or vulnerable situation without support.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 곤경에 처하게 내버려 두다, 희생양으로 삼다
meanings.example: His partners hung him out to dry when the investigation started.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조사가 시작되자 그의 동업자들은 그를 곤경에 처하게 내버려 두었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lose control of one's emotions or behavior; to go crazy (slang).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 정신이 나가다, 미친 듯이 행동하다, 버럭 화를 내다
meanings.example: Don't spazz out! It's not that big of a deal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 흥분하지 마! 그렇게 큰 일 아니야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To practice coitus interruptus (slang/euphemism).
meanings.example: They decided to get off at Haymarket to avoid pregnancy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 임신을 피하기 위해 질외사정을 하기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To succeed in communicating an idea or piece of information.
meanings.example: I'm trying to get my point across to the manager.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 내 요점을 매니저에게 이해시키려 노력 중이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move a vehicle backwards into a space.
meanings.example: He backed the car into the garage.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 차를 후진시켜 차고에 넣었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accidentally bump into something while moving backwards.
meanings.example: I backed into a lamppost because I wasn't looking.
meanings.exampleKorean: 뒤를 보지 않아서 가로등에 부딪혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stand in a line or queue.
meanings.example: People were lining up for tickets outside the stadium.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사람들이 경기장 밖에서 티켓을 사려고 줄을 서 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrange people or things in a row.
meanings.example: Line up the bottles on the shelf.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선반 위에 병들을 일렬로 세우세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To organize or prepare something for the future.
meanings.example: I've lined up a few interviews for next week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다음 주에 면접 몇 건을 잡아놓았습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing what others are doing or follow a common goal (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 흐름을 따르다, 협조하다, 상황 파악을 하다
meanings.example: If you want to keep your job, you need to get with the program.
meanings.exampleKorean: 직장을 계속 다니고 싶으면 분위기 파악하고 흐름을 따라야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consider something as a possible option or cause.
meanings.example: The investigators haven't ruled in any suspects yet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조사관들은 아직 어떤 용의자도 가능성 안에 포함하지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To exclude or eliminate a possibility.
meanings.example: The police have ruled out foul play in his death.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 그의 죽음에서 타살 가능성을 배제했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To learn something so that you can remember it perfectly.
meanings.example: She committed the entire poem to memory.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 시 전체를 암기했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ignore someone or treat them as unimportant.
meanings.example: He tried to talk to her, but she just brushed him off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그녀에게 말을 걸려 했지만, 그녀는 그를 그냥 무시했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spread or affect a larger area or a different situation.
meanings.example: The conflict in the city could spill over into neighboring regions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 도시의 갈등이 인근 지역으로 번질 수도 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To request something.
meanings.example: He asked for a glass of water.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 물 한 잔을 부탁했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reach the maximum limit of something, like a credit card.
meanings.example: I've maxed out my credit cards this month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이번 달에 내 신용카드 한도를 다 썼어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly stop talking, usually because of fear or shyness (slang).
meanings.example: As soon as the police arrived, he clammed up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰이 도착하자마자 그는 입을 꾹 다물었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very close to a particular quality or state.
meanings.example: His confidence borders on arrogance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 자신감은 거의 오만에 가깝다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reach a crisis or a point where a decision must be made.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 절정에 달하다, 위기 상황에 이르다, (일이) 터지다
meanings.example: The tension between the two nations finally came to a head.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 국가 간의 긴장이 마침내 최고조에 달했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To present or explain an idea or plan clearly.
meanings.example: He set forth his arguments in a long essay.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 긴 에세이를 통해 자신의 주장을 설명했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin a journey.
meanings.example: They set forth on their expedition at dawn.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 새벽에 탐험을 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suggest an idea or plan for consideration.
meanings.example: Several proposals have been put forward to solve the traffic problem.
meanings.exampleKorean: 교통 문제를 해결하기 위해 여러 제안이 나왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To visit someone informally and without an appointment.
meanings.example: Please drop by our house anytime you're in the neighborhood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 근처에 오실 때 언제든 저희 집에 들러주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To retire from a job or activity, especially a sport.
meanings.example: After thirty years in the factory, he decided it was time to hang up his boots.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공장에서 30년을 일한 뒤, 그는 이제 은퇴할 때가 되었다고 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lift something up from a surface.
meanings.example: He picked up the keys from the floor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 바닥에서 열쇠를 집어 들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To collect someone in a vehicle.
meanings.example: I'll pick you up at the station at 6 o'clock.
meanings.exampleKorean: 6시에 역으로 태우러 갈게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To learn something new without much effort.
meanings.example: She picked up a lot of French while she was living in Paris.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 파리에 사는 동안 프랑스어를 많이 익혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To improve or increase (of business or health).
meanings.example: Sales are finally starting to pick up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매출이 마침내 나아지기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give something to someone else after you have used it or received it.
meanings.example: Please read this memo and pass it on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 메모를 읽고 다음 사람에게 전달해 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To die (a euphemism).
meanings.example: He passed on peacefully in his sleep.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 잠결에 평온하게 세상을 떠났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay for something, especially a meal in a restaurant.
meanings.example: My company is picking up the tab for this business lunch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 회사가 이번 비즈니스 점심값을 계산할 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something less forceful or offensive.
meanings.example: The politician was advised to tone down his rhetoric.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인은 자신의 수사를 완화하라는 권고를 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find or get something.
meanings.example: I've been trying to lay hands on a copy of that rare book.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그 희귀 서적 한 권을 구하려고 애쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To touch or hit someone, often with the intention of harming them.
meanings.example: If you lay hands on my brother again, you'll regret it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 동생에게 한 번만 더 손을 대면 후회하게 될 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To watch or listen to a particular television or radio program.
meanings.example: Millions of people tuned in to watch the World Cup final.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수백만 명의 사람들이 월드컵 결승전을 보기 위해 채널을 맞췄다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop listening to or paying attention to something.
meanings.example: He just tunes out whenever his wife starts complaining.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아내가 불평을 시작할 때마다 그냥 귀를 닫아버린다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fight or compete against someone until one side wins (informal).
meanings.example: The two candidates are dukiing it out in the final debate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 후보가 마지막 토론회에서 끝장 승부를 벌이고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suffer the unpleasant consequences of one's own actions without help from others.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 자업자득으로 고생하다, 스스로 초래한 곤경에 빠지다
meanings.example: He refused my help, so I'm letting him stew in his own juices.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 내 도움을 거절했으니, 그가 자업자득으로 고생하게 내버려 둘 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish something that you have started.
meanings.example: He's great at coming up with ideas, but he never follows through.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아이디어를 내는 데는 능숙하지만, 절대 끝까지 마무리를 짓지 못한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To look at someone or something carefully, often with interest or desire.
meanings.example: She was eyeing up the cakes in the bakery window.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 빵집 창문에 있는 케이크들을 유심히 살피고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To end a romantic relationship.
meanings.example: They broke up after dating for three years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 3년 동안 사귀고 헤어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To break into many pieces.
meanings.example: The ship broke up on the rocks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 배는 바위에 부딪혀 산산조각 났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start laughing uncontrollably.
meanings.example: He really breaks me up with his jokes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 농담으로 나를 정말 빵 터지게 만든다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop something at an early stage before it can develop into a bigger problem.
meanings.example: It's important to nip bad habits in the bud.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나쁜 습관은 싹을 자르는 것이 중요하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To repeat something someone has said without explaining the situation in which they said it, so that its meaning is changed.
meanings.example: My remarks were taken out of context by the media.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 발언은 언론에 의해 문맥이 무시된 채 보도되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be required to do something; must.
meanings.example: I have to leave now.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나 지금 가야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mark items on a list as finished.
meanings.example: Tick off each item as you pack it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 짐을 쌀 때마다 각 항목에 체크 표시를 하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone angry or annoyed (informal).
meanings.example: It really ticks me off when people are late.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사람들이 늦으면 정말 화가 난다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop the supply of something (water, electricity, etc.).
meanings.example: The electricity was cut off because we didn't pay the bill.
meanings.exampleKorean: 전기료를 내지 않아서 전기가 끊겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To interrupt someone who is speaking.
meanings.example: I'm sorry to cut you off, but we're out of time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 말을 가로막아 미안하지만, 시간이 다 됐어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent someone from leaving a place or receiving help.
meanings.example: The village was cut off by the heavy snow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭설로 마을이 고립되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be able to see, hear, or understand something with difficulty.
meanings.example: I can't make out what he's saying.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 무슨 말을 하는지 알아들을 수 없어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To kiss and touch someone in a sexual way (informal).
meanings.example: We saw them making out in the back of the cinema.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 그들이 영화관 뒷좌석에서 애무하는 것을 보았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pretend that something is true.
meanings.example: He makes himself out to be a successful businessman.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자기가 성공한 사업가인 체한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish something quickly and easily, especially food.
meanings.example: He polished off the whole pizza in ten minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 10분 만에 피자 한 판을 다 먹어치웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue doing something with energy and speed (British informal).
meanings.example: We need to crack on if we want to finish before dark.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어두워지기 전에 끝내려면 서둘러 계속해야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To change into more comfortable clothing, often with a romantic or sexual suggestion.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 더 편한 옷으로 갈아입다 (종종 유혹적인 뉘앙스)
meanings.example: I'll just go and slip into something more comfortable.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가서 좀 더 편한 옷으로 갈아입고 올게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dress oneself or someone else in a very attractive or formal way.
meanings.example: She got all dolled up for the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 파티를 위해 아주 화려하게 꾸몄다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To allow someone or something to enter.
meanings.example: Can you open the door and let the cat in?
meanings.exampleKorean: 문 열어서 고양이 좀 들여보내 줄래?

meanings.englishDefinition: To allow a liquid to enter.
meanings.example: My boots are letting in water.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 장화에 물이 새어 들어온다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move towards a place.
meanings.example: We made for the nearest shelter when it started to rain.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비가 내리기 시작하자 우리는 가장 가까운 대피소로 향했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To help make something possible.
meanings.example: The new laws make for a safer environment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 법률은 더 안전한 환경을 만드는 데 도움이 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force someone or something to leave a place.
meanings.example: The settlers drove the indigenous people out of their lands.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정착민들은 원주민들을 그들의 땅에서 몰아냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To end a telephone conversation.
meanings.example: Don't hang up! I haven't finished talking.
meanings.exampleKorean: 전화 끊지 마! 아직 말 안 끝났어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To place something on a hook or hanger.
meanings.example: Please hang up your coat in the hallway.
meanings.exampleKorean: 복도에 코트를 걸어 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have something near you or in your possession.
meanings.example: I like to keep a dictionary around when I'm reading.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 책을 읽을 때 사전을 곁에 두는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter information into a computer using a keyboard.
meanings.example: I'll key in the data as soon as I get it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 데이터를 받는 대로 입력하겠습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a silly way or play jokes.
meanings.example: Stop kidding around and get back to work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그만 장난치고 일로 돌아가.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go down on one's knees.
meanings.example: He knelt down to tie his shoelaces.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 신발 끈을 묶으려고 무릎을 꿇었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To show respect or submission by kneeling in front of someone or something.
meanings.example: The knights knelt before the king.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기사들은 왕 앞에 무릎을 꿇었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To support or defend someone or something strongly.
meanings.example: She took up the cudgel for environmental rights.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 환경권을 옹호하기 위해 강력히 나섰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To direct one's eyes towards someone or something.
meanings.example: Look at that beautiful sunset!
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 아름다운 일몰 좀 봐!

meanings.englishDefinition: To examine or consider something.
meanings.example: The doctor needs to look at your throat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의사 선생님이 당신의 목 상태를 살펴보셔야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give something to someone for free.
meanings.example: They are giving away free samples of the new shampoo.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 새 샴푸의 무료 샘플을 나누어 주고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reveal a secret, often unintentionally.
meanings.example: He didn't want to give away the surprise ending of the movie.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 영화의 깜짝 결말을 누설하고 싶지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be destined for or belong to someone.
meanings.example: That last piece of cake has my name on it!
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 마지막 케이크 조각은 내 거야!

meanings.englishDefinition: To approach someone secretly in order to persuade them to join your team or company (British slang).
meanings.example: The football club was accused of tapping up a rival player.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 축구 클럽은 라이벌 팀 선수를 사전 접촉했다는 혐의를 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid talking about something unpleasant or to say as little as possible about it.
meanings.example: The report glossed over the company's financial problems.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 보고서는 회사의 재정 문제를 대충 얼버무리고 넘어갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become dull and lifeless (of eyes), usually because of boredom.
meanings.example: His eyes glazed over as she started explaining the technical details.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 기술적인 세부 사항을 설명하기 시작하자 그의 눈이 멍해졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat something very quickly and greedily.
meanings.example: The children gobbled up the cookies in no time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 순식간에 쿠키를 다 먹어치웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use up a large amount of something very quickly.
meanings.example: The new project is gobbling up all our budget.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 프로젝트가 우리 예산을 몽땅 잡아먹고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A phrase used to encourage someone to be brave or cheerful in a difficult situation.
meanings.example: Chin up! Things will get better soon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기운 내! 곧 상황이 나아질 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to increase support or business for something.
meanings.example: They are trying to drum up support for the new environmental policy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 새로운 환경 정책에 대한 지지를 끌어내려 애쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To examine or check something carefully.
meanings.example: I need to go over these figures again.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 숫자들을 다시 한번 검토해봐야겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move from one place to another, often by crossing something.
meanings.example: Let's go over to the park.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공원으로 건너가자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have sex with someone (slang).
meanings.example: He's just trying to get into her pants.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그저 그녀와 잠자리를 같이 하려고 수작을 부리는 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return a phone call.
meanings.example: I'll call you back in ten minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 10분 뒤에 다시 전화할게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remember or bring to mind.
meanings.example: That song calls back memories of my childhood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 노래는 내 어린 시절의 기억을 회상하게 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gain muscle or body mass.
meanings.example: He's been going to the gym to bulk up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 몸집을 키우기 위해 체육관에 다니고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fill something with air or liquid with a pump.
meanings.example: I need to pump up my bicycle tires.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자전거 타이어에 바람을 넣어야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone feel more excited or confident.
meanings.example: The coach gave a speech to pump up the players before the game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코치는 경기 전 선수들의 사기를 북돋우기 위해 연설을 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent someone or something from entering a place.
meanings.example: The sign said 'Private Property: Keep Out'.
meanings.exampleKorean: 표지판에 '사유지: 출입 금지'라고 적혀 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a great effort to do something.
meanings.example: She really knocked herself out to make the party a success.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 파티를 성공시키기 위해 정말 혼신의 힘을 쏟았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A phrase used to give someone permission to do something as much as they want.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 마음껏 하세요, 하고 싶은 대로 하세요
meanings.example: Can I use your computer? - Sure, knock yourself out!
meanings.exampleKorean: 컴퓨터 좀 써도 될까? - 그럼, 마음껏 써!

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something more certain or definite.
meanings.example: We need to firm up the details of the contract.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 계약의 세부 사항을 확정해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To understand the context or main points of something.
meanings.example: The marketing strategy is keyed into the needs of young consumers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 마케팅 전략은 젊은 소비자들의 요구에 맞춰져(연동되어) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accelerate very quickly in a vehicle, causing the tires to spin and make noise.
meanings.example: He peeled out of the driveway in his new car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 새 차를 타고 진입로에서 급출발해 나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To complete a form by providing information.
meanings.example: Please fill out this application form.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 신청서를 작성해 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become larger or heavier (of a person).
meanings.example: He's really filled out since I last saw him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지난번에 봤을 때보다 몸집이 정말 좋아졌네(살이 좀 붙었네).

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something unwanted using a filter.
meanings.example: The software is designed to filter out spam emails.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 소프트웨어는 스팸 메일을 걸러내도록 설계되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be the right size and shape for a space.
meanings.example: The sofa won't fit into the living room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 소파는 거실에 들어가지 않을 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To feel like you belong in a particular group or situation.
meanings.example: It took him a while to fit into his new school.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 새 학교에 적응하는 데 시간이 좀 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a surface smooth by rubbing it with sandpaper.
meanings.example: You need to sand down the wood before you paint it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 페인트를 칠하기 전에 나무를 사포로 문질러야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place in a single line.
meanings.example: The students filed out of the classroom after the bell rang.
meanings.exampleKorean: 종이 울리자 학생들은 교실 밖으로 줄지어 나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter a place in a single line.
meanings.example: The audience filed in and took their seats.
meanings.exampleKorean: 관객들은 줄지어 들어와 자리에 앉았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bury a dead person.
meanings.example: He was laid to rest in the village cemetery.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 마을 묘지에 안치되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finally stop thinking about or worrying about something.
meanings.example: The report finally laid to rest any doubts about the project's safety.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 보고서는 마침내 프로젝트 안전에 대한 모든 의구심을 가라앉혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave one's house for social reasons.
meanings.example: Are you going out tonight?
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 밤에 나갈 거야?

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop burning or shining.
meanings.example: The fire went out because there was no more wood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나무가 없어서 불이 꺼졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a romantic relationship with someone.
meanings.example: How long have they been going out?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들 사귄 지 얼마나 됐어?

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay a short, informal visit.
meanings.example: Drop in and see us whenever you're in town.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시내에 올 때 언제든 잠깐 들러주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start working harder or faster (British informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 서둘러 일을 시작하다, 열심히 하기 시작하다
meanings.example: If we don't pull our fingers out, we'll never finish on time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리가 서둘러서 열심히 하지 않으면 절대 제시간에 끝내지 못할 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make an effort to improve one's work or behavior.
meanings.example: You'll have to pull your socks up if you want to pass the exam.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시험에 합격하고 싶으면 마음 단단히 먹고 노력해야 할 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To die (old-fashioned).
meanings.example: After a long illness, he finally yielded up the ghost.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오랜 병환 끝에 그는 마침내 숨을 거두었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do the same thing as someone else did before you.
meanings.example: He followed in his father's footsteps and became a doctor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아버지의 뒤를 이어 의사가 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish or complete something; to settle an account.
meanings.example: The store is having a sale to close out its summer stock.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 가게는 여름 재고를 처분하기 위해 세일을 하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat a meal in a restaurant rather than at home.
meanings.example: We decided to eat out to celebrate her promotion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 그녀의 승진을 축하하기 위해 외식하기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gain weight or muscle quickly.
meanings.example: He's packed on ten pounds of muscle this year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 올해 근육이 10파운드나 붙었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop wasting time and start working hard (British informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 서둘러 일을 시작하다, 열심히 하기 시작하다
meanings.example: You need to get your finger out if you want to finish by Friday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 금요일까지 끝내고 싶으면 서둘러서 열심히 해야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accuse someone of doing something wrong.
meanings.example: Don't point the finger at me; I wasn't even there!
meanings.exampleKorean: 나한테 책임을 돌리지 마. 난 거기 있지도 않았어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To get rid of something that you no longer want or need.
meanings.example: I threw out my old magazines yesterday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어제 낡은 잡지들을 다 버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To refuse to accept a suggestion or idea.
meanings.example: The committee threw out our proposal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 위원회는 우리의 제안을 거절했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To examine or read something very carefully and in a lot of detail.
meanings.example: She spent the evening poring over the maps.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 저녁 내내 지도를 꼼꼼히 들여다보며 시간을 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To listen to a private conversation without being noticed.
meanings.example: I think someone is listening in on our phone calls.
meanings.exampleKorean: 누군가 우리 전화를 도청하고 있는 것 같아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue doing something although it is difficult.
meanings.example: Despite the setbacks, the team decided to soldier on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여러 차례의 좌절에도 불구하고 팀은 꿋꿋이 계속하기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very frightened or nervous.
meanings.example: I was quaking in my boots before the job interview.
meanings.exampleKorean: 면접을 보기 전에 나는 사시나무 떨듯 떨었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have discovered something important or to know about someone's secret actions.
meanings.example: The police are on to him, so he'd better be careful.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰이 그를 간파했으니 조심하는 게 좋을 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To visit a place for a short time (informal).
meanings.example: Why don't you pop in for a coffee on your way home?
meanings.exampleKorean: 집에 가는 길에 커피 한잔하러 잠깐 들르지 그래?

meanings.englishDefinition: To go out for a short time (informal).
meanings.example: I'm just popping out to the shops for a minute.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가게에 잠깐만 갔다 올게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To connect electronic equipment together.
meanings.example: I need to hook up the new printer to my computer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 프린터를 컴퓨터에 연결해야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start a romantic or sexual relationship with someone (slang).
meanings.example: They hooked up at a party last weekend.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 지난 주말 파티에서 만나 관계를 맺었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent people from knowing about a mistake or a crime.
meanings.example: The company tried to hush up the scandal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 스캔들을 은폐하려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To invite someone to go on a date.
meanings.example: He finally gathered the courage to ask her out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 마침내 용기를 내어 그녀에게 데이트를 신청했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To extinguish a flame by blowing.
meanings.example: She blew out the candles on her birthday cake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 생일 케이크의 촛불을 불어서 껐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To burst (of a tire).
meanings.example: The back tire blew out while we were on the motorway.
meanings.exampleKorean: 고속도로를 달리는 동안 뒷바퀴 타이어가 터졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move a meeting or event to an earlier time.
meanings.example: The meeting has been brought forward to 10 o'clock.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의 시간이 10시로 앞당겨졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suggest an idea or plan for discussion.
meanings.example: The committee brought forward several new proposals.
meanings.exampleKorean: 위원회는 몇 가지 새로운 제안들을 제시했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish a letter or a broadcast.
meanings.example: The radio host signed off with his usual catchphrase.
meanings.exampleKorean: 라디오 진행자는 늘 하던 유행어로 방송을 끝맺었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give official approval to something.
meanings.example: The manager has to sign off on all new projects.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매니저가 모든 새 프로젝트를 공식 승인해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To invent a false accusation or excuse.
meanings.example: He was arrested on trumped-up charges.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 날조된 혐의로 체포되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To disappoint someone by failing to do what you agreed to do.
meanings.example: I'm sorry I let you down; I'll try harder next time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 실망시켜 드려 죄송합니다. 다음엔 더 노력할게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lower something.
meanings.example: Can you help me let down the blinds?
meanings.exampleKorean: 블라인드 내리는 것 좀 도와줄래?

meanings.englishDefinition: To consider something carefully before making a decision.
meanings.example: I'll need a few days to think it over.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 문제를 심사숙고할 며칠간의 시간이 필요합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deteriorate or be ruined through neglect (informal).
meanings.example: The garden has really gone to pot since they moved away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들이 이사 간 뒤로 정원이 정말 엉망이 되어버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become much worse in quality or character.
meanings.example: Some people think the country is going to the dogs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어떤 이들은 나라가 엉망이 되어가고 있다고 생각한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move away from someone or something, usually in fear or dislike.
meanings.example: She drew back in horror when she saw the spider.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 거미를 보자 공포에 질려 뒷걸음질 쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take revenge on someone.
meanings.example: I'll find a way to get back at him for what he did.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 한 짓에 대해 어떻게든 복수하고 말 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be resolved or work out successfully in the end.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 결국 다 잘 해결되다, 나중에 다 밝혀지다
meanings.example: Don't worry about the small mistakes; it will all come out in the wash.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작은 실수는 걱정하지 마. 결국 다 잘 해결될 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: A phrase said to avoid bad luck after saying something positive.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 부정을 타지 않길!, 행운이 계속되길! (미신적 감탄사)
meanings.example: Everything is going great so far, knock on wood!
meanings.exampleKorean: 지금까지는 모든 게 아주 잘 풀리고 있어, 계속 운이 좋기를!

meanings.englishDefinition: To discuss something thoroughly to reach an agreement or solution.
meanings.example: The two sides met to thrash out a deal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 양측은 합의를 도출하기 위해 치열한 논의를 벌였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remain cheerful in a difficult situation (British informal).
meanings.example: Keep your pecker up! Things aren't that bad.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기운 내! 상황이 그렇게 나쁘진 않아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat someone very badly or unfairly; to cheat someone (vulgar slang).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 뒤통수를 치다, 엿 먹이다, 아주 곤란하게 만들다
meanings.example: He really fucked me over when he left with all the money.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 돈을 다 가지고 떠났을 때 그는 나를 정말 엿 먹였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat someone unfairly or cheat them (informal).
meanings.example: The company really screwed over its employees.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 직원들을 정말 곤란하게 만들었다 (속였다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To ignore or fail to notice someone or something.
meanings.example: He was passed over for promotion in favor of a younger colleague.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 젊은 동료에게 밀려 승진에서 제외되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste time or behave in a silly way.
meanings.example: Stop fooling around and finish your homework.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그만 장난치고 숙제 끝내.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a casual sexual relationship with someone.
meanings.example: He was caught fooling around with his secretary.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 비서와 바람을 피우다 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To aim for a very difficult or ambitious goal.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 원대한 꿈을 갖다, 아주 높은 목표를 세우다
meanings.example: Don't be afraid to reach for the stars in your career.
meanings.exampleKorean: 네 커리어에 있어 원대한 꿈을 갖는 것을 두려워하지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To set a price so high that people cannot afford to buy something or competitors cannot compete.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 너무 비싸서 엄두를 못 내게 하다, (비싼 가격으로) 시장에서 몰아내다
meanings.example: High rents are pricing local businesses out of the market.
meanings.exampleKorean: 높은 임대료 때문에 지역 상점들이 시장에서 버티지 못하고 쫓겨나고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a natural ability to recognize or appreciate something good.
meanings.example: She has a good eye for detail.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 세부 사항을 보는 안목이 뛰어나다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent people from discovering the truth about a mistake or a crime.
meanings.example: They tried to cover up the evidence of the leak.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 유출 증거를 은폐하려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To examine something or someone in order to make a judgment.
meanings.example: The boxers sized each other up before the fight began.
meanings.exampleKorean: 복서들은 경기가 시작되기 전에 서로를 가늠했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue doing something without changing it.
meanings.example: You should stick to your original plan.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너는 원래 계획을 고수해야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To solve a problem or deal with a difficult situation.
meanings.example: We need to sort out these issues before the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의 전에 이 문제들을 해결해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To organize things into different groups.
meanings.example: I spent the afternoon sorting out my old books.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 오후에 낡은 책들을 정리하며 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To collapse inwards (of a roof or wall).
meanings.example: The ceiling of the old house fell in during the storm.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭풍우 동안 그 낡은 집의 천장이 무너져 내렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To form a line (of soldiers).
meanings.example: The sergeant ordered the troops to fall in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하사는 부대원들에게 대열을 정렬하라고 명령했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually become louder or clearer (of a sound or image).
meanings.example: The music began to fade in slowly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 음악 소리가 서서히 커지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually become quieter or less clear (of a sound or image).
meanings.example: The scene fades out as the screen goes black.
meanings.exampleKorean: 화면이 검게 변하면서 장면이 서서히 사라진다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move sideways to make room for someone else.
meanings.example: Could you scoot over a bit so I can sit down?
meanings.exampleKorean: 나도 좀 앉게 옆으로 조금만 비켜 줄래?

meanings.englishDefinition: To the maximum possible level.
meanings.example: The tension in the room was turned up to eleven.
meanings.exampleKorean: 방 안의 긴장감이 극도로 높아졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To write something in full detail.
meanings.example: He wrote out a list of everything he needed to do.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 해야 할 모든 일의 목록을 상세히 적었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To masturbate (vulgar slang, especially of a male).
meanings.example: He was caught jacking off in the bathroom.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 화장실에서 자위하다 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To search for information in a book or on the internet.
meanings.example: I'll look up the word in the dictionary.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사전에서 그 단어를 찾아볼게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To improve or become better.
meanings.example: Things are finally starting to look up for the company.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사의 상황이 마침내 나아지기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To admire or respect someone.
meanings.example: He has always looked up to his older brother.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 형을 존경해왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To realize the truth behind someone's lies or deception.
meanings.example: I could see through his lies immediately.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그의 거짓말을 즉시 간파할 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue doing something until it is finished.
meanings.example: I'm determined to see this project through to the end.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 이 프로젝트를 끝까지 완수하기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be satisfied with your past successes and stop trying to achieve more.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 현재의 영광에 안주하다, 승리에 도취되어 노력을 멈추다
meanings.example: We cannot afford to rest on our laurels if we want to stay competitive.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경쟁력을 유지하고 싶다면 현재의 성공에 안주해서는 안 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop taking part in an activity or to leave a position.
meanings.example: She decided to bow out of the race for the presidency.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 대통령 선거전에서 물러나기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a disagreement and stop being friends.
meanings.example: They fell out over a silly argument.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 유치한 말다툼 끝에 사이가 틀어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall from a place.
meanings.example: Her baby teeth are starting to fall out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 젖니가 빠지기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To signal a vehicle to stop by waving one's hand.
meanings.example: I managed to flag down a taxi.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 간신히 택시 한 대를 세웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take action to solve a problem or handle a situation.
meanings.example: She's very good at dealing with difficult customers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 까다로운 고객들을 다루는 데 아주 능숙하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a mistake or spoil something (Australian/NZ slang).
meanings.example: I really stuffed up that exam.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나 그 시험 정말 완전히 망쳤어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To block something up, such as a nose.
meanings.example: My nose is all stuffed up with a cold.
meanings.exampleKorean: 감기 때문에 코가 꽉 막혔어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep reminding someone about something unpleasant or a mistake they made.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 염장을 지르다, 아픈 곳을 건드리다, (잘못을) 자꾸 들먹이다
meanings.example: I know I made a mistake, you don't have to rub it in!
meanings.exampleKorean: 나도 내가 실수한 거 알아, 자꾸 들먹거리지 마!

meanings.englishDefinition: To be transferred or passed on to someone else through contact or association.
meanings.example: His enthusiasm really rubs off on everyone around him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 열정은 주변의 모든 사람에게 전염된다(영향을 준다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To have an influence on someone through association.
meanings.example: I hope some of his enthusiasm will rub off on me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 열정 중 일부가 나에게도 전염되기를 바란다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something by cutting.
meanings.example: She cut out the coupon from the newspaper.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 신문에서 쿠폰을 오려냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop doing something, especially something annoying.
meanings.example: Cut it out! I'm trying to work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그만 좀 해! 나 지금 일하는 중이잖아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be naturally suited for something (usually used in passive 'be cut out for').
meanings.example: I don't think I'm cut out for this job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 이 일에 적성이 맞지 않는 것 같아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To perform or complete a task or instruction.
meanings.example: The soldiers carried out the orders without question.
meanings.exampleKorean: 병사들은 질문 없이 명령을 수행했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To challenge someone to a fight or competition.
meanings.example: The company threw down the gauntlet to its competitors by lowering prices.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 가격을 낮춤으로써 경쟁사들에게 도전장을 내밀었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept a challenge.
meanings.example: The smaller firm decided to take up the gauntlet and compete.
meanings.exampleKorean: 더 작은 회사가 도전에 응하여 경쟁하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get rid of something that is no longer needed.
meanings.example: Don't throw away that magazine; I haven't read it yet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 잡지 버리지 마. 나 아직 안 읽었어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste an opportunity or advantage.
meanings.example: He threw away a great chance to win the game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 경기에서 이길 좋은 기회를 허비했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remind someone of something in the past.
meanings.example: That song threw me back to my high school years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 노래는 나를 고등학교 시절로 되돌려놓았다 (상기시켰다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To return something that has been caught (e.g., a fish) to where it came from.
