meanings.example: The fish was too small, so we threw it back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 물고기가 너무 작아서 다시 놓아주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cut something into several pieces.
meanings.example: He cut up the carrots for the stew.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 스튜에 넣을 당근을 잘게 썰었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very upset or distressed.
meanings.example: She was really cut up about her dog's death.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 강아지의 죽음으로 정말 마음 아파했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue to work or operate slowly but steadily without any problems.
meanings.example: The business is just ticking over at the moment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 사업은 현재 그냥 순조롭게(현상 유지하며) 돌아가고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To limit someone's freedom by giving them responsibilities.
meanings.example: I don't want to be tied down by a full-time job yet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 아직 풀타임 직장에 얽매이고 싶지 않다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing something with a lot of energy and enthusiasm (British informal).
meanings.example: We've got a lot of work to do, so let's get stuck in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 할 일이 많으니 어서 열심히 시작합시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To vomit (informal).
meanings.example: He felt sick and nearly chucked up his dinner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 속이 안 좋아서 저녁 먹은 걸 거의 토할 뻔했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To follow or chase someone.
meanings.example: Get after him! He stole my bag!
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 사람 쫓아가! 내 가방 훔쳐 갔어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To scold someone or pressure them to do something.
meanings.example: I need to get after the kids to finish their homework.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들에게 숙제 끝내라고 다그쳐야겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To escape from a person or place.
meanings.example: The thief managed to get away before the police arrived.
meanings.exampleKorean: 도둑은 경찰이 오기 전에 도망치는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave for a holiday or break.
meanings.example: I'm hoping to get away for a few days next month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다음 달에 며칠 동안 좀 떠나고 싶어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have sexual intercourse (British slang).
meanings.example: They were having it off in the back of the car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 차 뒷좌석에서 관계를 맺고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To knock someone or something to the ground.
meanings.example: He threw down his bags in frustration.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 좌절해서 가방을 바닥에 내동댕이쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to get or win something.
meanings.example: She's decided to go after the manager's job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 매니저 자리를 노려보기로(추구하기로) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To chase or follow someone in order to catch them.
meanings.example: The police went after the suspect through the crowded streets.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 붐비는 거리를 뚫고 용의자를 뒤쫓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fill a container that is already partly full.
meanings.example: Let me top up your glass.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잔 좀 더 채워 드릴게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To add more money to a phone or card balance.
meanings.example: I need to top up my travel card.
meanings.exampleKorean: 교통카드를 충전해야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To add up numbers to find the total (informal).
meanings.example: She totted up the bill quickly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 재빨리 고지서 금액을 합산했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be much taller than someone or something nearby.
meanings.example: The skyscraper towers over the old buildings below.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 마천루는 아래의 낡은 건물들 위로 높이 솟아 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Exactly right or accurate (British informal).
meanings.example: Your prediction was bang on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너의 예측은 정확했어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk about something for a long time in a boring way.
meanings.example: He's always banging on about his new car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 자기 새 차에 대해 장황하게 떠들어댄다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become stuck in something and unable to make progress.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 수렁에 빠지다, (일이) 정체되다, 꼼짝 못 하다
meanings.example: We shouldn't get bogged down in small details.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사소한 세부 사항들에 얽매여 시간을 허비해서는 안 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give an old item as part of the payment for a new one.
meanings.example: I decided to trade in my old car for a new SUV.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 낡은 차를 중고로 넘기고 새 SUV를 사기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To settle down in a place for a long period; to prepare to survive a difficult situation.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 자세를 낮추고 버티다, 잠복하다, 본격적으로 착수하다
meanings.example: We had to hunker down in the cellar during the tornado.
meanings.exampleKorean: 토네이도가 부는 동안 우리는 지하실에서 자세를 낮추고 버텨야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start working hard, especially after a period of wasting time.
meanings.example: It's time to knuckle down and study for the exams.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이제 마음 잡고 시험 공부를 본격적으로 시작할 때다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To share or divide something between several people (informal).
meanings.example: They decided to divvy up the prize money equally.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 상금을 똑같이 나누기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become extinct or disappear completely.
meanings.example: Many ancient traditions are starting to die out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 많은 고대 전통들이 사라지기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become more and more successful.
meanings.example: Since the new CEO took over, the company has gone from strength to strength.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 CEO가 부임한 이후로 회사는 승승장구하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To annoy someone or make them angry.
meanings.example: His arrogant attitude really gets my back up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 오만한 태도는 정말 나를 화나게 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To offend or annoy someone.
meanings.example: His critical remarks put the whole team's back up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 비판적인 발언은 팀 전체의 비위를 건드렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To frighten someone very much (informal).
meanings.example: That horror movie scared the pants off me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 공포 영화는 나를 정말 몹시 겁나게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have dealings or associations with someone (usually negative).
meanings.example: I will have no truck with criminals.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 범죄자들과는 절대 상종하지 않을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give an official or legal decision about something.
meanings.example: The court will rule on the case next week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 법원은 다음 주에 그 사건에 대해 판결을 내릴 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To travel to many different places.
meanings.example: He gets around a lot for his job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 직업상 여기저기 많이 돌아다닌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid a problem or rule.
meanings.example: They found a way to get around the new regulations.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 새로운 규제를 피할 방법을 찾아냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have the power, skill, or opportunity to do something.
meanings.example: Will you be able to help me tomorrow?
meanings.exampleKorean: 내일 저 좀 도와주실 수 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To finally find the time to do something.
meanings.example: I finally got around to answering your email.
meanings.exampleKorean: 드디어 짬을 내서 네 이메일에 답장을 썼어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a vehicle or building.
meanings.example: I got out of the taxi at the airport.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 공항에서 택시에서 내렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid doing something that you should do.
meanings.example: He tried to get out of doing the dishes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 설거지하는 것을 피하려고 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat someone or something without enough respect.
meanings.example: He is not a man to be trifled with.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 함부로 대할 사람이 아니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put on a piece of clothing to see if it fits.
meanings.example: Can I try on this jacket, please?
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 재킷 좀 입어볼 수 있을까요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To test something to see how well it works.
meanings.example: I'm going to try out the new software tonight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 밤에 새 소프트웨어를 시험해 볼 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To agree to become involved in an organized activity.
meanings.example: I've signed up for a yoga class.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 요가 수업에 등록했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give someone else legal ownership of something by signing a document.
meanings.example: He signed over the house to his children.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 집을 자녀들에게 양도했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To record your arrival in a register.
meanings.example: All visitors must sign in at the front desk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 방문객은 프런트에서 도착 기록을 해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something bad and not be punished for it (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 잘못을 하고도 무사히 넘어가다, 벌을 면하다
meanings.example: He's so charming that he gets away with murder.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 너무 매력적이어서 어떤 잘못을 해도 그냥 넘어간다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop a journey and return to the place you started from.
meanings.example: The weather was so bad that we had to turn back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 날씨가 너무 안 좋아서 우리는 되돌아가야만 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone feel uncomfortable or strange (slang).
meanings.example: That guy really weirds me out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 남자 기분이 정말 이상하게 만들어 (찝찝하게 해).

meanings.englishDefinition: To become very excited, angry, or afraid suddenly.
meanings.example: I freaked out when I saw the spider in my bed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 침대에 있는 거미를 보고 나는 기겁했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay at a place for one night or a few days on the way to somewhere else.
meanings.example: We stopped over in Dubai for two nights on our way to London.
meanings.exampleKorean: 런던으로 가는 길에 두 번 밤을 두바이에서 경유했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sign a formal legal document or agreement.
meanings.example: Once you sign on the dotted line, you are legally bound to the contract.
meanings.exampleKorean: 일단 서명란에 서명하면, 당신은 법적으로 계약에 구속됩니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To say something suddenly and without thinking, usually because you are excited or nervous.
meanings.example: She blurted out the secret before she could stop herself.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 자신도 모르게 비밀을 불쑥 내뱉어 버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To raise your fists in preparation for a fight (informal).
meanings.example: Come on, put up your dukes!
meanings.exampleKorean: 자, 어디 한판 붙어보자고!

meanings.englishDefinition: To admit that you are wrong or that you have been defeated.
meanings.example: Neither side was willing to back down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어느 쪽도 물러서려(주장을 굽히려) 하지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent something bad from happening for a short time.
meanings.example: We did everything we could to stave off bankruptcy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 파산을 막기 위해 할 수 있는 모든 것을 다 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To perform oral sex on a male (vulgar slang).
meanings.example: The movie contained a scene of someone being sucked off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 영화에는 누군가 구강 성교를 받는 장면이 포함되어 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to find someone or something.
meanings.example: I'm looking for my keys; have you seen them?
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 열쇠를 찾고 있는데, 본 적 있니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To spread or distribute something.
meanings.example: The news was given about that the war was over.
meanings.exampleKorean: 전쟁이 끝났다는 소식이 여기저기 퍼졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To emit or release something, such as a smell or sound.
meanings.example: The flowers gave forth a sweet fragrance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 꽃들이 달콤한 향기를 내뿜었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To show that something is false.
meanings.example: The new evidence gave the lie to his alibi.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 증거는 그의 알리바이가 거짓임을 입증했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To distribute something to a group of people.
meanings.example: They were giving out free tickets to the show.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 공연 무료 티켓을 나누어 주고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop working or to be used up.
meanings.example: Her patience finally gave out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 인내심이 마침내 바닥났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop doing something annoying (British informal).
meanings.example: Give over! I'm trying to sleep.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그만 좀 해! 나 자려고 하잖아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hand something over to someone else.
meanings.example: The building was given over to the local community.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 건물은 지역 사회에 넘겨졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause something to happen or exist.
meanings.example: The new policy could give rise to many problems.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 정책은 많은 문제를 초래할 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try any idea or method to solve a problem, even if it is not likely to succeed.
meanings.example: He's just grasping at straws because he has no real evidence.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 진짜 증거가 없어서 그냥 지푸라기라도 잡으려 하는 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent something from happening.
meanings.example: We need to head off any potential conflicts.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 어떠한 잠재적 갈등도 미연에 방지해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave or depart.
meanings.example: I think it's time we headed off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이제 우리가 떠나야 할 시간인 것 같아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To invent or create a new idea or plan.
meanings.example: Can you think up a better way to do this?
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 일을 할 더 좋은 방법을 생각해낼 수 있니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To hit someone or something with a vehicle.
meanings.example: He was run over by a truck.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 트럭에 치였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue past a set limit of time.
meanings.example: The meeting ran over by thirty minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의가 30분 정도 더 길어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To read or check something quickly.
meanings.example: Let's run over the main points once more.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주요 요점들을 다시 한번 빨리 훑어봅시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To draw a line through something you have written.
meanings.example: If you make a mistake, just cross it out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 실수하면 그냥 줄을 그어 지우세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove a name from a list by drawing a line through it.
meanings.example: Cross my name off the list.
meanings.exampleKorean: 제 이름을 목록에서 지워주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be good enough to meet a standard or expectation.
meanings.example: The new film didn't really measure up to his previous work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 새 영화는 그의 전작만큼의 기대에 부응하지 못했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter a building by force, usually to steal something.
meanings.example: Someone broke into my car last night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어젯밤 누군가 내 차에 침입했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly start doing something, like laughing or running.
meanings.example: She broke into tears when she heard the news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그 소식을 듣고 갑자기 울음을 터뜨렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to do something, especially for the first time.
meanings.example: I've never played golf, but I'll take a crack at it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 골프는 한 번도 안 쳐봤지만, 한번 시도해볼게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attempt something or to make a guess.
meanings.example: I'll take a stab at the answer, although I'm not sure.
meanings.exampleKorean: 확실하진 않지만, 짐작으로라도 답을 말해볼게.

meanings.englishDefinition: A polite way of saying that you disagree with someone.
meanings.example: I'm sorry, but I beg to differ on that point.
meanings.exampleKorean: 죄송하지만, 그 점에 대해서는 저와 의견이 다릅니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To refuse to allow someone to enter a place.
meanings.example: The club was full, so many people were turned away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 클럽이 꽉 차서 많은 사람이 발길을 돌려야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To look away from someone or something.
meanings.example: She turned away in disgust.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 혐오감을 느끼며 고개를 돌려버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To kill an animal humanely because it is very old or sick.
meanings.example: The vet advised us to have the old dog put to sleep.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수의사는 늙은 개를 안락사시키라고 권고했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To invent a story or plan, often to deceive someone.
meanings.example: He cooked up a crazy excuse for being late.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 늦은 것에 대해 말도 안 되는 변명을 꾸며냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To record the time you arrive at work.
meanings.example: What time did you clock in this morning?
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 아침 몇 시에 출근 도장 찍었어?

meanings.englishDefinition: To record the time you leave work.
meanings.example: I forgot to clock out when I left.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나갈 때 퇴근 기록하는 걸 깜빡했어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish work for the day (British).
meanings.example: I usually clock off at 5 p.m.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 보통 오후 5시에 퇴직한다 (업무를 마친다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To destroy or defeat someone or something completely.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 완전히 박살 내다, 압도적으로 이기다, 무색하게 만들다
meanings.example: The new model will blow all the competitors out of the water.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 모델은 모든 경쟁사 제품들을 완전히 박살 낼(압도할) 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To humble oneself and beg for forgiveness or to submit to someone else's will.
meanings.example: The politician was forced to go to Canossa and apologize to the public.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인은 굴욕을 참고 대중에게 사과해야만 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tightly contract one's lips to prepare to kiss someone.
meanings.example: He told her to pucker up and then gave her a kiss.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그녀에게 입술을 오므리라고 말한 뒤 키스를 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to suffer from an illness, especially one that is not serious.
meanings.example: I think I'm coming down with a cold.
meanings.exampleKorean: 감기에 걸리려나 봐요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To defend oneself against an attack or criticism.
meanings.example: The politician managed to fend off the difficult questions from the press.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인은 언론의 까다로운 질문들을 잘 막아냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase in amount or quantity.
meanings.example: Work is starting to pile up on my desk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 책상 위에 일이 쌓이기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be in charge of a group or an organization.
meanings.example: She was chosen to head up the new research department.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 새로운 연구 부서를 이끌 적임자로 선정되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be careful because of a possible danger.
meanings.example: Watch out! There's a car coming.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조심해! 차가 오고 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wear formal or special clothes.
meanings.example: We like to dress up when we go to the theater.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 극장에 갈 때 옷을 차려입는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wear a costume for fun or a play.
meanings.example: The children dressed up as pirates for the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 파티를 위해 해적으로 변장했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage to live or do something with difficulty.
meanings.example: It's hard to get by on such a small salary.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그렇게 적은 월급으로 그럭저럭 살아가기는 힘들다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To organize your life or work so that you can achieve something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 정신 차리고 잘하다, 체계적으로 행동하다, 일을 수습하다
meanings.example: You need to get your act together if you want to pass this course.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 과정을 통과하고 싶으면 정신 차리고 제대로 해야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To organize your life or work more effectively so that you can achieve what you want (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 마음을 다잡다, 제대로 하다, 정신을 차리다
meanings.example: He needs to get his act together if he wants to keep his job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 직장을 계속 다니고 싶다면 그는 마음을 다잡아야(제대로 행동해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make an extra effort to do something, especially to help someone.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 특별히 노력하다, 일부러 수고를 아끼지 않다
meanings.example: The neighbors went out of their way to help us move in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이웃들이 우리가 이사 오는 것을 돕기 위해 일부러 수고를 아끼지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To support or strengthen something that is weak or failing.
meanings.example: The government is trying to shore up the struggling economy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 어려움을 겪고 있는 경제를 떠받치기 위해 노력하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move a vehicle to the side of the road and stop.
meanings.example: The police officer told him to pull over.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰관이 그에게 차를 길가에 세우라고 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start moving away (of a vehicle).
meanings.example: The train pulled away from the platform.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기차가 승강장에서 움직이기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move your body away from someone who is trying to touch you.
meanings.example: She pulled away when he tried to kiss her.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 키스하려 하자 그녀는 몸을 뺐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gather in the streets to protest or celebrate.
meanings.example: Thousands of people took to the streets to protest the new law.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수천 명의 사람들이 새 법률에 반대하기 위해 거리로 쏟아져 나왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join an activity that has become very popular or successful.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 시류에 편승하다, 인기 있는 것에 합류하다
meanings.example: Many companies are jumping on the bandwagon of environmental sustainability.
meanings.exampleKorean: 많은 기업이 환경적 지속가능성이라는 시류에 편승하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To succeed in doing something with great difficulty, especially an exam.
meanings.example: He just managed to scrape through his final exams.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 기말고사를 간신히 통과했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discuss something thoroughly to find a solution (informal).
meanings.example: They spent hours trying to trash out the details of the agreement.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 합의안의 세부 사항을 철저히 논의하는 데 몇 시간을 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become conscious again after being unconscious.
meanings.example: He's starting to come around after the surgery.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 수술 후 이제 막 의식을 회복하기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To change your opinion to agree with someone else's.
meanings.example: I'm sure she'll come around to our way of thinking eventually.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀도 결국에는 우리 생각에 동조하게 될 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail completely; to go bankrupt.
meanings.example: The company went belly up during the financial crisis.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 금융 위기 동안 완전히 망해버렸다 (파산했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop a machine, business, or system from operating.
meanings.example: The factory was forced to shut down due to the lack of orders.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 공장은 주문 부족으로 인해 가동을 중단해야만 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak more loudly so that people can hear you.
meanings.example: Could you speak up a bit? I can't hear you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조금만 더 크게 말씀해 주시겠어요? 잘 안 들려요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To express your opinion freely.
meanings.example: If you don't agree with the plan, you should speak up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 계획에 동의하지 않는다면, 당당하게 의견을 밝혀야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To state your opinion publicly, especially in opposition to something.
meanings.example: He spoke out against the new tax laws.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 새로운 세법에 반대하는 목소리를 분명히 냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place quickly (informal).
meanings.example: I'm just going to pop off to the shops for a minute.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가게에 잠깐만 후딱 갔다 올게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk loudly or complain angrily (informal).
meanings.example: He's always popping off about the government.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 정부에 대해 버럭버럭 떠들어댄다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To belong to a different religion or political party, or to have a different sexual orientation (idiomatic).
meanings.example: In that town, you could always tell who kicked with the other foot.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 마을에서는 누가 다른 성향(또는 다른 종교)을 가졌는지 언제나 알 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To touch someone sexually in a way that they do not like (informal).
meanings.example: She accused him of trying to feel her up on the bus.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 버스에서 그가 자신을 더듬으려 했다고 비난했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move closer to someone or something, often in order to attack or catch them.
meanings.example: The police were closing in on the suspect.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰이 용의자에게 바짝 다가가고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop a vehicle.
meanings.example: A black car pulled up outside the house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 검은색 차 한 대가 집 밖에 멈춰 섰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move a chair closer to something.
meanings.example: Pull up a chair and join us.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의자 하나 당겨 앉아서 우리랑 같이하자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To scold someone severely (informal).
meanings.example: The boss chewed him out for being late again.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사장은 그가 또 늦은 것에 대해 호되게 꾸짖었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be forced to leave a school or college because of failing grades.
meanings.example: He flunked out of university in his second year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 2학년 때 성적 미달로 대학에서 제적당했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin or carry out an activity.
meanings.example: How should I go about finding a job?
meanings.exampleKorean: 구직 활동을 어떻게 시작해야 할까요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something last longer than is necessary.
meanings.example: There's no need to drag out the meeting for three hours.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의를 3시간이나 질질 끌 필요는 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To answer a telephone call.
meanings.example: Why don't you pick up the phone? It's been ringing for ages.
meanings.exampleKorean: 전화 왜 안 받아? 한참 동안 울리고 있잖아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To interfere in something that does not concern you.
meanings.example: Stop sticking your nose in my business!
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 일에 간섭 좀 하지 마!

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept and deal with a difficult situation.
meanings.example: You have to face up to the fact that you need help.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신에게 도움이 필요하다는 사실을 직시해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A phrase used to tell someone to start asking questions or speaking.
meanings.example: I'm ready to answer your questions, so fire away!
meanings.exampleKorean: 질문에 답할 준비가 됐으니, 말씀해 보세요!

meanings.englishDefinition: To feel that you belong to a group and are accepted by them.
meanings.example: He never really felt like he fitted in at school.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 학교에서 자신이 잘 어울린다고 느껴본 적이 한 번도 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find time to do something or see someone.
meanings.example: The doctor can fit you in at 3 o'clock.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의사 선생님이 3시에 시간을 내주실 수 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually disappear into darkness (of a film scene or a situation).
meanings.example: The final scene fades to black.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마지막 장면이 서서히 어두워지며 끝난다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate an area with a fence.
meanings.example: They fenced off the garden to keep the deer out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 사슴이 들어오지 못하게 정원에 울타리를 쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put a document in a specific place so that you can find it later.
meanings.example: I'll file these letters away for future reference.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 편지들은 나중에 참고할 수 있도록 철해 두겠습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To contribute money to a common fund.
meanings.example: Everyone chipped in ten dollars for the gift.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모두가 선물을 위해 10달러씩 보탰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To interrupt a conversation with a comment.
meanings.example: May I chip in here? I have a suggestion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여기서 한마디 거들어도 될까요? 제안이 하나 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid work or responsibility; to waste time (slang).
meanings.example: He spent the whole afternoon goofing off with his friends.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 오후 내내 친구들과 빈둥거리며 시간을 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sleep longer than usual in the morning.
meanings.example: I love to sleep in on Saturdays.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 토요일에 늦잠 자는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be thought of immediately.
meanings.example: When I think of Paris, the Eiffel Tower immediately springs to mind.
meanings.exampleKorean: 파리를 생각하면 에펠탑이 즉시 머릿속에 떠오른다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come quickly into your thoughts.
meanings.example: Her name immediately sprang to mind.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 이름이 즉시 머릿속에 떠올랐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept something unpleasant without complaining or fighting back.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 군소리 없이 받아들이다, (부당한 대우 등에) 저항하지 않다
meanings.example: I'm not going to take that insult lying down!
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그 모욕을 군소리 없이 받아들이지 않을 거야!

meanings.englishDefinition: To tell a truth disguised as a joke (American slang).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 농담 반 진담 반으로 말하다, 진담을 농담처럼 하다
meanings.example: He's kidding on the square about moving to Hawaii.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 하와이로 이사 간다는 말을 농담 반 진담 반으로 하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay in a place for a little longer, waiting for something to happen (informal).
meanings.example: I think I'll stick around for a while and see what happens.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잠시 여기 머물면서 무슨 일이 일어나는지 지켜볼게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept a challenge or insult without reacting strongly.
meanings.example: He's not the kind of person to take an insult sitting down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 모욕을 고분고분 받아들일 사람이 아니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be completely opposed to something that is usual or expected.
meanings.example: This decision flies in the face of all common sense.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 결정은 모든 상식에 정면으로 반하는 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become extremely tired or sick by working too hard.
meanings.example: If he doesn't take a holiday soon, he'll burn out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조만간 휴가를 내지 않으면 그는 완전히 지쳐버릴 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop burning because there is no more fuel.
meanings.example: The fire has finally burnt out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 불이 마침내 다 타서 꺼졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To divide something into small parts and give them to different people.
meanings.example: The land was parcelled out among the farmers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 땅은 농부들에게 나누어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone go through a very difficult or stressful experience.
meanings.example: The interviewers really put her through the wringer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 면접관들은 그녀를 정말 몹시 고생시켰다 (까다로운 질문 공세를 퍼부었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to do something, especially after waiting for permission.
meanings.example: Can I use the phone? - Sure, go ahead.
meanings.exampleKorean: 전화 좀 써도 될까요? - 네, 어서 쓰세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To work hard and steadily at something, especially something difficult.
meanings.example: She's been plugging away at her novel for years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 몇 년째 자신의 소설을 묵묵히 써오고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gain an understanding of something or to be able to control it.
meanings.example: I'm finally starting to get a handle on this new software.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 드디어 이 새로운 소프트웨어를 파악하기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a good understanding of something or to have it under control.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 잘 알고 있다, 파악하고 있다, 통제하다
meanings.example: He seems to have a good handle on the situation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 상황을 잘 파악하고 있는 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To visit someone for a short time.
meanings.example: I'll call on you tomorrow evening.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내일 저녁에 너를 찾아갈게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ask someone to do something or to answer a question.
meanings.example: The teacher called on me to answer the question.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님은 나를 지명하여 질문에 답하게 하셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop talking or making noise; to be quiet (informal).
meanings.example: Pipe down, kids! I'm trying to talk on the phone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조용히 좀 해라, 얘들아! 전화 중이잖아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To watch something happening without being involved in it.
meanings.example: A large crowd looked on as the firefighters put out the fire.
meanings.exampleKorean: 소방관들이 불을 끄는 동안 많은 군중이 그 모습을 지켜보았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be ringing constantly, especially because many people are calling with requests or information.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 전화가 빗발치다, 쉴 새 없이 울리다
meanings.example: Since the advert appeared, the phone has been ringing off the hook.
meanings.exampleKorean: 광고가 나간 이후로 전화가 빗발치고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become less drunk or to make someone less drunk.
meanings.example: He needs a few hours to sober up before he can drive.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 운전하기 전에 술이 깨려면 몇 시간이 필요하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a small mistake, often due to carelessness.
meanings.example: I'm sorry, I slipped up and gave you the wrong address.
meanings.exampleKorean: 미안해요, 제가 실수로 잘못된 주소를 알려드렸네요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place or person secretly and unexpectedly.
meanings.example: He ran away from home when he was fifteen.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 15살 때 가출했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To walk in a circle or in different directions within a place.
meanings.example: We spent the afternoon walking around the park.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 오후 시간을 공원을 돌아다니며 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pull something out of the ground by its roots.
meanings.example: The gardener is rooting up the weeds.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정원사가 잡초를 뿌리째 뽑고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do a lot of difficult or unnecessary things in order to achieve something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 온갖 고생을 다 하다, 시키는 대로 다 하다, 까다로운 절차를 밟다
meanings.example: We had to jump through hoops to get a loan from the bank.
meanings.exampleKorean: 은행 대출을 받기 위해 우리는 온갖 까다로운 절차를 다 밟아야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use the toilet (euphemism).
meanings.example: Can I go to the bathroom, please?
meanings.exampleKorean: 화장실 좀 다녀와도 될까요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To become cooler in temperature.
meanings.example: Let the soup cool off before you eat it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 국이 식으면 드세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become less angry or excited.
meanings.example: He needs some time to cool off after the argument.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 말다툼 후에 진정할 시간이 좀 필요하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discover information or a fact.
meanings.example: I'll find out the train times for you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신을 위해 기차 시간을 알아봐 드릴게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste time near the end of a game so the opponent doesn't have a chance to win.
meanings.example: The team tried to run out the clock in the final minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 팀은 마지막 몇 분 동안 시간 벌기를 시도했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move or do something faster.
meanings.example: Hurry up, or we'll miss the train!
meanings.exampleKorean: 서둘러, 안 그러면 기차 놓칠 거야!

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something become thin or smooth by using it a lot.
meanings.example: The heels of my shoes have worn down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 구두 굽이 다 닳았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually make someone weaker or less determined.
meanings.example: They managed to wear down the enemy's resistance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 적의 저항을 점차 약화시키는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be unfaithful to a partner in a romantic relationship.
meanings.example: He cheated on his wife for years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 수년간 아내 몰래 바람을 피웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish your drink completely.
meanings.example: Drink up! It's time to go.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다 마셔! 이제 갈 시간이야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To help someone to survive a difficult period, especially by lending them money.
meanings.koreanDefinition: (어려움을) 넘기게 해주다, (돈 등이) 당분간 버티게 해주다
meanings.example: Can you lend me some money to tide me over until payday?
meanings.exampleKorean: 월급날까지 버틸 수 있게 돈 좀 빌려줄 수 있니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To shout something loudly.
meanings.example: He called out my name from the other side of the street.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 길 건너편에서 내 이름을 크게 불렀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize someone publicly for something they have done wrong.
meanings.example: It's time to call out people who spread fake news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가짜 뉴스를 퍼뜨리는 사람들을 지적해야 할 때입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone feel relaxed and confident.
meanings.example: She has a natural ability to put people at ease.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 사람들을 편안하게 해주는 타고난 능력이 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone feel very tired.
meanings.example: The long walk completely tired the children out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 긴 산책은 아이들을 완전히 녹초로 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cover a window or door with wooden boards.
meanings.example: The abandoned shop had been boarded up for years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 버려진 가게는 수년간 판자로 막혀 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To buy someone else's share in a business so that you own it all.
meanings.example: The larger corporation decided to buy out its smaller rival.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 대기업은 더 작은 라이벌 업체를 인수하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start sleeping.
meanings.example: I usually go to sleep around 11 p.m.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 보통 밤 11시쯤 잠자리에 든다 (잠이 든다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To become numb (of a body part).
meanings.example: My leg has gone to sleep because I was sitting on it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 깔고 앉아 있었더니 다리에 쥐가 났다 (다리가 저리다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To get into bed to sleep.
meanings.example: Go to bed! It's already past midnight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자러 가! 벌써 자정이 넘었어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very successful or to get a lot of profit from something (informal).
meanings.example: He made out like a bandit when he sold his tech company.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 기술 회사를 매각했을 때 엄청난 이득을 보았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To slowly disappear or become weaker.
meanings.example: The music slowly faded away into the distance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 음악 소리가 멀리서 서서히 사라졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To check an arithmetical calculation by a specific method involving the digit 9.
meanings.example: The accountant used the cast out nines method to check the totals.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회계사는 합계를 확인하기 위해 9거법 검산 방식을 사용했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start eating something with enthusiasm (informal).
meanings.example: The food's on the table, so dig in!
meanings.exampleKorean: 음식 차려놨으니, 어서들 먹어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare for a long and difficult task or to defend a position.
meanings.example: The company is digging in for a long legal battle.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 긴 법적 공방에 대비해 방어 태세를 굳히고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually become less strong, loud, or exciting.
meanings.example: We waited for the wind to die down before setting sail.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 출항하기 전에 바람이 잠잠해지기를 기다렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: At maximum speed or effort.
meanings.example: We've been working flat out to finish the project on time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 프로젝트를 제시간에 끝내기 위해 전력을 다해 일하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: In a direct and complete way; lying.
meanings.example: He just told me a flat out lie.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 나에게 아주 노골적인 거짓말을 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start talking about the most basic and important facts of a situation.
meanings.example: Let's stop wasting time and get down to brass tacks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시간 낭비 그만하고 이제 본론으로 들어갑시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have sexual intercourse (slang).
meanings.example: They were trying to find a place to get it on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 관계를 가질 만한 장소를 찾고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To recover from an illness or a bad experience.
meanings.example: It took him a long time to get over the break-up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 이별을 극복하는 데 오랜 시간이 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To succeed in communicating something.
meanings.example: He found it difficult to get his message over to the students.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 학생들에게 자신의 메시지를 전달하는 것이 어렵다는 것을 알았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Extremely tired; exhausted.
meanings.example: I'm completely worn out after that long hike.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 긴 하이킹 후에 나는 완전히 녹초가 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: No longer useful because of being used too much.
meanings.example: My old sneakers are finally worn out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 낡은 운동화가 마침내 다 해졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To like someone and become friendly with them immediately.
meanings.example: We hit it off from the moment we met.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 만난 순간부터 금방 친해졌다 (죽이 잘 맞았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste time or behave in a silly way (vulgar slang).
meanings.example: Stop fucking about and do some work!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그만 빈둥거리고 일 좀 해!

meanings.englishDefinition: To eagerly accept an opportunity.
meanings.example: She jumped at the chance to work in Paris.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 파리에서 일할 기회를 덥석 잡았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enjoy something very much and accept it eagerly.
meanings.example: The audience lapped up every word of his performance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 관객들은 그의 공연 한 마디 한 마디를 기쁘게 받아들였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To not punish someone for something they have done wrong.
meanings.example: The police let him off with a warning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 경고만 주고 그를 봐주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause something like a bomb or firework to explode.
meanings.example: The kids were letting off fireworks in the garden.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들이 정원에서 폭죽을 터뜨리고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something full.
meanings.example: Shall I fill up the car with petrol?
meanings.exampleKorean: 차에 기름 좀 가득 채울까요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce speed or to make something happen more slowly.
meanings.example: You need to slow down; you're driving too fast.
meanings.exampleKorean: 속도를 좀 줄여야 해. 너무 빨리 운전하고 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move or happen faster.
meanings.example: The car speeded up to overtake the truck.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 차는 트럭을 추월하기 위해 속도를 높였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave an important job or position.
meanings.example: The CEO decided to step down after ten years in the role.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 CEO는 10년 동안 그 자리를 지킨 뒤 사임하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have great confidence in the value or efficacy of something.
meanings.example: My grandmother swears by this herbal tea for a cold.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 할머니는 감기에는 이 허브차가 최고라고 아주 신뢰하신다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To complete the parts of a task that have not yet been finished.
meanings.example: We still have a few loose ends to tie up before the project is finished.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프로젝트를 끝내기 전에 아직 마무리해야 할 미진한 부분이 몇 가지 남아 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To choose or try to get something.
meanings.example: I think I'll go for the chocolate cake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 초콜릿 케이크를 선택할래.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be attracted to a certain type of person or thing.
meanings.example: She usually goes for taller men.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 보통 키 큰 남자들에게 끌린다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very eager or impatient to do something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 안달이 나다, 하고 싶어서 못 견디다
meanings.example: The kids are champing at the bit to go to the amusement park.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 놀이공원에 가고 싶어서 안달이 나 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop annoying or criticizing someone.
meanings.example: I wish my boss would get off my back about that report.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 상사가 그 보고서 건으로 나를 그만 좀 들볶았으면 좋겠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take no action when it is needed.
meanings.example: We can't just sit on our hands while the environment is being destroyed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 환경이 파괴되고 있는데 그냥 수수방관하고 있을 수는 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To end a connection to a computer system.
meanings.example: Don't forget to log off before you leave the office.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사무실을 나가기 전에 로그오프하는 걸 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go away; used as a command to tell someone to leave (British slang).
meanings.example: Just bog off and leave me alone!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그냥 저리 가서 나 좀 내버려 둬!

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very impatient to do something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 안달이 나다, 몹시 하고 싶어 하다
meanings.example: The athletes are chomping at the bit for the race to start.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선수들은 경주가 시작되기를 안달하며 기다리고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move backwards away from someone or something, often because you are afraid.
meanings.example: The crowd backed away as the fire spread.
meanings.exampleKorean: 불길이 번지자 군중들은 뒷걸음질하며 물러났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to feel happier or to make someone feel happier.
meanings.example: Cheer up! Things aren't that bad.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기운 내! 상황이 그렇게 나쁘지는 않아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ask someone for money (American slang).
meanings.example: He's always trying to put the bee on his friends for a few bucks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 친구들에게 몇 달러만 빌려달라고 손을 벌린다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop doing business or to stop an activity for the day.
meanings.example: It was getting late, so we decided to shut up shop and go home.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시간이 늦어서 우리는 일을 끝내고 집에 가기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start happening successfully.
meanings.example: The new business finally got off the ground last month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 새 사업은 지난달에 마침내 순조롭게 시작되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To travel to one's place of employment.
meanings.example: What time do you usually go to work?
meanings.exampleKorean: 보통 몇 시에 출근하세요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin doing a task or job.
meanings.example: Let's go to work and finish this report.
meanings.exampleKorean: 일을 시작해서 이 보고서를 끝냅시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start a journey.
meanings.example: They set out on their expedition at dawn.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 새벽에 탐험을 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To present or explain something clearly.
meanings.example: The report sets out the company's goals for the next year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 보고서는 회사의 내년 목표를 명확히 제시하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use a lot of physical or mental effort to do something.
meanings.example: If you put your back into it, you'll finish the work in no time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 네가 전력을 다한다면 그 일을 순식간에 끝낼 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lie flat on the ground, especially when ordered by police.
meanings.example: The suspect was ordered to prone out on the pavement.
meanings.exampleKorean: 용의자는 인도 위에 바짝 엎드리라는 명령을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause a difficult or violent situation to begin.
meanings.example: The protest touched off a series of riots.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 시위는 일련의 폭동을 촉발했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fill a vehicle's tank with fuel.
meanings.example: We should gas up before we head onto the highway.
meanings.exampleKorean: 고속도로에 진입하기 전에 주유를 해야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use all the resources or effort available to achieve something.
meanings.example: The company took out all the stops to ensure the product launch was a success.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 제품 출시 성공을 위해 전력을 기울였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove a layer of clothing.
meanings.example: He peeled off his wet coat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 젖은 코트를 벗었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a group or move in a different direction.
meanings.example: One of the planes peeled off from the formation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비행기 중 한 대가 대열에서 이탈했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To serve food onto plates for a meal.
meanings.example: Dinner is ready! Can you help me dish up?
meanings.exampleKorean: 저녁 다 됐다! 음식 담는 것 좀 도와줄래?

meanings.englishDefinition: To fill a place with too many things so that it is untidy.
meanings.example: Try not to clutter up the hallway with your bags.
meanings.exampleKorean: 복도에 가방을 두어서 어지럽히지 않도록 해라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To allow someone to share a secret or information.
meanings.example: Are you going to let me in on your little secret?
meanings.exampleKorean: 네 작은 비밀을 나한테도 알려줄 거니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To meet someone by chance.
meanings.example: I bumped into an old school friend at the supermarket today.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 슈퍼마켓에서 옛 학교 친구를 우연히 마주쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start a journey.
meanings.example: What time are you setting off tomorrow morning?
meanings.exampleKorean: 내일 아침 몇 시에 출발하시나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause a device to explode or an alarm to ring.
meanings.example: The smoke set off the fire alarm.
meanings.exampleKorean: 연기가 화재경보기를 울리게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move backwards away from someone or something.
meanings.example: Back off! Give me some space.
meanings.exampleKorean: 뒤로 좀 물러나! 공간 좀 줘.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop being involved in a situation or criticizing someone.
meanings.example: I told him to back off and let me do it my way.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그에게 간섭하지 말고 내 방식대로 하게 해달라고 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have the back part of something facing a particular place.
meanings.example: Our garden backs onto a small park.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 정원 뒤편은 작은 공원과 접해 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ask a professional person to come and help.
meanings.example: We had to call in a plumber to fix the leak.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 누수를 고치기 위해 배관공을 불러야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To telephone a place, like a radio station or your office.
meanings.example: He called in sick this morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 오늘 아침 병가를 내려고 전화했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something from under the ground by digging.
meanings.example: The dog was digging up bones in the garden.
meanings.exampleKorean: 강아지가 정원에서 뼈다귀를 파내고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discover new information about someone or something.
meanings.example: The reporters are trying to dig up some dirt on the politician.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기자들은 그 정치인에 대한 치부를 들춰내려 애쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To close all the doors and windows of a building with a key.
meanings.example: Don't forget to lock up before you go to bed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자기 전에 문 다 잠그는 거 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put someone in prison.
meanings.example: He was locked up for ten years for his crimes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 범죄로 인해 10년 동안 감금되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decide that a planned event will not happen.
meanings.example: The match was called off because of bad weather.
meanings.exampleKorean: 악천후 때문에 경기가 취소되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move directly towards something you are looking for.
meanings.example: The missile was homing in on the enemy target.
meanings.exampleKorean: 미사일이 적의 목표물을 향해 곧장 날아가고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To study a subject a lot in a short time (informal).
meanings.example: I need to bone up on my French before our trip to Paris.
meanings.exampleKorean: 파리 여행 전에 프랑스어 공부를 좀 열심히 해야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase prices or taxes significantly.
meanings.example: The government decided to hike up the interest rates.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 금리를 대폭 인상하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to say that you admire someone for something they have done.
meanings.example: I take my hat off to anyone who can run a marathon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마라톤을 완주할 수 있는 사람이라면 누구에게나 경의를 표한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clap your hands together to show approval or as a sign of welcome.
meanings.example: Let's put our hands together for our next guest!
meanings.exampleKorean: 다음 손님을 박수로 환영합시다!

meanings.englishDefinition: To have total control over someone, usually by exploiting a weakness (vulgar slang).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 꼼짝 못 하게 잡다, 완전히 통제하다 (비속어)
meanings.example: He's got the company by the balls because he knows all their secrets.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 회사의 모든 비밀을 알고 있기 때문에 회사를 꼼짝 못 하게 잡고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hide your feelings and not talk about them.
meanings.example: It's not healthy to bottle up your emotions like that.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그렇게 감정을 억누르기만 하는 것은 건강에 좋지 않다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do someone's job for a short time while they are away.
meanings.example: I'm sitting in for the manager while she's on holiday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매니저가 휴가 중이라 제가 대신 업무를 보고 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To disable or comment out a piece of code (jargon).
meanings.example: I dummied out the unused functions before shipping the software.
meanings.exampleKorean: 소프트웨어를 출시하기 전에 사용하지 않는 함수들을 무력화했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go away; used as a command to tell someone to leave you alone (informal).
meanings.example: I'm busy, so just buzz off!
meanings.exampleKorean: 바쁘니까 그냥 저리 좀 가!

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin to understand and deal with a difficult situation or problem.
meanings.example: The government is finally trying to get to grips with the housing crisis.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부가 드디어 주택 위기 문제를 본격적으로 다루기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To agree with or support an idea or plan.
meanings.example: I'm happy to go along with your suggestion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신의 제안에 기꺼이 동의합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue doing something difficult.
meanings.example: If you keep at it, you'll eventually master the piano.
meanings.exampleKorean: 끈기 있게 계속한다면 결국 피아노를 마스터하게 될 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: Following a simple, pre-set pattern without using any imagination.
meanings.example: His approach to design is a bit color by number.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 디자인 접근 방식은 다소 정해진 틀에만 박혀 있다 (독창성이 없다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid going near someone or something.
meanings.example: Keep away from the edge of the cliff!
meanings.exampleKorean: 절벽 끝에서 멀리 떨어져!

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop something from increasing in size or number.
meanings.example: The government is trying to keep down inflation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 인플레이션을 억제하려 애쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent your voice from being loud.
meanings.example: Keep your voice down! The baby is sleeping.
meanings.exampleKorean: 목소리 좀 낮춰! 아기가 자고 있잖아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent someone from doing something or something from happening.
meanings.example: I couldn't keep from laughing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 웃음을 참을 수가 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid standing or walking on something.
meanings.example: Please keep off the grass.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잔디밭에 들어가지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be involved with dangerous or unscrupulous people.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 위험한 사람들과 어울리다, 험난한 곳에서 살아남다
meanings.example: If you want to be a top lawyer, you have to learn to swim with sharks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 일류 변호사가 되고 싶다면 교활하고 위험한 사람들과 어울리는 법을 배워야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be so full of an emotion that you cannot hide it.
meanings.example: She was brimming over with confidence.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 자신감이 넘쳐흐르고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To automatically switch to a backup system when the primary system fails.
meanings.example: The database will fail over to the secondary server if there's a crash.
meanings.exampleKorean: 충돌이 발생하면 데이터베이스는 보조 서버로 자동 전환될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To follow someone or something until you catch them or find what you need.
meanings.example: The detective managed to chase down the lead.
meanings.exampleKorean: 형사는 실마리를 추적하여 찾아내는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be careful and watch for potential danger.
meanings.example: Look out! There's a car coming.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조심해! 차가 오고 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To kill someone (slang).
meanings.example: They hired a hitman to bump off the witness.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 목격자를 해치우기 위해 살인 청부업자를 고용했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give a performance that is so good that the audience cheers and applauds loudly.
meanings.example: Her singing performance really brought down the house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 노래 공연은 정말 열렬한 박수갈채를 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to express agreement or to emphasize the truth of something (slang).
meanings.example: He's the best player on the team. - Word up!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 팀에서 최고의 선수야. - 정말 맞아!

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay awake waiting for someone to arrive home.
meanings.example: Don't wait up for me; I'll be home very late.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나 기다리지 말고 자. 아주 늦게 들어올 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to tell someone to wait for you so you can catch up with them.
meanings.example: Wait up! I'm coming with you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잠깐 기다려! 나도 같이 가.

meanings.englishDefinition: To feel happy and excited about something that is going to happen.
meanings.example: I'm looking forward to meeting you next week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다음 주에 당신을 만나기를 고대하고 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A meal of fried food, such as eggs, bacon, and sausages (British).
meanings.example: We had a big fry up for breakfast on Sunday morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 일요일 아침 식사로 푸짐하게 구운 음식을 먹었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail completely; to be wasted.
meanings.example: All our hard work went down the tubes when the project was cancelled.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프로젝트가 취소되자 우리의 모든 노력이 수포로 돌아갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be wasted or to fail (British informal).
meanings.example: His career went down the pan after the scandal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스캔들 이후 그의 경력은 수포로 돌아갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spoil or ruin something (British slang).
meanings.example: I've buggered up my computer and it won't start.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 컴퓨터를 완전히 망가뜨려서 켜지지가 않아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually transition to a solid white screen (in film or video).
meanings.example: The movie ends with a slow fade to white.
meanings.exampleKorean: 영화는 서서히 화면이 하얗게 변하며 끝난다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A meal eaten in bed in the morning.
meanings.example: She surprised him with breakfast in bed on his birthday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그의 생일에 침대에서 하는 아침 식사로 그를 깜짝 놀라게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To repair or improve something old.
meanings.example: They are fixing up an old farmhouse.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 낡은 농가를 수리하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrange for someone to have something.
meanings.example: Can you fix me up with a ticket for the game?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 경기 티켓 한 장 마련해줄 수 있니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To discuss an idea or a plan in an informal way.
meanings.example: We've been batting around a few ideas for the new campaign.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 새 캠페인을 위한 몇 가지 아이디어를 이리저리 논의해오고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be much better or more successful than someone else.
meanings.example: She can run circles around any other lawyer in this city.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 이 도시의 어떤 변호사보다도 실력이 훨씬 뛰어나다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk to someone for a long time, often to the point of boring them.
meanings.example: My neighbor cornered me and talked my ear off for an hour.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이웃 사람이 나를 붙잡고 한 시간 동안이나 쉴 새 없이 떠들어댔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop acting in a superior or arrogant way.
meanings.example: It's time you got off your high horse and listened to our advice.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이제 그만 거만하게 굴고 우리 조언을 좀 들어야 할 때야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To send someone to prison.
meanings.example: He was sent down for five years for armed robbery.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 무장 강도죄로 5년 형을 받고 수감되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To expel a student from a university (British).
meanings.example: She was sent down from Oxford for failing her exams.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 시험 낙제로 옥스퍼드 대학에서 제적당했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fill a vehicle's tank with fuel; to eat a lot of food for energy.
meanings.example: We need to fuel up before the long hike.
meanings.exampleKorean: 긴 하이킹을 하기 전에 에너지를 보충해야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To think of an idea, plan, or solution.
meanings.example: How did you come up with such a brilliant idea?
meanings.exampleKorean: 어떻게 그런 훌륭한 아이디어를 생각해냈니?

meanings.englishDefinition: A reduction in or restriction of electric power, especially for a specific area.
meanings.example: The city experienced several brown outs during the heatwave.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭염 동안 그 도시에는 여러 차례 부분 정전이 발생했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase in price, value, or amount.
meanings.example: The price of petrol has gone up again.
meanings.exampleKorean: 휘발유 가격이 또 올랐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be built (of a building).
meanings.example: New apartments are going up all over the city.
meanings.exampleKorean: 도시 곳곳에 새 아파트들이 들어서고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be destroyed by fire or an explosion.
meanings.example: The whole building went up in flames.
meanings.exampleKorean: 건물 전체가 불길에 휩싸였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be full of people or things that are moving around.
meanings.example: The beach was crawling with tourists.
meanings.exampleKorean: 해변은 관광객들로 바글거렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join the edges of a cut together using a needle and thread.
meanings.example: The doctor had to stitch up the wound on my leg.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의사는 내 다리의 상처를 꿰매야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To trick someone so that they are blamed for something they did not do (British informal).
meanings.example: He claimed that the police had stitched him up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 경찰이 자신을 함정에 빠뜨렸다고 주장했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A short break from work or a difficult situation.
meanings.example: We need to take some time out to reconsider our strategy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 전략을 재검토하기 위해 잠시 휴식 시간을 가져야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop functioning because a process has taken too long (computing).
meanings.example: The connection timed out after 30 seconds.
meanings.exampleKorean: 연결이 30초 후에 시간 초과로 종료되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn off a machine or light.
meanings.example: Please switch off the television before you go to bed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자기 전에 텔레비전 좀 꺼주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop thinking about something or listening to someone.
meanings.example: I just switch off when he starts talking about politics.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 정치 이야기를 시작하면 나는 그냥 관심을 끊어버린다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn on a machine or light.
meanings.example: She switched on the light and entered the room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 불을 켜고 방으로 들어갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become more exciting or dangerous (informal).
meanings.example: The election campaign is really starting to hot up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선거 운동이 정말 치열해지기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hit someone or something repeatedly and forcefully (informal).
meanings.example: He started to whale on the drum set with all his might.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 전력을 다해 드럼 세트를 맹렬히 두드리기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To know everything about something.
meanings.example: He's lived here for twenty years and knows the city inside and out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 여기서 20년 동안 살아서 이 도시를 속속들이 알고 있다.
synonyms: know like the back of one's hand

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly stop working (of a machine or engine).
meanings.example: The car conked out in the middle of the motorway.
meanings.exampleKorean: 차가 고속도로 한복판에서 갑자기 멈춰버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall asleep quickly, especially because you are very tired.
meanings.example: I was so tired I conked out as soon as I hit the sofa.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 너무 피곤해서 소파에 눕자마자 곯아떨어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be completely deceived by someone.
meanings.example: I can't believe I got taken in by such a simple trick.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그렇게 단순한 속임수에 내가 속아 넘어갔다니 믿을 수가 없어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be tricked into believing something that is not true.
meanings.example: He told me he was a millionaire, and I fell for it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신이 백만장자라고 말했고, 나는 거기에 속아 넘어갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall in love with someone.
meanings.example: He fell for her the first time he saw her.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그녀를 처음 본 순간 반해버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To promise to stop doing or using something.
meanings.example: I've sworn off junk food for the next month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 다음 한 달 동안 정크 푸드를 끊기로 맹세했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To run away quickly.
meanings.example: The burglar took to his heels as soon as the alarm went off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경보가 울리자마자 도둑은 전속력으로 도망쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To regain consciousness.
meanings.example: When she came to, she found herself in a hospital bed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 정신을 차렸을 때, 그녀는 병원 침대에 누워 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare a meal very quickly.
meanings.example: I can whip up a quick salad for lunch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 점심으로 샐러드 정도는 금방 뚝딱 만들 수 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To encourage strong emotions or reactions in people.
meanings.example: The speaker tried to whip up the crowd's enthusiasm.
meanings.exampleKorean: 연설가는 군중의 열광을 자극하려 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish a period of time during which you have been using a computer system.
meanings.example: Remember to log out of your account when you're finished.
meanings.exampleKorean: 일이 끝나면 계정에서 로그아웃하는 것을 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To progress to a more difficult or advanced stage.
meanings.example: I need to practice more to level up my guitar skills.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기타 실력을 한 단계 높이려면 더 연습해야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have sexual intercourse (British slang).
meanings.example: He's only interested in getting his leg over.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 오직 자는 것(성관계)에만 관심이 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To look at someone in a way that shows a strong sexual interest.
meanings.example: He was undressing every woman in the room with his eyes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 방 안의 모든 여성을 노골적인 시선으로 훑어보고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drive a vehicle as fast as possible; to exert maximum effort.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 가속 페달을 밟다, 전속력으로 가다, 전력을 다하다
meanings.example: The police were chasing him, so he put the hammer down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰이 뒤쫓고 있어서 그는 가속 페달을 밟고 전속력으로 달렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To know someone or something very thoroughly.
meanings.example: I've read this book so many times I know it inside out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 책을 너무 많이 읽어서 내용을 아주 훤히 꿰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become very excited, nervous, or angry (informal).
meanings.example: I really wigged out when I saw the snake in my room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 방에서 뱀을 보고 나는 정말 기겁했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give a new appearance to someone or something.
meanings.example: The studio gave the old house a complete makeover.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스튜디오는 낡은 집을 완전히 새롭게 단장했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To legally transfer ownership of something to someone else.
meanings.example: He made over his property to his eldest son.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 재산을 큰아들에게 양도했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To injure or damage someone or something severely (informal).
meanings.example: That flu really did a number on me; I've been in bed for a week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 독감이 나한테 정말 큰 타격을 줬어. 일주일 내내 누워 있었거든.

meanings.englishDefinition: To happen unexpectedly.
meanings.example: I'm sorry, something's come up and I can't meet you tonight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 미안해, 갑자기 일이 생겨서 오늘 밤에 못 만날 것 같아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be mentioned or discussed in a conversation.
meanings.example: The subject of money didn't come up during the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의 중에 돈 문제는 언급되지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To flee or run away to escape from someone or something.
meanings.example: The villagers took to the hills when they heard the enemy was approaching.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마을 사람들은 적이 다가오고 있다는 소식을 듣고 산으로 도망쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To happen or occur (formal).
meanings.example: It came to pass just as the old man had predicted.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 노인이 예언했던 대로 그 일이 일어났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To politely decline an invitation or request to do something.
meanings.example: She had to beg off the dinner party because she wasn't feeling well.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 몸이 안 좋아서 저녁 파티에 양해를 구하고 빠져야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pass something such as knowledge or a tradition to younger people.
meanings.example: These stories have been handed down from generation to generation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 이야기들은 세대에서 세대로 전해 내려왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give an official decision or a sentence in a court of law.
meanings.example: The jury handed down a verdict of guilty.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배심원단은 유죄 판결을 내렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move around in a confused or aimless way within a limited area.
meanings.example: Crowds of fans were milling around outside the stadium.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경기장 밖에서 수많은 팬이 서성거리고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to encourage someone to do something or to hurry up.
meanings.example: Come on, we're going to be late!
meanings.exampleKorean: 서둘러, 늦겠어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To start working (of a machine or light).
meanings.example: The street lights come on automatically at dusk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가로등은 황혼이 되면 자동으로 켜진다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To allow fresh air into a room or container.
meanings.example: It's a beautiful day; let's air out the house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 날씨가 정말 좋네. 집안 좀 환기시키자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become involved in something that is someone else's business, usually to get an advantage (informal).
meanings.example: Other companies are trying to muscle in on our market share.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다른 회사들이 우리 시장 점유율에 억지로 끼어들려(잠식하려) 하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force one's way into a situation or place.
meanings.example: He tried to muscle in and take over the conversation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 억지로 끼어들어 대화를 주도하려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause someone to fight or compete with someone else.
meanings.example: The tournament pits the best players against each other.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 토너먼트에서는 최고의 선수들이 서로 맞대결하게 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause something to end.
meanings.example: We must put a stop to this senseless violence.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 이 몰상식한 폭력을 중단시켜야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop something from happening or continuing.
meanings.example: The new law will put an end to child labor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 법은 아동 노동을 종식할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To think carefully and deeply about something.
meanings.example: She took some time to reflect on her past mistakes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 자신의 과거 실수들을 되돌아보는 시간을 가졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To influence how people think about someone or something.
meanings.example: His bad behavior reflects on the whole school.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 나쁜 행동은 학교 전체의 평판에 영향을 미친다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To end a telephone conversation by putting the receiver down (British).
meanings.example: He suddenly rang off before I could finish my sentence.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 말을 끝내기도 전에 그가 갑자기 전화를 끊었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attack or treat someone in a violent way (informal).
meanings.example: The gang roughed up the shopkeeper for money.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 패거리들은 돈 때문에 가게 주인을 폭행했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To think deeply or carefully about something (formal).
meanings.example: We should reflect upon the lessons of history.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 역사의 교훈을 심사숙고해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be so excited that you lose control (usually in passive 'be carried away').
meanings.koreanDefinition: 넋을 잃게 하다, 열광하게 하다, (이성을) 잃게 하다
meanings.example: I got a bit carried away and spent too much money.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 좀 흥분해서 돈을 너무 많이 써버렸어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To untie a boat from a dock to start a journey.
meanings.example: The captain gave the order to cast off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선장이 출항 명령을 내렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get rid of someone or something that you no longer want.
meanings.example: She decided to cast off her old habits.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 낡은 습관들을 벗어던지기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make the first row of stitches in knitting.
meanings.example: I need to cast on fifty stitches for this scarf.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 목도리를 만들려면 코를 50개 잡아야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take responsibility for a task or to face a challenge.
meanings.example: We need someone to step up to the plate and lead the project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 누군가 책임지고 나서서 이 프로젝트를 이끌어줄 사람이 필요합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reply rudely to someone in authority.
meanings.example: Don't you dare talk back to your mother!
meanings.exampleKorean: 엄마한테 감히 말대꾸하지 마!

meanings.englishDefinition: To annoy or irritate someone very much.
meanings.example: That tapping sound is really getting on my nerves.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 톡톡거리는 소리가 정말 내 신경을 건드리네 (짜증 나게 하네).

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to express encouragement or to say that something is great (informal).
meanings.example: You did a great job, rock on!
meanings.exampleKorean: 정말 잘했어, 계속 힘내!

meanings.englishDefinition: To move towards the top of something.
meanings.example: We climbed up the steep hill to see the view.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 경치를 보기 위해 가파른 언덕을 기어올랐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move so that you are facing in the opposite direction.
meanings.example: Turn around and let me look at you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몸을 돌려봐, 내가 널 좀 볼 수 있게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a situation or organization successful after it has been failing.
meanings.example: The new manager has managed to turn the company around.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 매니저가 회사를 호전시키는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste time by doing silly or unnecessary things (slang).
meanings.example: Stop farting around and get to work!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그만 빈둥거리고 일 좀 해!

meanings.englishDefinition: To have an influence on something or be a part of it.
meanings.example: Stress can feed into many health problems.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스트레스는 많은 건강 문제의 한 요인이 될 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To search for something by touching with your hands.
meanings.example: I was feeling around in the dark for my keys.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 어둠 속에서 열쇠를 더듬어 찾고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move your arms and legs around in a wild and uncontrolled way.
meanings.example: The drowning man was flailing about in the water.
meanings.exampleKorean: 물에 빠진 남자가 물속에서 허우적거리고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent someone from doing something or to make it more difficult.
meanings.example: I hope I'm not getting in the way.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 방해가 되지 않았으면 좋겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend money carelessly on things that are not necessary.
meanings.example: Buying that expensive car was just throwing money away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 비싼 차를 산 것은 그냥 돈을 낭비한 것이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do what you have promised to do.
meanings.example: He finally made good on his promise to pay me back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 마침내 돈을 갚겠다는 약속을 지켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue to score points in a game even when you have already clearly won.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 큰 점수 차로 이기다, (불필요하게) 득점을 계속하다
meanings.example: The coach was criticized for trying to run up the score against a weaker team.
meanings.exampleKorean: 감독은 약한 팀을 상대로 점수 차를 더 벌리려 했다고 비판받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put something on quickly or forcefully.
meanings.example: He clapped his hat on and ran out of the door.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 모자를 재빨리 쓰고 문 밖으로 달려 나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To coil a rope neatly on the deck (nautical).
meanings.example: The sailors were ordered to cheese down the remaining lines.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선원들은 남은 밧줄들을 사리어 정리하라는 명령을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To transfer digital video to analog film.
meanings.example: The director decided to film out the final edit for the festival.
meanings.exampleKorean: 감독은 영화제를 위해 최종 편집본을 필름으로 옮기기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To beat to windward to avoid being blown onto a lee shore (nautical).
meanings.example: The ship managed to claw off the rocks despite the heavy storm.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭풍우에도 불구하고 그 배는 암초로부터 간신히 멀어질 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To position oneself between an opponent and the basket to get a rebound (basketball).
meanings.example: You need to box out your opponent if you want to get the rebound.
meanings.exampleKorean: 리바운드를 잡고 싶다면 상대를 박스아웃해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To agree to do a job or to join an organization.
meanings.example: The actor has finally signed on for the movie sequel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 배우는 마침내 영화 속편에 출연하기로 계약했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail to finish an activity; to be forgotten or disregarded.
meanings.example: Many small businesses fell by the wayside during the recession.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경기 침체 동안 많은 소기업이 중도에 무너졌다 (탈락했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop criticizing or attacking someone.
meanings.example: I've admitted I was wrong, so please call off the dogs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 잘못했다고 인정했으니, 이제 그만 좀 몰아세워.

meanings.englishDefinition: To watch someone very closely and check everything they do, in an annoying way.
meanings.example: I can't work properly with my boss breathing down my neck all day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상사가 하루 종일 가까이서 감시하니 일을 제대로 할 수가 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To recover quickly from a difficult situation or illness.
meanings.example: The economy is starting to bounce back after the crisis.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경제 위기 이후 경제가 다시 회복되기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To telephone someone.
meanings.example: I'll call you up when I get home.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집에 도착하면 너한테 전화할게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To order someone to join the army.
meanings.example: He was called up to serve in the war.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 전쟁에 참전하기 위해 소집되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cover a larger area by moving away from each other.
meanings.example: The search party spread out across the field.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수색대는 벌판 여기저기로 흩어져 퍼졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To compare the price and quality of the same or a similar object in different shops.
meanings.example: It's always worth shopping around before you buy a new laptop.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 노트북을 사기 전에는 항상 여러 군데 알아보는 것이 좋다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to be part of an activity, agreement, or discussion.
meanings.example: The two countries have entered into a trade agreement.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 국가는 무역 협정을 체결했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare oneself for a difficult task or experience.
meanings.example: We need to gird up our loins for the challenges ahead.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 다가올 도전에 대비해 마음을 단단히 먹어야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act without considering other people's feelings or rights.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~을 함부로 다루다, ~의 감정을 무시하고 제멋대로 하다
meanings.example: The management cannot just ride roughshod over the workers' concerns.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경영진은 노동자들의 우려를 제멋대로 무시해서는 안 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tell someone about some information, often because you think they do not know it.
meanings.example: She pointed out that we were missing a few details.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 우리가 몇 가지 세부 사항을 놓치고 있다고 지적했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To absorb something, such as liquid or information.
meanings.example: She sat on the beach to soak up the sun.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 햇볕을 쬐기(흡수하기) 위해 해변에 앉았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hide or store something, especially money, in order to use it later.
meanings.example: She has squirrelled away a lot of money over the years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 수년간 많은 돈을 몰래 저축해왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop taking part in something, like a race or a school course.
meanings.example: He dropped out of university after only one semester.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 단 한 학기 만에 대학교를 중퇴했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take someone to a place in a car and leave them there.
meanings.example: Can you drop me off at the station?
meanings.exampleKorean: 역에 좀 내려줄 수 있니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall asleep, especially without intending to.
meanings.example: I dropped off during the movie.
meanings.exampleKorean: 영화 보다가 깜빡 잠이 들었어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To test the quality, value, or ability of someone or something.
meanings.example: The crisis really put his leadership to the test.
