meanings.exampleKorean: 그 위기는 그의 리더십을 정말 시험대에 올렸다 (시험해 보았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To score many runs against the opposing team's relief pitchers (baseball).
meanings.example: The home team really roughed up the bullpen in the eighth inning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 홈 팀은 8회에 상대 구원 투수진을 정말 난타했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give something to a person in authority, such as a teacher or an employer.
meanings.example: Please hand in your homework by Friday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 금요일까지 숙제를 제출하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave someone who is badly injured, believing they are about to die.
meanings.example: The victim was beaten and left for dead in an alleyway.
meanings.exampleKorean: 피해자는 폭행을 당한 뒤 골목길에 죽은 줄로 내버려진 채 발견되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hit a ball easily into a hole or goal from a very short distance.
meanings.example: He had a simple tap in to win the game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 가볍게 툭 쳐서 골을 넣어 경기에 이겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start a game of golf.
meanings.example: We're planning to tee off at 8:00 tomorrow morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 내일 아침 8시에 경기를 시작할 계획이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone angry or annoyed (informal).
meanings.example: His arrogant attitude really tees me off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 오만한 태도는 정말 나를 짜증 나게 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To study a subject quickly, especially for an exam (British slang).
meanings.example: I need to mug up on my history before the test tomorrow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내일 시험 전에 역사 공부를 좀 벼락치기로 해야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very easy to notice.
meanings.example: She really sticks out in a crowd with her bright red hair.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 밝은 빨간 머리 때문에 군중 속에서 정말 눈에 띈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue doing something until the end, even if it is difficult.
meanings.example: I know the job is tough, but I've decided to stick it out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 일이 힘들다는 건 알지만, 끝까지 버텨보기로 했어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase the volume or intensity of something.
meanings.example: Crank up the radio! I love this song.
meanings.exampleKorean: 라디오 소리 좀 키워봐! 나 이 노래 정말 좋아해.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to tell a group of people to listen to what you are saying (informal).
meanings.example: Listen up, everyone! I have an important announcement.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모두 잘 들어보세요! 중요한 발표가 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin to like or be interested in someone or something (informal).
meanings.example: The children didn't cotton to the new teacher at first.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 처음에는 새 선생님을 별로 좋아하지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To exchange something for money.
meanings.example: I've decided to cash in my shares.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 주식을 현금화하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take advantage of a situation for your own benefit.
meanings.example: Some companies are trying to cash in on the current craze for organic food.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어떤 회사들은 현재의 유기농 식품 열풍을 이용해 이득을 보려 하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remember or talk about something that happened in the past.
meanings.example: The new building's design harks back to the traditional style of the area.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 새 건물의 디자인은 그 지역의 전통적인 스타일을 떠올리게 한다 (회상시킨다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To make important changes in an organization to improve it.
meanings.example: The new CEO plans to shake up the entire department.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 CEO는 부서 전체를 대대적으로 개편할 계획이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shock or upset someone.
meanings.example: The accident really shook her up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 사고는 그녀에게 정말 큰 충격을 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go away; used as a very rude command (British slang).
meanings.example: Why don't you just bugger off and leave me alone?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그냥 꺼져서 나 좀 내버려 두지 그래?

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave in a vehicle.
meanings.example: He got into his car and drove off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 차에 타서 떠났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be accepted as someone or something else.
meanings.example: With his white hair, he could easily pass for an older man.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 흰 머리 때문에 노인으로 쉽게 보일(통할) 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To masturbate (vulgar slang).
meanings.example: He spent all afternoon wanking off in his room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 오후 내내 방에서 자위하며 시간을 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become inattentive or distracted (slang).
meanings.example: I'm sorry, I spaced out for a moment. What did you say?
meanings.exampleKorean: 미안해, 잠시 멍하게 있었어. 뭐라고 했니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a vigorous effort to achieve something.
meanings.example: If you want the job, just go for it!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 직업을 원한다면, 그냥 도전해봐!

meanings.englishDefinition: To work as well as possible; to use all your energy or ability.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 전력을 다하다, 최고 속도로 돌아가다, 에너지를 풀가동하다
meanings.example: The team was firing on all cylinders during the final match.
meanings.exampleKorean: 팀은 결승전 동안 전력을 다해(풀가동하며) 뛰고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall asleep for a short time, especially while sitting in a chair.
meanings.example: I nodded off for a few minutes during the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의 중에 몇 분 동안 꾸벅꾸벅 졸았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave the shore in a boat by pushing with an oar.
meanings.example: We pushed off from the bank and headed downstream.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 강둑을 밀어 떠나 하류로 향했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to tell someone to go away (informal).
meanings.example: I'm busy, so push off!
meanings.exampleKorean: 바쁘니까 저리 좀 가!

meanings.englishDefinition: To cover written mistakes with white fluid.
meanings.example: I had to white out the wrong address on the envelope.
meanings.exampleKorean: 봉투에 적힌 잘못된 주소를 수정액으로 지워야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To divide something into parts and share it among several people.
meanings.example: The food was carefully portioned out to everyone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 음식은 모든 사람에게 정성껏 나누어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak in a language that you do not know, especially during a religious experience.
meanings.example: The congregation was moved as some members began to speak in tongues.
meanings.exampleKorean: 일부 신도들이 방언을 하기 시작하자 회중들이 감동했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very lucky in a particular situation (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 운이 정말 좋다, 큰 행운을 얻다
meanings.example: I really lucked out with the weather on my wedding day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 결혼식 날 날씨 운이 정말 좋았어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove all your clothes.
meanings.example: He stripped off and jumped into the lake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 옷을 다 벗고 호수로 뛰어들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To complete something that you have started or agreed to do, especially something difficult.
meanings.example: He decided to go through with the surgery despite the risks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 위험에도 불구하고 수술을 끝까지 진행하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bring something to an end, often by destroying it.
meanings.example: The injury put paid to his dreams of becoming a professional athlete.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 부상은 프로 선수가 되겠다는 그의 꿈을 끝장내버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To feel sympathy for someone.
meanings.example: I really feel for her after all she's been through.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 겪은 모든 일을 생각하니 정말 안타깝다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop working and leave a factory or office as a protest.
meanings.example: The workers walked out in support of their colleague.
meanings.exampleKorean: 노동자들은 동료를 지지하기 위해 작업을 중단하고 파업에 돌입했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place suddenly, especially to show that you are angry.
meanings.example: He walked out of the meeting in a rage.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 화가 나서 회의 도중에 나가버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly leave your husband, wife, or family and stop living with them.
meanings.example: He walked out on his wife and three children.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아내와 세 아이를 버리고 떠났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move a vehicle backwards into a parking space.
meanings.example: I find it easier to back in than to drive in forwards.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 앞으로 들어가는 것보다 후진 주차하는 게 더 편하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To think of a good idea or solution, often by chance.
meanings.example: She hit upon a brilliant plan to solve the problem.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 문제를 해결할 아주 훌륭한 계획을 우연히 생각해냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To persuade someone to do something (formal).
meanings.example: I tried to prevail upon him to stay for dinner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그에게 저녁을 먹고 가라고 설득하려 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To choose one person or thing from a group for special attention.
meanings.example: She was singled out for her outstanding performance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 뛰어난 성과 덕분에 특별히 발탁되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To refuse to help someone or to ignore them.
meanings.example: You can't just turn your back on your friends when they're in trouble.
meanings.exampleKorean: 친구들이 어려울 때 그냥 외면해서는 안 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To complete the last part of something.
meanings.example: I'll just finish off this report and then I'm done.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 보고서만 마무리 지으면 끝이야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To kill or defeat someone completely.
meanings.example: The final goal finished off the opposing team.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마지막 골이 상대 팀을 완전히 끝장냈다 (승부를 결정지었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To result in success or benefit.
meanings.example: All her hard work finally paid off when she got the promotion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 승진했을 때 그녀의 모든 노력이 마침내 결실을 맺었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish paying money that you owe for something.
meanings.example: We finally managed to pay off the mortgage.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 마침내 주택 담보 대출금을 다 갚았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return money that you have borrowed.
meanings.example: I'll pay you back as soon as I can.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가능한 한 빨리 돈을 갚을게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To punish someone for something bad they have done to you.
meanings.example: I'll pay him back for what he did to me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 나에게 한 짓을 똑같이 갚아줄 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force someone to obey you.
meanings.example: The government is trying to bring the rebel groups to heel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 반군 단체들을 굴복시키려 노력하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat a particular event or problem as being much more serious than it really is.
meanings.example: The media has blown the whole incident out of proportion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 언론이 그 사건 전체를 지나치게 과장했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to feel happier or to make someone feel happier.
meanings.example: He brightened up when he heard the good news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 좋은 소식을 듣자 기운이 났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become brighter (of weather).
meanings.example: The sky is starting to brighten up after the rain.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비가 그친 뒤 하늘이 개기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually become an adult.
meanings.example: What do you want to be when you grow up?
meanings.exampleKorean: 커서 무엇이 되고 싶니?

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to tell someone to stop behaving in a childish way.
meanings.example: Oh, grow up! Stop being so silly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이구, 철 좀 들어! 그만 좀 바보같이 굴어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To control or limit something that is increasing too much.
meanings.example: The government is trying to rein in public spending.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 공공 지출을 억제하려 애쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consider or remember something when judging a situation.
meanings.example: You must take the weather into account when planning the trip.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여행을 계획할 때는 날씨를 고려해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk to someone.
meanings.example: I need to speak to the manager.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매니저와 이야기해야겠습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To serve as evidence for something.
meanings.example: The success of the project speaks to his great leadership.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프로젝트의 성공은 그의 훌륭한 리더십을 말해준다 (증명한다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a place completely clean and tidy.
meanings.example: We spent all morning cleaning up after the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 파티가 끝난 뒤 아침 내내 청소하며 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a lot of money very quickly (informal).
meanings.example: They really cleaned up on the stock market last year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 작년에 주식 시장에서 정말 큰 이득을 봤다 (싹쓸이했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To become interested in an activity or subject.
meanings.example: How did you get into jazz music?
meanings.exampleKorean: 재즈 음악에 어떻게 관심을 가지게 되었니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To be admitted to a school or university.
meanings.example: She managed to get into medical school.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 의대에 들어가는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a model of something to show how it will look or work.
meanings.example: We mocked up the website design to show the client.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 고객에게 보여주기 위해 웹사이트 디자인 샘플을 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spoil or ruin something (British informal).
meanings.example: I really mucked up that interview.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그 인터뷰를 정말 완전히 망쳐버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bring down an aircraft with a bullet or missile.
meanings.example: The enemy plane was shot down over the ocean.
meanings.exampleKorean: 적군 비행기가 바다 위에서 격추되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reject an idea or proposal immediately.
meanings.example: The manager shot down all my suggestions for the new project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매니저는 새 프로젝트를 위한 내 모든 제안을 단칼에 거절했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start drinking alcohol again after a period of being sober (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 술을 다시 마시기 시작하다, (금주에 실패하여) 다시 손을 대다
meanings.example: He had been sober for a year before he fell off the wagon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 술을 다시 마시기 시작하기 전까지 일 년 동안 금주 중이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be unable to leave a place because of heavy snow.
meanings.example: We were snowed in for three days during the blizzard.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 눈보라 때문에 3일 동안 눈 속에 갇혀 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a foolish and avoidable mistake (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 멍청한 실수를 하다, 뻔한 함정에 빠지다
meanings.example: The politician really stepped on a rake with those comments.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인은 그 발언으로 정말 멍청한 실수를 저질렀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing something with a lot of energy and enthusiasm (British informal).
meanings.example: Let's get stuck into the gardening while the sun is out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 해 있을 때 정원 일을 열심히 시작해봅시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To create new stitches along an edge of knitting.
meanings.example: I need to pick up stitches for the neckline of the sweater.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스웨터 네크라인 부분을 위해 코를 주워야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join with another person or group in order to do something together.
meanings.example: The two companies decided to team up for the new project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 회사는 새 프로젝트를 위해 협력하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be about to experience something, especially something unpleasant.
meanings.koreanDefinition: (좋지 않은 일을) 겪게 될 예정이다, 직면하다
meanings.example: We're in for a long, cold winter this year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 올해 우리는 길고 추운 겨울을 겪게 될 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter a room and surprise someone who is doing something private.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~가 ...하고 있는 도중에 들어가다, (사적인 현장을) 우연히 목격하다
meanings.example: I accidentally walked in on him while he was changing his clothes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그가 옷을 갈아입고 있는 도중에 실수로 들어갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become fewer in number or less dense.
meanings.example: The crowd started to thin out after the concert finished.
meanings.exampleKorean: 콘서트가 끝난 뒤 군중이 드물어지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a way that is intended to attract attention or to get the approval of ordinary people.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 대중의 인기에 영합하다, 인기를 끌려고 애쓰다
meanings.example: The politician was accused of playing to the gallery instead of addressing the real issues.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인은 진짜 문제들을 다루는 대신 대중의 인기에만 영합하려 한다는 비난을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to tell someone to stop being angry or impatient (informal).
meanings.example: Keep your shirt on! We'll be there in a minute.
meanings.exampleKorean: 진정해! 금방 도착할 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find out about something or start to do something.
meanings.example: I'll get onto that task as soon as I finish this one.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 일을 끝내자마자 그 업무를 시작할게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To contact someone, especially to ask for help or give information.
meanings.example: I need to get onto the insurance company about my claim.
meanings.exampleKorean: 청구 건으로 보험사에 연락해봐야겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: A struggle or competition that continues until one side is completely defeated.
meanings.example: The two tribes were engaged in a fight to the death.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 부족은 결사적인 사투를 벌이고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do one's share of a task or responsibility.
meanings.example: I've done my part; now it's your turn to hold up your end.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 내 몫을 다했어. 이제 네가 네 역할을 다할 차례야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin to like someone or something.
meanings.example: I took to her the moment I met her.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그녀를 만난 순간부터 좋아하게 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing something as a habit.
meanings.example: He's taken to walking the dog every morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 매일 아침 개를 산책시키는 것을 습관적으로 하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to attract someone's romantic interest (old-fashioned).
meanings.example: It was obvious that she was setting her cap at the new doctor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 그 새로운 의사의 마음을 사려고 애쓰는 것이 분명했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lose energy, enthusiasm, or the ability to continue doing something.
meanings.example: The campaign started well, but it seems to have run out of steam.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선거 운동은 좋게 시작했지만, 이제 추진력을 잃은 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To meet someone by chance.
meanings.example: I ran into my old teacher at the grocery store.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 식료품점에서 옛 선생님을 우연히 마주쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To experience a problem or difficulty.
meanings.example: We've run into some unexpected problems with the project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프로젝트를 진행하다가 몇 가지 예상치 못한 문제에 부딪혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To imagine yourself in another person's situation, especially a difficult one.
meanings.example: You should try to stand in her shoes before you criticize her.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀를 비판하기 전에 그녀의 처지가 되어 생각해보아야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To surprise or impress someone very much.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 깊은 감명을 주다, 압도하다, 깜짝 놀라게 하다
meanings.example: The ending of the movie absolutely blew me away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 영화의 결말은 나를 정말 깜짝 놀라게 했다 (압도했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase the size or amount of something.
meanings.example: The airline bumped up the price of tickets for the summer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 항공사는 여름철 티켓 가격을 인상했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay an amount of money, especially unwillingly (informal).
meanings.example: I had to fork out £100 for a new tire.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 타이어를 사느라 100파운드를 마지못해 내야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something for the pleasure of it rather than for money.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 즐거움(또는 애정)을 위해 하다, 무보수로 하다
meanings.example: He's a true amateur who plays for love, not for prizes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 상금 때문이 아니라 오직 즐거움을 위해 경기하는 진정한 아마추어다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shout loudly in order to encourage someone who is in a competition.
meanings.example: The crowd cheered on the runners as they neared the finish line.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군중들은 결승선에 다가오는 주자들을 응원했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To perform a story or play.
meanings.example: The children acted out their favorite stories.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 자신들이 가장 좋아하는 이야기를 연극으로 보여주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To express one's negative feelings through bad behavior.
meanings.example: Children often act out when they are feeling neglected.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 소외감을 느낄 때 종종 말썽을 피운다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something by burning it; to use energy through physical activity.
meanings.example: You can burn off a lot of calories by swimming.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수영을 하면 많은 칼로리를 소모할 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be enough for everyone in a group.
meanings.example: Is there enough cake to go around?
meanings.exampleKorean: 모두에게 돌아갈 만큼 케이크가 충분한가요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To go to several different places or people.
meanings.example: There's a nasty flu virus going around.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지독한 독감이 유행하고(돌고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To block something up (British informal).
meanings.example: My nose is all bunged up with this cold.
meanings.exampleKorean: 감기 때문에 코가 꽉 막혔어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrange something on a flat surface.
meanings.example: She laid out her clothes on the bed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 침대 위에 옷을 펼쳐놓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend a large amount of money (informal).
meanings.example: They laid out a fortune on their new house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 새 집을 사는 데 거금을 들였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate something into smaller pieces by pulling.
meanings.example: He pulled the bread apart and shared it with his friends.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 빵을 뜯어서 친구들과 나누어 먹었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize someone's work or ideas very strongly.
meanings.example: The critics pulled his new novel apart.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비평가들은 그의 새 소설을 낱낱이 파헤치며 혹평했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase the efficiency, power, or excitement of something (informal).
meanings.example: The director tried to goose up the scene with more action.
meanings.exampleKorean: 감독은 액션을 더 많이 넣어서 그 장면의 활기를 북돋우려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To work very hard; to defeat someone easily (informal).
meanings.example: I've been beating my brains out trying to solve this puzzle.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 퍼즐을 풀려고 정말 머리를 쥐어짜며 애쓰고 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To agree with someone repeatedly without really meaning it.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 무조건 예예 하다, 영혼 없이 동의하다
meanings.example: Stop yessing me to death and tell me what you really think.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그냥 예예 거리지 말고 네 진짜 생각이 뭔지 말해봐.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop working suddenly (of a machine); to give up or fall asleep because of exhaustion (slang).
meanings.example: My old laptop finally crapped out on me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 낡은 노트북이 마침내 고장 나버렸어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually become smaller or less active.
meanings.example: The rain started to taper off in the afternoon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오후가 되자 비가 점점 줄어들기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To annoy, deceive, or interfere with someone (vulgar slang).
meanings.example: Don't fuck with me right now; I'm in a bad mood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지금 나 건드리지 마. 기분 안 좋으니까.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hide from the public or from people who are looking for you.
meanings.example: The politician went to ground after the scandal broke.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인은 스캔들이 터지자 자취를 감췄다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To escape from someone who is looking for you by going to a secret place.
meanings.example: The suspect has gone to ground and the police can't find him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 용의자가 잠적해서 경찰이 그를 찾지 못하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deal with difficulties in a flexible way without letting them upset you.
meanings.example: In this business, you have to learn to ride with the punches.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 업계에서는 어려움을 유연하게 대처하는 법을 배워야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a difficult situation or relationship instead of trying to deal with it.
meanings.example: You can't just walk away from your responsibilities.
meanings.exampleKorean: 책임을 그냥 외면하고 떠날 수는 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move or travel to a place.
meanings.example: I'm going to the doctor tomorrow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내일 병원에 갈 거예요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ask someone for help or advice.
meanings.example: He always goes to his father for advice.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 아버지에게 조언을 구하러 간다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To block a hole or a pipe.
meanings.example: She used a cloth to stop up the leak.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 누수를 막기 위해 헝겊을 사용했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause trouble or strong emotions.
meanings.example: The news stirred up a lot of controversy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 소식은 많은 논란을 불러일으켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To connect an electrical device to the main electricity supply.
meanings.example: Is the printer plugged in?
meanings.exampleKorean: 프린터 전원이 연결되어 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To spoil something by making a mistake (informal).
meanings.example: I really fouled up my chances of getting the job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그 직업을 얻을 기회를 정말 완전히 망쳐버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause something, especially something violent or difficult, to start suddenly.
meanings.example: The shooting sparked off a wave of protests.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 총격 사건은 일련의 항의 시위를 촉발시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop paying attention and start thinking about something else (informal).
meanings.example: I often zone out during long meetings.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 긴 회의 중에 종종 멍해진다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To joke and laugh a lot, especially in an annoying way (American slang).
meanings.example: They were yucking it up in the corner of the bar.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 바 구석에서 히히덕거리며 즐거워하고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become friendly with someone.
meanings.example: He tried to bud up with the new neighbors.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 새로 이사 온 이웃들과 친해지려 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase the price of something.
meanings.example: Retailers often mark up prices by 50% or more.
meanings.exampleKorean: 소매업자들은 종종 가격을 50% 이상 인상한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To travel through a place without staying there for long.
meanings.example: The river passes through the center of the city.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 강은 도시의 중심부를 통과하여 흐른다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To experience a particular period or process.
meanings.example: He's passing through a difficult phase in his life.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 인생에서 힘든 시기를 겪고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To surprise or shock someone very much (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 깜짝 놀라게 하다, 정신이 하나도 없게 만들다
meanings.example: The news of her resignation really knocked me for a loop.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 사임 소식은 정말 나를 깜짝 놀라게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reach an agreement or solution after a lot of discussion or argument.
meanings.example: The two sides finally managed to hammer out a deal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 양측은 마침내 가까스로 합의를 타결해냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make people forget a mistake or an embarrassing situation by behaving well later.
meanings.example: He'll never live down the time he forgot his lines on stage.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 무대에서 대사를 잊어버렸던 그 일을 결코 만회하지(사람들이 잊게 하지) 못할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To introduce something gradually in stages.
meanings.example: The new tax system will be phased in over the next three years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 조세 제도는 향후 3년에 걸쳐 단계적으로 도입될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop using or producing something gradually in stages.
meanings.example: Subsidies for fossil fuels should be phased out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 화석 연료에 대한 보조금은 단계적으로 폐지되어야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A form of protest where employees follow the official rules of their job exactly, so that work is done more slowly.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 준법 투쟁을 하다, 규정대로만 일하다 (태업의 일종)
meanings.example: The railway workers have decided to work to rule in support of their pay claim.
meanings.exampleKorean: 철도 노동자들은 임금 인상 요구를 지지하기 위해 준법 투쟁을 하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To interfere in something that does not involve you (informal).
meanings.example: I don't need you to stick your oar in; I can handle this myself.
meanings.exampleKorean: 네 참견은 필요 없어. 이건 내가 알아서 처리할 수 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drink all of something at once.
meanings.example: He drank off the glass of water in one gulp.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 물 한 잔을 한 입에 단숨에 들이켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use a new engine carefully until it works perfectly.
meanings.example: You should run in your new car carefully for the first 1,000 miles.
meanings.exampleKorean: 처음 1,000마일 동안은 새 차를 주의 깊게 길들여야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrest someone (slang).
meanings.example: The police ran him in for shoplifting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 상점 절도 혐의로 그를 연행했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to express great anger towards someone (impolite).
meanings.example: I told him to go to hell and slammed the door.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그에게 지옥에나 가버리라고 말하고 문을 쾅 닫았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be destroyed or ruined.
meanings.example: Since the new management took over, the company has gone to hell.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 경영진이 들어선 이후로 회사가 엉망이 되어버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used as a strong curse to someone you hate intensely.
meanings.example: I hope you burn in hell for what you've done!
meanings.exampleKorean: 네가 한 짓에 대해 지옥 불에나 떨어졌으면 좋겠어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat a lot because one is pregnant.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 두 사람 몫을 먹다 (임신 중에 많이 먹다)
meanings.example: Now that you're pregnant, you're literally eating for two.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이제 임신했으니 말 그대로 두 사람 몫을 먹고 있는 셈이네.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a dangerous or unpleasant place quickly (American slang).
meanings.example: Things are getting tense here; let's get out of Dodge.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상황이 긴박해지고 있어. 어서 여기서 빠져나가자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spread information or rumors that may not be true.
meanings.example: Someone has been putting about rumors that the company is closing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 누군가가 회사가 문을 닫는다는 소문을 퍼뜨리고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To express your opinions loudly and clearly (informal).
meanings.example: If you're unhappy with the decision, you should sound off about it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 결정이 마음에 들지 않는다면, 그에 대해 당당하게 목소리를 높여야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop doing business or to end an activity for the day.
meanings.example: We decided to close up shop early because of the blizzard.
meanings.exampleKorean: 눈보라 때문에 일찍 영업을 마치기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize someone simply because they are connected with someone else who is unpopular or guilty.
meanings.example: Even though he was innocent, he was damned by association with the corrupt group.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 무죄였음에도 불구하고 부패한 집단과 연루되었다는 이유로 비난을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lose interest or enthusiasm for something (British informal).
meanings.example: The project was going well, but then the team seemed to go off the boil.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프로젝트가 잘 진행되고 있었지만, 그 후 팀원들의 열정이 식은 것 같았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To distribute something to many people; to produce a signal, sound, or light.
meanings.example: The company sent out a press release about the new product.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 신제품에 대한 보도자료를 배포했다 (발송했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To phone a restaurant and ask them to deliver food to your home or office.
meanings.example: Let's send out for a pizza tonight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 밤에 피자 배달시켜 먹자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To absorb liquid with something such as a cloth or a piece of bread.
meanings.example: He used a piece of bread to sop up the gravy on his plate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 접시에 남은 그레이비 소스를 빵 조각으로 닦아 먹었다 (흡수했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay money, often reluctantly, to help with the cost of something.
meanings.example: The partners were asked to ante up another $10,000 each.
meanings.exampleKorean: 파트너들은 각자 1만 달러씩 더 지불하라는 요청을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give a punishment or reward to someone (formal).
meanings.example: Severe punishments were meted out to the rioters.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭동 가담자들에게 가혹한 처벌이 가해졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage to use something in a way that brings good results.
meanings.example: The company is trying to tap into the younger market.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 젊은 층 시장을 활용하려(공략하려) 애쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A slang term used to say goodbye.
meanings.example: I'm heading home now. Peace out, guys!
meanings.exampleKorean: 나 이제 집에 간다. 얘들아 안녕!

meanings.englishDefinition: To refuse to speak to someone, especially as a punishment.
meanings.example: After he reported the incident, his coworkers sent him to Coventry.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 사건을 보고한 뒤, 동료들은 그를 집단으로 따돌렸다 (말을 걸지 않았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to say that a situation has ended and there is no more hope.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 게임 오버, 다 끝났어, 가망이 없어
meanings.example: If the boss finds out about this, it's game over for us.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사장님이 이 일을 알게 되면 우리에겐 가망이 없어 (다 끝난 거야).

meanings.englishDefinition: To register at a hotel or airport.
meanings.example: We need to check in at the hotel by 3 PM.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 오후 3시까지 호텔 체크인을 해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To contact someone to let them know you are okay or to see how they are.
meanings.example: I'll check in with you later to see how the project is going.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나중에 프로젝트가 어떻게 되어가는지 확인하러 연락할게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To improve your appearance or behavior.
meanings.example: You need to smarten up for your job interview.
meanings.exampleKorean: 취업 면접을 위해 외모를 좀 더 깔끔하게 단장해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return to a place after being somewhere else.
meanings.example: What time did you get back last night?
meanings.exampleKorean: 어젯밤에 몇 시에 들어왔니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To receive something again that you had before.
meanings.example: I never got back the money I lent him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그에게 빌려준 돈을 결코 돌려받지 못했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish something in a satisfactory or suitable way.
meanings.example: We rounded off the evening with a delicious dessert.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 맛있는 디저트로 저녁 시간을 좋게 마무리했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shine very strongly and make it feel very hot (of the sun).
meanings.example: The sun was beating down on the desert sand.
meanings.exampleKorean: 태양이 사막의 모래 위로 강렬하게 내리쬐고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To persuade someone to reduce the price of something.
meanings.example: I managed to beat the seller down to $50.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 판매자를 설득해서 가격을 50달러까지 깎았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish something, often in a way that is even better or worse than what happened before.
meanings.example: It rained all day, and to cap it all off, we missed the bus.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하루 종일 비가 왔고, 게다가 (설상가상으로) 우리는 버스까지 놓쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find or collect something with difficulty.
meanings.example: He managed to scrounge up enough money for a taxi fare.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 택시비를 낼 만큼의 돈을 어렵사리 긁어모았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be fully understood or realized.
meanings.example: The news of his death took a long time to sink in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 사망 소식은 실감이 나기까지 오랜 시간이 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail to keep a promise or agreement.
meanings.example: She promised to help us, and she won't go back on her word.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 우리를 돕기로 약속했고, 결코 약속을 어기지 않을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To break something by pushing or hitting it inwards.
meanings.example: The front of the car was staved in by the impact.
meanings.exampleKorean: 차 앞부분이 충격으로 인해 찌그러졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A spiritual concept of working with a higher power to shape one's life or the world.
meanings.example: She believes that through prayer, she is co-creating her future with God.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 기도를 통해 신과 함께 자신의 미래를 창조하고 있다고 믿는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have sex with many different people.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 여러 사람과 성관계를 갖다, 난잡하게 놀다
meanings.example: He has a reputation for sleeping around.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 여러 사람과 자고 다닌다는 평판이 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To transport someone from a planet's surface to a spacecraft using a teleporter (science fiction).
meanings.example: Beam me up, Scotty!
meanings.exampleKorean: 스카티, 나를 전송해줘! (스타트렉의 유명한 대사)

meanings.englishDefinition: To relax after a period of work or excitement.
meanings.example: I like to wind down with a good book before bed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 자기 전에 책을 읽으며 긴장을 푸는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually bring something to an end.
meanings.example: The government is starting to wind down the relief program.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 구제 프로그램을 단계적으로 종료하기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To listen to everything someone has to say before making a judgment.
meanings.example: Please hear me out before you get angry.
meanings.exampleKorean: 화내기 전에 내 말을 끝까지 좀 들어봐.

meanings.englishDefinition: To look at or examine something to get information about it (informal).
meanings.example: We went to the beach to scope out the best spot for our picnic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 소풍 가기 가장 좋은 자리를 알아보기 위해 해변에 갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give someone or something the same name as someone or something else.
meanings.example: He was named after his grandfather.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 할아버지의 이름을 따서 이름이 지어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To think about something carefully for a long time.
meanings.example: I need a few days to mull over your offer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신의 제안을 심사숙고할 며칠간의 시간이 필요합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fight or argue until a situation is resolved.
meanings.example: I'm prepared to go to the mat for what I believe is right.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 옳다고 믿는 것을 위해 끝까지 싸울 준비가 되어 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a video or recording play faster so that you can skip a part.
meanings.example: You can fast forward through the commercials.
meanings.exampleKorean: 광고 부분은 빨리 감기 해서 넘겨도 돼.

meanings.englishDefinition: To skip ahead in time in a story or discussion.
meanings.example: If we fast forward to the end of the year, we can see the results.
meanings.exampleKorean: 올해 말로 시간을 앞당겨 보면(건너뛰면), 결과를 알 수 있을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a room or place clean and neat.
meanings.example: Can you tidy up your room before guests arrive?
meanings.exampleKorean: 손님들이 오기 전에 네 방 좀 정리해줄래?

meanings.englishDefinition: To enjoy a particular activity or interest.
meanings.example: I don't really go in for team sports.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 팀 운동에는 별로 취미가 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take part in a competition or exam.
meanings.example: Are you going in for the photography contest?
meanings.exampleKorean: 사진 공모전에 참가하실 건가요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin to understand something (informal).
meanings.example: It took me a while to cotton on to what was happening.
meanings.exampleKorean: 무슨 일이 일어나고 있는지 눈치채는 데 시간이 좀 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To praise someone so little that it seems like you actually dislike them.
meanings.example: The critic's review damned the play with faint praise.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 비평가의 리뷰는 미온적인 칭찬을 통해 그 연극을 교묘히 깎아내렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To replace a word in a television or radio program with a short, high sound so that people cannot hear it.
meanings.example: They had to bleep out all the swear words in the interview.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 인터뷰에 나온 모든 욕설을 삐 소리로 가려야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become nervous or frightened (British informal).
meanings.example: He's getting the wind up about the upcoming exam.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 다가오는 시험 때문에 겁을 먹고(불안해하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get information or a person out of a place where they are hidden, with difficulty.
meanings.example: The investigators tried to winkle out the truth from the witness.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수사관들은 목격자로부터 진실을 캐내려 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ask for something that is impossible to give or achieve.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 불가능한 것을 요구하다, 무리한 부탁을 하다
meanings.example: I'm only asking for a small raise, not asking for the moon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그저 약간의 임금 인상을 요구하는 것이지, 불가능한 것을 요구하는 게 아니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return something to its original place.
meanings.example: Please put the books back on the shelf when you're finished.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다 읽은 후에는 책을 선반 제자리에 돌려놓으세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To change the time or date of an event to a later one.
meanings.example: The meeting has been put back to next Tuesday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의가 다음 주 화요일로 미뤄졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean the inside of something by licking it.
meanings.example: The dog licked out the bowl.
meanings.exampleKorean: 강아지가 그릇을 핥아 먹었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To steal something from a place.
meanings.example: Someone thieved the jewelry out of the safe.
meanings.exampleKorean: 누군가 금고에서 보석을 훔쳐 갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To exchange your gambling chips for money at the end of a game; to sell an investment for profit.
meanings.example: He decided to cash out his stock options while the price was high.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 주가가 높을 때 스톡옵션을 현금화하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To surprise or impress someone very much.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 깜짝 놀라게 하다, 깊은 감명을 주다
meanings.example: I was completely bowled over by their hospitality.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그들의 환대에 완전히 감동했다 (깜짝 놀랐다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To work hard for something, such as a promotion or a favor (American slang).
meanings.example: He's been bucking for a promotion all year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 일 년 내내 승진을 하려고 애써왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize someone or something strongly.
meanings.example: The mayor hit out at his critics during the speech.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시장은 연설 중에 자신을 비판하는 사람들에게 맹공격을 퍼부었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To order food from a restaurant to be delivered to your home.
meanings.example: Should we cook dinner or order out?
meanings.exampleKorean: 저녁 요리할까, 아니면 배달 시킬까?

meanings.englishDefinition: To start living a quiet life in one place, especially when you get married.
meanings.example: They decided to settle down and start a family.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 정착해서 가정을 꾸리기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become calm or quiet.
meanings.example: Settle down, class! Let's begin the lesson.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자 모두 조용히 하세요(진정하세요)! 수업 시작합시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Annoyed or angry (American slang).
meanings.example: She was really teed off when he arrived late again.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 또 늦게 오자 그녀는 정말 화가 났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return to a place.
meanings.example: When will you come back from your trip?
meanings.exampleKorean: 여행에서 언제 돌아오시나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To become successful or popular again after a period of failure.
meanings.example: Vinyl records are starting to come back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 레코드판이 다시 유행하기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To aim a weapon carefully at a target; to focus one's attention precisely on something.
meanings.example: The hunter drew a bead on the deer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사냥꾼은 사슴을 정확히 겨냥했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce something such as heat, light, or a smell.
meanings.example: That flower gives off a strange smell at night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 꽃은 밤에 이상한 냄새를 내뿜는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reach a particular total in distance, speed, or time (informal).
meanings.example: The car had clocked up over 100,000 miles.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 차는 주행 거리 10만 마일 이상을 기록했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To read something aloud so that other people can hear it.
meanings.example: He read out the names of the winners.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 우승자들의 명단을 낭독했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be found successful or correct after a period of testing.
meanings.example: We need to wait and see if the new strategy proves out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 전략이 효과가 있는지 좀 더 지켜봐야 한다 (입증되어야 한다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To sing while someone else is singing or while music is playing.
meanings.example: Everyone in the audience started to sing along with the band.
meanings.exampleKorean: 관객들 모두가 밴드의 노래에 맞춰 따라 부르기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go to sleep in a place that is not your usual bed; to start to work or live in a new place.
meanings.example: The hikers decided to bed down in a cave for the night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하이킹객들은 하룻밤을 보내기 위해 동굴에서 잠자리에 들기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To relax and enjoy yourself without worrying about what other people will think.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 느긋하게 쉬다, (격식을 차리지 않고) 마음껏 즐기다
meanings.example: It's time to let your hair down and have some fun!
meanings.exampleKorean: 이제 긴장을 풀고 마음껏 즐길 시간이야!

meanings.englishDefinition: To be destroyed by fire; to be consumed by heat.
meanings.example: The spacecraft burned up as it entered the atmosphere.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우주선이 대기권에 진입하면서 다 타버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone very angry (informal).
meanings.example: The way he treats his staff really burns me up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 직원들을 대하는 방식은 나를 정말 화나게 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take aim at something or someone.
meanings.example: The critic took a bead on the director's new film.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 비평가는 감독의 새 영화를 정확히 겨냥(비판)했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To complete the process of making maple sugar.
meanings.example: They spent the whole day sugaring off in the cabin.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 오두막에서 하루 종일 단풍 설탕을 만들며 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bargain with someone to lower a price (offensive and anti-Semitic).
meanings.example: He tried to jew the merchant down on the price (Warning: This is an offensive term).
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 상인에게 가격을 깎으려고 끈질기게 매달렸다. (주의: 매우 무례한 표현임)

meanings.englishDefinition: To telephone someone (British).
meanings.example: I'll ring you up later this evening.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 저녁에 나중에 전화할게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To record an amount of money on a cash register.
meanings.example: The shop assistant rang up the items one by one.
meanings.exampleKorean: 점원은 물건들을 하나하나 계산했다 (금전 등록기에 찍었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To say something in a very clear and honest way, even if it is unpleasant.
meanings.example: He makes no bones about his dislike for his boss.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 사장을 싫어한다는 것을 아주 솔직하게 말한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To escape from someone who is holding you.
meanings.example: The prisoner managed to break away from the guards.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 죄수는 간신히 경비원들을 뿌리치고 달아났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a political party or organization and form a new one.
meanings.example: The group broke away from the main party to form their own movement.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 집단은 자신들만의 운동을 조직하기 위해 본당에서 탈퇴했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To like someone very much, especially when you have just met them (informal).
meanings.example: My dog took a shine to her right away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 강아지가 그녀를 보자마자 아주 좋아했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deal with your own problems or organize your life.
meanings.example: He took a year off to sort himself out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 문제를 정리하고 추스르기 위해 1년을 쉬었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a silly and noisy way (informal).
meanings.example: Stop horsing around and get ready for school!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그만 장난치고 학교 갈 준비해!

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase very quickly in amount or level.
meanings.example: Prices have shot up in the last month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지난 한 달 동안 가격이 치솟았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To grow very quickly.
meanings.example: My nephew has really shot up since I last saw him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 조카는 지난번에 봤을 때보다 정말 쑥쑥 자랐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop being available.
meanings.example: As the funds dried up, the project had to be abandoned.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자금이 바닥나자 프로젝트를 포기해야만 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop talking because you have forgotten what to say.
meanings.example: The actor dried up in the middle of his speech.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배우는 연설 중간에 말이 막혀버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: One should think carefully before taking action.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 돌다리도 두들겨 보고 건너라, 실행 전에 신중히 생각하라
meanings.example: It's a big investment, so you should look before you leap.
meanings.exampleKorean: 큰 투자니까, 돌다리도 두들겨 보고 건너야(신중히 생각해야) 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase the speed of an engine; to make something more active or exciting.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 활기를 띠게 하다, 고조시키다, (엔진) 속도를 올리다
meanings.example: The team is revving up for the start of the season.
meanings.exampleKorean: 팀은 시즌 시작을 앞두고 분위기를 고조시키고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing something with enthusiasm; to start eating with great enjoyment.
meanings.example: The food looks delicious, let's dive in!
meanings.exampleKorean: 음식이 맛있어 보이네, 어서 먹자!

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat a lot of food quickly (American slang).
meanings.example: The kids were chomping to chow down on some pizza.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 피자를 게걸스럽게 먹고 싶어 안달이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To surround an area with tape to stop people from entering it.
meanings.example: The police taped off the crime scene.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 범죄 현장에 테이프를 둘러 출입을 차단했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To live together as a couple without being married (old-fashioned).
meanings.example: His grandmother was shocked to hear that they were living in sin.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 할머니는 그들이 결혼도 안 하고 동거한다는 소식에 충격을 받으셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be left in a place after a ship has been destroyed.
meanings.example: They were cast away on a desert island for months.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 몇 달 동안 무인도에 난파되어 고립되어 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To adjust or align something so that it is accurate or correct.
meanings.example: We need to true up the wheels on the bicycle.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 자전거 바퀴를 정확하게 조정해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A thorough examination or cleaning; an attack (informal).
meanings.example: The car needs a thorough going over before the long trip.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장거리 여행 전에 차를 철저히 점검(조사)해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To call someone on an intercom (informal).
meanings.example: Can you buzz me up when you get to the building?
meanings.exampleKorean: 건물에 도착하면 벨을 눌러(나를 호출해) 줄래?

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat a meal (American informal).
meanings.example: I'm starving; let's put the feedbag on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배고파 죽겠어. 어서 밥 먹자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fill a vehicle or container to its maximum volume capacity before reaching the weight limit.
meanings.example: The truck cubed out because the packages were so large.
meanings.exampleKorean: 택배 상자들이 너무 커서 트럭의 공간이 금방 가득 찼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To happen in a particular way.
meanings.example: The demonstration passed off peacefully.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시위는 평화롭게 진행되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pretend that someone or something is something else in order to deceive.
meanings.example: He tried to pass himself off as a doctor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 의사 행세를 하려 (의사인 양 속이려) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become successful or powerful again after being completely destroyed.
meanings.example: The city has truly risen from the ashes after the war.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 도시는 전쟁 후 진정으로 잿더미를 딛고 다시 일어섰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn the pages of a book or magazine quickly without reading everything.
meanings.example: I was leafing through a magazine while waiting for the doctor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 의사 선생님을 기다리는 동안 잡지를 대충 훑어보고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Done according to a very simple and predictable plan, without any imagination.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 정해진 규칙대로만 하는, 독창성 없는, 판에 박힌
meanings.example: The movie's plot was a bit paint by numbers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 영화의 줄거리는 다소 판에 박힌 듯 뻔했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be in a risky or dangerous situation.
meanings.example: You're skating on thin ice by coming in late every day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매일 지각하는 건 정말 살얼음판을 걷는 것처럼 위험한 짓이야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To engage in a struggle or argument with someone where you will only end up looking bad yourself.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 수준 낮은 사람과 다투다 (본인만 손해라는 뉘앙스)
meanings.example: I realized that arguing with him was like wrestling with a pig.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그와 말다툼하는 건 수준 낮은 싸움일 뿐이라는 걸 깨달았다 (나만 손해라는 걸 깨달았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To send someone to prison.
meanings.example: He was sent up for ten years for fraud.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 사기죄로 10년 형을 선고받고 투옥되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mock or make fun of someone or something by imitating them.
meanings.example: The show really sends up the latest fashion trends.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 프로는 최신 패션 유행을 정말 우스꽝스럽게 풍자한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tell someone to leave.
meanings.example: He was busy, so he sent the visitors away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 바빠서 방문객들을 돌려보냈다 (가라고 했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To write to an organization and ask them to send you something.
meanings.example: I've sent away for a brochure about the holiday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 휴가 관련 브로셔를 우편으로 신청했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return to a place or a situation.
meanings.example: I need to go back and get my keys.
meanings.exampleKorean: 되돌아가서 열쇠를 가져와야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have existed for a particular period of time.
meanings.example: Our family's history goes back to the 18th century.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 가족의 역사는 18세기까지 거슬러 올라간다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To adjust a musical instrument so that it plays the correct notes.
meanings.example: The orchestra is tuning up before the concert.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오케스트라가 콘서트 전에 조율을 하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To adjust an engine so that it works perfectly.
meanings.example: I need to get my car tuned up before the long trip.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장거리 여행 전에 차를 정비해야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay all the money you owe, especially when you do not want to.
meanings.example: He finally paid up after several reminders.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 여러 차례 독촉을 받은 뒤에야 마침내 돈을 다 갚았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become covered with clouds (of the sky); to become misty (of glass).
meanings.example: The sky started to cloud up in the afternoon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오후가 되자 하늘에 구름이 끼기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To block an opening such as a door or window with bricks.
meanings.example: The old fireplace had been bricked up years ago.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오래된 벽난로는 수년 전에 벽돌로 막혀 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean out a place where animals live.
meanings.example: I have to muck out the stables every morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 매일 아침 마구간을 청소해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean the inside of something thoroughly.
meanings.example: I need to clean out my kitchen cupboards.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주방 찬장을 깨끗이 비우고 치워야겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use all of someone's money (informal).
meanings.example: The holiday really cleaned us out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이번 휴가 때문에 우리는 아주 거덜이 났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To live with someone as a sexual partner without being married (informal).
meanings.example: They've been shacking up together for years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 수년째 동거하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave in a vehicle; to force someone or something to leave.
meanings.example: The high prices are driving away customers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비싼 가격이 고객들을 쫓아내고(떠나게 하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fasten all the buttons on a piece of clothing.
meanings.example: Button up your coat, it's cold outside.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코트 단추 채워, 밖이 추워.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish a task successfully.
meanings.example: We should be able to button up the deal by Friday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 금요일까지는 계약을 마무리 지을 수 있을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to return to a situation that existed in the past.
meanings.example: We can't just put the clock back to the way things were before the internet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 인터넷 이전의 상황으로 시간을 되돌릴 수는 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fasten something; to wrap something.
meanings.example: Do up your jacket, it's cold.
meanings.exampleKorean: 재킷 좀 잠가, 추워.

meanings.englishDefinition: To repair and decorate a building or car.
meanings.example: They are doing up an old farmhouse.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 낡은 농가를 수리하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fasten something using a zipper.
meanings.example: He zipped up his fly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 바지 지퍼를 올렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep quiet (informal).
meanings.example: Zip up your mouth!
meanings.exampleKorean: 입 닥쳐! (입 좀 다물어!)

meanings.englishDefinition: To use the bathroom.
meanings.example: I need to go to the toilet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 화장실 좀 다녀올게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To punish someone as severely as possible.
meanings.example: The judge threw the book at him because it was his third offense.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 벌써 세 번째 범죄였기 때문에 판사는 그에게 엄벌을 내렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To win all of someone's money; to cheat someone of all their money.
meanings.koreanDefinition: (돈을) 싹쓸이하다, 거덜 내다, 호되게 당하게 하다
meanings.example: They really took him to the cleaners in that poker game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 그 포커 게임에서 그의 돈을 완전히 싹쓸이해버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To imprison someone for a very long time, often for life.
meanings.example: For a crime like that, they should lock him up and throw away the key.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그런 죄라면 그를 가두고 평생 나오지 못하게 해야 한다 (열쇠를 던져버려야 한다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To take responsibility for a failure and resign from your job.
meanings.example: The CEO was forced to fall on his sword after the scandal.
meanings.exampleKorean: CEO는 스캔들 이후 책임을 지고 사임할 수밖에 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay an amount of money, especially when you do not want to (British informal).
meanings.example: I had to stump up £50 for the repairs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수리비로 50파운드를 마지못해 내야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue for longer than expected.
meanings.example: The meeting ran on for hours.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의가 몇 시간 동안이나 계속되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use a particular type of power or fuel.
meanings.example: Some cars can run on electricity.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어떤 차들은 전기로 돌아간다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To read, look at, or explain something quickly; to practice a play or piece of music.
meanings.example: Let's run through the presentation one more time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 발표 내용을 한 번 더 연습해봅시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join two things together; to start a romantic relationship with someone.
meanings.example: The railway cars were coupled up and ready to go.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기차 차량들이 연결되어 출발 준비가 끝났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly try to hit someone; to suddenly speak to someone in an angry way.
meanings.example: He lashed out at his critics in a series of tweets.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 일련의 트윗을 통해 비판자들에게 맹공격을 퍼부었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hit something at an angle and then bounce off it.
meanings.example: The bullet glanced off the helmet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 총알이 헬멧을 스치고 튕겨 나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to tell a horse to move faster; to encourage someone to work harder.
meanings.example: The manager tried to gee up the team for the final quarter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매니저는 마지막 쿼터를 위해 팀원들을 독려하려 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn your body to face a different direction.
meanings.example: I rolled over and went back to sleep.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 몸을 뒤척여(뒤집어) 다시 잠들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To extend a financial agreement or a debt.
meanings.example: They decided to roll over the loan for another year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 대출을 1년 더 연장하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk too much or to talk about something you should keep secret.
meanings.example: He's always shooting his mouth off about how much money he earns.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 자기가 돈을 얼마나 많이 버는지 떠벌리고 다닌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drink an alcoholic drink quickly; to reject an offer (informal).
meanings.example: He knocked back a double whiskey.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 위스키 더블을 단숨에 들이켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start a conversation or a relationship; to start playing music.
meanings.example: He struck up a conversation with the woman sitting next to him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 옆자리에 앉은 여자와 대화를 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To look away from someone or something.
meanings.example: She glanced away when their eyes met.
meanings.exampleKorean: 눈이 마주치자 그녀는 시선을 돌렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To change the time on a clock to a later time.
meanings.example: Don't forget to put your clocks forward by one hour tonight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 밤 시계를 한 시간 앞당기는 거 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be extremely successful in a particular place or among a particular group of people.
meanings.example: The young singer took the music world by storm.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 젊은 가수는 음악계를 단숨에 사로잡았다 (휩쓸었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To draw a line through a word to show that it is deleted.
meanings.example: Please strike through any items that are no longer needed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 더 이상 필요 없는 항목에는 취소선을 그어주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail completely; to be wasted (informal).
meanings.example: All our hard work just went down the toilet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리의 모든 노력이 그냥 수포로 돌아가버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fold the sleeves of a shirt upwards; to arrive in a vehicle (informal).
meanings.example: He rolled up his sleeves and got to work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 소매를 걷어 올리고 일에 착수했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To feel sympathy or pity for someone.
meanings.example: I really feel sorry for him after he lost his job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 직장을 잃은 그가 정말 안타깝다 (불쌍하다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To pass (of time); to follow or use a particular rule or name.
meanings.example: As time goes by, you'll feel better.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시간이 지나면 기분이 나아질 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter a building; to be understood or remembered.
meanings.example: I explained it twice, but it just didn't go in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 번이나 설명했지만 전혀 이해가 되지 않은 모양이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find or get something that you want very much.
meanings.example: I've been trying to get my hands on a copy of that book for years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 수년 동안 그 책을 손에 넣으려고 애써왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be murdered and have one's body disposed of in a body of water (slang).
meanings.example: If you talk to the police, you'll be sleeping with the fishes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰에 불면 너는 물속에 처박히게 될 거야 (살해당할 거야).

meanings.englishDefinition: To lift something heavy with a jack; to increase a price or amount suddenly.
meanings.example: The oil companies have jacked up the price of gas again.
meanings.exampleKorean: 석유 회사들이 가스 가격을 또 대폭 인상했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop doing a job or an activity (British informal).
meanings.example: He decided to jack in his job and go traveling.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 직장을 때려치우고 여행을 떠나기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To disagree with someone or something.
meanings.example: I must take issue with some of the points you made.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신이 제기한 몇몇 점들에 대해 이의를 제기하지 않을 수 없습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone feel nervous or frightened (British informal).
meanings.example: The threat of layoffs really put the wind up the staff.
meanings.exampleKorean: 해고 위협은 직원들을 정말 불안하게 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrive at a place; to start doing something.
meanings.example: What time did you get to the office?
meanings.exampleKorean: 사무실에 몇 시에 도착했니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To annoy someone.
meanings.example: Don't let his comments get to you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 말이 네 신경을 건드리게(너를 괴롭히게) 두지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To travel to many different places; to be spread (of news).
meanings.example: She gets about a lot in her new job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 새 직장에서 여기저기 많이 돌아다닌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reach something; to suggest or imply something.
meanings.example: What exactly are you trying to get at?
meanings.exampleKorean: 정확히 무엇을 말하려는(암시하려는) 건가요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To perform or enjoy rock music with great energy (informal).
meanings.example: The band really rocked out during the final song.
meanings.exampleKorean: 밴드는 마지막 곡을 하는 동안 정말 신나게 연주했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing something with confidence and at a good speed after a slow start.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 제 실력을 발휘하다, 본궤도에 오르다, 익숙해지다
meanings.example: He's finally getting into his stride after a few weeks in the new job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 새 직장에서 몇 주를 보낸 뒤 마침내 제 실력을 발휘하기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A type of property investment where you buy a house or flat to rent it out.
meanings.example: Buy-to-let properties have become very popular recently.
meanings.exampleKorean: 임대용 주택 투자가 최근 매우 인기를 끌고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be nervous or frightened (British informal).
meanings.example: They really have the wind up about the possible changes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 일어날 수 있는 변화들에 대해 정말 불안해하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something seem less good or less important.
meanings.example: The minor errors don't take away from the overall quality of the book.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사소한 오류들이 책의 전반적인 품질을 떨어뜨리지는 않는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go to an airport or station with someone to say goodbye.
meanings.example: My parents came to the station to see me off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 부모님께서 나를 배웅하러 역에 오셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To defeat a person or team; to force someone to leave a place.
meanings.example: The team managed to see off their rivals in the final minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 팀은 마지막 순간에 라이벌들을 물리치는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To raise something to a higher position; to make someone feel happier.
meanings.example: His kind words really lifted up my spirits.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 친절한 말은 정말 내 기운을 북돋아 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something by rubbing it with a cloth or your hand.
meanings.example: She wiped away her tears and started to smile.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 눈물을 닦아내고 웃기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce something; to give birth to (formal).
meanings.example: The trees bring forth fruit every autumn.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나무들은 매년 가을에 열매를 맺는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate a part from something larger; to end a relationship or discussion suddenly.
meanings.example: She broke off a piece of chocolate and gave it to me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 초콜릿 한 조각을 떼어 나에게 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very busy doing many different things.
meanings.example: I've been running around all day trying to get everything ready.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 것을 준비하느라 하루 종일 바쁘게 돌아다녔어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend a lot of time with a particular group of people.
meanings.example: He's been running around with a bad crowd lately.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 요즘 나쁜 무리와 어울려 다니고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place with someone to have a secret relationship; to steal something.
meanings.example: The accountant ran off with all the company's money.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회계사는 회사의 모든 돈을 훔쳐 달아났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something, especially something that other people might not approve of.
meanings.example: The kids are very quiet; I wonder what they're getting up to.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들이 아주 조용하네. 무슨 장난을 치고 있는지(저지르고 있는지) 궁금해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hear news or rumors through informal communication with other people.
meanings.example: I heard on the grapevine that you're getting married.
meanings.exampleKorean: 네가 결혼한다는 소문을 풍문으로 들었어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To relax and do what other people are doing or what the situation requires.
meanings.example: I don't have a plan for the trip; let's just go with the flow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여행 계획은 없어. 그냥 상황(흐름)에 맡기자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wait until the next day before making a decision about something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~에 대해 하룻밤 자며 생각해보다, 결정을 미루다
meanings.example: That's a big decision. Why don't you sleep on it?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그건 큰 결정이야. 하룻밤 자면서 좀 더 생각해보는 게 어때?

meanings.englishDefinition: To have sex with someone.
meanings.example: She found out that he had been sleeping with his secretary.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그가 비서와 잠자리를 같이해왔다는 사실을 알게 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall asleep, especially when you did not intend to.
meanings.example: I dozed off in front of the TV.
meanings.exampleKorean: TV 앞에서 깜빡 졸았어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep someone or something in a small space.
meanings.example: The children were cooped up inside all day because of the rain.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비 때문에 아이들이 하루 종일 집 안에 갇혀 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tidy a place; to solve a problem or misunderstanding; to become fine (of weather).
meanings.example: We need to clear up this mess before Mom gets home.
meanings.exampleKorean: 엄마 오시기 전에 이 난장판을 좀 치워야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase the amount or speed of something significantly.
meanings.example: The company is ramping up production to meet the demand.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 수요를 맞추기 위해 생산량을 늘리고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To betray someone who trusts you.
meanings.example: I can't believe he stabbed me in the back after all I did for him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 그를 위해 해준 게 얼만데, 그가 나를 배신하다니 믿을 수 없어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very busy taking care of someone else's needs.
meanings.example: I spend all my time running around after the kids.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 아이들 챙겨주느라 시간을 다 보낸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fasten something to a wall with tacks; to put a saddle and bridle on a horse.
meanings.example: He tacked up the poster on his bedroom wall.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 침실 벽에 포스터를 압정으로 붙였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become less attractive or healthy because of lack of care; to deteriorate.
meanings.example: After he retired, he really started to go to seed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 은퇴한 후에 정말 외모가 망가지기 시작했다 (자기 관리를 안 했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To return something to the person who gave it to you.
meanings.example: Can you give me back my pen when you're finished?
meanings.exampleKorean: 다 쓰면 내 펜 좀 돌려줄래?

meanings.englishDefinition: To help people in your local community.
meanings.example: The athlete wants to give back to the neighborhood where he grew up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 운동선수는 자신이 자란 지역 사회에 환원하기를 원한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be useful.
meanings.example: Keep those boxes; they might come in handy when we move.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 상자들 버리지 마. 이사할 때 유용하게 쓰일 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something from a surface by rubbing it with a sharp tool.
meanings.example: I need to scrape off the old paint before I repaint the door.
meanings.exampleKorean: 문을 다시 칠하기 전에 오래된 페인트를 긁어내야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something more attractive, exciting, or interesting.
meanings.example: We can jazz up this room with some colorful cushions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 색깔 있는 쿠션 몇 개로 이 방의 분위기를 더 살릴 수 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To break into pieces; to fail or stop working effectively.
meanings.example: My old car is starting to fall apart.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 낡은 차가 여기저기 고장 나기(부서지기) 시작했어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lose control of your emotions because of a difficult situation.
meanings.example: She fell apart after her cat died.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 고양이가 죽은 뒤 정신적으로 무너져 내렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay a large sum of money for something.
meanings.example: The insurance company refused to pay out for the damage.
meanings.exampleKorean: 보험사는 그 손상에 대해 보험금 지급을 거부했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To annoy or interfere with someone; to handle something in a way that might damage it.
meanings.example: Don't mess with me today; I'm not in a good mood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 나 건드리지 마. 기분 안 좋으니까.

meanings.englishDefinition: To end a relationship; to divide into smaller groups.
meanings.example: They split up after being together for ten years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 10년 동안 함께한 뒤 결별했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To elect someone to a position by voting.
meanings.example: The party was voted in with a huge majority.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정당은 압도적인 다수표로 당선되었다 (집권하게 되었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To force someone out of a position by voting for someone else.
meanings.example: The government was voted out at the last election.
meanings.exampleKorean: 현 정부는 지난 선거에서 투표에 의해 물러나게 되었다 (낙선했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To find or collect something with difficulty.
meanings.example: See if you can scare up some extra chairs for the guests.
meanings.exampleKorean: 손님들을 위해 여분의 의자를 좀 찾아낼 수 있는지 봐봐.

meanings.englishDefinition: To think carefully about the possible results of something.
meanings.example: I need some time to think the proposal through.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 제안에 대해 충분히 생각할 시간이 필요합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sacrifice someone in order to save yourself.
meanings.example: They threw their manager to the wolves to avoid responsibility for the failure.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 실패에 대한 책임을 피하기 위해 매니저를 희생양으로 삼았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use something so much that it is destroyed; to work someone so hard that they become exhausted.
meanings.example: He's running himself into the ground with all this overtime work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 이 모든 초과 근무로 자기 자신을 혹사하고 있다 (몸을 망치고 있다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To meet someone or find something by chance.
meanings.example: I ran across an old friend at the library yesterday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어제 도서관에서 옛 친구를 우연히 마주쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To chase someone or something.
meanings.example: The dog was running after a cat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 강아지가 고양이를 뒤쫓고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enjoy life by spending a lot of money and having fun (often 'live it up').
meanings.example: They were living it up in Las Vegas.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 라스베이거스에서 신나게 인생을 즐기고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To investigate and find out scandalous information about someone.
meanings.example: The reporter was trying to dig up dirt on the politician.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기자는 그 정치인의 치부를 캐내려 애쓰고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reach a place; to reach a decision or agreement after discussion.
meanings.example: We finally arrived at a decision after hours of debate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 몇 시간의 토론 끝에 마침내 결론에 도달했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To persuade someone to accept something that has no value or that you do not want.
meanings.example: He tried to palm off his old car as a collectors' item.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 낡은 차를 수집가용 아이템인 양 속여서 팔아먹으려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce the level of something; to make a government or leader lose power.
meanings.example: The scandal eventually brought down the government.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 스캔들은 결국 정부를 무너뜨렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move something from a higher to a lower position; to write down something that is spoken.
meanings.example: The reporter took down every word of the interview.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기자는 인터뷰의 모든 말을 받아적었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get rid of something bad; to escape from someone who is following you.
meanings.example: I can't seem to shake off this cold.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 감기가 좀처럼 떨어지질 않네 (떨쳐낼 수가 없네).

meanings.englishDefinition: Lasting longer than is necessary or expected.
meanings.example: The legal battle was long and drawn out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 법정 싸움은 길고 지루하게 이어졌다 (질질 끌었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To show that you are completely frustrated or have given up all hope.
meanings.example: I just threw up my hands in frustration when the computer crashed again.
meanings.exampleKorean: 컴퓨터가 다시 고장 나자 나는 좌절감에 그냥 자포자기해버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin to apply or be used (especially of a law).
meanings.example: The new law will come into effect next month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 법률은 다음 달부터 시행될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take over or destroy something completely.
meanings.example: Small shops are being swallowed up by large supermarkets.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작은 가게들이 대형 슈퍼마켓들에 의해 집어삼켜지고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become less shy and more confident and social.
meanings.example: She has really come out of her shell since she started university.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 대학에 들어간 이후로 정말 수줍음을 벗고 활발해졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend time and effort trying to improve something.
meanings.example: You have to work at a marriage to make it successful.
meanings.exampleKorean: 결혼 생활을 성공적으로 유지하려면 공을 들여야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste time and energy fighting imaginary enemies or impossible problems.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 가상의 적과 싸우다, 부질없는 짓을 하다
meanings.example: He's just tilting at windmills; there is no conspiracy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그냥 부질없는 짓을 하고 있을 뿐이야. 음모 같은 건 없어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave badly (of a child); to not work properly (of a machine).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 말썽을 피우다, (기계 등이) 말을 안 듣다
meanings.example: My back has been playing up again lately.
meanings.exampleKorean: 요즘 내 등(허리)이 다시 말썽이야 (아파).

meanings.englishDefinition: To pretend to agree with someone or something for a short time.
meanings.example: I decided to play along with his joke just to see what would happen.
meanings.exampleKorean: 무슨 일이 일어나는지 보려고 그냥 그의 농담에 맞장구쳐주기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly say something, especially when you have been quiet.
meanings.example: Suddenly, a small voice piped up from the back of the room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 갑자기 방 뒷쪽에서 작은 목소리가 들려왔다 (말하고 나섰다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to make something seem less important than it really is.
meanings.example: The government is trying to play down the severity of the crisis.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 위기의 심각성을 별거 아닌 것처럼 다루려(경시하려) 애쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start a revolution or to fight against a government or leader.
meanings.example: The people rose up against the dictator.
meanings.exampleKorean: 민중들은 독재자에 맞서 봉기했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put different things into the same group, often unfairly.
meanings.example: You can't just lump all young people together as if they were all the same.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 젊은이들이 다 똑같은 것처럼 그냥 하나로 묶어서 생각해서는 안 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To create or produce something quickly and unexpectedly, as if by magic.
meanings.example: I'll see if I can magic up some sandwiches for lunch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 점심으로 먹을 샌드위치를 뚝딱 만들어낼 수 있는지 볼게.

meanings.englishDefinition: A euphemism for dying, especially used in the Salvation Army.
meanings.example: Their father was promoted to Glory last week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들의 아버지는 지난주에 소천하셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare for an activity or event.
meanings.example: The company is gearing up for the launch of its new product.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 신제품 출시를 준비하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To not include someone or something.
meanings.example: You left out the most important part of the story.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너는 그 이야기에서 가장 중요한 부분을 빠뜨렸어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take care of yourself without help from anyone else.
meanings.example: When her parents died, she had to fend for herself.
meanings.exampleKorean: 부모님이 돌아가셨을 때, 그녀는 스스로의 힘으로 살아가야 했다.
