meanings.englishDefinition: To make a decision about something too quickly, before you have all the facts.
meanings.example: Wait until you've heard the whole story before you jump to conclusions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 성급하게 결론을 내리기 전에 이야기 전체를 다 들을 때까지 기다려.

meanings.englishDefinition: To appear suddenly and unexpectedly.
meanings.example: New problems seem to pop up every day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매일 새로운 문제들이 불쑥 나타나는 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sleep in a place that is not a proper bed (British informal).
meanings.example: We had to doss down on the floor for the night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 하룻밤 동안 바닥에서 대충 잠자리를 잡아야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sit or lie in a position with your arms and legs close to your body.
meanings.example: She curled up on the sofa with a good book.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 소파에서 몸을 웅크리고 앉아 좋은 책을 읽었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To show that someone is doing something wrong or making a mistake.
meanings.example: The examiner tried to catch me out with some tricky questions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시험관은 까다로운 질문 몇 개로 나를 곤란하게 만들려(실수를 유도하려) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take strong action to stop or limit something.
meanings.example: The police are clamping down on drunk driving.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰이 음주 운전을 엄중히 단속하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep money so that you can buy something in the future.
meanings.example: I'm saving up for a new laptop.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 노트북을 사려고 돈을 모으고 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be forgotten or ignored; to fail.
meanings.example: During the crisis, all their safety rules went by the board.
meanings.exampleKorean: 위기 동안 그들의 모든 안전 수칙은 무시되었다 (수포로 돌아갔다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To recognize the difference between two people or things.
meanings.example: The twins look so similar that I can't tell them apart.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 쌍둥이는 너무 비슷하게 생겨서 구별할 수가 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste time, money, or effort on unimportant things.
meanings.example: He frittered away his inheritance on expensive cars.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 유산을 비싼 차들을 사는 데 헛되이 써버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To describe something in a general way without giving any details.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 포괄적으로 다루다, 세세한 점을 무시하고 일반화하다
meanings.example: The politician was accused of painting the issue with too broad a brush.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인은 문제를 너무 포괄적으로만 다룬다(일반화한다)는 비난을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sell all of something; to stop being loyal to your principles in order to get money.
meanings.example: The concert was sold out within minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 콘서트는 몇 분 만에 매진되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become lower in amount or quality.
meanings.example: Membership has fallen off since the price increase.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가격 인상 이후 회원 수가 줄어들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak in a rude or complaining way (informal).
meanings.example: He's always mouthing off about the government.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 정부에 대해 함부로 떠든다 (불평한다).

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to tell someone to mind their own business or to stop being annoying (Australian/NZ informal).
meanings.example: You'd better pull your head in before you get into trouble.
meanings.exampleKorean: 문제 생기기 전에 자중하는 게(참견 안 하는 게) 좋을 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat a particular type of food only; to have a particular amount of money to buy food and other necessary things.
meanings.example: He lives on nothing but pizza and beer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 피자와 맥주만 먹고 산다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue to exist or be remembered.
meanings.example: Her memory will live on in our hearts.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀에 대한 기억은 우리 마음속에 영원히 남을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To include something extra with something that you are selling, without increasing the price.
meanings.example: If you buy the car, I'll throw in a set of new tires.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 차를 사시면 새 타이어 한 세트를 덤으로 드릴게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To steal something and take it away.
meanings.example: Thieves made off with over $10,000 in cash.
meanings.exampleKorean: 도둑들이 1만 달러 이상의 현금을 훔쳐 달아났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attach a microphone to someone so that their voice can be recorded or broadcast.
meanings.example: The guest was miked up and ready for the interview.
meanings.exampleKorean: 게스트는 마이크를 착용하고 인터뷰 준비를 마쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone feel nervous or frightened (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 소름 끼치게 하다, 기분 나쁘게 하다
meanings.example: That old house really creeps me out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 낡은 집은 정말 나를 소름 끼치게 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To understand a story or situation by taking different pieces of information and putting them together.
meanings.example: Investigators are trying to piece together what happened on the night of the crime.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수사관들은 범행 당일 밤에 무슨 일이 있었는지 상황을 종합하려 애쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop a business from operating permanently.
meanings.example: Many small shops have been forced to close down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 많은 작은 가게들이 문을 닫을 수밖에 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shut a shop or building at the end of the day; to heal (of a wound).
meanings.example: He's closing up the shop for the night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 오늘 영업을 마치고 가게 문을 닫고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To block an entrance or a road to stop people from using it.
meanings.example: The police closed off the street after the accident.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 사고 후에 그 거리를 차단했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cry very hard and for a long time.
meanings.example: She cried her eyes out when she heard the news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그 소식을 듣고 엉엉 울었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be logical or obvious.
meanings.example: It stands to reason that if you don't study, you'll fail the exam.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공부를 안 하면 시험에 떨어지는 건 이치에 맞는 (당연한) 일이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To show that something is true or that someone is telling the truth.
meanings.example: The facts bear out her story.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사실들이 그녀의 이야기가 옳음을 뒷받침해준다 (입증해준다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To show that you do not think something is serious by laughing about it.
meanings.example: She laughed off the rumors about her resignation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 자신의 사임에 관한 소문을 웃어넘겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter a room or interrupt a conversation rudely and suddenly.
meanings.example: Sorry to barge in on your meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의 중에 불쑥 끼어들어 죄송합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a silly way; to waste time doing unimportant things.
meanings.example: Stop messing around and get to work!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그만 빈둥거리고 일 좀 해!

meanings.englishDefinition: To end in failure or disaster.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 실패로 끝나다, 불행을 당하다, 사고가 나다
meanings.example: The business plan came to grief because of poor management.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 사업 계획은 부실한 경영 때문에 실패로 끝났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decorate or dress someone or something in a showy way.
meanings.example: He tricked out his car with a new paint job and fancy wheels.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 새 도색과 멋진 휠로 자신의 차를 화려하게 꾸몄다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To show that you do not support something by leaving a place or by no longer using a service.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 떠남으로써 반대(불만)를 표시하다, (상품 등의 이용을) 중단하다
meanings.example: If the prices keep rising, customers will vote with their feet and shop elsewhere.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가격이 계속 오르면 고객들은 이용을 중단하고 다른 곳에서 쇼핑함으로써 불만을 표시할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very good at dealing with someone or something.
meanings.example: She has a way with children; they always listen to her.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 아이들을 잘 다룬다 (아이들에게 능숙하다). 아이들은 항상 그녀의 말을 잘 듣는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak to someone as if they are less important or less intelligent than you.
meanings.example: He always talks down to his younger colleagues.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 어린 동료들을 깔보며 말한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To persuade someone to lower a price.
meanings.example: I managed to talk him down to $200.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그를 설득해서 가격을 200달러까지 깎았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move to a position further back in a group or race.
meanings.example: The runner started to drop back as the race entered its final stage.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경주가 마지막 단계에 접어들자 그 주자는 뒤처지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To land (of an aircraft or spacecraft).
meanings.example: The plane touched down right on schedule.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비행기는 예정대로 정확히 착륙했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To laugh uncontrollably (British informal).
meanings.example: We all fell about when he told us the story.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 우리에게 그 이야기를 해주었을 때 우리는 모두 자지러지게 웃었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To end a connection to a computer system; to sign a document when leaving a place.
meanings.example: Don't forget to sign out before you close the browser.
meanings.exampleKorean: 브라우저를 닫기 전에 로그아웃하는 것을 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To explain the reason for something; to form a particular amount or part of something.
meanings.example: Computers account for 5% of the company's sales.
meanings.exampleKorean: 컴퓨터가 회사 매출의 5%를 차지한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To include something as an extra part.
meanings.example: We've added on a new wing to the hospital.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 병원에 새로운 병동을 추가로 증축했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To experience a difficulty or problem.
meanings.example: We ran up against some unexpected opposition to the plan.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 그 계획에 대한 몇 가지 예상치 못한 반대에 부딪혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attend a meeting or class without taking an active part in it.
meanings.example: I'd like to sit in on your lecture tomorrow, if that's okay.
meanings.exampleKorean: 괜찮으시다면 내일 당신의 강의를 청강하고 싶습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something less enjoyable or less exciting.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 찬물을 끼얹다, 흥을 깨다, 기를 꺾다
meanings.example: The rain really put a damper on our picnic plans.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비가 와서 우리의 소풍 계획에 정말 찬물을 끼얹었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shoot people or things one by one from a distance.
meanings.example: The sniper picked off the enemy guards one by one.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저격수는 적군 경비병들을 하나하나 차례로 제거했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To express your ideas and opinions clearly so that people understand them.
meanings.example: He's very good at putting his ideas across.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 생각을 전달하는 데 아주 능숙하다 (잘 이해시킨다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To delay making a decision about something; to keep something secret.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 결정을 미루다, (정보 등을) 묵혀두다, 비밀로 하다
meanings.example: The committee has been sitting on the report for months.
meanings.exampleKorean: 위원회는 그 보고서를 몇 달째 묵혀두고 (결정을 미루고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To break something down by kicking it.
meanings.example: The police had to kick down the door to get into the house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 집 안으로 들어가기 위해 문을 발로 차서 부숴야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come into contact with someone or something, often by chance.
meanings.example: In this job, you rub up against all kinds of people.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 직업을 갖게 되면 온갖 종류의 사람들과 부딪히게(접하게) 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take action based on something.
meanings.example: The police acted upon a tip-off from a member of the public.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 시민의 제보에 따라 조치를 취했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To resist or oppose someone or something.
meanings.example: It takes courage to stand up against injustice.
meanings.exampleKorean: 불의에 당당히 맞서는 데는 용기가 필요하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To express one's ideas or personality clearly and successfully to others.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 자기 생각을 잘 전달하다, 좋은 인상을 주다
meanings.example: He's very intelligent, but he doesn't put himself across very well in interviews.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 매우 영리하지만, 면접에서 자기 자신을 잘 표현하는 편은 아니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consider someone or something to be a particular type of person or thing.
meanings.example: I put him down as a bit of a bore at first, but he's actually very interesting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 처음에는 그를 좀 따분한 사람이라고 생각했지만, 사실 그는 매우 흥미로운 사람이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To believe that something is caused by a particular thing.
meanings.example: The team's success can be put down to hard work and dedication.
meanings.exampleKorean: 팀의 성공은 고된 노력과 헌신 덕분이라고 할 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To appear or develop suddenly and quickly.
meanings.example: Fast-food restaurants are springing up all over the city.
meanings.exampleKorean: 패스트푸드점들이 도시 전역에 갑자기 생겨나고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To annoy someone, especially by being always near them or by constantly asking questions.
meanings.example: The kids were getting in my hair all morning while I was trying to work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 일하려 하는 동안 아침 내내 아이들이 나를 귀찮게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to tell someone to either do what they are talking about or stop talking about it (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 실천하거나 아니면 입 다물어라, 행동으로 보여라
meanings.example: You keep saying you can do better, so it's time to put up or shut up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 더 잘할 수 있다고 계속 말하는데, 이제 행동으로 보여주든지 아니면 입을 다물든지 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To save money for a particular purpose; to ignore or forget something for a while.
meanings.example: Try to put aside some money every month for your vacation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 휴가를 위해 매달 조금씩 돈을 저축하도록 하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop thinking about a problem or a disagreement.
meanings.example: They decided to put aside their differences and work together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 서로의 차이점을 도외시하고(잠시 잊고) 협력하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start using a plan or idea.
meanings.example: It's time to put our new plan into practice.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이제 우리의 새로운 계획을 실행에 옮길 시간이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lose weight; to make an organization smaller.
meanings.example: He has slimmed down a lot since he started exercising.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 운동을 시작한 뒤로 살이 많이 빠졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail to use an opportunity to enjoy or get an advantage from something.
meanings.example: Don't miss out on the chance to win a free holiday!
meanings.exampleKorean: 무료 휴가에 당첨될 기회를 놓치지 마세요!

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place quickly, especially in order to escape.
meanings.example: The burglars made off in a stolen car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 강도들은 훔친 차를 타고 급히 달아났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid becoming involved in something, especially something unpleasant.
meanings.example: Try to keep out of trouble.
meanings.exampleKorean: 말썽에 휘말리지 않도록 해라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk in a way that is indirect and confusing, often repeating the same points without reaching a conclusion.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 요점 없이 뱅뱅 돌아 말하다, 제자리걸음을 하듯 말하다
meanings.example: We've been talking in circles for an hour and still haven't solved anything.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 한 시간 동안 요점 없이 뱅뱅 돌아가며 말하기만 했고 여전히 아무것도 해결하지 못했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To smooth hair back from the face, especially using oil or gel.
meanings.example: He slicked back his hair with gel for the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 파티를 위해 젤로 머리를 매끈하게 뒤로 넘겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be overwhelmed by an enormous amount of information or work (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 감당하기 힘든 양의 정보(업무)를 처리하다, 정보의 홍수에 빠지다
meanings.example: The first week of training felt like drinking from a firehose.
meanings.exampleKorean: 훈련 첫 주는 감당하기 힘든 양의 정보가 쏟아지는 것 같았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To push your tongue out of your mouth, especially as a rude or funny gesture.
meanings.example: The little boy stuck his tongue out at his sister.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어린 소년은 누나에게 혀를 내밀며 메롱을 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone very angry (American informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 몹시 화나게 하다, 짜증 나게 하다
meanings.example: It really torques me off when people cut in line.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사람들이 새치기할 때 정말 화가 머리끝까지 난다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail suddenly and spectacularly; to stop working (of a jet engine).
meanings.example: The startup flamed out after just one year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 스타트업은 단 1년 만에 갑자기 실패하며 문을 닫았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To settle in a place and start a life there.
meanings.example: After traveling for years, she finally decided to put down roots in this town.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수년간의 여행 끝에 그녀는 마침내 이 마을에 정착하기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become completely dry; to stop drinking alcohol.
meanings.example: Leave the wood to dry out before you burn it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나무를 태우기 전에 바짝 마르도록 두세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be consistent or connected with something else.
meanings.example: This new evidence ties in with what we already knew.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 새로운 증거는 우리가 이미 알고 있던 사실과 일치한다 (연관된다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To report someone's bad behavior to an authority figure.
meanings.example: If you don't stop, I'm going to tell on you!
meanings.exampleKorean: 너 그만 안 하면 일러바칠 거야!

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend time sitting and doing nothing useful.
meanings.example: We spent the whole afternoon just sitting around and talking.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 오후 내내 그냥 빈둥거리며 이야기만 나누었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing something earlier than others in order to get an advantage.
meanings.example: We need to get a jump on the competition by launching the app next week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 다음 주에 앱을 출시함으로써 경쟁사들보다 선수를 쳐야 한다 (앞서 나가야 한다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep quiet and refuse to provide information (American informal).
meanings.example: The suspect dummied up as soon as the lawyer arrived.
meanings.exampleKorean: 변호사가 도착하자마자 용의자는 입을 다물어버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something extremely dangerous and risk being killed.
meanings.example: Overtaking on that bend is dicing with death.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 커브 길에서 추월하는 건 죽음을 무릅쓰는 위험한 짓이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To admire and respect someone.
meanings.example: I've always looked up to my older brother.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 항상 우리 형을 존경해왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be not yet certain.
meanings.example: It remains to be seen whether the new plan will work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 계획이 효과가 있을지는 두고 봐야 할 일이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something warm or hot; to become more intense or exciting.
meanings.example: The race for the presidency is starting to heat up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대권 경쟁이 서서히 달아오르기(고조되기) 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To annoy someone very much (British informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 몹시 짜증 나게 하다, 신경을 건드리다
meanings.example: That whistling sound is really getting on my wick.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 휘파람 소리가 정말 짜증 나게 하네 (내 신경을 건드리네).

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish something difficult; to pass an exam; to survive a difficult time.
meanings.example: I don't know how I'll get through the week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 이번 주를 어떻게 버텨낼지(이겨낼지) 모르겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To say something in a loud voice; to give a public expression of thanks.
meanings.example: I want to give a shout out to all my fans.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저의 모든 팬분들께 감사의 인사를 전하고 싶습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend a lot of time and effort reading or dealing with something long or boring.
meanings.koreanDefinition: (지루하고 많은 일을) 간신히 해내다, 헤쳐 나가다
meanings.example: I spent all morning wading through a pile of reports.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 오전 내내 산더미 같은 보고서들을 간신히 헤치며(검토하며) 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To interrupt a discussion or situation in a forceful way.
meanings.example: The police waded in to separate the fighting fans.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰이 싸우는 팬들을 떼어놓기 위해 거침없이 끼어들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To swallow food or drink quickly in large amounts.
meanings.example: He gulped down a glass of water before continuing his speech.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 연설을 계속하기 전에 물 한 잔을 벌컥벌컥 마셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Having beliefs that will never change; complete (usually 'dyed-in-the-wool').
meanings.example: He's a dyed-in-the-wool conservative.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 철저한 보수주의자이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in an especially friendly way towards someone in order to get an advantage from them (informal).
meanings.example: He's always kissing up to the boss to get a promotion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 승진하려고 항상 상사에게 아부한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept something that is not exactly what you want but is the best that is available.
meanings.example: I wanted a blue car, but I had to settle for a silver one.
meanings.exampleKorean: 파란색 차를 원했지만, (재고가 없어서) 아쉬운 대로 은색 차에 만족해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone feel very disgusted (informal).
meanings.example: He really grosses me out with his bad habits.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 나쁜 습관들은 정말 나를 역겹게 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become familiar with a new way of life, place, or job.
meanings.example: How are you settling in at your new school?
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 학교에는 적응 잘하고 있니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop people from entering an area by putting a rope or tape around it.
meanings.example: The area was cordoned off while the police searched for clues.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰이 단서를 찾는 동안 그 구역은 차단되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fight or argue until a definite result is reached.
meanings.example: The two finalists will battle it out for the title tonight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 결승 진출자가 오늘 밤 타이틀을 거머쥐기 위해 끝까지 승부를 겨룰 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To work extremely hard (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 뼈 빠지게 일하다, 매우 열심히 일하다
meanings.example: I worked my tail off to get this project done on time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 프로젝트를 제시간에 끝내려고 뼈 빠지게 일했다.
synonyms: work one's fingers to the bone

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very careful not to offend or upset someone.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 살얼음판을 걷다, 몹시 조심스럽게 행동하다 (눈치를 보다)
meanings.example: We've all been walking on eggshells since he lost his job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 직장을 잃은 뒤로 우리 모두는 몹시 그의 눈치를 보며 조심스럽게 행동하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to stay brave or confident in a difficult or frightening situation.
meanings.example: He says the company is safe, but I think he's just whistling in the dark.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 회사가 안전하다고 말하지만, 내 생각엔 그냥 애써 태연한 척하는 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be in a very dangerous or uncertain situation.
meanings.example: His career is hanging by a thread after the scandal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스캔들 이후 그의 경력은 풍전등화와 같이 위태롭다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to talk more about yourself and your feelings; to start a business or a shop.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 마음을 열다, (가게 등이) 문을 열다, 시작하다
meanings.example: It took a while for him to open up about his problems.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 자신의 문제에 대해 마음을 여는 데 시간이 좀 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move by jumping from one place to another.
meanings.example: The children were jumping around with excitement.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 신이 나서 이리저리 뛰고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shout something loudly; to sing loudly.
meanings.example: Sing out if you need any help!
meanings.exampleKorean: 도움이 필요하면 크게 소리쳐!

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something look more attractive, often in a cheap or gaudy way (British informal).
meanings.example: They tried to tart up the old house with some cheap decorations.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 값싼 장식물로 그 낡은 집을 화려하게 꾸미려 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To achieve something by using trickery or clever methods that are not obvious.
meanings.example: The company's profits seem too good to be true; I think they're doing it with mirrors.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사의 수익이 너무 좋아서 믿기지 않네. 교묘한 수법(분식 회계 등)을 쓰는 것 같아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drink a lot of alcohol; to make someone drunk.
meanings.example: They liquored up before going to the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 파티에 가기 전에 술을 잔뜩 마셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept an opportunity or offer with great enthusiasm.
meanings.example: I'd jump at the chance to work in New York.
meanings.exampleKorean: 뉴욕에서 일할 기회가 생긴다면 나는 기꺼이 받아들일 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To interrupt a conversation; to start doing something very quickly.
meanings.example: Feel free to jump in if you have any questions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 질문이 있으면 언제든지 대화에 끼어드세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be thinking of someone or something as a candidate for a role or as a plan.
meanings.example: What kind of budget did you have in mind for the project?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 프로젝트에 대해 어느 정도의 예산을 염두에 두고 계셨나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To become friendly with someone, especially someone who can help you.
meanings.example: He's trying to get in with the right people to help his career.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 경력에 도움이 될 만한 유력한 사람들과 친해지려 애쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start eating something with enthusiasm.
meanings.example: The kids really tucked into the pizza.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 피자를 정말 신나게 먹었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lose your balance and fall to the ground.
meanings.example: The child tripped and fell over.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이가 발을 헛디뎌 넘어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize someone as soon as they do something wrong.
meanings.example: The boss jumped on me as soon as I made a small mistake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 작은 실수를 하자마자 상사가 나를 호되게 비난했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To count the time remaining before a specific event occurs.
meanings.example: We are counting down the days until the holiday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 휴가까지 남은 날짜를 거꾸로 세며 기다리고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To obtain something, especially something that is hard to get; to visit someone.
meanings.example: Good jobs are hard to come by these days.
meanings.exampleKorean: 요즘은 좋은 일자리를 구하기가 어렵다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decide that someone or something is not useful or important; to cancel a debt.
meanings.example: They wrote off the debt as a bad investment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 그 부채를 잘못된 투자로 보고 탕감해주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To watch someone secretly in order to find out information about them.
meanings.example: He was caught spying on his neighbors.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 이웃들을 몰래 지켜보다가 들켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To trust or depend on someone or something.
meanings.example: You can always rely on him to help you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너는 그가 도와줄 거라는 걸 항상 믿어도 돼 (그에게 의지해도 돼).

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize someone in an unpleasant way (British informal).
meanings.example: Stop slagging off your friends behind their backs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 친구들 뒤에서 헐뜯는 짓 좀 그만해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move to a lower position by jumping; to scold someone severely.
meanings.example: He jumped down from the wall.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 담벼락에서 뛰어내렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To jump from a high place; to start an activity.
meanings.example: The project will jump off next Monday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 프로젝트는 다음 주 월요일에 본격적으로 시작될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have something remaining after part of it has been used.
meanings.example: There were some sandwiches left over from the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 파티에서 남은 샌드위치가 좀 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To kill someone or oneself by shooting them in the head.
meanings.example: He threatened to blow his brains out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 머리를 쏘아 자살하겠다고 위협했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A call for the next batter to take their position (baseball); a signal to get ready to start something.
meanings.example: Okay everyone, batter up! The meeting is starting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자 여러분, 시작합시다! 회의 시작해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage to collect enough of something, especially money, with difficulty.
meanings.example: I managed to scrape together enough money for a new bike.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 새 자전거를 살 돈을 간신히 긁어모았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be responsible for something; to be punished for something bad you have done.
meanings.example: He will have to answer for his crimes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 범죄에 대해 책임을 져야(벌을 받아야) 할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To expect something to happen and include it in your plans.
meanings.example: We didn't reckon on the weather being so bad.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 날씨가 그렇게 나쁠 줄은 계산에 넣지 못했다 (기대하지 못했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To intend to do something.
meanings.example: We're planning on going to Italy this summer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 이번 여름에 이탈리아에 갈 계획이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take something into consideration; to deal with a powerful person or organization.
meanings.example: He is still a force to be reckoned with in politics.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 여전히 정치계에서 무시할 수 없는 힘을 가진 존재이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To resist someone or something; to remain in good condition despite rough treatment.
meanings.example: You must learn to stand up to bullies.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너는 괴롭히는 사람들에게 맞서는 법을 배워야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To defend or support a particular idea or person.
meanings.example: Thank you for standing up for me during the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의 중에 저를 위해 나서주셔서(옹호해주셔서) 감사합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate an area with a wall to prevent access.
meanings.example: The back part of the garden was walled off from the rest.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정원의 뒷부분은 나머지 부분과 벽으로 차단되어 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move to a lower position; to fall as rain or snow; to decrease in price.
meanings.example: The rain really started to come down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비가 정말 억수같이 쏟아지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To represent something; to support a particular set of ideas or values.
meanings.example: What does the 'VIP' stand for?
meanings.exampleKorean: 'VIP'는 무엇의 약자(상징)인가요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To allow or tolerate something (often negative).
meanings.example: I won't stand for such behavior in my house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 집에서 그런 행동을 하는 건 참을 수 없어 (허용하지 않겠어).

meanings.englishDefinition: To twist something tightly to remove liquid.
meanings.example: Wring out the cloth before you wipe the table.
meanings.exampleKorean: 탁자를 닦기 전에 행주를 꽉 짜라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To involve someone in a situation or activity when they do not want to be involved.
meanings.example: I didn't want to get sucked into their argument.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그들의 논쟁에 휘말리고 싶지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start a new activity or talk about a new subject; to leave the past behind and start a new life.
meanings.example: Let's move on to the next item on the agenda.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의제의 다음 항목으로 넘어갑시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be experienced and not easily fooled (informal).
meanings.example: Don't try to lie to me; I've been to the rodeo before.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나한테 거짓말하려고 하지 마. 나도 산전수전 다 겪은 사람이야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly understand or realize something (British informal).
meanings.example: He finally tumbled to the fact that they were playing a trick on him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 마침내 그들이 자신을 속이고 있다는 사실을 깨달았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To complete or extend something by adding pieces.
meanings.example: He had to piece out his small income by doing odd jobs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 잡일을 해서 적은 수입을 보충해야만 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to dislike someone for no clear reason (British informal).
meanings.example: I don't know why, but I've really taken against the new neighbor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 왜 그런지는 모르겠지만, 새로 이사 온 이웃이 정말 싫어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate something into its different parts; to criticize someone or something severely.
meanings.example: He took the whole engine apart to find the problem.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 문제를 찾기 위해 엔진 전체를 분해했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a very difficult or unpleasant experience.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 지옥 같은 고통을 겪다, 몹시 힘든 시간을 보내다
meanings.example: She went through hell during the divorce.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 이혼 과정에서 지옥 같은 고통을 겪었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To look at something behind you; to think about something that happened in the past.
meanings.example: When I look back on my school days, I realize how lucky I was.
meanings.exampleKorean: 학창 시절을 회상해보면 내가 얼마나 운이 좋았는지 깨닫게 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent something or someone from being included or considered.
meanings.example: The software is designed to screen out spam emails.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 소프트웨어는 스팸 메일들을 걸러내도록 설계되어 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop living in a particular home.
meanings.example: My roommate moved out last week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 룸메이트는 지난주에 이사 나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discover someone or something by using your sense of smell or by searching carefully.
meanings.example: The dog managed to sniff out the hidden drugs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 강아지가 숨겨진 마약을 냄새로 찾아내는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To supply with electrical energy; to make someone feel excited or energetic.
meanings.example: The coach's speech really charged up the team.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코치의 연설은 팀원들의 기운을 정말 북돋아 주었다 (고조시켰다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize someone or something very severely.
meanings.example: The critics ripped his new play to shreds.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비평가들은 그의 신작 연극을 갈갈이 찢어발겼다 (혹평했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue to hope for something even though it seems very unlikely to happen.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 가망 없는 희망을 품다, 간절히 바라다
meanings.example: We are hoping against hope that the missing hikers will be found safe.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 실종된 등산객들이 무사히 발견되기를 실낱같은 희망을 품고 바라고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be patient with someone or something.
meanings.example: Please bear with me while I find the relevant documents.
meanings.exampleKorean: 관련 서류를 찾는 동안 잠시만 참고 기다려주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something more exciting or interesting.
meanings.example: He decided to spice up his presentation with some funny videos.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 재미있는 영상들로 발표에 흥미를 더하기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take a risk by saying or doing something that might be criticized or punished.
meanings.example: I'm willing to stick my neck out and say that this plan will work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 이 계획이 성공할 것이라고 위험을 무릅쓰고 당당히 말할 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rely on someone to provide something; to expect something to happen.
meanings.example: The company is looking to expand into Asian markets next year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 내년에 아시아 시장으로의 확장을 기대하고(목표로 하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go to sleep (American informal).
meanings.example: I was so tired that I sacked out at 9 o'clock.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너무 피곤해서 9시에 바로 골아떨어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone very tired (American informal).
meanings.example: The long hike really tuckered the kids out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 긴 하이킹으로 아이들은 완전히 지쳐버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shake or pat something so that it looks full and soft.
meanings.example: She fluffed up the cushions before the guests arrived.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 손님들이 도착하기 전에 쿠션들을 두드려 부풀렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove a lid by turning it; to waste time (slang).
meanings.example: Screw off the cap to open the bottle.
meanings.exampleKorean: 병을 열려면 뚜껑을 돌려서 빼세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin a journey or an activity.
meanings.example: We started off the meeting with a brief introduction.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 짧은 소개와 함께 회의를 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become calm and behave normally again after being very upset or afraid.
meanings.example: I know it's hard, but you need to pull yourself together and focus on the job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 힘든 건 알지만, 마음을 추스르고 업무에 집중해야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give someone information about something that they do not know about.
meanings.example: Can you clue me in on what happened at the meeting?
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의에서 무슨 일이 있었는지 좀 알려줄래?

meanings.englishDefinition: To heat a liquid until it boils; to cause a situation to reach a critical stage.
meanings.example: The latest tax increase has brought the public's anger to a boil.
meanings.exampleKorean: 최근의 세금 인상은 대중의 분노를 최고조로 격화시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remain in one's house rather than going out.
meanings.example: I think I'll just stay at home and watch a movie tonight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 밤엔 그냥 집에 있으면서 영화나 볼까 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place with someone to have a relationship; to be so excited by an idea that you stop being practical.
meanings.example: Don't let your imagination run away with you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상상력에 너무 휩쓸리지(지나치게 상상하지) 마라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move back away from someone; to use something as a second choice when the first choice fails.
meanings.example: The army was forced to fall back. / I have some savings to fall back on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군대는 후퇴할 수밖에 없었다. / 나에게는 의지할(쓸 수 있는) 저축해둔 돈이 좀 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall to the ground; to fail because of a particular problem.
meanings.example: The old house is starting to fall down. / This is where the plan falls down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 낡은 집은 무너지기 시작했다. / 이 부분이 바로 그 계획이 실패하는(허점이 있는) 지점이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shake or pat a cushion or pillow so that it becomes full and soft again.
meanings.example: She plumped up the pillows to make the bed look more inviting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 침대를 더 안락해 보이게 하려고 베개를 다독여 부풀렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk too much or to say things that are boastful or indiscreet (informal).
meanings.example: He's always shooting off at the mouth about how important he is.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자기가 얼마나 중요한 사람인지 항상 떠벌리고 다닌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a silly way; to have a secret sexual relationship with someone who is not your partner.
meanings.example: Stop playing around and do your homework! / He was accused of playing around.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장난 그만하고 숙제해! / 그는 바람을 피웠다는 비난을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place very quickly.
meanings.example: I'll have to shoot off as soon as the meeting finishes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의가 끝나자마자 급히 가봐야 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter a place; to get a job very easily; to be trapped in a situation.
meanings.example: He just walked into a top job in the city. / I walked right into their trap.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 도심에서 아주 좋은 일자리를 쉽게 얻었다. / 나는 그들의 함정에 딱 걸려들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to tell a child to go away (informal).
meanings.example: Run along now, I'm busy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자 이제 그만 가보렴, 나 바쁘단다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go away; to stop annoying someone (slang).
meanings.example: Bug off and leave me alone!
meanings.exampleKorean: 저리 꺼져, 나 좀 혼자 내버려 둬!

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend time doing nothing useful or traveling with no clear plan.
meanings.example: He spent the summer bumming around Europe.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 여름을 유럽 여기저기를 빈둥거리며 여행하며 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decrease in number or amount; to stop supporting someone.
meanings.example: Support for the president is starting to fall away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대통령에 대한 지지도가 떨어지기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend time with someone so that you gradually learn more about them.
meanings.example: I'd like to get to know you better.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신을 더 잘 알고 싶어요 (더 친해지고 싶어요).

meanings.englishDefinition: To find or discover something by chance.
meanings.example: I stumbled upon a rare book in the old shop.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그 오래된 가게에서 우연히 희귀한 책 한 권을 발견했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To run very quickly in order to escape from someone or something.
meanings.example: When the alarms went off, the thieves made a run for it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경보가 울리자 도둑들은 급히 도망쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become friendly with a group of people; to agree with a plan or idea.
meanings.example: He fell in with a group of local musicians. / She fell in with my suggestion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 지역 음악가 무리와 어울리게 되었다. / 그녀는 나의 제안에 동의했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have an exciting time and spend a lot of money.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 신나게 즐기다, 인생을 즐기다, 흥청망청 놀다
meanings.example: They were living it up in Paris for the weekend.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 주말 동안 파리에서 신나게 인생을 즐기고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a loud sound; to allow someone or something to leave a place; to reveal a secret.
meanings.example: She let out a scream of terror. / Who let the dog out?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 공포에 질려 비명을 질렀다. / 누가 개를 밖으로 내보냈니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To work very hard and for a long time at something.
meanings.example: He's been beavering away at his desk all morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 오전 내내 책상에서 부지런히 일하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop something immediately; to extinguish a flame.
meanings.example: The rebellion was quickly snuffed out by the army.
meanings.exampleKorean: 반란은 군대에 의해 빠르게 진압되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find and get rid of something or someone that is causing a problem.
meanings.example: The government is determined to root out corruption.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 부패를 뿌리 뽑기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To abandon someone that you have a relationship with or responsibility for.
meanings.example: He ran out on his wife and children.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아내와 아이들을 버리고 떠났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tell someone your ideas so that they can give their opinion.
meanings.example: I'll run the proposal by my manager before I send it to you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신에게 보내기 전에 매니저에게 그 제안서를 보여주고 검토를 받을게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reach a particular amount or level; to go to someone for help.
meanings.example: The cost of the repairs will run to thousands of dollars.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수리 비용이 수천 달러에 달할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become detached; to succeed.
meanings.example: The handle came off the door. / I hope the party comes off well.
meanings.exampleKorean: 문 손잡이가 떨어졌다. / 파티가 잘 이루어지길(성공하길) 바라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place very quickly (American informal).
meanings.example: The kids lit out as soon as they saw the police car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 경찰차를 보자마자 급히 달아났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place secretly and quickly, especially without paying money you owe.
meanings.example: He skipped out on the bill.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 돈을 안 내고 몰래 달아났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn around quickly to face the opposite direction.
meanings.example: He wheeled around to see who was following him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 누가 따라오는지 보려고 휙 돌아섰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return to a place quickly; to rewind a video or audio tape.
meanings.example: Can you run that part of the video back again?
meanings.exampleKorean: 비디오의 그 부분을 다시 한 번 되감아 줄래?

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a way that is not expected or allowed by the organization you belong to.
meanings.example: The agent went off the reservation and started his own investigation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 요원은 조직의 통제를 벗어나 독단적으로 조사를 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become less strong or to stop (especially of rain or snow); to stop working as hard as you were.
meanings.example: The rain didn't let up all day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비가 하루 종일 그치지 않았다 (누그러지지 않았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To develop or create something; to gradually make yourself feel a particular emotion.
meanings.example: I've worked up a real appetite after that long walk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 긴 산책을 하고 났더니 정말 식욕이 돋는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shout loudly because you are in pain, surprised, or frightened.
meanings.example: She cried out in pain when she bumped her knee.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 무릎을 부딪혔을 때 통증 때문에 비명을 질렀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get rid of something that is considered bad and harmful.
meanings.example: The government is determined to stamp out drug abuse.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 약물 남용을 근절하기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something from a piece of writing or a film.
meanings.example: His comments were edited out of the final broadcast.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 발언은 최종 방송분에서 편집되어 삭제되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To slowly destroy something by erosion or chemicals.
meanings.example: The waves had eaten away at the base of the cliffs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 파도가 절벽의 아랫부분을 서서히 침식시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To throw something away because you do not want it; to force someone to leave a place (British informal).
meanings.example: I need to chuck out those old magazines. / He was chucked out of the pub for being too noisy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 낡은 잡지들을 버려야겠어. / 그는 너무 시끄럽게 굴어서 펍에서 내쫓겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start a friendship or a romantic relationship with someone.
meanings.example: She's taken up with a very strange group of people.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 아주 이상한 무리의 사람들과 어울리기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something move more slowly.
meanings.example: The roadworks really slowed us up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 도로 공사 때문에 우리의 속도가 정말 많이 늦춰졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To need something very much.
meanings.example: This old house is crying out for a coat of paint.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 낡은 집은 페인트칠이 절실히 필요한 상태다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To say that you cannot do something that you have already agreed to do (British informal).
meanings.example: He agreed to help us, but then he cried off at the last minute.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 우리를 도와주기로 동의했지만, 마지막 순간에 못 하겠다고 취소했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make your body or muscles stronger and firmer.
meanings.example: These exercises will help you tone up your stomach muscles.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 운동들은 복근을 탄력 있게 만드는 데 도움이 될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give up control of something; to reveal a secret.
meanings.example: The city finally yielded up its secrets to the archaeologists.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 도시는 마침내 고고학자들에게 자신의 비밀을 드러냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pull something off very quickly and forcefully.
meanings.example: He yanked off his boots and threw them on the floor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 장화를 확 벗어 던져서 바닥에 놓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To die from the tips of the branches or leaves down towards the roots.
meanings.example: The roses die back in the winter but grow again in the spring.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장미는 겨울에 시들지만 봄에 다시 자란다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste time doing silly or unimportant things (British informal).
meanings.example: Stop footling around and get some work done!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그만 빈둥거리고 일 좀 해!

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop working and go on strike.
meanings.example: The factory workers threw down their tools in protest over pay.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공장 노동자들은 임금 문제에 항의하며 일손을 놓고 파업에 들어갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To introduce a new law or system; to earn an amount of money.
meanings.example: The government is bringing in a new law to protect the environment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 환경을 보호하기 위한 새로운 법을 도입하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a particular quality or detail noticeable; to produce a new product and start selling it.
meanings.example: That dress really brings out the color of your eyes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 드레스는 정말 당신의 눈동자 색을 잘 살려주네요 (드러내 주네요).

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter a room or building; to become fashionable.
meanings.example: Please come in and have a seat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 안으로 들어오셔서 앉으세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lie down at full length; to extend your arms or legs.
meanings.example: He stretched out on the sofa and fell asleep.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 소파에 몸을 쭉 뻗고 누워 잠이 들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become known to the public.
meanings.example: New evidence has come to light that could change the whole case.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사건 전체를 바꿀 수 있는 새로운 증거가 밝혀졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be successful or lucky after a difficult period.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 어려움을 딛고 일어서다, 운 좋게 위기를 모면하다
meanings.example: Don't worry about him; he always lands on his feet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그에 대해 걱정하지 마. 그는 항상 위기를 잘 모면하니까 (결국 잘 일어서니까).

meanings.englishDefinition: To state something clearly and honestly; to risk your reputation or job.
meanings.example: He laid his reputation on the line to support the project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그 프로젝트를 지지하기 위해 자신의 명예를 걸었다 (위험을 무릅썼다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To interfere in something that does not involve you.
meanings.example: Stop sticking your nose into my private life!
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 사생활에 참견 좀 그만해!

meanings.englishDefinition: To use your power or influence in an unpleasant way to get what you want.
meanings.example: He's always throwing his weight around and telling everyone what to do.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 위세를 부리며 모두에게 이래라저래라 명령한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very frightened or shocked (of hair).
meanings.example: The scream made my hair stand on end.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 비명 소리에 내 머리카락이 곤두섰다 (소름이 돋았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To swell or make something swell; to make someone feel proud or important.
meanings.example: The bee sting made his arm puff up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 벌에 쏘여서 그의 팔이 부어올랐다 (부풀었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone feel sad or disappointed (American informal).
meanings.example: It really bummed me out that I couldn't go to the concert.
meanings.exampleKorean: 콘서트에 갈 수 없었다는 사실이 정말 나를 낙담시켰다 (기분 나쁘게 했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To delay making a decision; to keep something secret.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 결정을 미루다, (정보 등을) 뭉개다, 공개하지 않다
meanings.example: The committee has been sitting on the application for weeks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 위원회는 그 신청서를 몇 주째 뭉개고 (결정을 미루고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return to a time in the past.
meanings.example: You can't just wind back the clock and pretend it never happened.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그냥 시간을 되돌려서 그런 일이 없었던 것처럼 할 수는 없어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove or destroy something by the movement of water.
meanings.example: The flood washed away the bridge.
meanings.exampleKorean: 홍수가 다리를 쓸어가 버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To happen to someone or to be felt by someone in a way that they cannot control.
meanings.example: A feeling of relief washed over her.
meanings.exampleKorean: 안도감이 그녀에게 밀려왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone remember something; to make something that existed in the past exist again.
meanings.example: The smell of baking bread always brings back memories of my childhood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빵 굽는 냄새는 항상 나의 어린 시절 기억을 떠올리게 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become friends again after a fight or argument.
meanings.example: Life is too short to stay angry; let's just kiss and make up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 화를 내며 살기엔 인생이 너무 짧아. 그냥 화해하자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To relax and stop making an effort; to watch something happen without becoming involved.
meanings.example: You can't just sit back and wait for things to happen.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그냥 가만히 앉아서 일이 일어나기만을 기다릴 수는 없어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To delay making a decision; to avoid choosing between two different sides in an argument.
meanings.example: You can't sit on the fence forever; you need to choose a side.
meanings.exampleKorean: 영원히 중립만 지킬 수는 없어. 한쪽을 선택해야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something known to people.
meanings.example: The investigation brought to light several cases of corruption.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수사를 통해 여러 건의 부패 사례가 밝혀졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue to do something as well as you are doing it now.
meanings.example: You're doing a great job! Keep it up!
meanings.exampleKorean: 정말 잘하고 있어! 계속 수고해 (힘내서 계속해)!

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually start to like someone or something more.
meanings.example: I didn't like the song at first, but it's starting to grow on me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 처음에는 그 노래를 좋아하지 않았지만, 점점 좋아지기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become too big for a piece of clothing; to stop doing something as you get older.
meanings.koreanDefinition: (옷 등이) 몸에 안 맞게 되다, (습관 등을) 버리다 (나이가 들면서)
meanings.example: He's already grown out of his new shoes. / Most children grow out of their fear of the dark.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 벌써 새 신발이 안 맞을 정도로 자랐다. / 대부분의 아이들은 자라면서 어둠에 대한 공포를 버리게 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To unite as a group against someone.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~를 집단으로 괴롭히다, 몰매를 때리다, 합세하여 공격하다
meanings.example: The older boys ganged up on him in the playground.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나이 많은 형들이 운동장에서 그를 집단으로 괴롭혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To plan something in detail.
meanings.example: He's mapped out his entire career for the next ten years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 향후 10년 동안의 자신의 경력을 상세히 설계해두었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue doing something even though you have no energy or money left.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 기력이 다한 채 버티다, 간신히 버티다
meanings.example: By the end of the marathon, he was running on fumes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마라톤이 끝날 때쯤 그는 간신히 기력으로만 버티고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tell lies or to say one thing and mean another.
meanings.example: The politician was accused of speaking with a forked tongue.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인은 거짓말을 한다(표리부동하게 말한다)는 비난을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become happier or more energetic; to encourage someone.
meanings.example: Buck up! Things aren't that bad.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기운 내! 상황이 그렇게 나쁘지는 않아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To work together as a group to achieve something.
meanings.example: If we all pull together, we can finish the project on time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 모두가 힘을 합친다면 프로젝트를 제시간에 끝낼 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To succeed in doing something difficult.
meanings.example: It was a difficult deal, but he managed to bring it off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어려운 거래였지만, 그는 그것을 성공적으로 해냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay until the end of a long or boring speech, film, etc.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 끝까지 자리에 앉아 있다, 참고 보다
meanings.example: I could barely sit through the two-hour lecture.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그 두 시간짜리 강의를 끝까지 참고 앉아 있기가 힘들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shoot someone with a gun and kill or seriously injure them.
meanings.example: He was gunned down in the street.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 거리에서 사살당했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give an opinion or enter an argument in a forceful way.
meanings.example: He weighed in with some very strong arguments against the plan.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그 계획에 반대하는 아주 강력한 의견을 내놓으며 논쟁에 끼어들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To run after someone or something in order to catch them.
meanings.example: The dog was chasing after a rabbit.
meanings.exampleKorean: 강아지가 토끼를 뒤쫓고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try very hard to achieve something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 무엇이든 다 하다, 수단과 방법을 가리지 않다, 노력을 아끼지 않다
meanings.example: She went to great lengths to ensure the party was a success.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 파티를 성공시키기 위해 노력을 아끼지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail or be forgotten because people stop paying attention to it.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 중도에 포기되다, 관심 밖으로 밀려나다, 실패하다
meanings.example: Many good ideas go by the wayside because of a lack of funding.
meanings.exampleKorean: 많은 좋은 아이디어들이 자금 부족 때문에 중도에 포기된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak to someone in a very angry and critical way (informal).
meanings.example: I only asked a simple question; there's no need to bite my head off!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그냥 간단한 질문 하나 했을 뿐인데, 그렇게 호되게 화낼 필요는 없잖아!

meanings.englishDefinition: To phone someone again or to phone someone who has phoned you.
meanings.example: I'm busy right now; can I ring you back in ten minutes?
meanings.exampleKorean: 지금 좀 바빠서요. 10분 뒤에 다시 전화 드려도 될까요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To deal with or take care of something or someone.
meanings.example: Don't worry about the drinks; I'll see to them.
meanings.exampleKorean: 음료는 걱정 마세요. 제가 처리할게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To complain very loudly and angrily about something.
meanings.example: He kicked up a real fuss when he heard his flight was delayed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 비행기가 지연되었다는 소식을 듣고 정말 강하게 항의했다 (소동을 피웠다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To examine something quickly.
meanings.example: I'd like you to look over this report and tell me what you think.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 보고서를 좀 살펴보고 의견을 주셨으면 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To win after being in a losing position during a race or competition.
meanings.example: The team came from behind to win 3-2.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 팀은 역전해서 3대 2로 승리했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare to work hard.
meanings.example: There's a lot of work to do, so let's roll up our sleeves and get started.
meanings.exampleKorean: 할 일이 많으니, 이제 소매를 걷어붙이고 시작해봅시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent someone from entering a place; to prevent an opponent from scoring any points in a game.
meanings.example: The team shut out their rivals in the final game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 팀은 결승전에서 라이벌 팀을 완봉했다 (한 점도 주지 않았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be connected with or to influence something.
meanings.example: This information doesn't really bear on the case.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 정보는 이 사건과 별로 관계가 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To empty something by turning it over.
meanings.example: He dumped out the contents of his bag on the bed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 가방 안에 든 것들을 침대 위에 확 쏟아버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To phone several people or places to get information.
meanings.example: I'll ring around and see if anyone has a spare ticket.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 여러 군데 전화를 돌려서 남는 티켓이 있는지 알아볼게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dress oneself up in a showy or suggestive way (informal).
meanings.example: She hussied herself up for the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 파티를 위해 화려하게(야하게) 치장했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To buy or get something quickly because it is very cheap or exactly what you want.
meanings.example: All the best tickets were snapped up within minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가장 좋은 티켓들은 몇 분 만에 모두 매진되었다 (사람들이 낚아채 갔다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop being in an unhappy or confused state.
meanings.example: I was feeling a bit down, but I managed to snap out of it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기분이 좀 가라앉아 있었지만, 곧 기운을 차리고 떨쳐냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To approach someone quietly and unexpectedly.
meanings.example: Don't sneak up on me like that! You scared me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그렇게 몰래 다가오지 마! 놀랐잖아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave quietly without being noticed; to disappear gradually.
meanings.example: We managed to slip away before the party ended.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 파티가 끝나기 전에 슬쩍 빠져나올 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use information or experience that you have to help you do something.
meanings.example: He drew on his experience as a teacher to write the book.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 책을 쓰기 위해 교사로서의 자신의 경험을 활용했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To solve a complicated problem by thinking about it for a long time.
meanings.example: I'm still trying to puzzle out how this machine works.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 이 기계가 어떻게 작동하는지 여전히 알아내려 애쓰는 중이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To choose to be part of an activity, arrangement, etc.
meanings.example: Employees can opt in to the company's pension scheme.
meanings.exampleKorean: 직원들은 회사의 연금 제도에 가입하기로 선택할 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to do something, especially something you have not done before.
meanings.example: I've never played golf before, but I'd like to have a crack at it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 골프를 쳐본 적은 없지만, 한번 시도해보고 싶어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To search for someone or something until you find them and capture or kill them.
meanings.example: The police are hunting down the person responsible for the crime.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 그 범죄를 저지른 사람을 추격하여 찾아내고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone or something more energetic or interesting.
meanings.example: The upbeat music really pepped up the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경쾌한 음악이 파티에 정말 활기를 불어넣었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go past someone or something; to happen without affecting you.
meanings.example: I saw him pass by the window. / Sometimes I feel like life is passing me by.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그가 창문 옆을 지나가는 것을 보았다. / 가끔 인생이 나를 비껴지나가고 있다는 느낌이 든다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter a place quietly and secretly; to manage to include something without being noticed.
meanings.example: We managed to sneak in through the back door.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 뒷문을 통해 몰래 들어가는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove a piece of clothing quickly and easily; to leave a place quietly.
meanings.koreanDefinition: (옷 등을) 훌딱 벗다, 조용히 자리를 뜨다
meanings.example: She slipped off her shoes at the door.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 문에서 신발을 훌딱 벗었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To say that someone or something is responsible for something bad.
meanings.example: Don't try to put the blame on me for your mistakes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 네 실수에 대해 나에게 책임을 돌리려 하지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move out very quickly; to have a gunfight.
meanings.example: A flame shot out from the engine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 엔진에서 불꽃이 튀어 나왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To provoke someone to respond, usually with anger or annoyance (informal).
meanings.example: He's just trying to get a rise from you; don't let it bother you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그냥 네 화를 돋우려는 것뿐이야. 신경 쓰지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To follow someone in order to catch or attack them; to happen later than something else.
meanings.example: The police are coming after him for the robbery.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰이 강도 사건으로 그를 뒤쫓고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid going near someone or something.
meanings.example: Keep away from the edge of the cliff.
meanings.exampleKorean: 절벽 끝에 가까이 가지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move forward in a strong and determined way; to make a lot of progress quickly.
meanings.example: The company is forging ahead with its plans for expansion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 확장 계획을 힘차게 추진하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consider or regard someone or something in a particular way.
meanings.example: He is looked upon as one of the best players in the league.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 리그 최고의 선수 중 한 명으로 간주된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave as if you are more important or higher in social class than you really are.
meanings.example: Since she got that promotion, she's been putting on airs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 승진한 뒤로 젠체하며 거드름을 피우고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start a business or to start doing something in a particular place.
meanings.example: He decided to set up shop in his hometown after living abroad.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 해외 생활을 마치고 고향에서 사업을 시작하기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To defeat someone by a very small amount; to gradually force someone out of a position.
meanings.example: She managed to edge out her rival in the final seconds of the race.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 경주 마지막 몇 초를 남기고 라이벌을 간발의 차이로 물리쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join an activity without being very involved in it or without having a strong interest in it.
meanings.example: I don't really care where we go; I'm just going along for the ride.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리가 어디로 가든 상관없어. 난 그냥 따라가는 것뿐이야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail to do something because of fear (slang).
meanings.example: He was going to go bungee jumping, but he wussed out at the last minute.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 번지점프를 하려고 했지만, 마지막 순간에 겁을 먹고 포기했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat someone in a rude or aggressive way, often telling them what to do.
meanings.example: Don't let him push you around; stand up for yourself.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 널 함부로 대하게 두지 마. 네 권리를 당당히 주장해.

meanings.englishDefinition: Not working because of a fault; in a very poor physical or mental condition.
meanings.example: The car is broken down on the side of the road.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 차는 도로 옆에 고장 난 채 서 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To participate in a sit-in (a form of protest); to substitute for someone.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 연좌 시위를 하다, 대리 근무를 하다
meanings.example: The students are sitting in to protest the tuition hike.
meanings.exampleKorean: 학생들이 등록금 인상에 반대하여 연좌 시위를 벌이고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay money that you owe; to prepare to fight someone.
meanings.example: We need to square up before we leave the restaurant. / The two men squared up to each other.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 식당을 떠나기 전에 정산해야 한다. / 두 남자가 싸울 기세로 서로 마주 섰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to say that you do not want to be part of an activity.
meanings.example: If you're going to start an argument, include me out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 싸움을 시작할 거라면 나는 빼줘 (참여하지 않겠어).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop gradually but completely.
meanings.example: Traffic ground to a halt during the heavy snowstorm.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭설이 내리는 동안 교통이 서서히 마비되었다 (멈춰 섰다).

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to tell someone that if they do not improve their behavior or performance, they will have to leave.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 똑바로 행동해라 아니면 나가라, 정신 차려라
meanings.example: The manager told his lazy employees to shape up or ship out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매니저는 게으른 직원들에게 똑바로 일하든지 아니면 회사를 나가라고 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hurt someone's feelings deeply.
meanings.example: Her cruel remarks cut him to the quick.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 잔인한 발언은 그에게 깊은 상처를 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare to use something again after a long time.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 오랫동안 안 쓰던 것을 다시 꺼내다, 먼지를 털어내다
meanings.example: It's time to dust off my old guitar and start practicing again.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이제 낡은 기타를 다시 꺼내어 연습을 시작할 시간이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To explain something in a very clear and detailed way.
meanings.example: I had to spell out the instructions for him step by step.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그를 위해 지침을 단계별로 아주 상세히 설명해주어야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to find out what someone thinks about a plan or idea.
meanings.example: I'll sound out the boss about the possibility of a pay rise.
meanings.exampleKorean: 임금 인상 가능성에 대해 상사의 의중을 떠볼게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discourage someone or to say something that makes a plan seem less likely to succeed.
meanings.example: I don't want to throw cold water on your plans, but have you considered the cost?
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신의 계획에 찬물을 끼얹고 싶지는 않지만, 비용은 고려해보셨나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue to work or be active even though you have no energy left.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 기력이 다한 채 버티다, 간신히 버티다
meanings.example: I've been working so hard that I'm running on empty.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너무 열심히 일해서 이제는 정말 기력으로만 버티고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare a formal document; to stop (of a vehicle).
meanings.example: The lawyers are drawing up the contract now. / A taxi drew up outside the hotel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 변호사들이 지금 계약서를 작성하고 있다. / 택시 한 대가 호텔 앞에 와서 섰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join the army; to combine to form a single unit.
meanings.example: He decided to join up as soon as he finished school.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 학교를 마치자마자 입대하기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become friendly with someone, often for your own benefit; to work in pairs.
meanings.example: The children had to buddy up for the field trip.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 현장 학습을 위해 짝을 지어야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To run away quickly in order to escape from a difficult or dangerous situation.
meanings.example: When the investors heard the bad news, they headed for the hills.
meanings.exampleKorean: 투자자들은 나쁜 소식을 듣자마자 급히 자취를 감추었다 (투자금을 회수해 떠났다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a bill become an official law by signing it.
meanings.example: The president signed the environment bill into law today.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대통령은 오늘 환경 법안에 서명하여 법으로 공포했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make your feelings obvious to everyone.
meanings.example: He's the kind of person who wears his heart on his sleeve.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 감정을 숨기지 않고 다 드러내는 사람이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pull something away very quickly and forcefully; to remove clothing quickly.
meanings.example: She tore off a piece of paper from the notebook.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 공책에서 종이 한 장을 확 찢어냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop something from having any effect.
meanings.example: The cost of the new equipment will be canceled out by the savings in energy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 장비 비용은 에너지 절감액에 의해 상쇄될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be remembered or thought of.
meanings.example: Nothing immediately comes to mind when you ask that question.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 질문을 들었을 때 즉시 떠오르는 것이 아무것도 없네요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to achieve a lot, even though it involves taking a risk.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 큰 것을 노리다, (위험을 감수하고) 대성공을 꾀하다
meanings.example: The company decided to swing for the fences with their new product launch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 신제품 출시와 함께 대성공을 노리기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move more slowly than other people in a group; to fail to do something by a particular time.
meanings.example: I fell behind with my schoolwork because I was ill.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 아파서 학교 과제가 뒤처졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept something unpleasant without complaining.
meanings.example: I know the work is hard, but you just have to suck it up and finish it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 일이 힘들다는 건 알지만, 그냥 꾹 참고 끝내야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To disapprove of something.
meanings.example: Smoking is frowned on in many public places.
meanings.exampleKorean: 많은 공공장소에서 흡연은 좋지 않게 여겨진다 (눈살을 찌푸리게 한다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To hire a lawyer to represent you in a legal matter (informal).
meanings.example: As soon as the investigation started, he decided to lawyer up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수사가 시작되자마자 그는 변호사를 선임하기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tell someone what to do in a way that is annoying or rude.
meanings.example: Stop bossing me around! I'm not your servant.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나한테 이래라저래라 명령 좀 하지 마! 난 네 하인이 아니야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing something without intending to; to be able to be divided into a particular group.
meanings.example: She fell into the habit of drinking too much coffee. / The books fall into three categories.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 커피를 너무 많이 마시는 습관에 빠졌다. / 그 책들은 세 가지 카테고리로 분류된다 (속한다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide a computer or other electrical device with power so that it is ready to use.
meanings.example: The system will take a few minutes to power up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시스템이 작동을 시작하는 데 몇 분 정도 걸릴 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To send a message or ask a question very quickly.
meanings.example: She fired off a quick email before she left the office.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 사무실을 떠나기 전에 이메일을 재빨리 한 통 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend time doing small, pleasant tasks around the house without any particular purpose (British informal).
meanings.example: I spent Sunday afternoon just pottering around in the garden.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 일요일 오후를 그냥 정원에서 소일거리를 하며 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put a saddle on a horse; to prepare to do something.
meanings.example: It's time to saddle up and hit the road.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이제 준비를 마치고 길을 떠날 시간이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become heavier.
meanings.example: I've put on a lot of weight over the winter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 겨울 동안 살이 많이 쪘다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be completely destroyed by fire.
meanings.example: The old factory burned down last night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 낡은 공장이 어젯밤에 전소되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move the cursor over a specific area on a computer screen using a mouse.
meanings.example: If you mouse over the image, a caption will appear.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이미지 위에 마우스 커서를 올리면 설명이 나타납니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail to get an advantage or a profit that other people get.
meanings.example: Small businesses are losing out to the large supermarkets.
meanings.exampleKorean: 소규모 상점들이 대형 슈퍼마켓에 밀려 기회를 놓치고(손해를 보고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something unpleasant that you have to do so that you do not have to worry about it anymore.
meanings.example: I'll be glad to get the exam over with.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시험을 얼른 끝내버리고 싶다 (끝내면 홀가분할 것 같다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To wait in a line of people or vehicles in order to do something or go somewhere.
meanings.example: People were queuing up outside the cinema for hours.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사람들이 극장 밖에서 몇 시간 동안 줄을 서서 기다리고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find something by chance.
meanings.example: I stumbled across some old letters while I was cleaning the attic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다락방을 청소하다가 우연히 낡은 편지들을 발견했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to find out what someone's opinions or intentions are without asking them directly.
meanings.example: I'll try to feel out my boss about a possible promotion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 승진 가능성에 대해 상사의 의중을 조심스럽게 떠볼게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To complete or finish something in a satisfactory way.
meanings.example: He rounded out his meal with a delicious dessert.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 맛있는 디저트로 식사를 완벽하게 마무리했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attack someone physically or with words (informal).
meanings.example: The manager really laid into the team after they lost the game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경기에 진 뒤 매니저는 팀원들을 정말 호되게 비난했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a hole in something by removing the inside part.
meanings.example: We hollowed out the pumpkin to make a Jack-o'-lantern.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 잭오랜턴을 만들기 위해 호박의 속을 파냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To abandon someone to a group of people who will treat them badly or destroy them.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 희생양으로 던져주다, 무방비 상태로 내버려 두다
meanings.example: The politician was thrown to the dogs by his own party when the scandal broke.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스캔들이 터지자 그 정치인은 자신의 당에 의해 희생양으로 내던져졌다 (버림받았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be left in a difficult or embarrassing situation without help.
meanings.example: They left their candidate to twist in the wind while the accusations grew.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 비난이 거세지는 동안 자신들의 후보를 아무런 도움 없이 곤경에 방치했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To not include someone in a plan or activity.
meanings.example: If you're going hiking in the rain, you can count me out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빗속에 하이킹을 갈 거라면 나는 빼줘 (제외해줘).

meanings.englishDefinition: To store something for future use; to force someone to stay in bed because of illness or injury.
meanings.example: He was laid up with the flu for a week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 독감으로 일주일 동안 누워 지냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return to the matter at hand or the main subject of discussion.
meanings.example: But let us return to our muttons and discuss the budget.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자, 이제 본론으로 돌아가서 예산에 대해 논의해봅시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fit into place with a snapping sound.
meanings.example: The battery should snap in easily.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배터리는 딸깍 하고 쉽게 끼워져야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause something bad to happen to yourself or someone else.
meanings.example: He brought this trouble upon himself.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 이 문제를 스스로 초래했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a new product, service, or system available for the first time.
meanings.example: The company plans to roll out the new software next month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 다음 달에 새로운 소프트웨어를 출시할 계획이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start and seem likely to continue (especially of something unpleasant like rain or infection).
meanings.example: Panic set in when the fire alarm went off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 화재 경보가 울리자 공포가 시작되었다 (엄습했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To ask for someone to come to you or for something to be brought to you.
meanings.example: We had to send for a doctor immediately.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 즉시 의사를 부르러 사람을 보내야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find something that you have not used for a long time; to remove something from the ground by digging.
meanings.example: I need to dig out my old winter coat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 낡은 겨울 코트를 (옷장 깊숙한 곳에서) 찾아내야겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: Involved in something, often unwillingly; completely absorbed in something.
meanings.example: He got caught up in the excitement of the crowd.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 군중의 흥분에 휩쓸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to say that you need or would like to have something.
meanings.example: I could really do with a cup of coffee right now.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지금 커피 한 잔 마시면 정말 좋겠네 (필요하네).

meanings.englishDefinition: To make you remember something.
meanings.example: That melody brings to mind the summers of my youth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 멜로디는 내 젊은 시절의 여름날들을 생각나게 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To choose to be more careful or more generous than necessary in order to avoid a mistake.
meanings.example: It's better to err on the side of caution when investing money.
meanings.exampleKorean: 돈을 투자할 때는 차라리 지나치다 싶을 정도로 조심하는 편이 낫다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare to fight or compete with someone.
meanings.example: The two champions are ready to square off in the final.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 챔피언은 결승에서 대결할 준비가 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak in a way that is inappropriate or indiscreet.
meanings.example: I hope I'm not talking out of turn, but I think you should know the truth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 주제넘게 말하는 게 아니길 바라지만, 당신이 진실을 알아야 한다고 생각해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become firmly attached to something; to understand something or become interested in it.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 찰싹 달라붙다, (생각 등을) 자기 것으로 만들다, 이해하다
meanings.example: He latched on to the idea immediately.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 즉시 그 아이디어에 매료되었다 (자기 것으로 만들었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To reject or dismiss an idea or question easily.
meanings.example: She batted away questions about her private life.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 자신의 사생활에 관한 질문들을 단호히 거절했다 (쳐냈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To change the way you do something, especially in order to improve it.
meanings.example: The team decided to change up their strategy for the second half.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 팀은 후반전을 위해 전략에 변화를 주기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get a lot of pleasure from doing something.
meanings.example: He delights in proving other people wrong.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 다른 사람들의 틀린 점을 증명해내는 것을 몹시 즐긴다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Dressed in a very formal or attractive way; repaired and decorated.
meanings.example: The whole house has been done up beautifully.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집 전체가 아름답게 수리되었다 (말끔히 단장되었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To fasten something so that it is held away from your face or kept in place.
meanings.example: She tied back her hair before she started cooking.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 요리를 시작하기 전에 머리를 뒤로 묶었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go to war or into a period of intense conflict.
meanings.example: The two companies are going to the mattresses over the patent rights.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 회사는 특허권을 두고 치열한 전쟁(싸움)에 돌입했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use something as a second choice when the first choice fails.
meanings.example: If I don't get the job, I have my savings to fall back on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 직장을 구하지 못하면, 의지할(쓸 수 있는) 저축해둔 돈이 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go somewhere with someone; to make progress or improve.
meanings.example: Would you like to come along with us to the cinema? / How is your French coming along?
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리랑 같이 영화 보러 갈래? / 프랑스어 공부는 어떻게 돼가고 있니 (진전되고 있니)?

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide something that will be a benefit or an advantage to a group or an activity.
