meanings.example: He brings years of experience in marketing to the table.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 수년간의 마케팅 경험이라는 가치를 (우리 팀에) 제공하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue to have your own opinion or belief about something, even though other people are trying to tell you that you are wrong.
meanings.example: Despite the criticism, she decided to stick to her guns.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비난에도 불구하고, 그녀는 자신의 신념을 굽히지 않기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be in an uncertain or dangerous situation.
meanings.example: The future of the company hangs in the balance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사의 미래가 풍전등화와 같이 위태로운 상태다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To think about something carefully for a period of time.
meanings.example: I'll need some time to chew over your proposal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신의 제안을 심사숙고해볼 시간이 좀 필요합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk too much or to say things that should be kept secret.
meanings.example: He's always running off at the mouth about other people's business.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 남의 일에 대해 함부로 지껄이고 다닌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start a computer system.
meanings.example: It takes forever for this old laptop to boot up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 낡은 노트북은 부팅하는 데 한참 걸린다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To match or look good with something; to accept a plan or an idea.
meanings.example: This tie goes with your shirt. / I'll go with your suggestion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 넥타이는 셔츠와 잘 어울린다. / 당신의 제안을 따를게요 (동의할게요).

meanings.englishDefinition: To become equal or to make things equal in importance, amount, etc.
meanings.example: The costs and benefits should balance out in the long run.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장기적으로는 비용과 이익이 균형을 이룰(상쇄될) 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hide and wait for someone in order to attack them or surprise them.
meanings.example: The soldiers were lying in wait for the enemy convoy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군인들은 적군의 호송대를 공격하기 위해 숨어서 기다리고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come out from a place; to be produced or revealed.
meanings.example: Several witnesses have come forth with new information.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몇몇 목격자들이 새로운 정보를 가지고 앞으로 나왔다 (나타났다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be independent and not need support from anything else.
meanings.example: This department should be able to stand on its own within a year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 부서는 1년 안에 자립할 수 있어야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To say the same things in public about a subject so that people believe you agree with each other.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 목소리를 하나로 모으다, 일치된 의견을 보이다
meanings.example: We need to make sure everyone in the department is singing from the same hymnbook.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 부서원 모두가 일치된 목소리를 내고 있는지 확인할 필요가 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to tell someone that you do not believe what they are saying or that they are being silly (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 말도 안 되는 소리 마, 집어쳐 (강한 부정)
meanings.example: Come off it! You know that's not true.
meanings.exampleKorean: 말도 안 되는 소리 좀 마! 그게 사실이 아니라는 건 너도 알잖아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To record the time you arrive at work.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 출근 카드를 찍다, 출근 시간을 기록하다
meanings.example: I have to punch in by 9 o'clock every morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 매일 아침 9시까지 출근 카드를 찍어야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone confused or to make them lose their physical balance.
meanings.example: The unexpected question threw him off balance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 예상치 못한 질문은 그를 당황하게 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep a secret for the rest of one's life.
meanings.example: He promised to take the secret to the grave.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그 비밀을 무덤까지 가져가겠다고 (영원히 비밀로 하겠다고) 약속했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wear informal clothes; to scold someone severely.
meanings.example: The employees are allowed to dress down on Fridays. / The boss gave him a real dressing down for his mistake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 직원들은 금요일에 간편한 복장을 하는 것이 허용된다. / 상사는 그의 실수에 대해 정말 호되게 꾸짖었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take someone's place and do their job for a short time.
meanings.example: I'll stand in for her while she's on holiday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 휴가 간 동안 내가 그녀를 대신할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To listen very carefully to everything someone says because you are so interested.
meanings.example: The audience hung on every word of his speech.
meanings.exampleKorean: 청중은 그의 연설 한마디 한마디에 귀를 기울였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be completely destroyed or to fail suddenly and completely.
meanings.example: Our plans for a summer holiday went up in smoke when the car broke down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자동차가 고장 나면서 우리의 여름휴가 계획은 수포로 돌아갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have no successful result.
meanings.example: All their hard work came to nothing in the end.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들의 모든 노고는 결국 아무런 성과 없이 끝났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop talking about unimportant details and say what is most important.
meanings.example: Stop beating around the bush and get to the point!
meanings.exampleKorean: 돌려 말하지 말고 본론만 말해!

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat someone in a fair and honest way.
meanings.example: The company always tries to do right by its employees.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 항상 직원들을 정당하게 대우하려 노력한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To follow a particular course of action.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 그런 식으로 행동하다, 특정한 길을 밟다
meanings.example: I don't think we want to go down that road again.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리가 다시 그런 식으로 행동하고 싶지는(그런 경로를 밟고 싶지는) 않은 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend a lot of money on a celebration (British informal).
meanings.example: They really pushed the boat out for their wedding anniversary.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 결혼기념일을 축하하기 위해 정말 돈을 아끼지 않고 펑펑 썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn off a computer or other electrical device.
meanings.example: Please remember to power down your laptop before leaving.
meanings.exampleKorean: 떠나기 전에 노트북 전원을 끄는 것을 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To think only of yourself and your own interests (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 자기 실속을 차리다, 자기 자신만을 생각하다
meanings.example: In this business, you have to look out for number one.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 업계에서는 자기 실속을 먼저 차려야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To think only about your own interests and not about other people.
meanings.example: In this competitive world, you have to look out for Number One.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 경쟁적인 세상에서, 당신은 자신의 이익을 가장 먼저 챙겨야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate a part of a company or organization and make it independent.
meanings.example: The group is planning to hive off its chemicals division.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 그룹은 화학 부문을 분할하여 독립시킬 계획이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clear up (of the weather).
meanings.example: It looks like it's going to fair off this afternoon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 오후에는 날씨가 갤 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin a new business; to start an engine.
meanings.example: He decided to start up his own software company.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 소프트웨어 회사를 창업하기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To understand and accept an idea or a piece of information.
meanings.example: I've listened to your suggestions and I've taken them on board.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신의 제안을 들었고, 그것들을 받아들였습니다 (고려하고 있습니다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To ask several people for information or help.
meanings.example: I don't know where the best restaurant is, but I'll ask around.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어디가 가장 좋은 식당인지 모르겠지만, 여기저기 물어볼게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To complete or finalize a deal or arrangement successfully.
meanings.example: They hope to sew up the deal by the end of the week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 이번 주말까지 계약을 매듭짓기를 바라고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Having very little money (informal).
meanings.example: I'm a bit hard up at the moment, so I can't go out for dinner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지금 돈이 좀 쪼들려서 저녁 먹으러 나갈 수가 없어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To write a full report or article based on notes you have made.
meanings.example: I need to write up the results of my research.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 연구 결과에 대한 보고서를 작성해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pull something out of a bag, pocket, etc., after searching for it.
meanings.example: He fished out a few coins from his pocket.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 주머니에서 동전 몇 개를 찾아 꺼냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To replace one thing with another.
meanings.example: I need to swap out the old batteries for new ones.
meanings.exampleKorean: 낡은 배터리를 새것으로 교체해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce the price of something; to give a student a lower grade.
meanings.example: The winter clothes have been marked down by 50%.
meanings.exampleKorean: 겨울 옷 가격이 50% 인하되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attack or criticize someone severely (American informal).
meanings.example: My dad really lit into me when I got home late.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 집에 늦게 들어갔을 때 아빠는 정말 나를 호되게 꾸짖으셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something again; to decorate a room again.
meanings.example: This homework is so messy that you'll have to do it over.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 숙제는 너무 지저분해서 다시 해야겠구나.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attack someone suddenly; to be the responsibility of someone.
meanings.example: The responsibility for the project fell upon him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프로젝트에 대한 책임이 그에게 돌아갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fasten a seat belt; to be quiet (British informal).
meanings.example: Just belt up and let me finish my work!
meanings.exampleKorean: 제발 좀 조용히 해! 나 일 좀 끝내게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay someone so that they will not do something that would cause you trouble.
meanings.example: They tried to buy off the witnesses.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 목격자들을 매수하려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase or become larger or stronger.
meanings.example: He's trying to build up his confidence.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신감을 기르려(강화하려) 노력하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To forget to take something with you; to make much more progress than someone else.
meanings.example: I realized I'd left my phone behind at the hotel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 호텔에 휴대폰을 두고 왔다는 사실을 깨달았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be so excited about something that you do not control what you say or do.
meanings.example: I got a bit carried away and spent too much money.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 좀 흥분해서 (자제력을 잃고) 돈을 너무 많이 썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To think carefully about the advantages and disadvantages of a situation before making a decision.
meanings.example: We need to weigh up the pros and cons before we decide.
meanings.exampleKorean: 결정하기 전에 장단점을 꼼꼼히 따져봐야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return to a previous state or price; to reduce the power or influence of something.
meanings.example: The government is under pressure to roll back the tax increases.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 세금 인상을 철회(되돌리기)하라는 압박을 받고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put on special clothes or a uniform for a particular activity.
meanings.example: The players are suiting up for the big game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선수들이 중요한 경기를 위해 유니폼을 갖춰 입고 있다 (준비하고 있다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To say something suddenly or unexpectedly.
meanings.example: He comes out with the strangest things sometimes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 가끔 아주 이상한 말들을 내뱉곤 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop someone from doing something or to criticize them severely.
meanings.example: His suggestion was slapped down by the committee.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 제안은 위원회에 의해 단칼에 거절당했다 (억눌렸다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To divide something among a group of people.
meanings.example: The profits were shared out among the partners.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수익금은 파트너들에게 골고루 배분되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate a machine into its individual parts.
meanings.example: He stripped down the engine to see what was wrong.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 무엇이 잘못되었는지 보려고 엔진을 분해했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to tell someone to stop being angry or upset (British informal).
meanings.example: Keep your hair on! It was only a joke.
meanings.exampleKorean: 진정해! 그냥 농담이었어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To celebrate the beginning of a new year or period.
meanings.example: We went to a party to see in the New Year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 새해를 맞이하기 위해 파티에 갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Prevented from entering a building because the door is locked.
meanings.example: I'm locked out of my apartment because I lost my keys.
meanings.exampleKorean: 열쇠를 잃어버려서 아파트에 못 들어가고 있다 (문이 잠겼다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To attack someone suddenly and unexpectedly.
meanings.example: He was set upon by a gang of youths.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 청년 무리에게 습격을 당했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move towards someone in a threatening way; to discover the truth about something.
meanings.example: The dog came at me with its teeth bared.
meanings.exampleKorean: 강아지가 이빨을 드러내며 나에게 달려들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To meet someone, especially by arrangement.
meanings.example: Let's meet up for coffee next week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다음 주에 커피 한잔하러 만나자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something that is not needed so that only the most important parts remain.
meanings.example: The renovation stripped away the old wallpaper.
meanings.exampleKorean: 개보수 공사를 통해 낡은 벽지를 걷어냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To approach someone quietly and slowly; to increase slowly but significantly.
meanings.example: Don't creep up on me like that! / Prices are starting to creep up again.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그렇게 몰래 다가오지 마! / 가격이 다시 서서히 오르기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue to exist during a difficult period and be successful again after it.
meanings.example: The company managed to ride out the economic recession.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 경기 침체라는 고비를 잘 넘겼다 (견뎌냈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To invent something new by using your imagination.
meanings.example: I don't know how he dreams up these crazy ideas.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 어떻게 이런 기발한(미친) 아이디어들을 생각해내는지 모르겠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To publicly ask for something to happen; to need a particular quality or action.
meanings.example: The opposition party called for an early election. / This situation calls for patience.
meanings.exampleKorean: 야당은 조기 선거를 요구했다. / 이 상황은 인내심을 필요로 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tell someone your secret feelings and thoughts.
meanings.example: She poured out her heart to me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 나에게 속마음을 다 털어놓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail or go bankrupt.
meanings.example: Many small businesses went to the wall during the pandemic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 팬데믹 동안 많은 소상공인이 파산했다 (실패했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To become very angry or excited (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 버럭 화를 내다, 정신이 나가다, 몹시 흥분하다
meanings.example: My mom flipped out when she saw the mess in my room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 엄마는 내 방이 엉망인 것을 보고 버럭 화를 내셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a way that is different from most people.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 독자적인 길을 걷다, 남과 다른 방식으로 행동하다
meanings.example: He's always marched to a different drum, even as a child.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 어릴 때부터 항상 남들과 다른 독자적인 길을 걸어왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay at a distance; to delay or prevent an attack.
meanings.example: The two armies stood off for several days before the battle.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 군대는 전투 전 며칠 동안 서로 대치하며 서 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To disappear gradually into a background or a crowd.
meanings.example: The thief melted into the crowd and escaped.
meanings.exampleKorean: 도둑은 군중 속으로 녹아들어(섞여 들어가) 도망쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be provided together with something.
meanings.example: The phone comes with a charger and headphones.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 휴대폰에는 충전기와 헤드폰이 딸려 나온다 (포함되어 있다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very rich.
meanings.example: Since he won the lottery, he's been rolling in wealth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 복권에 당첨된 뒤로 그는 돈방석에 앉아 있다 (돈이 아주 많다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To become available or to be received.
meanings.example: No new information has come to hand yet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아직 입수된 새로운 정보는 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be present or available; to be active in a particular field or profession.
meanings.example: Will you be around tomorrow morning? / This brand has been around for decades.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내일 아침에 근처에 있을 거니? / 이 브랜드는 수십 년 동안 존재해 왔다 (활동해 왔다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a lot of experience of life.
meanings.example: He's been around, so he knows how to handle people like that.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 세상 물정에 밝아서 그런 사람들을 어떻게 다루어야 할지 안다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to follow rules or behave in a way that is expected of you.
meanings.example: The rest of the party quickly fell in line with the leader's decision.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당의 나머지 구성원들은 지도자의 결정에 빠르게 동조했다 (따랐다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To depend on someone for help; to try to influence someone by threatening them.
meanings.example: You can always lean on me if you have a problem. / The police leaned on him to get the information.
meanings.exampleKorean: 문제가 생기면 언제든 나에게 의지해도 돼. / 경찰은 정보를 얻기 위해 그에게 압력을 가했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very worried or frustrated about a situation.
meanings.example: I've been tearing my hair out trying to solve this problem.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 문제를 해결하려고 머리를 쥐어뜯으며 고민하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop moving or working correctly (of a machine or part of the body).
meanings.example: The car engine seized up because it ran out of oil.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오일이 떨어져서 자동차 엔진이 멈춰버렸다 (눌어붙었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop the supply of something like water or electricity.
meanings.example: Remember to shut off the gas before you leave.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나가기 전에 가스를 차단하는 것 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something more attractive or exciting (informal).
meanings.example: We decided to snazz up the living room with some new cushions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 새 쿠션들로 거실을 화려하게 꾸미기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To surround someone and prevent them from moving or escaping.
meanings.example: The army was hemmed in by mountains on both sides.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 군대는 양쪽 산들에 둘러싸여 (포위되어) 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have something as a main and necessary part or quality.
meanings.example: Happiness consists in being satisfied with what you have.
meanings.exampleKorean: 행복은 자신이 가진 것에 만족하는 데 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enjoy something very much, often in a way that other people find strange (informal).
meanings.example: He really gets off on power.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 권력을 휘두르는 것을 몹시 즐긴다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a way that is different from most people.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 독자적인 길을 걷다, 남과 다르게 행동하다
meanings.example: She has always marched to a different drummer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 항상 남들과 다른 독자적인 길을 걸어왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hang someone by the neck until they are dead; to hang something using string.
meanings.example: They were going to string up the outlaw without a trial.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 재판도 없이 그 무법자를 교수형에 처하려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start talking about or doing the most important part of something.
meanings.example: Enough small talk, let's get down to business.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잡담은 그만하고, 이제 본론으로 들어갑시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent someone from making progress in an activity.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 수렁에 빠지다, 지체되다, 꼼짝 못 하게 하다
meanings.example: We shouldn't get bogged down in small details.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 사소한 세부 사항에 얽매여 (수렁에 빠져) 지체되어서는 안 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to tell someone to say something that they seem reluctant to say (informal).
meanings.example: If you have something to say, just spit it out!
meanings.exampleKorean: 할 말이 있으면, 그냥 시원하게 말해봐!

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually fall asleep.
meanings.example: I was just beginning to drift off when the phone rang.
meanings.exampleKorean: 막 잠이 들려던 찰나에 전화가 울렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To say that you know from experience that something is true or good.
meanings.example: I can vouch for her honesty; she's a very reliable person.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 정직함은 제가 보증할 수 있습니다. 그녀는 아주 신뢰할 만한 사람이에요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consider something when you are making a plan.
meanings.example: You should allow for possible traffic delays when planning your trip.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여행 계획을 세울 때 가능한 교통 체증을 고려해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come from or have been born in a particular place.
meanings.example: They both hail from the same small town in Texas.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들 둘 다 텍사스의 같은 작은 마을 출신이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To support something that is in a difficult position; to prevent something from falling.
meanings.example: The government is trying to prop up the local economy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 지역 경제를 지지하려(유지시키려) 노력하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay strong during a difficult period (informal).
meanings.example: It was a hard year, but they managed to tough it out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 힘든 한 해였지만, 그들은 꿋꿋이 버텨냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To want someone or something very much.
meanings.example: After months of traveling, she was aching for home.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몇 달간의 여행 끝에 그녀는 집을 간절히 그리워하고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To result in a particular total or effect.
meanings.example: The small savings each week will soon add up to a significant amount. / His behavior adds up to a total lack of respect.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매주 조금씩 아끼는 돈이 곧 큰 금액이 될 것이다. / 그의 행동은 결국 전혀 존중하지 않는다는 뜻이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a room or building for a short time; to go out with someone socially.
meanings.example: Mr. Kim has just stepped out for a moment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 김 선생님은 지금 막 잠시 자리를 비우셨습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attack someone who has attacked you; to try to control a feeling.
meanings.example: The small company is fighting back against the larger competitors. / She fought back her tears.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 작은 회사는 거대 경쟁사에 맞서 반격하고 있다. / 그녀는 눈물을 억눌렀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To point a weapon at someone or something; to intend to achieve something.
meanings.example: The new magazine is aimed at teenagers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 잡지는 십 대들을 겨냥하고 있다 (대상으로 하고 있다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To experience a difficult or unpleasant situation; to examine something carefully.
meanings.example: He's going through a very difficult time right now. / Let's go through the report together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 지금 매우 힘든 시간을 보내고(겪고) 있다. / 보고서를 함께 검토해봅시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak or write to someone again later, especially to give a piece of information.
meanings.example: I'll find out and get back to you as soon as possible.
meanings.exampleKorean: 알아보고 가능한 한 빨리 다시 연락 드릴게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something very messy; to cause a lot of trouble or change in someone's life.
meanings.example: The burglars turned the house upside down. / Moving to a new country turned her life upside down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 도둑들이 집안을 완전히 뒤집어 놓았다 (엉망으로 만들었다). / 새로운 나라로 이사한 것이 그녀의 삶을 완전히 바꿔 놓았다 (뒤흔들었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To succeed in stopping someone or something from attacking you or making you ill.
meanings.example: The body's immune system fights off infections.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몸의 면역 체계는 감염을 물리친다 (이겨낸다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to get something by hinting at it rather than asking for it directly.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 은근히 노리다, 떠보다, 교묘히 얻으려 하다
meanings.example: He's clearly angling for an invitation to the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 분명히 파티 초대를 은근히 노리고 있다 (초대받고 싶어 떠보고 있다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To achieve something, such as a victory or a particular score.
meanings.example: The team notched up another win last night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 팀은 어젯밤에 또 한 번의 승리를 달성했다 (기록했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause something to exist or happen.
meanings.example: The crisis called forth a spirit of unity among the people.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 위기는 사람들 사이에 단결의 정신을 불러일으켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To write something down; to state officially what should happen.
meanings.example: The rules are clearly set down in the handbook.
meanings.exampleKorean: 규칙들이 안내서에 명확히 규정되어 (기록되어) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become covered with clouds; to look sad or angry.
meanings.example: The sky suddenly clouded over. / Her face clouded over when she heard the news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하늘에 갑자기 구름이 끼었다. / 소식을 듣자 그녀의 표정이 어두워졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sell something at an auction.
meanings.example: The family decided to auction off their old furniture.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 가족은 낡은 가구들을 경매로 팔아치우기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave or go somewhere quickly (informal).
meanings.example: It's getting late; I think we'd better make tracks for home.
meanings.exampleKorean: 늦었네. 이제 서둘러 집으로 가는 게 좋겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop supporting someone or something; to fail to do something you promised.
meanings.example: He bailed out on me at the last minute.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 마지막 순간에 나를 저버리고 (약속을 어기고) 도망쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To value an asset based on its current market price.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 시가 평가를 하다, 시장 가격으로 평가하다
meanings.example: The bank had to mark its assets to market.
meanings.exampleKorean: 은행은 자산을 시장 가격으로 재평가해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To invite someone to come into your house.
meanings.example: She's at the door; why don't you ask her in?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 문앞에 있는데, 안으로 들어오라고 하지 그래요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To rely on something happening or someone doing something.
meanings.example: I wouldn't bank on the weather staying fine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 날씨가 계속 좋을 거라고 너무 믿지는 마세요 (기대를 걸지 마세요).

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare to fight or compete with someone.
meanings.example: The two candidates will face off in a televised debate tonight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 후보는 오늘 밤 TV 토론에서 대결할 예정이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go from being a complete unknown to a famous or successful person in a very short time.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 밑바닥에서 영웅이 되다, 단숨에 대성공을 거두다
meanings.example: He went from zero to hero after his first app became a global hit.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 첫 번째 앱이 세계적인 히트를 치면서 그는 단숨에 밑바닥에서 영웅이 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become calm after being very angry or excited.
meanings.example: Come on, simmer down and let's talk about this reasonably.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자, 진정하고 이 일에 대해 이성적으로 이야기해봅시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give a reason that makes a mistake or a problem seem less serious.
meanings.example: He tried to explain away the missing money, but no one believed him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 사라진 돈에 대해 해명하려 했지만, 아무도 그를 믿지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: In a very bad situation and certain to fail or die.
meanings.example: If the police find us, we're done for.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰이 우릴 찾아내면 우린 끝장이야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To study a subject very hard, especially for an exam (British informal).
meanings.example: I need to swot up on my history before the test tomorrow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내일 시험 전에 역사 공부를 열심히 해야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make an opponent feel less confident by behaving in a very confident or threatening way.
meanings.example: The champion tried to psych out his opponent before the match.
meanings.exampleKorean: 챔피언은 경기 전에 상대의 기선을 제압하려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move quickly towards someone or something in a determined or threatening way.
meanings.example: The storm was bearing down on the island.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭풍이 섬을 향해 무섭게 돌진하고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce something quickly and without much care, especially by typing it.
meanings.example: I'll just bash out a quick report before I leave.
meanings.exampleKorean: 떠나기 전에 보고서를 대충 빨리 하나 써낼게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start a discussion, a series of events, etc.
meanings.example: Who would like to lead off the discussion?
meanings.exampleKorean: 누가 먼저 토론을 시작해 보시겠습니까?

meanings.englishDefinition: To go away; used to tell someone to go away rudely.
meanings.example: Clear off, you lot! This is private property.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너희들 다 저리 가! 여기는 사유지야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To exert power or authority over someone in a forceful way.
meanings.example: The principal is really laying the smack down on students who skip class.
meanings.exampleKorean: 교장 선생님은 수업을 빼먹는 학생들에게 정말 본때를 보여주고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To defeat someone in a competition.
meanings.example: Our company managed to beat out the competition for the contract.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 회사는 경쟁사들을 물리치고 그 계약을 따내는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce the number of possibilities or choices.
meanings.example: We managed to narrow down the list of candidates to three.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 후보자 명단을 3명으로 좁히는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be completely under someone's control or influence.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~의 마음대로 되다, 고분고분해지다, 시키는 대로 하다
meanings.example: Within a week, she had the whole class eating out of her hand.
meanings.exampleKorean: 일주일 만에 그녀는 반 전체 아이들이 자신의 말에 고분고분하게 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go to bed later than usual.
meanings.example: I stayed up late last night to finish my project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 어제 프로젝트를 끝내려고 늦게까지 자지 않고 깨어 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly show a lot of interest in an idea or opportunity.
meanings.example: The media seized upon the scandal immediately.
meanings.exampleKorean: 언론은 즉시 그 스캔들을 덥석 물어 중요하게 다루었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing a task or job.
meanings.example: We set to work as soon as the materials arrived.
meanings.exampleKorean: 재료가 도착하자마자 우리는 일에 착수했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spill over the side of a pot; to reach a point where feelings cannot be controlled.
meanings.example: The tension between the two groups finally boiled over into violence.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 집단 사이의 긴장감이 결국 폭력 사태로 폭발했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pass slowly (especially of time).
meanings.example: As the afternoon wore on, I began to feel tired.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오후 시간이 지루하게 흐르면서 나는 피곤함을 느끼기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get an advantage over someone by doing something before they do.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 기선을 제압하다, 허를 찌르다, 먼저 손을 쓰다
meanings.example: Our company managed to get the drop on the competition by launching our product early.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 회사는 제품을 일찍 출시함으로써 경쟁사보다 기선을 제압할 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To perform in an exaggerated way.
meanings.example: He really hammed up the role for the comedy sketch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 코미디 스케치를 위해 그 역할을 정말 과장되게 연기했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become quieter or less busy.
meanings.example: The kids finally quieted down and went to sleep.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들이 마침내 조용해졌고 잠이 들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce the size or amount of something by removing unimportant parts.
meanings.example: The company had to pare down its workforce to save money.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 비용을 절감하기 위해 인력을 감축해야(줄여야) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To believe that someone has the same bad qualities as other people in their group.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 싸잡아 비난하다, 도마에 올리다, 같은 부류로 취급하다
meanings.example: Just because his brother is a criminal, don't tar him with the same brush.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 형이 범죄자라고 해서 그까지 싸잡아 비난하지는 마라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put a mark next to an item on a list to show that it has been dealt with; to show the limits of an area.
meanings.example: I've marked off all the names of the people who have paid.
meanings.exampleKorean: 돈을 낸 사람들의 명단을 모두 표시하여 지웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do gentle exercise after a period of intense physical activity.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 정리 운동을 하다, 마무리 운동을 하다
meanings.example: It's important to warm down after running a marathon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마라톤을 뛴 후에는 마무리 운동을 하는 것이 중요하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be extremely happy.
meanings.example: He jumped for joy when he heard the news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그 소식을 듣고 기뻐서 날뛰었다 (펄쩍펄쩍 뛰며 좋아했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To complete the last part of something.
meanings.example: I'll just finish up this report and then I can go.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 보고서만 마무리하고 바로 갈게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give something to a younger person or to someone who comes after you.
meanings.example: This recipe has been passed down through generations.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 레시피는 대대로 전수되어 내려온 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To betray someone, especially for your own advantage.
meanings.example: He felt that his partner had sold him down the river.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 동업자가 자신을 배신했다고 느꼈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become very interested in or attached to something or someone.
meanings.example: She glommed onto the idea immediately.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 즉시 그 아이디어에 매료되었다 (자기 것으로 만들었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To sit and relax while watching people and events happen around you.
meanings.example: I like to sit in a cafe and just watch the world go by.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 카페에 앉아 그냥 세상 구경하는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To annoy or irritate someone (British informal).
meanings.example: His constant complaining is really starting to get up my nose.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 끊임없는 불평이 정말 나를 짜증 나게 하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remind someone of an embarrassing mistake or defeat.
meanings.example: I know I failed the test; you don't have to rub it in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시험 떨어진 거 알아. 자꾸 상기시켜서(헤집어서) 기분 나쁘게 하지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To kill someone as a punishment for a crime.
meanings.example: He was put to death for his crimes in 1995.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 1995년에 저지른 죄로 사형에 처해졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place very quickly; to open your eyes very wide in surprise (American informal).
meanings.example: We had to bug out before the storm hit. / His eyes bugged out when he saw the bill.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭풍이 오기 전에 우리는 서둘러 떠나야 했다. / 그는 청구서를 보고 눈이 휘둥그레졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat someone badly or to give them a lot of unpleasant work.
meanings.example: She's always dumping her problems on me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 항상 자신의 문제들을 나에게 떠넘긴다 (퍼붓는다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To survive by eating only things that you can grow, hunt, or gather in the wild.
meanings.example: The survivors had to learn how to live off the land.
meanings.exampleKorean: 생존자들은 땅에서 나는 것만으로 살아가는(자급자족하는) 법을 배워야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become covered with ice.
meanings.example: The lake has iced over completely.
meanings.exampleKorean: 호수가 완전히 얼어붙었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a mistake; to fail at something (American informal).
meanings.example: I really flubbed up my presentation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나 정말 발표를 완전히 망쳐버렸어 (실수했어).

meanings.englishDefinition: To communicate with someone by calling them or writing to them.
meanings.example: I'll get in touch with you as soon as I have any news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 소식이 있는 대로 곧 연락드릴게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone feel too proud or confident.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 우쭐하게 만들다, 자만하게 하다, (술 기운이) 오르다
meanings.example: Don't let this success go to your head.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이번 성공으로 너무 우쭐해하지 마세요 (자만하지 마세요).

meanings.englishDefinition: To tell people what they must do in a very firm and authoritative way.
meanings.example: My father really laid down the law about when I should be home.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아버지는 내가 언제까지 집에 와야 하는지에 대해 아주 단호하게 명령하셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone make a mistake; to fall or make someone fall.
meanings.example: The interviewer tried to trip him up with some tricky questions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 면접관은 몇몇 까다로운 질문들로 그가 실수하게 만들려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be able to persuade someone to do anything you want.
meanings.example: She knows exactly how to wrap her parents around her little finger.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 부모님을 어떻게 자기 마음대로 조종하는지 정확히 알고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To surround someone or something by moving closer from all sides.
meanings.example: The enemy troops were closing in on the city.
meanings.exampleKorean: 적군이 도시를 향해 포위하며 좁혀 들어오고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a lot of difficult or unpleasant experiences.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 모진 시련을 겪다, 고생을 많이 하다
meanings.example: He's really been through the mill lately with his health and work problems.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 최근 건강과 직장 문제로 정말 모진 시련을 겪어 왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep something at a low level; to keep a job for a period of time.
meanings.example: The government is trying to hold down inflation. / It's hard to hold down a job when you're ill.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 인플레이션을 억제하려 노력하고 있다. / 아플 때는 직장 생활을 유지하기가 힘들다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sell something quickly and at a low price, often because you need money.
meanings.example: The company decided to sell off its non-core businesses.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 비핵심 사업 부문들을 매각하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place, especially to go to another country for work or war; to send goods somewhere.
meanings.example: The troops are ready to ship out tomorrow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군인들은 내일 파견을 떠날 준비가 되어 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause something to be thought about in a completely new and different way.
meanings.example: The new evidence turned the traditional theory on its head.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 증거는 전통적인 이론을 완전히 뒤엎었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to be the best or achieve the highest possible result.
meanings.example: You've trained hard, now go for the gold!
meanings.exampleKorean: 열심히 훈련했으니, 이제 최고를 향해 달려가 봐 (우승을 노려봐)!

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail completely and embarrassingly.
meanings.example: His business plan fell on its face within a month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 사업 계획은 한 달 만에 완전히 실패했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tell everyone about something that you are very proud of or happy about.
meanings.example: I'm so happy I want to shout it from the rooftops!
meanings.exampleKorean: 너무 행복해서 동네방네 떠들고 싶어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To think that someone is less important than you.
meanings.example: She looks down on people who haven't been to university.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 대학에 가지 않은 사람들을 무시한다 (얕본다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly become angry; to suddenly become worse (of a disease or violence).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 확 타오르다, 갑자기 화내다, (병 등이) 재발하다
meanings.example: He flares up at the slightest criticism. / Violence has flared up again in the region.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아주 작은 비판에도 버럭 화를 낸다. / 그 지역에 다시 폭력 사태가 발생했다 (확 타올랐다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be accepted into a group after being excluded or ignored for a long time.
meanings.example: After years in the political wilderness, he has finally come in from the cold.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수년간의 정치적 공백기 끝에, 그는 마침내 다시 (주류 사회로) 받아들여졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wear very fashionable or expensive clothes in order to impress people (informal).
meanings.example: She arrived at the party dressed to kill.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 파티에 아주 화려하게 차려입고 나타났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase the size, amount, or importance of something.
meanings.example: The company is scaling up production to meet the demand.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 수요를 맞추기 위해 생산 규모를 확대하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce the size, amount, or importance of something.
meanings.example: They had to scale down their wedding plans because of the budget.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 예산 때문에 결혼식 계획을 축소해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very excited or full of energy (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 너무 흥분하다, 에너지가 넘치다, 가만히 못 있다
meanings.example: The kids have been bouncing off the walls all day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들이 하루 종일 에너지가 넘쳐서 가만히 있질 못한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To know nothing about a subject (American informal).
meanings.example: He doesn't know beans about computers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 컴퓨터에 대해 쥐뿔도 모른다 (전혀 모른다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone take an official oath when they start a new job.
meanings.example: The new president will be sworn in next month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 대통령은 다음 달에 선서하고 취임할 예정이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To officially start a meeting or session.
meanings.example: The chairman called the meeting to order at 10 AM.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의장은 오전 10시에 개회를 선언했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To scold or criticize someone severely for a past mistake.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 호되게 꾸짖다, 과거의 일을 들춰 비난하다
meanings.example: He was raked over the coals for his handle of the situation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 상황을 처리한 방식 때문에 호되게 비난을 받았다 (호되게 꾸지람을 들었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be responsible to someone and have to explain your actions to them.
meanings.example: In this job, you only answer to the CEO.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 일에서 당신은 오직 CEO에게만 책임을 지면 된다 (CEO의 지휘만 받는다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To participate in a conference call; to adjust something precisely.
meanings.example: I'll be dialing in from New York for the meeting. / He finally got his racing car dialed in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 뉴욕에서 회의에 전화로 참석할 것이다. / 그는 마침내 자신의 경주용 차를 세밀하게 조정했다 (최적화했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a formal meeting with someone; to experience a particular reaction.
meanings.example: The president will meet with senior officials today. / The proposal met with strong opposition.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대통령은 오늘 고위 관리들과 만날 예정이다. / 그 제안은 강력한 반대에 부딪혔다 (마주했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To listen to, look at, or experience something with great interest and enjoyment.
meanings.example: They sat on the shore, drinking in the beautiful sunset.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 해변에 앉아 아름다운 일몰을 즐겼다 (온몸으로 만끽했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To examine something quickly.
meanings.example: I'll look through my notes before the exam.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시험 전에 내 노트를 한번 훑어볼 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: If a quality or ability runs in the family, many members of the family have it.
meanings.example: Musical talent seems to run in the family.
meanings.exampleKorean: 음악적 재능은 집안 내력인 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become less strong or severe; to slow down.
meanings.example: The rain is beginning to ease up at last.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마침내 비가 잦아들기 (진정되기) 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find out what is happening with something that should have been done (British informal).
meanings.example: I'll chase up that report for you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 보고서가 어떻게 됐는지 내가 한번 알아볼게 (재촉해볼게).

meanings.englishDefinition: To tidy a place by removing things you do not want; to leave a place quickly.
meanings.example: We need to clear out the garage this weekend. / I'm clearing out of here tomorrow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이번 주말에 차고를 청소해야(치워야) 해. / 난 내일 여기서 떠날 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a car or its engine more powerful (informal).
meanings.example: He's spent a lot of money souping up his old car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 낡은 차의 성능을 높이느라(개조하느라) 많은 돈을 썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To raise a glass and drink to wish someone success or happiness.
meanings.example: Let's drink to your new job!
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신의 새 직장을 위해 건배합시다!

meanings.englishDefinition: To be burned very badly.
meanings.example: I left the toast in too long and it burned to a crisp.
meanings.exampleKorean: 토스트를 너무 오래 뒀더니 바짝 타버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wash something using a hose.
meanings.example: I need to hose down the car before we go.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가기 전에 차를 물로 좀 씻어야겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To write off a debt that is not expected to be paid.
meanings.example: The bank had to charge off millions in bad loans.
meanings.exampleKorean: 은행은 수백만 달러의 부실 대출을 손실 처리해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be asking for money or help, especially when you do not deserve it.
meanings.example: He always has his hand out for more money.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 더 많은 돈을 위해 손을 벌린다 (구걸한다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize someone or something (Australian/New Zealand informal).
meanings.example: Stop bagging out his new haircut; it looks fine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 새 머리 모양을 비웃지 마. 괜찮아 보여.

meanings.englishDefinition: To buy something that you owned before.
meanings.example: The company decided to buy back its shares from the investors.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 투자자들로부터 주식을 다시 사들이기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To earn a lot of money very easily (informal).
meanings.example: With their new product, the company is really raking it in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 신제품 덕분에 그 회사는 정말 돈을 긁어모으고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide information that makes something easier to understand.
meanings.example: New evidence has shed light upon the mysterious disappearance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 증거가 그 미스터리한 실종 사건을 밝혀주었다 (설명해주었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To sit down and relax (informal).
meanings.example: Come in and take a load off; you look exhausted.
meanings.exampleKorean: 들어와서 좀 편히 쉬어. 정말 지쳐 보이는구나.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue for a longer time than is necessary or attractive.
meanings.example: The meeting dragged on for three hours.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의가 3시간 동안 지루하게 계속되었다 (끌었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail to take advantage of an opportunity.
meanings.example: I can't believe you passed up the chance to go to Hawaii.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하와이에 갈 기회를 놓쳤다니(거절했다니) 믿을 수가 없어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To repair a relationship after an argument; to repair something quickly and temporarily.
meanings.example: They finally managed to patch up their differences. / He patched up the leaky pipe.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 마침내 서로의 차이점(갈등)을 수습하고 화해했다. / 그는 새는 파이프를 임시로 고쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To step to one side to let someone pass; to leave a job so that someone else can do it.
meanings.example: The crowd stood aside to let the ambulance through.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군중들은 앰뷸런스가 통과할 수 있도록 옆으로 비켜났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use someone or something as a source of the money or food you need to live.
meanings.example: He's thirty and still living off his parents.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 서른 살인데 여전히 부모님께 의지해 살고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very eager to do something, especially to please someone.
meanings.example: The staff were falling over themselves to help the famous actor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 직원들은 그 유명한 배우를 돕기 위해 지나칠 정도로 애를 쓰고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To touch or move something with your hands in a nervous or bored way; to make small changes to something.
meanings.example: Stop fiddling with your pen! / He spent the afternoon fiddling with the engine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 펜 좀 그만 만지작거려! / 그는 오후 내내 엔진을 만지작거리며 조작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become friends with someone (British informal).
meanings.example: They palled up while they were on holiday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 휴가 동안 서로 친해졌다 (친구가 되었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To wash your hands thoroughly before a medical operation; to dress in formal or smart clothes (British informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: (수술 전) 손을 깨끗이 씻다, 말끔히 단장하다
meanings.example: The doctor is scrubbing up now. / You scrub up well!
meanings.exampleKorean: 의사가 지금 손을 씻고 (수술 준비를 하고) 있다. / 너 오늘 정말 말끔히 단장했구나 (멋있구나)!

meanings.englishDefinition: To hang clothes outside to dry; to die (British informal).
meanings.example: I'll go and peg out the washing. / I'm so tired, I'm about to peg out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가서 빨래 좀 널게요. / 너무 피곤해서 쓰러질 것(죽을 것) 같아요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cross something out with an X; to exclude or eliminate.
meanings.example: Just X out the names of the people who aren't coming.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오지 않는 사람들의 이름 위에 X표를 그으세요 (지우세요).

meanings.englishDefinition: To choose one person or thing from a group; to recognize someone or something in a group.
meanings.example: She picked out a beautiful dress for the wedding. / See if you can pick out your father in this old photo.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 결혼식을 위해 아름다운 드레스를 골랐다. / 이 오래된 사진 속에서 아버지를 알아볼 수 있는지 봐봐.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue doing something in a determined way.
meanings.example: Despite the rain, the hikers decided to press on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비가 오는데도 불구하고, 하이킹하는 사람들은 계속해서 나아가기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrange for something to happen on a particular date, knowing the arrangement might be changed later.
meanings.example: Let's pencil in a meeting for next Tuesday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다음 주 화요일로 일단 회의 일정을 잡아둡시다 (임시로 정해둡시다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To improve a skill by practicing it.
meanings.example: I need to polish up my French before I go to Paris.
meanings.exampleKorean: 파리에 가기 전에 프랑스어 실력을 좀 다듬어야(닦아야) 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually get more points, profits, etc.
meanings.example: The company racked up record profits last year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 지난해 기록적인 수익을 달성했다 (올렸다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be forgotten or ignored; to no longer be noticed by people.
meanings.example: After his last movie flopped, the actor seemed to drop off the radar.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마지막 영화가 실패한 뒤로, 그 배우는 대중의 관심 밖으로 사라진 듯했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To read a lot about a particular subject in order to learn about it.
meanings.example: I need to read up on the history of the company before the interview.
meanings.exampleKorean: 면접 전에 그 회사의 역사에 대해 좀 공부해야(조사해야) 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move in front of someone or something else.
meanings.example: The runner managed to pull ahead in the final lap.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 주자는 마지막 바퀴에서 앞서 나가는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce a printed copy of a document from a computer.
meanings.example: Could you print out a copy of the agenda for me?
meanings.exampleKorean: 의제 복사본 하나만 출력해 주시겠어요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To pull something towards you; to attract people to a business.
meanings.example: The fisherman reeled in a massive salmon. / The new advertising campaign helped reel in many new customers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어부는 거대한 연어를 끌어올렸다. / 새로운 광고 캠페인이 많은 신규 고객을 유치하는 데 도움을 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gain a lot of weight (informal).
meanings.example: He's really piled on the pounds since he stopped exercising.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 운동을 그만둔 뒤로 정말 살이 많이 쪘다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue doing something that is difficult or boring.
meanings.example: It was a very long book, but I decided to plow on until the end.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아주 두꺼운 책이었지만, 나는 끝까지 꿋꿋이 읽어나가기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something start burning.
meanings.example: Vandals set fire to the old warehouse.
meanings.exampleKorean: 파괴범들이 낡은 창고에 불을 질렀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause something to start burning; to ignite passion or interest in someone.
meanings.example: The protesters set fire to several cars in the street. / Her speech really set fire to the audience's imagination.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시위대들이 거리의 차 몇 대에 불을 질렀다. / 그녀의 연설은 청중들의 상상력을 정말로 자극했다 (불을 지폈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop at the side of the road (of a vehicle); to arrive at a station (of a train).
meanings.example: The car pulled in at the gas station. / The train is just pulling in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 차는 주유소에 멈춰 섰다. / 기차가 지금 막 역으로 들어오고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To say or repeat something quickly and easily.
meanings.example: She reeled off the names of all the kings and queens of England.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 영국의 모든 왕과 왕비의 이름을 거침없이 말해 나갔다 (술술 읊었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To become exciting, active, or lively.
meanings.example: The city really comes to life at night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 도시는 밤이 되면 정말 활기를 띤다 (생기가 돈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually learn to accept a difficult or unpleasant situation.
meanings.example: It took her a long time to come to terms with her father's death.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 아버지의 죽음을 받아들이는 데는 오랜 시간이 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To invest profits back into a business.
meanings.example: The company plows back most of its profits into research and development.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 이익의 대부분을 연구 개발에 재투자한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be suitable for a particular purpose.
meanings.example: The novel's structure lends itself well to a film adaptation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 소설의 구조는 영화화하기에 아주 적합하다 (좋다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to understand or deal with a difficult situation or problem.
meanings.example: The government is finally coming to grips with the housing crisis.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부가 마침내 주택 위기 문제를 본격적으로 해결하려 애쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To look at something quickly without reading it carefully.
meanings.example: She glanced over the report before the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 회의 전에 보고서를 대충 훑어보았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To step to one side; to leave a job so that someone else can do it.
meanings.example: It's time for the older generation to step aside.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이제 기성세대가 물러나야 할 때이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a silly or humorous way.
meanings.example: Stop clowning around and get to work!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그만 까불고(장난치고) 이제 일 시작해!

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish something that takes a long time and is boring or difficult.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 어렵게 헤쳐나가다, (지루한 것을) 끈기 있게 끝내다
meanings.example: I spent the whole weekend ploughing through my revision.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 주말 내내 복습을 끝내기 위해 지루한 공부를 끈기 있게 해 나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tear something into small pieces.
meanings.example: He ripped up the letter and threw it in the bin.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 편지를 갈갈이 찢어서 휴지통에 던져버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To weigh a particular amount.
meanings.example: The champion tipped the scales at 200 pounds.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 챔피언은 몸무게가 200파운드였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decide to join a person or group and support them.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~와 뜻을 같이하다, 행동을 함께하다, 한패가 되다
meanings.example: He decided to throw in with the opposition party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 야당과 뜻을 같이하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To succeed in staying alive or to help someone do this, after a serious illness or accident.
meanings.example: The doctors said he was lucky to pull through after the operation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의사들은 그가 수술 후에 고비를 넘긴(회복한) 것이 다행이라고 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start a process or a series of events.
meanings.example: The committee has set in motion a series of reforms.
meanings.exampleKorean: 위원회는 일련의 개혁안들을 가동하기(시작하기) 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discuss a problem with other people to try to find a solution.
meanings.example: If we put our heads together, I'm sure we can solve this.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리가 머리를 맞대고 의논한다면, 분명히 이 문제를 해결할 수 있을 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To survive or persevere through a difficult situation (informal).
meanings.example: Hang in there! Things will get better soon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조금만 더 버텨봐! 곧 좋아질 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent someone or something from moving or acting freely.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 가두다, 포위하다, 꼼짝 못 하게 하다
meanings.example: His car was boxed in by two other vehicles.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 차는 다른 두 차량 사이에 갇혀 꼼짝 못 하게 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place quietly or secretly, especially to avoid work (British informal).
meanings.example: He sloped off early so he wouldn't have to help with the washing up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 설거지를 돕지 않으려고 일찍 슬그머니 빠져나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get rid of something by giving it to someone else, often by deceiving them.
meanings.example: He tried to pawn off his old computer as a new one.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 낡은 컴퓨터를 새것인 양 속여서 떠넘기려(팔아치우려) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail when you are very close to finishing or succeeding.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 마지막 고비에서 실패하다, 다 된 밥에 재 뿌리다
meanings.example: The team played well but fell at the last hurdle in the final.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 팀은 잘 싸웠지만 결승전 막바지에 실패하고 말았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place and go somewhere else (literary/formal).
meanings.example: The explorers went forth into the unknown territory.
meanings.exampleKorean: 탐험가들은 미지의 영토를 향해 앞으로 나아갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrive at a place by chance.
meanings.example: A passing motorist happened along just after the accident.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사고 직후에 마침 운전자 한 명이 우연히 그곳을 지나가게 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be attractive, successful, or capable (informal).
meanings.example: She's smart and stylish; she really gets it going on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 똑똑하고 스타일도 좋아서 정말 매력이 넘친다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join together as a group in order to achieve something.
meanings.example: Local residents banded together to protest the new development.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지역 주민들은 새로운 개발에 항의하기 위해 단결했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something before someone else does it.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 기선을 제압하다, 선수 치다, 먼저 손을 쓰다
meanings.example: I was going to buy that car, but someone beat me to the punch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 그 차를 사려고 했는데, 누군가 선수 쳐서 (먼저 사버려서) 놓쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To write something quickly on a piece of paper so that you remember it.
meanings.example: Let me just jot down your phone number.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신의 전화번호를 메모해(적어)둘게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sing or play a musical instrument very loudly.
meanings.example: She belted out her hit song to the cheering crowd.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 환호하는 군중들에게 자신의 히트곡을 우렁차게 불러주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To look after someone; to like or love someone.
meanings.example: He's spent years caring for his elderly mother. / I don't much care for modern art.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 연세 드신 어머니를 돌보며 수년을 보냈다. / 나는 현대 미술을 그리 좋아하지 않는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pull something into pieces; to make someone very unhappy; to separate people who love each other.
meanings.example: The dog tore the cushion apart. / It tears me apart to see you so sad.
meanings.exampleKorean: 강아지가 쿠션을 갈갈이 찢어 놓았다. / 네가 그렇게 슬퍼하는 걸 보니 내 마음이 찢어지는구나 (너무 아프구나).

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat someone badly by always making them do what you want; to defeat someone easily.
meanings.example: If you don't stand up for yourself, people will walk all over you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 네 권리를 주장하지 않으면, 사람들이 너를 함부로 대할(만만하게 볼) 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize someone's ideas or work by looking at the small details.
meanings.example: Critics picked the movie's plot apart.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비평가들은 그 영화의 줄거리를 낱낱이 분석하며 비판했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To search an area to find information or something you want.
meanings.example: We should scout out a good place for the picnic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 소풍 가기에 좋은 장소가 있는지 미리 좀 찾아봐야겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become used to a new situation or to make a new process start to work effectively.
meanings.example: It will take a few weeks for the new system to bed in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 시스템이 자리를 잡는 데는 몇 주가 걸릴 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate something into smaller parts or groups.
meanings.example: They divided up the profits equally.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 수익금을 똑같이 나누었다 (분배했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be mentally sound or intelligent (usually used in the negative).
meanings.example: Sometimes I wonder if he's playing with a full deck.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가끔은 그가 제정신인지 의심스러울 때가 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly start laughing loudly.
meanings.example: The whole class burst out laughing when the teacher made a joke.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님이 농담을 하시자 반 전체가 웃음보를 터뜨렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tell someone about something that has been worrying you or making you feel guilty.
meanings.example: I had to get it off my chest and tell her how I really felt.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 내 진짜 감정을 그녀에게 털어놓아 속이 시원해져야만 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To think about what is likely to happen in the future and plan for it.
meanings.example: We need to look ahead and decide what our next step will be.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 앞을 내다보고 우리의 다음 단계가 무엇이 될지 결정해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave the ground (of a spacecraft).
meanings.example: The rocket is scheduled to blast off at 10 AM.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 로켓은 오전 10시에 발사될 예정이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hurry up (British informal).
meanings.example: Get your skates on or we'll be late for the movie!
meanings.exampleKorean: 서둘러! 안 그러면 영화에 늦겠어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause something to happen, especially something unpleasant.
meanings.example: Stress can bring on a variety of health problems.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스트레스는 다양한 건강 문제를 초래할(일으킬) 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spread out over a wide area.
meanings.example: The search party fanned out across the field.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수색대는 들판을 가로질러 사방으로 퍼졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly forget something; to cover or remove something so it cannot be seen.
meanings.example: I just blanked out during the exam and couldn't remember anything. / The names in the document were blanked out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시험 도중에 갑자기 머리가 멍해져서 아무것도 기억이 안 났다. / 문서의 이름들이 지워져 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone feel confused or annoyed (British informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~를 아주 짜증 나게 하다, 혼란스럽게 하다
meanings.example: This loud music is doing my head in!
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 시끄러운 음악 때문에 정말 짜증 나 죽겠어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To become dry or to make something dry by wiping it.
meanings.example: He dried himself off with a large towel after the swim.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 수영 후에 큰 수건으로 몸의 물기를 닦았다 (말렸다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To become extremely relaxed and happy (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 황홀경에 빠지다, 더할 나위 없이 행복해지다
meanings.example: I spent the whole afternoon blissing out on the beach.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 오후 내내 해변에서 더할 나위 없는 행복에 푹 빠져 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gather things together from different places.
meanings.example: Could you collect up all the empty glasses?
meanings.exampleKorean: 빈 유리잔들을 다 좀 모아 주겠니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To be too unpleasant or frightening to imagine.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 상상조차 하기 싫다, 생각만 해도 끔찍하다
meanings.example: What would happen if we lost our jobs? It doesn't bear thinking about.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리가 직장을 잃으면 어떻게 될까? 상상조차 하기 싫다 (생각만 해도 끔찍하다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To connect two or more people or things; to join together in a group.
meanings.example: The two companies have linked up to develop a new engine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 회사는 새로운 엔진을 개발하기 위해 제휴했다 (합류했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To depend entirely on something.
meanings.example: The success of the project hinges on the cooperation of all departments.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프로젝트의 성공은 모든 부서의 협력에 달려있다 (협력이 관건이다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something because you have to, without any enthusiasm or real effort.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 시늉만 하다, 마지못해 하다, 형식적으로 하다
meanings.example: He's not really interested in the job; he's just going through the motions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그 일에 전혀 관심이 없다. 그냥 형식적으로 시늉만 하고 있을 뿐이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do gentle exercises to stretch your muscles before doing a sport or physical activity.
meanings.example: The athletes were limbering up before the race.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선수들이 경주 전에 준비 운동을 하며 몸을 풀고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something helpful for someone, in the hope that they will do something helpful for you in return.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 서로 돕다, 상부상조하다, (내 부탁을 들어주면) 보답하다
meanings.example: You help me with this report, and I'll scratch your back when you need a favor later.
meanings.exampleKorean: 네가 이 보고서를 도와주면, 나중에 네가 부탁할 일이 있을 때 내가 도와줄게 (서로 돕자).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop light or sound from reaching a place; to try not to think about an unpleasant memory or feeling.
meanings.example: These thick curtains block out the sunlight. / She tried to block out the memories of the accident.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 두꺼운 커튼들이 햇빛을 차단해준다. / 그녀는 사고의 기억들을 지우려(생각하지 않으려) 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do everything for someone so that they do not have to do anything for themselves.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 지극정성으로 보살피다, 손발이 되어 시중을 들다
meanings.example: His wife waited on him hand and foot while he was ill.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 아픈 동안 그의 아내는 지극정성으로 그를 보살폈다 (그의 손발이 되어 시중을 들었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To become equal or regular; to make something equal or regular.
meanings.example: Prices should even out over the next few months.
meanings.exampleKorean: 물가는 앞으로 몇 달에 걸쳐 균형을 맞추게 (진정되게) 될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone more likely to do what you want by being very nice to them.
meanings.example: He took his boss out to dinner to soften him up before asking for a raise.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 월급 인상을 요구하기 전에 상사의 마음을 누그러뜨리려고 저녁 식사에 초대했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fill a hole or a narrow space so that nothing can pass through it.
meanings.example: The fallen leaves have blocked up the drain.
meanings.exampleKorean: 낙엽들이 배수구를 완전히 막아버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move very slowly.
meanings.example: The old truck was poking along at 20 miles per hour.
meanings.exampleKorean: 낡은 트럭이 시속 20마일로 느릿느릿 가고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent someone from leaving a place by closing the door.
meanings.example: The valley is shut in by high mountains.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 계곡은 높은 산들로 에워싸여 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To allow someone to use your property in exchange for money.
meanings.example: He decided to rent out his apartment while he was traveling.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 여행하는 동안 자신의 아파트를 임대하기로(빌려주기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force someone to leave a place where they are hiding; to find out something that is being kept secret.
meanings.example: The police used tear gas to smoke out the gunman. / They hope this investigation will smoke out the truth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 최루탄을 사용하여 총을 든 범인을 몰아냈다. / 그들은 이번 조사가 진실을 밝혀내길(찾아내길) 바라고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To write or draw the main parts of something without including all the details.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 초안을 잡다, 대충 그리다, 개요를 설명하다
meanings.example: I've roughed out a schedule for the conference.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 컨퍼런스 일정의 초안을 대충 잡아 보았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To destroy something completely, especially a car.
meanings.example: His car was smashed up in the accident.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 차는 사고로 완전히 박살이 났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall or be sent down in large amounts.
meanings.example: Bombs rained down on the city. / Questions rained down on the speaker.
meanings.exampleKorean: 도시에 폭탄이 비 오듯 쏟아졌다. / 연사에게 질문이 쏟아져 나왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dry or clean something by rubbing it; to give someone a massage.
meanings.example: He rubbed down the horse after the race.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 경주 후에 말의 몸을 문질러 닦아주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide someone with special clothes or equipment.
meanings.example: They were rigged out in traditional costumes for the festival.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 축제를 위해 전통 의상을 차려입고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mention something unpleasant that happened in the past and that people would prefer to forget.
meanings.example: There's no need to rake up old arguments.
meanings.exampleKorean: 옛날 논쟁거리를 다시 들추어낼 필요는 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: If an event is rained off, it is stopped or cancelled because it is raining (British).
meanings.example: The cricket match was rained off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 크리켓 경기가 우천으로 중단되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare something or yourself for an activity.
meanings.example: Everyone, ready up! The game is about to start.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모두 준비해! 게임이 곧 시작될 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide a room, ship, etc., with all the furniture or equipment it needs.
meanings.example: The office has been fitted out with the latest technology.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 사무실은 최신 기술 설비를 갖추고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something rough; to treat someone in a violent way.
meanings.example: He was roughed up by some thugs in the alley.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 골목에서 깡패들에게 폭행을 당했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To seem much less serious or important when compared with someone or something else.
meanings.example: His problems pale in comparison with the ones you have.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 문제는 당신의 문제와 비교하면 아무것도 아니다 (무색해진다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To reach the lowest point and stop getting worse.
meanings.example: The recession seems to have bottomed out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경기 침체가 이제 바닥을 친 것으로 보인다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put a large quantity of things into a vehicle or container; to fill a computer's memory with data.
meanings.example: We loaded up the car with camping gear.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 캠핑 장비를 차에 가득 실었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move the top part of your body forwards and downwards.
meanings.example: She bent down to pick up the fallen keys.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 떨어진 열쇠를 줍기 위해 몸을 숙였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shoot continuously; to remove something by a powerful explosion or force.
meanings.example: The cannons blasted away at the fortress walls.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대포들이 요새 벽을 향해 계속 쏘아댔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tie a bandage around a wound; to unite people or things strongly.
meanings.example: The nurse bound up the soldier's wounds. / A common goal bound up the group.
meanings.exampleKorean: 간호사가 병사의 상처를 싸매주었다. / 공통의 목표가 그 집단을 하나로 결속시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: If something is blown down, it falls to the ground because of a strong wind.
meanings.example: The old oak tree was blown down during the storm.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 오래된 오크 나무가 폭풍 중에 바람에 쓰러졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide yourself or others with weapons.
meanings.example: The rebels began to arm up for the coming battle.
meanings.exampleKorean: 반군들은 다가올 전투를 위해 무장하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to burn strongly and brightly; to suddenly become very angry.
meanings.example: The fire suddenly blazed up when he added more wood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 나무를 더 넣자 불길이 갑자기 확 타올랐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wind something into a series of loops.
meanings.example: The sailor coiled up the rope on the deck.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선원이 갑판 위의 밧줄을 똘똘 말아 두었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be extremely beautiful or surprising.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 숨이 멎을 정도로 놀라게 하다, 감동시키다
meanings.example: The view from the top of the mountain took my breath away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 산 정상에서 바라본 풍경은 숨이 멎을 정도로 아름다웠다 (감동적이었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To succeed in understanding something difficult or strange.
meanings.example: I'm still trying to get my head around the new tax laws.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 여전히 새로운 세법을 이해하려 애쓰는 중이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lift something or someone with your hands or arms in a quick movement.
meanings.example: She scooped up the crying child and hugged him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 울고 있는 아이를 얼른 들어 올려서 안아주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something available to be used.
meanings.example: The new bypass will free up the city center from traffic jams.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 우회도로는 도심의 교통 체증을 해소하고 공간을 확보해 줄 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To obtain something, especially money, by moving with difficulty and effort.
meanings.example: We managed to scratch together enough money for the flight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 비행기 표를 살 돈을 간신히 긁어모았다 (어렵게 마련했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To wash your face and hands or change your clothes to feel more comfortable.
meanings.example: Give me a minute to freshen up before we go out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나가기 전에 세수 좀 하고 새로 단장할 시간 좀 줘.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give the same excuses, facts, or stories that have been used many times before.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 진부한 것을 내놓다, (똑같은 이야기를) 또 꺼내다
meanings.example: The politician trotted out the usual excuses for the tax increase.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인은 증세에 대해 늘 하던 진부한 변명을 또 꺼내 놓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To punish someone for something wrong they have done.
meanings.example: The perpetrators of this crime must be brought to book.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 범죄의 가해자들은 반드시 처벌받아야(책임을 물어야) 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find a husband or wife for someone, especially your child.
meanings.example: He's managed to marry off all three of his daughters.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 세 딸을 모두 시집보내는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something more exciting or powerful (informal).
meanings.example: They tried to juice up the story with some juicy details.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 흥미로운 세부 사항들을 추가해서 그 이야기를 더 활기차게(흥미롭게) 만들려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put things in boxes.
meanings.example: We spent the whole day boxing up our books for the move.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 이사를 위해 하루 종일 책을 상자에 담는 데 시간을 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move close together in a group; to form folds or wrinkles (of fabric).
meanings.example: The runners bunched up at the start of the race.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주자들이 경주 시작점에 옹기종기 모여 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go from one side to the other; to change from one style or field to another.
meanings.example: The band has successfully crossed over from country to pop music.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 밴드는 컨트리 음악에서 팝 음악으로 성공적으로 분야를 넓혔다(넘나들었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish or stop happening.
meanings.example: The meeting finally came to an end after three hours.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의는 3시간 만에 마침내 끝났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tell someone your secret feelings and thoughts.
meanings.example: She poured her heart out to her best friend.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 단짝 친구에게 자신의 속마음을 다 털어놓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To create something through effort, especially a career or a place in society.
meanings.example: He managed to carve out a successful career in marketing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 마케팅 분야에서 성공적인 경력을 쌓는(개척하는) 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be good at something; to have a natural ability for something.
meanings.example: She has a good head for figures.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 숫자 감각이 좋다 (수학적 소질이 있다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be present in a place but not in a fixed position; to be heard or discussed by many people (of a rumor).
meanings.example: I'm sure that document is floating around the office somewhere.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 문서가 분명히 사무실 어딘가에 굴러다니고 있을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become used to the conditions of a game so that you can play well (British informal).
meanings.example: It took a few minutes for the batsman to get his eye in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 타자가 감각을 익히는 데 몇 분이 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To change something in order to deceive people.
meanings.example: The accountant was accused of doctoring up the financial records.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회계사는 재무 기록을 조작했다는 혐의를 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat someone badly or dishonestly (informal).
meanings.example: Stop jerking me around and tell me the truth!
meanings.exampleKorean: 나 골탕 좀 그만 먹이고 진실을 말해!

meanings.englishDefinition: To rise to the surface; to become more obvious or intense.
meanings.example: Old resentments began to bubble up during the discussion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 토론 중에 옛 원한들이 수면 위로 나타나기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To happen or appear in the future (American informal).
meanings.example: You never know what kind of opportunities will come down the pike.
meanings.exampleKorean: 미래에 어떤 기회들이 생길지(나타날지)는 아무도 모른다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be interested in or attracted to only one person or thing.
meanings.example: He only has eyes for his wife.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 오직 아내에게만 마음이 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To need or want something.
meanings.example: I could really do with a cold drink right now.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지금 차가운 음료수 한 잔 마셨으면 정말 좋겠다 (필요하다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To form into a clump or group.
meanings.example: The wet snow began to clump up on the trees.
meanings.exampleKorean: 젖은 눈이 나무 위에서 뭉쳐지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid something that you find difficult or unpleasant.
meanings.example: He doesn't shy away from hard work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 힘든 일을 피하지 않는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remember something or to make you remember something.
meanings.example: The music called to mind happy memories of his childhood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 음악은 그의 어린 시절의 행복한 기억들을 상기시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use something for a particular purpose.
meanings.example: She was able to put her language skills to good use during the trip.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 여행 중에 자신의 언어 능력을 아주 잘 활용할 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a very formal way.
meanings.example: Please don't stand on ceremony; just help yourself to the food.
meanings.exampleKorean: 격식 차리지 말고 편하게 음식 드세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a difficult situation instead of trying to deal with it.
meanings.example: He just walked away from the problem. / He was lucky to walk away from the car crash with only minor injuries.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그냥 그 문제를 회피해버렸다. / 그는 자동차 사고에서 가벼운 부상만 입고 무사히 빠져나온 것이 다행이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a speech or piece of writing longer by adding unnecessary information.
meanings.example: I had to pad out my essay to reach the required length.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 요구된 길이를 맞추기 위해 에세이 내용을 늘려야만 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put something in a safe place and lock it; to put someone in prison.
meanings.example: The jewels are locked away in a safe. / He was locked away for ten years for his crimes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 보석들은 금고 안에 보관되어 있다. / 그는 범죄로 인해 10년 동안 감옥에 갇혀 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become more relaxed and feel less worried or nervous.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 긴장을 풀다, 마음을 편히 가지다, (몸을) 풀다
meanings.example: He needs to loosen up and enjoy himself more.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 긴장을 풀고 좀 더 즐길 필요가 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a meal at home rather than in a restaurant.
meanings.example: I'm too tired to go out, let's just eat in tonight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나가기 너무 피곤하네. 오늘 밤은 그냥 집에서 먹자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly say something loudly; to suddenly start to laugh, cry, etc.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 갑자기 터져 나오다, 느닷없이 말하다, (웃음 등을) 터뜨리다
meanings.example: "I can't take it anymore!" she burst out.
meanings.exampleKorean: "더 이상은 못 참겠어!" 그녀가 느닷없이 소리를 질렀다 (터뜨렸다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop being involved in a situation for a short time to think about it objectively.
meanings.example: We need to step back and look at the project as a whole.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 한 걸음 물러나서 프로젝트 전체를 바라볼 필요가 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find a target and follow its movements automatically; to focus all your attention on something.
meanings.example: The missile locked on to the enemy plane. / Her eyes locked on to the prize.
meanings.exampleKorean: 미사일이 적군 비행기에 조준을 고정했다. / 그녀의 눈은 상품에 딱 고정되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a law no longer valid; to cause someone to die or become ill suddenly.
meanings.example: The Supreme Court struck down the new law. / He was struck down by a mysterious virus.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대법원은 그 신법을 무효화했다. / 그는 정체불명의 바이러스로 쓰러졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To succeed in dealing with a difficult situation.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 위기에 잘 대처하다, (중요한 순간에) 제 실력을 발휘하다
meanings.example: He really rose to the occasion and gave a brilliant speech.
