meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 위기 상황에서 제 실력을 발휘하여 아주 훌륭한 연설을 해냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use something, such as influence or pressure, in order to achieve a result.
meanings.example: We must bring all our resources to bear on this problem.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 이 문제를 해결하기 위해 우리의 모든 자원을 쏟아부어야(행사해야) 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place; used to tell someone to go away (informal).
meanings.example: It's time we shoved off. / Shove off, I'm busy!
meanings.exampleKorean: 이제 우리 자리를 떠날 시간이야. / 저리 가, 나 바빠!

meanings.englishDefinition: To decorate someone or something with special clothes or ornaments.
meanings.example: The hall was decked out with flowers for the wedding.
meanings.exampleKorean: 결혼식을 위해 강당이 꽃들로 화려하게 꾸며졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To believe something without needing proof.
meanings.example: You have to take his word on faith.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신은 그의 말을 무조건 믿어야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To work very hard with little time to rest.
meanings.example: I've been slaving away in the kitchen all morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아침 내내 부엌에서 뼈빠지게 일하고 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be completely under someone's control or influence.
meanings.example: He had the audience eating from his hand.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 청중을 자신의 마음대로 움직이게(고분고분하게) 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be out of money; to give up in a fight.
meanings.example: I'm sorry, I've tapped out for this month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 미안해, 이번 달은 돈이 다 바닥났어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decide not to deal with something for a while (British informal).
meanings.example: The plan to build a new stadium has been kicked into touch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 경기장을 짓기로 한 계획은 보류되었다 (미뤄졌다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To complete a period of time in a job or prison.
meanings.example: She served out her term as mayor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 시장으로서의 임기를 모두 마쳤다 (채웠다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To obtain a legal document, such as a warrant, by making an official statement.
meanings.example: The victim swore out a warrant for his arrest.
meanings.exampleKorean: 피해자는 그에 대한 체포 영장을 발부받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave the place where you have been living or working for a long time.
meanings.example: After ten years in the same city, they decided to pull up stakes and move to the coast.
meanings.exampleKorean: 같은 도시에서 10년을 산 뒤, 그들은 살던 곳을 떠나 해안가로 이사하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To set very high goals for yourself.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 원대한 꿈을 가지다, 높은 목표를 세우다
meanings.example: You should always shoot for the stars; even if you fail, you'll achieve something great.
meanings.exampleKorean: 항상 원대한 꿈을 가져야 한다. 실패하더라도 대단한 무언가를 이룰 테니까.

meanings.englishDefinition: To earn a lot of money very easily.
meanings.example: The movie is raking in millions at the box office.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 영화는 박스오피스에서 수백만 달러를 긁어모으고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be wasted or lost completely.
meanings.example: Ten years of hard work went down the drain when the company went bankrupt.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사가 파산하면서 10년간의 노력이 수포로 돌아갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To encourage someone to do something or to work harder.
meanings.example: The cheering crowd spurred the runners on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 환호하는 군중들이 주자들을 자극하여 더 빨리 달리게 했다 (격려했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To ignore someone's suggestions or criticisms.
meanings.example: He brushed aside my concerns and continued with his plan.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 내 걱정을 무시하고(제쳐놓고) 자신의 계획을 계속 진행했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be willing to do anything, even if it is illegal or dishonest, to get what you want.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 수단과 방법을 가리지 않다, 무엇이든 다 하다
meanings.example: He will stop at nothing to get what he wants.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신이 원하는 것을 얻기 위해 수단과 방법을 가리지 않을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To result in an average amount over a period of time.
meanings.example: The cost of the materials should average out to about $50 per item.
meanings.exampleKorean: 재료비는 품목당 약 50달러 정도로 평균이 잡힐 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Decorated or adjusted in a stylish or impressive way (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 화려하게 꾸민, 성능을 높인, 멋을 낸
meanings.example: Check out his tricked out mountain bike.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 아주 화려하게 꾸민(멋진) 산악자전거 좀 봐.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start fighting.
meanings.example: The argument got so heated that they almost came to blows.
meanings.exampleKorean: 말다툼이 너무 격해져서 그들은 거의 주먹다짐까지 갈 뻔했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To look good together; to happen at the same time.
meanings.example: Red and white go together really well. / Responsibility and freedom go together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빨간색과 하얀색은 정말 잘 어울린다. / 책임과 자유는 항상 동반된다 (함께 간다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be so full of an emotion that you cannot hide it.
meanings.example: She was bubbling over with excitement after hearing the news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 소식을 듣고 흥분이 넘쳐흘렀다 (벅차올랐다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To collect several things together.
meanings.example: He gathered up his papers and left the room. / She gathered up her courage to speak.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 서류를 긁어모아서 방을 나갔다. / 그녀는 말하기 위해 용기를 가다듬었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To delay something; to oppose or resist something.
meanings.example: The meeting has been pushed back to 3 PM. / The workers are pushing back against the new rules.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의가 오후 3시로 미뤄졌다. / 노동자들은 새로운 규칙에 저항하고(반격하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue doing something until it is finished; to accompany someone to the door when they leave.
meanings.example: We must see this project out until the end. / I'll see you out to your car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 이 프로젝트를 끝까지 수행해야(지켜봐야) 한다. / 당신을 차 있는 곳까지 배웅해 드릴게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To feel extremely worried or nervous about something (informal).
meanings.example: I'm really stressing out about my final exams.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기말고사 때문에 정말 스트레스를 받고 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk about unpleasant events that happened in the past and that should be forgotten.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 과거의 잘못을 들춰내다, 옛날 일을 다시 끄집어내다
meanings.example: There's no point in raking over old coals; let's move on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 옛날 일을 다시 들춰내 봐야 소용없어. 이제 잊고 넘어가자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To choose someone or something randomly.
meanings.example: The winner will be picked out of a hat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우승자는 제비뽑기로(무작위로) 선정될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid dealing with a difficult or embarrassing subject in detail.
meanings.example: The report skates over the real causes of the problem.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 보고서는 문제의 진짜 원인에 대해서는 대충 넘어가고(가볍게 다루고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get rid of an unpleasant feeling, such as a headache or hangover, by sleeping.
meanings.example: I've got a headache, so I'm going to try to sleep it off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 머리가 좀 아파서 잠을 자서 좀 풀어보려고 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To visit a place for a short time on the way to somewhere else.
meanings.example: We stopped off in Paris for a couple of days on our way to Rome.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 로마로 가는 길에 파리에 이틀 정도 잠시 들렀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become involved in a difficult situation in order to help or stop it.
meanings.example: The government had to step in to save the bank from failing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 은행의 파산을 막기 위해 개입해야(돕고 나서야) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attack or criticize someone very strongly (informal).
meanings.example: The boss really ripped into him for making that mistake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상사는 그가 실수한 것에 대해 아주 호되게 비판했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To divide something into several smaller parts.
meanings.example: The territory was carved up between the two neighboring countries.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 영토는 이웃한 두 국가 사이에 분할되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stand or sit straight after bending; to make a place tidy.
meanings.example: He straightened up when his boss entered the room. / I need to straighten up the living room before the guests arrive.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 상사가 방에 들어오자 자세를 똑바로 폈다. / 손님들이 오기 전에 거실을 좀 정리해야겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop a cigarette from burning by pressing it against something hard.
meanings.example: She stubbed out her cigarette in the ashtray.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 재떨이에 담뱃불을 비벼 껐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be ready to help or support someone if they need it.
meanings.example: Don't worry about the meeting; I've got your back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의 걱정하지 마. 내가 뒤에서 든든하게 지켜줄게 (지지해줄게).

meanings.englishDefinition: To become more determined in your efforts or opinions, even though people are criticizing you.
meanings.koreanDefinition: (자신의 입장을) 더욱 고수하다, 강도를 높이다, 단호하게 대처하다
meanings.example: Instead of apologizing, the politician decided to double down on his controversial remarks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사과하는 대신, 그 정치인은 자신의 논란이 된 발언들을 더욱 고수하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To visit a place for a short time on your way to somewhere else (informal).
meanings.example: I'll swing by your office later today to drop off the report.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 늦게 보고서를 전해주러 당신 사무실에 잠시 들를게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To succeed after a long period of effort or competition.
meanings.example: Despite all the difficulties, common sense finally won out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 어려움에도 불구하고, 마침내 상식이 이겼다 (승리했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To start a struggle or argument in a very determined and aggressive way.
meanings.example: The defense attorney came out swinging in her opening statement.
meanings.exampleKorean: 변호인은 모두 발언부터 아주 공격적으로 나섰다 (기선을 제압했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To believe in an idea or plan.
meanings.example: I don't really buy into that theory.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그 이론을 정말로 믿지 않는다 (받아들이지 않는다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To withdraw a statement or promise.
meanings.example: The company had to walk back its claims about the new product's safety.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 신제품의 안전성에 대한 주장을 철회해야(취소해야) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wait anxiously for something to happen or for a result.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 끝까지 참고 기다리다, 초조하게 결과를 기다리다
meanings.example: We had to sweat it out for three days before the test results came back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 시험 결과가 나올 때까지 사흘 동안 초조하게 기다려야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To persuade someone to agree with you.
meanings.example: It took a while, but we finally managed to win him around to our way of thinking.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시간은 좀 걸렸지만, 마침내 그를 우리 생각대로 설득하는 데(우리 편으로 만드는 데) 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To buy all of something that is available.
meanings.example: Speculators are buying up all the land in the area.
meanings.exampleKorean: 투기꾼들이 그 지역의 모든 땅을 싹 다 사들이고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent someone from being heard by shouting more loudly than they are.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 큰 소리로 말을 가로막다, (소리 질러) 압도하다
meanings.example: The protesters tried to shout down the speaker.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시위대들은 연사의 말을 가로막으려(압도하려) 큰 소리를 질렀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To report something by telephone; to do something without much effort (informal).
meanings.example: He phoned in sick this morning. / The actor really phoned in his performance in that movie.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 오늘 아침에 전화로 병가를 냈다. / 그 배우는 그 영화에서 정말 형식적으로(대충) 연기했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean a room or surface using a broom; to be influenced by a strong feeling.
meanings.example: Could you sweep up the broken glass? / They were swept up in the excitement of the moment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 깨진 유리 조각들을 좀 쓸어 줄래? / 그들은 그 순간의 흥분에 휩쓸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be extremely surprised or frightened.
meanings.example: I nearly jumped out of my skin when the phone rang in the middle of the night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 한밤중에 전화가 울려서 소스라치게 놀랐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop being part of a group or following a custom.
meanings.example: He decided to break with family tradition and become an artist.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 가족의 전통을 깨고 예술가가 되기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To visit someone's house; to influence someone suddenly (of a feeling).
meanings.example: Why don't you come over for dinner tonight? / A sudden wave of dizziness came over her.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 저녁 먹으러 우리 집에 들르는 게 어때? / 갑작스러운 어지러움이 그녀를 덮쳤다 (느껴졌다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To gain weight quickly (informal).
meanings.example: He really packed on the pounds over the holidays.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 휴가 기간 동안 정말 살이 많이 쪘다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To disappear gradually.
meanings.example: All my worries melted away when I saw her smile.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 미소를 보자 내 모든 걱정들이 차츰 사라졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rain very heavily; to tie something down securely with ropes.
meanings.example: The rain was lashing down all night. / Make sure the equipment is lashed down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 밤새도록 비가 억수같이 쏟아졌다. / 장비가 단단히 묶여 있는지 확인하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To regain consciousness; to become calm again.
meanings.example: When he came to himself, he was lying in a hospital bed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 의식을 되찾았을 때, 그는 병원 침대에 누워 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spoil something by doing it badly (informal).
meanings.example: He completely botched up the interview.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 인터뷰를 완전히 망쳐버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something larger or thicker by adding more material.
meanings.example: I used breadcrumbs to bulk out the meatloaf.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 미트로프의 부피를 늘리기 위해 빵가루를 사용했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reserve all the available places or time.
meanings.example: The hotel is completely booked up for the summer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 호텔은 여름 시즌 동안 예약이 완전히 꽉 찼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find something by using the sense of smell; to discover something hidden.
meanings.example: The dog scented out the hidden drugs. / Reporters are trying to scent out the truth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 개는 숨겨진 마약을 냄새로 찾아냈다. / 기자들이 진실을 알아내려(찾아내려) 애쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cover yourself or something with soap bubbles.
meanings.example: Lather up your hands before rinsing them.
meanings.exampleKorean: 물로 헹구기 전에 손에 비누칠을 하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use something as a base from which you can make more progress.
meanings.example: We need to build on our success from last year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 작년의 성공을 기반으로 더 발전해 나가야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move backwards or away from someone; to decide not to do something you had planned.
meanings.example: The army began to pull back from the border. / The investors pulled back when the risk became too high.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군대가 국경에서 철수하기 시작했다. / 위험이 너무 커지자 투자자들은 발을 뺐다 (참여를 철회했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to exist.
meanings.example: How did this universe come into being?
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 우주는 어떻게 생성되었을까?

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to use a plan or a law.
meanings.example: The new regulations will be put into effect next month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 규정들이 다음 달에 시행될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid doing something that you do not want to do, often by being dishonest.
meanings.example: He's always trying to wriggle out of his chores.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 집안일을 교묘하게 빠져나가려(회피하려) 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attack or criticize someone who has attacked or criticized you.
meanings.example: The company hit back at its critics with a series of ads.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 일련의 광고를 통해 비판자들에게 반격했다 (되받아쳤다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend less money; to reduce the amount of something.
meanings.example: We need to cut back on our spending this month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 이번 달 지출을 줄여야(삭감해야) 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A situation in which two possibilities are equally likely; to throw a coin into the air to decide something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 동전을 던져 결정하기, 승패를 가리기 힘든 상황
meanings.example: It's a toss up who will win the election.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선거에서 누가 이길지는 정말 승패를 가리기 힘든 상황(팽팽한 상황)이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discuss an idea or suggestion in an informal way.
meanings.example: We tossed around a few ideas for the party theme.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 파티 주제에 대해 몇 가지 아이디어를 주고받으며 논의했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce something such as light or a smell; to make someone go somewhere (formal).
meanings.example: The sun sends forth light and heat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 태양은 빛과 열을 방출한다 (발한다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop a horse by pulling the reins; to stop something suddenly.
meanings.example: The rider reined up at the edge of the cliff.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기수는 절벽 끝에서 말을 멈춰 세웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move away from someone or something; to increase the distance between yourself and a competitor.
meanings.example: The train slowly drew away from the station. / The runner began to draw away from the rest of the pack.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기차가 역에서 천천히 멀어졌다. / 주자가 나머지 무리로부터 앞서 나가기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To look for someone or something, especially for a long time until you find them.
meanings.example: He decided to seek out the truth for himself.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 스스로 진실을 찾아내기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A loss of status, respect, or prestige.
meanings.example: The politician's fall from grace was sudden and dramatic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인의 지위 추락(신임을 잃은 것)은 갑작스럽고 극적이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage to do something although you are not organized and do not have a plan.
meanings.example: We didn't have much money, but we managed to muddle through.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 돈이 많지 않았지만, 어떻게든 그럭저럭 살아갈 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept something unpleasant without complaining.
meanings.example: When he lost his job, he took it on the chin and started looking for another one.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 직장을 잃었을 때 그것을 의연하게 받아들이고 다른 직장을 찾기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To help someone who is in a difficult or dangerous situation.
meanings.example: A passing motorist came to my rescue when my car broke down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 차가 고장 났을 때 지나가던 운전자가 나를 도우러(구하러) 왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give something to someone else, especially when you do not want to.
meanings.example: I'm reluctant to part with any of my old books.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 내 오래된 책들을 어느 것 하나 내주고(버리고) 싶지 않다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deliberately use up as much time as possible near the end of a game.
meanings.example: The leading team tried to run down the clock in the final minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 앞서고 있던 팀은 마지막 몇 분 동안 시간을 끌려 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join together with another person to do something.
meanings.example: Students were asked to pair up for the next activity.
meanings.exampleKorean: 학생들은 다음 활동을 위해 두 명씩 짝을 지으라는 지시를 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make yourself look more attractive by putting on makeup and expensive clothes (informal).
meanings.example: She really glammed up for the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 파티를 위해 정말 화려하게 단장했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend time doing small, unimportant things in a relaxed way.
meanings.example: He spent the afternoon puttering around in the garden.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 정원에서 이것저것 소소한 일을 하며 한가롭게 오후를 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall over or to make something fall over.
meanings.example: The glass tipped over and spilled milk everywhere.
meanings.exampleKorean: 유리잔이 넘어지면서(뒤집히면서) 우유를 사방에 쏟았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force someone to leave a place or group.
meanings.example: He was cast out from his community because of his beliefs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 신념 때문에 공동체에서 내쫓겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start performing well again after a period of poor performance.
meanings.example: The champion showed a return to form in yesterday's match.
meanings.exampleKorean: 챔피언은 어제 경기에서 기량을 회복한 모습을 보여주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To succeed in understanding something difficult or strange.
meanings.example: It's a complex theory, and I'm still trying to wrap my head around it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그것은 복잡한 이론이라서, 나는 여전히 그것을 이해하려 애쓰는 중이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue a journey or task, especially when it is difficult.
meanings.example: We were tired, but we decided to push on to the next village.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 피곤했지만, 다음 마을까지 계속 나아가기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a picture or idea appear in someone's mind; to create something unexpectedly.
meanings.example: The smell of baking always conjures up memories of my grandmother.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빵 굽는 냄새는 항상 할머니에 대한 기억을 불러일으킨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually end in a disappointing or weak way.
meanings.example: The protest started with hundreds of people, but it soon fizzled out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수백 명으로 시작한 시위는 곧 흐지부지되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent someone from being involved in something by being unfriendly to them.
meanings.example: Smaller companies are being frozen out of the market by big corporations.
meanings.exampleKorean: 중소기업들이 대기업들에 의해 시장에서 배제되고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be ready to fight or argue; to say unkind things about someone.
meanings.example: She really got her claws out during the debate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 토론 중에 정말 공격적인 태도를 보였다 (독설을 내뱉었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To refuse to consider someone's ideas or feelings.
meanings.example: The manager swept aside all our objections.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매니저는 우리의 모든 반대 의견을 일축했다 (제쳐놓았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To do or say something very quickly.
meanings.example: He rattled through the presentation in ten minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 10분 만에 발표를 빠르게 해치웠다 (술술 끝냈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To move or work faster (American informal).
meanings.example: Come on, get the lead out or we'll be late!
meanings.exampleKorean: 어서 서둘러! 안 그러면 늦겠어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To attack or criticize someone very strongly.
meanings.example: The critics really tore into his latest book.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비평가들은 그의 신간을 아주 호되게 비판했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tell someone in authority about something wrong that someone else has done; to fail to keep a promise.
meanings.example: He promised not to tell anyone, but he ratted on us. / He ratted on his deal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아무에게도 말하지 않겠다고 약속했지만, 우리를 밀고했다. / 그는 자신의 거래 약속을 어겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To explain something to someone by taking them through it step by step.
meanings.example: Let me walk you through the new software.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 소프트웨어 사용법을 단계별로 상세히 설명해 드릴게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be too difficult for someone to understand; to deal with someone at a higher level than your immediate boss.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 이해하기 너무 어렵다, (직속 상사를 건너뛰고) 상급자에게 직접 가다
meanings.example: Most of that lecture went over my head. / I had to go over my manager's head to get the project approved.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 강의의 대부분은 내가 이해하기에 너무 어려웠다. / 프로젝트 승인을 받기 위해 매니저를 건너뛰고 상급자에게 직접 가야만 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become aware of the truth about a situation.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 사정을 알게 되다, 영리해지다, 실상을 깨닫다
meanings.example: He finally wised up and realized they were lying to him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 마침내 실상을 깨닫고(영리해져서) 그들이 자신에게 거짓말을 하고 있다는 것을 알게 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hold onto something firmly; to try very hard to keep something.
meanings.example: The survivors clung on to the wreck until help arrived. / The leader is clinging on to power.
meanings.exampleKorean: 생존자들은 구조대가 올 때까지 잔해에 꽉 매달려 있었다. / 그 지도자는 권력을 끈질기게 고수하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remember or refer to something from the past.
meanings.example: The design of the new car hearkens back to the classic models of the 1960s.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 신차의 디자인은 1960년대의 클래식 모델들을 회상하게 한다 (닮아 있다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To get rid of something, such as fat or stress, by doing something active; to pay a debt by working.
meanings.example: She went for a run to work off her frustration. / He worked off his debt by helping in the garden.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 좌절감을 해소하기(털어내기) 위해 달리기를 하러 갔다. / 그는 정원 일을 도와주며 빚을 갚았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To improve something or make it look new; to invent something in a hurry.
meanings.example: They tried to vamp up the old report for the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 회의를 위해 낡은 보고서를 대충 개조하여(꾸며내어) 내놓으려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dismiss something as being unimportant.
meanings.example: She waved away my suggestions and continued with her work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 내 제안을 일축하고(무시하고) 자신의 일을 계속했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat someone badly because you know they will not resist; to defeat someone very easily.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 만만하게 보다, 함부로 대하다, 쉽게 이기다
meanings.example: He's the kind of boss who will walk over anyone to get what he wants.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신이 원하는 것을 얻기 위해 누구든 만만하게 대할(밟고 지나갈) 그런 종류의 상사다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To signal to a driver to stop by waving your hand.
meanings.example: We managed to wave down a passing taxi.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 지나가는 택시를 손을 흔들어 세울 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To send a letter to a radio or television station, or a newspaper; to add something in writing.
meanings.example: Listeners are encouraged to write in with their suggestions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 청취자들의 의견 투고(편지 보내기)를 환영합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To play in an aggressive and attacking way against an opponent.
meanings.example: Our team needs to take the game to the opposition right from the start.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 팀은 시작부터 상대 팀에게 공격적으로 임할 필요가 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To relax and do nothing, often watching television (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: (아무 생각 없이) 편히 쉬다, 멍하니 시간을 보내다
meanings.example: I'm just going to veg out in front of the TV tonight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 밤에는 그냥 TV 앞에서 멍하니 쉴 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return to the way things were in the past.
meanings.example: You can't turn back the clock and change what happened.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시간을 되돌려서 이미 일어난 일을 바꿀 수는 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To read small parts of a book; to spend some of your savings.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 잠시 들여다보다, (저축 등을) 조금씩 꺼내 쓰다
meanings.example: I've only had time to dip into the first chapter. / We had to dip into our savings to pay for the repairs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 1장을 잠시 들여다볼 시간밖에 없었다. / 수리비를 내기 위해 저축해둔 돈을 좀 꺼내 써야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce something slowly and with effort.
meanings.example: The factory continues to grind out hundreds of units every day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공장은 매일 수백 개의 제품을 힘들게 생산해내고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To help someone get out of a difficult situation.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 궁지에서 구해주다, 어려운 상황을 벗어나게 돕다
meanings.example: His teammate's goal really dug them out of a hole.
meanings.exampleKorean: 팀 동료의 골이 정말로 그들을 궁지에서 구해주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become accustomed to a new role or situation and become good at it.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~에 익숙해지다, 점차 잘하게 되다, (옷 등이) 맞을 만큼 자라다
meanings.example: She is really growing into her new job as a manager. / Don't worry, he'll grow into that oversized jacket soon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 매니저라는 새로운 직책에 정말 익숙해지고(잘해나가고) 있다. / 걱정 마, 저 큰 재킷도 곧 그에게 맞을 만큼 자랄 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To write something down so that you will remember it.
meanings.example: I'll note down your address so I don't forget it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잊어버리지 않게 당신의 주소를 메모해(적어)둘게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try very hard to get something; to try to harm or criticize someone.
meanings.example: He's gunning for the top job in the company. / The media is really gunning for that politician.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 회사의 최고 직책을 얻으려고 애쓰고 있다. / 언론이 그 정치인을 정말 비난하려고 노리고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce something unexpected and impressive.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 예기치 못한 것을 내놓다, 기발한 해결책을 찾아내다
meanings.example: They managed to pull a victory out of the hat in the final minute.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 마지막 순간에 예기치 못한 승리를 쟁취해냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find a time for someone or something in a busy schedule.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 시간을 내어 끼워 넣다, 짬을 내다
meanings.example: I can slot you in for a quick meeting at 2 PM.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오후 2시에 잠깐 짬을 내서 당신과 회의를 할 수 있어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat someone harshly over a long period so that they lose their confidence or hope.
meanings.example: Years of poverty had ground them down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수년간의 가난이 그들을 지치게(의지를 꺾게) 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To perform as well as you did in the past.
meanings.example: The veteran striker rolled back the years with a stunning goal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 베테랑 공격수는 멋진 골로 옛 기량을 다시 보여주었다 (젊은 시절로 돌아간 듯했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To persuade someone to accept something that is of low quality or is not real.
meanings.example: He tried to foist off his old car on me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 낡은 차를 나에게 속여서 떠넘기려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To agree with something; to progress or develop.
meanings.example: I'll go along with whatever you decide. / How is your new book going along?
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신이 무엇을 결정하든 나는 찬성할(따를) 거예요. / 당신의 새 책은 어떻게 진행되고 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate from a larger group.
meanings.example: A small group split off from the main political party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작은 그룹이 주요 정당에서 갈라져 나왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To succeed very easily in something, especially a test.
meanings.example: She sailed through her final exams.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 기말고사를 거뜬히 통과했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To quickly put on a piece of clothing; to gradually start to be in a bad state or habit.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~을 슥 입다, (어떤 상태에) 빠지다, 몰래 들어가다
meanings.example: She slipped into her bathrobe. / The economy is slipping into a recession.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 목욕 가운을 슥 입었다. / 경제가 불황으로 빠져들고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force yourself to be brave enough to do something.
meanings.example: He finally plucked up the courage to ask her out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 마침내 용기를 내어 그녀에게 데이트 신청을 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something very quickly.
meanings.example: I whipped through the report in half an hour.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 30분 만에 그 보고서를 빠르게 해치웠다 (끝냈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To happen or develop in a particular way; to continue until the end.
meanings.example: We'll have to see how the situation plays out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상황이 어떻게 전개될지 지켜봐야 할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To flow slowly in a thin stream; to have an effect on people at a lower level of an organization or society.
meanings.example: The benefits of the tax cuts are expected to trickle down to the poor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 감세의 혜택이 저소득층에게까지 전달될(낙수 효과가 있을) 것으로 기대된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To compare the relative value or importance of two things.
meanings.example: You must weigh the benefits against the risks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신은 위험과 비교하여 혜택을 꼼꼼히 따져봐야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put something in a place where it cannot be seen; to go to a place where no one can find you.
meanings.example: She hid the letters away in a drawer. / He spent the weekend hiding away in the mountains.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 편지들을 서랍 속에 숨겨 두었다. / 그는 산속에 은둔하며(숨어서) 주말을 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To destroy or remove something completely and quickly.
meanings.example: The floods swept away the bridge. / The new government swept away the old regulations.
meanings.exampleKorean: 홍수가 다리를 휩쓸고 가버렸다. / 새 정부는 낡은 규제들을 완전히 없애버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sell something and buy something that is less expensive.
meanings.example: Many families are trading down to smaller cars to save money.
meanings.exampleKorean: 많은 가정이 돈을 아끼기 위해 더 작은 차로 바꾸고(내려가고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To destroy something by hitting it hard.
meanings.example: The police had to smash down the door to get into the house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 집 안으로 들어가기 위해 문을 때려 부수어야(박살 내야) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To include something, often secretly; to enter a place quietly.
meanings.example: He managed to slip in a joke during his serious speech.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 진지한 연설 도중에 농담 한마디를 슬쩍 끼워 넣는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste time at the end of a game when you are winning.
meanings.example: The team was just trying to run the clock down in the final minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 팀은 마지막 몇 분 동안 단지 시간을 끌려고(보내려고) 노력 중이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sell something and buy something that is more expensive.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 더 좋은 것으로 바꾸다, 상급 기종으로 갈아타다
meanings.example: Many car owners are trading up to luxury models.
meanings.exampleKorean: 많은 차 주인들이 고급 모델로 차를 바꾸고(상급 기종으로 갈아타고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To destroy a building; to criticize someone or something strongly.
meanings.example: They are going to tear down the old factory. / He spent the whole interview tearing down his rival's policies.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 낡은 공장을 허물 예정이다. / 그는 인터뷰 내내 라이벌의 정책을 호되게 비판했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join together as a group; to start working well as a whole.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 하나로 뭉치다, (일이) 잘 돌아가다, 조화를 이루다
meanings.example: The community came together to rebuild the park. / After months of work, the project is finally starting to come together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지역 사회가 공원을 재건하기 위해 하나로 뭉쳤다. / 몇 달간의 작업 끝에, 프로젝트가 마침내 잘 돌아가기(조화를 이루기) 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay someone the money that you owe them.
meanings.example: Let's settle up for the dinner now.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이제 저녁 식사비 계산을 치릅시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare yourself mentally for a difficult task or competition (informal).
meanings.example: I need to psych myself up for the big game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 큰 경기를 앞두고 정신을 가다듬을 필요가 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To compare with something else; to increase in number or amount.
meanings.example: How does their new car stack up against the competition? / Work is starting to stack up on my desk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들의 신차가 경쟁사 제품과 비교하여 어느 정도인가요? / 내 책상 위에 할 일이 쌓이기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To search a person or place thoroughly; to become accustomed to a new situation.
meanings.example: The police gave the suspect a thorough shake down. / The new team will need a few weeks to shake down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 용의자를 철저히 수색했다. / 새로운 팀이 자리를 잡는 데는(익숙해지는 데는) 몇 주가 걸릴 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mix things together in a confused way; to mistake one person or thing for another.
meanings.example: Don't muddle up these files! / I always muddle up the twins.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 파일들을 뒤섞지 마세요! / 나는 항상 그 쌍둥이들을 착각한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage to find or collect enough of something, especially money, with difficulty.
meanings.example: We managed to scrape up enough money for the tickets.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 표를 살 돈을 간신히 긁어모았다 (어렵게 마련했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To land in the ocean (of a spacecraft).
meanings.example: The space capsule splashed down safely in the Pacific Ocean.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우주 캡슐이 태평양에 안전하게 착수했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To involve someone or something in a confused or difficult situation; to cause a traffic jam.
meanings.example: The accident snarled up traffic for miles.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 사고 때문에 수 마일 동안 교통이 정체되었다 (혼잡해졌다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase gradually in amount or number.
meanings.example: Our debts are starting to mount up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리의 부채가 쌓이기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To assert something very strongly or solemnly (British informal).
meanings.example: He swore down that he had never seen her before.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그녀를 전에는 한 번도 본 적이 없다고 단호하게 주장했다 (맹세코 말했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to make a situation normal again after something very bad has happened.
meanings.example: After the divorce, she had to pick up the pieces of her life.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이혼 후에 그녀는 자신의 삶을 수습해야만 했다 (정상으로 되돌려야 했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To happen in a satisfactory way; to become easy to understand.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 딱 들어맞다, 일이 잘 풀리다, 명확해지다
meanings.example: Once I solved the first problem, everything else started to fall into place.
meanings.exampleKorean: 첫 번째 문제를 풀고 나자, 다른 모든 것들이 딱 들어맞기 시작했다 (순조롭게 풀렸다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be useful or helpful to someone in the future.
meanings.example: His experience in the army will stand him in good stead in his new job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 군대 경험은 새 직장에서 그에게 큰 도움이 될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become unable to speak because you are feeling a strong emotion.
meanings.example: She choked up as she thanked her parents.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 부모님께 감사를 표하며 목이 메었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wear enough clothing to keep very warm.
meanings.example: Make sure you bundle up before going outside into the snow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 눈 내리는 밖으로 나가기 전에 옷을 따뜻하게 챙겨 입으렴.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually make something weaker or less effective.
meanings.example: The opposition is trying to chip away at the president's popularity.
meanings.exampleKorean: 야당은 대통령의 인기를 조금씩 깎아내리려(약화시키려) 애쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To refuse something because you do not think it is good enough for you.
meanings.example: She turned her nose up at the cheap wine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 값싼 와인을 비웃으며 거절했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to tell someone that what they want is impossible (informal).
meanings.example: "I think I'll win the lottery this week." "Dream on!"
meanings.exampleKorean: "나 이번 주에 복권 당첨될 것 같아." "꿈 깨! (말도 안 돼!)"

meanings.englishDefinition: To become unable to think or behave normally because you are very upset; to break into small pieces.
meanings.example: The old car is starting to fall to pieces. / He completely fell to pieces after his cat died.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 낡은 차는 산산조각 나기(망가지기) 시작하고 있다. / 그는 고양이가 죽은 뒤에 정신적으로 완전히 무너졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To examine something carefully to make sure it is correct or in good condition.
meanings.example: Please check over your work before you hand it in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 제출하기 전에 본인이 한 작업을 철저히 점검하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very nice to someone so that they will do what you want.
meanings.example: He's been trying to sweeten up the boss before asking for a raise.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 월급 인상을 요구하기 전에 상사의 비위를 맞추려(환심을 사려) 애쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a tree fall by cutting it with an axe.
meanings.example: They are going to chop down all the old trees in the park.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 공원의 낡은 나무들을 모두 베어 넘길 예정이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop being sad and start being happy.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 미소를 짓다, 기운을 내다, 우울함을 떨치다
meanings.example: Come on, turn that frown upside down and let's go have some fun!
meanings.exampleKorean: 자, 기운 내서 미소를 지어봐. 가서 재밌게 놀자!

meanings.englishDefinition: To get back something that you had lost, especially money or power, with great effort.
meanings.example: The company managed to claw back some of its market share.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 시장 점유율의 일부를 간신히 되찾았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to be used (of a law or rule).
meanings.example: The new law comes into force on January 1st.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 법은 1월 1일부터 시행된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk about something bad that happened in the past that everyone else has forgotten.
meanings.example: Why do you have to dredge up all those old arguments?
meanings.exampleKorean: 왜 그런 옛날 말다툼들을 다 들춰내야만 하니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage to get information or a solution from a complicated situation.
meanings.example: It took hours of questioning to tease out the truth from the witness.
meanings.exampleKorean: 증인으로부터 진실을 알아내는(뽑아내는) 데 몇 시간의 심문이 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To not tell anyone about something; to avoid meeting other people.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 혼자만 알고 있다, 남과 어울리지 않다
meanings.example: I'd prefer it if you kept this information to yourself. / He's a quiet person who keeps to himself.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신이 이 정보를 혼자만 알고 있어 준다면 좋겠어요. / 그는 다른 사람과 잘 어울리지 않는 조용한 사람이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a place or person famous.
meanings.example: This film really put the small town on the map.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 영화가 그 작은 마을을 정말 유명하게 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something good for someone you have treated badly in the past.
meanings.example: I'm sorry I forgot your birthday; I'll make it up to you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 네 생일을 잊어서 미안해. 내가 나중에 꼭 보답할게(미안함을 갚을게).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay at a place or visit a person for longer than they want you to.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 너무 오래 머물러 눈총을 받다, 폐를 끼치다
meanings.example: I don't want to wear out my welcome, so I'll leave tomorrow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 더 이상 폐를 끼치고 싶지 않아서 내일 떠날게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To say that an idea or suggestion is so silly that it is not worth considering.
meanings.example: His claims were laughed out of court.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 주장은 너무 터무니없어서 일축당했다 (비웃음거리가 되었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To resist or fight against someone or something.
meanings.example: The victim put up a good fight against the attacker.
meanings.exampleKorean: 피해자는 공격자에게 아주 잘 맞서 싸웠다 (저항했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay at home rather than going out.
meanings.example: I think I'll just stay in and watch a movie tonight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 밤에는 그냥 집에 있으면서 영화나 볼까 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To click on a link to go to another website or page.
meanings.example: Click through to our website for more details.
meanings.exampleKorean: 더 자세한 내용을 보려면 클릭하여 우리 웹사이트로 넘어오세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be completely finished or decided (informal).
meanings.example: They have the contract all sewn up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 그 계약을 완전히 마무리 지었다 (성사시켰다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To notice or understand something, often something that other people have not noticed.
meanings.example: He quickly picked up on her nervousness.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그녀의 긴장한 상태를 빠르게 알아차렸다 (포착했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail at an early stage, especially because of a lack of support.
meanings.example: Without proper funding, the project will just die on the vine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 적절한 자금이 없으면, 그 프로젝트는 그냥 초기에 좌절될(흐지부지될) 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To upset someone by getting involved in something that is their responsibility.
meanings.example: I don't want to step on anyone's toes, but I have some suggestions for the project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 누구의 영역을 침범하고 싶지는 않지만, 프로젝트에 대해 몇 가지 제안이 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly become very angry.
meanings.example: He flew into a rage when he saw the mess in the kitchen.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 부엌이 엉망인 것을 보고 버럭 화를 냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be consistent or believable; to support each other as a group.
meanings.example: His story just doesn't hang together. / If we don't hang together, we'll all fail.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 이야기는 그냥 앞뒤가 맞지 않는다. / 우리가 단결하지 않으면, 우리 모두 실패할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay close to each other and support each other.
meanings.example: We must stick together if we want to survive.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리가 살아남고 싶다면 서로 돕고 단결해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To compete with other people for a particular job or advantage.
meanings.example: Several candidates are already jockeying for position in the upcoming election.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이미 여러 후보가 다가올 선거에서 유리한 고지를 점하기 위해 경쟁하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wait or move more slowly than others because you are shy or nervous.
meanings.example: She hung back while the others ran ahead.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다른 사람들이 앞으로 달려 나가는 동안 그녀는 뒤에 남았다 (주저했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To save money for the future, especially secretly (informal).
meanings.example: He's managed to salt away a considerable amount of money over the years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 수년간 꽤 상당한 금액의 돈을 모으는(저축하는) 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To divide into two parts (of a road); to start talking about a different subject.
meanings.example: A small path branches off from the main road. / Let's not branch off into other topics.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작은 길이 큰 도로에서 갈라져 나온다. / 다른 주제로 옆길로 새지 맙시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become more confident and successful.
meanings.example: She has really blossomed out since she started her new job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 새 직장을 시작한 이후로 정말 활짝 피어났다 (자신감이 생겼다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To win something very easily; to steal something.
meanings.example: He walked off with all the top prizes. / Someone walked off with my umbrella.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 모든 1등 상을 휩쓸었다 (쉽게 이겼다). / 누군가 내 우산을 훔쳐갔다 (가져갔다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be the first thing you think of.
meanings.example: Several names leaped to mind when I heard the news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 소식을 들었을 때 몇몇 이름이 제일 먼저 떠올랐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to feel that something you want is likely to happen.
meanings.example: I don't want to get your hopes up, but there's a chance you got the job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너무 기대하게 하고 싶지는 않지만, 당신이 취업했을 가능성도 있어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take someone or something somewhere very quickly.
meanings.example: The celebrities were whisked away by their security team.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 유명 인사들은 보안팀에 의해 재빨리 다른 곳으로 옮겨졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hide something in a safe place; to hide on a ship or aircraft to travel secretly.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 밀항하다, 몰래 타다, (안전한 곳에) 치워 두다
meanings.example: He stowed away on a cargo ship. / The winter clothes were stowed away in the attic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 화물선에 몰래 숨어 탔다 (밀항했다). / 겨울옷들은 다락방에 치워 두었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To involve someone in a situation; to start to get dark earlier in the evening.
meanings.example: I didn't want to be drawn in to their argument. / The nights are starting to draw in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그들의 말다툼에 말려들고 싶지 않았다. / 밤이 점점 빨리 저물기 시작한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail or be destroyed completely.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 수포로 돌아가다, 가루가 되다, 허사가 되다
meanings.example: All his dreams of glory turned to dust.
meanings.exampleKorean: 영광을 향한 그의 모든 꿈이 수포로 돌아갔다 (허사가 되었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To move into a warm and comfortable position, close to another person.
meanings.example: The children snuggled up to their mother to hear the story.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 이야기를 들으려고 엄마 곁으로 바짝 다가앉았다 (파고들었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To collect and keep something for future use; to cause problems for the future.
meanings.example: The squirrels are storing up nuts for the winter. / You're storing up trouble for yourself.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다람쥐들이 겨울을 나기 위해 견과류를 비축하고 있다. / 당신은 나중에 골치 아픈 일을 자초하고 있다 (쌓아 두고 있다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To agree with the group in order to avoid conflict and maintain harmony.
meanings.example: In politics, sometimes you have to go along to get along.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정치에서는 때때로 원만하게 지내기 위해 대세를 따라야 할 때가 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become healthy again after an injury.
meanings.example: The cut on my finger has healed up nicely.
meanings.exampleKorean: 손가락의 베인 상처가 깨끗하게 다 나았다 (아물었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To become red in the face because you are embarrassed or angry.
meanings.example: She colored up when he complimented her dress.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 그녀의 드레스를 칭찬하자 그녀는 얼굴을 붉혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To destroy something completely; to criticize someone very harshly.
meanings.example: The critics cut his latest movie to pieces.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비평가들은 그의 최신 영화에 대해 혹평을 쏟아냈다 (호되게 비판했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To deal successfully with a difficult situation.
meanings.example: It's hard to cope with the loss of a loved one.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사랑하는 사람을 잃은 슬픔에 대처하는(극복하는) 것은 힘들다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To react or speak quickly without thinking carefully.
meanings.example: He tends to shoot from the hip, which often leads to misunderstandings.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 성급하게 말하는 경향이 있어서 종종 오해를 불러일으킨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave as if you are more important than someone else.
meanings.example: Ever since he got promoted, he's been trying to lord it over his former colleagues.
meanings.exampleKorean: 승진한 이후로 그는 전 동료들 위에서 거들먹거리려(군림하려) 애쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To look like the things or people around you so that you are not noticed.
meanings.example: The new building blends in well with its surroundings.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 새 건물은 주변 환경과 아주 잘 조화된다 (어우러진다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To look directly at someone until they feel uncomfortable and look away.
meanings.example: He managed to stare down the angry protesters.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 분노한 시위대들과 당당히 맞서(기싸움에서 이겨) 냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To think about and decide on the details of a future activity.
meanings.example: We need to plan out our route before we start the trip.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 여행을 시작하기 전에 경로를 철저히 계획해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To explain something complex in simple steps.
meanings.example: Can you break it down for me so I can understand better?
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 더 잘 이해할 수 있도록 좀 더 상세히 설명해 줄 수 있니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To give someone a small or insignificant reward to keep them satisfied.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 생색을 내다, 작은 성의를 보이다, 달래다
meanings.example: The company threw a bone to the employees by offering an extra day off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 직원들에게 하루의 추가 휴가를 제공하며 생색을 냈다 (달랬다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To return quickly to a normal condition after a difficult period.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 금방 회복하다, 다시 튀어 오르다, 원상태로 돌아가다
meanings.example: Children often spring back quickly after an illness.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 아프고 난 뒤에도 종종 금방 회복한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase something rapidly; to exaggerate.
meanings.example: The criticism started to pile on after the scandal broke. / Don't pile on the guilt.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스캔들이 터진 후 비난이 한꺼번에 몰리기 시작했다. / 죄책감을 너무 퍼붓지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pass quickly (of time); to let an opportunity pass.
meanings.example: The years have slipped by so quickly. / Don't let this opportunity slip by.
meanings.exampleKorean: 세월이 정말 순식간에 지나가 버렸다. / 이 기회를 놓치지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become unsteady and fall down.
meanings.example: The stack of books toppled over.
meanings.exampleKorean: 쌓여 있던 책들이 와르르 넘어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To encourage someone to continue doing something.
meanings.example: The crowd urged the runners on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군중들은 주자들에게 힘을 북돋우며 독려했다 (응원했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To refuse to change your plan or your mind, especially when other people are trying to persuade you to do something else.
meanings.example: The company dug in its heels and refused to lower the price.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 요지부동하며 가격 인하를 거부했다 (단호하게 버텼다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To react very angrily to something that someone says or does.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 호되게 꾸짖다, 버럭 화를 내다, 대들다
meanings.example: I only asked a simple question; there's no need to jump down my throat!
meanings.exampleKorean: 난 그냥 간단한 질문을 했을 뿐이야. 그렇게 버럭 화를 낼(호되게 꾸짖을) 필요는 없잖아!

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn around suddenly and walk away in another direction.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 홱 돌아서 가버리다, 갑자기 등을 돌리다
meanings.example: Without saying a word, he turned on his heel and left the room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아무 말도 없이, 그는 홱 돌아서 방을 나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To persuade someone to help you with something or to join a group.
meanings.example: I managed to rope in a few friends to help me move.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 친구 몇 명을 꼬드겨서 이사를 도와주게 만들었다 (참여시켰다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a person or animal so frightened that they go away.
meanings.example: The loud noise scared off the birds. / High prices are scaring off potential buyers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 큰 소음이 새들을 쫓아버렸다. / 높은 가격이 잠재적인 구매자들을 단념하게 만들고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrive in large numbers or amounts; to arrive somewhere late and relaxed.
meanings.example: The donations have been rolling in all week. / He finally rolled in at midnight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기부금이 일주일 내내 밀려 들어오고 있다. / 그는 자정이 되어서야 느긋하게 나타났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept something unpleasant that you cannot change.
meanings.example: He has to learn to live with his disability. / It was a difficult decision, but we have to live with it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 장애를 감수하며 사는 법을 배워야 한다. / 그것은 힘든 결정이었지만, 우리는 그것을 받아들여야만 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To close a road or path so that people cannot use it.
meanings.example: The police blocked off the street after the accident.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 사고 후에 그 거리의 통행을 차단했다 (가로막았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place very quickly; to produce or publish something very quickly.
meanings.example: She rushed out to catch the bus. / The publisher rushed out a biography of the late singer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 버스를 타기 위해 급히 나갔다. / 출판사는 고인이 된 가수의 전기를 서둘러 출시했다 (발간했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove liquid from a surface using a cloth.
meanings.example: Can you wipe up that spilled milk?
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 엎질러진 우유 좀 닦아 줄래?

meanings.englishDefinition: To attack someone physically or with words (informal).
meanings.example: The manager really laced into the team for their poor performance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매니저는 부진한 실적에 대해 팀을 아주 호되게 비난했다 (공격했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To tell someone in authority about something wrong that another person has done (informal).
meanings.example: He ratted out his accomplices to the police.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 경찰에 자신의 공범들을 밀고했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Deeply ingrained in one's character or nature.
meanings.example: His love for music was bred in the bone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 음악에 대한 사랑은 뼛속까지 배어 있었다 (천성적이었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To break into pieces; to fail (of a plan or relationship).
meanings.example: The old book just came apart in my hands. / Their marriage is starting to come apart.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 낡은 책은 내 손안에서 그냥 부서져 버렸다. / 그들의 결혼 생활은 산산조각 나기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To offer help or information.
meanings.example: Several witnesses have come forward with new information.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몇몇 목격자들이 새로운 정보를 가지고 제보하고 나섰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize or punish someone severely.
meanings.example: The government is coming down hard on tax evaders.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 탈세자들을 아주 호되게 처벌하고(비판하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To receive something, especially criticism.
meanings.example: The mayor has come in for a lot of criticism over his decision.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시장은 자신의 결정에 대해 많은 비난을 받아왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make or assemble something quickly; to bring people together unexpectedly.
meanings.example: I threw together a quick salad for lunch. / The team was thrown together at the last minute.
meanings.exampleKorean: 점심으로 샐러드를 서둘러 간단히 만들었다. / 그 팀은 마지막 순간에 갑작스럽게 꾸려졌다 (모였다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing something quickly (informal).
meanings.example: We have a lot to do, so let's hop to it!
meanings.exampleKorean: 할 일이 많으니까, 어서 서둘러 시작하자!

meanings.englishDefinition: To give something to a lot of people, especially in small amounts.
meanings.example: The teacher poceeded to dole out the exam papers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님은 시험지를 나눠주기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat a meal at a restaurant rather than at home.
meanings.example: We like to dine out at least once a week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 적어도 일주일에 한 번은 외식하는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reach a particular level or standard; to move towards someone.
meanings.example: The service didn't come up to our expectations. / A stranger came up to me and asked for directions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 서비스는 우리 기대에 부응하지 못했다. / 낯선 사람이 나에게 다가와 길을 물었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put on a piece of clothing quickly.
meanings.example: She threw on a coat and ran outside.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 코트를 급히 걸쳐 입고 밖으로 달려 나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To die one by one until none are left.
meanings.example: The native plants are slowly dying off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 토착 식물들이 서서히 죽어 없어지고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a different opinion; to make someone feel slightly ill (of food).
meanings.example: Spicy food usually disagrees with me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매운 음식은 보통 내 체질에 잘 안 맞는다 (먹으면 탈이 난다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop using something; to end a relationship with someone.
meanings.example: Have you finished with the newspaper? / She's finished with him for good.
meanings.exampleKorean: 신문 다 보셨나요 (다 쓰셨나요)? / 그녀는 그와 영원히 관계를 끊었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually become less friendly and stop having a relationship.
meanings.example: Over the years, the two friends began to drift apart.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수년에 걸쳐 그 두 친구는 사이가 서서히 멀어지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find out something by searching or asking many questions.
meanings.example: It took some time, but he managed to ferret out the truth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시간은 좀 걸렸지만, 그는 진실을 찾아내는(캐내는) 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become less strong or severe; to start to work less hard.
meanings.example: The rain finally began to ease off. / You should ease off a bit; you've been working too hard.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마침내 비가 약해지기 시작했다. / 좀 느긋하게 일하세요. 너무 열심히 일해왔잖아요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be said accidentally (of a secret); to leave a place quietly so that no one notices.
meanings.koreanDefinition: (비밀 등이) 입 밖에 나다, 몰래 빠져나가다
meanings.example: I didn't mean to tell her; it just slipped out. / He managed to slip out of the meeting unnoticed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀에게 말하려던 게 아니었어. 그냥 입 밖에 나와버렸어. / 그는 눈에 띄지 않게 회의에서 몰래 빠져나갈 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To allow someone's past mistakes to influence your opinion of them in a negative way.
meanings.koreanDefinition: (과거의 일로) ~를 나쁘게 생각하다, 원망하다, 불리하게 여기다
meanings.example: He made a mistake, but please don't hold it against him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 실수를 했지만, 부디 그 일로 그를 나쁘게 생각하지(원망하지) 말아 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bend your body forward quickly, especially because you are laughing or in pain.
meanings.example: The joke was so funny that we were all doubled over with laughter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 농담이 너무 웃겨서 우리 모두 웃느라 몸을 웅크릴(굽힐) 정도였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk for a long time in a very boring way.
meanings.example: The professor continued to drone on about the history of economic theory.
meanings.exampleKorean: 교수는 경제 이론의 역사에 대해 지루하게 계속 이야기를 늘어놓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To praise someone too much and give them a lot of attention because you want them to like you.
meanings.example: The staff were all fawning over the famous movie star.
meanings.exampleKorean: 직원들은 모두 그 유명한 영화배우에게 알랑거리며(아부하며) 지극정성으로 대했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall asleep because you are extremely tired; to cancel a plan at the last minute (informal).
meanings.example: I was so exhausted that I just flaked out on the sofa. / He flaked out on our dinner plans again.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너무 지쳐서 소파에 그냥 뻗어버렸다. / 그가 또 우리 저녁 약속을 펑크 냈다 (취소했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To sleep at someone's house for one night.
meanings.example: You're welcome to stay over if it's too late to drive home.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집에 운전해서 가기에 너무 늦었으면 자고 가도 좋아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To touch or play with something in a restless way; to spend time doing unimportant things.
meanings.example: Stop fiddling around with your phone and listen to me!
meanings.exampleKorean: 핸드폰 좀 그만 만지작거리고 내 말 좀 들어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To move up slowly or by small amounts.
meanings.example: Prices are starting to edge up again.
meanings.exampleKorean: 물가가 다시 조금씩 오르기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin a journey, a career, or a period of time in a particular way.
meanings.example: He started out as a junior clerk and eventually became the CEO.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 하급 점원으로 시작해서 마침내 CEO가 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To succeed in dealing with a difficult problem or situation; to make an important discovery.
meanings.example: Scientists hope to break through the barriers to find a cure for the disease.
meanings.exampleKorean: 과학자들은 질병의 치료법을 찾기 위해 장벽을 돌파하기를(비약적인 발전을 이루기를) 바라고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove problems or difficulties; to make something flat or smooth.
meanings.example: They are trying to smooth out the difficulties in the peace process.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 평화 프로세스의 어려움들을 해결하려(순조롭게 하려) 노력하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hide a problem or disagreement rather than finding a satisfactory solution to it.
meanings.example: The agreement only papers over the deep divisions within the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 합의는 당 내의 깊은 분열을 임시변통으로 숨기는(봉합하는) 것에 불과하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To defeat someone by a very small distance or amount.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 근소한 차이로 이기다, 간발의 차로 이기다
meanings.example: The incumbent mayor nosed out his challenger in the final count.
meanings.exampleKorean: 현직 시장이 최종 개표에서 도전자에게 근소한 차이로 이겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To flow or move out of a container or space in large numbers or amounts.
meanings.example: The crowd spilled out of the stadium after the game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경기 후에 군중들이 경기장 밖으로 쏟아져 나왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mention or discuss a subject briefly.
meanings.example: The report touches on several important issues.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 보고서는 몇 가지 중요한 이슈들을 간단히 다루고(언급하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To buy something quickly because it is cheap or exactly what you want.
meanings.example: The best tickets were snapped up within minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가장 좋은 표들은 몇 분 만에 덥석 팔려 나갔다 (낚아채 갔다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To barely manage to achieve something or to say something in a high, thin voice.
meanings.example: They managed to squeak out a one-point victory.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 간신히 1점 차 승리를 거두었다 (해냈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To send someone away, especially in a hurry.
meanings.example: She packed the children off to their grandparents for the holidays.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 휴가 동안 아이들을 조부모님 댁으로 급히 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To search someone for hidden weapons or drugs by feeling their clothes.
meanings.example: The security guards patted down all the fans at the concert.
meanings.exampleKorean: 보안 요원들이 콘서트장의 모든 팬을 대상으로 몸수색을 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To work steadily and persistently at something (British informal).
meanings.example: If you keep pegging away, you'll eventually succeed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 네가 계속 꾸준히 성실히 해 나간다면, 결국 성공할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go to bed and stay there because you are ill or unhappy.
meanings.example: She took to her bed with a severe cold.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 심한 감기로 앓아누웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue with a plan or activity in a determined way, especially when there are problems.
meanings.example: The company decided to press ahead with the new project despite the costs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 비용에도 불구하고 새로운 프로젝트를 강행하기로(계속 추진하기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get back something that you used to have, such as support, power, or a market share.
meanings.example: The team is working hard to win back the championship title.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 팀은 우승 타이틀을 되찾기 위해 열심히 노력하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop rising or falling and stay at the same level.
meanings.example: House prices have finally begun to level off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주택 가격이 마침내 안정되기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To relax and enjoy yourself, doing very little.
meanings.example: We spent the whole weekend lazing around by the pool.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 주말 내내 수영장 옆에서 빈둥거리며 시간을 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become known to the public (of a secret).
meanings.example: News of the merger leaked out before the official announcement.
meanings.exampleKorean: 합병 소식이 공식 발표 전에 밖으로 새어 나왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue to exist or have an effect in a new situation; to move something from one period to the next.
meanings.example: The stress of her job often carries over into her home life. / Unused vacation days can be carried over to next year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 직장에서의 스트레스가 종종 가정생활에까지 영향을 미친다. / 사용하지 않은 휴가는 내년으로 이월될 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hunt and eat another animal; to take advantage of someone weaker; to make someone feel very worried.
meanings.example: Eagles prey on small mammals. / Scammers often prey on elderly people. / Guilt began to prey on his mind.
meanings.exampleKorean: 독수리는 작은 포유류를 잡아먹는다. / 사기꾼들은 종종 노인들을 이용한다 (괴롭힌다). / 죄책감이 그의 마음을 괴롭히기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop doing something; to not include someone or something on a list.
meanings.example: We'll start again where we left off yesterday. / Why was my name left off the invitation list?
meanings.exampleKorean: 어제 중단했던 부분부터 다시 시작합시다. / 왜 제 이름이 초대 명단에서 제외되었나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To show great respect to someone; to submit to someone's authority.
meanings.example: They refused to bow down to the dictator.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 독재자에게 굴복하기를(복종하기를) 거부했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide something for people, especially food or entertainment; to provide a supply of something like water or gas.
meanings.example: The company laid on a buffet lunch for the guests.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 손님들을 위해 뷔페 점심을 제공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone believe something that is not true, especially that you love them or will do something for them.
meanings.example: I think he's just leading her on; he has no intention of marrying her.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 생각에 그는 그녀를 그냥 현혹시키고 있는 것 같아. 결혼할 의사도 없으면서.

meanings.englishDefinition: To flow out or be produced in large quantities.
meanings.example: Smoke poured forth from the chimney. / She poured forth her anger in a long letter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 굴뚝에서 연기가 쏟아져 나왔다 (나왔다). / 그녀는 긴 편지에 자신의 분노를 쏟아냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To praise someone or something very highly.
meanings.example: The critics praised her latest performance to the skies.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비평가들은 그녀의 최신 공연에 대해 입이 마르도록 칭찬했다 (극찬했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To win by a very small distance or amount.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 간발의 차로 이기다, 근소한 차이로 승리하다
meanings.example: He managed to win the race by a nose.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 간발의 차로 그 경주에서 승리할 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find or meet someone or something by accident.
meanings.example: I chanced upon an old friend in the supermarket.
meanings.exampleKorean: 슈퍼마켓에서 우연히 옛 친구를 만났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To combine things into a series; to manage to say or write something clearly.
meanings.example: He was so nervous he could hardly string two sentences together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 너무 긴장해서 두 문장조차 조리 있게 엮어내지(말하지) 못했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To block something so that nothing can pass through it.
meanings.example: The leaves are clogging up the drain. / Heavy traffic is clogging up the city center.
meanings.exampleKorean: 낙엽들이 배수구를 꽉 막고 있다. / 심한 교통 체증이 도심을 정체시키고(막고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To think about something carefully for a long time.
meanings.example: Let me chew on your suggestion for a while. / The dog is chewing on a bone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신의 제안에 대해 잠시 심사숙고해(깊이 생각해) 볼게요. / 개가 뼈다귀를 계속 씹고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become stronger or more determined; to make rules stricter.
meanings.example: He needs to toughen up if he wants to succeed in this business. / The government is toughening up the laws on crime.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 업계에서 성공하려면 그는 더 강해질 필요가 있다. / 정부는 범죄에 대한 법률을 강화하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend time doing nothing; to be left in a place where it should not be.
meanings.example: I'm just going to lie around all day. / There are old magazines lying around everywhere.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 하루 종일 그냥 빈둥거릴 거야. / 낡은 잡지들이 사방에 여기저기 굴러다니고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take the best part or people from a group.
meanings.example: Big companies often cream off the best graduates.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대기업들은 종종 가장 우수한 졸업생들(인재들)을 골라간다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To explain a plan or an idea in a general way.
meanings.example: The manager chalked out the main points of the new strategy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매니저는 새로운 전략의 주요 포인트들에 대한 윤곽을 잡았다 (설명했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To force a person or animal to leave a place.
meanings.example: The dog chased off the intruder.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 개가 침입자를 쫓아버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mark items on a list that have been dealt with.
meanings.example: She checked off each item as she packed it into the suitcase.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 여행 가방에 짐을 쌀 때마다 각 항목에 체크 표시를 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish or end.
meanings.example: The meeting finally came to a close after three hours.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의는 3시간 만에 마침내 마무리되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become involved in an activity that someone else has started, especially to get an advantage for yourself.
meanings.example: Now that the business is successful, everyone wants to get in on the act.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사업이 성공하자 이제는 모두가 한몫 끼려(편승하려) 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to tell someone to stop interfering in a situation that does not involve them (informal).
meanings.example: This is none of your business, so just butt out!
meanings.exampleKorean: 이건 네가 상관할 일이 아니니까 참견 좀 하지 마!

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase very quickly in value or amount.
meanings.example: Housing prices have rocketed up in recent years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 최근 몇 년 동안 주택 가격이 급증했다 (치솟았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To happen again (of a regular event).
meanings.example: I can't believe Christmas has rolled around again so soon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 크리스마스가 벌써 이렇게 빨리 다시 돌아왔다니 믿기지 않아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tell someone your ideas to see what they think about them.
meanings.koreanDefinition: (반응을 보려 아이디어를) 던져 보다, 의견을 묻다
meanings.example: Can I bounce a few ideas off you for the new project?
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 프로젝트에 대해 몇 가지 아이디어를 당신에게 던져 봐도(의견을 물어봐도) 될까요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To force someone to retire because they are old or no longer useful.
meanings.example: He felt like he was being put out to pasture before he was ready.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 준비가 되기도 전에 은퇴하게 된 기분이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To polish something until it shines; to improve your knowledge or skills.
meanings.example: I need to buff up my French before the trip.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여행 전에 프랑스어 실력을 좀 연마해야(다듬어야)겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide or suggest something, especially a prayer or a reason.
meanings.example: They offered up a prayer for peace. / He offered up several excuses for his absence.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 평화를 위해 기도를 올렸다 (봉헌했다). / 그는 결석에 대해 몇 가지 핑계를 제시했다 (내놓았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To become more intense or dangerous (of a feeling or situation).
meanings.example: Anger began to boil up inside him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 내면에서 분노가 끓어오르기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To register your arrival at a hotel, hospital, etc.
meanings.example: I've booked us in at a nice hotel in the city center.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시내에 있는 좋은 호텔에 우리를 예약해 두었어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue firing a gun; to work very hard at something.
meanings.example: The soldiers blazed away at the enemy. / She spent the afternoon blazing away at her typewriter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군인들은 적을 향해 계속 총을 쏘아댔다. / 그녀는 오후 내내 타자기를 두드리며 열심히 일했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To live a life that is full of adventure and risk.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 위태로운 삶을 살다, 아슬아슬한 삶을 살다
meanings.example: He likes to live on the edge, always looking for the next adrenaline rush.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 위태로운 삶을 사는 것을 좋아해서 항상 다음번의 짜릿함을 찾아다닌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To save something from failure at the last moment.
meanings.example: The team managed to pull a victory out of the fire in the final minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 팀은 마지막 몇 분 만에 가까스로 승리를 거머쥐었다 (위기에서 구해냈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To attack or criticize someone who has attacked or criticized you.
meanings.example: The company is striking back at its competitors with a new advertising campaign.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 새로운 광고 캠페인으로 경쟁사들에 반격하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take something out very quickly.
meanings.example: He whipped out his notebook and started taking notes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 재빨리 공책을 꺼내서 필기를 하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To defeat someone by dealing with them in a very firm and confident way.
meanings.example: The president managed to face down his critics and pass the new law.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대통령은 비판자들에 당당히 맞서서(그들을 굴복시키고) 새 법률을 통과시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To offer to help or provide information.
meanings.example: Several witnesses stepped forward to describe the accident.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몇몇 목격자들이 사고를 묘사하기 위해 앞으로 나섰다 (제보했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To take the best people or things from a group; to secretly take money from a business.
meanings.example: The insurance company was accused of skimming off the best customers. / He was caught skimming off profits from the company.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 보험사는 알짜 고객들만 골라 챙겼다는 비난을 받았다. / 그는 회사의 수익금을 가로채다가 적발되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something last as long as possible; to lose control of a vehicle and turn around and around.
meanings.example: He tried to spin out the meeting until the end of the day. / The car spun out on the icy road.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 회의를 하루가 끝날 때까지 오래 끌려고(늘리려고) 했다. / 그 차는 빙판길에서 회전하며 미끄러졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move back or away from something because you are frightened or disgusted.
meanings.example: She shrank away from the spider on the wall.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 벽에 붙은 거미를 보고 뒷걸음질 쳤다 (움츠러들었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to tell someone to be careful (British informal).
meanings.example: Mind out! There's a car coming.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조심해! 차가 오고 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn off an electrical device.
meanings.example: Please power off your mobile phones during the performance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공연 중에는 휴대폰 전원을 꺼 주시기 바랍니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize or attack someone; to try to do something.
meanings.example: The press had a pop at the minister for his comments. / Why don't you have a pop at the crossword?
meanings.exampleKorean: 언론은 장관의 발언에 대해 비난을 쏟아냈다 (공격했다). / 십자말풀이를 한 번 시도해보는 게 어때?

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a quick decision or give a good answer when something unexpected happens.
meanings.example: In this job, you need to be able to think on your feet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 일에서는 빠르게 판단하고 대처하는 능력이 필요합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reject or discard someone or something in a harsh way.
meanings.example: He was kicked to the curb by his girlfriend. / The old policies were finally kicked to the curb.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 여자친구에게 차였다 (버림받았다). / 낡은 정책들은 마침내 폐기되었다 (버려졌다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend a lot of money on something especially a luxury (British informal).
meanings.example: We decided to splash out on a fancy dinner for our anniversary.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 기념일을 위해 고급스러운 저녁 식사에 돈을 펑펑 쓰기로(지르기로) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn out very well, even if there were problems at the beginning.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 결과가 아주 좋게 끝나다, 만사가 잘 풀리다
meanings.example: Despite all the initial difficulties, everything is coming up roses for the new business.
meanings.exampleKorean: 초기의 모든 어려움에도 불구하고, 새로운 사업은 만사가 아주 잘 풀리고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something that seems impossible or to be perfect.
meanings.example: Some employees think the new manager can walk on water.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어떤 직원들은 새 매니저가 불가능한 일도 해낼 수 있는 사람(완벽한 사람)이라고 생각한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To visit a person or a place for a short time on your way to somewhere else.
meanings.example: I'll stop by your office on my way home.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집에 가는 길에 당신 사무실에 잠시 들를게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To watch someone or something carefully; to want to buy or have something.
meanings.example: I've had my eye on that watch for months. / The teacher has her eye on the students during the exam.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 몇 달 동안 저 시계를 눈여겨봐 왔다 (가지고 싶어 했다). / 선생님은 시험 중에 학생들을 주의 깊게 지켜보고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something that is no longer needed.
meanings.example: Could you help me clear away the dishes?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그릇들 치우는 것 좀 도와줄래?

meanings.englishDefinition: To buy and sell a particular type of goods; to involve oneself in a particular activity.
meanings.example: The shop deals in rare books. / His philosophy deals in universal truths.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 가게는 희귀 서적을 거래한다 (취급한다). / 그의 철학은 보편적 진리를 다룬다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a fire burn more slowly; to make a feeling or situation less strong.
meanings.example: The central bank is trying to damp down inflation. / She tried to damp down her excitement.
meanings.exampleKorean: 중앙은행은 인플레이션의 기세를 꺾으려 노력하고 있다. / 그녀는 흥분을 가라앉히려 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find information that proves someone has done something wrong (informal).
meanings.example: The detective finally got the goods on the suspect.
meanings.exampleKorean: 형사는 마침내 용의자의 약점(증거)을 잡았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be at the very back of a line of people or at the end of a race.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 맨 뒤에 처지다, 꼴찌를 하다, 후미를 맡다
meanings.example: The youngest children brought up the rear of the procession.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가장 어린 아이들이 행렬의 맨 뒤에 처져서 따라왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become lost or confused about where you are.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 길을 잃다, 방향 감각을 잃다, 헤매다
meanings.example: I got a bit turned around in the subway station.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 지하철역에서 방향 감각을 좀 잃고 헤맸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: An action that insults or upsets someone, especially when they were expecting support.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 모욕, 뒤통수를 치는 일, 갑작스러운 거절
meanings.example: Losing the funding was a real slap in the face for the research team.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자금 지원을 잃은 것은 연구팀에게 정말 모욕적인 일(뒤통수를 치는 일)이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reach a highest point, level, or amount.
meanings.example: The housing market seems to have finally topped out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주택 시장이 마침내 최고점에 도달한 것(한계에 이른 것)으로 보인다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To look around or search for something, especially an idea or a solution.
meanings.example: He was casting about for a way to explain his mistake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 실수를 설명할 방법을 궁리하고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mention something unpleasant from the past; to bring up a child without proper manners.
meanings.example: Why do you have to drag up all those old arguments? / Those children were just dragged up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 왜 그런 옛날 말다툼들을 다 들춰내야만 하니? / 저 아이들은 그냥 막 자랐다 (제대로 교육받지 못했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To mean or imply something.
meanings.example: I don't quite understand what you're driving at.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신이 무엇을 의도하는지(말하려 하는지) 잘 이해하지 못하겠어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To figure something out by using information or logic (informal).
meanings.example: I'm trying to dope out how this machine works.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 기계가 어떻게 작동하는지 알아내려고 노력 중이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone less successful or happy; to lower the quality or level of something.
