meanings.example: Don't let their negativity drag you down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들의 부정적인 기운이 당신을 지치게(기운 빠지게) 하지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize someone in order to make them seem less important (British informal).
meanings.example: He's always trying to do his colleagues down to look better himself.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신이 더 나아 보이기 위해 항상 동료들을 비방하려(깎아내리려) 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become unresponsive or stare blankly, usually due to exhaustion or boredom.
meanings.example: I usually just zombie out in front of the TV after work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 보통 퇴근 후에 TV 앞에서 그냥 멍하니 있는다 (넋을 놓는다).

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to invite someone to visit your home or location.
meanings.example: Why don't you come on over for coffee this afternoon?
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 오후에 우리 집에 커피 마시러 놀러 오는 게 어때?

meanings.englishDefinition: To make progress with something; to advance towards a better situation.
meanings.example: We need to find a way to move forward with the peace talks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 평화 회담을 진척시킬 방법을 찾아야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be successful in your career or life.
meanings.example: She's very ambitious and wants to get ahead in the company.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 야심이 아주 커서 회사에서 성공하고(출세하고) 싶어 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to tell someone to stop doing something annoying (informal).
meanings.example: Cut it out! You're making too much noise.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그만 좀 해! 너 너무 시끄럽게 굴고 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To capture someone and make them face trial in a court of law.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 법의 심판을 받게 하다, 검거하여 처벌하다
meanings.example: The police are determined to bring the killers to justice.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 살인범들이 법의 심판을 받게 하겠다고 다짐하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To delay something because you are not ready or need more time to think.
meanings.example: I tried to play for time by asking a lot of questions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 질문을 많이 해서 시간을 벌려고 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take a vehicle for a short drive to test it or enjoy it.
meanings.example: I just bought a new car; do you want to take it for a spin?
meanings.exampleKorean: 나 방금 새 차 샀는데, 한 바퀴 시운전해볼래?

meanings.englishDefinition: To investigate something thoroughly; to start eating something with enthusiasm.
meanings.example: The reporters began to dig into the company's financial records. / Dinner is ready, so dig into!
meanings.exampleKorean: 기자들은 그 회사의 재무 기록을 파헤치기 시작했다. / 저녁 다 됐으니까 맛있게 먹으렴 (먹기 시작하렴)!

meanings.englishDefinition: To discuss something thoroughly to solve a problem or reach an agreement.
meanings.example: We need to thresh out the details of the contract.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 계약서의 세부 사항들을 철저히 논의해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use language that is too difficult for someone to understand.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 이해 못 할 수준으로 말하다, 너무 어렵게 말하다
meanings.example: The scientist was talking over the students' heads.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 과학자는 학생들이 이해하기 너무 어려운 수준으로 말하고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give advice or warnings to someone who pays no attention to them.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 쇠귀에 경 읽기, 아무리 말해도 듣지 않다
meanings.example: Trying to convince him to quit smoking is like preaching to deaf ears.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그에게 담배를 끊으라고 설득하는 것은 쇠귀에 경 읽기(아무리 말해도 듣지 않는 일)이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move around in a confused or aimless way in a small area.
meanings.example: A crowd of fans was milling about outside the hotel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 팬 한 무리가 호텔 밖에서 서성거리고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become slower or less active.
meanings.example: Business usually slackens off during the winter months.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사업은 대개 겨울철 동안 느슨해진다 (활발하지 않다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage to fit into a small space or a busy schedule.
meanings.example: We managed to squeeze into the crowded elevator. / I'll try to squeeze you into my schedule this afternoon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 만원 엘리베이터에 비집고 들어가는 데 성공했다. / 오늘 오후에 제 일정에 당신을 끼워 넣어 볼게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly become very active or busy; to start working (of a machine).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 활기를 띠다, 갑자기 생기를 되찾다, 작동하기 시작하다
meanings.example: The town springs to life during the festival. / The engine suddenly sprang to life.
meanings.exampleKorean: 축제 기간 동안 마을은 활기를 띤다. / 엔진이 갑자기 작동하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall asleep very quickly; to lose consciousness suddenly.
meanings.example: He was so tired that he went out like a light as soon as his head hit the pillow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 너무 피곤해서 베개에 머리가 닿자마자 금방 잠들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone explain their mistakes or bad behavior and punish them if necessary.
meanings.example: The minister was called to account for the misuse of public funds.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장관은 공금 유용에 대해 책임을 추궁당했다 (해명을 요구받았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To scream very loudly and for a long time.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 악을 쓰며 비명을 지르다, 목청껏 소리 지르다
meanings.example: The baby was screaming its head off all night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아기가 밤새도록 악을 쓰며 비명을 질러댔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deceive someone for a long time about what your real intentions are.
meanings.example: She had no intention of marrying him; she was just stringing him along.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그와 결혼할 의사가 없었다. 그냥 그를 희망 고문하고 있었을 뿐이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone or something look more stylish or clean.
meanings.example: We need to spiff up the living room before the guests arrive.
meanings.exampleKorean: 손님들이 오기 전에 거실을 좀 깔끔하게 단장해야겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk enthusiastically and in great detail about a technical or niche subject.
meanings.example: The two programmers spent the whole evening nerding out over the new software update.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 프로그래머는 저녁 내내 새로운 소프트웨어 업데이트에 대해 열광하며 이야기를 나눴다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid discussing a difficult subject directly.
meanings.example: He skirted around the issue of his recent resignation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 최근 사임 문제에 대해 언급을 피하며 회피했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To succeed after a long and difficult struggle.
meanings.example: It's been a tough year, but we'll win through in the end.
meanings.exampleKorean: 힘든 한 해였지만, 우리는 결국 끝내 이겨낼(성공할) 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a pot of tea; to start to develop (of a problem or a storm).
meanings.example: I'll brew up some tea. / There's a storm brewing up in the distance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 차를 좀 끓일게. / 멀리서 폭풍이 몰려오려(일어나려) 하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be elected or appointed to a position of political leadership.
meanings.example: The new party came to power after the last election.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 새로운 정당은 지난 선거 이후에 권력을 잡았다 (집권했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To travel somewhere by plane, especially for a holiday.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 비행기 타고 떠나다, 훌쩍 여행 가다
meanings.example: They're jetting off to Hawaii for their honeymoon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 신혼여행을 위해 하와이로 훌쩍 떠날 예정이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a lighthearted or silly way to make people laugh.
meanings.example: Stop joking around and get back to work!
meanings.exampleKorean: 농담하며 노는 것 좀 그만하고 다시 업무로 돌아가!

meanings.englishDefinition: To do or say something sooner than it should be done or said; to be too excited about the future.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 너무 앞서가다, 김칫국부터 마시다, 성급하게 굴다
meanings.example: I'm getting ahead of myself; let's finish this part before talking about the next project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 너무 앞서가고 있네요. 다음 프로젝트를 말하기 전에 이 부분부터 끝냅시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be familiar with a place or a subject.
meanings.example: He really knows his way around a computer. / She knows her way around London.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 정말 컴퓨터에 정통하다. / 그녀는 런던 지리에 아주 밝다 (잘 안다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue to support someone or something, especially in a difficult situation.
meanings.example: She promised to stick by him no matter what. / You should stick by your original decision.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 무슨 일이 있어도 그의 곁을 지키겠다고 약속했다. / 당신은 당신의 원래 결정을 고수해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To defeat someone by a very small amount or at the last moment (British informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 간발의 차로 이기다, 마지막 순간에 앞서다
meanings.example: He was pipped to the post by his rival in the final minute.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 마지막 순간에 라이벌에게 간발의 차로 패했다 (라이벌이 그를 간발의 차로 이겼다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To find out if someone is doing what they should be doing; to have a medical examination.
meanings.example: My parents are always checking up on me. / You should go to the doctor for a check-up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 부모님은 항상 내가 잘하고 있는지 확인하신다. / 건강 검진을 받으러 병원에 가보는 게 좋겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue moving forward or making progress, despite difficulties.
meanings.example: The soldiers pressed forward despite the heavy rain.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군인들은 폭우에도 불구하고 계속 전진했다 (밀고 나갔다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a computer icon appear light gray to show that it is not available for use.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 비활성화되다, (회색으로 변해) 선택할 수 없게 되다
meanings.example: The 'Delete' button is grayed out because you haven't selected a file.
meanings.exampleKorean: 파일을 선택하지 않았기 때문에 '삭제' 버튼이 비활성화되어(회색으로 변해) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decorate something in a very flashy or extravagant way (informal).
meanings.example: He spent thousands of dollars to bling out his new car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 새 차를 화려하게 꾸미기 위해 수천 달러를 썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To laugh secretly or quietly at someone else's mistake.
meanings.example: He was so confident in his wrong answer that everyone else was laughing in their sleeves.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 오답을 너무 자신 있게 말해서 다른 사람들은 모두 속으로 몰래 비웃고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To compete with the goal of winning, not just for fun.
meanings.example: He's a serious competitor who always plays to win.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 이기기 위해 경기하는 진지한 경쟁자이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove a scratch or minor problem by polishing; to resolve a minor conflict.
meanings.example: We need to buff out the minor disagreements before the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의 전에 사소한 이견들을 해결할(풀어낼) 필요가 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone responsible for something bad that they have done.
meanings.example: The public is calling to hold the officials to account for the disaster.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대중은 참사에 대해 공무원들에게 책임을 물을 것을 요구하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be hidden by being in a place where it is very easy to see but people do not notice it.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 눈앞에 두고도 못 찾다, 등잔 밑이 어둡다
meanings.example: The stolen painting was hiding in plain sight in the museum's storage room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 도난당한 그림은 박물관 저장실의 아주 잘 보이는 곳에 숨겨져(방치되어) 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very successful and to be recognized for your skills and qualities.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 진가를 발휘하다, 역량을 충분히 나타내다, 빛을 보다
meanings.example: She has really come into her own as a manager.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 매니저로서 정말 자신의 진가를 발휘하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To admire someone so much that you do not see their faults.
meanings.example: He put her on a pedestal and was devastated when he discovered she wasn't perfect.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그녀를 지나치게 받들었기에(우상시했기에) 그녀가 완벽하지 않다는 것을 알았을 때 큰 충격을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very easy to say or to sound pleasant when spoken.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 입에 착 붙다, 발음하기 쉽다, 술술 나오다
meanings.example: Her name is quite long, but it rolls off the tongue.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 이름은 꽤 길지만, 입에 아주 착 붙는다 (발음하기 쉽다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be an unavoidable part of a job or situation.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 당연히 따라오다, (그 일의) 숙명이다, 어쩔 수 없는 일이다
meanings.example: Stress comes with the territory when you are a surgeon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 외과 의사라면 스트레스는 당연히 따라오는(어쩔 수 없는) 일이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become more careful or less active, especially because you have less money or have failed in some way.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 의욕을 꺾다, 조심스럽게 행동하다, 지출을 줄이다
meanings.example: The company has had to pull in its horns because of the economic crisis.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경제 위기 때문에 그 회사는 활동을 줄이고 조심스럽게 행동해야(지출을 줄여야) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become unable to speak or move because of fear or nervousness; to stop working (of a machine).
meanings.example: He completely froze up during the interview. / My computer freezes up whenever I try to open this file.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 면접 중에 완전히 얼어붙어(굳어버려) 버렸다. / 이 파일을 열려고 할 때마다 내 컴퓨터가 멈춘다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To call a meeting to start officially; to make people in a noisy place behave quietly.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 개회를 선언하다, 질서를 잡다, 정숙하게 하다
meanings.example: The chairman brought the meeting to order at 10 AM.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의장은 오전 10시에 개회를 선언했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To destroy something completely by an explosion.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 산산조각 내다, 완전히 파괴하다, 박살 내다
meanings.example: The bridge was blown to kingdom come by the air strike.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 다리는 공습에 의해 완전히 파괴되었다 (산산조각 났다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to achieve something difficult; to attack or criticize someone.
meanings.example: She decided to take a run at the presidency.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 대통령직에 도전하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wash your hands and arms thoroughly before performing surgery.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 수술 전 손을 씻다, (수술 준비를 위해) 소독하다
meanings.example: The surgeon began to scrub in for the operation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 외과 의사는 수술을 위해 손을 씻고 소독하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become quiet and behave correctly in a meeting or a courtroom.
meanings.example: The courtroom came to order as the judge entered.
meanings.exampleKorean: 판사가 입장하자 법정이 정숙해졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become covered with ice (of a lake or river).
meanings.example: The lake usually freezes over in late December.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 호수는 보통 12월 말에 얼음으로 뒤덮인다 (꽁꽁 언다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To reveal a secret or a scandal to the public.
meanings.example: The whistleblower blew the lid off the company's corruption.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내부 고발자가 회사의 부패 비리를 폭로했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something less strong or severe, especially something unpleasant.
meanings.example: A hot bath will take the edge off your stress. / I had a small snack to take the edge off my hunger.
meanings.exampleKorean: 따뜻한 목욕이 당신의 스트레스를 누그러뜨려(덜어) 줄 거예요. / 배고픔을 좀 가시려고 간단한 간식을 먹었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To include a particular amount or fact when you are calculating or planning something.
meanings.example: You need to factor in the cost of insurance when buying a car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 차를 살 때 보험 비용을 반드시 고려해야(포함해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wait until an unpleasant event or situation has finished.
meanings.example: We decided to wait out the storm in a small cafe.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 작은 카페에서 폭풍이 지나가기를(끝나기를) 기다리기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make problems or difficulties seem less important; to calm down a difficult situation.
meanings.example: She tried to smooth over the disagreement between her two brothers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 두 형제 사이의 갈등을 잘 수습하려(무마하려) 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use something for a purpose other than its original one, because of an urgent need.
meanings.example: With so many people sick, the retired doctors were pressed into service.
meanings.exampleKorean: 환자가 너무 많아서 은퇴한 의사들이 긴급 투입되었다 (동원되었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To support someone or something; to guarantee the quality of a product.
meanings.example: The company stands behind every product it sells.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 판매하는 모든 제품의 품질을 책임진다 (보증한다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To experience a difficult or dangerous situation and survive.
meanings.example: He has lived through two world wars.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 두 번의 세계 대전을 겪어냈다 (그 속에서 살아남았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to like someone or something.
meanings.example: She took a liking to her new neighbor immediately.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 즉시 새 이웃에게 호감을 가졌다 (이웃이 좋아졌다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To perfect a performance or process through focus and adjustment (slang).
meanings.example: After weeks of practice, the band finally dialed it in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수주간의 연습 끝에 밴드는 마침내 완벽한 합을 맞췄다 (완벽하게 해냈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To mention something so that people notice it or deal with it (British informal).
meanings.example: I'd like to flag up a few potential problems with the plan.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 계획의 몇 가지 잠재적인 문제점들을 미리 언급하고(알리고) 싶습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To appear above the surface (of rocks); to appear or happen unexpectedly.
meanings.example: Granite crops out along the coast. / New problems often crop out during the testing phase.
meanings.exampleKorean: 화강암이 해안을 따라 노출되어 있다. / 테스트 단계에서 종종 새로운 문제들이 나타나곤 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage to live on a very small amount of money; to barely succeed in something.
meanings.example: We can just about scrape by on my salary. / He managed to scrape by in the final exam.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 월급으로 우리는 그냥저냥 간신히 살아가고 있다. / 그는 기말고사에서 간신히 턱걸이했다 (합격했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To consider something to be very important or valuable.
meanings.example: He sets great store by honesty.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 정직을 아주 중요하게 여긴다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid becoming too friendly with someone.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 적당한 거리를 두다, 가까이 하지 않다
meanings.example: She keeps her colleagues at arm's length.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 동료들과 적당히 거리를 두고 지낸다 (지나치게 친해지지 않는다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To annoy someone without intending to.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 기분을 상하게 하다, 짜증 나게 하다
meanings.example: She has a way of rubbing people up the wrong way.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 본의 아니게 사람들 기분을 상하게 만드는 면이 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A person or thing that is certain to win a competition or election (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 확실한 우승 후보, 당선이 확실한 사람
meanings.example: He is a shoo-in for the award.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그 상의 확실한 수상 후보(우승 후보)이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find or get something that you have been looking for.
meanings.example: I've been trying to lay my hands on a copy of that rare book.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 저 희귀 서적 한 권을 손에 넣으려(찾아내려) 애써왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To experience a severe failure or collapse; to become extremely upset and lose control.
meanings.example: The global economy experienced a total meltdown. / The toddler had a complete meltdown in the store.
meanings.exampleKorean: 세계 경제가 완전히 붕괴(노심 용융과 같은 참사)를 겪었다. / 그 어린아이는 가게에서 완전히 울고불고 난리가 났다 (정신적으로 무너졌다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop yourself from showing an emotion; to not tell someone something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 억제하다, 비밀로 하다, 뒤로 물러나 있다
meanings.example: She struggled to keep back her tears. / He kept back some important information from the police.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 눈물을 억제하려 (참으려) 애썼다. / 그는 경찰에게 중요한 정보 몇 가지를 비밀로 했다 (말하지 않았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To discuss something in detail; to start doing a particular type of work.
meanings.example: I don't want to go into details right now. / She decided to go into medicine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지금 당장 상세한 내용을 논의하고 싶지는 않아요. / 그녀는 의학계로 나가기로(의사 일을 시작하기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something with very little effort or interest (informal).
meanings.example: It felt like the band was just mailing it in during their performance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 밴드가 공연 내내 그냥 대충 건성으로 하고 있다는 느낌이 들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put something in a place where it is kept until it is needed.
meanings.example: The winter clothes were stored away in the attic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 겨울옷들은 다락방에 치워(저장해) 두었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start living in a new house; to start to be involved in a new area of business.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 이사 오다, (시장 등에) 진출하다, 공격적으로 나서다
meanings.example: Our new neighbors are moving in today. / Competitors are starting to move in on our market.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 새 이웃들이 오늘 이사 온다. / 경쟁자들이 우리 시장으로 공격적으로 진출하기(밀고 들어오기) 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To show the shape or position of something by drawing lines around it; to make someone seem different from others.
meanings.example: The football pitch was marked out with white lines. / His talent marks him out from the rest of the students.
meanings.exampleKorean: 축구장은 흰 선으로 구획되어(표시되어) 있었다. / 그의 재능은 그를 다른 학생들과 구별되게(특징짓게) 해준다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find good things in a bad situation.
meanings.example: Look on the bright side—at least you didn't lose any money.
meanings.exampleKorean: 긍정적으로 생각해 봐. 적어도 돈은 하나도 안 잃었잖아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to happen or have an effect.
meanings.example: Personal feelings should not come into play when making a business decision.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비즈니스 결정을 내릴 때 개인적인 감정이 작용해서는(영향을 미쳐서는) 안 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce the size, amount, or importance of something.
meanings.example: The company had to scale back its expansion plans due to the recession.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 불황 때문에 확장 계획을 줄여야(축소해야) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deceive or cheat someone.
meanings.example: I think that used car salesman really took you for a ride.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 생각에 저 중고차 외판원이 널 정말 속인(기만한) 것 같아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make an effort to produce a particular quality or feeling, especially courage.
meanings.example: He had to summon up all his courage to ask her out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그녀에게 데이트 신청을 하기 위해 모든 용기를 쥐어짜 내야(불러일으켜야) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fight or compete with someone until one person wins.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 끝까지 싸우다, 결판을 내다, 격렬히 경쟁하다
meanings.example: The two teams had to slug it out in overtime to decide the winner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 팀은 승자를 결정하기 위해 연장전에서 끝까지 격렬하게 맞붙어야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave as if you are not ashamed of something, even though you should be.
meanings.example: She decided to brazen it out and act as if nothing had happened.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 아무 일도 없었던 것처럼 뻔뻔하게 행동하기로(당당한 척하기로) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move quickly with short steps, especially to escape.
meanings.example: The mouse scurried away as soon as I turned on the light.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 불을 켜자마자 생쥐가 황급히 달아났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use something as the foundation for an idea or a story.
meanings.example: The movie is based on a true story.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 영화는 실화를 바탕으로 하고(실화에 근거하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take someone or something from one place to another; to persuade someone to change their opinion.
meanings.example: Why don't you bring your friends over tonight? / We managed to bring them over to our way of thinking.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 밤에 친구들을 우리 집으로 데려오는 게 어때? / 우리는 그들을 설득해서 우리 생각에 동조하게 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat something quickly and without enough attention; to ignore a mistake or problem.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 대충 훑어보다, 흐지부지 넘기다, 얼렁뚱땅 넘어가다
meanings.example: The report slurs over the main causes of the failure.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 보고서는 실패의 주요 원인들을 대충 훑어보고(얼렁뚱땅 넘어가고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk for a long time about things that are not important (British informal).
meanings.example: He's been rabbiting on about his new hobby for an hour.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 새로운 취미에 대해 한 시간째 지루하게 떠들고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To want to get or achieve something very much.
meanings.example: She had set her heart on becoming a doctor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 의사가 되기로 마음을 굳혔다 (의사가 되기를 간절히 원했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To read something quickly to get the main idea.
meanings.example: I only had time to skim through the report before the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의 전에 보고서를 대충 훑어볼 시간밖에 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid trouble by not being noticed; to focus on your work without distraction.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 몸을 사리다, 조용히 지내다, 일에만 몰두하다
meanings.example: He decided to keep his head down and wait for the scandal to blow over.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 몸을 사리며 스캔들이 잠잠해지기를 기다리기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish something in a very exciting or impressive way.
meanings.example: The band wanted to go out with a bang on their final tour.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 밴드는 마지막 투어에서 화려하게 마무리하기를 원했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To create a new, independent company from part of an existing one; to produce a new TV show based on a previous one.
meanings.example: The company plans to spin off its luxury car division. / The popular show has already spun off two new series.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 고급차 부문을 분사할 계획이다. / 그 인기 프로그램은 벌써 두 개의 새로운 시리즈를 파생시켰다 (속편으로 만들었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay friendly with someone, especially because they are powerful and can help you.
meanings.example: It's a good idea to keep in with the boss.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상사와 친하게 지내는(비위를 맞추는) 것은 좋은 생각이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To include someone in a group that receives information about something (informal).
meanings.example: Please loop me in on any further updates regarding this project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 프로젝트에 관한 추가 업데이트 사항이 있으면 저에게도 공유해 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To visit someone or somewhere unexpectedly in large numbers.
meanings.example: A horde of tourists descended upon the quiet village.
meanings.exampleKorean: 관광객 무리가 조용한 마을에 갑자기 들이닥쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To experience the more exciting or dangerous parts of life.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 모험적인(위험한) 삶을 살다, 아슬아슬한 행동을 하다
meanings.example: After years of working in an office, he decided to walk on the wild side and travel the world alone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수년간 사무실에서 일한 후, 그는 위험한 모험을 떠나기로(모험적인 삶을 살기로) 결심하고 혼자 세계 여행을 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrive and register at a hotel or hospital; to investigate something.
meanings.example: He checked into the hospital for a minor operation. / The police are checking into his story.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 간단한 수술을 위해 병원에 입원했다. / 경찰은 그의 이야기가 사실인지 조사하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To share the cost of something with a group of people (British informal).
meanings.example: We all clubbed together to buy her a retirement present.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 그녀의 퇴직 선물을 사기 위해 모두 돈을 모았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make it very obvious that you want to have a romantic relationship with someone.
meanings.example: She was practically throwing herself at him all night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 밤새도록 그에게 거의 노골적으로 매달렸다 (구애했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To break up a relationship or a group, especially with a fight.
meanings.example: They had a huge argument and finally busted up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 크게 말다툼을 하고 결국 싸우고 헤어졌다 (결별했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To take time to enjoy the simple pleasures of life, especially when you are busy.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 여유를 즐기다, 일상의 소소한 기쁨을 누리다
meanings.example: You're working too hard; you need to stop and smell the roses occasionally.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너 너무 열심히 일하고 있어. 가끔은 좀 쉬면서 여유를 즐길(일상의 기쁨을 누릴) 필요가 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become successful or active again after a period of failure or absence.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 재기하다, 기적적으로 되살아나다, 다시 활기를 찾다
meanings.example: The company was near bankruptcy but managed to come back from the dead.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 거의 파산 직전이었지만 기적적으로 재기했다 (되살아났다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To oppose or contradict something, such as a rule or a belief.
meanings.example: Stealing goes against everything I believe in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 도둑질은 내 모든 신념에 어긋나는(위배되는) 일이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force someone or something out of a place or situation by taking up all the space or resources.
meanings.example: Small shops are being crowded out by large supermarkets.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작은 상점들이 대형 슈퍼마켓들에 의해 밀려나고(도태되고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To work less hard than usual; to become less active.
meanings.example: Workers tend to slack off on Friday afternoons.
meanings.exampleKorean: 노동자들은 금요일 오후가 되면 게으름을 피우는(느슨해지는) 경향이 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue to exist or be enough for a period of time, especially a difficult one.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 끝까지 버티다, 견뎌내다, (식량 등이) 떨어지지 않고 가다
meanings.example: Do you think our supplies will last out the week?
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 보급품이 이번 주 끝까지 버틸(충분할) 것 같니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop or destroy something bad completely.
meanings.example: The government is determined to stomp out corruption.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 부패를 근절하겠다고(뿌리 뽑겠다고) 다짐하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To seem less good when compared with someone or something else.
meanings.example: His second novel suffers by comparison with his first one.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 두 번째 소설은 첫 번째 소설과 비교했을 때 손색이 있다 (좀 뒤떨어진다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be more successful or helpful than expected (British informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 기대 이상의 결과를 내다, (예상외로) 일이 잘 풀리다
meanings.example: I was worried about the interview, but everything turned up trumps.
meanings.exampleKorean: 면접 때문에 걱정했는데, 모든 것이 예상외로 아주 잘 풀렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make sure that someone is doing what they should be doing.
meanings.example: My boss always checks up on me to see if I'm working.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 사장님은 내가 일하고 있는지 확인하기 위해(살피기 위해) 항상 체크하신다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrive somewhere without intending to; to end up in a particular situation.
meanings.example: We took the wrong turn and fetched up in a tiny village.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 길을 잘못 들어서 결국 작은 마을에 도달하게 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To teach students only the information that they need to know to pass an exam.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 시험 위주로 가르치다, 입시 위주 교육을 하다
meanings.example: Many critics argue that schools are simply teaching to the test.
meanings.exampleKorean: 많은 비평가들은 학교가 단순히 시험 위주로만 가르치고 있다고 주장한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly start crying.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 갑자기 울음을 터뜨리다, 왈칵 눈물을 쏟다
meanings.example: She burst into tears when she heard the news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그 소식을 듣고 갑자기 울음을 터뜨렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sit down with other people who are already sitting at a table.
meanings.example: Pull up a chair and join us for lunch!
meanings.exampleKorean: 의자 당겨 앉아서 우리랑 같이 점심 먹자!

meanings.englishDefinition: To help someone who is in a difficult or dangerous situation.
meanings.example: A passerby came to the old man's aid after he fell.
meanings.exampleKorean: 노인이 넘어진 후 한 행인이 그를 도우러 왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use up or destroy something gradually; to use up a part of a supply of money or time.
meanings.example: Commuting costs are really eating into my savings. / Acid eats into the metal surface.
meanings.exampleKorean: 통근 비용이 정말 내 저축을 잠식하고(축내고) 있다. / 산이 금속 표면을 부식시킨다 (좀먹는다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly start burning with a lot of heat and light.
meanings.example: The car burst into flame immediately after the crash.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 차는 충돌 직후 갑자기 불길이 치솟았다 (확 타올랐다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To believe someone or something to be someone or something else, especially by mistake.
meanings.example: I took you for your brother; you look so much alike. / What do you take me for? An idiot?
meanings.exampleKorean: 너를 네 형으로 잘못 알았어. 둘이 정말 닮았네. / 나를 뭘로 보는 거야? 바보로 알아?

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat small amounts of food without enthusiasm; to criticize someone repeatedly.
meanings.example: She was just pecking at her salad. / Why are you always pecking at me about my chores?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 샐러드를 그냥 깨작거리고만 있었다. / 왜 집안일 가지고 나한테 항상 잔소리하는 거야?

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare for or begin a war or a fight.
meanings.example: The two countries are ready to take up the hatchet over the border dispute.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 나라는 국경 분쟁 때문에 전쟁에 나설(싸움을 시작할) 준비가 되어 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pull a piece of clothing higher on your body; to connect a vehicle to another.
meanings.example: He hitched up his trousers and sat down. / They hitched up the trailer to the truck.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 바지를 끌어올려(고쳐 입고) 자리에 앉았다. / 그들은 트레일러를 트럭에 연결했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give something away for much less than its value, especially as part of an agreement.
meanings.example: The government was accused of bargaining away the nation's resources.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 국가 자원을 헐값에 넘겼다는(포기했다는) 비난을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To throw something out violently; to say something suddenly and without thinking.
meanings.example: He flung out his arms in frustration. / She flung out a sharp retort.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 좌절감에 팔을 휘둘렀다 (내던졌다). / 그녀는 날카로운 반박을 갑자기 내뱉었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To follow someone around in an attempt to get their attention or favor.
meanings.example: He's been dangling after her all semester, hoping for a date.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 데이트 한 번 해보려고 이번 학기 내내 그녀 뒤를 쫓아다니고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be saved from a difficult situation by something that happens at the last minute.
meanings.example: I didn't know the answer, but then the phone rang—I was saved by the bell.
meanings.exampleKorean: 답을 몰라 쩔쩔맸는데 그때 전화가 울렸어. 덕분에 가까스로 위기를 모면했지.

meanings.englishDefinition: To happen or be given to someone without any effort from them.
meanings.example: He didn't even apply for the job; it just fell into his lap.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그 일자리에 지원조차 하지 않았어. 그냥 행운처럼 굴러들어 왔지.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be much better than someone or something else.
meanings.example: Her cooking skills put mine to shame.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 요리 실력은 내 실력을 무색하게 만든다 (훨씬 뛰어나다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To complete something successfully; to help someone survive a difficult time.
meanings.example: He is determined to carry through his plans. / His faith carried him through the tragedy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 계획을 완수하기로 다짐했다. / 그의 신념이 그 비극적인 시간을 견디게 해주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have good intentions, even if the result is not good.
meanings.example: I know her advice was annoying, but it came from a good place.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 조언이 짜증 났다는 건 알지만, 그래도 좋은 의도에서 한 말이었어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place very quickly.
meanings.example: I'm sorry I have to rush off, but I have a meeting in ten minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 서둘러 떠나야 해서 미안해요. 10분 후에 회의가 있거든요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move something to a future time or the next stage of a process.
meanings.example: The surplus from this year will be carried forward to next year's budget.
meanings.exampleKorean: 올해의 흑자분은 내년 예산으로 이월될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be extremely angry.
meanings.example: The boss was frothing at the mouth when he saw the sales report.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사장은 매출 보고서를 보고 몹시 분개했다 (입에 거품을 물었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To live or work together in a reasonably friendly way (British informal).
meanings.example: They manage to rub along together despite their differences.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 서로의 차이에도 불구하고 그럭저럭 잘 지낸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To retire from a job or activity; to settle down in a place.
meanings.example: He decided it was finally time to hang up his hat after 40 years of teaching.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 40년간의 교직 생활을 마치고 마침내 은퇴할 때라고 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something in an incomplete or careless way (usually used in the negative).
meanings.example: When she throws a party, she doesn't do things by halves.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 파티를 열 때 대충 하는 법이 없다 (아주 철저하게 한다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To fight or compete until one side wins or the problem is solved.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 끝까지 싸워 결판을 내다, 승부를 겨루다
meanings.example: The two companies are fighting it out in court.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 회사는 법정에서 끝까지 싸워 결판을 내고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act in a way that prevents oneself from being successful.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 자신의 앞길을 스스로 가로막다, 스스로에게 손해를 끼치다
meanings.example: If you refuse this offer, you'll just be standing in your own light.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신이 이 제안을 거절한다면, 그건 그냥 당신의 앞길을 스스로 가로막는 일이 될 거예요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attack or criticize someone strongly; to start doing something (especially eating) with enthusiasm.
meanings.example: The critics pitched into the actor's performance. / We were so hungry we pitched into the pizza immediately.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비평가들은 그 배우의 연기를 맹렬히 공격했다 (비판했다). / 우리는 너무 배가 고파서 즉시 피자를 열심히 먹기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone feel ashamed or embarrassed; to outshine someone.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 얼굴을 붉히게 하다, 무색하게 만들다, 부끄럽게 하다
meanings.example: His generosity put the rest of us to the blush.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 관대함은 나머지 우리를 무색하게 만들었다 (부끄럽게 했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone feel nervous or excited.
meanings.example: The athletes were all keyed up before the big race.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선수들은 큰 경기를 앞두고 모두 긴장해 있었다 (흥분해 있었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To repeat the same thing over and over again in a boring way.
meanings.example: I'm tired of hearing him harp on that one string about his past success.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 자신의 과거 성공에 대해 같은 말만 되풀이하는 것을 듣는 데 질렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To describe or imitate someone perfectly; to get along well with someone (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 똑같이 흉내 내다, 딱 들어맞다, (금방) 친해지다
meanings.example: The comedian hit off the president's voice perfectly. / The two of them hit it off immediately.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 코미디언은 대통령의 목소리를 완벽하게 흉내 냈다. / 그들 둘은 즉시 죽이 잘 맞아 금방 친해졌다 (hit it off).

meanings.englishDefinition: To add up numbers to find a total amount.
meanings.example: Let me figure up the total cost of the trip.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여행 총비용이 얼마인지 제가 계산해 볼게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clap very loudly and for a long time to show great approval.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 우레와 같은 박수를 보내다, 열렬히 환호하다
meanings.example: The pianist's performance was applauded to the echo.
meanings.exampleKorean: 피아니스트의 연주에 우레와 같은 박수가 쏟아졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate an area from a larger space by using walls or partitions.
meanings.example: We need to box off a small area for the office.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사무실을 위해 작은 구역을 나눌(칸막이를 할) 필요가 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To swallow something quickly; to consume or destroy something completely.
meanings.example: The child gulped up his milk. / The small company was gulped up by its competitor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이는 우유를 꿀꺽 다 삼켰다. / 그 작은 회사는 경쟁사에 의해 집어삼켜졌다 (인수되었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To shout offensive words at someone because you are angry (informal).
meanings.example: He cussed out the driver who cut him off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 끼어든 운전자에게 심한 욕설을 퍼부었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue to do something so that you do not lose your skill.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 기량을 유지하다, 감각을 잃지 않게 계속하다
meanings.example: He still does some part-time teaching just to keep his hand in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 감각을 잃지 않으려고(기량을 유지하려고) 여전히 파트타임으로 교직 일을 조금씩 하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be involved in something; to have an influence on something.
meanings.example: She has a finger in every project in the department.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 부서 내의 모든 프로젝트에 관여하고(개입하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To result in nothing; to fail completely after a grand start.
meanings.example: All his ambitious plans for the new company ended in smoke.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 회사를 향한 그의 모든 야심 찬 계획들은 허무하게 수포로 돌아갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To compete with someone to see who is better or stronger.
meanings.example: He was eager to measure swords with the reigning world champion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 현 세계 챔피언과 실력을 겨루고 싶어 안달이 나 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin to do something with enthusiasm, especially eating.
meanings.example: Dinner was served and we all fell to with great appetite. / They fell to discussing the new project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저녁 식사가 차려졌고 우리 모두는 아주 맛있게 먹기 시작했다. / 그들은 새로운 프로젝트를 논의하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept a challenge or invitation to a competition.
meanings.example: The young lawyer was ready to take up the glove and fight the big corporation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 젊은 변호사는 도전을 받아들여(대결에 응해) 대기업과 맞서 싸울 준비가 되어 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To expose oneself to danger or difficulty in order to protect or help others.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 위험을 무릅쓰고 방어하다, 대신 맞서다, 희생하다
meanings.example: The teachers stood in the gap to protect the students' rights during the budget cuts.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님들은 예산 삭감 기간 동안 학생들의 권리를 보호하기 위해 위험을 무릅쓰고 대신 맞섰다 (방패막이가 되었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent a plan or an idea from happening or continuing (informal).
meanings.example: The bad news knocked our holiday plans on the head.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 나쁜 소식이 우리의 휴가 계획을 완전히 중단시켜(좌절시켜) 버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something by scratching; to manage to escape from a difficult situation with effort.
meanings.example: The climber managed to claw away the loose rocks. / The team had to claw away from a three-goal deficit.
meanings.exampleKorean: 등반가는 헐거운 바위들을 긁어내(치워내)는 데 성공했다. / 그 팀은 3골 차의 열세에서 필사적으로 빠져나와야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To scatter things carelessly; to use your money or power in an obvious and annoying way.
meanings.example: Stop throwing your money about! / He likes to throw his weight about in the office.
meanings.exampleKorean: 돈 좀 그렇게 함부로 뿌리고(펑펑 쓰고) 다니지 마! / 그는 사무실에서 자신의 권력을 휘두르는 것을 좋아한다 (throw one's weight about).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stand or walk with the toes pointed inward; (of vehicle wheels) to be angled inward at the front.
meanings.example: The alignment is off; the front wheels toe in too much.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정렬이 잘못되었네요. 앞바퀴가 너무 안쪽으로 모여(toe in) 있어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sell something at an auction.
meanings.example: The estate will be brought to the hammer next month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 토지는 다음 달에 경매에 부쳐질 예정이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To succeed in getting a law or a plan officially accepted, especially quickly or despite opposition.
meanings.example: The government is trying to push through the new tax reform.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 새로운 세제 개혁안을 통과시키려 노력하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a different way from other people; to have your own unique ideas.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 독자적인 길을 걷다, 자신만의 방식대로 살다
meanings.example: He's always marched to a different beat, never caring what others think.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 남들이 어떻게 생각하든 신경 쓰지 않고 항상 자신만의 방식대로 살아왔다.
synonyms: march to the beat of a different drummer

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop trying to do something because you know you cannot succeed.
meanings.example: After trying for hours to fix the computer, he finally threw up the sponge.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몇 시간 동안 컴퓨터를 고치려 애쓰던 그는 마침내 포기했다 (단념했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing something or to work faster (informal).
meanings.example: If you want to finish this by tonight, you'd better get on the stick.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 밤까지 이 일을 끝내고 싶다면, 서두르는 게(정신 차리고 시작하는 게) 좋을 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get someone's attention in a very strong or exciting way.
meanings.example: The opening scene of the movie really grabs you by the lapels.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 영화의 오프닝 장면은 정말 시선을 확 사로잡는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do what you have promised or planned to do; to stay within a limit.
meanings.example: It's important to keep to the schedule. / He kept to his original story despite the pressure.
meanings.exampleKorean: 일정을 지키는 것이 중요하다. / 그는 압박에도 불구하고 자신의 원래 이야기를 고수했다 (지켰다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To get closer to someone or something that you are chasing or competing with.
meanings.example: The leading runner is fast, but the others are gaining on him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선두 주자가 빠르긴 하지만, 다른 주자들이 그를 따라잡고(거리를 좁히고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do or use something so many times that it becomes boring or no longer effective.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 진부하게 만들다, 너무 많이 써먹다, 지겹게 반복하다
meanings.example: That theme has been done to death in Hollywood movies.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 주제는 할리우드 영화에서 이미 너무 많이 써먹어서 진부해졌다 (지겹게 반복되었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop yourself from showing how you feel.
meanings.example: It was hard to hold in my laughter during the serious speech.
meanings.exampleKorean: 진지한 연설 중에 웃음을 참는 것이 힘들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue moving forward or making progress, often with effort.
meanings.example: We must push forward with our plans for expansion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 확장 계획을 강력하게 밀고 나가야(추진해야) 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decide to share the same fate or outcome as someone else.
meanings.example: He decided to cast in his lot with the rebels.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 반란군과 운명을 같이하기로(생사를 같이하기로) 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat someone in an unkind way by telling them what to do.
meanings.example: Don't let him push you about like that.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 너한테 그렇게 이래라저래라하며 함부로 부리게 두지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something that gives your opponent an advantage.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 남의 속셈에 놀아나다, (의도치 않게) 상대에게 유리한 일을 하다
meanings.example: If we overreact, we'll be playing right into their hands.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리가 과잉 반응하면, 결국 그들의 속셈에 놀아나는 꼴이 될 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To change from a bad situation to an even worse one.
meanings.example: The weather went from bad to worse as the day progressed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시간이 지날수록 날씨가 점점 더 나빠졌다 (악화되었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause someone or something to be viewed with doubt.
meanings.example: Her recent actions have called her honesty into question.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 최근 행동은 그녀의 정직함에 의문을 제기하게 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start thinking in a practical way about real life again.
meanings.example: After the holiday, it was time to come down to earth and get back to work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 휴가가 끝난 후, 이제 현실로 돌아와 다시 업무에 복귀할 시간이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To see someone or something for the first time.
meanings.example: I knew she was the one as soon as I set eyes on her.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀를 처음 본 순간 나는 그녀가 운명의 상대임을 알았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing a new activity or job, especially one you are good at.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 새로운 일에 도전하다, (무엇이든) 잘 해내다, 솜씨를 보이다
meanings.example: She can turn her hand to almost anything, from gardening to web design.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 정원 가꾸기부터 웹 디자인까지 거의 무슨 일이든 잘 해낼(도전할) 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something that makes someone annoyed or angry with you.
meanings.example: Be careful not to get on the boss's bad side.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사장님의 눈 밖에 나지 않도록(미움을 사지 않도록) 조심해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent people from entering an area.
meanings.example: Police sealed off the street after the accident.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사고 직후 경찰이 그 거리를 봉쇄했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become gradually weaker and then stop.
meanings.example: The sound of the footsteps gradually died away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 발자국 소리가 점차 서서히 사라졌다 (잦아들었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean a surface by using a cloth.
meanings.example: Please wipe down the table after you finish eating.
meanings.exampleKorean: 식사가 끝나면 테이블을 깨끗이 좀 닦아 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To examine something carefully to make sure it is correct.
meanings.example: I'll check through the documents one more time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 서류들을 한 번 더 꼼꼼히 확인해(검토해) 볼게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To estimate or calculate something roughly (informal).
meanings.example: Can you pencil out the costs for the new project?
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 프로젝트 비용을 대략적으로 계산해(견적을 뽑아) 볼 수 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To think about something carefully when making a decision.
meanings.example: We must take his age into consideration when judging his behavior.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 행동을 판단할 때 우리는 그의 나이를 고려해야(참작해야) 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage to survive with very little money or food.
meanings.example: In those days, it was hard to keep body and soul together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 시절에는 겨우 먹고살기(생계를 유지하기)가 힘들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To damage someone's reputation by saying bad things about them publicly.
meanings.example: The politician's name was dragged through the mud during the election campaign.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인의 이름(명성)은 선거 운동 기간 동안 진흙탕 속에 처박혔다 (더럽혀졌다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn something over; to become very excited or angry.
meanings.example: He flipped the pancake over. / The car flipped over several times.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 팬케이크를 뒤집었다. / 그 차는 여러 번 뒤집혔다 (전복되었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something more interesting, exciting, or active.
meanings.example: A few flowers will really liven up the room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 꽃 몇 송이가 방을 정말 활기 있게(생기 있게) 만들어 줄 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To achieve the first step towards a goal (often used in the negative).
meanings.example: I haven't even been able to get to first base with my application.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 내 지원서로 첫 단계조차 통과하지(성공하지) 못했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something so expensive that people can no longer afford to buy it.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 비싼 가격 때문에 배제하다, (가격 경쟁력에서) 밀려나다
meanings.example: Many local residents are being priced out of the neighborhood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 많은 지역 주민들이 비싼 주택 가격 때문에 그 동네에서 밀려나고(배제되고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid work or school by staying away or leaving early (British informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 몰래 땡땡이치다, (할 일을 안 하고) 빠지다
meanings.example: He's always skiving off early on Fridays.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 금요일마다 일찍 몰래 땡땡이친다 (도망간다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be brave and face a difficult situation; to take responsibility.
meanings.example: You need to woman up and tell him how you really feel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너는 용기를 내서(당당해져서) 그에게 네 진짜 기분이 어떤지 말해야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sell all your property or your business, especially to move somewhere else.
meanings.example: They decided to sell up and move to the countryside.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 다 팔아치우고 시골로 이사 가기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To close a piece of clothing by using buttons or a zipper.
meanings.example: Fasten up your coat; it's cold outside.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코트 단추를 꽉 채우렴(잠그렴). 밖이 춥단다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrive somewhere or find yourself in a situation that you did not expect.
meanings.example: If you keep driving like that, you'll land up in the hospital.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그렇게 계속 운전하다가는 결국 병원 신세를 지게 될 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay positive and optimistic during a difficult time.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 기운을 내다, 낙담하지 않다, 당당히 맞서다
meanings.example: Keep your chin up! Things will get better soon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기운 내! 상황이 곧 좋아질 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To improve your performance or the quality of your work.
meanings.example: If you want to win the championship, you really need to step up your game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우승하고 싶다면, 정말 실력을 더 키워야(분발해야) 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finally understand or accept something that is difficult to believe.
meanings.example: I can't get it through my head why he would quit such a good job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 왜 그렇게 좋은 직장을 그만뒀는지 도무지 이해가 안 가 (이해가 머릿속에 안 들어와).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be practical and wise; to have good judgment.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 정신이 제대로 박혀 있다, 사리 분별이 밝다, 현명하다
meanings.example: She's only twenty, but she really has her head screwed on correctly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 겨우 스무 살이지만, 정말 정신이 제대로 박혀(사리 분별이 밝게) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attack someone's weakest point in a very aggressive way.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 급소를 찌르다, 사정없이 공격하다, 공격적으로 나서다
meanings.example: The lawyer went for the throat during the cross-examination.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 변호사는 반대 심문 중에 사정없이 (급소를 찌르며) 공격했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue doing something in a confused way without any clear plan.
meanings.example: We don't have much money, but we manage to muddle along.
meanings.exampleKorean: 돈이 많지는 않지만, 우리는 그럭저럭 해나가고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrive in great numbers or amounts.
meanings.example: Complaints have been pouring in since the new policy was announced.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 정책이 발표된 이후 불만이 쏟아져 들어오고(쇄도하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to make someone behave more sensibly by talking to them severely or using force.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 정신 차리게 하다, 사리 분별을 가르치다
meanings.example: I tried to knock some sense into him, but he wouldn't listen.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그를 정신 차리게 하려고 노력해 봤지만, 그는 들으려 하지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be part of a group only so that there are enough people, not because you are good or important.
meanings.example: I'm not a good player; I'm just here to make up the numbers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저는 잘하는 선수가 아니에요. 그냥 인원수 맞추려고(머릿수 채우려고) 여기 있는 거예요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consider something to be significant or valuable.
meanings.example: Our school places great importance on sportsmanship.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 학교는 스포츠맨십을 아주 중요하게 여긴다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remain away from home late at night.
meanings.example: My parents don't like it when I stay out late.
meanings.exampleKorean: 부모님은 내가 밤늦게까지 밖에 있는 것(외박하는 것)을 좋아하지 않으신다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue doing something difficult or boring.
meanings.example: If you stick at your studies, you'll eventually succeed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공부를 끈기 있게 계속한다면, 결국 성공할 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To explain something in a very clear and detailed way so that there is no misunderstanding.
meanings.example: I don't understand. Can you spell it out for me?
meanings.exampleKorean: 이해가 안 가요. 저를 위해 좀 더 자세히 설명해 주실 수 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To discover the truth about a situation through an unpleasant experience.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 어렵게 깨닫다, 뼈아픈 경험을 통해 알게 되다
meanings.example: He found out the hard way that you shouldn't borrow money from strangers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 낯선 사람에게 돈을 빌려서는 안 된다는 것을 뼈아픈 경험을 통해(어렵게) 깨달았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be possessed by people who will use it for bad or harmful purposes.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 잘못된 사람의 손에 들어가다, 악용될 위험이 있는 곳으로 넘어가다
meanings.example: If this information falls into the wrong hands, it could be disastrous.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 정보가 잘못된 사람의 손에 들어간다면, 재앙이 될 수도 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop or slow down an activity or a process.
meanings.example: The central bank decided to put the brakes on lending.
meanings.exampleKorean: 중앙은행은 대출에 제동을 걸기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very soft and delicious (of food).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 입안에서 살살 녹다, 아주 부드럽고 맛있다
meanings.example: This steak is so tender, it just melts in the mouth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 스테이크는 정말 연해서 입안에서 그냥 살살 녹는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To break into small parts; to lose control of your emotions because you are very upset.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 산산조각 나다, (정신적으로) 무너지다, 감정을 추스르지 못하다
meanings.example: The old chair just fell to pieces when I sat on it. / She fell to pieces when she heard the news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 앉자마자 그 낡은 의자는 그냥 산산조각이 나 버렸다. / 그녀는 그 소식을 듣고 완전히 무너졌다 (감정을 추스르지 못했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept responsibility for something, especially without being asked.
meanings.example: He took it upon himself to organize the office party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 스스로 사무실 파티를 준비하는 일을 떠맡았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay a lot of attention to someone or something, especially in a nervous or over-excited way.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 호들갑을 떨다, 지나치게 신경 쓰다, 애지중지하다
meanings.example: Grandmothers always fuss over their grandchildren.
meanings.exampleKorean: 할머니들은 항상 손주들에게 지나치게 신경을 쓰시며 애지중지하신다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use information, experience, or money that you have for a particular purpose.
meanings.example: The writer drew upon her childhood experiences for the novel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 작가는 소설을 쓰기 위해 자신의 어린 시절 경험을 이용했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter a place or start doing something very quickly and without much thought.
meanings.example: Fools rush in where angels fear to tread. / Don't rush in without knowing the facts.
meanings.exampleKorean: 바보들은 천사들도 두려워하는 곳에 서둘러 뛰어든다 (속담). / 사실을 알지도 못한 채 서둘러 행동하지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To smell very bad; to be very suspicious or dishonest.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 아주 고약한 냄새가 나다, 몹시 구리다(의심스럽다)
meanings.example: This fish stinks to high heaven! / The whole deal stinks to high heaven.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 생선 정말 고약한 냄새가 나네! / 이 거래 전체가 몹시 구리고 의심스러워.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give someone a job; to use something in a productive way.
meanings.example: I need to put my brain to work to solve this problem. / The refugees were put to work in the local factories.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 문제를 해결하려면 머리를 좀 써야(가동해야)겠어. / 난민들은 지역 공장에서 일을 하게 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To persuade someone to do something.
meanings.example: He managed to talk me into going to the party with him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 나를 설득해서 파티에 같이 가게 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To persuade someone not to do something.
meanings.example: My friends tried to talk me out of quitting my job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 친구들은 내가 직장을 그만두지 않도록 나를 설득했다 (단념시키려 했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To change your ideas or behavior so that they are more modern.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 시대의 흐름에 따르다, 현대적인 방식을 받아들이다
meanings.example: You need to get with the times and start using a smartphone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신도 이제 시대의 흐름을 따라 스마트폰을 쓰기 시작해야 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very careful and alert to danger, especially when you are resting.
meanings.example: He's making threats, so you'd better sleep with one eye open.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 협박을 하고 있으니, 너는 경계를 늦추지 않는(아주 조심하는) 게 좋을 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk to someone as if they are less intelligent or important than you.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 깔보며 말하다, 무시하며 말하다, 거만하게 굴다
meanings.example: I don't like the way he talks down to his employees.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그가 직원들을 깔보며 말하는(무시하는) 방식이 마음에 안 들어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wait a short time before reacting to something that has made you angry.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 화를 참다, 침착함을 유지하다, (화를 내기 전) 숫자를 열까지 세다
meanings.example: Whenever I feel angry, I try to count to ten before I say anything.
meanings.exampleKorean: 화가 날 때마다 나는 아무 말도 하기 전에 마음속으로 열까지 세며 화를 참으려 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To formally ask someone to do something.
meanings.example: The chairman called upon the director to present the report.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의장은 이사에게 보고서를 발표해 달라고 요청했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wait anxiously for something to be finished or for a decision to be made.
meanings.example: I had to sweat out the results of my medical exam all weekend.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 주말 내내 건강 검진 결과를 걱정하며(초조하게) 기다려야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish with something completely; to end a relationship or activity.
meanings.example: I'll be glad to have done with these exams. / I've had done with that old car; it's too much trouble.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 시험들을 다 끝내버리면 정말 기쁠 거야. / 그 낡은 차는 이제 정리했어. 너무 말썽이라서.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fold something so that it takes up less space when not in use.
meanings.example: These chairs are great because they can be folded away when you don't need them.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 의자들은 필요 없을 때 접어서 치워둘 수 있어서 아주 좋아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat small amounts of food without enthusiasm; to criticize someone repeatedly.
meanings.example: Stop picking at your food and eat your dinner! / She's always picking at her husband about his clothes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 음식 좀 깨작거리지 말고 저녁 다 먹으렴! / 그녀는 남편의 옷차림에 대해 항상 잔소리를 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use the toilet.
meanings.example: May I go to the restroom, please?
meanings.exampleKorean: 화장실에 좀 다녀와도 될까요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To experience a period of poverty or struggle.
meanings.example: The family fell on hard times after the father lost his job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아버지가 직장을 잃은 후 그 가족은 어려운 시기를 겪었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To show others how to behave by acting in that way yourself.
meanings.example: A good manager should always lead by example.
meanings.exampleKorean: 좋은 관리자는 항상 솔선수범해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To laugh a lot, or to make someone laugh a lot (British informal).
meanings.example: The comedian's jokes really creased me up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 코미디언의 농담 때문에 나는 정말 배꼽을 잡고 웃었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go through something that is blocking you; to solve a complicated problem quickly.
meanings.example: We need to cut through all this bureaucracy. / Let's cut through the park to save time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 이 모든 관료주의를 헤치고 나아가야(타파해야) 한다. / 시간을 아끼기 위해 공원을 가로질러 가자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase the amount, intensity, or degree of something.
meanings.example: The movie dials up the tension in the final scene.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 영화는 마지막 장면에서 긴장감을 최고조로 높인다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force someone or something to leave a hiding place; to discover something hidden.
meanings.example: The police used dogs to flush out the suspect. / We need to flush out the facts before we decide.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 용의자를 밖으로 몰아내기 위해 개를 이용했다. / 결정을 내리기 전에 사실관계를 밝혀낼 필요가 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be brave and persevere through a difficult situation without complaining (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 용기를 내다, 당당히 맞서다, 꿋꿋이 견디다
meanings.example: I know it hurts, but you just have to cowboy up and finish the race.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아픈 건 알지만, 용기를 내서 경기를 마쳐야만 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mention or discuss something frequently or casually.
meanings.example: His name has been bandied about as a potential candidate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 이름이 잠재적 후보로 사람들의 입방아에 자주 오르내리고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something less strong or intense.
meanings.example: The government is trying to dampen down expectations of a tax cut.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 감세에 대한 기대를 가라앉히려 노력하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare to have fun or to be active.
meanings.example: Put on your dancing shoes; it's going to be a great party tonight!
meanings.exampleKorean: 신나게 즐길 준비를 하렴. 오늘 밤 파티는 정말 멋질 거야!

meanings.englishDefinition: To be the most important thing in life or the reason why things happen.
meanings.example: They say that love makes the world go around.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사랑이 세상을 움직인다는 말이 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become extremely angry and lose control (informal).
meanings.example: Don't mention his ex-wife or he might hulk out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 전 부인 이야기는 꺼내지 마. 안 그러면 그가 흥분해서 폭발할지도 몰라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a broad smile because you are very happy.
meanings.example: He was smiling from ear to ear when he saw his test results.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 시험 결과를 보고 입이 귀에 걸릴 정도로 활짝 웃고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be looking for a solution or someone to blame in the wrong place.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 번지수를 잘못 찾다, 엉뚱한 사람을 의심하다
meanings.example: If you think I stole your wallet, you're barking at the wrong tree.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 네 지갑을 훔쳤다고 생각한다면, 넌 번지수를 잘못 찾은(엉뚱한 사람을 의심하는) 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce the amount, intensity, or degree of something.
meanings.example: Both sides need to dial down the rhetoric to avoid a conflict.
meanings.exampleKorean: 갈등을 피하기 위해 양측 모두 비판의 수위를 낮출 필요가 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become more relaxed and less angry or nervous (informal).
meanings.example: He used to be very aggressive, but he's mellowed out as he's gotten older.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 예전에 매우 공격적이었지만, 나이가 들면서 많이 느긋해졌다 (차분해졌다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place in an angry way.
meanings.example: She stormed out of the room after the argument.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 말다툼 끝에 벌컥 화를 내며 방을 뛰쳐나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To depend on or believe in something completely.
meanings.example: This is a good plan, but I wouldn't hang my hat on it just yet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 좋은 계획이긴 하지만, 아직 그것에 전적으로 의지하고(믿고) 싶지는 않아요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move back away from someone or something because you are frightened or shocked.
meanings.example: The child shrank back from the snarling dog.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이는 으르렁거리는 개를 보고 뒷걸음질 쳤다 (움츠러들었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To become smaller and thinner than usual and not look fresh; to become much smaller or weaker.
meanings.example: The leaves shriveled up in the heat. / His confidence shriveled up after the failure.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나뭇잎들이 열기 때문에 바싹 말라 쪼그라들었다. / 실패 후 그의 자신감은 급격히 위축되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hit someone very hard so that they become unconscious (informal).
meanings.example: If you don't stop, I'm going to punch your lights out!
meanings.exampleKorean: 당장 멈추지 않으면 널 호되게 때려눕힐(기절시킬) 거야!

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce a clear and lasting effect on someone's mind or memory.
meanings.example: The horrific scene was permanently imprinted on his memory.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 끔찍한 장면은 그의 기억 속에 영원히 각인되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To search for something by moving things around.
meanings.example: I've been ferretting around in my drawer for that old key.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그 낡은 열쇠를 찾으려고 서랍 속을 샅샅이 뒤지고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To press your upper and lower teeth together tightly.
meanings.example: He had to bite down on a piece of leather to endure the pain.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 고통을 참기 위해 가죽 조각을 꽉 깨물어야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To write a document using a computer or typewriter, especially from handwritten notes.
meanings.example: Could you type up these notes for me by tomorrow?
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 메모들을 내일까지 타이핑해서 문서로 만들어 주실 수 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a rule or system more strict; to make something firmer or tighter.
meanings.example: The airport is tightening up security after the incident. / You need to tighten up the screws on that chair.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 사건 이후 공항은 보안을 강화하고 있다. / 저 의자의 나사들을 꽉 조여야겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tell someone a secret or discuss your private feelings with them because you trust them.
meanings.example: She's nice, but I don't feel I can confide in her.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 좋은 사람이지만, 내 비밀을 털어놓을 수 있을 것 같지는 않아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find or meet someone or something by chance.
meanings.example: I happened upon an old friend while I was walking in the park.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공원에서 걷다가 우연히 옛 친구를 마주쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To approach someone or something, especially in a threatening way or to take control.
meanings.example: Competitors are moving in on our most profitable markets.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경쟁자들이 우리의 가장 수익성 높은 시장들로 공격적으로 진출하고(파고들고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discuss all the details of something; to help someone understand a process by explaining it clearly.
meanings.example: Let's talk through the proposal before we make a decision.
meanings.exampleKorean: 결정을 내리기 전에 그 제안에 대해 상세히 논의해 봅시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce the amount or level of something; to suppress or restrain something.
meanings.example: The government tried to tamp down rumors of a financial crisis.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 경제 위기설(루머)을 억누르려(진정시키려) 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force someone to leave a job or group because they have done something wrong.
meanings.example: He was drummed out of the army for his misconduct.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 품행 불량으로 군에서 불명예 퇴진당했다 (쫓겨났다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To become gradually quieter and then stop.
meanings.example: His voice trailed off as he realized no one was listening.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아무도 듣고 있지 않다는 것을 깨닫자 그의 목소리가 점점 잦아들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend as little money as possible on something, especially when it results in poor quality.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 돈을 아끼려 대충 하다, 인색하게 굴다
meanings.example: Don't cheap out on the tires; safety is more important than money.
meanings.exampleKorean: 타이어에 돈 아끼려 대충 하지 마세요. 안전이 돈보다 더 중요하니까요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in an aggressive or confrontational way towards someone (informal).
meanings.example: He got up in my face and started shouting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 내 면전에 대고 공격적으로 대들며 소리를 지르기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly remember something that happened in the past.
meanings.example: When I saw the old house, I flashed back to my childhood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 낡은 집을 보았을 때, 내 어린 시절이 갑자기 생생하게 떠올랐다 (회상했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To agree with or support an idea or plan.
meanings.example: We need the team to buy in to the new strategy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 팀원들이 새로운 전략을 받아들이고(동의하고) 참여하는 것이 필요하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become familiar with a new way of life, a new job, or a new home.
meanings.example: It took a few months for her to settle into her new job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 새 직장에 적응하는(안착하는) 데 몇 달이 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To involve oneself fully in a task or goal; to seize an opportunity.
meanings.example: To be successful, you need to lean in and embrace the challenges.
meanings.exampleKorean: 성공하려면 적극적으로 관여하고(뛰어들고) 도전을 받아들여야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause people to be friendly with each other; to gather things in one place.
meanings.example: The crisis brought the community together. / The goal is to bring together the best minds in the industry.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 위기가 지역 사회를 하나로 모았다. / 목표는 업계 최고의 인재들을 한데 모으는 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pass through something unnoticed or without being caught; to be missed or lost.
meanings.example: A few errors managed to slip through the final check. / He let the opportunity slip through his fingers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몇 가지 오류가 마지막 점검을 교묘히 빠져나갔다. / 그는 기회를 손가락 사이로(부주의하게) 놓쳐버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To examine a group of things carefully in order to choose the ones you want.
meanings.example: Customers were picking over the clothes on the sale rack.
meanings.exampleKorean: 고객들이 세일 매대에 있는 옷들을 꼼꼼히 고르고(살피고) 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To disappear gradually.
meanings.example: All the tension drained away when she heard the good news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 좋은 소식을 듣자 모든 긴장감이 서서히 사라졌다 (빠져나갔다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To choose or support one person or thing from a group (British informal).
meanings.example: I think I'll plump for the chocolate cake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 초콜릿 케이크를 선택할(고를) 것 같아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To offer someone or something as an example of something.
meanings.example: He was held up as a model student for everyone to follow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 모두가 따라야 할 모범생의 본보기로 내세워졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To appear suddenly and unexpectedly.
meanings.example: Old problems tend to bob up when you least expect them.
meanings.exampleKorean: 옛 문제들은 전혀 예상치 못한 순간에 갑자기 나타나는(떠오르는) 경향이 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move quickly towards someone to attack them; to be at a particular level or amount.
meanings.example: The dog ran at the intruder. / Inflation is currently running at 5%.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 개는 침입자에게 달려들었다 (공격하려 했다). / 인플레이션이 현재 5% 수준에 도달해 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To understand what someone is saying.
meanings.example: I'm sorry, I'm not with you. Could you explain that again?
meanings.exampleKorean: 죄송해요, 이해가 안 가네요 (당신 말을 못 따라가고 있어요). 다시 한번 설명해 주실래요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To stick out further than the surrounding surface.
meanings.example: A sharp ridge of rock juts out into the sea.
meanings.exampleKorean: 날카로운 바위 능선이 바다 쪽으로 툭 튀어나와(돌출해) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To face a difficult situation with confidence and determination rather than avoiding it.
meanings.example: Instead of hiding from the problem, we need to lean into it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 문제로부터 숨는 대신, 우리는 그것에 정면으로 직면할(적극적으로 해결하려 할) 필요가 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be clearly evident or noticeable, despite attempts to hide it.
meanings.example: Her true personality shines through in her writing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 글에서는 그녀의 진정한 성격이 자연스럽게 드러난다 (비친다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To help or take care of someone; to deal with something.
meanings.example: I have some urgent business to attend to. / A nurse was attending to the injured soldier.
meanings.exampleKorean: 처리해야 할 급한 용무가 좀 있어요. / 간호사가 부상당한 군인을 돌보고(보살피고) 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hold onto something tightly; to refuse to stop believing in or hoping for something.
meanings.example: The survivors clung to the raft. / He still clings to the hope that his business will succeed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 생존자들은 뗏목에 꽉 매달렸다. / 그는 여전히 자신의 사업이 성공할 것이라는 희망을 고수하고(버리지 않고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something with a lot of energy, enthusiasm, or without restraint (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 아주 열정적으로 하다, (돈/에너지를) 아낌없이 쏟다, 화려하게 장식하다
meanings.example: They really went to town on the decorations for the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 파티 장식에 정말 아낌없이 에너지를 쏟았다 (화려하게 장식했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin to understand or realize something (British informal).
meanings.example: It took me a while to cotton on to what they were planning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들이 무엇을 계획하고 있는지 내가 알아차리는(눈치채는) 데 시간이 좀 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consider various possible outcomes and plan a strategy accordingly.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 모의 연습을 하다, 전략을 짜다, 시뮬레이션해 보다
meanings.example: The military is gaming out various scenarios for a possible conflict.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군은 일어날 수 있는 분쟁에 대비해 다양한 시나리오로 전략을 짜고(시뮬레이션하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk or complain about something repeatedly in an annoying way.
meanings.example: I'm tired of hearing you harp on about your old job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 네가 예전 직장 이야기를 지겹게 되풀이하는 걸 듣는 데 질렸어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To crush something into a ball; to collapse suddenly.
meanings.example: He crumpled up the letter and threw it away. / She crumpled up in pain.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 편지를 구겨서 던져 버렸다. / 그녀는 고통 때문에 풀썩 쓰러졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To achieve a better result than something else or someone else's previous effort.
meanings.example: The team hopes to improve on last year's performance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 팀은 작년 실적보다 더 나은 결과를 내기를 희망하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To throw something away; to reject an idea or suggestion.
meanings.example: I decided to toss out my old clothes. / The judge tossed out the case due to lack of evidence.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 낡은 옷들을 버리기로(내던지기로) 했다. / 판사는 증거 부족으로 그 사건을 기각했다 (거부했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To succeed in something easily and without much effort.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 수월하게 해내다, 낙승하다, (시험 등에) 쉽게 합격하다
meanings.example: She breezed through the exam without even studying much.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 공부를 많이 하지 않고도 시험을 수월하게 해냈다 (쉽게 합격했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay calm and sensible during a difficult or emotional time.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 정신을 차리다, 자제력을 유지하다, 평정심을 잃지 않다
meanings.example: It was a tough situation, but he managed to keep it together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 힘든 상황이었지만, 그는 정신을 차리려 (자제력을 유지하려) 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put something to one side; to save money for the future; to stop thinking about a problem or a difference.
meanings.example: We should lay aside our differences and work together. / He laid aside a little money each month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 차이점을 제쳐두고 함께 협력해야 한다. / 그는 매달 조금씩 돈을 저축했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go across something like a field to save time; to affect or be applicable to different groups of people.
meanings.example: Let's cut across the field; it's much faster. / This issue cuts across all political parties.
meanings.exampleKorean: 들판을 가로질러 가자. 그게 훨씬 빨라. / 이 사안은 모든 정당에 공통으로 영향을 미친다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize or punish someone severely; to stop someone from doing something (informal).
meanings.example: The boss smacked down his suggestion immediately.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사장은 그의 제안을 즉각 호되게 비판하며 (제압하며) 묵살했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give information to the police or authorities about someone's illegal activities.
meanings.example: He was accused of informing on his colleagues.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 동료들을 (경찰에) 고발했다는 혐의를 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To defend or support someone who is being criticized.
meanings.example: She always takes up for her younger brother when he gets into trouble.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 남동생이 말썽을 피울 때면 항상 그의 편을 들어준다 (옹호해 준다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To find or meet something or someone by chance.
meanings.example: I happened across an interesting article in an old magazine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 낡은 잡지에서 흥미로운 기사 하나를 우연히 발견했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give someone a lot of something, such as gifts or praise.
meanings.example: They showered her with gifts on her birthday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 그녀의 생일에 선물을 듬뿍 주었다 (퍼부었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stick out from a hole or an opening.
meanings.example: The little girl poked her tongue out at me. / His toes were poking out of his socks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 어린 소녀는 나에게 혀를 내밀었다. / 그의 발가락이 양말 밖으로 비죽이 튀어나와 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remain in a relationship or a group.
meanings.example: They decided to stay together for the sake of the children.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 아이들을 위해 계속 함께 지내기로 (관계를 유지하기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something dirty or unclear; to complicate a situation unnecessarily.
meanings.example: Let's not muddy up the issue with irrelevant details.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상관없는 세부 사항들로 쟁점을 흐리게(혼란스럽게) 하지 맙시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something quickly and without much care, using whatever is available.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 대충 짜 맞춰 만들다, 서둘러 조합하다
meanings.example: We managed to cobble together a meal from the leftovers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 남은 음식들을 긁어모아 대충 식사를 짜 맞춰(준비해) 냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cooperate with someone to achieve a goal.
meanings.example: If we all work together, we can finish the project on time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 모두가 협력한다면(힘을 합친다면), 프로젝트를 제시간에 끝낼 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remain united or connected; to remain logically consistent.
meanings.example: The family held together despite the tragedy. / The story doesn't really hold together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 가족은 비극에도 불구하고 잘 결속했다 (함께했다). / 그 이야기는 사실 논리적으로 앞뒤가 잘 맞지(일관성이 있지) 않는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join with another person or group to do something together.
meanings.example: The local school partnered up with a tech company to improve its computer lab.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지역 학교는 컴퓨터실을 개선하기 위해 기술 기업과 협력했다 (파트너가 되었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To bring people or things closer together; to become more united.
meanings.example: The tragedy drew the community together. / We need to draw together all the different strands of the argument.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 비극이 지역 사회를 하나로 모았다 (가까워지게 했다). / 우리는 논쟁의 모든 다른 줄기들을 하나로 모을 필요가 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage to stay calm and not lose control of your emotions during a difficult time.
meanings.example: She struggled to hold herself together at the funeral.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 장례식에서 평정심을 유지하려 (마음을 가다듬으려) 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gather in a large group, especially with people who are similar to you.
meanings.example: Birds of a feather flock together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 유유상종 (깃털이 같은 새들끼리 떼 지어 모인다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be the right size and shape to be joined; to work well with each other.
