meanings.koreanDefinition: 서로 잘 맞다, 조화를 이루다, 결합하다
meanings.example: These puzzle pieces don't fit together. / The two ideas fit together perfectly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 퍼즐 조각들은 서로 잘 맞지 않는다. / 그 두 아이디어는 완벽하게 조화를 이룬다 (잘 맞는다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To fight or compete until a winner is decided.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 끝까지 싸우다, 결판을 내다, 승부를 겨루다
meanings.example: The two teams had to battle it out in overtime.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 팀은 연장전에서 끝까지 싸워 결판을 내야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To summon a group of people for a meeting or other purpose.
meanings.example: The manager called together the entire staff for an emergency meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 관리자는 비상 회의를 위해 전 직원을 소집했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To create something by joining different parts together, often quickly or carelessly.
meanings.example: The report was patched together from several different sources.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 보고서는 여러 다른 출처들을 서둘러 조합해서 만든 것이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decrease the speed of rotation; to stop or slow down an activity.
meanings.example: The hard drive will spin down after ten minutes of inactivity.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하드 드라이브는 10분 동안 사용하지 않으면 회전 속도를 줄여 정지할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To show that you can deal with a difficult situation successfully.
meanings.example: He really rose to the challenge and led the team to victory.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 난관에 당당히 맞서 팀을 승리로 이끌었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deal with a complicated problem or process gradually; to overcome an emotional difficulty.
meanings.example: It will take time to work through all these documents. / They are working through their marriage problems.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 모든 서류를 처리하는(해결하는) 데는 시간이 좀 걸릴 거야. / 그들은 결혼 생활의 문제들을 해결하려(헤쳐나가려) 노력하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do what someone else wants you to do, especially because they have power over you.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 남이 시키는 대로 하다, 남의 장단에 놀아나다
meanings.example: She's tired of having to dance to her boss's tune.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 상사가 시키는 대로 해야 하는 것에 진저리가 났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remain united and support each other during a difficult time.
meanings.example: We must stand together if we want to win this battle.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리가 이 싸움에서 이기고 싶다면 반드시 단결해야(함께 맞서야) 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give all your attention to something.
meanings.example: He completely absorbed himself in his work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 업무에 완전히 열중했다 (몰두했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone very angry or excited (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 화나게 하다, 짜증 나게 하다, 휘젓다
meanings.example: Don't let him rile you up with his teasing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 놀림 때문에 화내지(짜증 내지) 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize or scold someone for something they have done wrong.
meanings.example: The manager called him to task for his frequent lateness.
meanings.exampleKorean: 관리자는 그가 자주 늦는 것에 대해 호되게 꾸짖었다 (책망했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To attack or criticize people who are less powerful or have less status than you.
meanings.example: Comedians should avoid punching down at marginalized groups.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코미디언들은 소외된 계층(약자들)을 비웃는(공격하는) 일을 피해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have the same opinion as someone; (of food or climate) to be good for someone's health.
meanings.example: I totally agree with you on this matter. / Spicy food doesn't always agree with me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 문제에 대해 당신에게 전적으로 동의합니다. / 매운 음식은 내 입에 잘 맞지(체질에 맞지) 않아요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reach an age at which you are no longer eligible for something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 나이가 차서 제외되다, 연령 제한에 걸리다
meanings.example: He aged out of the foster care system when he turned 18.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 18세가 되었을 때 위탁 가정 시스템에서 나이가 차서 제외되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To say bad things about someone to damage their reputation.
meanings.example: They tried to throw dirt on the candidate during the election.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 선거 기간 동안 후보자를 비방하려 (명예를 더럽히려) 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To persuade someone to drop their opposition or change their opinion; to persuade someone to reduce a price.
meanings.example: She argued him down until he finally agreed to the plan. / I managed to argue the price down to $50.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그가 마침내 계획에 동의할 때까지 그를 설득하여 승복하게 했다. / 나는 가격을 50달러까지 깎는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remember something that happened in the past.
meanings.example: I sometimes think back to the time we spent in Paris.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 가끔 우리가 파리에서 보냈던 시간을 회상하곤 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lose energy or enthusiasm and be unable to continue.
meanings.example: The project was going well, but we seem to have run out of gas lately.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프로젝트가 잘 진행되고 있었는데, 최근에는 우리가 기운이 다한(지친) 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something exceptionally well; to achieve a great success.
meanings.example: Her presentation was amazing; she really hit it out of the park.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 발표는 놀라웠어. 정말 대성공을 거두었지 (완벽하게 해냈지).
synonyms: knock it out of the park

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail spectacularly or completely.
meanings.example: The whole strategy went down in flames after the scandal was revealed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스캔들이 드러난 후 전체 전략이 처참하게 실패했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To set very high goals for oneself.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 큰 포부를 갖다, 높은 목표를 세우다
meanings.example: Our teacher always encouraged us to reach for the sky.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 선생님은 항상 우리가 큰 포부를 갖도록(높은 목표를 세우도록) 격려해 주셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail to do something well or successfully.
meanings.example: The company fell down on the marketing of its new product.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 신제품 마케팅에서 부족함(실패)을 보였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hit someone repeatedly; to criticize someone severely.
meanings.example: Stop beating up on yourself for the mistake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 실수 때문에 너 자신을 너무 몰아세우지(비판하지) 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be ignored or have no effect (of a suggestion or advice).
meanings.example: My warnings about the budget cuts fell on stony ground.
meanings.exampleKorean: 예산 삭감에 대한 나의 경고는 아무런 반응이 없었다 (무시당했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To destroy something by pulling it apart; to criticize something very severely.
meanings.example: The critics pulled his new play to pieces.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비평가들은 그의 새로운 연극을 호되게 비판했다 (갈기갈기 찢어 놓았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To die or finish in a very unpleasant or violent way.
meanings.example: In those movies, the villain always comes to a sticky end.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그런 영화에서 악당은 항상 비극적인 최후를 맞이한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To introduce or start something; to serve as an introduction.
meanings.example: The short film served as a lead-in to the main feature.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 단편 영화는 본편의 도입부 역할을 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bite down hard on something.
meanings.example: The dog chomped down on the bone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 개는 뼈다귀를 꽉 깨물었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be destroyed by fire; to fail completely and suddenly.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 불길에 휩싸이다, 완전히 실패하다, 수포로 돌아가다
meanings.example: Their dreams of winning the title went up in flames. / The old warehouse went up in flames last night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우승의 꿈이 수포로 돌아갔다 (불길에 휩싸였다). / 그 오래된 창고가 어젯밤 불길에 휩싸였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sell very quickly because it is very popular.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 불티나게 팔리다, 날개 돋친 듯 팔리다
meanings.example: The new smartphone is flying off the shelves.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 스마트폰이 불티나게 팔리고(날개 돋친 듯 팔리고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To further improve something that is already successful.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 한 단계 격상시키다, 한 차원 높이다
meanings.example: He wants to take his business to the next level.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 사업을 한 단계 격상시키기를 (한 차원 높이기를) 원한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop annoying someone or interrupting their work (informal).
meanings.example: I'll finish my questions and then I'll get out of your hair.
meanings.exampleKorean: 질문 다 끝내고 당신을 방해하지 않게 (성가시게 하지 않게) 자리를 비켜 드릴게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make sure that something happens or is done.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 꼭 그렇게 되도록 조치하다, 확실히 하다
meanings.example: Please see to it that the doors are locked before you leave.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나가기 전에 문이 확실히 잠겼는지 꼭 확인(조치)해 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail completely; to be wasted.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 수포로 돌아가다, 망조가 들다, 헛수고가 되다
meanings.example: All our hard work just went down the tube.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리의 모든 노력이 그냥 수포로 돌아갔다 (하수구로 빠져버렸다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To communicate information to a lot of people.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 소문을 퍼뜨리다, 널리 알리다, 정보를 전하다
meanings.example: We're trying to get the word out about the new product.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 신제품에 대해 소문을 퍼뜨리려 (널리 알리려) 노력하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cut something off; to reduce something by a large amount.
meanings.example: The company lopped off 10% of its workforce. / He lopped off the dead branches.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 인력의 10%를 감축했다. / 그는 죽은 나뭇가지들을 잘라냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reach a stage where something is about to happen or explode.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 끓기 시작하다, 절정에 이르다, 비등점에 달하다
meanings.example: The long-standing tensions finally came to a boil. / Bring the soup to a boil, then lower the heat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오랫동안 지속된 긴장감이 마침내 비등점에 달했다 (폭발했다). / 수프를 한소끔 끓인 후 불을 낮추세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To highlight or emphasize something.
meanings.example: The report points up the need for better communication.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 보고서는 더 나은 소통의 필요성을 강조하고(분명히 보여주고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a brief visit to a place during a journey.
meanings.example: I'll swing through the office to pick up the files on my way home.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집에 가는 길에 파일을 가지러 사무실에 잠시 들를게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce something with effort, such as music on a piano or a document on a typewriter.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 힘겹게 만들어 내다, (피아노 등을) 세게 연주하다, (글을) 써 내려가다
meanings.example: The two sides managed to pound out a deal after hours of negotiation. / He pounded out a quick melody on the piano.
meanings.exampleKorean: 양측은 수 시간의 협상 끝에 간신히 합의안을 만들어 냈다. / 그는 피아노로 빠른 멜로디를 세게 연주했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To learn the basic skills of something before trying to do something more difficult.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 기초부터 배우다, 걷기 전에 기는 법부터 배워야 한다
meanings.example: You can't start a business without any experience; you need to crawl before you walk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경험 없이 사업을 시작할 수는 없어요. 걷기 전에 기는 법부터 배워야(기초부터 다져야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop thinking that you are more important than other people (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 착각에서 벗어나다, 잘난 척 좀 그만하다, 겸손해지다
meanings.example: He really needs to get over himself; he's not the only talented person here.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 정말 착각에서 벗어날(잘난 척 그만할) 필요가 있어. 여기 재능 있는 사람이 그뿐인 건 아니거든.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be excessively kind to someone, often in a way that is overwhelming or makes them uncomfortable.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 지나친 친절로 곤란하게 하다, 과잉 친절을 베풀다
meanings.example: The hotel staff almost killed us with kindness during our stay.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리가 머무는 동안 호텔 직원들은 거의 지나칠 정도의 친절로 우리를 대했다 (곤란하게 할 정도였다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To support a person or a plan; to fail to do something as quickly as you should.
meanings.example: The whole community got behind the new project. / I've gotten behind with my mortgage payments.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지역 사회 전체가 그 새로운 프로젝트를 지지했다. / 나는 주택 담보 대출 상환금이 밀렸다 (뒤처졌다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very strict or firm about something; to refuse to allow something to happen.
meanings.example: The boss finally put his foot down on overtime work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사장은 마침내 초과 근무에 대해 단호한 태도를 취했다 (엄격히 금했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin to cooperate or take part in something.
meanings.example: We need all the stakeholders to come to the party if we want to solve this crisis.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 위기를 해결하려면 모든 이해관계자가 참여(협력)해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act in a very reckless or impulsive way without considering the danger.
meanings.example: You're leading with your chin by investing all your money in that risky startup.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 위험한 스타트업에 전 재산을 투자하는 건 무모하게 행동하는(위험을 자초하는) 일이야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce to zero; to eliminate a budget or a debt.
meanings.example: The government plans to zero out the funding for that program next year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 내년에 그 프로그램에 대한 예산 지원을 0으로 만들 (완전히 삭감할) 계획이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To interrupt or involve oneself in something without being invited (informal).
meanings.example: I'm sorry to horn in, but I think I can help you with that.
meanings.exampleKorean: 끼어들어서 죄송합니다만, 그 문제라면 제가 도와드릴 수 있을 것 같아요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone or something heavy; to cause someone to feel worried or burdened.
meanings.example: She was weighted down with heavy bags. / The responsibility of the new job is weighting him down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 무거운 가방들에 짓눌려 있었다. / 새 직장의 책임감이 그를 압박하고(짓누르고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce the speed of an engine; to slow down an activity.
meanings.example: The pilot throttled down the engines as the plane approached the runway.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비행기가 활주로에 접근하자 조종사는 엔진 속도를 줄였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do or say something that is very unpleasant or harmful to someone, especially when they are already in a difficult situation.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 비수를 꽂다, 교묘하게 해치다, 치명적인 타격을 주다
meanings.example: He pretended to be my friend, but then he stuck the knife in by telling everyone my secret.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 내 친구인 척했지만, 내 비밀을 모두에게 말함으로써 내 등에 비수를 꽂았다 (교묘하게 해쳤다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To punish someone very severely or to take very strict action against them.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 엄벌을 처하다, 철퇴를 내리다, 단호한 조치를 취하다
meanings.example: The regulator is planning to bring down the hammer on banks that mislead customers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 규제 당국은 고객을 속이는 은행들에 대해 엄벌을 처할 (단호한 조치를 취할) 계획이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To happen or exist as a preparation for something more important.
meanings.example: The weeks leading up to the election were very intense.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선거로 이어지는 (선거 전의) 몇 주 동안은 매우 치열했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move away from a place where you should be or from a person you are with.
meanings.example: The child wandered off in the crowded mall. / Don't wander off the subject.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이가 붐비는 쇼핑몰에서 길을 잃고 헤맸다. / 주제에서 벗어나지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pass very quickly (of time).
meanings.example: Summer vacation always seems to fly by.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여름 방학은 항상 어느덧 빨리 지나가 버리는 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep something that is valuable; to continue to believe in something.
meanings.example: Hold onto your receipt in case you want to return the item. / She held onto her dreams despite the difficulties.
meanings.exampleKorean: 물건을 반품할 경우를 대비해 영수증을 잘 가지고(붙잡고) 계세요. / 그녀는 어려움에도 불구하고 자신의 꿈을 고수했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sit or stand with your back and shoulders curved forward.
meanings.example: He was hunched over his desk, studying hard.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 책상 앞에 구부정하게 앉아 열심히 공부하고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force something out of your mouth; to say something quickly or with difficulty.
meanings.example: The baby spit out the medicine. / Come on, spit it out! What did he say?
meanings.exampleKorean: 아기가 약을 내뱉었다. / 어서, 빨리 말해봐(내뱉어봐)! 그가 뭐라고 했어?

meanings.englishDefinition: To find time to do something; to include something in a plan or a piece of writing.
meanings.example: I'll try to work in a quick meeting this afternoon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 오후에 짬을 내서 짧은 회의를 해보도록 할게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To visit a place and see what is there; to search for something in several places.
meanings.example: We spent the afternoon looking around the old town.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 오후 시간을 구동네를 둘러보며(구경하며) 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something by tearing it; to leave a place very quickly.
meanings.example: She tore out a page from her notebook. / He tore out of the house in a rage.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 공책에서 페이지 한 장을 뜯어냈다. / 그는 화가 머리끝까지 나서 집을 급히 떠났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become covered with a thin layer of liquid so that it is difficult to see through.
meanings.example: My glasses misted up as soon as I came inside. / Her eyes misted up as she read the letter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 실내로 들어오자마자 내 안경에 김이 서렸다. / 그녀가 편지를 읽을 때 눈시울이 붉어졌다 (시야가 흐려졌다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To enclose someone or something in a small area; to write a date or event in a diary or schedule.
meanings.example: We felt penned in by the tall buildings. / I've penned you in for a meeting at 10 AM on Tuesday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 높은 빌딩들에 갇힌(둘러싸인) 기분이었다. / 화요일 오전 10시에 당신과의 회의 일정을 적어 놓았습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to think clearly and behave sensibly again; to regain consciousness.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 제정신이 들다, 판단력을 되찾다, 정신을 차리다
meanings.example: I hope he comes to his senses and apologizes. / She finally came to her senses after the fainting spell.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 제정신이 들어서 사과했으면 좋겠어. / 그녀는 기절했다가 마침내 정신을 차렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To work extremely hard to achieve something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 등골이 빠지게 일하다, 뼈빠지게 노력하다, 고생하다
meanings.example: I've been breaking my back all week to finish this report.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 이 보고서를 끝내기 위해 일주일 내내 등골이 빠지게 일했다.
synonyms: work one's fingers to the bone

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste money, time, or effort on something that is not successful.
meanings.example: We've poured thousands of dollars down the drain on this project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 이 프로젝트에 수천 달러를 허무하게 낭비했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To persuade someone to do something they do not really want to do.
meanings.example: I got roped into helping with the school play.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 학교 연극을 돕는 일에 (설득당해) 끌려 들어갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Disturbed, upset, or frightened by a shocking experience (informal).
meanings.example: He was pretty shook up after the car accident.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자동차 사고 이후 꽤 심란해하고(충격을 받고) 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decide to support a person or group and share their success or failure.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 운명을 같이하다, 행동을 함께하다, 공동 운명체가 되다
meanings.example: The small company decided to throw in its lot with the larger corporation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 작은 회사는 대기업과 운명을 같이하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To repeatedly remind someone of a mistake or failure to embarrass them (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: (실수 등을) 자꾸 들먹여 괴롭히다, 면박을 주다
meanings.example: I know I failed the test; you don't have to rub my nose in it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시험 떨어진 거 나도 알아. 자꾸 들먹여서 괴롭히지 좀 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be extremely angry.
meanings.example: My father was foaming at the mouth when he saw the damage to his car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아버지는 차가 파손된 것을 보고 입에 거품을 물며 격분하셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent something from happening or developing normally.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 계획을 방해하다, 지장을 주다, 찬물을 끼얹다
meanings.example: The heavy rain really put a crimp in our outdoor wedding plans.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭우가 우리 야외 결혼식 계획에 정말 큰 지장을 주었다 (방해했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To perform very well; to have a lot of fun and energy at a social event (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 아주 잘하다, 신나게 즐기다, (기량을) 맘껏 발휘하다
meanings.example: The band really tore it up on stage last night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 밴드는 어젯밤 무대에서 정말 기량을 맘껏 발휘했다 (무대를 찢어 놓았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat someone in a very dishonest or unfair way (British informal).
meanings.example: I can't believe he did the dirty on me by stealing my ideas.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 내 아이디어를 훔쳐서 내 뒤통수를 칠(배신할) 줄은 몰랐어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attack someone physically or verbally (informal).
meanings.example: The critics really lammed into his new movie.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비평가들은 그의 신작 영화를 정말 혹독하게 비판했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually become less friendly or close to someone as time passes.
meanings.example: We were best friends in high school, but we've grown apart over the years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 고등학교 때 절친이었지만, 수년이 흐르면서 사이가 멀어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop rising or falling and stay at the same level; to become stable.
meanings.example: Prices are expected to level out in the second half of the year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 물가가 하반기에는 안정될(일정해질) 것으로 예상된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To focus all your attention on a particular detail or problem.
meanings.example: The report zooms in on the causes of the financial crisis.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 보고서는 경제 위기의 원인들에 집중하고(초점을 맞추고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a long and honest argument with someone to settle a dispute.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 담판을 짓다, 결판을 내다, (불만을) 다 털어놓고 논쟁하다
meanings.example: I've been upset with her for weeks, so we finally had it out last night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몇 주 동안 그녀에게 화가 나 있어서, 어젯밤 마침내 담판을 지었다 (불만을 다 털어놓고 얘기했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To state your opinion or support something publicly, even if it is difficult to do so.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 당당히 소신을 밝히다, 지지 의사를 표명하다
meanings.example: If you disagree with the decision, now is the time to stand up and be counted.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 결정에 동의하지 않는다면, 지금이 바로 당당히 소신을 밝힐 때입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find a way to get something done despite a problem or obstacle.
meanings.example: We can't fix the bug yet, but we've found a way to work around it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아직 버그를 고칠 수는 없지만, 그것을 피해 갈 (우회할) 방법을 찾아냈습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue doing something despite difficulties; to persevere (informal).
meanings.example: Life is tough sometimes, but you just have to keep on keeping on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 삶이 가끔 힘들긴 하지만, 그냥 꿋꿋이 계속해 나가는 수밖에 없어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate something useful from something that is not useful.
meanings.example: We need to sift out the important information from these reports.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 이 보고서들에서 중요한 정보들을 걸러낼(선별할) 필요가 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a way that is likely to cause problems or an argument.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 화를 자초하다, 싸움을 걸다, 문제를 일으키려 하다
meanings.example: If you keep talking like that, you're just looking for trouble.
meanings.exampleKorean: 계속 그렇게 말한다면, 너는 그냥 화를 자초하는(문제를 일으키려는) 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bring out or use something that has been used many times before, often to influence people (informal).
meanings.example: The politician wheeled out the same old excuses for the failure.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인은 실패에 대해 똑같은 변명들을 지겹도록 다시 꺼냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk too much, especially in a boastful or disrespectful way (informal).
meanings.example: He's always running his mouth off about how much money he makes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신이 얼마나 많은 돈을 버는지에 대해 항상 함부로 지껄인다 (떠벌린다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone or something look more stylish or attractive.
meanings.example: She decided to style up for the evening party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 저녁 파티를 위해 세련되게 꾸미기로(멋을 내기로) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mind one's own business and not interfere in things that do not concern you.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 참견하지 않다, 자신의 본분을 지키다, 아는 체하지 않다
meanings.example: If you're not an expert on this, you should stay in your lane.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 분야의 전문가가 아니라면, 참견하지 말고 당신의 본분이나 지키는 게(아는 체하지 않는 게) 좋을 거예요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat someone in a very harsh and disrespectful way without considering their feelings.
meanings.example: The big corporations shouldn't be allowed to run roughshod over local businesses.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대기업이 지역 상권(작은 업체들)을 함부로 대하게(짓밟게) 놔두어서는 안 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decrease or decline very suddenly and sharply.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 급격히 떨어지다, 곤두박질치다, 벼랑 끝으로 내몰리다
meanings.example: The stock market fell off the cliff after the unexpected news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 예상치 못한 소식 이후 주식 시장이 급격히 곤두박질쳤다 (떨어졌다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be able to accept your own behavior without feeling guilty.
meanings.example: I don't know how he can live with himself after what he did.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 저지른 일을 생각하면 어떻게 양심의 가책 없이 (떳떳하게) 살아갈 수 있는지 모르겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop communicating with or helping other people; to prevent others from entering or joining.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 소통을 차단하다, 외부 접촉을 끊다, 폐쇄적인 태도를 취하다
meanings.example: After the scandal, the celebrity pulled up the drawbridge and refused to speak to the press.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스캔들 이후 그 연예인은 외부와의 소통을 차단하고(도개교를 들어 올리고) 언론과의 접촉을 거부했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fill with water (of a boat); to be in a difficult situation and likely to fail.
meanings.example: The company started taking on water after several key employees left.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주요 직원 몇 명이 떠난 후 회사가 위태로워지기 (어려움에 봉착하기) 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To guide someone into a room or building.
meanings.example: The secretary showed the visitor in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비서가 방문객을 안으로 안내해 들였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move something around violently; to make someone feel very upset or anxious.
meanings.example: The boat's propellers churned up the mud. / The news of the accident really churned up my stomach.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배의 프로펠러가 진흙을 휘저어 놓았다. / 그 사고 소식은 정말 내 마음을 뒤틀리게(심란하게) 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something from a larger object; to separate specific information from a total.
meanings.example: We stripped out the old wiring before installing the new system. / Strip out the one-time costs to see the real profit.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 새 시스템을 설치하기 전에 낡은 배선들을 제거했다. / 실질적인 수익을 보려면 일회성 비용들을 제외하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To change one's opinion or beliefs constantly depending on what is popular or beneficial.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 갈팡질팡하다, 주관 없이 대세를 따르다, 변덕을 부리다
meanings.example: He's a politician who turns with every wind.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 대세에 따라 입장을 바꾸는(주관 없는) 정치인이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go to live in a different area; to stop doing or thinking about something.
meanings.example: They moved away to a different city last year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 작년에 다른 도시로 이사했다 (떠났다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To live in the place where you work or study.
meanings.example: The family is looking for a live-in nanny.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 가족은 입주 가사 도우미(숙식하는 보모)를 찾고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be completely certain that something is true or will happen.
meanings.example: He said he'd finish the project by Friday, and you can take that to the bank.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 금요일까지 프로젝트를 끝내겠다고 했고, 그건 정말 확실한 사실이야 (전적으로 믿어도 돼).

meanings.englishDefinition: To bring something or someone into a good or desired condition through hard work or discipline.
meanings.example: The new manager is trying to whip the sales team into shape.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로 온 관리자는 영업팀의 체계를 잡으려(단련시키려) 노력하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To understand or manage a complex problem or situation completely.
meanings.example: I'm still trying to get my arms around the new tax laws.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 아직 새로운 세법을 충분히 파악하려(이해하려) 노력 중이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be gradually absorbed into something; to understand or experience something fully.
meanings.example: I sat quietly for a moment to let the news soak in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그 소식이 충분히 이해될(스며들) 수 있도록 잠시 조용히 앉아 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To run away as fast as possible to escape from danger.
meanings.example: When the tiger appeared, they had to run for their lives.
meanings.exampleKorean: 호랑이가 나타나자, 그들은 필사적으로 도망쳐야만 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To not punish someone who has done something wrong, or to give them a very light punishment.
meanings.example: The police officer let him off with just a warning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰관은 그냥 경고만 하고 그를 용서해(방면해) 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move quickly and suddenly from a place; to be very noticeable.
meanings.example: The mistake in the title jumped out at me immediately.
meanings.exampleKorean: 제목의 오류가 내 눈에 즉시 (확) 띄었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To search for something by moving things around in a casual or nosy way.
meanings.example: I caught him poking around in my desk drawers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그가 내 책상 서랍을 여기저기 뒤지고 있는 것을 붙잡았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To raise one's ears to listen carefully; to suddenly start listening with interest.
meanings.example: The dog pricked up its ears when it heard the doorbell.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 개는 초인종 소리를 듣자 귀를 쫑긋 세웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave the ground and begin to fly (of a rocket or aircraft).
meanings.example: The space shuttle lifted off exactly on schedule.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우주 왕복선이 예정된 일정에 정확히 맞춰 이륙했다 (발사되었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To settle a disagreement by fighting or arguing until one side wins.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 끝까지 싸우다, 담판을 짓다, 결판을 내다
meanings.example: The two sides will have to fight it out in court.
meanings.exampleKorean: 양측은 법정에서 끝까지 싸워 결판을 내야 할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a lot of effort to do something correctly.
meanings.example: She went to great pains to make sure the party was perfect.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 파티가 완벽하도록 정말 많은 정성을 들였다 (애를 썼다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be well-prepared and organized; to behave in a sensible and effective way (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 준비가 철저하다, 체계가 잡혀 있다, 일을 잘 처리하다
meanings.example: He really needs to get his act together if he wants to keep his job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 직장을 계속 다니고 싶다면 정말 일을 제대로 처리할 (체계적으로 준비할) 필요가 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail to develop or succeed, especially because of a lack of support.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 흐지부지 무산되다, 중도에 시들해지다, 결실을 못 맺다
meanings.example: Without funding, the new project will just wither on the vine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자금 지원이 없으면, 그 새로운 프로젝트는 그냥 흐지부지 무산될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have learned how to do something perfectly through experience.
meanings.example: She has the process of organizing large events down to a science.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 대규모 행사를 준비하는 과정을 완벽하게 익히고 있다 (능통하다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To respond to a person's kindness by being kind to someone else.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 선행을 다음 사람에게 베풀다, 호의를 이어가다
meanings.example: Instead of paying me back, just pay it forward by helping someone else.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나에게 갚는 대신, 다른 누군가를 도와줌으로써 그 선행을 다음 사람에게 베풀어 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To submit something, such as an application or a report, to an organization.
meanings.example: Don't forget to send in your application by Friday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 금요일까지 지원서를 제출하는 걸 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To embrace someone; to fully understand or accept a complex idea or situation.
meanings.example: It took me a while to wrap my arms around the new corporate strategy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 기업 전략을 충분히 이해하는(받아들이는) 데 시간이 좀 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be filling and substantial (of food).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 속이 든든하다, 영양가가 높다, 배가 부르다
meanings.example: This thick beef stew really sticks to your ribs on a cold day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 추운 날에는 이 걸쭉한 소고기 스튜가 정말 속을 든든하게 해준다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause a disturbance or trouble; to create a commotion.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 소란을 피우다, 논란을 일으키다, 먼지를 일으키다
meanings.example: The media is trying to kick up some dust about the minister's private life.
meanings.exampleKorean: 언론은 장관의 사생활에 대해 소란을 피우려 (논란을 일으키려) 하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To experience physical or mental pain; to be affected by a problem or disease.
meanings.example: Many people in this area suffer from seasonal allergies.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 지역의 많은 사람들이 계절성 알레르기로 고통받고(시달리고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To express a feeling or idea in speech or writing.
meanings.example: I'm so happy I can't even put it to words.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너무 기뻐서 말로 다 표현할 수가 없을 정도예요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To share the cost of something with other people.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 함께 투자하다, 비용을 분담하다, 공동으로 하다
meanings.example: My friends and I decided to go in on a beach house for the summer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 친구들과 나는 여름 동안 해변가 집을 빌리는 비용을 분담하기로(공동으로 빌리기로) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak aloud to yourself.
meanings.example: I often talk to myself when I'm trying to solve a problem.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 문제를 해결하려고 할 때 자주 혼잣말을 하곤 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start something again with a fresh beginning, forgetting past mistakes or failures.
meanings.example: After serving his sentence, he wanted to start with a clean slate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 형기를 마친 후, 그는 백지상태에서 새롭게 다시 시작하고 싶어 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cook a lot of food with great energy and enthusiasm; to create a lot of activity or excitement.
meanings.example: My mother was in the kitchen cooking up a storm for the holiday dinner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어머니는 명절 저녁 식사를 위해 주방에서 열정적으로 요리를 하고 계셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make people feel very excited about something by talking about it a lot; to advertise something excessively.
meanings.example: The media is really hyping up the new movie release.
meanings.exampleKorean: 언론은 새로운 영화 개봉에 대해 정말 과대 광고를 하고(열광시키고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to win an advantage from a difficult or confused situation that involves other people.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 혼란을 틈타 이득을 취하다, 어부지리를 얻다
meanings.example: The lawyer was accused of fishing in troubled waters by taking advantage of the family's dispute.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 변호사는 가족 간의 분쟁을 틈타 이득을 취하려 했다는 비난을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly start talking or thinking about a completely different subject.
meanings.example: It's hard to follow her conversations because she always flies off at a tangent.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 항상 갑자기 옆길로 새기 때문에 대화 내용을 따라가기가 어렵다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bring something to an end.
meanings.example: The ceremony will ring down the curtain on a decade of cooperation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 기념식은 10년간의 협력 관계에 마침표를 찍을 (막을 내릴) 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return to a previous state or way of behaving (redundant but common usage).
meanings.example: After a few weeks of dieting, he reverted back to his old eating habits.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몇 주간의 다이어트 후에, 그는 다시 예전의 식습관으로 되돌아갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone or something leave; to get rid of a negative feeling.
meanings.example: The bright sunshine helped to chase away her morning gloom.
meanings.exampleKorean: 밝은 햇살이 그녀의 아침 우울함을 털어내는(쫓아내는) 데 도움이 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remain at a distance from something; to stay in a place after others have left.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 뒤에 남다, 물러나 있다, 안전거리를 두다
meanings.example: The police told the crowd to stay back. / I stayed back after class to talk to the teacher.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 군중에게 뒤로 물러나 있으라고 말했다. / 나는 선생님과 이야기하기 위해 수업 후에 뒤에 남았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to tell someone to stop doing or saying something (British informal).
meanings.example: Leave it out! I've heard enough of your excuses.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그만 좀 해(집어치워)! 네 변명은 들을 만큼 들었어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay a fee for the privilege of participating in something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 참여하기 위해 돈을 내다, 대가를 치르다
meanings.example: In this business, you often have to pay to play.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 업계에서는 참여하기 위해 종종 비용(대가)을 치러야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase sharply and suddenly; to make hair stand up in points.
meanings.example: Oil prices spiked up after the news of the conflict. / He spiked up his hair with some gel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 분쟁 소식 이후 유가가 급등했다. / 그는 젤을 발라 머리카락을 뾰족하게 세웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very interested in something or to consider it very important.
meanings.example: He's not very big on sports. / Our company is big on teamwork.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 스포츠를 별로 좋아하지 않는다. / 우리 회사는 팀워크를 매우 중요하게 여긴다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be ready to take over a role or job when the opportunity arises.
meanings.example: Several younger candidates are waiting in the wings to replace the retiring CEO.
meanings.exampleKorean: 은퇴하는 CEO를 대신하기 위해 몇몇 젊은 후보들이 기회를 기다리고(대기하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To control someone completely and make them do whatever you want.
meanings.example: He's so infatuated that she can lead him by the nose.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그녀에게 너무 빠져 있어서 그녀가 그를 마음대로 휘두를(조종할) 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat someone very harshly or to defeat them completely and then discard them.
meanings.example: This industry will chew you up and spit you out if you're not careful.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조심하지 않으면 이 업계는 당신을 호되게 다루어(망가뜨려) 매정하게 버릴 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept someone's authority or a particular situation because you have no choice.
meanings.example: The government finally bowed to public pressure and changed the law.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 마침내 여론의 압력에 굴복하여 법을 개정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue for a long time without being resolved (especially of a disagreement).
meanings.example: The dispute over the contract has rumbled on for months.
meanings.exampleKorean: 계약에 대한 분쟁이 수개월째 지루하게 계속되고(질질 끌고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To divide something into specific amounts by measuring it.
meanings.example: She measured out two cups of flour for the cake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 케이크를 만들기 위해 밀가루 두 컵을 정확히 측정하여 덜어냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fill someone's mind with many thoughts or worries at once.
meanings.example: Sad memories began to crowd in on her as she sat alone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 홀로 앉아 있자 슬픈 기억들이 그녀의 머릿속에 밀려들기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to persuade someone to do something that they are already willing to do.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 이미 찬성하는 일을 설득하다, 헛수고를 하다
meanings.example: You're pushing against an open door; I've already decided to help you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이미 찬성한 일을 설득하고 있네요 (헛수고네요). 저는 이미 당신을 돕기로 결정했거든요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To calculate the total of something.
meanings.example: At the end of the day, we tally up the sales figures.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하루가 끝나면, 우리는 매출 수치를 집계한다 (합계를 낸다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To overcome a difficult situation by pretending that you know more than you do or are stronger than you are.
meanings.example: He didn't have the facts, but he managed to bluff it out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 사실관계를 몰랐지만, 허세를 부려 그 상황을 모면했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat at home or in a restaurant rather than taking the food away.
meanings.example: We decided to dine in tonight instead of ordering pizza.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 피자를 시키는 대신 오늘 밤 집에서 식사하기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To say something positive about someone to another person, especially to help them get a job or a favor.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 좋게 말해주다, 추천하다, 말을 잘해 주다
meanings.example: I'll put in a good word for you with the manager.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 관리자에게 당신에 대해 좋게 말해(추천해) 줄게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use something in a way that is beneficial or effective.
meanings.example: I'm sure you will put the scholarship money to good use.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신이 장학금을 아주 유용하게 쓸(잘 활용할) 것이라고 확신해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To restrict people's movement or access to a place for security or health reasons.
meanings.example: The city was locked down to prevent the spread of the virus.
meanings.exampleKorean: 바이러스 확산을 막기 위해 도시가 봉쇄되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone feel worried or unhappy.
meanings.example: The decision has been weighing on my mind all week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 결정이 일주일 내내 내 마음을 무겁게 짓누르고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be known by a particular name.
meanings.example: He goes by the name of 'Lefty' among his friends.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 친구들 사이에서 '왼손잡이'라는 이름으로 불린다 (알려져 있다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To feel able to do or agree to something, especially after considering it carefully.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~을 기꺼이 허락하다, 할 수 있다고 생각하다, 동의하다
meanings.example: Could you see your way clear to lending me some money?
meanings.exampleKorean: 제게 돈을 좀 빌려주실 수 있을까요? (기꺼이 빌려주실 수 있다고 생각하시나요?)

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave confidently in an embarrassing situation so that people do not notice your mistake (British informal).
meanings.example: She tripped on the stairs but managed to style it out by turning it into a dance move.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 계단에서 발이 걸렸지만, 그것을 춤 동작으로 바꿔서 당당하게 (태연하게) 넘겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage to hit or harm someone, even slightly.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 타격을 주다, 해를 입히다, (부정문에서) 손끝 하나 대지 못하다
meanings.example: The champion was so fast that his opponent couldn't lay a glove on him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 챔피언이 너무 빨라서 상대 선수는 그에게 손끝 하나 대지(타격을 주지) 못했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pull something heavy upwards; to force someone to appear in court or before someone in authority for punishment.
meanings.example: He was hauled up before the judge for speeding.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 과속 때문에 판사 앞에 소환되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take a very long time to say something or do something in a complicated way (British informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 빙 둘러 말하다, 장황하게 하다, 에둘러 말하다
meanings.example: Just tell me the truth; don't go around the houses.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그냥 진실을 말해줘. 빙 둘러 말하지(에둘러 말하지) 말고.

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage to live with very little money.
meanings.example: With so many bills to pay, we're just scratching by.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내야 할 고지서가 너무 많아서, 우리는 그냥 간신히 살아가고(생계를 꾸리고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To place your foot on something; to increase speed in a vehicle.
meanings.example: He stepped on the gas to overtake the truck.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 트럭을 추월하기 위해 가속 페달을 밟았다 (속도를 냈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To express something in speech or writing.
meanings.example: It's hard to put into words how much I appreciate your help.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신의 도움에 얼마나 감사하는지 말로 표현하기가 어렵네요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To intentionally delay something in order to gain more time.
meanings.example: He tried to stall for time by asking a lot of irrelevant questions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 무관한 질문들을 많이 던짐으로써 시간을 벌려(지연시키려) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce what is expected or needed.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 기대에 부응하다, 성과를 내다, 약속을 지키다
meanings.example: The striker finally came up with the goods and scored the winning goal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 공격수는 마침내 기대에 부응하여(성과를 내어) 결승 골을 터뜨렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To believe or trust in something; to consider something to be important.
meanings.example: I don't put much stock in what those tabloids say.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 저 타블로이드 신문들이 말하는 것을 별로 신뢰하지(믿지) 않는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop being in love with someone.
meanings.example: They realized they had fallen out of love and decided to separate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 서로에 대한 사랑이 식었다는 것을 깨닫고 헤어지기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone angry (informal).
meanings.example: His arrogant attitude really gets my dander up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 오만한 태도는 정말 나를 화나게(짜증 나게) 만든다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dislike someone or something very much and not want to spend any time on them.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~을 싫어하다, 관심을 주지 않다, 용납하지 않다
meanings.example: I have no time for people who are dishonest.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 정직하지 못한 사람들을 아주 싫어한다 (관심을 주지 않는다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To support or defend someone, especially when they are in trouble.
meanings.example: He really went to bat for me when my boss was criticizing my work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상사가 내 업무를 비난할 때 그는 정말 나를 위해 나서서(지지해) 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move your arms and legs around violently and without control.
meanings.example: The injured animal thrashed about in pain.
meanings.exampleKorean: 부상당한 동물은 고통으로 몸부림쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tell a particular story frequently to entertain people and gain their attention or favors.
meanings.example: He's been dining out on his stories of Hollywood for years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 수년째 할리우드에서의 경험담을 이야깃거리 삼아 즐기고(우려먹고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To order food or something else to be brought to you; to command something to be done.
meanings.example: We ordered up some sandwiches for the meeting. / The general ordered up more troops.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 회의를 위해 샌드위치를 좀 주문했다. / 장군은 더 많은 병력을 투입하라고 지시했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To disassemble a machine or vehicle and sell its individual parts.
meanings.example: Instead of fixing the old car, he decided to part it out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 낡은 차를 고치는 대신 부품별로 분해해 팔기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get rid of or stop using something that is no longer necessary.
meanings.example: Modern laptops have dispensed with internal CD drives.
meanings.exampleKorean: 현대적인 노트북들은 내장 CD 드라이브를 없앴다 (그것 없이 지내게 되었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be extremely happy.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 더할 나위 없이 기쁘다, 기분이 최고다
meanings.example: I've been walking on sunshine ever since I got the job offer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 일자리 제안을 받은 이후로 나는 더할 나위 없이 기분이 좋다 (햇살 위를 걷는 기분이다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To pretend to be happy or confident when you are actually feeling upset or frightened.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 태연한 척하다, (어려움 속에서) 용기를 내다, 씩씩하게 행동하다
meanings.example: She was heartbroken, but she put on a brave face for her children.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 가슴이 아팠지만, 아이들을 위해 태연한 척했다 (씩씩하게 행동했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a way that is likely to cause problems for yourself.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 화를 자초하다, 문제를 일으킬 행동을 하다
meanings.example: Leaving your car unlocked is just asking for trouble.
meanings.exampleKorean: 차 문을 잠그지 않고 두는 것은 그냥 화를 자초하는 일이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To change from a calm state to a very intense or angry state very quickly.
meanings.example: He's usually quiet, but his temper can go from 0 to 100 in a second.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 평소에 조용하지만, 화가 나면 순식간에 (급격히) 폭발할 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take steps to prevent something unpleasant or harmful from happening.
meanings.example: We must guard against the risk of identity theft.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 신원 도용의 위험에 대비해야(경계해야) 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To exclude a particular detail from a calculation or a decision.
meanings.example: If you factor out the seasonal fluctuations, the sales are actually growing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 계절적 변동을 제외하면, 매출은 사실 성장하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get something desirable by chance rather than through planning or merit.
meanings.example: He lucked into a very well-paying job right after graduation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 졸업 직후에 운 좋게도 아주 보수가 좋은 직장을 얻었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend money in a very careless or showy way.
meanings.example: Since he won the lottery, he's been throwing money around like crazy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 복권에 당첨된 이후로, 그는 미친 듯이 돈을 펑펑 쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone suddenly stop what they are doing or saying because they are surprised or shocked.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 갑자기 멈추게 하다, 깜짝 놀라게 하다
meanings.example: His blunt question brought her up short.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 직설적인 질문은 그녀를 깜짝 놀라게 하여 (말을) 멈추게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop annoying someone or being in their way (informal).
meanings.example: I told him to just get out of my face and leave me alone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그에게 내 앞에서 물러나서 나를 좀 내버려 두라고 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to say that something proves that a particular thing is true.
meanings.example: It just goes to show that hard work pays off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그것은 노력이 결실을 맺는다는 것을 분명히 보여준다 (입증한다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept something exactly as it appears to be, without thinking that it might have a hidden meaning.
meanings.example: You shouldn't always take what he says at face value.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 하는 말을 항상 액면 그대로 받아들여서는 (곧이곧대로 믿어서는) 안 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To look straight ahead without looking at anything in particular, usually because you are thinking deeply or are bored.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 멍하니 허공을 응시하다, 넋을 잃고 바라보다
meanings.example: She sat there staring into space, lost in her own thoughts.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 자신의 생각에 빠져 멍하니 허공을 응시하며 거기 앉아 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consider all the details of something very carefully.
meanings.example: The plan was carefully thought out before it was implemented.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 계획은 실행되기 전에 세심하게 심사숙고(계획)되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give a brief description of the main points of something.
meanings.example: He quickly sketched out his ideas on a napkin.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 아이디어를 냅킨 위에 대략적으로 설명했다 (개요를 그렸다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To respond quickly and sharply to a criticism or insult (informal).
meanings.example: The celebrity clapped back at her critics on social media.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 연예인은 소셜 미디어에서 자신을 비판하는 사람들에게 날카롭게 응수했다 (맞받아쳤다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To quickly decide not to participate in or to leave a situation that seems unpleasant or dangerous (informal).
meanings.example: As soon as I saw the spider, I noped out of the room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 거미를 보자마자 나는 즉시 그 방에서 빠져나왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To improve one's behavior and start acting in a sensible and responsible way.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 마음을 다잡고 똑바로 행동하다, 행실을 고치다
meanings.example: If you don't straighten up and fly right, you're going to get fired.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너 계속 행실을 고치고(마음 다잡고) 똑바로 행동하지 않으면 해고당할 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very nervous or easily frightened.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 겁이 아주 많다, 사소한 일에도 깜짝 놀라다
meanings.example: She's so nervous lately that she jumps at her own shadow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 요즘 너무 예민해서 사소한 일에도 깜짝깜짝 놀란다 (겁이 아주 많다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin a journey, especially a long one by car.
meanings.example: We decided to take to the road and explore the countryside.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 길을 나서서(여행을 떠나서) 시골 지역을 탐험하기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spread something out in a long line; to make something last longer than necessary.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 길게 늘어뜨리다, 연장하다, 한 줄로 세우다
meanings.example: The runners were strung out along the marathon course. / Don't string out the meeting; let's finish quickly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주자들이 마라톤 코스를 따라 길게 늘어서 있었다. / 회의를 너무 끌지(연장하지) 마세요. 빨리 끝냅시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To burst into flames; to appear suddenly on a screen; to suddenly feel angry.
meanings.example: The dry grass flashed up as soon as the match touched it. / His name flashed up on the caller ID.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마른 풀에 성냥이 닿자마자 불길이 갑자기 타올랐다. / 발신 번호 표시창에 그의 이름이 순식간에 나타났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To create or invent something false, such as a document or a story (informal).
meanings.example: The accountant was caught trying to phony up the company's financial records.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회계사는 회사의 재무 기록을 조작하려다 붙잡혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a very difficult task to do.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 해야 할 일이 태산이다, 매우 힘들다, 벅찬 일을 맡다
meanings.example: With only two days left, the team really has its work cut out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이틀밖에 남지 않아서, 팀원들은 정말 해야 할 일이 태산이다 (매우 힘든 상황이다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To save money for future use (informal).
meanings.example: He's been socking away money every month for his retirement.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 은퇴 자금을 위해 매달 돈을 따로 챙겨두고(저축하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stand very straight with your hands at your sides, especially in the military.
meanings.example: The soldiers stood at attention as the general walked past.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장군이 지나가자 병사들은 차렷 자세로 섰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very successful and exciting (especially of a party or event).
meanings.example: The charity auction really went with a swing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 자선 경매는 정말 활기차게 (성공적으로) 진행되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get revenge for something that someone did to you in the past.
meanings.example: He used his new power to pay off some old scores with his enemies.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 새로운 권력을 이용해 적들에게 옛 원한을 갚았다 (앙갚음했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste time or money on unimportant things (informal).
meanings.example: Stop piddling away your time on video games and start studying.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비디오 게임으로 시간 낭비(허송세월)하지 말고 공부 시작해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to support or defend someone or something strongly.
meanings.example: Several experts have taken up the cudgels for the new environmental policy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몇몇 전문가들이 새로운 환경 정책을 옹호하고(지지하고) 나섰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter a place in large numbers; to fill someone's mind (of thoughts).
meanings.example: Journalists began to crowd in as the celebrity appeared. / Memories of the accident crowded in on him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 연예인이 나타나자 기자들이 밀려들기(들이닥치기) 시작했다. / 사고의 기억들이 그의 머릿속에 가득 찼다 (밀려들었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To dry someone or something using a towel.
meanings.example: He stepped out of the pool and toweled himself off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 수영장에서 나와 수건으로 몸을 닦았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To measure a specific amount of something by weight.
meanings.example: The merchant weighed out a pound of coffee beans.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상인은 커피 원두 1파운드를 달아냈다 (측정하여 나누어 주었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To push something so that it moves away; to reject someone's affection or help.
meanings.example: She pushed her plate away, feeling full. / Why do you always push people away when they try to help?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 배가 불러서 접시를 밀어냈다. / 왜 사람들이 도우려 할 때마다 항상 그들을 밀어내는(거부하는) 거야?

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place on a horse, bicycle, or motorcycle.
meanings.example: The cowboy jumped on his horse and rode off into the sunset.
meanings.exampleKorean: 카우보이는 말에 올라타 노을 속으로 타고 떠났다 (사라졌다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a short visit to someone, especially to check if they are okay.
meanings.example: I'll look in on my grandmother on my way home.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집에 가는 길에 할머니를 잠시 들여다볼(살피러 방문할) 거예요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To examine someone or something to make sure they are safe or correct.
meanings.example: The nurse came in to check on the patient every hour.
meanings.exampleKorean: 간호사는 매시간 환자의 상태를 확인하러(살피러) 들어왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to do something that has been planned or discussed.
meanings.example: We have decided to go ahead with the new marketing campaign.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 새로운 마케팅 캠페인을 추진하기로(진행하기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To think about what will happen in the future and plan for it.
meanings.example: We need to look ahead to the next few years and set our goals.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 향후 몇 년의 앞날을 생각하고(내다보고) 목표를 세워야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use something as an excuse to avoid dealing with a problem or responsibility.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~ 뒤에 숨다, 핑계 대다, 방패막이 삼다
meanings.example: You can't just hide behind company policy to avoid answering the question.
meanings.exampleKorean: 질문에 답하지 않으려고 회사 규정 뒤에 숨어서(핑계 대서)는 안 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be the real reason or cause of something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~의 배후에 있다, 원인이 되다, 숨겨진 이유이다
meanings.example: What reasons lie behind the government's decision?
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부의 이번 결정 배후에는 어떤 이유가 있는가 (무엇이 원인인가)?

meanings.englishDefinition: To win something very easily.
meanings.example: The young actor walked away with the Best Actor award.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 젊은 배우가 남우주연상을 쉽게 차지했다 (우승했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing something again that was interrupted some time ago.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 중단했던 일을 다시 시작하다, 관계를 다시 이어가다
meanings.example: After a long break, she's trying to pick up the threads of her career.
meanings.exampleKorean: 긴 공백기 끝에, 그녀는 자신의 경력을 다시 시작하려(중단했던 일을 이어가려) 노력 중이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become thin and weak because you are very unhappy or in love with someone you cannot have.
meanings.example: He pined away after his wife died.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아내가 죽은 후 그리움으로 수척해졌다 (야위어갔다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To work hard and continuously at something.
meanings.example: He's been hammering away at that report all day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 하루 종일 그 보고서를 쓰기 위해 부단히 노력하고(매달리고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put something in a safe or private place where it is not easily seen.
meanings.example: She had a small fortune tucked away in a secret bank account.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 비밀 은행 계좌에 적지 않은 재산을 숨겨두고(보관하고) 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To expect or plan for something to happen.
meanings.example: I hadn't figured on the traffic being so heavy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 교통 체증이 이렇게 심할 줄은 예상하지(고려하지) 못했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have been made or begun at a particular time in the past.
meanings.example: This church dates back to the 13th century.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 교회는 13세기까지 역사가 거슬러 올라간다 (13세기에 세워졌다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To move or develop more slowly than others; to fail to keep up.
meanings.example: The company is lagging behind its competitors in new technology.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 신기술 분야에서 경쟁사들에 비해 뒤처지고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move towards someone quietly and slowly; to happen to someone gradually and unexpectedly.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 몰래 다가가다, (시간 등이) 어느덧 다가오다, 살금살금 접근하다
meanings.example: Don't creep up on me like that! You scared me. / Old age tends to creep up on you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그렇게 몰래 다가오지 마! 놀랐잖아. / 노년은 어느덧 당신에게 다가오기 마련이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrange for work to be done by another person or company rather than by yourself.
meanings.example: The council has decided to contract out the cleaning services.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의회는 청소 서비스를 외부 업체에 외주를 주기로(맡기기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To show that you think someone or something is funny or ridiculous.
meanings.example: It's not polite to laugh at other people's mistakes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다른 사람의 실수를 비웃는 것은 예의가 아니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail to keep the same pace as others in a group.
meanings.example: He started to drop behind the other runners after the first mile.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 첫 1마일 이후 다른 주자들보다 뒤처지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dismiss something serious by laughing about it.
meanings.example: She tried to laugh away her embarrassment after the slip.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 미끄러진 후의 창피함을 웃어넘기려 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give your full support to a person or an idea.
meanings.example: The party members lined up behind their new leader.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당원들은 새로운 지도자를 전폭적으로 지지하고 나섰다 (그의 뒤에 줄을 섰다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be delayed or late in a schedule.
meanings.example: The trains are running behind schedule because of the snow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 눈 때문에 열차 운행이 예정보다 지연되고(늦어지고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing something with a lot of energy and determination.
meanings.example: We set to and cleaned the whole house in two hours.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 열심히 달려들어 두 시간 만에 집 전체를 청소했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To travel from place to place; to change the position of something.
meanings.example: He spent his youth moving around from city to city. / Let's move the sofa around.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 젊은 시절을 이 도시 저 도시 돌아다니며 보냈다. / 소파 위치를 좀 옮겨보자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move the upper part of the body backwards.
meanings.example: He reared back to throw the ball as hard as he could.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 공을 최대한 세게 던지려고 상체를 크게 뒤로 젖혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remain calm and sensible in a difficult or emotional situation.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 침착함을 유지하다, 평정심을 잃지 않다, 간신히 버티다
meanings.example: She was heartbroken, but she managed to hold it together for the sake of her family.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 가슴이 찢어지는 듯했지만, 가족을 위해 간신히 침착함을 유지했다 (버텨냈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To receive a letter, phone call, or email from someone.
meanings.example: I haven't heard from him in several months.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 몇 달째 그로부터 소식을 듣지(연락을 받지) 못했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid talking about a difficult or embarrassing subject directly.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 교묘하게 회피하다, 에둘러 말하다, 언급을 피하다
meanings.example: The politician tried to skate around the issue of tax increases.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인은 세금 인상 문제를 교묘하게 회피하려(에둘러 말하려) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To present something to someone for discussion or decision; to consider something to be more important than something else.
meanings.example: The proposal was put before the committee last week. / He always puts his family before his career.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 제안은 지난주에 위원회에 제시되었다. / 그는 항상 경력보다 가족을 우선시한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get through a situation with minimal effort or by just doing enough to avoid failure.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 간신히 넘기다, 적당히 요령을 피우다, 모면하다
meanings.example: He's been skating by on his charm rather than doing any real work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 실제 업무를 하기보다는 매력으로 적당히 상황을 넘겨(요령을 피워) 왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give something to someone else after you have used it or finished with it.
meanings.example: These traditions have been handed on from generation to generation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 전통들은 대대로 물려 내려왔다 (전달되었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To rub your hands together because you are cold or because you are very excited about something.
meanings.example: He's rubbing his hands together in anticipation of a big profit.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 큰 이익에 대한 기대로 몹시 흥분하고(즐거워하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cancel or postpone an outdoor event because of rain.
meanings.example: The baseball game was rained out in the third inning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 야구 경기가 3회에 우천으로 취소되었다 (중단되었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To become covered with new skin (of a wound).
meanings.example: The cut on his arm is starting to heal over.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 팔에 난 상처가 아물기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To surround someone or something; to restrict someone's freedom of action.
meanings.example: She felt hedged in by the many rules and regulations.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 많은 규칙과 규제들에 의해 제한받고(억눌리고) 있다고 느꼈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move quickly from one place to another; to discuss ideas with several people.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 이리저리 튀다, 아이디어를 주고받다, (자주) 옮겨 다니다
meanings.example: We bounced some ideas around for the new project. / He's been bouncing around from job to job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 새 프로젝트를 위해 몇 가지 아이디어를 주고받았다. / 그는 이 직장 저 직장으로 자주 옮겨 다니고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To heat food completely until it is hot all the way through.
meanings.example: Make sure you heat the pie through before serving it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 파이를 내놓기 전에 속까지 충분히 데웠는지 확인하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have someone or something as the main interest or focus.
meanings.example: His whole life revolves around his children.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 전 생애는 자녀들을 중심으로 돌아간다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become unable to think clearly or control your feelings because you are very upset or shocked.
meanings.example: She completely went to pieces when she heard the bad news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 나쁜 소식을 들었을 때 완전히 무너져 내렸다 (자제력을 잃었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To remain careful and alert for possible danger or difficulty.
meanings.example: In this business, you always have to keep your guard up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 업계에서는 항상 경계를 늦추지 않아야(항상 조심해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make an extra effort to do something, even if it is inconvenient.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 애써 ~하다, 수고를 아끼지 않다, 번거로움을 무릅쓰다
meanings.example: Thank you for going to the trouble of finding a hotel for us.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리를 위해 애써 호텔을 찾아봐 주시는 수고를 해주셔서 감사합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep someone or something in a place away from other people; to isolate.
meanings.example: She shut herself away in her study to finish the book. / The truth was shut away for many years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 책을 끝내기 위해 서재에 자신을 가두었다(격리했다). / 진실은 수년 동안 숨겨져 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To order food from a restaurant to be delivered to your home or office.
meanings.example: I'm too tired to cook; let's just order in tonight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 요리하기 너무 피곤하네요. 오늘 밤엔 그냥 시켜 먹읍시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gain weight (informal).
meanings.example: I've really put on the pounds over the winter holidays.
meanings.exampleKorean: 겨울 휴가 동안 정말 살이 많이 쪘다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter a place without an appointment; to enter a room unexpectedly.
meanings.example: He just walked in while I was changing clothes. / The clinic accepts walk-ins until 5 PM.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 옷을 갈아입는 동안 그가 그냥 불쑥 들어왔다. / 이 병원은 오후 5시까지 예약 없는 방문객을 받습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To receive an additional payment based on the future performance of a business you have sold; to earn enough to cover an advance payment.
meanings.example: The founders will receive a bonus if the company earns out its performance targets.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사가 실적 목표를 달성하면 창업자들은 보너스를 받을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To calculate the total amount of something.
meanings.example: Can you reckon up the total cost of the trip?
meanings.exampleKorean: 이번 여행의 총비용을 집계해(계산해) 주시겠어요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide something that is needed or has been requested (informal).
meanings.example: The witness finally came across with the information we needed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 증인은 마침내 우리가 필요로 하는 정보를 내놓았다 (제공했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To blow air from the mouth onto something; to affect or influence something slightly.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 숨을 불어넣다, 영향을 주다, (거울 등을) 입김으로 흐리게 하다
meanings.example: She breathed upon the cold glass to draw a heart. / His reputation was breathed upon by rumors.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 하트를 그리려고 차가운 유리에 입김을 불어넣었다. / 그의 평판은 소문들에 의해 약간의 타격을 입었다 (영향을 받았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be remembered as a significant event or person in the future.
meanings.example: This discovery will go down in history as one of the greatest achievements of our time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 발견은 우리 시대의 가장 위대한 업적 중 하나로 역사에 남을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move in front of people who are already waiting in a line.
meanings.example: Hey, don't cut in line! We've been waiting for an hour.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이봐요, 새치기하지 마세요! 우리 한 시간째 기다리고 있다고요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To change something, especially a routine or strategy, to make it more interesting or effective.
meanings.example: We decided to switch up our workout routine this week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 이번 주에 운동 루틴을 좀 바꿔보기로(변화를 주기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use a situation or experience in a way that is beneficial.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 유용하게 이용하다, 잘 활용하다, 이득을 보다
meanings.example: She turned her natural curiosity to good account in her career as a journalist.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 타고난 호기심을 기자라는 직업에서 아주 유용하게 잘 활용했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get money, food, or other things from people without paying or working for them (informal).
meanings.example: He's thirty years old and still mooching off his parents.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 서른 살인데도 여전히 부모님께 빌붙어 살고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become ill with a particular disease (especially a minor one).
meanings.example: Half the office has gone down with the flu.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사무실 인원의 절반이 독감에 걸려 앓아누웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop working or progressing; to fail suddenly.
meanings.example: The peace talks stalled out after a day of negotiations.
meanings.exampleKorean: 평화 협상은 하루 만에 중단되었다 (멈췄다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be so much better than someone or something else that they seem unimportant or of poor quality.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 무색하게 만들다, 빛을 잃게 하다, 압도하다
meanings.example: His performance was so brilliant that it put all the other actors in the shade.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 연기는 너무나 훌륭해서 다른 모든 배우를 무색하게 만들었다 (빛을 잃게 했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually stop or come to an end in a weak or disappointing way.
meanings.example: The protests eventually sputtered out as winter approached.
meanings.exampleKorean: 겨울이 다가오면서 시위는 결국 흐지부지 끝났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move quickly and suddenly from one place to another.
meanings.example: Small fish were darting about in the clear water.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작은 물고기들이 맑은 물속에서 잽싸게 이리저리 움직이고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat a very large amount of food (informal).
meanings.example: He's been eating his head off ever since he got home from the hike.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 하이킹에서 돌아온 이후로 계속 엄청나게 먹어 대고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat someone with great affection; to accept an idea or person warmly.
meanings.example: The community took the orphans to its bosom.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지역 사회는 그 고아들을 따뜻하게 받아들였다 (가슴에 품었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be not used or to be thrown away without being used.
meanings.example: It's a shame to let all that food go to waste.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 많은 음식을 그냥 버려지게(낭비되게) 두는 것은 유감스러운 일이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To examine a large amount of information or objects very carefully to find something.
meanings.example: Police are sifting through the evidence to find the killer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 범인을 찾기 위해 증거물들을 면밀히 조사하고(샅샅이 뒤지고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go to an event for a short time, often because you feel you should rather than because you want to.
meanings.example: I don't really want to go, but I suppose I should put in an appearance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정말 가고 싶지는 않지만, 그래도 잠시 얼굴을 비쳐야(참석해야) 할 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To seem much less important when compared with something else.
meanings.example: Our money problems pale into insignificance when compared with theirs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들의 문제와 비교하면 우리의 돈 문제는 무색해진다 (사소해 보인다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To die for a cause or for another person.
meanings.example: They were willing to lay down their lives for their country.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 나라를 위해 기꺼이 목숨을 바칠 각오가 되어 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage to swallow something that is unpleasant; to suppress a strong emotion.
meanings.example: I had to choke down the bitter medicine. / She tried to choke down her anger.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그 쓴 약을 억지로 삼켜야 했다. / 그녀는 분노를 억누르려 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start crying, especially to get sympathy or something you want (informal).
meanings.example: As soon as she didn't get her way, she turned on the waterworks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자신의 뜻대로 되지 않자마자, 그녀는 눈물을 짜내기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get out of bed very early in the morning.
meanings.example: My grandfather always rises with the lark to work in his garden.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 할아버지는 정원에서 일하시기 위해 항상 아침 일찍 일어나신다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be angry or annoyed; to be stubborn and uncooperative.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 화가 나 있다, 고집을 피우다, 비협조적이다
meanings.example: He's had his back up ever since the meeting started.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의가 시작된 이후로 그는 계속 화가 나 있다 (비협조적이다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To guide someone to the exit of a building or room.
meanings.example: Please show the gentleman out after the interview.
meanings.exampleKorean: 면접이 끝난 후에 그 신사분을 밖으로 안내해 드리세요(배웅해 주세요).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop fighting and surrender.
meanings.example: The rebels were ordered to lay down their arms.
meanings.exampleKorean: 반군들은 무기를 내려놓으라는(항복하라는) 명령을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be able to see or understand something very easily and immediately.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 척 보면 알다, 한눈에 알아보다, 아주 명백하다
meanings.example: You can see with half an eye that they are in love.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들이 서로 사랑하고 있다는 건 척 보면 알 수 있다 (아주 명백하다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To phone your office or place of work to say that you are ill and cannot come to work.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 병가를 내다, 아파서 못 간다고 전화하다
meanings.example: Several people have called in sick today with the flu.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 몇몇 사람들이 독감으로 병가를 냈다 (아파서 못 간다고 전화했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To pretend that a situation is not as bad as it really is.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 태연한 척하다, 의연하게 대처하다, 씩씩한 척하다
meanings.example: She's putting a brave face on her illness.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 자신의 병에 대해 태연한 척하고(의연하게 대처하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To save as much money as it cost to buy.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 본전을 뽑다, 비용을 충당하다, 투자 가치가 있다
meanings.example: The solar panels will pay for themselves in about five years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 태양광 패널은 약 5년 후면 본전을 뽑을 (비용을 충당할) 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very obvious or noticeable (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 아주 눈에 잘 띄다, 아주 분명하다, 척 봐도 알 수 있다
meanings.example: His guilt stuck out a mile.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 죄책감은 척 봐도 알 수 있을 정도로 분명했다 (아주 눈에 잘 띄었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a strong feeling of love for someone, especially when it is not shared (informal).
meanings.example: I had a huge crush on my English teacher in high school.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 고등학교 때 영어 선생님께 완전히 반해 있었다 (짝사랑했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To expect or be prepared for something.
meanings.example: The job was more difficult than I had bargained for.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 일은 내가 예상했던 것보다 더 어려웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force yourself not to show an emotion, such as anger or grief.
meanings.example: She managed to choke back her tears and continue the speech.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 간신히 눈물을 억누르고 연설을 계속했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop yourself from saying something; to respond sharply to a criticism.
meanings.example: I had to bite back a sharp reply. / She bit back at her critics on social media.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 날카로운 대답을 꾹 참아야 했다. / 그녀는 소셜 미디어에서 자신을 비판하는 사람들에게 날카롭게 응수했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish or stop something.
meanings.example: The peace treaty brought the long war to an end.
meanings.exampleKorean: 평화 조약이 그 긴 전쟁을 종결지었다 (끝냈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone or something go away by shouting 'shoo' or by waving your arms.
meanings.example: She shooed the cat off the table.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 고양이를 테이블 위에서 쫓아냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage to say something when other people are talking; to move gradually into a small space.
meanings.example: I couldn't even edge a word in during the discussion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 토론 중에 말 한마디조차 간신히 끼워 넣을 수 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To unfold or flow out continuously, like thread from a spool.
meanings.example: The details of the plan began to spool out during the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의 중에 계획의 세부 사항들이 끊임없이 흘러나오기(전개되기) 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To change your ideas or way of doing things so that they represent the latest trends.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 시대의 흐름에 맞추다, 시대에 발맞추어 나가다
meanings.example: Traditional businesses need to move with the times if they want to survive.
meanings.exampleKorean: 전통적인 기업들이 살아남으려면 시대의 흐름에 맞춰야(발맞추어 나가야) 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop criticizing or annoying someone about something (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 간섭을 그만두다, 잔소리를 멈추다, 괴롭히지 않다
meanings.example: I'll do my homework later, so just get off my case!
meanings.exampleKorean: 숙제는 나중에 할 테니까 제발 간섭 좀 그만해(잔소리 좀 그만해)!

meanings.englishDefinition: To force a price or value to a lower level.
meanings.example: The competition between companies helped to drive down the prices.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기업 간의 경쟁이 가격을 내리는(떨어뜨리는) 데 도움이 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To annoy someone so much that they become very upset or unable to concentrate.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 미치게 만들다, 정신을 못 차리게 하다, 아주 짜증 나게 하다
meanings.example: The constant noise from the construction site is driving me to distraction.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공사 현장에서 끊임없이 들리는 소음이 나를 미치게(정신을 못 차리게) 만들고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk to someone in an angry or serious way because they have done something wrong.
meanings.example: The teacher gave the students a good speaking to after they were caught cheating.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님은 학생들이 부정행위를 하다 걸린 후 그들을 호되게 나무라셨다 (훈계하셨다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To reject or show disapproval of something.
meanings.example: The committee thumbed down the proposal for the new stadium.
meanings.exampleKorean: 위원회는 새로운 경기장 건설 제안을 거절했다 (반대했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a strong sexual desire for someone; to want something very much (like power or money).
meanings.example: He spent his life lusting after power and wealth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 권력과 부를 갈망하며(탐내며) 평생을 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move from one place to another quickly or frequently.
meanings.example: The children were hopping about with excitement.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 흥분해서 이리저리 깡충깡충 뛰어다니고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall asleep, especially while sitting up or because of drugs (informal).
meanings.example: The old man nodded out in front of the television.
meanings.exampleKorean: 노인은 텔레비전 앞에서 꾸벅꾸벅 졸았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing something in a way that is likely to fail because it is not logical or correct.
meanings.example: If you try to build the roof before the walls, you're beginning at the wrong end.
meanings.exampleKorean: 벽을 세우기도 전에 지붕부터 만들려고 한다면, 당신은 잘못된 방식으로 시작하는(순서가 틀린) 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To park a car and leave it there.
meanings.example: Just park up by the side of the road.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그냥 길가에 차를 세우세요(주차하세요).

meanings.englishDefinition: To move one's body around because of being uncomfortable; to change one's position or opinion frequently.
meanings.example: He shifted about in his chair, trying to get comfortable. / Stop shifting about and give me a straight answer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 편한 자세를 잡으려고 의자에서 몸을 이리저리 뒤척였다. / 입장 자꾸 바꾸지 말고 솔직하게 대답해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To experience many difficulties or dangers in order to achieve something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 물불 가리지 않다, 온갖 고난을 무릅쓰다
meanings.example: He was willing to go through fire and water to protect his family.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 가족을 지키기 위해서라면 물불 가리지 않을(온갖 고난을 무릅쓸) 각오가 되어 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue doing what you usually do, especially without being distracted by something else.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 제 할 일을 하다, 일상 업무를 보다
meanings.example: Despite the news, people in the city continued to go about their business.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 소식에도 불구하고, 도시 사람들은 계속해서 제 할 일을(일상 업무를) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be extremely full or crowded.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 터질 듯이 가득 차다, 발 디딜 틈이 없다
meanings.example: The stadium was bursting at the seams with excited fans.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경기장은 흥분한 팬들로 터질 듯이 가득 찼다 (발 디딜 틈이 없었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide new information that helps people understand something better.
meanings.example: New evidence has thrown light on the cause of the accident.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 증거가 그 사고의 원인을 밝히는(명확하게 하는) 데 도움을 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak a lot about something in an annoying or boastful way (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 떠들어 대다, 지껄이다, 잘난 체하며 말하다
meanings.example: He's always spouting off about his political views.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 자신의 정치적 견해에 대해 잘난 체하며 떠들어 댄다 (지껄인다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail or be unsuccessful (especially of a plan or theory).
meanings.example: The whole scheme fell to the ground after the main sponsor withdrew.
