meanings.exampleKorean: 주요 후원자가 철회한 후 전체 계획이 무산되었다 (수포로 돌아갔다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To try very hard to get something or to participate in something.
meanings.example: Advertisers are banging down the door to get a spot during the Super Bowl.
meanings.exampleKorean: 광고주들은 슈퍼볼 기간에 광고 자리를 얻기 위해 강력히 요구하고(열렬히 달려들고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place slowly; to avoid a responsibility or task.
meanings.example: He tried to shuffle off the blame onto his assistant.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 조수에게 책임을 회피하려(떠넘기려) 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To confront a difficult situation or person with confidence and determination.
meanings.example: She decided to face out her critics instead of hiding from them.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 비평가들로부터 숨는 대신 그들에게 대담하게 맞서기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk too much about unimportant things or secrets (informal).
meanings.example: He's always running at the mouth about things he doesn't understand.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자기가 이해하지 못하는 일들에 대해 항상 함부로 지껄인다 (입을 놀린다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To meet someone unexpectedly; to approach someone quickly and often aggressively.
meanings.example: I ran up on an old friend at the supermarket. / The guards ran up on the intruder.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 슈퍼마켓에서 옛 친구를 갑자기 마주쳤다. / 경비원들이 침입자에게 위협적으로 접근했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remind someone of a mistake or fault in a critical and insulting way.
meanings.example: It's unfair to cast his past failures in his teeth now.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지금 그의 과거 실패를 들춰내어 비난하는(면박을 주는) 것은 불공평하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be involved or have an influence in something.
meanings.example: She certainly had a hand in the success of the project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 분명히 그 프로젝트의 성공에 관여했다 (참여했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To do what is easiest in a difficult situation, rather than what is right or best.
meanings.example: He took the easy way out and resigned instead of fixing the problem.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 문제를 해결하는 대신 쉬운 길을 택해 사임해 버렸다 (회피했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To take an active role in a task of which you are the leader, rather than just giving orders.
meanings.example: The general was known for leading from the front in every battle.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 장군은 모든 전투에서 솔선수범하는(앞장서서 이끄는) 것으로 유명했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To engrave something using heat; to fix an image or idea permanently in someone's mind.
meanings.example: The image of the accident was burned into my memory forever.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사고의 장면은 내 기억 속에 영원히 각인되었다 (깊이 새겨졌다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin doing something, especially something that has been delayed; to start criticizing or attacking someone.
meanings.example: I need to start in on that pile of paperwork. / Don't start in on me about my grades again.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 쌓인 서류 작업들을 본격적으로 시작해야 해요. / 내 성적 가지고 또 잔소리(비판)하기 시작하지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To survive or come safely through a storm or a difficult situation.
meanings.example: The small ship managed to weather out the storm. / We have to weather out this economic crisis.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 작은 배는 간신히 폭풍우를 견뎌냈다. / 우리는 이 경제 위기를 무사히 넘겨야(견뎌내야) 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To perform a headstand; to be able to do something very easily.
meanings.example: He's been doing this job for so long he could do it standing on his head.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 이 일을 아주 오래 해서 물구나무서서도 할(아주 쉽게 해낼) 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be an obstacle that prevents someone from doing something.
meanings.example: Lack of funding lies in the way of our research project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자금 부족이 우리 연구 프로젝트의 앞길을 가로막고(방해가 되고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To carry something away with a powerful movement; to make someone feel very excited or enthusiastic.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 휩쓸고 가다, 심취하게 하다, 정신을 못 차리게 하다
meanings.example: The floodwaters swept along everything in their path. / I was swept along by the enthusiasm of the crowd.
meanings.exampleKorean: 홍수가 경로에 있는 모든 것을 휩쓸고 갔다. / 나는 군중의 열기에 완전히 심취했다 (휩쓸렸다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To move to a lower position; to fall to the ground suddenly.
meanings.example: The temperature dropped down below zero last night. / He dropped down in exhaustion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어젯밤 기온이 영하로 내려갔다. / 그는 기진맥진해서 주저앉았다 (쓰러졌다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce the level or standard of something to match a lower one.
meanings.example: The new policy might level down the quality of education in the region.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 정책이 그 지역의 교육 수준을 하향 평준화할(낮출) 수도 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To catch someone when they are unprepared or in a weak position.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 허점을 찌르다, 유리하게 이용하다, 방심할 때 공격하다
meanings.example: The enemy was taken at advantage during the night raid.
meanings.exampleKorean: 야간 기습 중에 적의 허점을 찔렀다 (방심한 적을 공격했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing something with a lot of enthusiasm and energy.
meanings.example: She threw herself into her new job and worked late every night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 새 직장에 몸을 던져(열중하여) 매일 밤늦게까지 일했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To recover one's control or composure after a shock or a difficult situation.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 마음을 가다듬다, 기운을 차리다, 평정심을 되찾다
meanings.example: Take a moment to gather yourself together before you go on stage.
meanings.exampleKorean: 무대에 오르기 전에 잠시 마음을 가다듬을(기운을 차릴) 시간을 가지세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause something to be used or to have an effect.
meanings.example: The manager decided to bring all his best players into play for the final match.
meanings.exampleKorean: 감독은 결승전을 위해 모든 최고의 선수들을 활용하기로(투입하기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail to seize an opportunity; to lose something that you almost had.
meanings.example: We let a great business opportunity slip through our fingers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 좋은 사업 기회를 손에서 빠져나가게(놓치게) 내버려 두었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop doing something while you are still successful or in a good position, to avoid losing what you have gained.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 잘 나갈 때 그만두다, 적당한 때에 멈추다
meanings.example: You've won three games in a row; maybe you should quit while you're ahead.
meanings.exampleKorean: 벌써 세 판 연속으로 이겼네요. 잘 나갈 때 그만두는 게 좋겠어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move by rolling around on a surface; to laugh very hard.
meanings.example: The kids were rolling about on the grass. / We were rolling about laughing at his jokes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 풀밭에서 굴러다니고 있었다. / 우리는 그의 농담에 배를 잡고 굴러다니며(뒹굴며) 웃었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To delay dealing with a difficult problem or to ignore a suggestion (British informal).
meanings.example: The government has kicked the decision into the long grass until after the election.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 선거 이후까지 그 결정을 뒤로 미뤘다 (보류했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To join a conversation by saying something suddenly.
meanings.example: "I agree!" she chimed up from the back of the room.
meanings.exampleKorean: "저도 동의해요!" 그녀가 방 뒤쪽에서 말을 꺼냈다 (끼어들었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove the rough edges of someone's character or to refine something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 모난 부분을 깎아내다, 다듬다, 세련되게 만들다
meanings.example: University life helped to knock the corners off him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대학 생활은 그의 모난 성격을 다듬는 데 도움이 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hope that an unpleasant situation or problem will disappear without having to do anything about it.
meanings.example: You can't just wish your problems away; you have to deal with them.
meanings.exampleKorean: 문제가 그냥 사라지기를 바랄(빌고 있을) 수는 없어요. 직접 해결해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have one's turn to act or to face a challenge.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 기회가 오다, 차례가 되다, 타석에 서다
meanings.example: Now that it's your turn to lead, you finally come to bat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이제 당신이 이끌 차례가 되었으니, 드디어 기회가 온(타석에 선) 것이네요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause something to fail or to have no result.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 무산시키다, 수포로 돌아가게 하다, 실패하게 하다
meanings.example: All our hard work was brought to nought by the sudden change in policy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리의 모든 노력은 갑작스러운 정책 변화로 인해 무산되었다 (수포로 돌아갔다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase the intensity or amount of something; to make something more exciting.
meanings.example: The team decided to amp up their marketing efforts before the product launch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 팀은 제품 출시 전에 마케팅 노력을 강화하기로(증폭시키기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do the work that someone else has stopped doing or is unable to do; to improve the use of resources.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 부족한 부분을 채우다, 느슨한 곳을 죄다, 남의 몫까지 하다
meanings.example: When the manager left, the rest of the staff had to take up the slack.
meanings.exampleKorean: 관리자가 떠나자, 나머지 직원들이 그 부족한 부분(공백)을 채워야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a way that is inappropriate or that breaks the rules.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 규칙을 어기다, 정도를 벗어나다, 부적절하게 행동하다
meanings.example: If any students step out of line, they will be sent to the principal's office.
meanings.exampleKorean: 규칙을 어기는(정도를 벗어나는) 학생이 있다면, 교장실로 보내질 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone do what you want them to do.
meanings.example: He tried to bend the committee to his will, but they refused to change their decision.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 위원회를 자신의 뜻대로 굴복시키려(조종하려) 했지만, 그들은 결정을 바꾸기를 거부했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start a race or an activity; to score the first point in a game.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 순조롭게 출발하다, 시작하다, 첫 득점을 하다
meanings.example: The new business has finally got off the mark after a slow start.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 새로운 사업은 느린 시작 끝에 마침내 순조롭게 출발했다(시작했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend time doing small, unimportant things in a relaxed way.
meanings.example: On Sundays, I like to just putter about in the garden.
meanings.exampleKorean: 일요일에는 그냥 정원에서 어정버정하며(소일하며) 시간을 보내는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage to control a strong feeling or physical reaction.
meanings.example: She tried to fight down her rising panic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 치솟는 공포심을 억누르려(참아내려) 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get all your affairs or preparations perfectly organized (informal).
meanings.example: We need to get our ducks in a row before we meet with the investors.
meanings.exampleKorean: 투자자들을 만나기 전에 우리는 만반의 준비를 갖춰야(철저히 준비해야) 합니다.
synonyms: get one's ducks in a row

meanings.englishDefinition: To exclude or ignore someone in a cold or unfriendly way (informal).
meanings.example: After the argument, her friends started to ice her out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 말다툼 후에 그녀의 친구들은 그녀를 무시하기(따돌리기) 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone agree to conditions or to surrender.
meanings.example: The prolonged strike finally brought the management to terms.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장기화된 파업이 결국 경영진을 합의하게(굴복하게) 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To look for information using a computer or the internet.
meanings.example: I'll search up the restaurant's address on my phone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 휴대폰으로 그 식당 주소를 찾아볼게요(검색해 볼게요).

meanings.englishDefinition: To complete the final details of something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 마무리 손질을 하다, 완성하다, 종지부를 찍다
meanings.example: The artist is putting the last hand to his masterpiece.
meanings.exampleKorean: 예술가는 자신의 걸작에 마무리 손질을 하고(완성하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To read something from the beginning to the end, especially to look for mistakes.
meanings.example: I need to read through the contract once more before signing it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 서명하기 전에 계약서를 한 번 더 처음부터 끝까지 읽어봐야(통독해야) 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually destroy or use up something; to make someone feel worried or unhappy over a period of time.
meanings.example: Rust is eating away at the car's body. / Guilt began to eat away at him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 녹이 차체를 서서히 갉아먹고(부식시키고) 있다. / 죄책감이 그를 괴롭히기(마음을 갉아먹기) 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be familiar with something because you have experienced it many times.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~에 익숙하다, 잘 알다, ~을 경험해 본 적이 있다
meanings.example: She is no stranger to hard work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 힘든 일에 익숙하다 (힘든 일을 많이 겪어보았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To start liking someone or something, often without a clear reason.
meanings.example: He's recently taken a fancy to sailing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 최근에 항해하는 것에 호감을 느끼기(재미를 붙이기) 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To retire from a job or an activity that you have done for a long time.
meanings.example: After thirty years in the police force, it's finally time for him to hang up his spurs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰직에서 30년을 보낸 후, 마침내 그가 은퇴할(그만둘) 시간이 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try very hard to understand or solve something; to work extremely hard (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 머리를 쥐어짜다, 필사적으로 노력하다, 애를 쓰다
meanings.example: I've been beating my brains out trying to remember his name.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 이름을 기억해 내려고 머리를 쥐어짜고(필사적으로 노력하고) 있어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find or get something that you need.
meanings.example: I need that file, but I can't put my hands on it right now.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 파일이 필요한데, 지금 당장은 찾을 수가(손에 넣을 수가) 없네요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean something that has not been used for a long time so that it can be used again.
meanings.example: He dusted down his old tuxedo for the wedding.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 결혼식을 위해 오래된 턱시도를 다시 꺼내 먼지를 털었다 (준비했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To give information about how something is being done so that it can be improved.
meanings.example: We'd like you to feed back your thoughts on the new system.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 시스템에 대한 당신의 의견을 전달해(피드백을 주) 주셨으면 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pretend that something is true, especially as a joke.
meanings.example: Don't listen to him, he's just kidding you on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 말 듣지 마, 그냥 너한테 농담하는(속이는) 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tease or criticize someone constantly (informal).
meanings.example: My older brother is always ragging on me about my clothes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 형은 항상 내 옷차림을 가지고 나를 괴롭힌다(잔소리한다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To draw a line through a piece of writing to show that it is incorrect or not wanted.
meanings.example: The teacher scored out several unnecessary words in my essay.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님은 내 에세이에서 몇몇 불필요한 단어들을 줄을 그어 지우셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be caught in a trick or a difficult situation prepared by someone else.
meanings.example: He fell into a trap set by his competitors.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 경쟁자들이 쳐 놓은 함정에 빠졌다 (속임수에 넘어갔다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish dealing with something; to stop working on a problem or investigation.
meanings.example: The police have finally closed the book on the cold case.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 마침내 그 미제 사건을 종결지었다 (마무리했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something that makes someone annoyed or angry with you.
meanings.example: Try not to get on the manager's wrong side on your first day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 첫날부터 매니저의 미움을 사지(기분을 상하게 하지) 않도록 조심하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To show someone that they are not as important or clever as they think they are.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 콧대를 꺾어놓다, 길을 죽이다, 겸손하게 만들다
meanings.example: The unexpected loss really cut the champion down to size.
meanings.exampleKorean: 예상치 못한 패배는 챔피언의 콧대를 완전히 꺾어놓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do someone's job when they are suddenly unable to do it.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 대신 나서다, 위기를 수습하다, 빈자리를 채우다
meanings.example: When the lead actor fell ill, his understudy stepped into the breach.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주연 배우가 아플 때, 대역 배우가 대신 나서서(빈자리를 채워서) 역할을 수행했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become involved in a situation or activity very quickly and with great enthusiasm.
meanings.example: She jumped in with both feet and volunteered for the most difficult task.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 적극적으로 뛰어들어 가장 어려운 일에 자원했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop someone from going somewhere by getting in front of them; to prevent a problem from happening by taking action early.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 미리 가서 차단하다, 선수를 치다, (문제를) 방지하다
meanings.example: The company tried to head off the takeover bid at the pass.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 기업 인수 시도를 미리 가서 차단하려(방지하려) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be able to persuade someone to do anything you want.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 자기 마음대로 조종하다, 손안에 넣고 흔들다
meanings.example: She has her father twisted around her little finger.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 아빠를 자기 마음대로 조종한다 (손안에 넣고 흔든다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be born.
meanings.example: A new baby came into the world early this morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 이른 아침 새로운 아기가 세상에 태어났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sacrifice oneself for the benefit of others, especially by taking the blame or doing a difficult task.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 희생을 자처하다, (남을 위해) 비난을 대신 받다
meanings.example: He fell on a grenade for the team by admitting his mistake in public.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 공개적으로 자신의 실수를 인정함으로써 팀을 위해 희생을 자처했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop a vehicle next to someone or something; to approach or catch up with someone.
meanings.example: The police car pulled up on the suspect's vehicle.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰차가 용의자의 차량 옆에 바짝 다가가 차를 세웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept an insult without showing anger; to take money dishonestly.
meanings.example: He had to pocket up the insult to keep his job. / The corrupt official pocketed up the bribe.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 직장을 유지하기 위해 모욕을 꾹 참아야 했다. / 부패한 관리는 뇌물을 몰래 챙겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something quickly or with great intensity because you started late or missed an opportunity.
meanings.example: After a year of traveling, he's working hard to make up for lost time in his career.
meanings.exampleKorean: 1년간의 여행 끝에, 그는 자신의 경력에서 잃어버린 시간을 만회하기 위해 열심히 일하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly pay attention to something because it is surprising or important.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 정신을 바짝 차리고 주목하다, 관심을 기울이다
meanings.example: The company's innovative new product made the whole industry sit up and take notice.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사의 혁신적인 신제품은 업계 전체가 정신을 바짝 차리고 주목하게 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To happen as expected or intended.
meanings.example: The surprise party went to plan, and she was completely shocked.
meanings.exampleKorean: 깜짝 파티는 계획대로 되었고, 그녀는 완전히 놀랐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare to fight a war or to resist something.
meanings.example: The citizens took up arms to defend their city.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시민들은 자신들의 도시를 지키기 위해 무기를 들었다 (싸우러 나섰다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep something under control; to stop or end something.
meanings.example: The government is trying to put a lid on inflation. / Let's put a lid on this discussion and move on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 인플레이션을 억제하려 애쓰고 있다. / 이 논의는 이쯤에서 끝내고 다음으로 넘어갑시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay in one place without doing anything useful while expecting something to happen.
meanings.example: I'm tired of waiting around for the bus.
meanings.exampleKorean: 버스 오기를 빈둥거리며 기다리는(하릴없이 기다리는) 게 지겹네요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause someone to experience a difficult or unpleasant process.
meanings.example: The recruits were really put through the mill during basic training.
meanings.exampleKorean: 신병들은 기초 훈련 동안 정말 호된 시련을 겪었다 (고생했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To get information about someone or something.
meanings.example: I finally got a line on a good apartment for rent.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 마침내 괜찮은 임대 아파트에 대한 정보를 얻었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To expand or strengthen something; to make something more substantial.
meanings.example: The author decided to beef out the second chapter with more examples.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작가는 더 많은 예시를 넣어 두 번째 장을 보강하기로(내용을 늘리기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To write something that is not intended for publication or performance.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 발표할 목적 없이 글을 쓰다, 자신만을 위해 쓰다
meanings.example: For years, the poet wrote only for the drawer, never sharing his work with anyone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수년 동안 그 시인은 자신의 작품을 누구와도 공유하지 않고 오직 자신만을 위해 (발표할 목적 없이) 글을 썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move or run about quickly and energetically.
meanings.example: The children were cutting about in the park.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 공원에서 이리저리 활기차게 뛰어다니고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove people or things from a group because they are not suitable.
meanings.example: The program is designed to select out students who are likely to struggle.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 프로그램은 어려움을 겪을 가능성이 있는 학생들을 골라내도록(제외하도록) 설계되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep a business or organization operating at a basic level.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 사업을 유지하다, 운영을 계속하다, 현행을 유지하다
meanings.example: We need at least three new clients a month just to keep the lights on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 단지 사업을 유지하기(운영을 계속하기) 위해서만이라도 매달 최소 세 명의 새로운 고객이 필요합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To refuse to change your plans or ideas, especially when someone is trying to persuade you to do so.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 고집을 피우다, 완강히 버티다, 물러서지 않다
meanings.example: The opposition party has dug its heels in and refuses to negotiate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 야당은 완강히 버티며(물러서지 않으며) 협상을 거부하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To surprise someone, especially by doing something they did not expect.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 깜짝 놀라게 하다, 기습하다, 불시에 닥치다
meanings.example: The sudden rainstorm took everyone by surprise.
meanings.exampleKorean: 갑작스러운 폭풍우는 모든 사람을 깜짝 놀라게 했다 (불시에 닥쳤다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing something new in a very careful way to see if it will be successful.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 시험 삼아 해보다, 간을 보다, 조심스럽게 시작하다
meanings.example: She's decided to dip her toe into the world of investments with a small amount of money.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 소액으로 투자 세계를 시험 삼아 경험해 보기로(조심스럽게 시작해 보기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To work very hard at something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 온 힘을 다하다, 전력을 다하다, 열심히 노력하다
meanings.example: If we all put our shoulders into it, we can finish the project by Friday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 모두가 온 힘을 다한다면(열심히 노력한다면), 금요일까지 프로젝트를 끝낼 수 있어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone or something behave in the same way as others or follow the same rules.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 발맞추게 하다, 동의하게 하다, 순응하게 하다
meanings.example: The government is trying to bring local councils into line with national policy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 지방 의회들이 국가 정책에 발맞추게(순응하게) 하려 애쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To represent something truthfully, showing its faults or reality.
meanings.example: The documentary holds a mirror to the harsh realities of city life.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 다큐멘터리는 도시 생활의 가혹한 현실을 있는 그대로 보여준다 (반영한다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To blame someone for something, especially when they are not responsible.
meanings.example: They tried to pin the robbery on him, but he had an alibi.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 강도 사건을 그의 탓으로 돌리려(그에게 누명을 씌우려) 했지만, 그에게는 알리바이가 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To encourage and organize supporters to go and vote in an election.
meanings.example: Campaign volunteers are working hard to get out the vote before the polls close.
meanings.exampleKorean: 캠페인 자원봉사자들은 투표소가 닫히기 전에 투표를 독려하기 위해 열심히 노력하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a very aggressive or determined way, especially to attract someone or convince them.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 강하게 몰아붙이다, 적극적으로 대시하다, 공격적으로 굴다
meanings.example: I liked him at first, but he started coming on too strong.
meanings.exampleKorean: 처음엔 그가 좋았는데, 너무 적극적으로 대시해서(몰아붙여서) 부담스러워졌어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause something to deviate from its intended direction or plan.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 진로에서 이탈하게 하다, 계획을 어긋나게 하다
meanings.example: The sudden economic downturn blew our expansion plans off course.
meanings.exampleKorean: 갑작스러운 경제 침체가 우리의 확장 계획을 어긋나게(진로에서 이탈하게) 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use something so much that a hole appears in it.
meanings.example: The soles of my shoes have worn through.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 신발 밑창이 닳아서 구멍이 났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be a source of worry or pressure for someone because it is not yet resolved.
meanings.example: The threat of layoffs is hanging over everyone's head.
meanings.exampleKorean: 해고의 위협이 모든 사람의 마음을 불안하게 만들고(짓누르고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start a legal case against someone.
meanings.example: If you don't pay the debt, I'll be forced to take you to court.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빚을 갚지 않으면 당신을 고소할(법정에 세울) 수밖에 없어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To think about something for a long time before making a decision (informal).
meanings.example: That's an interesting proposal; let me marinate on it for a few days.
meanings.exampleKorean: 흥미로운 제안이네요. 며칠 동안 곰곰이 생각(심사숙고)해 볼게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop talking about or to ignore something because it is unpleasant or embarrassing.
meanings.example: Let's draw a veil over that unfortunate incident and focus on the future.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 불행한 사건은 그냥 덮어두고(비밀로 하고) 미래에 집중합시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To end something that has continued for a long time.
meanings.example: The legendary singer finally brought down the curtain on his fifty-year career.
meanings.exampleKorean: 전설적인 가수가 마침내 자신의 50년 경력에 막을 내렸다 (끝냈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To control someone or something strictly.
meanings.example: The government keeps a tight rein on public spending.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 공공 지출을 엄격히 통제하고(고삐를 죄고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have been friends or known each other for a very long time.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 오랜 인연이 있다, 오래전부터 알고 지내다
meanings.example: Jack and I go back a long way; we've been friends since primary school.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잭과 나는 오랜 인연이 있어요. 초등학교 때부터 친구였거든요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sit down and rest (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 자리에 앉아 쉬다, 다리를 쉬게 하다
meanings.example: Come in and take the weight off your feet for a few minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 들어오셔서 몇 분 동안이라도 자리에 앉아 좀 쉬세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use another person's success or a situation to help you achieve something.
meanings.example: Small companies often ride on the back of larger ones to gain market share.
meanings.exampleKorean: 소규모 회사들은 시장 점유율을 얻기 위해 종종 대기업에 편승한다(이용한다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To volunteer for something; to admit that you have done something wrong.
meanings.example: Who wants to put their hand up to lead the new project? / He was the only one to put his hand up and admit the mistake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 프로젝트를 이끌고 싶은 자원자(지원자) 있나요? / 그는 자신의 실수를 인정하고 나선 유일한 사람이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To progress or make advances; to start to do something as planned.
meanings.example: The project is moving ahead as scheduled.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프로젝트가 예정대로 진행되고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a very loud noise; to emit something with great force.
meanings.example: Rock music was blasting out from the speakers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스피커에서 록 음악이 큰 소리로 울려 퍼지고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To intentionally stop thinking about something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 잊어버리다, 염두에 두지 않다, 생각을 떨쳐내다
meanings.example: I tried to put the accident out of my mind and focus on work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 사고에 대한 생각을 떨쳐내고(잊어버리고) 업무에 집중하려 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause problems for someone in the future because of a mistake they made in the past.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 나중에 화가 되어 돌아오다, (과거의 실수가) 발목을 잡다
meanings.example: If you lie on your resume, it might come back to bite you later.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이력서에 거짓말을 하면, 나중에 그것이 화가 되어 돌아올(발목을 잡을) 수도 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To follow the established rules or to act fairly.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 규칙을 준수하다, 정직하게 행동하다, 공정하게 하다
meanings.example: In this competition, everyone is expected to play by the rules.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 경쟁에서는 모든 사람이 규칙을 준수할(공정하게 할) 것으로 기대됩니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To test an idea or a plan to see if it is suitable or effective.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 시험 삼아 해보다, 검토하다, (아이디어 등을) 떠보다
meanings.example: Here's a new strategy; let's try it on for size and see how it works.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 전략입니다. 이게 어떻게 돌아가는지 시험 삼아 한 번 해봅시다(검토해 봅시다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be determined to oppose or disapprove of something.
meanings.example: The local residents have set their faces against the new highway project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지역 주민들은 새로운 고속도로 프로젝트에 대해 단호히 반대하고 나섰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decrease or worsen very suddenly and severely.
meanings.example: Sales of the product fell off a cliff after the scandal was revealed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스캔들이 밝혀진 후 그 제품의 판매량은 급격히 떨어졌다 (곤두박질쳤다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To listen without giving your full attention.
meanings.example: I was watching TV, so I only listened to him with half an ear.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 TV를 보고 있었기에 그의 말을 그냥 건성으로 들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk or complain about the same thing repeatedly in a way that is annoying.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 같은 말을 되풀이하다, 같은 불만을 늘어놓다
meanings.example: Stop harping on the same string; we already know what happened.
meanings.exampleKorean: 똑같은 말 좀 그만 되풀이하세요. 무슨 일이 있었는지 이미 다 알고 있거든요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start working hard or to set about a task with determination.
meanings.example: After the break, we all buckled to and finished the work in an hour.
meanings.exampleKorean: 휴식 후에 우리 모두 본격적으로 매달려(열심히 시작해서) 한 시간 만에 일을 끝냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To see someone or something, especially for the first time (informal).
meanings.example: I haven't clapped eyes on him since we graduated from college.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대학을 졸업한 이후로 그를 처음 봤어요 (본 적이 없어요).

meanings.englishDefinition: To drive a vehicle backwards out of a space.
meanings.example: Be careful when you reverse out of the driveway.
meanings.exampleKorean: 진입로에서 후진해서 나올 때 조심하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find or think of something by chance.
meanings.example: I lit upon an old photograph while cleaning the attic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다락방을 청소하다가 우연히 오래된 사진 한 장을 발견했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To look at something personally to find out the truth about it.
meanings.example: If you don't believe me, go and see for yourself.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 말이 안 믿기면, 가서 직접 확인해(눈으로 직접 봐) 보세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To focus on the work that needs to be done and avoid irrelevant talk.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 본론으로 돌아가다, 업무에 집중하다, 사담을 삼가다
meanings.example: We have a lot to cover, so let's stick to business.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다룰 내용이 많으니, 이제 본론으로 돌아갑시다(업무에 집중합시다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To move someone away from a group in order to speak to them privately.
meanings.example: My teacher pulled me aside after class to discuss my grade.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님은 내 성적을 상의하기 위해 수업 후에 나를 따로 불러내셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To invite someone to come to your house.
meanings.example: We should ask the new neighbors over for dinner sometime.
meanings.exampleKorean: 언제 한번 새 이웃들을 저녁 식사에 초대해야(집으로 오라고 해야)겠어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To connect yourself with someone who is successful in order to become successful yourself.
meanings.example: He decided to hitch his wagon to the rising political star.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 떠오르는 정치 신예에게 자신의 운명을 맡기기로(편승하기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To buy more of a stock at a lower price than you originally paid, in order to lower the average cost per share.
meanings.example: The stock price dropped further, so he decided to average down his position.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주가가 더 떨어지자, 그는 매수 단가를 낮추기로(물타기를 하기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mark a date or an event as fixed or definite.
meanings.example: We've inked in the date for the wedding next June.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 내년 6월로 결혼식 날짜를 확정했다 (확실히 기입했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To set a price for something that is low enough to encourage people to buy it quickly.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 잘 팔리도록 가격을 낮게 책정하다, 매력적인 가격을 매기다
meanings.example: These used cars are priced to sell.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 중고차들은 잘 팔리도록 가격이 아주 낮게 책정되어 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To think only about your own interests and success.
meanings.example: In this competitive environment, everyone is just looking for number one.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 경쟁적인 환경에서, 모두가 그냥 자기 자신만을 생각하고(자기 이익만 챙기고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To record the time you arrive at work (especially in a factory).
meanings.example: What time do you usually clock on in the morning?
meanings.exampleKorean: 아침에 보통 몇 시에 출근 시간을 기록하나요(출근하나요)?

meanings.englishDefinition: To show a lot of affection and love to someone (informal).
meanings.example: The children were loving on the new puppy all afternoon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 오후 내내 새로운 강아지를 듬뿍 사랑해 주었다 (애정을 표현했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To disappear completely; to follow the current trend or situation.
meanings.example: All his promises went with the wind once he got what he wanted.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 원하던 것을 얻자마자 그의 모든 약속은 바람과 함께 사라졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To advance from the lowest level of an organization to a high position.
meanings.example: He started as a mailroom clerk and rose through the ranks to become CEO.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 우편물실 직원으로 시작하여 CEO가 될 때까지 밑바닥에서 차근차근 승진했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause a problem that stops a plan from succeeding.
meanings.example: The sudden change in the law threw a wrench in our expansion plans.
meanings.exampleKorean: 갑작스러운 법 개정이 우리의 확장 계획에 재를 뿌렸다 (방해했다).
synonyms: throw a spanner in the works

meanings.englishDefinition: To imagine yourself in someone else's situation to understand how they feel.
meanings.example: Before you criticize him, try walking in his shoes for a day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그를 비판하기 전에, 하루만이라도 그의 입장이 되어 생각해(역지사지해) 보세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reveal a secret, usually by accident.
meanings.example: I accidentally let the cat out about the surprise party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 실수로 깜짝 파티에 대한 비밀을 누설해 버렸다.
synonyms: let the cat out of the bag

meanings.englishDefinition: To ask someone to come towards you or to visit a place.
meanings.example: The teacher called the student over to her desk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님은 학생을 자신의 책상 쪽으로 오라고 부르셨다 (호출하셨다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To force someone or something to move backwards or go away.
meanings.example: The army managed to beat back the invaders. / Firefighters worked hard to beat back the flames.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군대는 침략자들을 격퇴하는 데 성공했다. / 소방관들은 불길을 잡기 위해(물러나게 하려고) 열심히 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a way that is intended to please someone, especially to get an advantage.
meanings.example: He's always playing up to the boss to get a promotion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 승진을 하기 위해 항상 상사에게 아첨하고(비위를 맞추고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To forget or ignore something (like feelings or ego) before entering a place or starting a task.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 문 앞에 두고 오다, (감정 등을) 버리고 오다, 잊어버리다
meanings.example: You need to leave your personal problems at the door when you come to work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사에 출근할 때는 개인적인 문제들은 문 앞에 두고 와야(버리고 와야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone work extremely hard or to give them a very difficult burden.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 몹시 힘들게 하다, 등골을 휘게 하다, 기력을 소진시키다
meanings.example: The heavy taxes are breaking the backs of small business owners.
meanings.exampleKorean: 과중한 세금이 소상공인들의 등골을 휘게 하고(몹시 힘들게 하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be officially discharged from military service.
meanings.example: He was mustered out after the war ended.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 전쟁이 끝난 후 제대했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be destroyed or removed by fire or heat; to disappear gradually.
meanings.example: The logs slowly burned away in the fireplace. / The morning mist soon burned away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장작들이 벽난로 안에서 천천히 타서 없어졌다. / 아침 안개가 곧 서서히 사라졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wake up and get out of bed at an early hour.
meanings.example: I have to get up early tomorrow for my flight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비행기 때문에 내일 일찍 일어나야(새벽같이 일어나야) 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to go faster; to increase the speed at which something is being done.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 속도를 올리다, 더 빨리 하다, 보조를 당기다
meanings.example: We need to pick up the pace if we want to finish the project on time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 제시간에 프로젝트를 끝내고 싶다면 우리는 속도를 더 올려야(더 빨리 해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take action to protect yourself against future criticism or responsibility.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 만일의 사태에 대비하다, 자신을 보호하다, 책임을 면할 길을 마련하다
meanings.example: I'm keeping all these emails just to cover my back in case anything goes wrong.
meanings.exampleKorean: 무슨 일이 잘못될 경우를 대비해 나를 보호하려고(만일의 사태에 대비해) 이 이메일들을 모두 보관하고 있어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To say your thoughts as they occur to you, rather than keeping them to yourself.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 생각을 입 밖으로 내어 말하다, 중얼거리다
meanings.example: Sorry, I wasn't talking to you; I was just thinking out loud.
meanings.exampleKorean: 미안해요, 당신한테 말한 게 아니라 그냥 생각을 입 밖으로 내어 말하고(중얼거리고) 있었어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone realize that they are not as important or good as they think they are.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 콧대를 꺾어놓다, 길을 죽이다, 겸손하게 만들다
meanings.example: The coach's criticism took the star player down a notch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코치의 비판은 스타 선수의 콧대를 꺾어놓았다 (겸손하게 만들었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To act as a guide for someone who is visiting a place for the first time.
meanings.example: Welcome to our office! Let me show you around.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 사무실에 오신 것을 환영합니다! 제가 구경시켜(안내해) 드릴게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To read something quickly or carefully from beginning to end to check for mistakes.
meanings.example: Could you read over my application and tell me what you think?
meanings.exampleKorean: 제 지원서를 한 번 훑어보고(꼼꼼히 읽어보고) 어떻게 생각하시는지 말씀해 주시겠어요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail to show or express your feelings, especially anger or grief.
meanings.example: It's not healthy to cork up your emotions all the time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 감정을 항상 억누르고(참고) 있는 것은 건강에 좋지 않습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To choose someone or something randomly, often to be fair.
meanings.example: The winner of the contest was picked from a hat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대회의 우승자는 제비뽑기로(무작위로) 결정되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To close something tightly so that nothing can get in or out.
meanings.example: The workers sealed up the cracks in the wall.
meanings.exampleKorean: 인부들이 벽의 틈새를 완전히 막았다(봉인했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To take a moment to notice and evaluate your own feelings and well-being.
meanings.example: It's important to check in with yourself every day to manage stress.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스트레스를 관리하기 위해 매일 자신의 감정을 살피는(스스로를 돌아보는) 것이 중요합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put food onto plates ready to be served.
meanings.example: The chef began to plate up the main courses.
meanings.exampleKorean: 요리사가 메인 요리들을 접시에 담기(플레이팅하기) 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay a debt; to get revenge on someone for a past wrong.
meanings.example: I have an old score to settle with him. / He finally settled accounts with his former business partner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그와 결판을 내야 할(복수해야 할) 오랜 원한이 있다. / 그는 마침내 전 동업자와의 관계를 청산했다 (결판을 냈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To consider something to be very important or valuable.
meanings.example: Modern employers put a premium on creativity and problem-solving skills.
meanings.exampleKorean: 현대의 고용주들은 창의성과 문제 해결 능력을 중요시한다 (가치를 둔다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To become calm and sensible after being upset or confused.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 마음을 가다듬다, 정신 차리다, 평정심을 되찾다
meanings.example: Give me a minute to get myself together before the interview.
meanings.exampleKorean: 면접 전에 마음을 가다듬을(정신 차릴) 시간을 조금만 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To express a strong emotion, such as anger or frustration, in a loud or forceful way.
meanings.example: He finally gave vent to his frustration and shouted at his boss.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 결국 불만을 터뜨리며(쏟아내며) 상사에게 소리를 질렀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop discussing or dealing with something for a while, with the intention of returning to it later (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 나중에 다시 다루다, 보류하다, 이쯤에서 멈추다
meanings.example: Let's put a pin in that idea for now and move on to the next topic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 아이디어는 나중에 다시 다루기로 하고(보류하고) 다음 주제로 넘어갑시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become more successful, wealthier, or have a higher social status.
meanings.example: She's really moving up in the world since she got that promotion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 승진한 이후로 정말 출세하고(성공하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deal with something or to find out information about something.
meanings.example: I'll see about getting us some tickets for the show.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공연 티켓을 구할 수 있는지 제가 알아볼게요(처리할게요).

meanings.englishDefinition: To feel nervous or anxious that someone might harm you or that you might lose your job or position.
meanings.example: Ever since the layoffs began, everyone has been looking over their shoulder.
meanings.exampleKorean: 해고가 시작된 이후로 모든 사람이 노심초사하며 경계하고(뒤를 돌아보고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a happy ending; to retire gracefully after a successful career.
meanings.example: After winning the championship, the legendary coach decided to ride off into the sunset.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우승을 차지한 후, 그 전설적인 코치는 명예롭게 은퇴하기로(행복한 결말을 맞기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To show deep respect and admiration for someone or something.
meanings.example: The film pays homage to the classic movies of the 1940s.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 영화는 1940년대의 고전 영화들에 경의를 표한다 (오마주한다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To sit down heavily and suddenly because you are tired or relaxed.
meanings.example: He plopped down on the sofa and turned on the TV.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 소파에 털썩 주저앉아 TV를 켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat someone harshly or to oppose someone in authority (informal).
meanings.example: The fans really stuck it to the referee after the controversial call.
meanings.exampleKorean: 팬들은 논란이 된 판정 이후 심판을 정말 따끔하게 혼내주었다 (공격했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To study a secondary subject as a part of a university degree.
meanings.example: I'm majoring in history but minoring in Spanish.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 역사를 전공하고 있지만 스페인어를 부전공하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent someone from doing something or to prevent something from happening.
meanings.example: I won't let anything stand in the way of my dreams.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 꿈을 가로막는(방해가 되는) 그 어떤 것도 용납하지 않겠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly start doing something or working very hard.
meanings.example: As soon as the alarm went off, the firefighters sprang into action.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경보가 울리자마자 소방관들이 즉시 행동을 시작했다 (기민하게 대처했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be discovering or thinking of something that is important and useful.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 중요한 것을 알아내다, 실마리를 찾다, 뭔가 아는 게 있다
meanings.example: The scientist believed she was onto something big with her new research.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 과학자는 자신의 새로운 연구로 뭔가 중요한 것을 알아내고(큰 실마리를 찾고) 있다고 믿었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rely on someone or something to give you the result that you want.
meanings.example: The team is pinning its hopes on the new star player.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 팀은 새로운 스타 선수에게 희망을(기대를) 걸고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force yourself to be brave enough to do something.
meanings.example: I finally plucked up my courage and asked her for a date.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 마침내 용기를 내어 그녀에게 데이트 신청을 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause a person's life to change completely, usually in a disturbing way.
meanings.example: The war turned their lives upside down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 전쟁은 그들의 삶을 완전히 뒤바꾸어 놓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reveal a secret or a plan, often by accident.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 비밀을 누설하다, 정체를 드러내다, 속셈을 들키다
meanings.example: He tried to look serious, but his smile gave the game away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 심각해 보이려 애썼지만, 미소 때문에 속셈을 들키고(정체를 드러내고) 말았다.
synonyms: let the cat out of the bag

meanings.englishDefinition: To believe that a situation or someone's words have a particular meaning, even though they may not.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 지나치게 의미를 부여하다, 확대 해석하다, (의도를) 읽으려 하다
meanings.example: Don't read too much into his silence; he's just tired.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 침묵에 너무 많은 의미를 부여하지(확대 해석하지) 마세요. 그냥 피곤해서 그런 거예요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To experience a significant positive transformation in appearance or style (slang).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 완전히 예뻐지다, 멋져지다, (외모 등이) 급격히 좋아지다
meanings.example: She had a major glow up over the summer break.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 여름 방학 동안 완전히 예뻐졌다 (멋진 변신을 했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To blame someone for something.
meanings.example: The failure of the project cannot be laid at his door alone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프로젝트의 실패를 그의 책임으로만 돌릴(그의 탓으로만 할) 수는 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue walking or moving forward; (of time) to continue to pass.
meanings.example: The soldiers marched on despite the rain. / Time marches on for everyone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군인들은 비에도 불구하고 계속 행진했다. / 시간은 모든 사람에게 계속 흐른다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consider something to be the most important thing in your life.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~을 위해 살다, 삶의 낙으로 삼다, 갈망하다
meanings.example: She lives for her children. / Some people live for the weekend.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 자식들을 위해 산다. / 어떤 사람들은 주말을 삶의 낙으로 삼고(주말만 기다리며) 산다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage to live or do something with the limited amount of something you have.
meanings.example: We can get by with just a small car for now.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 지금으로선 작은 차 한 대만으로도 그럭저럭 살아갈(견딜) 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To look at a number of things to find something or to organize them.
meanings.example: I need to sort through these old photos and decide which ones to keep.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 오래된 사진들을 자세히 살펴보고(분류해서) 어떤 것을 보관할지 결정해야 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stretch out your arm to get something; to try hard to achieve a difficult goal.
meanings.example: She reached for the top shelf. / We should always reach for the stars.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 맨 위 선반을 향해 손을 뻗었다. / 우리는 항상 높은 목표를 향해 노력해야(꿈을 크게 가져야) 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish moving or doing something.
meanings.example: The train slowly came to a stop at the station.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기차가 역에서 천천히 멈췄다 (정지했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To show admiration or respect for someone's achievement.
meanings.example: I doff my hat to anyone who can manage to work and study at the same time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 일과 학업을 동시에 해내는 모든 분께 경의를 표합니다 (찬사를 보냅니다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent a process or system from working properly (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 일을 엉망으로 만들다, 방해하다, 지장을 주다
meanings.example: His constant interference really gummed up the works.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 끊임없는 간섭이 일을 정말 엉망으로 만들었다 (지장을 주었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very few in number, so that they can be easily counted.
meanings.example: I can count the number of times I've been to the cinema this year on one hand.
meanings.exampleKorean: 올해 영화관에 간 횟수는 한 손으로 꼽을 정도로 적다 (아주 드물다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To state your opinion publicly or officially so that it is recorded.
meanings.example: The senator has gone on record as opposing the new tax law.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 상원의원은 새로운 세법에 반대한다는 입장을 공식적으로 밝혔다 (공식 발표했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To give up on something or stop trying because you think you cannot succeed.
meanings.example: After months of struggling, the company was forced to throw in its hand.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몇 달간의 고군분투 끝에 그 회사는 결국 포기할(손을 뗄) 수밖에 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deal with the most basic and fundamental facts or principles of a situation.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 본질을 파헤치다, 근본 원인을 찾다, 핵심을 찌르다
meanings.example: We need to get down to bedrock and find out why this project is failing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 본질을 파헤쳐서(근본 원인을 찾아서) 왜 이 프로젝트가 실패하고 있는지 알아내야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a strong feeling of dislike or lack of respect for someone or something.
meanings.example: He holds the new regulations in utter contempt.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 새로운 규정들을 완전히 경멸하고(무시하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a very relaxed and uninhibited way, showing all your feelings (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 속내를 다 털어놓다, 있는 그대로 드러내다, 자유롭게 행동하다
meanings.example: At the party, everyone just let it all hang out and had a great time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 파티에서 모두가 있는 그대로의 모습을 드러내며(자유롭게 행동하며) 즐거운 시간을 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly start talking or thinking about something that is not directly connected with what you were saying or thinking before.
meanings.example: He's hard to follow because he's always going off on a tangent during meetings.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 회의 중에 항상 이야기가 옆길로 새서 따라가기 힘들어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing something with a lot of enthusiasm; to suddenly experience a bad situation.
meanings.example: She plunged into her work to forget her troubles. / The country was plunged into a financial crisis.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 고민을 잊기 위해 업무에 뛰어들었다 (매달렸다). / 그 나라는 금융 위기에 빠졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone gradually stop using something that is bad for them or that they are dependent on.
meanings.example: The doctor is trying to wean the patient off the painkillers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의사는 환자가 진통제를 서서히 끊게 하려 애쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have been friends or known each other for a very long time.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 오랜 인연이 있다, 오래전부터 알고 지내다
meanings.example: Tom and I go way back; we were in kindergarten together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 톰과 나는 오랜 인연이 있어요. 유치원 때부터 같이 지냈거든요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause a situation or problem to reach a point where it must be dealt with immediately.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 곪아 터지게 하다, 위기 상황으로 몰고 가다, 결판을 내게 하다
meanings.example: The sudden increase in oil prices brought the economic crisis to a head.
meanings.exampleKorean: 유가의 갑작스러운 상승은 경제 위기를 곪아 터지게(결정적인 국면에 이르게) 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay inside a building during an emergency because it is safer than going outside.
meanings.example: During the chemical spill, residents were told to shelter in place.
meanings.exampleKorean: 화학 물질 유출 사고 동안 주민들은 현 위치에서 대피하라는(실내 대피하라는) 지시를 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To survive a dangerous experience.
meanings.example: The mountain climber was lucky to live to tell the tale after the avalanche.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 등산가는 눈사태 이후에 무사히 살아남아(사선을 넘나들어) 그 이야기를 들려줄 수 있어 다행이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept something as true without seeing any proof.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 무조건 믿다, 신용하고 받아들이다, 그대로 믿다
meanings.example: I took his story on trust, only to find out later he was lying.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그의 이야기를 무조건 믿었지만, 나중에야 그가 거짓말을 했다는 것을 알게 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: (of liquid) To be gradually absorbed into the ground or a surface.
meanings.example: The rainwater will soon soak away into the sandy soil.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빗물은 곧 모래 토양 속으로 서서히 스며들어 사라질 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To confront something or someone directly and honestly.
meanings.example: You need to look your problems in the face if you want to solve them.
meanings.exampleKorean: 문제를 해결하고 싶다면 그 문제들을 직시해야(똑바로 마주해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To visit someone for a short time, especially on your way to another place.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 잠시 들르다, 얼굴을 비추다, 안부를 확인하다
meanings.example: I'll look in on my grandmother on my way home.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집에 가는 길에 할머니 댁에 잠시 들를게요(안부를 확인할게요).

meanings.englishDefinition: To spill some of a drink on the ground in memory of someone who has died (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 추모하며 술을 따르다, 술을 부어 경의를 표하다
meanings.example: We poured one out for our fallen comrade.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 먼저 떠난 동료를 추모하며 술을 따랐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start something again after an interruption, especially a relationship or a conversation.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 중단했던 일을 다시 시작하다, (관계 등을) 다시 이어가다
meanings.example: After a ten-year separation, they found it easy to pick up the threads of their friendship.
meanings.exampleKorean: 10년간의 이별 후에도, 그들은 우정의 끈을 다시 이어가는(친구 관계를 다시 시작하는) 것이 쉽다는 것을 알게 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be honest with someone, especially about something that has been kept secret.
meanings.example: I'll level with you; the situation is much worse than we expected.
meanings.exampleKorean: 솔직하게 털어놓을게요. 상황이 우리가 예상했던 것보다 훨씬 더 나쁩니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pause and think about a situation objectively, rather than being too involved in the details.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 한 걸음 물러나 생각하다, 객관적으로 돌아보다
meanings.example: We need to take a step back and look at the big picture.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 한 걸음 물러나서 전체적인 상황(큰 그림)을 볼 필요가 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To trust your instincts or feelings when making a decision.
meanings.example: When I'm not sure what to do, I usually just go with my gut.
meanings.exampleKorean: 무엇을 해야 할지 확신이 서지 않을 때, 나는 보통 그냥 내 직감을 따른다 (본능에 맡긴다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To start discussing the most important or basic facts of a situation.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 본론으로 들어가다, 핵심을 짚다, 구체적인 논의를 시작하다
meanings.example: Let's stop talking about general ideas and get down to brass tacks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 일반적인 이야기는 그만두고, 이제 본론으로 들어갑시다 (핵심을 짚어봅시다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To tell someone bad news in a careful and gentle way.
meanings.example: I hate to be the one to break it to you, but the trip has been cancelled.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이런 소식을 전하게 되어 유감이지만, 여행이 취소되었습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To build on the work or discoveries of those who came before.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 선구자의 업적을 빌리다, 거인의 어깨 위에 서다
meanings.example: Modern science stands on the shoulders of giants like Newton and Einstein.
meanings.exampleKorean: 현대 과학은 뉴턴과 아인슈타인 같은 거인들의 어깨 위에 서 있다 (선구자들의 업적을 바탕으로 한다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail unexpectedly and cause problems for the person who planned it.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 완전히 어긋나다, (계획 등이) 실패하여 화를 입다
meanings.example: His attempt to hide the truth blew up in his face.
meanings.exampleKorean: 진실을 숨기려던 그의 시도는 완전히 어긋나 그에게 화가 되어 돌아왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail to work as hard or as well as you should.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 일을 소홀히 하다, 게으름을 피우다, 태만히 하다
meanings.example: The manager warned him not to lie down on the job during the busy season.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매니저는 그에게 바쁜 시즌 동안 일을 소홀히 하지(게으름 피우지) 말라고 경고했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be working the day shift.
meanings.example: I'm on days this week, so I can meet you for dinner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이번 주는 주간 근무를 해서 저녁에 만날 수 있어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be involved in an activity without playing an active role, just for fun or interest.
meanings.example: I'm not part of the organizing committee; I've just gone along for the ride.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저는 조직 위원회 소속이 아니에요. 그냥 재미 삼아 따라온(동참한) 것뿐입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remember the name of someone or something you recognize.
meanings.example: I recognize her face, but I can't put a name to it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 얼굴은 기억나는데 이름이 기억나지 않네요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To change your life so that it becomes positive and successful after a period of failure or bad behavior.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 인생을 반전시키다, 새 삶을 살다, 갱생하다
meanings.example: With the help of his mentor, he managed to turn his life around.
meanings.exampleKorean: 멘토의 도움으로 그는 인생을 반전시키는(새 삶을 사는) 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reach a point where you cannot continue with a plan or activity because of a major problem.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 난관에 부딪히다, 벽에 가로막히다, 진척이 없다
meanings.example: Our research project has run into a brick wall because of a lack of funding.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리의 연구 프로젝트는 자금 부족으로 인해 난관에 부딪혔다 (벽에 가로막혔다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk to someone who will not listen or respond.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 벽 보고 이야기하다, 소귀에 경 읽기다, 대화가 안 통하다
meanings.example: Trying to convince him is like talking to a brick wall.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그를 설득하려는 것은 벽 보고 이야기하는 것(소귀에 경 읽기)과 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue on a journey or with an activity, especially in a relaxed or unconcerned way.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 즐겁게 제 갈 길을 가다, 계속 가던 길을 가다
meanings.example: He just smiled and went on his merry way.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그냥 미소를 짓고는 즐겁게 제 갈 길을 갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To barely succeed in passing a test, election, or difficult situation.
meanings.example: I managed to squeak through the final exam.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 간신히 기말고사를 통과했다 (아슬아슬하게 합격했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To send a direct message to someone on social media, especially to start a romantic relationship (informal).
meanings.example: I'm thinking about sliding into her DMs after seeing her latest post.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 최신 포스트를 보고 나서 그녀에게 DM을 보낼까(접근해 볼까) 생각 중이야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hope that a particular person or team will win.
meanings.example: Which team are you pulling for in the World Cup?
meanings.exampleKorean: 월드컵에서 어느 팀을 응원하고 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To finally understand something or make someone understand something (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 이해시키다, 머리에 박히게 하다, 알아듣게 하다
meanings.example: I finally got it through his thick skull that he needs to study harder.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 마침내 그에게 공부를 더 열심히 해야 한다는 것을 기어이 이해시켰다 (머리에 박히게 했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place or vehicle quickly and in large numbers.
meanings.example: As soon as the bell rang, the students piled out of the classroom.
meanings.exampleKorean: 종이 울리자마자 학생들이 교실에서 우르르(한꺼번에 쏟아져) 나왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To defeat someone decisively; to criticize or embarrass someone publicly (informal).
meanings.example: He completely dunked on his opponent during the debate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 토론 중에 상대방을 완전히 압도했다 (망신을 주었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be out of stock; to sell out of a particular item.
meanings.example: The popular sneakers stocked out within an hour of being released.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 인기 있는 운동화는 출시된 지 한 시간 만에 품절되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To malfunction or break down (informal).
meanings.example: My computer started to fritz up after the power surge.
meanings.exampleKorean: 과전압 이후에 내 컴퓨터가 오작동하기(고장 나기) 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To state the reasons why something should be done or used.
meanings.example: The report makes a strong case for increasing the education budget.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 보고서는 교육 예산 증액의 정당성을 강력하게 입증하고(주장하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To survive a difficult situation so that you can try again in the future.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 훗날을 기약하며 살아남다, 다음 기회를 위해 물러나다
meanings.example: We lost this game, but we'll live to fight another day in the tournament.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 이번 경기에서 졌지만, 토너먼트에서 훗날을 기약하며 살아남을(다음 기회를 노릴) 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To help someone feel more lively and think more clearly, especially by going outside or doing some exercise.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 기분 전환을 하다, 머리를 식히다, 정신을 맑게 하다
meanings.example: A brisk walk in the park should help blow the cobwebs away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공원에서의 활기찬 산책은 기분 전환을 하는(정신을 맑게 하는) 데 도움이 될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a moral judgment on someone else's behavior, especially when you have no right to do so.
meanings.example: It's not my place to sit in judgement on your personal life.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신의 사생활을 제가 멋대로 판단할(심판하려 들) 자리는 아닙니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue doing what you normally do during the day.
meanings.example: Despite the news, I tried to just go about my day as usual.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 소식에도 불구하고, 나는 평소처럼 하루를 보내려(일상을 계속하려) 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To die while still working or active in your profession, rather than after retiring.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 죽을 때까지 일하다, 업무 중에 죽다
meanings.example: The famous professor always said he wanted to die in harness.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 유명한 교수는 항상 죽을 때까지 일하고 싶다고 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter a place or vehicle quickly and in large numbers; to join an activity with many other people.
meanings.example: All the kids piled into the back of the car. / Investors are piling in to buy the company's stock.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 아이가 자동차 뒷좌석에 우르르 올라탔다. / 투자자들이 그 회사의 주식을 사기 위해 몰려들고(합세하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To state your opinion or show your support for something publicly, even if it is difficult or dangerous.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 자신의 소신을 밝히다, 지지 의사를 표명하다
meanings.example: It's time for those who support the change to stand up and be counted.
meanings.exampleKorean: 변화를 지지하는 사람들이 이제는 자신의 소신을 밝히고(지지 의사를 표명하고) 행동에 나서야 할 때입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend too much time thinking about things that happened in the past and not focus on the present.
meanings.example: You can't move forward if you keep living in the past.
meanings.exampleKorean: 계속 과거에 집착하며(과거에 살며) 살면 앞으로 나아갈 수 없어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To greet someone by bumping fists or palms together (slang).
meanings.example: He dapped up his teammates before the game started.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 경기 시작 전에 팀원들과 주먹을 부딪치며 인사했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a long, casual conversation with someone (slang).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 대화를 나누다, 담소를 즐기다, 수다 떨다
meanings.example: We stayed after school to chop it up for a while.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 한동안 대화를 나누기(담소를 즐기기) 위해 방과 후에 남았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bring up or train someone or an animal.
meanings.example: The family has bred up several generations of champion horses.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 가문은 여러 세대에 걸쳐 챔피언 말들을 키워냈다(양육했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To give someone information about something that you have been asked to find out or do.
meanings.example: Please research the market and report back to me by Friday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시장을 조사해서 금요일까지 저에게 보고해(결과를 알려) 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something more interesting, exciting, or realistic.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 생기를 불어넣다, 활기를 주다, 실감 나게 하다
meanings.example: The actor's performance really brought the historical character to life.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 배우의 연기는 역사 속 인물에 정말 생기를 불어넣었다 (실감 나게 했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To look for something or try to find out information about someone, especially in a secret or sneaky way.
meanings.example: I saw a stranger nosing around the neighborhood yesterday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어제 한 낯선 사람이 동네 주변을 기웃거리는(염탐하는) 것을 보았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cry for a long time until you feel better; to let a baby cry until it falls asleep.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 실컷 울다, 울다 잠들게 하다, 울어서 감정을 풀다
meanings.example: Sometimes you just need to cry it out to feel better.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가끔은 기분이 나아지기 위해 그냥 실컷 울(울어서 감정을 풀) 필요가 있어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To write a computer program to implement an idea or a system.
meanings.example: I spent all night coding up the new feature for the app.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 밤새도록 앱의 새로운 기능을 코딩했다 (프로그램을 짰다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place like a restaurant or shop without paying for the goods or services provided.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 무전취식하다, 돈 안 내고 튀다, 값을 치르지 않고 도망가다
meanings.example: A group of teenagers tried to make off without payment from the café.
meanings.exampleKorean: 한 무리의 십 대들이 카페에서 돈을 내지 않고 튀려(무전취식하려) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bring in extra people to help with a task or to join a team.
meanings.example: More security guards were drafted in to control the crowd.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군중을 통제하기 위해 더 많은 보안 요원들이 투입되었다 (동원되었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To quickly put something into a usable or organized state, even if it is not perfect.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 대강 모양을 잡다, 다듬다, 틀을 잡다
meanings.example: I need to bash this report into shape before the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의 전에 이 보고서의 대강의 틀을 잡아야(모양을 다듬어야) 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something look old or dirty, especially for a theatrical production.
meanings.example: The costumes were dirtied down to look like they had been worn in a war.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의상들은 마치 전쟁터에서 입었던 것처럼 보이게 하려고 일부러 낡게(더럽게) 만들어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To record the starting time of an activity or work.
meanings.example: Don't forget to time in when you arrive at the office.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사무실에 도착하면 출근 시간을 기록하는 것을 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To supply missing information; to understand the details of a situation that were not explicitly stated.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 여백을 채우다, (빠진 정보를) 알아내다, 상황을 파악하다
meanings.example: He didn't tell me the whole story, but I was able to fill in the blanks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 전체 이야기를 다 해주지는 않았지만, 나는 빠진 정보들을 알아낼(상황을 파악할) 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be careful because someone may try to harm or betray you.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 등 뒤를 조심하다, 경계하다, 스스로를 보호하다
meanings.example: In this competitive business world, you always have to watch your back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 경쟁적인 비즈니스 세계에서는 항상 등 뒤를 조심해야(경계해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start breathing normally again after exercise or a shock.
meanings.example: Wait a minute, I need to get my breath back after that run.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잠시만요, 달리기하고 나서 숨을 좀 골라야(호흡을 가다듬어야)겠어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deal directly with someone instead of using an agent or distributor.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 중간 상인을 배제하다, 직거래하다, 중개인 없이 하다
meanings.example: By selling online, the company was able to cut out the middleman.
meanings.exampleKorean: 온라인 판매를 통해 회사는 중간 상인을 배제할(직거래할) 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To press the brakes of a vehicle very hard and suddenly; to stop an activity suddenly.
meanings.example: He had to slam on the brakes to avoid hitting the deer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 사슴을 치지 않으려고 급브레이크를 밟아야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be less famous or successful than someone you are closely associated with.
meanings.example: He spent most of his life living in the shadow of his famous father.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 평생의 대부분을 유명한 아버지의 그늘에 가려 살았다 (주목받지 못하고 지냈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To mix things together in a confused or untidy way.
meanings.example: The papers on my desk were all jumbled up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 책상 위의 서류들이 모두 뒤섞여 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move your body to one side so that there is room for someone else.
meanings.example: Could you shove over a bit so I can sit down?
meanings.exampleKorean: 제가 앉을 수 있게 옆으로 좀 밀어(비켜) 주시겠어요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To have an advantage because you know someone influential in a group or organization.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 연줄이 있다, 친분이 있다, 유리한 입장에 있다
meanings.example: He has an in with the local government, so he knows about the new projects first.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 지방 정부와 연줄이 있어서 새로운 프로젝트에 대해 가장 먼저 안다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very close to achieving something or reaching a certain level.
meanings.example: The company is now knocking on the door of becoming a global leader.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 이제 세계적인 리더가 되는 문을 두드리고(거의 도달하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take full advantage of a great opportunity, especially to buy a lot of something (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 기회를 잡다, 대량 매수하다, 큰 이득을 취하다
meanings.example: When the stock price dropped, smart investors backed up the truck.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주가가 떨어졌을 때, 영리한 투자자들은 기회를 놓치지 않고 대량 매수했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To not turn off a light, machine, etc.; to not take off a piece of clothing.
meanings.example: Don't leave the lights on when you leave the room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 방을 나갈 때 전등을 켜두지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have your own opinions and make your own decisions without being influenced by others.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 스스로 생각하다, 주체적으로 판단하다, 독자적으로 사고하다
meanings.example: Parents should encourage their children to think for themselves.
meanings.exampleKorean: 부모는 자녀들이 스스로 생각할(주체적으로 판단할) 수 있도록 격려해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To watch something bad happening without doing anything to stop it.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 방관하다, 수수방관하다, 아무것도 하지 않고 지켜보다
meanings.example: We cannot just sit idly by while our environment is being destroyed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리의 환경이 파괴되는 동안 그냥 방관하고(수수방관하고) 있을 수는 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To end or finish in a very successful, impressive, or exciting way.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 화려하게 마무리하다, 대미를 장식하다, 떠들썩하게 끝나다
meanings.example: The show went out with a bang in its final season.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 쇼는 마지막 시즌에서 화려하게 마무리했다 (대미를 장식했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To give up your rights or property by signing a legal document.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 권리 등을 포기하다, 양도하다, 서명하여 넘기다
meanings.example: She signed away her inheritance to help her brother.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 남동생을 돕기 위해 자신의 상속권을 포기했다 (양도했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To get some rest before doing something active or difficult.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 충분히 쉬다, 기력을 회복하다, 푹 쉬다
meanings.example: You should rest up tonight for tomorrow's long journey.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내일의 긴 여정을 위해 오늘 밤은 충분히 쉬어(푹 쉬어) 두어야 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrive or enter a situation with great speed, energy, or aggression (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 기세 좋게 등장하다, 빠르게 접근하다, 공격적으로 나오다
meanings.example: The negotiator came in hot, demanding immediate answers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 협상가는 즉각적인 답변을 요구하며 기세 좋게 등장했다 (공격적으로 나왔다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay awake all night to take care of someone who is ill or in trouble.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 밤새 곁을 지키다, 밤샘 간호를 하다
meanings.example: She sat up all night with her sick child.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 아픈 아이의 곁을 밤새도록 지켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take an examination; to pose for a portrait.
meanings.example: He's sitting for his bar exam next week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 다음 주에 변호사 시험을 치른다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join with someone and share their success or failure.
meanings.example: He decided to throw his luck in with the new startup company.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 새로운 스타트업 회사와 운명을 같이하기로(그 회사에 미래를 걸기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be close to death.
meanings.example: After the accident, he was knocking on heaven's door for several days.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사고 이후 그는 며칠 동안 죽음의 문턱에 이르러(사경을 헤매고) 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become completely soaked with a liquid.
meanings.example: My shoes wetted out after walking in the heavy rain for an hour.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭우 속을 한 시간 동안 걸었더니 신발이 완전히 젖어버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something quickly and all at once, especially something painful or unpleasant, to get it over with.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 매를 먼저 맞다, 단숨에 처리하다, 고통스러운 일을 빨리 끝내다
meanings.example: Let's just rip off the band-aid and tell him the bad news now.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그냥 매를 먼저 맞는 셈 치고(고통스러운 일을 빨리 끝내기 위해) 지금 그에게 나쁜 소식을 전합시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cooperate with members of an opposing political party or a rival group.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 초당적으로 협력하다, 반대편과 손을 잡다, 타협하다
meanings.example: The senator is known for his ability to reach across the aisle to get bills passed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 상원의원은 법안 통과를 위해 반대 당과 손을 잡는(초당적으로 협력하는) 능력으로 알려져 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To successfully complete a task or achieve a goal, especially one that is difficult.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 결승선을 넘다, 어렵게 목표를 달성하다, 승인을 받아내다
meanings.example: We had some setbacks, but we finally managed to get the deal over the line.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몇 가지 차질이 있었지만, 우리는 마침내 그 계약을 어렵게 성사시켰다 (목표를 달성했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone feel extremely cold; to frighten someone severely.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 뼛속까지 춥게 만들다, 소름 끼치게 하다, 오싹하게 하다
meanings.example: The icy wind chilled me to the bone. / The mysterious sound chilled her to the bone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 얼음장 같은 바람이 나를 뼛속까지 춥게 만들었다. / 그 의문의 소리는 그녀를 소름 끼치게(오싹하게) 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force someone to accept your ideas or beliefs.
meanings.example: I'm tired of him forcing his political views down my throat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 자신의 정치적 견해를 나에게 억지로 강요하는(주입하는) 것에 진저리가 난다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay part of the money you owe so that the total debt becomes smaller.
meanings.example: He's working extra hours to pay down his student loans.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 학자금 대출을 갚기(줄이기) 위해 추가 근무를 하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accidentally swallow something into your windpipe instead of your esophagus, causing coughing.
meanings.example: I can't stop coughing; some water went down the wrong pipe.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기침이 안 멈춰요. 물이 사레들렸거든요(엉뚱한 데로 넘어갔거든요).

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide the place and facilities for an event; (of a plant or animal) to provide a home for a parasite.
meanings.example: London played host to the Olympic Games in 2012.
meanings.exampleKorean: 런던은 2012년에 올림픽 경기를 주최했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly realize something important about your life or situation after a long time.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 어느 날 문득 깨닫다, 갑자기 생각이 바뀌다
meanings.example: He woke up one day and realized he didn't want to be a lawyer anymore.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 어느 날 문득 더 이상 변호사가 되고 싶지 않다는 것을 깨달았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to like someone or something very much, often suddenly.
meanings.example: My daughter has really taken a shining to her new teacher.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 딸은 새로 오신 선생님을 정말 좋아하게 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat all the food that has been served to you.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 음식을 남김없이 다 먹다, 접시를 비우다
meanings.example: He's a growing boy, so he always cleans up his plate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 성장기 아이라 항상 음식을 남김없이 다 먹는다 (접시를 비운다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To rain very heavily (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 비가 마구 쏟아지다, 비가 억수같이 오다
meanings.example: It's really tipping it down outside, so don't forget your umbrella.
meanings.exampleKorean: 밖에 비가 정말 마구 쏟아지고(억수같이 오고) 있으니 우산 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be accustomed to something so that it no longer seems strange or difficult.
meanings.example: I'm used to getting up early for work. / She's used to the hot weather here.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 일 때문에 일찍 일어나는 것에 익숙하다. / 그녀는 이곳의 더운 날씨에 익숙하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To show your support or lack of support for something by choosing to spend or not spend your money on it.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 소비로 의사를 표현하다, (구매 거부 등으로) 반대 의사를 나타내다
meanings.example: If you don't like the company's ethics, you can always vote with your wallet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 기업의 윤리가 마음에 들지 않는다면, 언제든지 소비로 당신의 의사를 표현할(구매 거부 등을 할) 수 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop doing what you are currently doing in order to rest or do something else.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 잠시 휴식을 취하다, 짬을 내다, 중단하고 쉬다
meanings.example: You need to take some time out and relax before you burn out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 번아웃이 오기 전에 잠시 휴식을 취하며(짬을 내어) 쉴 필요가 있어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone feel confused or less confident; to be constantly thought about by someone.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~의 머릿속을 복잡하게 만들다, (생각 등이) 뇌리에서 떠나지 않다
meanings.example: The opponent's trash talk really got into his head. / That catchy song has really gotten into my head.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상대방의 도발적인 언행이 정말 그의 머릿속을 복잡하게 만들었다(심리를 흔들었다). / 그 중독성 있는 노래가 정말 내 뇌리에서 떠나지 않는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wear several layers of clothing to keep warm.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 옷을 여러 겹 껴입다, 겹쳐 입다
meanings.example: It's freezing outside, so make sure to layer up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 밖이 엄청나게 추우니 꼭 옷을 여러 겹 껴입도록(겹쳐 입도록) 하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lower your head or body quickly to avoid being seen or hit.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 몸을 휙 낮추다, 머리를 숙이다, 몸을 굽히다
meanings.example: He ducked down behind the wall to avoid the cameras.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 카메라를 피하기 위해 벽 뒤로 몸을 휙 낮췄다 (머리를 숙였다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To warm something up slightly so that it is no longer cold.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 한기를 가시다, 미지근하게 데우다, 약간 따뜻하게 하다
meanings.example: I'll put the milk in the microwave for a few seconds to take the chill off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 한기를 가시게 하려고(미지근하게 데우려고) 우유를 전자레인지에 몇 초간 돌릴게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To catch someone doing something wrong with undeniable digital evidence (slang).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 결정적 증거를 잡다, 현장을 포착하다, 빼도 박도 못하게 잡다
meanings.example: He tried to deny the cheating, but he was caught in 4K on the security camera.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 부정행위를 부인하려 했지만, 보안 카메라에 결정적 증거가 찍히고(빼도 박도 못하게 잡히고) 말았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dance very energetically and skillfully (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 춤 실력을 뽐내다, 무대를 찢다, 열정적으로 춤추다
meanings.example: She really tore up the dance floor at the wedding reception.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 결혼 피로연에서 정말 춤 실력을 뽐냈다 (무대를 찢어놓았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To react to criticism or an attack with great anger or force.
meanings.example: The politician lashed back at his critics during the interview.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인은 인터뷰 중에 비판자들에게 강력하게 반격했다 (세게 몰아붙였다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To refresh your skills or mind after a period of inactivity.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 기분 전환을 하다, (실력을) 다시 가다듬다, 머리를 식히다
meanings.example: I need to go for a run to dust off the cobwebs before I start studying.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공부를 시작하기 전에 머리를 식히기(기분 전환을 하기) 위해 한 바탕 뛰고 와야겠어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of a dead person, especially a famous one, to be placed in a public building so that people can go to see the body to show their respect.
meanings.example: The former president's body will lie in state for three days.
meanings.exampleKorean: 전직 대통령의 시신은 3일 동안 공식 안치되어 조문을 받을 예정이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend a lot of time and effort to achieve something.
meanings.example: If you want to become a professional athlete, you have to put in the work every single day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프로 운동선수가 되고 싶다면, 매일매일 노력해야(공을 들여야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use all your influence or resources to support a person, plan, or organization.
meanings.example: The President has thrown his weight behind the new environmental policy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대통령은 새로운 환경 정책에 강력하게 힘을 실어주었다 (강력히 지지했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To use your charm and charisma to attract or seducer someone (slang).
meanings.example: He's trying to rizz up his classmate by telling jokes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 농담을 던지며 반 친구에게 매력을 발산하려(작업을 걸려) 애쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop thinking about something, especially something unpleasant or worrying.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 생각을 떨쳐내다, 잊어버리다, 염두에 두지 않다
meanings.example: I tried to put the argument out of my head and get some sleep.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 말다툼에 대한 생각을 떨쳐내고(잊어버리고) 잠을 좀 자려고 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To develop or expand something by adding more details or features.
meanings.example: The company plans to build out its 5G network across the country.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 전국적으로 5G 네트워크를 확장할(구축할) 계획이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be sensible and able to understand what is happening in a situation (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 사리분별을 하다, 판세를 파악하다, 세상 물정을 알다
meanings.example: He's been in the business for years, so he definitely knows which end is up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 이 업계에 수년 동안 있었기 때문에 확실히 사리분별을 할 줄 안다(판세를 잘 안다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be not nearly as good, clever, or successful as someone or something else.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~보다 나을 게 없다, 상대도 안 되다, 비교가 안 되다
