meanings.example: This new smartphone has nothing on the latest model from their competitor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 새로운 스마트폰은 경쟁사의 최신 모델보다 나을 게 전혀 없다 (상대도 안 된다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something with a lot of enthusiasm, passion, and sincere effort.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~에 온 정성을 쏟다, 열정을 다하다, 진심을 다하다
meanings.example: You can tell she really put her heart in this painting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 이 그림에 정말 온 정성을 쏟았다는(진심을 다했다는) 것을 알 수 있다.
synonyms: put one's heart and soul into

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a wish while looking at a star; to hope for something that is unlikely to happen.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 별에게 소원을 빌다, 불가능한 꿈을 꾸다, 간절히 바라다
meanings.example: Don't just wish upon a star; take action to make your dreams come true.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그저 별에게 소원만 빌지(불가능한 꿈만 꾸지) 말고, 꿈을 이루기 위해 행동하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be stuck in a situation or a routine; to bind someone to a contract or agreement.
meanings.example: He felt locked into a career that he didn't enjoy. / The price is locked into the contract.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 즐겁지 않은 직업에 갇혀(고착되어) 있다고 느꼈다. / 가격은 계약서에 명시되어(확정되어) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid giving advice or being involved in things that you do not know about; to focus on your own specialty.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 자신의 본분에 충실하다, 아는 것만 말하다, 주제넘게 나서지 않다
meanings.example: I told the accountant to stick to his last and let me handle the marketing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 회계사에게 마케팅은 나에게 맡기고 본인의 업무에나 충실하라고(주제넘게 나서지 말라고) 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be the responsibility of someone; to be someone's fault.
meanings.example: The blame for the mistake lies at his door.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 실수의 책임은 그에게 있다 (그의 잘못이다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To connect an electrical device to a power supply; to become involved with or use a particular system or group of people.
meanings.example: We need to plug into the local business network to find new clients.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 고객을 찾으려면 지역 비즈니스 네트워크에 참여해야(네트워크를 활용해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To complete the last part of a period of time or a task.
meanings.example: He decided to finish out the school year before moving to a new city.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 새로운 도시로 이사하기 전에 학년도를 끝까지 마치기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discuss something again later; to return to a previous topic or person.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 다시 논의하다, 나중에 다시 이야기하다, 다시 돌아오다
meanings.example: Let's move on for now and circle back to this issue at the end of the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지금은 다음으로 넘어가고, 회의 끝에 이 문제에 대해 다시 논의합시다(나중에 다시 이야기합시다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To support or show solidarity with a person or a group.
meanings.example: We stand with the victims of the disaster and will provide all necessary aid.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 재난 피해자들과 연대하며(그들을 지지하며) 모든 필요한 원조를 제공할 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue doing your job or staying in your position, especially in a difficult or dangerous situation.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 자신의 자리를 지키다, 본분을 다하다, 임무를 완수하다
meanings.example: The captain stuck to his post until every passenger was safely off the ship.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선장은 모든 승객이 안전하게 배에서 내릴 때까지 자신의 자리를 지켰다 (본분을 다했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To destroy or defeat someone or something completely; to prove an idea or argument to be completely wrong.
meanings.example: The new evidence blew the defense's argument out of the water.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 증거는 피고 측의 주장을 완전히 깨부수었다(무력화했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To assign someone to a particular shift or duty on a schedule.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 당번을 정하다, 근무 명단에 올리다, 배치하다
meanings.example: Could you roster me on for the morning shift next Monday?
meanings.exampleKorean: 다음 주 월요일 아침 근무조로 저를 배치해(명단에 올려) 주시겠어요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To destroy a building or structure completely.
meanings.example: The entire village was leveled to the ground by the earthquake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마을 전체가 지진으로 인해 완전히 허물어졌다 (파괴되었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To record or write something down; to charge something to someone's account.
meanings.example: Just chalk down the drinks to my room number.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 음료들은 제 방 번호로 외상 달아(기록해) 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find a major flaw in an argument, plan, or theory that makes it invalid.
meanings.example: The latest data blows a hole through the scientist's original theory.
meanings.exampleKorean: 최신 데이터는 그 과학자의 원래 이론의 허점을 찌른다(무력화한다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To start working effectively or smoothly.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 본격적으로 가동되다, 원활하게 돌아가다, 효율적으로 작동하다
meanings.example: The team really clicked into gear in the second half of the game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 팀은 경기 후반전에 정말 본격적으로 원활하게 돌아가기(가동되기) 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase the pressure on someone to do something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 압박을 가하다, 수위를 높이다, 강도를 높이다
meanings.example: The police are turning up the heat on the suspects.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰이 용의자들에 대한 압박을 가하고(수위를 높이고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To work hard or to make a significant effort (informal).
meanings.example: Everyone on the team really put a shift in today.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 팀원 모두가 정말 열심히 일했다 (수고했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To pretend that someone or something is different from what they really are.
meanings.example: He tried to pass off the fake painting as an original.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 가짜 그림을 진품인 척하며 속이려(위장하려) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail when you are very close to achieving something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 마지막 문턱에서 좌절하다, 다 된 밥에 재 뿌리다
meanings.example: The deal was almost signed, but it fell at the last hurdle.
meanings.exampleKorean: 계약이 거의 성사될 뻔했지만, 마지막 문턱에서 좌절되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To describe something in a general way, ignoring detailed differences.
meanings.example: You shouldn't paint all politicians with a wide brush.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 정치인을 성급하게 일반화해서는(포괄적으로 다루어서는) 안 됩니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To protect someone too much from the dangers or difficulties of life.
meanings.example: You can't wrap your children in cotton wool forever.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들을 영원히 과잉 보호할(애지중지할) 수는 없어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move at a fast and steady pace (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 상당히 빠른 속도로 가다, 쾌속으로 달리다
meanings.example: The car was going at a good clip down the highway.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 차는 고속도로를 상당히 빠른 속도로 달리고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To surrender; to admit that you have done something wrong; to raise your hand to ask or answer a question.
meanings.example: The robber finally put his hands up. / Who wants to go first? Put your hands up!
meanings.exampleKorean: 강도는 마침내 항복했다 (손을 들었다). / 누가 먼저 할래요? 손 드세요!

meanings.englishDefinition: To happen or to start to exist.
meanings.example: How did this project come to be in the first place?
meanings.exampleKorean: 애초에 이 프로젝트가 어떻게 생기게(존재하게) 된 건가요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall into a light sleep, especially during the day.
meanings.example: I snoozed off for a few minutes while watching the movie.
meanings.exampleKorean: 영화를 보다가 몇 분 동안 깜빡 졸았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get onto a vehicle; to join an activity or a conversation.
meanings.example: He hopped on the bus just before it pulled away. / Feel free to hop on the call if you have time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 버스가 출발하기 직전에 올라탔다. / 시간이 되면 부담 없이 통화에 합류하세요(참여하세요).

meanings.englishDefinition: To become calm and sensible after a period of confusion or emotional upset (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 마음을 가다듬다, 정신 차리다, 생각을 정리하다
meanings.example: I need some time alone to get my head together before I make a decision.
meanings.exampleKorean: 결정을 내리기 전에 마음을 가다듬을(생각을 정리할) 혼자만의 시간이 좀 필요해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually reduce the size or number of something.
meanings.example: We need to whittle down the list of candidates to just three.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 후보자 명단을 단 3명으로 서서히 줄여나가야(압축해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly start doing something (like crying or laughing); to enter a place suddenly and forcefully.
meanings.example: She burst into tears when she heard the news. / The police burst into the room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 소식을 듣자마자 갑자기 울음을 터뜨렸다. / 경찰이 방 안으로 난입했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To affect or damage the most basic and important part of something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~의 핵심을 찌르다, 근본을 뒤흔들다, 직격탄을 날리다
meanings.example: The new law strikes at the heart of our democratic principles.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 새로운 법은 우리의 민주주의 원칙의 핵심을 찌른다 (근본을 뒤흔든다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To enlarge a hole with a tool; to criticize someone very severely (informal).
meanings.example: The boss reamed him out for making such a silly mistake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상사는 그가 그런 어처구니없는 실수를 한 것에 대해 호되게 꾸짖었다 (야단쳤다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To use something until it becomes comfortable to use.
meanings.example: These new boots are still a bit stiff; I need to wear them in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 새 부츠는 아직 좀 뻣뻣하네요. 좀 길들여야겠어요 (사용해서 편하게 만들어야겠어요).

meanings.englishDefinition: To feel like doing something, especially something to show that you are angry or annoyed.
meanings.example: I have a good mind to tell him exactly what I think of his behavior.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 행동에 대해 내가 어떻게 생각하는지 그에게 똑똑히 말해주고 싶은 마음이 굴뚝같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wash something lightly with water to remove soap or dirt.
meanings.example: Make sure you rinse off the dishes before putting them in the dishwasher.
meanings.exampleKorean: 식기세척기에 넣기 전에 그릇들을 물로 가볍게 씻어내(헹궈) 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make an extremely loud noise; to cheer very loudly.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 엄청난 소리를 내다, 환호성을 지르다, 떠나갈 듯하다
meanings.example: The fans nearly blew the roof off the stadium when the home team scored.
meanings.exampleKorean: 홈 팀이 득점하자 팬들이 경기장이 떠나갈 듯한(엄청난 소리로) 환호성을 질렀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat someone in a careless or unfair way, often by changing plans suddenly (informal).
meanings.example: I'm tired of being yanked around by the management.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경영진이 나를 제멋대로 다루는(이랬다저랬다 하는) 것에 진저리가 난다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take something seriously and think about it carefully.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 가슴에 새기다, 깊이 명심하다, 중히 여기다
meanings.example: He laid his father's advice to heart and worked even harder.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아버지의 충고를 가슴에 새기고(깊이 명심하고) 더욱 열심히 일했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to achieve something that has already been achieved or to convince someone who already agrees with you.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 이미 해결된 일에 힘쓰다, 쉬운 일을 어렵게 하다, 다 된 일에 간섭하다
meanings.example: You're kicking at an open door; I already agree with your proposal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이미 해결된 일에 힘쓰고(설득하려 애쓰고) 있네요. 전 이미 당신의 제안에 동의했거든요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very ill or injured and likely to die; to try very hard to survive in a dangerous situation.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 사투를 벌이다, 목숨을 걸고 싸우다, 사경을 헤매다
meanings.example: The victim was rushed to the hospital and is currently fighting for his life.
meanings.exampleKorean: 피해자는 급히 병원으로 이송되었으며 현재 사투를 벌이고(사경을 헤매고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cry loudly and for a long time (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 눈이 붓도록 엉엉 울다, 대성통곡하다, 실컷 울다
meanings.example: She bawled her eyes out when she found out her dog had run away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 자신의 개가 도망쳤다는 사실을 알았을 때 눈이 붓도록 엉엉 울었다 (대성통곡했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To find an answer to a problem by thinking about it logically.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 논리적으로 생각하여 결론을 내다, 판단하다, 이해하다
meanings.example: He managed to reason out why the experiment had failed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 실험이 왜 실패했는지 논리적으로 생각하여 그 이유를 찾아냈다 (이해했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To become defensive and stop communicating; to retreat into a protective shell (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 몸을 사리다, 방어적으로 행동하다, 입을 다물다
meanings.example: Whenever we talk about finances, he just turtles up and refuses to listen.
meanings.exampleKorean: 재정 문제에 관해 이야기할 때마다 그는 그냥 몸을 사리고(입을 다물고) 들으려 하지 않는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stand or be very close to someone or something; to face a difficult limit or obstacle.
meanings.example: The crowd pushed up against the barriers. / We are pushing up against the deadline.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군중이 차단벽에 바짝 붙어 밀어붙였다. / 우리는 마감 기한이라는 한계에 직면해 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To listen carefully in order to hear a particular sound.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 귀를 기울이다, 소리를 기다리다, 주의 깊게 듣다
meanings.example: Can you listen out for the delivery truck? I'm expecting a package.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배달 트럭 소리가 나는지 귀를 기울여(주의 깊게 들어) 줄래? 택배 올 게 있거든.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue working hard at something for a long time.
meanings.example: She was working away at her computer all afternoon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 오후 내내 컴퓨터로 묵묵히 꾸준히 일하고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make or organize something very quickly and without much care (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 대강대강 만들다, 서둘러 준비하다, 대충 합치다
meanings.example: I had to bash together a presentation in just thirty minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 단 30분 만에 발표 자료를 서둘러(대강대행) 준비해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return to an old, usually bad, habit or situation.
meanings.example: It's easy to fall back into old habits when you're under stress.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스트레스를 받으면 예전의 나쁜 습관으로 다시 돌아가기(빠지기) 쉽다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter your name and password to access a computer system; to record your arrival at a place.
meanings.example: You need to sign into your account to see the details.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상세 내용을 보려면 당신의 계정으로 로그인해야(접속해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To purchase a ticket for a journey; to behave in a way that will lead to a particular result (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 표를 사다, ~하게 될 운명이다, (나쁜 결과를) 자초하다
meanings.example: By ignored the warnings, he just bought a ticket to disaster.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경고를 무시함으로써, 그는 재앙을 자초했다 (재앙을 맞을 운명에 처했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To go back to a place or person you have left (redundant but common).
meanings.example: She promised to return back home as soon as possible.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 가능한 한 빨리 집으로 돌아가겠다고 약속했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To write the first version of a document, such as a letter, report, or contract.
meanings.example: Could you draft up a proposal for the new project by tomorrow?
meanings.exampleKorean: 내일까지 새 프로젝트에 대한 제안서 초안을 작성해(기안해) 주시겠어요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To fasten a seatbelt in a vehicle; to prepare yourself for an exciting or difficult experience (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 벨트를 매다, 마음의 준비를 하다, 단단히 대비하다
meanings.example: Strap in, it's going to be a bumpy ride! / We need to strap in for a busy week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 벨트 꽉 매세요, 길이 험할 겁니다! / 바쁜 한 주를 위해 마음의 준비를(단단히 대비를) 해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To plan the details of something, such as a story or a project, especially in a systematic way.
meanings.example: The author spent months plotting out the sequence of events in her book.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작가는 책의 사건 전개를 구상하는(계획을 짜는) 데 몇 달을 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: (of a vehicle) To arrive at a place and stop.
meanings.example: The train pulled into the station ten minutes late.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기차는 10분 늦게 역에 들어왔다 (정차했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place, such as an office or someone's home, without being accompanied to the door by the host.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 혼자 나가다, 배웅 없이 떠나다, (작별 인사 후) 퇴장하다
meanings.example: It's fine, I know the way. I'll see myself out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 괜찮아요, 길을 아니까 혼자 나갈게요 (배웅 안 하셔도 돼요).

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce something, such as expenses or a workforce, as much as possible.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 최대한 줄이다, 뼈만 남기다, 대폭 삭감하다
meanings.example: The company's budget has been pared to the bone this year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 올해 회사의 예산이 최대한으로 줄어들었다 (대폭 삭감되었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To find someone or something by looking for them carefully and for a long time.
meanings.example: I'll try to search out that old letter from my grandfather.
meanings.exampleKorean: 할아버지의 그 오래된 편지를 어떻게든 찾아내(수색해) 볼게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop suddenly because you are surprised or have realized something.
meanings.example: He was about to complain, but pulled up short when he saw the boss's face.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 불평을 하려다가 상사의 얼굴을 보고는 갑자기 말을 멈췄다 (멈칫했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something by cutting it; to get rid of unnecessary parts.
meanings.example: The surgeon cut away the damaged tissue. / We need to cut away the fluff in this report.
meanings.exampleKorean: 외과의사가 손상된 조직을 잘라냈다 (제거했다). / 이 보고서에서 불필요한 군더더기들을 도려내야(제거해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To write something quickly and untidily.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 휘갈겨 쓰다, 대충 적다, 서툴게 쓰다
meanings.example: He scrawled out a quick note and left it on the table.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 짧은 쪽지를 휘갈겨 써서(대충 적어서) 테이블 위에 두었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide someone with all the necessary information about something (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 정보를 제공하다, 알려주다, 상황을 파악하게 하다
meanings.example: Could you clue me up on what happened at the meeting?
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의에서 무슨 일이 있었는지 저에게 좀 알려주실(정보를 주실) 수 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To fill something, such as a tire, with air.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 공기를 넣다, 팽팽하게 하다, 바람을 넣다
meanings.example: I need to stop at the gas station to air up my tires.
meanings.exampleKorean: 타이어에 공기를 넣으러(바람을 넣으러) 주유소에 들러야 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To distinguish or divide something from a group or mixture.
meanings.example: We need to separate out the useful data from the noise.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 유용한 데이터를 불필요한 정보(노이즈)로부터 따로 분리해내야(골라내야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move around in a sad or aimless way because you are unhappy or disappointed.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 빈둥거리다, 우울하게 돌아다니다, 의욕 없이 지내다
meanings.example: Stop moping around the house and go do something productive.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집안에서 우울하게 돌아다니는(의욕 없이 지내는) 것 좀 그만하고 생산적인 일이라도 좀 해봐.

meanings.englishDefinition: To establish a place to stay, especially in a tent; to stay in one place for a period of time.
meanings.example: We decided to set up camp near the river for the night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 하룻밤 동안 강가 근처에 캠프를 치기로(자리를 잡기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage to say something when other people are talking a lot.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 말할 기회를 얻다, 말참견하다, 한마디 거들다
meanings.example: She talked so fast that I couldn't even get a word in edgewise.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 말을 너무 빨리해서 나는 말 한마디 거들(말할 기회를 얻을) 수도 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To exert pressure on someone to do something or to pay money (informal).
meanings.example: The creditors are starting to put the squeeze on the struggling company.
meanings.exampleKorean: 채권자들이 어려움을 겪고 있는 그 회사를 압박하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be dismissed from a school or college because of poor grades.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 제적당하다, 학사 경고로 퇴학당하다, 낙제로 학교를 그만두다
meanings.example: If you don't study harder, you might fail out of college.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공부를 더 열심히 하지 않으면 대학에서 제적당할(퇴학당할) 수도 있어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be given a task or a piece of luck without any effort on your part.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~의 책임이 되다, (행운 등이) 굴러 들어오다, 갑자기 주어지다
meanings.example: A promotion just landed in her lap after her boss resigned. / This difficult case has landed in my lap.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상사가 사임한 후 승진 기회가 그녀에게 그냥 굴러 들어왔다. / 이 어려운 사건이 갑자기 내 책임이 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start a process or a series of events.
meanings.example: The committee has set in train a series of reforms.
meanings.exampleKorean: 위원회는 일련의 개혁안들에 착수했다 (진행시켰다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend time with someone as a friend (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 친하게 지내다, 어울리다, 함께 시간을 보내다
meanings.example: They used to pal around together when they were kids.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 어렸을 때 함께 어울려(친하게) 지내곤 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shout or sing something very loudly and in a deep voice.
meanings.example: He bellowed out the commands to his troops.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 부대원들에게 우렁찬 목소리로(크게 소리쳐) 명령을 내렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cooperate or conform to the ideas of others in order to avoid conflict and maintain harmony.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 원만하게 지내기 위해 순응하다, 대세에 따르다
meanings.example: Sometimes in politics, you have to go along to get along.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정치에서는 때로 원만하게 지내기 위해 대세에 따라야(순응해야) 할 때가 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drink a lot of alcohol; to make a garden or area look green and healthy.
meanings.example: They spent the whole evening lushing up at the pub.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 저녁 내내 펍에서 술을 잔뜩 마셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be consistent with or connected to something else.
meanings.example: Your story doesn't quite tie in with the facts we have.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신의 이야기는 우리가 가진 사실들과는 좀처럼 일치하지(맞물리지) 않네요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing something or dealing with a situation immediately.
meanings.example: The emergency services jumped into action as soon as the call came in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 전화가 오자마자 응급 구조대가 즉시 행동을 시작했다 (기민하게 대처했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause something to exist.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 세상에 태어나게 하다, 존재하게 하다, 창조하다
meanings.example: The new law brought a special task force into being.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 법안에 의해 특별 전담반이 세상에 태어났다 (존재하게 되었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage to survive with very little money or food.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 간신히 연명하다, 입에 풀칠하다, 죽지 않을 만큼 살다
meanings.example: In those days, we worked long hours just to keep soul and body together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 시절에는 그저 간신히 연명하기(입에 풀칠하기) 위해 장시간 일해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force someone to retire and give them a pension; to get rid of something that is old or useless.
meanings.example: The company decided to pension off the older managers. / We finally pensioned off the old printer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 나이 든 매니저들을 퇴직시키기로 결정했다. / 우리는 마침내 낡은 프린터를 폐기했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To learn new skills or to teach someone new skills.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 기술을 습득하다, 숙련도를 높이다, 능력을 키우다
meanings.example: Employees are encouraged to skill up in digital marketing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 직원들은 디지털 마케팅 기술을 습득하도록(능력을 키우도록) 장려받고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall or almost fall because your foot hits something; to stumble over words while speaking.
meanings.example: I tripped over a loose rug and fell. / He tripped over his words because he was nervous.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 들뜬 카펫에 걸려 넘어졌다. / 그는 긴장해서 말이 꼬였다 (비틀거렸다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To give someone too much to carry; to give someone too much work or responsibility.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 짐을 가득 싣다, 부담을 주다, 압박하다
meanings.example: The travelers were loaded down with heavy suitcases. / I don't want to load you down with extra work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여행자들은 무거운 여행 가방을 가득 들고 있었다. / 당신에게 추가 업무로 부담을 주고 싶지 않아요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start living in a particular place.
meanings.example: The Royal Family takes up residence in the palace during the summer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 왕실 가족은 여름 동안 궁전에 거주한다 (정착한다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To telephone several people or places to get information; to visit someone for a short time.
meanings.example: I'll call around and see if I can find a better price. / Why don't you call around this evening?
meanings.exampleKorean: 이곳저곳에 전화해서(수소문해서) 더 좋은 가격이 있는지 알아볼게요. / 오늘 저녁에 잠시 들르는 게 어때요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop something from flowing or being expressed.
meanings.example: She tried to dam up her tears. / Don't dam up your feelings; talk to someone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 눈물을 억누르려 애썼다. / 감정을 억누르지(가두지) 말고 누군가에게 이야기하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone work so hard that they become exhausted or ill.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 혹사시키다, 진을 빼놓다, 몹시 힘들게 일시키다
meanings.example: The boss is working his staff into the ground.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상사는 직원들을 혹사시키고(진을 빼놓고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something by pulling it with force; to take something out of a larger structure.
meanings.example: The old pipes were ripped out and replaced with new ones.
meanings.exampleKorean: 낡은 파이프들이 거칠게 떼어내졌고(철거되었고) 새것으로 교체되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take over a job or role that someone else used to have.
meanings.example: It will be difficult for anyone to step into the CEO's shoes.
meanings.exampleKorean: CEO의 역할을 이어받는(후임이 되는) 것은 누구에게나 어려운 일일 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To experience physical or mental pain without complaining or telling anyone about it.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 말 못 하고 속으로 앓다, 묵묵히 고통을 견디다
meanings.example: Many people with mental health issues suffer in silence for years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정신 건강 문제를 가진 많은 사람이 수년 동안 말 못 하고 속으로 앓는다 (묵묵히 고통을 견딘다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To start something enthusiastically or energetically, often without thinking much about it first.
meanings.example: He launched into a long explanation of why he was late.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신이 왜 늦었는지에 대해 장황한 설명을 신나게 시작했다 (설명에 돌입했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To not plan something or take action to ensure it happens, but instead rely on luck.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 운에 맡기다, 방치하다, 계획 없이 두다
meanings.example: We have planned every detail; we aren't leaving anything to chance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 모든 세부 사항을 계획했습니다. 어떤 것도 운에 맡기지(방치하지) 않을 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get information from someone who does not want to give it to you, especially by being very persistent.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 비밀 등을 캐내다, 정보를 알아내다, 유도 심문하다
meanings.example: She managed to worm the secret out of him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 간신히 그로부터 비밀을 캐냈다 (알아냈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To use a security badge to enter a building or restricted area.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 출입 카드를 찍다, (보안 카드로) 인증하고 들어가다
meanings.example: Don't forget to badge in when you arrive at the office.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사무실에 도착하면 출입 카드 찍는(인증하고 들어가는) 것을 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tell someone about a useful person, place, or thing that they did not know about before.
meanings.example: My friend put me on to a great new restaurant in town.
meanings.exampleKorean: 친구가 시내에 있는 아주 괜찮은 새 식당을 나에게 소개해(알려) 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To offer more and more money for something at an auction so that the price increases.
meanings.example: The two collectors kept bidding up the price of the painting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 수집가가 계속해서 그 그림의 가격을 올렸다 (더 높게 입찰했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To lose a fight, argument, or competition.
meanings.example: The small company came off second best in the legal battle against the giant corporation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 작은 회사는 대기업과의 법적 공방에서 패배했다 (지고 말았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To wash or clean something by pouring a large amount of water over or through it.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 물로 씻어내다, 물을 부어 청소하다, 헹구다
meanings.example: They used a hose to sluice out the horse stalls.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 호스를 사용하여 말 마구간을 물로 씻어냈다 (물을 부어 청소했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To encounter a problem or obstacle.
meanings.example: We've hit up against a few legal problems with the new development.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 새로운 개발 과정에서 몇 가지 법적 난관에 부딪혔다 (가로막혔다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To record an agreement, offer, or promise in a document to make it official or to have proof.
meanings.example: I'd like you to put your proposal in writing so I can review it carefully.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신의 제안을 서면으로 남겨(문서화해) 주시면 꼼꼼히 검토해 보겠습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing something or working on a task immediately and energetically.
meanings.example: The rescue team swung into action as soon as the alarm went off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경보가 울리자마자 구조대가 즉각 행동을 개시했다 (신속하게 착수했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To have everything that you need, especially because you are wealthy or well cared for.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 부족함이 없다, 아쉬운 게 없다, 풍족하게 지내다
meanings.example: Their children want for nothing; they have the best of everything.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들의 자녀들은 부족함이 없다. 모든 것에서 최고의 것들을 누리고 산다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing something immediately and quickly (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 서둘러 시작하다, 기민하게 행동하다, 즉시 착수하다
meanings.example: If the boss gives you a task, you'd better jump to it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상사가 일을 시키면, 서둘러 시작하는(기민하게 행동하는) 게 좋을 거예요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid looking at or dealing with something; to reject or refuse a suggestion or request.
meanings.example: He turned aside from the unpleasant sight. / The committee turned aside his request for funding.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 불쾌한 광경을 외면했다. / 위원회는 자금 지원에 대한 그의 요청을 물리쳤다 (거절했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stand very close to someone to watch what they are doing; to wait for a decision or result.
meanings.example: I don't like it when the manager stands over me while I'm working.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 일하는 동안 매니저가 내 옆에서 지켜보는(감독하는) 것을 좋아하지 않는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give someone a particular quality, ability, or advantage.
meanings.example: She was blessed with a beautiful singing voice.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 아름다운 노랫소리(라는 재능)를 축복으로 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To visit someone for a short time, often while you are going somewhere else.
meanings.example: I'll stop in and see you on my way home from work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 퇴근하는 길에 잠깐 당신에게 들를게요 (방문할게요).

meanings.englishDefinition: To reserve a place on a flight, train, etc.; to officially record someone's name in a police register.
meanings.example: I've booked on a flight to Tokyo next week. / He was booked on suspicion of robbery.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다음 주 도쿄행 비행기를 예약했다. / 그는 강도 혐의로 기록에 올랐다 (입건되었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be ready or prepared to do something very soon.
meanings.example: I was just about to call you when you walked in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신이 들어왔을 때 막 당신에게 전화하려던 참이었어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start speaking before another actor has finished their line; to interrupt someone.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 남의 말을 가로채다, 대사를 가로채다, 흐름을 끊다
meanings.example: Don't step on your co-star's lines; wait for your cue.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상대 배우의 대사를 가로채지(흐름을 끊지) 말고 당신의 큐 사인을 기다리세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To break something down forcefully; to reduce someone to a lower rank (informal).
meanings.example: The police had to bust down the door. / He was busted down to private for his misconduct.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 문을 부수어야 했다. / 그는 비위 행위로 인해 이병으로 강등되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To choose something or someone from a number of possibilities.
meanings.example: We eventually decided on the blue one for the living room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 결국 거실용으로 파란색을 선택하기로(파란색으로 결정하기로) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To not allow your feelings or a physical urge to be shown or released.
meanings.example: I was so angry, but I managed to hold it in until they left.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정말 화가 났지만, 그들이 떠날 때까지 간신히 억눌렀다 (참았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop looking at someone or something (usually used in the negative to mean you are fascinated or vigilant).
meanings.example: He's so handsome that I couldn't take my eyes off him. / You shouldn't take your eyes off the children at the pool.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 너무 잘생겨서 눈을 뗄 수가 없었다. / 수영장에서는 아이들에게서 눈을 떼면 안 됩니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To improve your behavior or start working harder (informal, British).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 생각을 고쳐먹다, 태도를 개선하다, 정신 차리고 열심히 하다
meanings.example: He'll have to buck up his ideas if he wants to pass the exam.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시험에 합격하고 싶다면 그는 생각을 고쳐먹어야(정신 차리고 열심히 해야) 할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To improve a skill or to make something more effective.
meanings.example: I need to sharpen up my interview skills before next week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다음 주가 되기 전에 면접 기술을 연마해야(기량을 높여야) 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return to bad habits or a previous, less desirable situation.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 예전의 나쁜 방식으로 되돌아가다, 나쁜 습관에 다시 빠지다
meanings.example: After a few weeks of dieting, she started to slip back to her old ways.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다이어트를 몇 주 한 뒤에, 그녀는 다시 예전의 나쁜 생활 방식으로 되돌아가기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To play a role that is different from the kind of character an actor usually plays.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 기존 이미지와 반대되는 역할을 맡다, 변신을 시도하다
meanings.example: The comedian decided to play against type by taking a serious role in a drama.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 코미디언은 드라마에서 진지한 역할을 맡음으로써 기존 이미지와 반대되는 역할에 도전하기로(변신을 시도하기로) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pull something in with effort; to take someone to a police station for questioning.
meanings.example: The suspect was hauled in for questioning by the police.
meanings.exampleKorean: 용의자는 조사를 받기 위해 경찰에 연행되었다 (잡혀 들어갔다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To cook something completely, especially until the inside is no longer raw.
meanings.example: Make sure the chicken is cooked through before serving.
meanings.exampleKorean: 음식을 내놓기 전에 닭고기가 속까지 완전히 익었는지 확인하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cut something into many small pieces; to criticize someone's ideas or work very severely.
meanings.example: The critic cut his new novel to ribbons. / The tent was cut to ribbons by the strong wind.
meanings.exampleKorean: 평론가는 그의 새 소설을 혹평했다 (무참히 깨뜨렸다). / 텐트가 강풍에 갈기갈기 찢어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To press something downwards; to keep a feeling or a price from increasing.
meanings.example: He pushed down the handle to open the door. / The government is trying to push down inflation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 문을 열기 위해 손잡이를 꾹 눌렀다. / 정부는 인플레이션을 억제하려(낮추려) 애쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To change the direction of a sailing ship; to change your course of action or opinion.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 침로를 바꾸다, 태도를 변경하다, 방향을 틀다
meanings.example: The ship had to tack about because of the shifting wind. / The politician decided to tack about on the tax issue.
meanings.exampleKorean: 변화하는 바람 때문에 배는 침로를 바꿔야(방향을 틀어야) 했다. / 그 정치인은 세금 문제에 대해 태도를 변경하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place in order to go somewhere else (informal).
meanings.example: We're planning to head out early tomorrow morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 내일 아침 일찍 출발할(나갈) 계획입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrive at work or a military station and announce that you are ready to start your duties.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 근무 신고를 하다, 출근하다, 임무에 투입되다
meanings.example: All soldiers must report for duty at 08:00 hours.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 병사는 08:00시에 근무 신고를 해야(출근해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use something as a foundation or starting point for an idea, story, or system.
meanings.example: This movie is based on a true story.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 영화는 실화를 바탕으로 하고(실화에 근거하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To not need something; to dislike or have no respect for someone or something.
meanings.example: I have no use for people who lie. / I have no use for this old printer anymore.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 거짓말하는 사람들을 정말 싫어한다 (경멸한다). / 나는 이 낡은 프린터가 더 이상 필요하지 않다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sit or fall down suddenly and heavily, especially because you are tired or disappointed.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 털썩 주저앉다, 쓰러지듯 앉다, 기운 없이 앉다
meanings.example: He slumped down in his chair after the long meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 긴 회의가 끝난 뒤 의자에 털썩 주저앉았다 (기운 없이 앉았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To use up money or resources very quickly; to create a hole in something by heat.
meanings.example: The company is burning through its cash reserves at an alarming rate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사가 현금 예비비를 놀라운 속도로 빨리 써버리고(소진하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something unpleasant immediately so that you do not have to worry about it anymore.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 불쾌한 일을 빨리 끝내버리다, 해치우다, 매를 먼저 맞다
meanings.example: I'd rather have the dental surgery over with as soon as possible.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 치과 수술을 가능한 한 빨리 끝내버리고(해치우고) 싶다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find someone or something by searching carefully for information or following clues.
meanings.example: The police managed to trace down the anonymous caller.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 간신히 그 익명의 발신자를 추적하여 찾아냈다 (밝혀냈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To unexpectedly cause problems for someone later on (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: (예상치 못한 문제가) 불거지다, 화가 되어 돌아오다, 뒤통수를 치다
meanings.example: Those small errors in the report might come up and bite us later.
meanings.exampleKorean: 보고서의 그 작은 오류들이 나중에 우리에게 화가 되어 돌아올(불거질) 수도 있어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To display or exhibit a quality or truth clearly.
meanings.example: He strove to show forth his gratitude through his actions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 행동을 통해 감사의 마음을 명확히 드러내려(보여주려) 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide a name for something; to be the origin of a name.
meanings.example: The scientist was given the honor to give name to the newly discovered planet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 과학자는 새로 발견된 행성에 이름을 지어주는(명명하는) 영광을 얻었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To experience bitter disappointment; to strive for something worthless or vain.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 비참한 결과를 맞이하다, 허망해지다, 헛된 노력을 하다
meanings.example: After his business failed, he felt like he was dining on ashes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사업이 실패한 후, 그는 비참하고 허망한 결과를 맞이한(비참해진) 기분이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discourage someone's enthusiasm; to spoil the fun or excitement of a situation.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 흥을 깨다, 찬물을 끼얹다, 의욕을 꺾다
meanings.example: I don't want to throw a wet blanket on your plans, but we don't have enough money for that.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신의 계획에 찬물을 끼얹고(흥을 깨고) 싶지는 않지만, 그럴 만한 예산이 없어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce something unexpectedly or as if by magic, especially a solution to a problem.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 뜻밖의 해결책을 내놓다, 깜짝 쇼를 하다, 자원 등을 짜내다
meanings.example: The manager really pulled a victory out of a hat in the final minutes of the game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매니저는 경기 마지막 몇 분 동안 정말 뜻밖의 승리를 짜내었다(깜짝 쇼처럼 승리를 일궈냈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop doing something while you are successful and before you start to fail.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 잘 나갈 때 그만두다, 적절한 시점에 물러나다, 박수칠 때 떠나다
meanings.example: After winning three games in a row, he decided to quit while he was ahead.
meanings.exampleKorean: 연속으로 세 판을 이긴 뒤, 그는 잘 나갈 때 그만두기로(적절한 시점에 물러나기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to make someone feel deep sympathy or emotion, often in order to persuade them.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~의 심금을 울리다, 동정심을 유발하다, 감정에 호소하다
meanings.example: The charity's advertisement really played on my heartstrings.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 자선 단체의 광고는 정말 나의 심금을 울렸다 (동정심을 유발했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be in the same difficult situation as other people.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 운명을 같이하다, 같은 처지에 있다, 한 배를 타다
meanings.example: We're all rowing in the same boat, so we need to help each other.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 모두 한 배를 탄(같은 처지에 있는) 사이니, 서로 도와야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something stay in one position; to make a plan or agreement definite.
meanings.example: Make sure you fasten down the luggage on the roof rack. / We need to fasten down the details of the contract.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지붕 위 선반의 짐을 단단히 고정하세요. / 우리는 계약서의 세부 사항들을 확정해야(명확히 해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To balance two opposing situations or qualities; to give up something in return for something else.
meanings.example: There's often a trade-off between speed and quality.
meanings.exampleKorean: 속도와 품질 사이에는 종종 균형을 맞춰야 하는(상충하는) 관계가 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To immediately and actively support or protect someone who is being criticized or attacked.
meanings.example: When the teacher criticized John, his friends immediately sprang to his defence.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님이 존을 비판하자, 그의 친구들이 즉각 그를 옹호하고 나섰다 (변호했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To answer or react to something very quickly and often sharply.
meanings.example: "No, I didn't!" she shot back angrily.
meanings.exampleKorean: "아니, 안 그랬어!" 그녀가 화를 내며 잽싸게 되받아쳤다 (응수했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To signal to someone with your hand to continue moving through a checkpoint; to allow something to pass without much inspection.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 통과시키다, 그냥 지나가게 하다, 관대하게 승인하다
meanings.example: The security guard recognized him and waved him through without checking his ID.
meanings.exampleKorean: 보안 요원은 그를 알아보고 신분증 확인 없이 그냥 통과시켰다 (지나가게 했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: A humorous or sarcastic way to say someone has been fired (slang).
meanings.example: The poor performance led the manager to promote him to customer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 실적이 저조해서 매니저는 그를 해고했다 (고객으로 승진(?)시켰다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to support someone or something strongly in an argument or fight.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 지지하며 나서다, 논쟁에 가담하다, 강력히 변호하다
meanings.example: The lawyer took up cudgels on behalf of the environmental group.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 변호사는 환경 단체를 대변하여(지지하며) 논쟁에 가담했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare for a performance by becoming the person you are going to play.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 역할에 몰입하다, 배역에 빠져들다, 캐릭터를 소화하다
meanings.example: He spent weeks researching the history of the King to really get into character.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 배역에 정말 몰입하기 위해 왕의 역사를 조사하는 데 몇 주를 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To win a competition or argument; to be more successful than everyone else.
meanings.example: Despite the tough competition, our company managed to come out on top.
meanings.exampleKorean: 치열한 경쟁에도 불구하고, 우리 회사는 결국 승리할(우위를 차지할) 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To change your ideas and behavior so that they are modern and up to date.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 시대의 흐름에 맞추다, 유행을 따르다, 뒤처지지 않다
meanings.example: To be successful in this industry, you have to keep up with the times.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 업계에서 성공하려면 시대의 흐름에 맞춰야(뒤처지지 말아야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use someone else's actions or behavior as an example for how you should act.
meanings.example: Investors are taking their cue from the latest economic report.
meanings.exampleKorean: 투자자들은 최신 경제 보고서로부터 힌트를 얻고(참고하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to deal with a situation or to control someone who is causing trouble.
meanings.example: The principal decided to take the unruly class in hand. / I need to take my finances in hand.
meanings.exampleKorean: 교장 선생님은 그 통제 불능인 학급을 직접 관리하기로(다루기로) 결정했다. / 나는 내 재정 상태를 본격적으로 관리해야(착수해야) 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gather and prepare all the equipment needed for a mission or activity.
meanings.example: The soldiers were ordered to load out for the night mission.
meanings.exampleKorean: 병사들은 야간 임무를 위해 장비를 챙기라는(준비하라는) 명령을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase speed or intensity in a systematic way (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 점차 속도를 높이다, 단계를 밟다, 강도를 높이다
meanings.example: The runner went through the gears and left everyone else behind.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 주자는 점차 속도를 높여서 다른 사람들을 모두 뒤로 따돌렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very close to death.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 죽음의 문턱에 있다, 사경을 헤매다, 죽기 직전이다
meanings.example: The old cat had been sitting on death's doorstep for weeks before it passed away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 늙은 고양이는 죽기 전 몇 주 동안 죽음의 문턱에 있었다 (사경을 헤매고 있었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To find the courage to do something difficult or frightening (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 용기를 내다, 마음을 다잡다, 배짱을 부리다
meanings.example: I finally nerved myself up to ask her for a raise.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 마침내 용기를 내어 그녀에게 급여 인상을 요청했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To focus all your attention on one particular thing (informal).
meanings.example: The athlete zoned in on the finish line and ignored the noise of the crowd.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 선수는 군중의 소음을 무시하고 결승선에만 집중했다 (몰입했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To wash soap or dirt off a surface using water; to help swallow food by drinking liquid.
meanings.example: He used a hose to rinse down the driveway. / I need some water to rinse down this dry cake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 호스를 사용하여 진입로를 씻어 내렸다. / 이 퍽퍽한 케이크를 넘기려면 물이 좀 필요해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To build the basic structure or frame of something.
meanings.example: The carpenters framed out the new room in just two days.
meanings.exampleKorean: 목수들은 단 이틀 만에 새 방의 뼈대를 세웠다 (틀을 잡았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To think something without saying it aloud to others; to speak to yourself.
meanings.example: "I can do this," he said to himself as he walked onto the stage.
meanings.exampleKorean: "난 할 수 있어," 그는 무대 위로 걸어가며 혼잣말을 했다 (마음속으로 생각했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To take care of someone and make sure that they are treated well.
meanings.example: He's always looked out for his younger brother. / We need to look out for each other.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 남동생을 보살펴(지켜주어) 왔다. / 우리는 서로를 신경 써야(보살펴야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove problems or difficulties so that something can happen easily.
meanings.example: The manager's job is to smooth away any difficulties that the team faces.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매니저의 역할은 팀이 직면한 어떤 어려움이든 순조롭게 해결하는(없애는) 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a place feel happy or exciting because of your presence or personality.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 분위기를 밝게 만들다, 주변을 환하게 하다
meanings.example: Her smile really lights up a room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 미소는 정말 주변 분위기를 밝게 만든다 (주변을 환하게 한다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To give something to someone else after you have received it or used it.
meanings.example: Please read this memo and pass it along to your colleagues.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 메모를 읽으시고 동료들에게 전달해(알려) 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to find out information about something, especially in a secret way (informal).
meanings.example: There are several reporters sniffing around for a story.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기삿거리를 위해 기웃거리는(정보를 캐는) 기자들이 몇 명 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop hiding your true feelings or character.
meanings.example: It's time for him to take the mask off and show his true self.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이제 그가 가면을 벗고 자신의 진실한 모습을 보여줄 때입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be serious about what you say or do; to stand by your principles or commitments (slang).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 자신의 신념을 굽히지 않다, 진지하게 임하다, 약속을 지키다
meanings.example: He told everyone he would finish the project on time, and he really stood on business.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 프로젝트를 제시간에 끝내겠다고 모두에게 말했고, 정말로 그 약속을 지켰다 (진지하게 임했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place very quickly; to start something in a hurry.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 급히 떠나다, 서둘러 가다, 경주를 시작하다
meanings.example: He raced off to the airport as soon as he heard the news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 소식을 듣자마자 공항으로 급히 떠났다 (서둘러 갔다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To engage in small talk, often to avoid awkwardness or serious topics.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 날씨 이야기를 하다, 잡담을 하다, 가벼운 대화를 나누다
meanings.example: They spent the entire dinner just talking about the weather.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 저녁 식사 내내 그저 날씨 이야기만 나누었다 (가벼운 대화만 했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly increase speed or effort significantly.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 최선을 다해 속도를 높이다, 전력을 다하다, 추진력을 얻다
meanings.example: The runner switched on the afterburners in the final lap.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 주자는 마지막 바퀴에서 최선을 다해 속도를 높였다 (전력을 다했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To delay or ignore a difficult problem in the hope that it will be forgotten.
meanings.example: The government is trying to hit this controversial issue into the long grass.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 이 논란이 되는 문제를 유야무야(방치)하려 애쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to find the good things in a bad situation.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 긍정적으로 보다, 낙관하다, 좋은 면을 보다
meanings.example: Even though we lost the game, we should look on the positive side.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경기에 졌지만, 우리는 긍정적으로 생각해야(좋은 면을 봐야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To perform the actions that allow you to start using a computer system or website.
meanings.example: You need to log into your account to check your balance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잔액을 확인하려면 당신의 계정으로 로그인해야(접속해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take someone or something with you to a place.
meanings.example: Can I bring along a friend to the party?
meanings.exampleKorean: 파티에 친구 한 명 데려가도(동반해도) 될까요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something for someone or use your skills in order to get something in return.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 대가를 위해 일하다, 재주를 부리다, 보답을 하다
meanings.example: I had to sing for my supper by helping my uncle with his taxes in exchange for staying at his house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 삼촌 댁에 머무는 대가로 삼촌의 세금 계산을 도와드려야(대가를 위해 일해야) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause a lot of excitement, controversy, or trouble.
meanings.example: His controversial comments whipped up a storm of protest.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 논란이 되는 발언은 거센 항의의 소동을 일으켰다 (선동했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To agree with or approve of something (usually used in negative sentences).
meanings.example: I don't hold with the idea of children watching too much TV.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 아이들이 TV를 너무 많이 보는 것에 동의하지(찬성하지) 않는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To expect the worst; to be pessimistic about a situation.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 비관적으로 보다, 최악을 예상하다, 부정적으로 생각하다
meanings.example: Don't always look on the dark side; things might turn out fine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 항상 비관적으로만 보지(부정적으로만 생각하지) 마세요. 일이 잘 풀릴 수도 있잖아요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use your authority to force people who are arguing to reach an agreement (informal).
meanings.example: If they don't stop arguing, I'll have to bang their heads together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들이 말다툼을 멈추지 않는다면, 내가 강제로 화해시켜야(중재해야)겠어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To crush or smash something; to create a new work by combining elements from different sources; to ruin something.
meanings.example: He mashed up two popular songs to create a new track. / He really mashed up his car in the accident.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 두 곡의 인기곡을 뒤섞어 새로운 트랙을 만들었다. / 그는 사고로 자신의 차를 완전히 망가뜨렸다 (으깨버렸다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To find out secret information about a place or an enemy by watching secretly.
meanings.example: The scouts were sent ahead to spy out the land.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정찰병들이 지형을 염탐하기(정찰하기) 위해 앞으로 보내졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To significantly increase your commitment to a particular strategy or belief, especially when it is failing.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 더욱 집착하다, 삼중으로 강화하다, 고집을 꺾지 않다
meanings.example: Instead of apologizing, the CEO decided to triple down on his controversial policy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사과하는 대신, CEO는 자신의 논란이 되는 정책을 더욱 고집하기로(강화하기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To record something new over a previous recording; to cover something with tape.
meanings.example: I accidentally taped over our wedding video.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 실수로 우리 결혼식 비디오 위에 다른 것을 덮어썼다 (새로 녹화했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To use all the resources or effort possible to achieve something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 온 힘을 다하다, 아낌없이 쏟아붓다, 총동원하다
meanings.example: The company pulled out all stops to finish the project on time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 프로젝트를 제시간에 끝내기 위해 모든 노력을 아낌없이 쏟아부었다 (총동원했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To look extremely happy; to have a very wide smile.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 입이 귀에 걸리도록 웃다, 싱글벙글하다, 활짝 웃다
meanings.example: He was grinning from ear to ear when he heard the good news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 좋은 소식을 들었을 때 입이 귀에 걸리도록 웃고(싱글벙글하고) 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give someone a job or responsibility that is difficult or unpleasant and that they do not want.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 부담을 지우다, 책임을 떠안기다, (빚 등을) 지게 하다
meanings.example: I don't want to be saddled with the responsibility of organizing the whole event.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 행사 전체를 조직해야 하는 책임을 떠안고(부담을 지고) 싶지 않다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize someone's behavior, especially when you have no right to do so.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 비판하다, 심판하려 들다, 옳고 그름을 따지다
meanings.example: Who are you to sit in judgment of my life decisions?
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신이 누군데 내 인생의 결정에 대해 비판하려(심판하려) 드나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay a short, unannounced visit; to fall into a particular state (like sleep).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 잠시 들르다, (잠 등에) 빠져들다, 잠깐 방문하다
meanings.example: Why don't you drop into our office if you're in the neighborhood? / He dropped into a deep sleep.
meanings.exampleKorean: 근처에 오시면 저희 사무실에 잠시 들르지(방문하지) 그래요? / 그는 깊은 잠에 빠져들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To write something on a piece of paper that someone else is saying or that is written somewhere else.
meanings.example: The students were busy copying down the notes from the board.
meanings.exampleKorean: 학생들은 칠판에 있는 판서 내용을 받아 적느라(옮겨 적느라) 바빴다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To immediately come to a position of attention; to start behaving or working alertly and quickly.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 정신을 차리다, 재빨리 행동하다, 차렷 자세를 취하다
meanings.example: The soldiers snapped to attention when the general entered. / You need to snap to it if we're going to finish this on time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장군이 들어오자 병사들이 즉시 차렷 자세를 취했다. / 이것을 제시간에 끝내려면 정신 차리고 재빨리 행동해야(착수해야) 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrive somewhere unexpectedly or casually (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 불쑥 나타나다, 불어 들다, 예고 없이 방문하다
meanings.example: Look who just blew in! I haven't seen you in years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 누가 불쑥 나타났는지(방문했는지) 좀 봐! 몇 년 만에 보는 거네.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decay completely; to waste away in a prison or a neglected place.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 서서히 썩어 없어지다, 쇠약해지다, 인생을 허비하다
meanings.example: The old wooden fence is rotting away. / He was left to rot away in a dungeon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 낡은 나무 울타리가 서서히 썩어 없어지고 있다. / 그는 지하 감옥에서 인생을 허비하며 쇠약해지도록(썩어 없어지도록) 방치되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use an automated system in a shop to pay for your goods yourself.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 셀프 계산을 하다, 무인 계산기를 이용하다
meanings.example: I usually prefer to self-check out to save time at the supermarket.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 보통 슈퍼마켓에서 시간을 아끼기 위해 셀프 계산을 하는(무인 계산기를 이용하는) 것을 선호한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To buy something based on what you hear rather than on your own judgment or inspection.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 남의 말만 듣고 사다, 소문만 믿고 구매하다
meanings.example: Don't just buy with your ears; check the product reviews yourself before buying the car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그저 남의 말만 듣고 사지(소문만 믿고 구매하지) 마세요. 차를 사기 전에 직접 제품 리뷰를 확인하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To delay something or prevent someone from doing something for a period of time.
meanings.example: He managed to stall off the creditors for another month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 간신히 한 달 더 채권자들로부터 시간을 벌어(위기를 모면해) 냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bring attention to something, especially something that was previously hidden or ignored.
meanings.example: The documentary shines a light on the living conditions of refugees.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 다큐멘터리는 난민들의 생활 여건을 밝히고(거기에 관심을 끌게 하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dismiss something as being unimportant or not worthy of attention.
meanings.example: He waved aside my objections and continued with his plan.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 내 반대를 무시하고(일축하고) 자신의 계획을 계속 진행했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize someone very severely and suddenly (informal).
meanings.example: The boss jumped all over me for being just five minutes late.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상사는 내가 단 5분 늦었다는 이유로 나를 심하게 비난했다 (몰아붙였다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent others from having the same success or advantages that you have had.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 기회를 차단하다, (자신만 성공한 뒤) 사다리를 걷어차다
meanings.example: Once he reached the top management, he seemed to pull up the ladder behind him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 최고 경영진에 오르자마자, 뒤따라오는 사람들의 기회를 차단하는(사다리를 걷어차는) 것처럼 보였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept an offer with great enthusiasm; to react very angrily to someone.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 기회를 덥석 잡다, 열렬히 환영하다, 맹렬히 덤비다
meanings.example: If you offered him that job, he'd bite your hand off. / Don't bite my hand off just because I asked a question!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그에게 그 일자리를 제안한다면, 그는 덥석 잡을(열렬히 환영할) 거예요. / 그냥 질문 하나 했다고 나한테 그렇게 맹렬히 덤비지 마세요!

meanings.englishDefinition: To absorb liquid with a sponge; to take as much money or resources as possible from someone.
meanings.example: Use this cloth to sponge up the spilled milk. / He's just trying to sponge up all your inheritance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 천을 사용해서 쏟은 우유를 흡수하세요(닦아내세요). / 그는 그저 당신의 상속 재산을 모두 뜯어내려(빨아들이려) 애쓰고 있을 뿐이에요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To visit a place or encounter a situation by chance, without planning to.
meanings.example: I just happened by the bookstore and saw your sister.
meanings.exampleKorean: 서점에 우연히 들렀다가(지나가다가) 당신의 여동생을 보았어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To organize your thoughts, style, or performance so that you are effective (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 자신의 스타일을 완성하다, 정신 차리다, 준비를 갖추다
meanings.example: He really needs to get his shtick together before the talent show.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 장기 자랑 전에 정말로 자신의 스타일을 완성해야(준비해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To improve a painting or surface by adding small amounts of paint to particular areas.
meanings.example: I need to touch in the scratches on the car door.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자동차 문에 난 흠집들을 부분적으로 칠해야(보정해야) 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare to eat a meal, especially a large one (humorous, informal).
meanings.example: It's dinner time, let's strap on the feedbag!
meanings.exampleKorean: 저녁 시간이에요, 이제 본격적으로 식사합시다!

meanings.englishDefinition: To vote in favor of a proposal; to give a positive vote or recommendation to a post on the internet.
meanings.example: The committee voted up the new budget proposal. / Don't forget to vote up this comment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 위원회는 새로운 예산안에 찬성 투표했다. / 이 댓글에 추천 누르는(추천하는) 것을 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enclose or mark out an area with ropes.
meanings.example: The police roped out the crime scene to keep people away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 사람들의 접근을 막기 위해 범죄 현장에 밧줄을 쳐서 구획을 나누었다 (경계를 표시했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To learn as much as you can about a particular subject for a specific reason.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~에 대해 자세히 공부하다, 깊이 있게 조사하다
meanings.example: I need to study up on the company's history before my interview.
meanings.exampleKorean: 면접 전에 그 회사의 역사에 대해 자세히 공부해야(조사해야) 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to fight or argue in a very determined and fierce way, without showing any mercy.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 본격적으로 싸우다, 엄하게 대하다, 수단과 방법을 가리지 않다
meanings.example: The candidates have taken off their gloves and are attacking each other's records.
meanings.exampleKorean: 후보자들이 본격적인 싸움에 돌입하여 서로의 경력을 공격하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: (of a machine or device) To stop working suddenly while you are using it; to fail unexpectedly.
meanings.example: My phone died on me just as I was about to make an important call.
meanings.exampleKorean: 중요한 전화를 하려던 참에 내 전화기가 갑자기 작동을 멈췄다 (꺼졌다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean an area or make it tidy by picking up litter and putting things in order.
meanings.example: The soldiers were told to police up the barracks before the inspection.
meanings.exampleKorean: 병사들은 점검 전 내무실을 정리정돈하라는(청소하라는) 명령을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean a surface or person thoroughly by rubbing hard with a brush and water.
meanings.example: We had to scrub down the kitchen walls before painting them.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 페인트칠을 하기 전에 주방 벽을 박박 문질러 닦아야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To perform with a group of musicians as a guest; to attend a meeting or session as an observer.
meanings.example: He was invited to sit in with the jazz band for a few songs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 재즈 밴드와 함께 몇 곡을 합주하도록(참여하도록) 초대받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become an emperor or to achieve a very high social or political rank.
meanings.example: Few expected him to rise to the purple given his humble beginnings.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 미천한 시작을 고려할 때, 그가 고위직에 오를(출세할) 것이라고 예상한 사람은 거의 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: (of an aircraft) To touch the ground; to criticize or punish someone suddenly.
meanings.example: The plane landed on the runway safely. / The teacher landed on him for his constant talking.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비행기가 활주로에 안전하게 착륙했다. / 선생님은 그가 끊임없이 말을 한다는 이유로 그를 비난했다 (꾸짖었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove dust from the inside of something; to clean out thoroughly.
meanings.example: I need to dust out these old cupboards before putting the new plates in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 접시를 넣기 전에 이 낡은 찬장들의 먼지를 털어내야(깨끗이 치워야) 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move something using a shovel; to eat a lot of food quickly; to gather information in large quantities.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 삽으로 퍼 올리다, 허겁지겁 먹다, 마구 긁어모으다
meanings.example: The kids were shoveling up the snow into big piles. / He shoveled up the popcorn during the movie.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 눈을 삽으로 퍼 올려서 큰 더미를 만들고 있었다. / 그는 영화를 보는 동안 팝콘을 허겁지겁 먹었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To apply makeup to someone's face; to decorate or refresh something by painting it.
meanings.example: She painted herself up for the party. / We decided to paint up the old fence.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 파티를 위해 화장을 했다 (단장했다). / 우리는 낡은 울타리에 페인트칠을 하기(덧칠하기)로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually decrease the size or amount of something in stages.
meanings.example: The company decided to pyramid down its workforce over the next three years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 향후 3년에 걸쳐 인력을 점차 줄여나가기로(단계적으로 삭감하기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very friendly or polite to someone in order to get an advantage for yourself.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~에게 아첨하다, 환심을 사려 애쓰다, 잘 보이다
meanings.example: He's always trying to make up to the boss to get a promotion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 승진을 하기 위해 항상 상사에게 아첨하려(환심을 사려) 애쓴다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cut food into small square pieces.
meanings.example: Dice up the onions and sauté them in a pan.
meanings.exampleKorean: 양파를 깍둑썰기해서 팬에 볶으세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pass something from a lower position to a higher one; to deliver a legal verdict officially.
meanings.example: Could you hand up that hammer to me on the ladder? / The jury handed up a guilty verdict.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사다리 위에 있는 나에게 그 망치 좀 건네주겠어요? / 배심원단은 유죄 평결을 제출했다 (내렸다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To climb something quickly using your hands and feet; to mix something together in a disorganized way.
meanings.example: We scrambled up the steep hillside to see the view. / He scrambled up some eggs for breakfast.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 경치를 보기 위해 가파른 산비탈을 기어올랐다. / 그는 아침 식사로 계란 몇 개를 휘저어 섞었다 (스크램블했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To cook food slowly in liquid; to worry about something or keep thinking about something unpleasant.
meanings.example: She stewed up a delicious beef stew for dinner. / Don't sit there stewing up about what he said.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 저녁으로 맛있는 소고기 전골을 뭉근히 끓였다. / 그가 한 말에 대해 거기 앉아서 마음 졸이지(곱씹지) 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To engage in a very intense argument or conflict until a resolution is reached (slang).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 끝까지 싸우다, 결판을 내다, 격렬하게 논쟁하다
meanings.example: The two brothers decided to nuke it out in a long argument instead of ignoring the problem.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 형제는 문제를 무시하는 대신 긴 논쟁을 통해 끝까지 싸워 결판을 내기로(격렬하게 논쟁하기로) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force someone to retire because they are considered too old to work; to put someone in an unimportant position.
meanings.example: After thirty years of service, they just pastured him out to a desk job in the basement.
meanings.exampleKorean: 30년의 근무 끝에, 그들은 그를 그저 지하 사무실의 한직으로 보내버렸다 (은퇴시켰다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To start an engine or process; to leave a place quickly; to ignite a rocket or explosive.
meanings.example: They lit off for the coast as soon as the sun came up. / They are ready to light off the firework display.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 해가 뜨자마자 해안을 향해 서둘러 떠났다. / 그들은 불꽃놀이를 시작할(터뜨릴) 준비가 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make bread or other food brown and crisp by heating it; to make someone or yourself feel warm.
meanings.example: I'll toast up some muffins for breakfast. / Come sit by the fire and toast up your feet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아침 식사로 머핀을 좀 구워낼게요. / 불가에 앉아서 발을 좀 데우세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare vegetables by putting them briefly in boiling water; to become pale because of shock or fear.
meanings.example: Blanch up the spinach before adding it to the salad. / He blanched up when he heard the news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 샐러드에 넣기 전에 시금치를 데치세요. / 그는 소식을 듣고 얼굴이 하얗게 질렸다 (창백해졌다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To mix different ingredients together, especially using a blender; to combine different things successfully.
meanings.example: Blend up all the fruit to make a healthy smoothie.
meanings.exampleKorean: 건강한 스무디를 만들기 위해 모든 과일을 갈아서 섞으세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To burn the surface of something slightly; to dry out something with heat; to travel at very high speed.
meanings.example: The grass was scorched up by the intense summer sun. / The sports car scorched up the highway.
meanings.exampleKorean: 풀들이 강렬한 여름 태양에 바짝 말라버렸다(그슬렸다). / 스포츠카가 고속도로를 질주했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To buy or get a lot of something so that you have it when you need it; to eat a lot of a particular food.
meanings.example: We need to load up on groceries before the storm hits.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭풍이 오기 전에 식료품을 많이 사두어야(비축해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce a large number of people or things to a smaller group by choosing only the best ones.
meanings.example: The committee has winnowed the list of candidates down to three.
meanings.exampleKorean: 위원회는 후보자 명단을 3명으로 추려냈다 (범위를 좁혔다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To move quickly to a different place or topic; to make a decision or conclusion too quickly.
meanings.example: Don't jump to conclusions before you have all the facts.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 사실을 알기 전에 성급하게 결론을 내리지(서둘러 결론짓지) 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To step on something; to offend someone or violate their rights.
meanings.example: I'm sorry, I didn't mean to tread on your toes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 죄송합니다, 당신의 발을 밟으려던(당신의 기분을 상하게 하려던) 건 아니었어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give someone or something to another person, especially because you are forced to or it is expected of you.
meanings.example: The rebels were forced to deliver up their weapons.
meanings.exampleKorean: 반군들은 자신들의 무기를 공식적으로 인계해야(넘겨주어야) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave as badly as people expect you to.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 나쁜 평판에 부합하다, 실망시킨 대로 살다, 기대 이하로 행동하다
meanings.example: Unfortunately, the sequel lived down to its poor reviews.
meanings.exampleKorean: 불행히도, 그 속편은 좋지 않은 비평들에 딱 들어맞게 실망스러웠다 (나쁜 평판에 부합했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To cut something into several thin pieces; to divide something into smaller parts.
meanings.example: Could you slice up the bread for dinner? / The land was sliced up and sold to different developers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저녁 식사용으로 빵을 좀 얇게 썰어줄래요? / 그 땅은 여러 조각으로 쪼개져서 서로 다른 개발자들에게 팔렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To offer a lower price for something; to force the price of something down.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 가격을 깎다, 입찰가를 낮추다, 낮게 부르다
meanings.example: The buyer tried to bid the price down during the negotiation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 구매자는 협상 중에 가격을 깎으려(입찰가를 낮추려) 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To apply a layer of paint over a surface, often to cover something else.
meanings.example: They decided to paint over the graffiti on the wall.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 벽에 있는 낙서를 페인트로 덧칠하여 가리기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To add sugar to food or drink; to be very sweet or flattering to someone in order to please them (informal).
meanings.example: Don't sugar up the kids right before bedtime. / He's trying to sugar up the teacher to get a better grade.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자기 직전에 아이들에게 설탕이 든 음식을 잔뜩 주지(뿌려주지) 마세요. / 그는 더 좋은 점수를 받기 위해 선생님에게 감언이설을 하고(아부하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To buy something at an auction by making the highest bid.
meanings.example: She managed to bid off several rare books at the estate sale.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 유산 정리 경매에서 몇 권의 희귀 서적을 성공적으로 낙찰받았다 (사들였다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate or gather the best parts of something, especially by winnowing.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 키질하여 날리다, 정리하다, 가치 있는 것만 모으다
meanings.example: The wind helped him winnow up the grain. / He spent the afternoon winnowing up his research notes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 바람이 그가 곡식을 키질하여 날리는 것을 도와주었다. / 그는 오후 내내 자신의 연구 노트를 정리하여 가치 있는 것만 모으는 데 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To escape from a difficult situation or a heavy burden, such as debt or responsibility.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 곤경에서 벗어나다, 빚을 갚다, 책임을 면하다
meanings.example: It took him years to get out from under all that debt.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 모든 빚에서 벗어나는(빚을 갚는) 데 수년이 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To depend on something for success or a particular result; to continue a journey.
meanings.example: A lot of money is riding on this deal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 계약에 아주 많은 돈이 달려있다 (걸려있다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop liking or being interested in someone or something (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~에 싫증이 나다, 관심을 잃다, 마음이 돌아서다
meanings.example: I've soured on the idea of moving to the city.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 도시로 이사하겠다는 생각에 싫증이 났다 (관심을 잃었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To attack or criticize someone vigorously; to work energetically at something for a long time (informal).
meanings.example: The boxer kept whaling away at his opponent. / She's been whaling away at that report all morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 복서는 상대방을 맹렬히 공격했다. / 그녀는 아침 내내 그 보고서 작성에 매진하고(열심히 일하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone or something look clean, neat, and attractive (informal).
meanings.example: He slicked himself up for the job interview. / We need to slick up the living room before the guests arrive.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 취업 면접을 위해 말끔하게 단장했다. / 손님들이 오기 전에 거실을 정돈해야(치워야) 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sentence someone to a particular punishment; to force someone to experience something very unpleasant for a long time.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~한 처지에 처하게 하다, 형을 선고하다, 운명지어지다
meanings.example: He was condemned to life imprisonment. / Poor education condemns many people to a life of poverty.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 무기징역을 선고받았다. / 열악한 교육은 많은 사람을 평생 빈곤한 처지에 처하게 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To betray someone by selling something that they need or rely on, often for your own profit.
meanings.example: The partner sold the business out from under him while he was away on vacation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 휴가를 떠난 사이 동료가 그를 배신하고 사업체를 팔아넘겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove a piece of clothing quickly; to get rid of something that is no longer wanted or useful.
meanings.example: He shucked off his coat as soon as he entered the warm house. / She managed to shuck off her shy image.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 따뜻한 집에 들어오자마자 코트를 벗어 던졌다. / 그녀는 자신의 내성적인 이미지에서 성공적으로 벗어났다 (이미지를 탈피했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To connect or link something to something else.
meanings.example: The bonus is tied to the company's annual performance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 보너스는 회사의 연간 실적과 결부되어(관련되어) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gather up a large amount of something quickly, especially money or prizes.
meanings.example: The movie scooped in several awards at the film festival.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 영화는 영화제에서 여러 개의 상을 긁어모았다 (한꺼번에 얻었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To become less interesting or enjoyable because it has been experienced too many times.
meanings.example: The constant parties began to pall on him after a few months.
meanings.exampleKorean: 끊임없는 파티들이 몇 달 뒤에는 그에게 싫증 나기(물리기) 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move something out of a narrow or difficult space carefully; to move like a snake.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 교묘하게 빠져나가다, 끌어내다, (길 등이) 구불구불 뻗다
meanings.example: The tow truck managed to snake the car out of the narrow alley.
meanings.exampleKorean: 견인차는 그 차를 좁은 골목에서 교묘하게 끌어냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To say something to express your opinion or to say that you have noticed something.
meanings.example: Several people remarked on how much she looked like her mother.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몇몇 사람들은 그녀가 자신의 어머니와 얼마나 닮았는지에 대해 언급했다 (의견을 말했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue walking past someone or something without stopping or showing any interest.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 그냥 지나치다, 모른 척 지나가다, 무시하다
meanings.example: He saw the homeless man asking for help but just walked on by.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 노숙자가 도움을 요청하는 것을 보았지만 그냥 모른 척 지나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To not use or give all of something; to hesitate to do or say something.
meanings.example: The committee decided to hold back on making a final decision until next month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 위원회는 다음 달까지 최종 결정을 내리는 것을 자제하기로(망설이기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin doing something; to start criticizing or complaining to someone (informal).
meanings.example: I'll start on the laundry after lunch. / Oh no, don't start on me about my messy room again!
meanings.exampleKorean: 점심 먹고 빨래를 시작할게요. / 오 안 돼, 내 지저분한 방에 대해 또 비난하기 시작하지(잔소리하지) 마세요!

meanings.englishDefinition: To become hard or firm; to become stronger and more resilient, often through difficult experiences.
meanings.example: Wait for the cement to harden up before walking on it. / The young recruits need to harden up during basic training.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시멘트가 완전히 굳어질(단단해질) 때까지 걷지 말고 기다리세요. / 신병들은 기초 훈련 기간 동안 정신적, 육체적으로 더 강해져야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To walk to a lower level; to catch someone or something by pursuing them until they are exhausted.
meanings.example: He slowly walked down the long spiral staircase. / The hunter managed to walk down the wounded deer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 긴 나선형 계단을 천천히 걸어서 내려갔다. / 사냥꾼은 부상당한 사슴을 끈질기게 추격하여 마침내 잡았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dry or clean something by dabbing; to greet someone with a friendly gesture like a fist bump (slang).
meanings.example: Use a tissue to dab up the spilled water. / He dabbed up all his friends when he arrived at the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 휴지를 사용해 쏟은 물을 가볍게 두드려 닦으세요. / 그는 파티에 도착했을 때 모든 친구와 주먹을 맞대며(반갑게) 인사했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move past very quickly; (of time) to pass very quickly.
meanings.example: The racing cars zoomed by the spectators. / The whole summer just zoomed by.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경주용 자동차들이 관객들 옆을 빠르게 지나갔다. / 여름 내내가 그냥 순식간에 지나가 버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hang something up using a rope or strap; to put up or build something quickly or roughly.
meanings.example: They slung up a hammock between the two trees.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 두 나무 사이에 해먹을 (급히) 매달았다 (설치했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove a stain or liquid by dabbing it with something absorbent.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 가볍게 눌러 지우다, 톡톡 두드려 닦다
meanings.example: Dab out the wine stain carefully so it doesn't spread.
meanings.exampleKorean: 와인 얼룩이 번지지 않도록 조심스럽게 톡톡 두드려 닦으세요(지우세요).

meanings.englishDefinition: To act in a cowardly or overly sentimental/cheesy way (slang).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 유치하게 행동하다, 겁먹고 그만두다, 오글거리게 하다
meanings.example: He was going to jump off the diving board but cheesed out at the last second.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 다이빙대에서 뛰어내리려 했으나 마지막 순간에 겁먹고 포기했다 (그만두었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To hit someone with the front of your head; to have a strong disagreement or conflict with someone.
meanings.example: He got into a fight and headbutted the other guy. / He often headbutts with his colleagues over minor details.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 싸움에 휘말려 상대방을 머리로 들이받았다. / 그는 사소한 세부 사항을 두고 동료들과 자주 충돌한다 (격렬하게 논쟁한다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To have information or knowledge about something or someone.
meanings.example: Do you know about the changes to the schedule?
meanings.exampleKorean: 일정 변경 사항에 대해 알고 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin the return journey to a place.
meanings.example: We should start back before it gets dark.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어두워지기 전에 되돌아가기 시작해야(귀로에 올라야) 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hang something loosely over your shoulder or something else.
meanings.example: She slung her bag over her shoulder and left.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 가방을 어깨에 메고(걸치고) 떠났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lower your body close to the ground; to crouch down low.
meanings.example: The hunters couched down in the tall grass.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사냥꾼들은 긴 풀숲 속에 몸을 숙였다 (웅크렸다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To cast light from above onto something or someone.
meanings.example: The sun was shining down on the peaceful valley.
meanings.exampleKorean: 태양이 평화로운 계곡을 내리쬐고(비추고) 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pull something up with a sudden, sharp movement; to raise one's head suddenly.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 홱 끌어올리다, 번쩍 들다, 갑자기 올리다
meanings.example: He jerked up the anchor and started the engine. / She jerked her head up when she heard her name.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 닻을 홱 끌어올리고 엔진을 걸었다. / 그녀는 자신의 이름이 들리자 고개를 번쩍 들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To add pepper to food; to make something more exciting or lively (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 활기를 불어넣다, 흥미롭게 만들다, 후추를 뿌리다
meanings.example: The comedian's jokes really peppered up the dull party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 코미디언의 농담이 지루한 파티에 정말 활기를 불어넣었다 (흥미롭게 만들었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To shave someone or oneself very cleanly; to smooth a surface by shaving small pieces off.
meanings.example: He needs to shave up before the formal dinner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 정식 저녁 식사 전에 깔끔하게 면도해야(다듬어야) 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mark or divide something using lines.
meanings.example: The workers lined off the parking spaces in the new lot.
meanings.exampleKorean: 인부들이 새 주차장의 주차 구역을 선을 그어 나누었다 (구획을 표시했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To cover food with breadcrumbs before cooking; to leave a trail of clues or navigation paths.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 빵가루를 입히다, 흔적을 남기다, 경로를 표시하다
meanings.example: Breadcrumb up the fish fillets before frying. / The website design breadcrumbs up the user's path for easy navigation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 튀기기 전에 생선 필레에 빵가루를 입히세요. / 그 웹사이트 디자인은 쉬운 탐색을 위해 사용자의 경로를 표시하고(흔적을 남기고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To think carefully or use one's brain to solve a problem (slang); to catch fish with one's bare hands.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 머리를 써서 생각하다, 지혜를 짜내다, 맨손으로 고기를 잡다
meanings.example: We need to noodle up a way to reduce our expenses. / They spent the afternoon noodling up catfish in the river.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 지출을 줄일 방법을 머리를 써서 생각해야(지혜를 짜내야) 합니다. / 그들은 오후 내내 강에서 맨손으로 메기를 잡으며 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue to shine; to ignore someone or their ideas (slang).
meanings.example: The lighthouse continues to shine on through the storm. / Just shine on him; he doesn't know what he's talking about.
meanings.exampleKorean: 등대는 폭풍 속에서도 계속해서 빛나고 있다. / 그냥 그를 무시하세요(가볍게 넘기세요). 그는 자기가 무슨 말을 하는지도 몰라요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be performing, showing, or happening.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 상영 중이다, 방송 중이다, 진행 중이다
meanings.example: What's on at the cinema tonight? / The local festival is on this weekend.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 밤 영화관에서 무엇을 상영하나요? / 이번 주말에 지역 축제가 열립니다 (진행 중입니다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a good feeling about a person or a situation; to enjoy something (slang).
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~와 마음이 맞다, 분위기가 통하다, 잘 어울리다
meanings.example: I really vibe with the music in this cafe. / We just didn't vibe with each other.
