meanings.exampleKorean: 이 카페 음악이 내 취향에 정말 딱 맞아요. / 우리는 그냥 서로 마음이(분위기가) 잘 안 맞았어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give all your support or energy to an idea, plan, or person.
meanings.example: The whole team threw themselves behind the new project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 팀 전체가 새로운 프로젝트를 전폭적으로 지지했다 (전력을 다해 도왔다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To move away by turning over and over; (of clouds or fog) to disappear; (of a feeling) to fade.
meanings.example: The ball rolled away into the street. / The morning mist slowly rolled away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공이 도로 쪽으로 굴러가 버렸다. / 아침 안개가 서서히 걷혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste time or play with something without a clear purpose (informal).
meanings.example: Stop futzing with the radio and just listen to the music. / I spent all afternoon futzing with the computer settings.
meanings.exampleKorean: 라디오 좀 그만 만지작거리고 그냥 음악이나 들으세요. / 나는 오후 내내 컴퓨터 설정을 만지작거리며 시간을 낭비했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep complaining to someone or keep asking them to do something (informal).
meanings.example: My parents are always on at me to clean my room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 부모님은 항상 나에게 방 청소하라고 잔소리하신다 (보채신다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To match or look good together with something else in terms of color or style.
meanings.example: The curtains really tone with the color of the sofa.
meanings.exampleKorean: 커튼이 소파 색상과 정말 잘 어우러진다 (조화를 이룬다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To give someone an unpleasant or difficult task or responsibility unexpectedly.
meanings.example: He quit his job and landed me with all his unfinished projects.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 직장을 그만두면서 나에게 그의 끝나지 않은 모든 프로젝트를 떠맡겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something stiff or rigid; to become more severe or strict.
meanings.example: My back always stiffs up after a long flight. / The government decided to stiff up the penalties for drug offenses.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장시간 비행 후에는 항상 허리가 뻣뻣해진다. / 정부는 마약 범죄에 대한 처벌을 더욱 강경하게 하기로(강화하기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drink coffee, especially in order to become more alert or energetic (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 커피를 마셔 정신을 차리다, 커피를 마시다
meanings.example: I need to coffee up before I can start working on this report.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 보고서를 시작하기 전에 커피를 마셔 정신을 좀 차려야겠어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make small cuts or scratches on the surface of something.
meanings.example: Be careful not to nick up the table while you're moving it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 테이블을 옮길 때 흠집을 내지 않도록 조심하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrange words so that they rhyme; to make something rhyme.
meanings.example: He spent all night trying to rhyme up the verses of his new song.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 밤새도록 그의 새 노래 가사들의 운을 맞추려(리듬을 만드려) 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove dirt or mess that someone else has made.
meanings.example: I'm tired of always having to clean up after the kids.
meanings.exampleKorean: 항상 아이들의 뒤처리를 해야(어지럽힌 것을 치워줘야) 하는 것에 지쳤어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone or something more likely to fail or be rejected.
meanings.example: His lack of experience will probably count against him in the interview.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 경험 부족이 면접에서 아마 그에게 불리하게 작용할(불리한 점이 될) 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take care of or be responsible for someone or something.
meanings.example: Could you see after my plants while I'm away on vacation?
meanings.exampleKorean: 제가 휴가 간 동안 제 식물들을 좀 돌봐줄(보살펴줄) 수 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To protest or resist something, especially a rule or authority.
meanings.example: Teenagers often kick against the rules imposed by their parents.
meanings.exampleKorean: 십 대들은 종종 부모님이 강요하는 규칙에 저항하곤(반대하곤) 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To search for something by looking in various places or by asking questions.
meanings.example: I had to dig around in the attic to find our old family albums. / The reporters are digging around for any information on the scandal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가족 앨범을 찾으려고 다락방을 이곳저곳 뒤져야 했다. / 기자들이 스캔들에 대한 어떤 정보라도 캐내기(수소문하기) 위해 애쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend time in a relaxed, lazy, or aimless way.
meanings.example: We spent the whole Sunday just lolling around the house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 일요일 내내 집에서 그냥 빈둥거리며(느긋하게 쉬며) 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deceive someone successfully; to communicate an idea effectively (informal).
meanings.example: He tried to put one across on me, but I knew he was lying. / He's very good at putting his ideas across.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 나를 속이려 했지만, 나는 그가 거짓말하는 걸 알고 있었다. / 그는 자신의 생각을 아주 잘 전달한다 (성공시킨다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place or continue a journey (informal).
meanings.example: It's getting late, I'd better be pushing along. / The guard pushed the crowd along to keep the path clear.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시간이 늦었네요, 이제 서둘러 가봐야(자리를 떠야)겠어요. / 경비원은 길을 비우기 위해 군중을 재촉했다 (밀어냈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To move around a place in a very busy or excited way.
meanings.example: Waiters were buzzing about the dining room, serving guests.
meanings.exampleKorean: 웨이터들이 손님들을 서빙하며 식당 안을 분주히 돌아다니고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To offer or give something to each person in a group.
meanings.example: Could you hand these leaflets around to the students?
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 전단들을 학생들에게 나누어줄(돌려줄) 수 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To apply or compare information from one context to another similar context.
meanings.example: We can read across the results from the first study to this new project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 첫 번째 연구 결과를 이 새로운 프로젝트에 수평적으로 적용하여 이해할 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a silly or playful way for fun (informal).
meanings.example: The boys were larking around in the pool all afternoon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 소년들은 오후 내내 수영장에서 장난치며 놀았다 (우스꽝스러운 짓을 하며 시간을 보냈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To make arrangements for something before it happens.
meanings.example: If you want the party to be a success, you need to plan ahead.
meanings.exampleKorean: 파티를 성공시키고 싶다면, 미리 계획을 세워야(사전에 준비해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have to deal with a problem or difficulty.
meanings.example: The project came up against several unexpected legal hurdles.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 프로젝트는 몇 가지 예상치 못한 법적 난관에 직면했다 (부딪혔다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be in the future; to be going to happen soon.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 앞에 놓여 있다, 기다리고 있다, (미래에) 닥치다
meanings.example: We don't know what difficulties lie ahead for the company.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사 앞에 어떤 어려움이 놓여 있을지(기다리고 있을지) 우리는 알지 못한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to persuade someone to act logically or change their mind by using sensible arguments.
meanings.example: It's impossible to reason with him when he's in such a bad mood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 그렇게 기분이 안 좋을 때는 그를 설득하는(논리적으로 따지는) 것이 불가능하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tell someone what to do in a rude or bossy way.
meanings.example: I'm tired of him ordering me about all the time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그가 항상 나에게 이래라저래라 명령하는(부려먹는) 것에 질렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To appear as a large, often frightening or unclear shape or as a large and important event in the future.
meanings.example: The final exams were looming ahead, and I was starting to panic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기말고사가 눈앞에 닥쳐오고 있었고(어렴풋이 보이기 시작했고), 나는 겁이 나기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move aimlessly or to live without any particular plan or purpose.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 느긋하게 흘러가다, 정처 없이 떠돌다, 목표 없이 살다
meanings.example: He's just been drifting along since he lost his job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 직장을 잃은 이후로 그냥 목표 없이 살아가고(정처 없이 떠돌고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay money that you owe; to deal with someone who has treated you badly.
meanings.example: I'll settle with the waiter and meet you outside. / I have an old score to settle with him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 웨이터와 계산을 끝내고(결제하고) 밖에서 만나요. / 나는 그에게 해결해야 할(복수해야 할) 오랜 원한이 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give someone an unwanted responsibility or job (informal).
meanings.example: I'm sorry to lumber you with all these extra tasks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신에게 이 모든 추가 업무의 부담을 지워서(떠맡겨서) 미안해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come near or move closer to someone; to reach an agreement; to engage in a close fight.
meanings.example: The ship closed with the enemy vessel. / We are ready to close with their offer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 배는 적선에 바짝 다가갔다 (가까워졌다). / 우리는 그들의 제안에 대해 협상을 매듭지을(합의할) 준비가 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give secret information to the media that is critical of a person or their ideas.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 은밀히 정보를 흘려 비판하다, 명성을 훼손하다
meanings.example: There were rumors that his rivals were briefing against him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 경쟁자들이 그에 대해 은밀히 정보를 흘려 비판하고(명성을 훼손하고) 있다는 소문이 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move forward or develop with great strength and speed.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 강력하게 추진하다, 힘차게 나아가다, 빠르게 발전하다
meanings.example: Despite the economic downturn, the company continued to power ahead with its expansion plans.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경제 침체에도 불구하고, 회사는 확장 계획을 강력하게 추진했다 (힘차게 나아갔다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate or break into many pieces; to end a relationship or organization.
meanings.example: The ship began to break apart on the rocks. / The band broke apart after their final tour.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배가 바위에 부딪혀 산산조각이 나기 시작했다. / 그 밴드는 마지막 투어 이후에 헤어졌다 (해체되었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To move something or someone out of the way; to refuse to consider something or someone.
meanings.example: He pushed aside his plate and stood up. / She tried to push aside her fears and concentrate on the task.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 접시를 옆으로 밀어내고 일어났다. / 그녀는 두려움을 무시하고(제쳐놓고) 업무에 집중하려 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To close one eye briefly as a signal; to pretend not to notice something wrong.
meanings.example: The guard winked at the children as they sneaked in. / The government seems to wink at these minor tax evasions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들이 몰래 들어올 때 경비원이 윙크를 했다. / 정부는 이러한 사소한 탈세들을 눈감아주는(묵인하는) 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat food very quickly.
meanings.example: He bolted down his breakfast and ran for the bus.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아침 식사를 허겁지겁 먹고 버스를 타기 위해 달려갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To teach something to someone by repeating it many times.
meanings.example: Our parents drummed into us the importance of honesty.
meanings.exampleKorean: 부모님은 우리에게 정직의 중요성을 반복해서 주입하셨다 (머리에 박히게 가르치셨다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To discuss something in detail, especially something that has already been discussed before (informal).
meanings.example: We spent hours hashing over the details of the agreement.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 합의안의 세부 사항들을 충분히 논의하며(되짚어 보며) 몇 시간을 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To not consider or include something for the time being.
meanings.example: Leaving aside the cost for a moment, let's look at the benefits.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비용 문제는 잠시 제쳐두고(고려하지 말고), 이점에 대해 살펴봅시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To involve someone in a situation or argument that they do not want to be part of.
meanings.example: Please don't drag me into your personal arguments.
meanings.exampleKorean: 제발 당신의 개인적인 논쟁에 나를 억지로 끌어들이지(연루시키지) 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate or distinguish things that are closely connected or mixed together.
meanings.example: Researchers are trying to tease apart the various factors that cause the disease.
meanings.exampleKorean: 연구자들은 그 질병을 일으키는 다양한 요인들을 세밀하게 분석하여(가려내려) 애쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay for something, especially as a treat for someone else (informal).
meanings.example: My dad offered to pop for dinner tonight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아빠가 오늘 저녁 식사를 한턱내겠다고(지불하겠다고) 하셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tear something into many pieces; to destroy a relationship or group completely.
meanings.example: The storm ripped the old tent apart. / The war has ripped many families apart.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭풍이 낡은 텐트를 갈기갈기 찢어버렸다. / 전쟁은 많은 가족을 무참히 갈라놓았다 (파괴했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To give a concrete form to an abstract idea or imagination; to represent clearly.
meanings.example: The poet bodies forth his inner feelings through vivid imagery.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 시인은 생생한 이미지를 통해 자신의 내면적 감정을 구체화한다 (드러낸다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To look through a book, magazine, etc. quickly by turning the pages with your thumb.
meanings.example: I spent the afternoon thumbing through some old fashion magazines.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 오후 내내 오래된 패션 잡지 몇 권을 휙휙 넘겨보며(훑어보며) 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tie or group things together; to sell different products or services as a single package.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 한데 묶다, 끼워 팔다, 일괄 처리하다
meanings.example: The software is often bundled together with new computers. / We bundled the old newspapers together with string.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 소프트웨어는 종종 새 컴퓨터와 한데 묶여(끼워 팔기로) 제공된다. / 우리는 끈으로 낡은 신문들을 한데 묶었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually become less close to someone over time.
meanings.example: Since they went to different colleges, the two friends have started to grow away from each other.
meanings.exampleKorean: 서로 다른 대학에 간 이후로, 그 두 친구는 서로 서서히 멀어지기(소원해지기) 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To link or match something in one area with something in another area.
meanings.example: The researchers tried to map the brain activity onto specific physical movements.
meanings.exampleKorean: 연구자들은 뇌 활동을 특정 신체 움직임에 대응시키려(연결하려) 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get into a car or other vehicle quickly; to get into bed or a bath quickly.
meanings.example: Hop into the car, we're going to be late! / I can't wait to hop into a hot bath.
meanings.exampleKorean: 차에 빨리 타세요, 늦겠어요! / 빨리 따뜻한 욕조에 쏙 들어가고 싶네요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move quickly through a place; to spend money very fast (informal).
meanings.example: The storm blew through the town in just an hour. / He blew through his inheritance in less than a year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭풍이 단 한 시간 만에 마을을 통과해 지나갔다. / 그는 1년도 채 되지 않아 유산을 다 써버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decide on something; to look at someone or something with all your attention.
meanings.example: They finally fixed on a date for the wedding. / Her eyes were fixed on the screen.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 마침내 결혼식 날짜를 정했다. / 그녀의 시선은 화면에 고정되어 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move very slowly and carefully away from someone or something.
meanings.example: The cat started to inch away from the barking dog.
meanings.exampleKorean: 고양이는 짖는 개로부터 조금씩 멀어지기(살금살금 물러나기) 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To persuade someone to support you or join your side.
meanings.example: The politician managed to gain over several members of the opposing party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인은 야당 의원 몇 명을 자기편으로 끌어들이는(포섭하는) 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To travel to a place by plane; to suddenly enter a particular emotional state.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 갑자기 ~한 상태가 되다, 날아 들다, (비행기로) 가다
meanings.example: He flew into a rage when he saw the broken vase.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 깨진 꽃병을 보자 갑자기 격분한 상태가 되었다 (화가 폭발했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To move close together to form a tight group.
meanings.example: The sheep bunched together to keep warm.
meanings.exampleKorean: 양들이 온기를 유지하기 위해 다닥다닥 모여(뭉쳐) 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: (of an engine) To start working; to overthrow or overturn something.
meanings.example: The old car engine finally kicked over. / They wanted to kick over the old system of government.
meanings.exampleKorean: 낡은 차 엔진이 마침내 시동이 걸렸다. / 그들은 낡은 정부 체제를 뒤집어엎고(전복시키고) 싶어 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a safe place in a brave or confident way in order to do something.
meanings.example: Early the next morning, we sallied forth to explore the city.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다음 날 아침 일찍, 우리는 도시를 탐험하기 위해 기운차게 나섰다 (출발했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To ignore or exclude specific details in order to focus on general principles or patterns.
meanings.example: When building the model, we decided to abstract away from the minor economic fluctuations.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모델을 만들 때, 우리는 사소한 경제적 변동들은 도외시하기로(제외하기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hit something or someone by accident; to meet someone unexpectedly (informal).
meanings.example: I was looking at my phone and banged into a lamppost. / I banged into an old school friend at the mall.
meanings.exampleKorean: 휴대폰을 보다가 가로등에 쾅 부딪혔다. / 쇼핑몰에서 옛 학교 친구를 우연히 만났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide a large amount of money, time, or effort to something.
meanings.example: The government is pouring money into the new high-speed rail project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 새로운 고속철도 프로젝트에 거액의 돈을 쏟아붓고(투자하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fight or compete very hard until someone wins (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 격렬하게 싸우다, 결판을 내다, 끝장 승부를 내다
meanings.example: The two companies are slugging it out for market dominance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 회사는 시장 지배력을 놓고 격렬하게 싸우고(결판을 내고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove dirt or knots from something by using a brush.
meanings.example: She carefully brushed out the tangles in her long hair. / You can easily brush out that dried mud from your pants.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 긴 머리의 엉킨 부분을 조심스럽게 빗질하여 폈다. / 바지에 묻은 마른 진흙은 솔로 쉽게 제거할(털어낼) 수 있어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To brush your hair away from your face; (in baseball) to throw a pitch close to a batter to make them move back.
meanings.example: She brushed her hair back from her forehead. / The pitcher tried to brush back the aggressive hitter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 이마에서 머리카락을 뒤로 넘겨 빗었다. / 투수는 공격적인 타자를 위협하기 위해 타자 가까이 공을 던졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gather or collect something together indiscriminately or in a hurry.
meanings.example: The general sharked up a band of lawless resolutes for the expedition.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장군은 원정을 위해 무법자 집단을 닥치는 대로 긁어모았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize or scold someone very harshly (slang).
meanings.example: There's no need to harsh down on him for a simple mistake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 단순한 실수 때문에 그를 그렇게 호되게 꾸짖을(비난할) 필요는 없어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get control of the whole supply of something; to drive someone into a corner.
meanings.example: The speculator tried to corner up the wheat market.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 투기꾼은 밀 시장을 매점매석하려(싹쓸이하려) 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hesitate to do something; to keep at a distance.
meanings.example: He hung off from joining the conversation. / The ship hung off the coast waiting for a signal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 대화에 끼어드는 것을 주저했다 (거리감을 두었다). / 그 배는 신호를 기다리며 해안에서 일정 거리를 유지한 채 머물렀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use charm to please or influence someone; to brighten up a place or person.
meanings.example: She tried to charm up the grumpy receptionist. / A few new cushions really charmed up the living room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 퉁명스러운 접수원의 마음을 사로잡으려(기쁘게 하려) 애썼다. / 새 쿠션 몇 개가 거실 분위기를 정말 화사하게 단장해(밝혀) 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare yourself for something difficult or unpleasant by becoming stronger or more determined.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 마음을 단단히 먹다, 강하게 만들다, 대비하다
meanings.example: He steeled himself up before delivering the bad news to his family.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 가족들에게 나쁜 소식을 전하기 전에 마음을 단단히 먹었다 (대비했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To become covered with a thick, hard layer of something.
meanings.example: The mud had caked up on his boots after the hike.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하이킹 후에 그의 장화에는 진흙이 두껍게 덮여(딱딱하게 굳어) 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tell someone what to do in a rude or bossy way.
meanings.example: Stop ordering me around; I don't work for you!
meanings.exampleKorean: 나한테 이래라저래라 명령하지(나를 부려먹지) 마세요. 난 당신 부하가 아니에요!

meanings.englishDefinition: To crush or crumple something; to tighten the muscles of your face, for example because of bright light.
meanings.example: He scrunched up the letter and threw it in the bin. / She scrunched up her face in disgust.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 편지를 구겨서(뭉쳐서) 쓰레기통에 던졌다. / 그녀는 혐오감에 얼굴을 찡그렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To raise a flag; to increase the amount of a debt or bill quickly.
meanings.example: They ran up a huge bill at the hotel. / Run it up the flagpole and see who salutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 호텔에서 거액의 청구 금액을 쌓았다(늘렸다). / 깃발을 올려서 누가 경례하는지(반응을 보는지) 봅시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To agree to plead guilty to a less serious charge in exchange for a lighter sentence.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 유죄 답변으로 감형받다, 가벼운 죄목을 인정하다
meanings.example: His lawyer managed to get the felony charge pleaded down to a misdemeanor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 변호사는 중죄 혐의를 경범죄로 인정하여 감형받는 데(합의하는 데) 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become thinner; to narrow; to climb up a pole or tree.
meanings.example: The path skinnies up as you get closer to the summit. / The boy managed to skinny up the tree to get his kite.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정상에 가까워질수록 길은 좁아진다. / 그 소년은 연을 찾기 위해 나무를 기어오르는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase or expand resources flexibly; to show off one's muscles or power.
meanings.example: The company needs to flex up its customer support team during the holiday season.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 휴가철 동안 고객 지원팀 인력을 유연하게 확장할(늘릴) 필요가 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take a defensive position; to withdraw or protect oneself.
meanings.example: The defense team decided to turtle down and wait for the opponent's mistake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수비팀은 방어 자세를 취하고 상대의 실수를 기다리기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become dark; (of a person's face) to show anger or worry.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 어둡게 하다, 낯빛이 어두워지다, 화가 나다
meanings.example: His face darkened up as soon as he heard the news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 소식을 듣자마자 그의 낯빛이 어두워졌다 (화난 기색을 띠었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin doing something; to start speaking to someone in an angry or critical way.
meanings.example: Before I could explain, she started into me for being late.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 설명하기도 전에, 그녀는 내가 늦은 것에 대해 나를 공격적으로 비난하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become tangled or messy like spaghetti; to make a situation complicated (informal).
meanings.example: The cables behind the TV have all spaghettied up.
meanings.exampleKorean: TV 뒤의 케이블들이 모두 복잡하게 엉켜버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become thinner; to reduce the size, amount, or number of something.
meanings.example: He's really skinnied down since he started running. / The company decided to skinny down its marketing budget.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 달리기를 시작한 이후로 정말 날씬해졌다. / 회사는 마케팅 예산을 축소하기로(줄이기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fasten something firmly; to make rules or control more strict.
meanings.example: Tighten down the lid so the liquid doesn't leak. / The government is tightening down on illegal immigration.
meanings.exampleKorean: 액체가 새지 않도록 뚜껑을 단단히 조이세요. / 정부는 불법 이민에 대한 통제를 엄격하게 강화하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find or produce something quickly; to make someone move or work faster (informal).
meanings.example: See if you can hustle up some volunteers for the event. / Hustle up, or we'll miss the train!
meanings.exampleKorean: 행사를 위해 자원봉사자들을 좀 서둘러 마련해(찾아봐) 주세요. / 서두르세요, 안 그러면 기차를 놓칠 거예요!

meanings.englishDefinition: To add together several numbers or amounts to find the total.
meanings.example: I need to total up all my expenses for the month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이번 달의 모든 지출 합계를 내야(총계를 구해야) 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pull something out of water; to find something by searching carefully.
meanings.example: He fished up an old boot from the river. / She managed to fish up some old photographs from the drawer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 강에서 낡은 장화 하나를 끌어올렸다. / 그녀는 서랍에서 오래된 사진 몇 장을 찾아내는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something shorter in length or time.
meanings.example: We had to shorten up the meeting because of the emergency. / He shortened up his grip on the bat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비상사태 때문에 회의 시간을 단축해야(줄여야) 했다. / 그는 배트의 그립을 짧게 잡았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find the answer to a problem by using numbers or reasoning.
meanings.example: We need to calculate out the total cost of the project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 프로젝트의 총비용을 계산해내야(산출해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something longer in length or duration.
meanings.example: The shadows lengthened out as the sun began to set. / They decided to lengthen out the holiday by two days.
meanings.exampleKorean: 해가 지기 시작하자 그림자가 길게 늘어났다. / 그들은 휴가 기간을 이틀 더 연장하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become or make something stronger and more effective.
meanings.example: You need to strengthen up your core muscles. / The team strenthened up its defense in the second half.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신은 코어 근육을 강화해야(기러야) 합니다. / 그 팀은 후반전에 수비를 더욱 견고히 했다 (강화했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To become wider or to make something wider; to increase the range of something.
meanings.example: The river widens up as it flows toward the sea. / We need to widen up our search for a new office.
meanings.exampleKorean: 강은 바다를 향해 흐르면서 폭이 넓어진다. / 우리는 새 사무실을 찾기 위한 검색 범위를 넓혀야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To extend or prolong something as much as possible; to use resources sparingly to make them last longer.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 충분히 늘리다, 최대한 활용하다, 오래 끌다
meanings.example: He tried to stretch it out so the money would last until the end of the month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 돈이 월말까지 버틸 수 있도록 최대한 아껴서 활용하려(오래 끌려) 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something overly sentimental or emotional in an exaggerated way (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 지나치게 감상적으로 만들다, 뻔한 신파로 만들다
meanings.example: The director decided to schmaltz up the ending to please the audience.
meanings.exampleKorean: 감독은 관객들을 만족시키기 위해 결말을 지나치게 감상적으로(뻔한 신파로) 만들기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To need something very much or very obviously.
meanings.example: The dirty windows are screaming for a clean.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 더러운 창문들은 정말로 청소가 절실히 필요하다 (청소해달라고 아우성치는 것 같다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To write something quickly and untidily.
meanings.example: I scrawled down his phone number on a scrap of paper.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 종이 조각에 그의 전화번호를 휘갈겨 썼다 (대충 적었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To put something down with great force and noise; to criticize someone or something severely.
meanings.example: He slammed down the phone in anger. / The critics slammed down the actor's performance in the new play.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 화가 나서 수화기를 쾅 내려놓았다. / 비평가들은 그 새로운 연극에서 그 배우의 연기를 강하게 비난했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sleep in a place that is not your own home, or in a temporary bed (informal).
meanings.example: You can bunk down on the sofa for tonight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 밤은 소파에서 잠자리에 들어도(자도) 괜찮아요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To frighten someone so much that they do something they would not normally do.
meanings.example: The bully tried to scare the younger boy into giving him his lunch money.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 괴롭히는 아이는 어린 소년을 위협해서(겁주어서) 점심값을 내놓게 하려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something flat by pressing it down; to suppress or defeat someone or something.
meanings.example: Use a heavy book to flatten down the dried flowers. / The rebellion was quickly flattened down by the government forces.
meanings.exampleKorean: 말린 꽃을 납작하게 만들기(평평하게 하기) 위해 무거운 책을 사용하세요. / 반란은 정부군에 의해 빠르게 제압되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To play in water by making it move; to spend a lot of money freely and ostentatiously (informal).
meanings.example: The kids loved splashing around in the wading pool. / He's been splashing his inheritance around on luxury cars.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 물놀이장에서 물장구치며 노는 것을 아주 좋아했다. / 그는 상속받은 재산을 고급 차들을 사는 데 펑펑 쓰고(과시하며 소비하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become thicker or to make something thicker, especially a liquid.
meanings.example: Keep stirring the sauce until it begins to thicken up. / The fog thickened up as we reached the valley.
meanings.exampleKorean: 소스가 걸쭉해지기 시작할 때까지 계속 저어주세요. / 우리가 계곡에 도착하자 안개가 더욱 빽빽해졌다 (짙어졌다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To become unable to feel physical or emotional sensations (informal).
meanings.example: My toes started to numb out in the freezing water. / After the tragedy, he just numbed out and didn't want to talk to anyone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 얼음물 속에서 내 발가락들이 감각이 없어지기 시작했다. / 비극 이후, 그는 그저 멍해진 채(무감각해진 채) 아무와도 말하고 싶어 하지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent a speaker from being heard by shouting or booing; to dismiss an idea with contempt.
meanings.example: The audience hooted the politician down before he could finish his sentence.
meanings.exampleKorean: 관중들은 정치인이 말을 끝내기도 전에 야유하여 그의 말을 가로막았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean something by rubbing it hard; to find or gather something with difficulty by searching in many places.
meanings.example: I had to scour up all the pots and pans after the big dinner. / We managed to scour up enough votes to win the election.
meanings.exampleKorean: 큰 저녁 식사 후에 모든 냄비와 팬들을 박박 문질러 닦아야 했다. / 우리는 선거에서 이기기에 충분한 표를 (여기저기 뒤져서) 긁어모으는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wait in a hidden place in order to attack someone by surprise.
meanings.example: The soldiers lay in ambush for hours behind the ridge.
meanings.exampleKorean: 병사들은 능선 뒤에서 몇 시간 동안 잠복했다 (숨어서 기다렸다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To pursue or chase someone relentlessly until they are caught.
meanings.example: The police managed to hound down the suspect after a long investigation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 긴 수사 끝에 용의자를 끈질기게 추적하여 잡아냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To write something quickly and without much care.
meanings.example: She scribbled down the address on the back of an envelope.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 봉투 뒷면에 주소를 휘갈겨 썼다 (대충 적었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To consciously work with your boss to get the best possible results for you, your boss, and your company.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 상사를 잘 관리하다, 보좌하다, 상사와 좋은 관계를 맺다
meanings.example: Learning how to manage up is a key skill for professional success.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상사를 잘 관리하는(보좌하는) 법을 배우는 것은 직업적 성공의 핵심 기술이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gulp something down; to suppress a feeling or a desire to say something.
meanings.example: She swallowed down her pride and asked for help. / He swallowed down the bitter medicine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 자존심을 억누르고(꾹 참고) 도움을 요청했다. / 그는 쓴 약을 삼켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dig into the ground; to reach deep into your pocket to pay for something (informal).
meanings.example: We need to dig down deep to find the source of the leak. / Everyone was asked to dig down and donate to the charity.
meanings.exampleKorean: 누출의 원인을 찾기 위해 깊이 파내려가야 합니다. / 모든 사람이 돈을 내놓아(주머니를 털어) 자선 단체에 기부해달라는 요청을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have learned something perfectly; to have something written down or recorded.
meanings.example: I think I've finally got the piano piece have down. / Make sure you have down all the important dates.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 마침내 그 피아노곡을 완벽하게 이해한(숙달한) 것 같다. / 모든 중요한 날짜들을 꼭 기록해 두세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something quickly and violently, especially from a wall; to demolish a building.
meanings.example: The protesters ripped down the old posters from the walls. / They are going to rip down the old factory next month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시위대들이 벽에서 오래된 포스터들을 거칠게 뜯어냈다. / 그들은 다음 달에 낡은 공장을 철거할 예정이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drink all of something, especially in one go.
meanings.example: He drank down the glass of water in a few seconds.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 물 한 잔을 몇 초 만에 단숨에 마셔버렸다 (쭉 들이켰다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a ship ready for bad weather; to settle down comfortably in a warm place.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 안전하게 고정하다, 편안히 쉬다, 안락하게 자리 잡다
meanings.example: We snugged down in front of the fire as the storm raged outside.
meanings.exampleKorean: 밖에는 폭풍이 몰아쳤지만 우리는 벽난로 앞에서 안락하게 자리를 잡고 쉬었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a liquid less thick by adding something to it; to become thinner.
meanings.example: You need to thin down the paint with some water. / He's really thinned down since he started his diet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 물을 좀 섞어서 페인트를 희석해야(묽게 해야) 합니다. / 그는 다이어트를 시작한 이후로 정말 몸이 가늘어졌다 (날씬해졌다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To throw something down carelessly; (of rain) to fall very heavily (informal).
meanings.example: He chucked down his bag and ran outside. / It was chucking down so hard we couldn't see a thing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 가방을 집어던지고 밖으로 달려 나갔다. / 비가 너무 억수같이 내려서 아무것도 볼 수 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rub something thoroughly with soap and water.
meanings.example: Make sure to soap down the car before rinsing it with the hose.
meanings.exampleKorean: 호스로 헹구기 전에 차를 꼭 비누칠하여 꼼꼼히 닦으세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To expect or plan for something to happen.
meanings.example: I hadn't bargained on the traffic being so bad.
meanings.exampleKorean: 교통 체증이 이렇게 심할 줄은 예상하지(고려하지) 못했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To support or improve something or make it stronger.
meanings.example: She tried to bolster up his confidence before the speech.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 연설 전에 그의 자신감을 북돋워 주려(강화해 주려) 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To say something that should be kept secret, often by accident or without thinking.
meanings.example: He blabbed out the secret about the surprise party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 깜짝 파티에 대한 비밀을 무심코 누설했다 (입 밖에 냈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To help someone to survive or get through a very difficult or dangerous situation.
meanings.example: The doctors managed to bring him through the surgery. / Her courage brought her through those hard times.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의사들은 그가 수술(의 고비)을 무사히 헤쳐나갈 수 있도록 도왔다. / 그녀의 용기가 그 힘든 시기들을 이겨내게 해주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep something under control; to manage a place or situation successfully; to keep noise at a low level.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 상태를 유지하다, 억제하다, 책임지다, 조용히 하다
meanings.example: Can you hold it down in there? I'm trying to sleep. / Thanks for holding it down at the office while I was away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 거기 좀 조용히 해줄래요(소음을 억제해줄래요)? 자려고 노력 중이에요. / 제가 없는 동안 사무실을 잘 책임지고 관리해(유지해) 주셔서 감사합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To place something on a surface; to write something down; to attribute something to a particular cause.
meanings.example: Put it down on the table. / Just put it down to experience. / Did you put it down in your notebook?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그걸 테이블 위에 내려놓으세요. / 그냥 그것을 경험 탓으로 돌리세요(경험이라 생각하세요). / 노트에 그걸 적어두었나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To formally agree not to be part of an agreement or a legal system.
meanings.example: The employees chose to contract out of the government pension scheme.
meanings.exampleKorean: 직원들은 정부 연금 제도에서 탈퇴하기로(제외되기로) 선택했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn the palm of the hand outward.
meanings.example: He held his hands palm out to show he had nothing to hide.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 숨길 것이 없음을 보여주기 위해 손바닥을 밖으로 펼쳐 보였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause something unpleasant to happen to yourself or someone else.
meanings.example: His constant lies will eventually bring down trouble on him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 끊임없는 거짓말은 결국 그에게 문제를 초래할(불러일으킬) 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing something with a lot of energy and enthusiasm (informal).
meanings.example: He bogged into his dinner after a long day at work. / Let's bog into this project and finish it by Friday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 긴 업무 끝에 저녁 식사를 열심히 먹기 시작했다. / 이 프로젝트에 전념해서(열심히 시작해서) 금요일까지 끝냅시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone feel more confident and able to communicate with other people.
meanings.example: The drama classes really helped to bring her out of herself.
meanings.exampleKorean: 연극 수업은 그녀가 자신감을 갖고 자신을 표현하도록(자신감을 갖게 하도록) 정말 큰 도움을 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be eliminated or excluded from a competition because of not meeting the criteria.
meanings.example: He qualified out of the finals due to a technical error.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 기술적 오류로 인해 결승전에서 탈락했다 (제외되었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To move slowly and quietly into a place; to begin to happen or affect something gradually.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 몰래 들어가다, 서서히 스며들다, 나타나기 시작하다
meanings.example: A few errors managed to creep in during the final editing. / He crept in after midnight so as not to wake his parents.
meanings.exampleKorean: 최종 편집 중에 몇 가지 오류들이 서서히 스며들었다 (나타나기 시작했다). / 그는 부모님을 깨우지 않기 위해 자정이 지나서 몰래 들어왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move or go about in a secretive or stealthy way.
meanings.example: Stop sneaking around and just tell me what you're doing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몰래 돌아다니지(은밀히 행동하지) 말고 당신이 뭘 하고 있는지 그냥 말해줘요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act in a bold and confident way even though you have done something wrong or are in a difficult situation.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 뻔뻔스럽게 행동하다, 버티다, 얼굴을 붉히지 않다
meanings.example: She knew she had been caught lying, but she decided to brass it out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 자신이 거짓말하다 걸린 것을 알았지만, 끝까지 뻔뻔스럽게 행동하기로(버티기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give up or stop doing something, especially because it is too difficult (informal).
meanings.example: He was so frustrated with the project that he almost tossed it in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 프로젝트에 너무 좌절해서 거의 포기할(그만둘) 뻔했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go into a trance-like state; to become unaware of one's surroundings (informal).
meanings.example: I just tranced out listening to that repetitive beat. / He often trances out during long meetings.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그 반복되는 비트를 들으며 그냥 무아지경에 빠졌다. / 그는 종종 긴 회의 중에 멍해지곤(넋을 잃곤) 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dress or decorate someone or something in a showy or elaborate way.
meanings.example: They tricked up the hall for the annual gala dinner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 연례 갈라 만찬을 위해 홀을 멋지게 꾸몄다 (장식했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something more exciting or lively; to produce or prepare something very quickly (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 활기를 불어넣다, 빨리 만들다, 생동감 있게 하다
meanings.example: We need some bright colors to zap up this room. / I'll zap up a quick snack for us.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 방에 활기를 불어넣기(생동감 있게 하기) 위해 밝은 색상들이 좀 필요해요. / 우리를 위해 간단한 간식을 빨리 만들어(준비해) 올게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To twist or distort something; to misinterpret or misrepresent someone's words.
meanings.example: He twisted up my words to make it sound like I was angry. / The wire got all twisted up in the drawer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 내 말을 왜곡해서 내가 화난 것처럼 들리게 했다. / 서랍 안에서 전선이 온통 꼬여버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To smarten up; to make someone or something look clean and tidy (British informal).
meanings.example: You'd better tosh yourself up before the guests arrive.
meanings.exampleKorean: 손님들이 오기 전에 말끔하게 단장하는(정돈하는) 게 좋겠어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce something quickly and without much care; to exhaust someone (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 대충 만들다, 지치게 하다, 빨리 해치우다
meanings.example: I can whack out a draft of the report in an hour. / That long hike really whacked me out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 한 시간 안에 보고서 초안을 대충 만들어낼(빨리 해치울) 수 있어요. / 그 긴 하이킹이 나를 정말 지치게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To add sugar to something; to try to please or influence someone with sweet talk.
meanings.example: He's trying to sugar down his boss to get a day off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 하루 쉬기 위해 상사에게 감언이설을 하고(아부하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To walk away slowly and laboriously, often because of tiredness or unhappiness (informal).
meanings.example: After losing the match, he just trogged off to the locker room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경기에서 진 후, 그는 그냥 라커룸으로 터덜터덜 가버렸다 (힘들게 떠났다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To consider or evaluate two or more things very carefully, often to make a decision.
meanings.example: The committee is weighing the proposed changes in the balance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 위원회는 제안된 변경 사항들을 신중히 저울질하고(평가하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To recover from a hangover or a minor illness by sleeping.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 잠을 자서 (숙취 등을) 해소하다, 한잠 자고 나아지다
meanings.example: He had a terrible headache, so he decided to go to bed and sleep it off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 두통이 너무 심해서 잠을 자서 해소하기(한잠 자고 나아지기)로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To endure a difficult or painful situation with courage and determination (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 용기 있게 견뎌내다, 버티다, 끝까지 해내다
meanings.example: It's going to be a tough climb, but we just have to man it out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 힘든 등반이 되겠지만, 우리는 그저 용기 있게 견뎌내야(버텨야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get onto a moving vehicle; to join a trend or activity enthusiastically.
meanings.example: He jumped onto the moving bus at the last second. / Many companies are jumping onto the AI bandwagon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 마지막 순간에 움직이는 버스에 재빨리 올라탔다. / 많은 기업이 AI 유행에 동참하고(편승하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To save a small amount of money over a long period by spending as little as possible.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 한 푼 두 푼 아껴서 모으다, 절약하여 마련하다
meanings.example: They managed to scrimp together enough money for a down payment on a house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 집 계약금을 내기 위해 충분한 돈을 한 푼 두 푼 아껴서 모으는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a hole through something with a drill; to teach something through repetitive practice.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 구멍을 뚫다, 철저히 교육하다, 반복 훈련시키다
meanings.example: The mechanic had to drill through the metal plate. / The coach drilled the team through the defensive plays until they knew them perfectly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정비공은 금속판에 구멍을 뚫어야 했다. / 코치는 팀이 완벽하게 익힐 때까지 수비 전술을 철저히 반복 훈련시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force a final test or confrontation to resolve a dispute.
meanings.example: The two rivals are ready to show down in the upcoming debate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 경쟁자는 다가오는 토론에서 정면 대결할(결판을 낼) 준비가 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To crush something into very small pieces or powder.
meanings.example: Grind up the coffee beans just before brewing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 커피를 내리기 직전에 원두를 가루로 가세요(분쇄하세요).

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue a journey or task; to put on a piece of clothing quickly and roughly.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 계속 가다, (모자 등을) 눌러쓰다, 밀어 넣다
meanings.example: It's getting late, let's shove on. / He shoved on his hat and ran out the door.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시간이 늦었네요, 계속 갑시다. / 그는 모자를 푹 눌러쓰고 문밖으로 달려 나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cut or shape something with a chisel; to get something by using trickery or hard bargaining.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 정으로 깎아내다, 속여서 빼앗다, 간신히 얻어내다
meanings.example: The sculptor chiseled out a beautiful figure from the marble. / He managed to chisel out a better deal from the supplier.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조각가는 대리석에서 아름다운 형상을 깎아냈다. / 그는 공급업체로부터 더 나은 계약을 교묘하게(간신히) 얻어냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To set fire to something; to make a situation or atmosphere more exciting or intense (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 불을 붙이다, 활기를 불어넣다, 분위기를 띄우다
meanings.example: The protesters torched up several cars in the street. / His arrival really torched up the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시위대들이 거리의 차 몇 대에 불을 붙였다. / 그의 도착이 파티 분위기를 정말 활기 있게(뜨겁게) 띄웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To push someone roughly in various directions; to treat someone rudely or bossily.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 이리저리 밀치다, 무례하게 대하다, 함부로 다루다
meanings.example: Stop shoving me about! / He's tired of being shoved about by his older brothers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나를 이리저리 밀치지 마세요! / 그는 형들에게 무례하게 대접받는(함부로 다뤄지는) 것에 진저리가 났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter a place; to start a new job or role; to involve oneself in a situation.
meanings.example: He was ready to step into the role of CEO. / Can you step into the meeting for a moment?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 CEO 역할을 맡을(시작할) 준비가 되어 있었다. / 회의에 잠시 발을 들여놓아(참석해) 줄 수 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To move in a spiral shape; to increase or decrease rapidly in a way that cannot be controlled.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 나선형으로 퍼지다, 제어 불능이 되다, 급격히 악화되다
meanings.example: The situation began to spiral out of control. / The argument spiraled out into a physical fight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상황이 제어 불능 상태가 되기(급격히 악화되기) 시작했다. / 그 논쟁은 걷잡을 수 없이 물리적인 싸움으로 번졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To push something down with force; to force someone to accept an idea or rule.
meanings.example: He shoved his clothes down into the suitcase. / They are trying to shove these new regulations down our throats.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 옷들을 여행 가방 속에 억지로 밀어 넣었다. / 그들은 이 새로운 규정들을 우리에게 강요하려(주입하려) 하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To connect electronic equipment; to provide someone with something they need (slang).
meanings.example: Can you help me hook it up to the TV? / My friend can hook it up with some concert tickets.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이걸 TV에 연결하는 걸 도와줄 수 있나요? / 내 친구가 콘서트 티켓을 구해주어 해결해줄(제공해줄) 수 있어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dress or decorate someone or something in a very stylish or cool way; to show off one's confidence (slang).
meanings.example: He really swagged out for the music video. / They swagged out the entire room for the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 뮤직비디오를 위해 정말 멋지게 꾸몄다. / 그들은 파티를 위해 방 전체를 화려하게 단장했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move your body along a seat to make room for someone else; to arrive quickly.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 옆으로 비켜앉다, 서둘러 오다, 자리를 좁히다
meanings.example: Could you scoot up a bit so I can sit down? / We need to scoot up to the airport or we'll be late.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 앉을 수 있게 옆으로 좀 비켜앉아 줄래요? / 서둘러 공항으로 가야 해요, 안 그러면 늦을 거예요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something by using steam; to leave a place in an angry or indignant way (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 김을 쐬어 떼어내다, 화가 나서 떠나다
meanings.example: He steamed off the labels from the old jars. / After the argument, she just steamed off without saying a word.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 낡은 단지에서 김을 쐬어 라벨들을 떼어냈다. / 논쟁 후에 그녀는 한마디 말도 없이 화가 나서 떠나버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To divide something and give a portion to each person in a group.
meanings.example: The children shared up the candy they gathered. / We decided to share up the responsibilities for the project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 모은 사탕을 나누어 가졌다. / 우리는 프로젝트에 대한 책임을 공평하게 나누기로(공유하기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reach the legal limit for catching fish or game; to reach the maximum capacity or limit of something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 한도에 도달하다, 제한을 채우다, 한계에 이르다
meanings.example: The fisherman limited out within three hours. / The server limited out due to the sudden surge in traffic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 낚시꾼은 3시간 만에 하루 허용 한도에 도달했다 (제한을 채웠다). / 갑작스러운 트래픽 증가로 서버가 한계에 이르렀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place while showing off; to relax your muscles after exercise.
meanings.example: He flexed off after winning the championship. / Don't forget to stretch and flex off after your workout.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 챔피언십에서 우승한 후 과시하며 떠났다. / 운동 후에 스트레칭하고 근육을 푸는(이완시키는) 것을 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To select someone or something very carefully for a particular purpose.
meanings.example: Each student was handpicked out for the special program.
meanings.exampleKorean: 각 학생은 특별 프로그램을 위해 엄선되었다 (세심하게 선발되었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To convert a currency into US dollars; to increase the price or value of something.
meanings.example: We need to dollar up these figures for the international report. / They dollared up the old car before selling it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 국제 보고서를 위해 이 수치들을 달러로 환산해야 합니다. / 그들은 낡은 차를 팔기 전에 돈을 들여 가치를 올렸다 (단장했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To search for information about something or someone on the internet using the Google search engine.
meanings.example: I'll google up that new restaurant to see the menu.
meanings.exampleKorean: 메뉴를 확인하기 위해 그 새로운 식당을 구글로 검색해(인터넷으로 찾아) 볼게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act in a proud or superior way; to look down on others.
meanings.example: Ever since she got promoted, she's been holding her nose up at everyone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 승진한 이후로 모든 사람에게 거만하게 굴고(콧대를 높이고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To absorb liquid using something absorbent like paper or cloth.
meanings.example: Use a paper towel to blot up the spilled juice.
meanings.exampleKorean: 종이 타월을 사용해 쏟은 주스를 흡수하세요(닦아내세요).

meanings.englishDefinition: To register that you are taking time off from work.
meanings.example: I need to book off next Friday for a doctor's appointment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 병 진료 예약 때문에 다음 주 금요일에 휴가를 내야(근무를 빠져야) 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce the intensity or excitement of something.
meanings.example: We need to amp down the rhetoric and focus on the facts. / Try to amp down before you go to sleep.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 수사적 표현을 자제하고(강도를 낮추고) 사실에 집중해야 합니다. / 자기 전에 몸과 마음을 진정시키려(완화하려) 노력하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove the bones from meat or fish.
meanings.example: The butcher showed me how to bone out a chicken.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정육점 주인이 닭의 뼈를 발라내는 법을 보여주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hit something lightly and quickly; to touch a screen on a smartphone.
meanings.example: Tap on the icon to open the app. / He tapped on my shoulder to get my attention.
meanings.exampleKorean: 앱을 열려면 아이콘을 터치하세요(가볍게 두드리세요). / 그는 내 주의를 끌기 위해 내 어깨를 가볍게 두드렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing or dealing with something (formal).
meanings.example: The committee will begin upon the task of reviewing the budget tomorrow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 위원회는 내일부터 예산 검토 업무를 시작할(착수할) 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To run as fast as possible to reach a base in baseball; to complete something through effort and persistence.
meanings.example: He managed to leg out an infield single. / It was a long day, but I decided to leg it out until the end.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 전력 질주하여 내야 안타를 만들어냈다. / 긴 하루였지만, 나는 끝까지 끈기로 버텨내기로(해내기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To extend one's arm outward, for example to reach something or to give a signal.
meanings.example: He armed out to grab the falling vase. / The cyclist armed out to signal a left turn.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 떨어지는 꽃병을 잡기 위해 팔을 뻗었다. / 자전거 타는 사람이 좌회전 신호를 보내기 위해 팔을 내밀었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take a signal from someone to start an action; to use someone's behavior as a guide.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~의 신호를 받다, ~을 가이드로 삼다, 힌트를 얻다
meanings.example: The band members cued off the drummer to start the next song. / I'll cue off your reaction to know if I should continue.
meanings.exampleKorean: 밴드 멤버들은 드러머의 신호를 받아 다음 곡을 시작했다. / 제가 계속해야 할지 알기 위해 당신의 반응을 가이드로 삼을(힌트를 얻을)게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove liquid; to wiretap or listen in on a conversation secretly.
meanings.example: The police suspected that someone was trying to tap off their communication lines.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 누군가가 자신들의 통신 회선을 도청하려(불법으로 연결하려) 한다고 의심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enjoy the warmth of the sun; to enjoy praise, success, or attention.
meanings.example: After winning the award, he took a moment to bask in the sunshine of his success.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상을 받은 후, 그는 잠시 자신의 성공이 주는 영광을 만끽했다(누렸다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter a room or building by force; to interrupt a conversation or activity suddenly (informal).
meanings.example: The police busted in and arrested the suspects. / I'm sorry to bust in on your meeting, but there's an emergency.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰이 급습하여 용의자들을 체포했다. / 회의 중에 불쑥 끼어들어(참견해서) 죄송합니다만, 비상사태입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To contribute money or effort to a joint activity; to interrupt a conversation with a remark.
meanings.example: Everyone chipped into the gift for the teacher. / She chipped into the discussion with a helpful suggestion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모두가 선생님 선물을 위해 돈을 조금씩 냈다(기여했다). / 그녀는 유용한 제안을 하며 토론에 말참견했다(끼어들었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To have difficulty breathing because something is stuck in your throat; to be unable to speak or accept something because of strong emotion.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~이 목에 걸리다, 숨이 막히다, 말을 잇지 못하다
meanings.example: Be careful not to choke on that fish bone. / He almost choked on his words when he saw the surprise.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 생선 가시가 목에 걸리지 않도록 조심하세요. / 그는 깜짝 놀랄 일을 보자 너무 당황해서 거의 말을 잇지 못했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To brush your hair to remove knots; to search through a place or group very carefully to find something or someone.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 빗질하다, 철저히 수색하다, 이 잡듯 뒤지다
meanings.example: She spent ten minutes combing out the tangles in her hair. / The police are combing out the forest for any signs of the missing hiker.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 엉킨 머리카락을 빗질하는 데 10분을 보냈다. / 경찰은 실종된 하이커의 흔적을 찾기 위해 숲을 철저히 수색하고(이 잡듯 뒤지고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become calm or make someone calm; to relax and spend time doing nothing (slang).
meanings.example: Just take a deep breath and cool out for a minute. / We spent the whole afternoon just cooling out by the pool.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그냥 심호흡하고 잠시만 진정하세요. / 우리는 오후 내내 수영장 옆에서 그냥 느긋하게 쉬며 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To expel something from the throat by coughing; to give information or money unwillingly (informal).
meanings.example: The patient coughed out some thick phlegm. / The suspect finally coughed out the truth under pressure.
meanings.exampleKorean: 환자는 기침하며 걸쭉한 가래를 내뱉었다. / 용의자는 압박 수사 끝에 마침내 마지못해 진실을 털어놓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move very fast; to progress quickly and successfully (informal).
meanings.example: The boat was cracking along at a good speed. / The new project is really cracking along now.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 배는 좋은 속도로 빠르게 나아가고 있었다. / 새로운 프로젝트가 이제 정말 순조롭게 진행되고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To interrupt someone or something; to reduce the amount of something, such as time or money.
meanings.example: I'm sorry to cut into your conversation. / These unexpected expenses are really cutting into our savings.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대화를 가로막고(끼어들어서) 죄송합니다. / 이 예상치 못한 지출들이 우리 저축을 정말 깎아 먹고(손해를 입히고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To perform well or show talent; to accept the consequences or conditions of a situation (slang).
meanings.example: If you want to win, you really have to come with it. / The stress of the job just comes with it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이기고 싶다면, 정말로 당신의 실력을 보여줘야(발휘해야) 해요. / 이 직업의 스트레스는 그냥 (직업의 특성으로) 받아들여야 하는 부분이에요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To purchase something that someone else is in the process of buying or already has rights to, often in a deceptive way.
meanings.example: A rival developer bought the land out from under them before they could sign the contract.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경쟁 개발업자가 그들이 계약서에 서명하기도 전에 그 땅을 몰래 가로채어 샀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To select or pick out something or someone very carefully from a larger group (formal).
meanings.example: The finest grapes were chosen out for this special vintage wine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 특별한 빈티지 와인을 위해 가장 좋은 포도들이 엄선되었다 (정성껏 골라졌다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To focus all your attention or efforts on one particular thing.
meanings.example: The investigators decided to laser in on the CEO's financial records.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수사관들은 CEO의 재무 기록에 온 신경을 집중하기로(조준하기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wash something quickly in clean water to remove soap or dirt.
meanings.example: Rinse out the glass before you use it. / She rinsed out her swimsuit after swimming in the ocean.
meanings.exampleKorean: 유리잔을 사용하기 전에 깨끗이 헹궈내세요. / 그녀는 바다에서 수영한 후 수영복을 물에 헹궈냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become aware of and follow the latest trends or ideas; to start paying attention and doing what you are supposed to do (informal).
meanings.example: If you want to stay in business, you need to get with it. / Get with it, John! We have a lot of work to do.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사업을 계속하고 싶다면 최신 유행에 따라야(정신 차려야) 해요. / 정신 차려, 존! 할 일이 태산이야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start enjoying something very much; to start an argument or a fight with someone (informal).
meanings.example: I'm really starting to get into it now that the plot is thickening. / They really got into it over who should pay the bill.
meanings.exampleKorean: 줄거리가 흥미진진해지니 이제 정말 (이야기에) 빠져들기(즐기기) 시작했어요. / 그들은 누가 계산해야 할지를 두고 정말 격렬하게 싸웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To close a shop for the day; to stop doing business permanently; to stop communicating with others.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 문을 닫다, 영업을 중단하다, 소통을 거부하다
meanings.example: Many small shops had to pull the shutters down during the recession. / He pulled the shutters down on his private life.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경기 침체 동안 많은 작은 가게들이 문을 닫아야(영업을 중단해야) 했다. / 그는 자신의 사생활에 대해 소통을 거부했다 (폐쇄적인 태도를 취했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize or scold someone severely (informal).
meanings.example: Don't get too down on him; he's only a beginner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그를 너무 비난하지(몰아세우지) 마세요. 그는 단지 초보자일 뿐이에요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend time with someone regularly as a friend or romantic partner.
meanings.example: She's been going around with a strange crowd lately. / Who is he going around with these days?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 최근에 좀 이상한 무리와 어울리고 있다. / 그는 요즘 누구와 사귀고(교제하고) 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To become stuck to something because of cold; to become fixed or frozen in a particular state.
meanings.example: The wet clothes froze onto the washing line. / The government froze onto the company's assets during the investigation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 젖은 옷들이 빨랫줄에 꽁꽁 얼어붙었다. / 정부는 수사 기간 동안 그 회사의 자산을 동결했다(고정했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To die as a result of something you have done or failed to do.
meanings.example: Many brave soldiers paid with their lives for our freedom.
meanings.exampleKorean: 많은 용감한 병사들이 우리의 자유를 위해 목숨으로 대가를 치렀다 (죽음을 당했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To explode or blow something up; to increase volume or amount very quickly (informal).
meanings.example: They blasted up the old bridge to make room for the new one. / Can you blast up the radio? I love this song.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 새 다리를 놓기 위해 낡은 다리를 폭파했다. / 라디오 소리 좀 크게 높여 줄래? 나 이 노래 정말 좋아해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decorate or dress someone or something in a beautiful or elaborate way.
meanings.example: The streets were decked up with flags for the festival.
meanings.exampleKorean: 축제를 위해 거리들이 깃발로 화려하게 장식되어(단장되어) 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To group several items or tasks together to be processed at the same time.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 일괄 처리하다, 한데 모으다, 묶음으로 만들다
meanings.example: The computer system will batch up the orders and send them out every hour.
meanings.exampleKorean: 컴퓨터 시스템은 주문들을 일괄 처리하여(한데 모아) 매시간 발송할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go to different places in a lighthearted or casual way (informal).
meanings.example: We spent the afternoon bopping around the city center.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 오후 시간을 시내 중심가를 가볍게 돌아다니며(구경하며) 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fasten a seat belt; to prepare for a difficult or exciting situation.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 안전벨트를 매다, 대비하다, 마음을 단단히 먹다
meanings.example: Buckle in, it's going to be a bumpy ride! / Everyone needs to buckle in for the upcoming project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 안전벨트를 매세요, 험난한 여정이 될 거예요! / 다들 다가올 프로젝트에 대비해 마음을 단단히 먹어야(준비해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter a place in a very energetic and confident way.
meanings.example: She bounced in and announced the good news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 활기차게 들어오더니 좋은 소식을 발표했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To look at or examine a place carefully, especially to plan something (informal).
meanings.example: The burglars cased out the house for days before the break-in. / Let's case out the venue before we decide on the layout.
meanings.exampleKorean: 도둑들은 침입하기 전 며칠 동안 그 집을 미리 살폈다(답사했다). / 배치를 결정하기 전에 행사장을 미리 살핍시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take something with you when you leave; to gain a specific lesson or feeling from an experience.
meanings.example: What did you bring away from the conference? / I brought away a feeling of hope from the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 컨퍼런스에서 무엇을 얻어왔나요(배웠나요)? / 나는 그 모임에서 희망적인 느낌을 얻어왔다(느꼈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To study or work very hard (informal).
meanings.example: I really need to bone down for my final exams.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 기말고사를 위해 정말 열심히 공부해야(전념해야) 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay in one's home or a safe place during an emergency rather than evacuating (informal).
meanings.example: If the storm hits, we'll just bug in and wait it out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭풍이 몰아치면, 우리는 그냥 실내에 머물며(대피하며) 폭풍이 지나가길 기다릴 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To break against something; (of news or emotions) to start suddenly.
meanings.example: The waves broke on the rocky shore. / The news of the scandal broke on the internet this morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 파도가 바위투성이 해안에 부서졌다. / 오늘 아침 인터넷에서 그 스캔들 소식이 갑자기 터져 나왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To play loud, upbeat music; to liven up a situation with music and dancing (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 음악을 크게 틀다, 활기를 돋우다, 신나게 놀다
meanings.example: Let's bop it up and get everyone dancing!
meanings.exampleKorean: 음악을 크게 틀고 모두 춤추게 해서 분위기를 띄워봅시다(활기를 돋워봅시다)!

meanings.englishDefinition: To attribute something, usually a failure or error, to a particular cause or person.
meanings.example: You can chalk that mistake against his lack of experience.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 실수는 그의 경험 부족 탓으로 돌릴(기록할) 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To compete against someone in an election; (of luck, time, etc.) to be unfavorable to someone.
meanings.example: She decided to run against the incumbent senator. / Luck has been running against us all season.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 현역 상원의원에 맞서 출마하기로 결정했다. / 이번 시즌 내내 운이 우리에게 불리하게 작용하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fight with someone; to try very hard to deal with a difficult problem or decision.
meanings.example: I've been wrestling with this math problem for hours. / She is still wrestling with the decision to leave her job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 몇 시간째 이 수학 문제와 씨름하고 있다. / 그녀는 직장을 그만둘 결정을 두고 여전히 고심하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place or person secretly; to try to avoid dealing with a difficult situation or feeling.
meanings.example: You can't keep running away from your problems forever. / He ran away from home when he was sixteen.
meanings.exampleKorean: 문제로부터 영원히 도망칠(문제를 영원히 회피할) 수는 없어요. / 그는 16살 때 집에서 도망쳤다(가출했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To join pieces of cloth together with stitches; to combine different things quickly or with difficulty to create a whole.
meanings.example: The doctor had to stitch the wound together. / The government is trying to stitch together a coalition.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의사는 상처 부위를 꿰매야(봉합해야) 했다. / 정부는 연립 정부를 서둘러 조합하려(구성하려) 애쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get into a car, bed, etc.; to put on a piece of clothing (informal).
meanings.example: He climbed into the truck and started the engine. / It's time to climb into your pajamas.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 트럭에 올라타서(타서) 시동을 걸었다. / 이제 잠옷을 챙겨 입을 시간이에요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly understand or realize something (British informal).
meanings.example: He finally clocked onto what they were trying to do.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그들이 무엇을 하려는지 마침내 이해했다 (알아차렸다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop doing something, especially because you are bored or frustrated (informal).
meanings.example: I was so tired of my job that I decided to chuck it in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 일이 너무 지겨워서 그냥 그만두기로(포기하기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make or assemble something in a careless or dishonest way.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 대충 이어 붙이다, 적당히 만들다, 날조하다
meanings.example: He fudged together a report from various old documents.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 여러 낡은 문서들을 대충 이어 붙여서 보고서를 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a bold or impudent way to get through a difficult situation (informal).
meanings.example: He didn't have a ticket, but he tried to cheek it out anyway.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 티켓이 없었지만, 어쨌든 뻔뻔스럽게 버텨보려(당당하게 들어가려) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To brush hair downwards to make it neat; to calm someone down.
meanings.example: He combed down his messy hair before the interview. / The mother tried to comb down her angry child.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 인터뷰 전에 헝클어진 머리를 빗질했다. / 어머니는 화난 아이를 달래려(진정시키려) 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop working suddenly; to fall asleep quickly because of tiredness (informal).
meanings.example: The car engine conked off in the middle of the highway. / I was so exhausted I just conked off on the sofa.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자동차 엔진이 고속도로 한복판에서 갑자기 멈췄다. / 너무 기진맥진해서 그냥 소파에서 잠들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall asleep; to pass out from drinking too much alcohol (slang).
meanings.example: He corked out as soon as his head hit the pillow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 머리가 베개에 닿자마자 골아떨어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To involve oneself in a public argument, political contest, or difficult competition.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 경쟁에 뛰어들다, 논쟁에 가담하다, 전장에 나가다
meanings.example: The respected scholar decided to descend into the arena of public debate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 존경받는 학자는 공개 토론의 장(경쟁의 장)에 직접 뛰어들기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone feel very confused or nervous; to worry someone a lot.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 당황하게 하다, 혼란에 빠뜨리다, 안절부절못하게 하다
meanings.example: The lawyer's tricky questions tied the witness in knots.
meanings.exampleKorean: 변호사의 교묘한 질문들이 증인을 매우 당황하게(혼란스럽게) 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid discussing or dealing with a difficult subject directly.
meanings.example: He's just dancing around the issue of his debt.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 부채 문제를 그저 회피하고(핵심을 피하고) 있을 뿐이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove dirt or unwanted marks from the surface of something.
meanings.example: Clean off the whiteboard after the class. / You should clean off the mud from your shoes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수업 후에 화이트보드를 말끔히 닦아내세요. / 신발에 묻은 진흙을 닦아내야(제거해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deal with something quickly and without much detail; to touch something lightly with a brush.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 대충 훑어보다, 가볍게 다루다, 스치듯 지나가다
meanings.example: The report just brushes over the most important problems. / He brushed over the canvas with a light coat of paint.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 보고서는 가장 중요한 문제들을 그저 대충 훑어보고(가볍게 다루고) 있을 뿐이다. / 그는 캔버스 위를 페인트로 가볍게 덧칠했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give up or stop doing something; to add something for free to a deal; to interject a comment.
meanings.example: If you buy the car today, I'll toss in a set of new tires. / He tossed in a few jokes during his presentation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 차를 사시면 새 타이어 한 세트를 덤으로 드릴게요. / 그는 발표 중에 농담 몇 마디를 보탰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a telephone call to a number outside a private telephone system.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 외부로 전화를 걸다, 외선 연결을 하다
meanings.example: You need to press nine before you can dial out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 외부로 전화를 걸려면(외선 연결을 하려면) 먼저 9번을 눌러야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To like or enjoy someone or something very much (slang).
meanings.example: I really dig on that new song they keep playing. / Do you dig on jazz music?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들이 계속 틀어주는 그 신곡 정말 좋아해요. / 재즈 음악 즐기시나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To put handcuffs on someone; to restrain someone's actions.
meanings.example: The officer had to cuff up the suspect before putting him in the car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰관은 용의자를 차에 태우기 전에 수갑을 채워야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean something thoroughly, especially the walls of a room or a large piece of equipment.
meanings.example: We spent all day cleaning down the kitchen walls.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 하루 종일 주방 벽을 철저히 청소했다 (말끔히 닦았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To distribute something; to give out punishment or criticism, often severely.
meanings.example: He's quick to deal it out, but he can't take criticism himself. / The teacher dealt out the test papers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 남을 비난하는 데(비난을 가하는 데)는 빠르지만, 정작 자신은 비판을 받아들이지 못한다. / 선생님이 시험지를 나누어 주셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place or situation quickly or secretly, especially to avoid a responsibility (slang).
meanings.example: He decided to ditch out of the meeting early. / Don't ditch out on me now; I need your help.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 회의에서 일찍 빠지기로(도망치기로) 결정했다. / 지금 나를 두고 내빼지 마세요. 당신 도움이 필요해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discover something by chance, or to meet someone unexpectedly.
meanings.example: Researchers stumbled onto the cure while studying a different disease.
meanings.exampleKorean: 연구원들은 다른 질병을 연구하던 중 우연히 그 치료법을 발견했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become involved in something by chance or without planning.
meanings.example: I just stumbled into acting when I was in college.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 대학 시절에 그냥 우연히 연기를 시작하게 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To end in a weak or disappointing way after a strong start (informal).
meanings.example: The protests started with a lot of energy but eventually fizzed out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시위는 엄청난 에너지와 함께 시작되었지만 결국 흐지부지되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish the edge of a piece of knitting so that it will not unravel.
meanings.example: Once you reach the desired length, you need to bind off the stitches.
meanings.exampleKorean: 원하는 길이에 도달하면 코를 막아야(마무리해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pass a ball to a teammate; to give something to someone, often in a casual way (informal).
meanings.example: The point guard dished off the ball for an easy layup. / He's always trying to dish off his work to others.
meanings.exampleKorean: 포인트 가드는 쉬운 레이업 슛을 위해 공을 패스했다. / 그는 항상 자신의 일을 남들에게 떠넘기려 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To create a false version of something; to arrange or decorate something to make it look better than it is.
meanings.example: He tried to fake up a doctor's note to miss work. / They faked up the old house for the potential buyers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 결근하기 위해 의사 소견서를 위조하려(꾸며내려) 했다. / 그들은 잠재적 구매자들을 위해 낡은 집을 대충 보기 좋게 꾸몄다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall in love with someone suddenly; to be easily deceived by something (slang).
meanings.example: He really flopped for that new girl in the office. / I can't believe you flopped for such an obvious trick.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 사무실에 새로 온 아가씨에게 정말 푹 빠졌다. / 그런 뻔한 속임수에 넘어가다니 믿기지 않네요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To face a particular direction; to confront someone or something directly.
meanings.example: The hotel rooms front on the ocean. / We need to front on this problem before it gets worse.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 호텔 객실들은 바다를 마주하고 있다. / 우리는 이 문제가 더 악화되기 전에 정면으로 대면해야(맞서야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage to live or continue, despite having very little money or success (informal).
meanings.example: They've been grubbing along on a very small pension for years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 몇 년 동안 아주 적은 연금으로 근근이 살아가고(하루하루 버티고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come under the control or possession of someone, especially an enemy.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~의 손아귀에 들어가다, ~의 지배를 받다, 수중에 들어가다
meanings.example: If these documents fall into the wrong hands, our plan will be ruined.
meanings.exampleKorean: 만약 이 문서들이 잘못된 사람의 손아귀에 들어가면(수중에 들어가면), 우리 계획은 망가질 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give someone credit or praise for their skills or achievements, even if you do not like them.
meanings.example: I don't like his methods, but you have to hand it to him: he gets results.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 방식이 마음에 들지는 않지만, 그가 결과를 낸다는 점은 인정해야(칭찬해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wash something using a hose.
meanings.example: Wait a minute, I need to hose off the car before we leave.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잠시만요, 출발하기 전에 차를 호스로 씻어야(물로 닦아내야) 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enjoy something very much; to be very interested in something (slang).
meanings.example: The crowd was really grooving on the band's new sound.
meanings.exampleKorean: 관중들은 그 밴드의 새로운 사운드에 정말 심취해(즐기고) 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drink or eat something quickly and in large amounts (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 벌컥벌컥 마시다, 허겁지겁 먹다, 게걸스럽게 먹다
meanings.example: He guzzled down the whole bottle of water in one go.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 물 한 병을 한 번에 벌컥벌컥 마셔 버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To distribute something to a group of people; to give someone a punishment or criticism.
meanings.example: The volunteers are handing out flyers in the street. / The judge was known for handing it out to repeat offenders.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자원봉사자들이 거리에서 전단지를 나누어 주고(배포하고) 있다. / 그 판사는 상습범들에게 엄한 처벌을 가하는 것으로 유명했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cut something into small pieces in a rough or violent way.
meanings.example: The vandals hacked up the park benches. / He hacked up the wood for the fire.
meanings.exampleKorean: 파괴범들이 공원 벤치들을 난도질했다. / 그는 땔감을 위해 나무를 작게 잘랐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fasten something using a hammer and nails; to create or assemble something quickly.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 못을 박아 고정하다, 세우다, (계획 등을) 급히 만들다
meanings.example: We hammered up a few shelves in the garage. / They managed to hammer up a temporary solution.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 차고에 선반 몇 개를 못을 박아 고정했다. / 그들은 임시방편의 해결책을 급히 만드는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give something away too easily, especially an advantage or opportunity.
meanings.example: You shouldn't hand away such a great opportunity to your rival.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그렇게 좋은 기회를 경쟁자에게 거저 주어서는(내주어서는) 안 됩니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a holiday or period of time away from work; to remove something.
meanings.example: I'm having next Monday off for my birthday. / She managed to have the sticky label off the jar.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 생일이라 다음 주 월요일에 휴가를 낼(쉴) 거예요. / 그녀는 단지에서 끈적거리는 라벨을 떼어내는(제거하는) 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force something open with a tool; to arrange or assemble something in a makeshift or clever way.
meanings.example: He had to jimmy up the window because he lost his keys. / Can you jimmy up a temporary repair for this pipe?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 열쇠를 잃어버려서 창문을 억지로 열어야 했다. / 이 파이프를 임시로 급히 수리해 줄 수 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To catch or stop a ball as a fielder in a game like baseball or cricket.
meanings.example: The shortstop fielded out the ground ball and threw it to first base.
meanings.exampleKorean: 유격수가 땅볼을 잡아내어(수비하여) 1루로 던졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing something or eating something with a lot of energy and enthusiasm (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 열심히 시작하다, 허겁지겁 먹다, 본격적으로 착수하다
meanings.example: He really hoed into his breakfast after the morning run. / Let's hoe into the project and finish it by noon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아침 조깅 후에 아침 식사를 정말 허겁지겁 먹기 시작했다. / 이 프로젝트에 본격적으로 착수해서(열심히 시작해서) 정오까지 끝냅시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To audition for a role in a play or film; to study a particular subject at university.
meanings.example: She's going to read for the part of the lead actress tomorrow. / He's reading for a degree in history.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 내일 여주인공 역할을 위해 오디션을 볼 예정이다. / 그는 역사학 학위 취득을 위해 공부하고(전공하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be badly in need of something; to lack something important.
meanings.example: The team is really hurting for a good goalkeeper right now. / With his new job, he's not hurting for money anymore.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 팀은 지금 좋은 골키퍼를 정말 절실히 필요로 하고 있다. / 새 직장을 구했으니, 그는 더 이상 돈 때문에 고생하지(돈이 부족하지) 않는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To connect an electronic device to another; to connect to a computer network or virtual reality system (slang).
meanings.example: He jacked into the mainframe to retrieve the hidden data.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 숨겨진 데이터를 회수하기 위해 메인프레임에 접속했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To push something upwards; to move along a seat to make room for someone else (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 위로 밀어 올리다, 옆으로 비켜앉다, 자리를 좁히다
meanings.example: Can you shove up a bit so I can sit down? / He helped me shove the heavy box up onto the shelf.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 앉을 수 있게 옆으로 좀 비켜앉아 줄래요? / 그는 무거운 상자를 선반 위로 밀어 올리는 것을 도와주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a very fun and noisy time; to enjoy oneself loudly (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 신나게 즐기다, 떠들썩하게 놀다, 유쾌하게 지내다
meanings.example: They were hooting it up at the bar until after midnight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 자정이 넘을 때까지 바에서 떠들썩하게 놀며 신나게 즐겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sacrifice oneself for something; to feel great sympathy or pity for someone.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~을 위해 희생하다, 몹시 동정하다, 마음 아파하다
meanings.example: My heart bleeds for the families who lost their homes in the fire. / Many people have bled for the cause of freedom.
meanings.exampleKorean: 화재로 집을 잃은 가족들을 생각하면 마음이 찢어질 듯이 동정하게 된다. / 많은 사람이 자유라는 대의를 위해 희생했다 (피를 흘렸다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To relax and do nothing, especially after a period of stress (slang).
meanings.example: After the exams, I just want to jell out for a few days.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시험이 끝나면 그냥 며칠 동안 느긋하게 쉬고(빈둥거리고) 싶어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move around in an energetic way; to act or behave in a non-serious or silly way (informal).
meanings.example: Stop jazzing around and get back to work! / The kids were jazzing around in the backyard.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장난치지(제멋대로 행동하지) 말고 가서 일이나 하세요! / 아이들이 뒷마당에서 활기차게 뛰어놀고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To realize or understand something (informal).
meanings.example: It took him a while to jerry to the fact that it was all a joke.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그게 모두 농담이라는 사실을 그가 알아차리는(이해하는) 데 시간이 좀 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To proofread a text carefully before it is printed.
meanings.example: The editor is reading for press the final version of the novel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 편집자가 소설의 최종본에 대해 인쇄 전 교정을 보고(최종 검토를 하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force someone out of a place or position; to exclude someone.
meanings.example: The larger companies tried to horn out the smaller competitors from the market.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대기업들은 시장에서 작은 경쟁업체들을 밀어내려(제외하려) 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To look at something in a way that includes all the facts that surround it and help you understand it.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 맥락에 비추어 파악하다, 배경을 설명하다, 상황과 연결하다
meanings.example: To understand the current economic crisis, we need to put it into context.
meanings.exampleKorean: 현재의 경제 위기를 이해하려면, 우리는 그것을 맥락에 비추어 파악해야(배경과 연결해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To joke and have fun with others, especially in a lively way (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 농담하며 즐겁게 놀다, 유쾌한 시간을 보내다
meanings.example: The cast members were joking it up during the break.
meanings.exampleKorean: 출연진들은 쉬는 시간 동안 농담하며 즐거운 시간을 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize someone suddenly and severely; to accept an opportunity or suggestion eagerly.
meanings.example: The boss jumped upon her as soon as he saw the error. / You should jump upon that offer before they change their minds.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사장은 오류를 보자마자 그녀를 갑자기 비난했다(몰아세웠다). / 그들이 마음을 바꾸기 전에 그 제안을 즉시 붙잡아야(받아들여야) 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hide or go underground, especially when being searched for.
meanings.example: The suspect went to earth in a remote village to avoid the police.
meanings.exampleKorean: 용의자는 경찰을 피하기 위해 외딴 마을에 숨었다(잠적했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide detailed specifications for a product or project.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 상세 명세를 작성하다, 사양을 정하다, 상세히 계획하다
meanings.example: We need to spec out the new software features before the developers start working.
meanings.exampleKorean: 개발자들이 작업을 시작하기 전에 새로운 소프트웨어 기능들에 대한 상세 명세를 작성해야(사양을 정해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go to a nearby place quickly and for a short time (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 잠깐 들르다, 재빨리 내려가다, 쓱 가다
meanings.example: I'm just going to pop down to the shops for some milk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우유 좀 사러 가게에 잠깐 들를(쓱 다녀올) 거예요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue doing something; to start to improve or develop after a period of slow progress.
