meanings.example: They decided to kick on with the construction despite the rain. / The economy is expected to kick on in the next quarter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 비에도 불구하고 건설 작업을 계속하기로 결정했다. / 내년 분기에는 경제가 활발히 발전할(힘차게 나아갈) 것으로 기대된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To adjust or fine-tune something; to act in a very nervous or strange way (slang).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 미세하게 조정하다, 몹시 불안해하다, 이상하게 행동하다
meanings.example: We spent hours tweaking out the audio settings. / He was tweaking out because of the pressure of the deadline.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 오디오 설정을 미세하게 조정하는 데 몇 시간을 보냈다. / 그는 마감 압박 때문에 몹시 불안해하며 이상하게 행동했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become withdrawn and focused on your own thoughts and feelings rather than on other people.
meanings.example: After the tragedy, she turned in on herself and stopped talking to her friends.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비극 이후, 그녀는 내면으로 침잠하여(자신에게 갇혀) 친구들과 대화하는 것을 그만두었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join or cooperate with someone or a group.
meanings.example: He decided to toss in with the rebels for the cause of justice.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 정의를 위해 반군과 합류하기로(운명을 같이하기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tie a knot at the end of a piece of string or thread to prevent it from slipping.
meanings.example: Make sure to knot off the thread securely so the button doesn't fall off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 단추가 떨어지지 않도록 실 끝을 매듭지어 단단히 마무리하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sleep outdoors or in a temporary place (British informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 야외에서 잠을 자다, 노숙하다, 임시로 자다
meanings.example: We had to kip out on the beach because the hostel was full.
meanings.exampleKorean: 호스텔이 꽉 차서 우리는 해변에서 야외 잠을 자야(노숙해야) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take a holiday or break from work or study.
meanings.example: I'm going to take some time off in August to go to the beach.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 해변에 가기 위해 8월에 며칠 휴가를 낼(쉴) 예정이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To not go near someone or something; to not go to work or school.
meanings.example: The doctor told him to stay away from fried foods. / He stayed away from school for three days.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의사는 그에게 튀긴 음식을 가까이하지(먹지) 말라고 말했다. / 그는 3일 동안 학교에 결석했다 (가지 않았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be successfully completed or to produce results after a period of effort or development.
meanings.example: Her long-term plans for the business are finally starting to come to fruition.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 사업을 위한 장기 계획들이 마침내 결실을 보기(실현되기) 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak quietly so as not to be heard by others.
meanings.example: Please keep your voice down; the baby is sleeping.
meanings.exampleKorean: 목소리를 좀 낮춰주세요. 아기가 자고 있어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To adapt to a situation as it develops; to accept things as they are (informal).
meanings.example: Things didn't go as planned, but we just had to roll with it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 일이 계획대로 되지는 않았지만, 우리는 그저 상황에 유연하게 대처해야(받아들여야) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare or create something very quickly (informal).
meanings.example: I'll whip together a quick salad for lunch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 점심으로 간단한 샐러드를 서둘러 만들(뚝딱 준비할)게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak or write in support of someone or something; to promote something enthusiastically.
meanings.example: The media is really beating the drum for the new movie.
meanings.exampleKorean: 언론에서 그 새로운 영화를 정말 열렬히 홍보하고(선전하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something more interesting or exciting; to create or give birth to something.
meanings.example: His illustrations really give life to the story. / The new entrepreneur gave life to several successful startups.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 삽화들이 이야기에 정말 생동감을 불어넣어 준다. / 그 새로운 기업가는 몇몇 성공적인 스타트업들을 창조했다(활기를 불어넣었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To copy what someone else is doing; to understand someone's explanation or instructions.
meanings.example: Watch the video and follow along with the exercises. / I'm having trouble following along with your explanation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 동영상을 보고 운동 동작을 따라 하세요. / 당신의 설명을 이해하며 따라가는 데 어려움을 겪고 있어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause someone to remember or think of something.
meanings.example: This song always puts me in mind of my childhood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 노래는 항상 내 어린 시절을 생각나게(기억나게) 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To plead with someone not to kill you.
meanings.example: The prisoner was begging for his life as the soldiers approached.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군인들이 다가오자 죄수는 살려달라고 빌고(목숨을 구걸하고) 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To say something unintentionally or by mistake.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 무심코 나오다, 실수로 말하다, 입 밖에 내다
meanings.example: I didn't mean to tell her about the party; it just slipped off my tongue.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀에게 파티에 대해 말할 의도는 아니었어요. 그냥 무심코 나온(실수로 말한) 거예요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly forget something that you should know or remember (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 깜빡 잊다, 머릿속이 하얘지다, 갑자기 생각이 안 나다
meanings.example: I'm sorry, I've completely blanked on your name.
meanings.exampleKorean: 죄송합니다, 당신 성함이 갑자기 생각이 안 나네요 (깜빡 잊었네요).

meanings.englishDefinition: To communicate with someone or something; to act together with something else and have an effect on it.
meanings.example: The software allows users to interact with each other in real-time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 소프트웨어는 사용자들이 실시간으로 서로 상호작용할(소통할) 수 있게 해준다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To not be as good, strong, or talented as someone or something else.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~의 상대가 되지 않다, 도저히 따라갈 수 없다
meanings.example: Our team was brave, but we were no match for the champions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 팀은 용감했지만, 챔피언 팀의 상대가 되지는 못했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place quietly and secretly so that no one notices you.
meanings.example: We tried to sneak out of the meeting without being seen.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 눈에 띄지 않게 회의에서 몰래 빠져나가려 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop doing something while you are still successful and before you start to fail.
meanings.example: He decided to retire after winning the championship, choosing to quit while he was ahead.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 챔피언십에서 우승한 후, 잘될 때 그만두기로(정점에서 멈추기로) 선택하며 은퇴를 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a deal or issue to be discussed later; to fail to get all the benefits or money that were possible.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 검토 중인 채로 두다, 보류하다, (이익 등을) 남겨두다/포기하다
meanings.example: The committee decided to leave the proposal on the table until the next meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 위원회는 그 제안을 다음 회의 때까지 검토 중인 채로 두기로(보류하기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To revive someone or something; to make something active or successful again.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 생명을 다시 불어넣다, 부활시키다, 활기를 주다
meanings.example: The new manager managed to breathe back to life the struggling department.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 매니저는 고전하던 부서에 생명을 다시 불어넣는(부활시키는) 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a sound louder; to increase the intensity or emphasis of an activity or message.
meanings.example: The opposition party is turning up the volume on environmental issues.
meanings.exampleKorean: 야당은 환경 문제에 대한 비판의 강도를 높이고(강조하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be so eager to do something or please someone that you act in a confused or clumsy way.
meanings.example: He was tripping over himself to impress the new boss.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 새 상사에게 잘 보이려고 열의가 지나쳐 허둥대고(서두르고) 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go onto a sports field to play a game; to go into battle or onto a site for duty.
meanings.example: The players took to the field amidst cheers from the crowd.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선수들은 관중의 환호 속에 경기장에 나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To end a relationship with someone; to stop following a tradition or custom.
meanings.example: She decided to break from tradition and travel solo. / He finally broke from his old group of friends.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 전통에서 벗어나 혼자 여행하기로 결정했다. / 그는 마침내 예전 친구 무리와 헤어졌다 (결별했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To sit down (informal and slightly rude).
meanings.example: Just sit your butt down and listen to what I have to say.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그냥 자리에 조용히 앉아서 내가 할 말을 들으세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find flaws or weaknesses in an argument, theory, or plan.
meanings.example: The defense attorney managed to poke holes in the witness's testimony.
meanings.exampleKorean: 피고측 변호인은 증인의 증언에서 허점을 찌르는(반박하는) 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take further action on something that has already started; to find out more about something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~을 후속 조치하다, 더 알아보다, 끈기 있게 쫓다
meanings.example: The police are following up on several new leads in the case.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 그 사건에 대한 몇 가지 새로운 단서들을 후속 조치하고(더 알아보고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase speed; to resume an activity after a break; to learn a new skill easily.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 속도를 높이다, 다시 시작하다, (기술 등을) 익히다/배우다
meanings.example: The runner started to pick it up in the final lap. / Let's pick it up where we left off yesterday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 주자는 마지막 바퀴에서 속도를 높이기 시작했다. / 어제 멈췄던 부분부터 다시 시작합시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To publicly state your support for something, even if it is difficult or unpopular.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 당당히 소신을 밝히다, 지지를 표명하다, 책임 있게 나서다
meanings.example: In times of injustice, you must stand up to be counted.
meanings.exampleKorean: 불의의 시대에는 당당히 소신을 밝히고(책임 있게 나서서) 지지를 표명해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deal with a very sensitive or dangerous situation with great care.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 살얼음판을 걷듯 조심하다, 극도로 신중히 행동하다
meanings.example: Negotiating the peace treaty was like tiptoeing through a minefield.
meanings.exampleKorean: 평화 조약 협상은 마치 살얼음판을 걷듯 조심하는(지뢰밭을 걷는) 것과 같았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop someone or something before they can succeed or reach their goal.
meanings.example: We need to cut them off at the pass before they sign the deal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들이 계약서에 서명하기 전에 우리가 미리 저지해야(길목을 차단해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat a particular food; to be strengthened or encouraged by something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~을 먹고 살다, ~을 자양분 삼아 강해지다, 이용하다
meanings.example: Many birds feed on insects during the summer. / The rumors only fed on the public's fear.
meanings.exampleKorean: 많은 새가 여름 동안 곤충을 먹고 산다. / 그 루머들은 대중의 공포를 자양분 삼아 더욱 강해질(확산될) 뿐이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get rid of a layer of dead skin; to ignore or disregard something like criticism or a problem.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 허물을 벗다, (나쁜 습관 등을) 버리다, 무시하다
meanings.example: The snake sloughs off its skin several times a year. / He just sloughed off the criticism and kept working.
meanings.exampleKorean: 뱀은 일 년에 여러 번 허물을 벗는다. / 그는 비난을 그냥 무시해버리고(버리고) 계속 일했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a person or animal go away by waving your arms or saying 'shoo'.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 훠이 하고 쫓아버리다, 물리치다, 가라고 하다
meanings.example: She shooed away the cat that was trying to steal the fish.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 생선을 훔치려던 고양이를 훠이 하고 쫓아버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue talking about something in a boring or annoying way (informal).
meanings.example: He kept on about his expensive new car all evening.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 저녁 내내 자신의 비싼 새 차에 대해 지겹게 계속 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To focus all your attention on a particular person or thing.
meanings.example: The investigators keyed in on a specific detail in the security footage.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수사관들은 보안 카메라 영상의 특정 세부 사항에 집중했다 (착안했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to do something, especially for the first time (informal).
meanings.example: I've never cooked Thai food, but I'll take a whack at it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 태국 요리를 한 번도 해본 적 없지만, 일단 한번 시도해(도전해) 볼게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to direct your efforts and attention towards something; to begin to discuss the most important part of a subject.
meanings.example: Enough small talk, let's get down to business. / It's time to get down to studying for the exams.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잡담은 이만하고, 이제 본격적으로 본론(사업 이야기)을 시작합시다. / 이제 본격적으로 시험 공부를 시작할 시간입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause a lot of trouble or to make people very angry by what you say or do.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 벌집을 건드리다, 평지풍파를 일으키다, 소란을 피우다
meanings.example: His comments on tax reform really stirred up a hornets' nest.
meanings.exampleKorean: 세제 개편에 대한 그의 발언은 정말 벌집을 건드린 격이 되었다 (큰 소란을 일으켰다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To compete strongly with someone who is currently more successful; to follow closely behind someone in a threatening way.
meanings.example: New tech companies are snapping at the heels of the established giants.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 기술 기업들이 기존의 거대 기업들을 바짝 뒤쫓고(압박하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue to struggle and resist until the end, even though failure or defeat is certain.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 끝까지 싸우다, 장렬히 패배하다, 굴복하지 않다
meanings.example: They lost the match, but they certainly went down fighting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 경기에서 졌지만, 확실히 끝까지 최선을 다해 싸웠다(굴복하지 않았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay somewhere longer than expected because you do not want to leave; to continue to exist for a long time.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 남아 있다, 끈질기게 이어지다, 오래 끌다
meanings.example: The smell of smoke lingered on in the room for days. / He lingered on for a few minutes after the meeting to talk to me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 연기 냄새가 며칠 동안 방안에 남아 있었다. / 그는 나랑 이야기하려고 회의가 끝난 후에도 몇 분 동안 더 남아 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: The ability to live in one's own home and community safely, independently, and comfortably, regardless of age, income, or ability level.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 살던 곳에서 노후를 보내다, 지역사회 계속 거주
meanings.example: Many seniors prefer to age in place rather than moving to an assisted living facility.
meanings.exampleKorean: 많은 노인은 요양 시설로 옮기기보다 자신이 살던 곳에서 노후를 보내는 것을 선호한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cover something with a blanket; to hide or suppress something so that it is not noticed.
meanings.example: The government tried to put a blanket over the details of the scandal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 그 스캔들의 세부 사항들을 덮어 가리려(은폐하려) 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To play music or voices through a sound system into a room; to interrupt a conversation with a comment (informal).
meanings.example: Classical music was piped in through the store's speakers. / She piped in with a suggestion during the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상점 스피커를 통해 클래식 음악이 송출되고 있었다. / 그녀는 회의 중에 제안을 하며 불쑥 끼어들었다 (목소리를 냈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To send a letter, document, etc., to everyone in a group; to dispatch someone to a place.
meanings.example: Please send around this memo to all staff members. / I'll send around a technician to fix your computer tomorrow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 메모를 모든 직원에게 회람시켜(전달해) 주세요. / 내일 당신의 컴퓨터를 고칠 기술자를 보낼게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To praise or blame someone excessively; to exaggerate something (informal).
meanings.example: He's really laying it on thick to get a promotion. / She's laying it on about how hard she's been working.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 승진하기 위해 정말 심하게 아부하고(과장하고) 있다. / 그녀는 자신이 얼마나 힘들게 일해왔는지에 대해 과장해서 늘어놓고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To complete the education of students already enrolled in a program that is being closed.
meanings.example: The university promised to teach out the remaining students before closing the department.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대학은 학과를 폐쇄하기 전에 남은 학생들을 마지막까지 가르쳐(과정을 마무리해) 주겠다고 약속했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To disappear completely and in many different directions.
meanings.example: As soon as the alarm went off, the crowd vanished to the four winds.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경보가 울리자마자 군중들은 흔적 없이 사라졌다 (사방으로 흩어졌다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To look quickly through a book, magazine, etc., without reading every word.
meanings.example: He flipped through the magazine while waiting for the doctor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 의사를 기다리는 동안 잡지를 휙휙 넘겨보았다 (훑어보았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause someone difficulty or make them feel embarrassed by asking them a difficult question or making them make a decision suddenly.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 난처하게 만들다, 곤경에 빠뜨리다, 즉답을 요구하다
meanings.example: I'm sorry to put you on the spot, but we need an answer right now.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신을 난처하게 해서(곤경에 빠뜨려서) 죄송합니다만, 지금 당장 답변이 필요합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To repair or decorate a building or car; to fasten something like a coat or a dress (informal).
meanings.example: They spent the summer doing up their new house. / Do your coat up; it's cold outside.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 여름 내내 새 집을 수리하고(단장하고) 지냈다. / 코트 단추를 채우세요. 밖이 추워요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To explain or describe something very thoroughly and with many facts.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 상세히 설명하다, 자세히 기술하다, 꼼꼼히 논의하다
meanings.example: The speaker didn't have time to go into detail about the new policy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 강연자는 새로운 정책에 대해 상세히 설명할(자세히 기술할) 시간이 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To store something in a safe or secret place (informal).
meanings.example: She has a lot of money stashed away in a secret bank account.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 비밀 은행 계좌에 많은 돈을 몰래 감춰두고(저축해두고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend too much time with each other; to be very close or intimately involved with someone.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 늘 붙어 다니다, 매우 친밀하다, 밀착되어 있다
meanings.example: The two brothers were in each other's pockets all summer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 두 형제는 여름 내내 늘 붙어 다녔다 (매우 친밀하게 지냈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To get into a place, position, or group by being clever or dishonest.
meanings.example: He managed to weasel into the exclusive club without an invitation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 초대장도 없이 그 독점적인 클럽에 교묘하게 기어드는(잠입하는) 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move or act in a clumsy or confused way; to progress without a clear plan (informal).
meanings.example: The characters in the movie just bumble along until they find the treasure.
meanings.exampleKorean: 영화 속 주인공들은 보물을 찾을 때까지 그저 갈팡질팡하며(서투르게 행동하며) 나아간다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent something from escaping, such as heat, moisture, or flavor; to trap something inside.
meanings.example: Searing the meat helps to seal in the juices. / The heavy snow sealed them in the cabin for two days.
meanings.exampleKorean: 고기를 센 불에 굽는 것은 육즙을 유지하는(가두는) 데 도움이 된다. / 폭설로 인해 그들은 이틀 동안 오두막에 갇혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To include something as an essential part of a system, deal, or product from the beginning.
meanings.example: Security features should be baked in from the start of the software development.
meanings.exampleKorean: 보안 기능들은 소프트웨어 개발 시작 단계부터 (필수 요소로) 포함되어야(내재화되어야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take the lead in a race; to take control of an activity or project and start leading it.
meanings.example: After the manager left, Sarah took up the running of the new department.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매니저가 떠난 후, 사라가 새로운 부서 운영의 주도권을 잡았다(선두에 섰다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be forgotten or put aside for a long time without being dealt with (British informal).
meanings.example: The environmental report seems to have gone into the long grass.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 환경 보고서는 보류되어(방치되어) 잊힌 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bring new ideas and energy to something that has become dull or unsuccessful.
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~에 새로운 생명을 불어넣다, 활기를 주다, 쇄신하다
meanings.example: The new CEO managed to breathe new life into the struggling company.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 CEO는 고전하던 회사에 새로운 생명을 불어넣는(활기를 주는) 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to do something again after failing or having a bad experience.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 다시 시작하다, 재기하다, 용기를 내어 다시 시도하다
meanings.example: I know you're discouraged, but you need to get back on the horse and keep searching for a job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신이 낙심했다는 건 알지만, 다시 용기를 내어(다시 시작하여) 계속 일자리를 찾아야 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To work very hard at something boring or difficult for a long time (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 묵묵히 열심히 일하다, 꾸준히 공부하다, 고군분투하다
meanings.example: She's been slogging away at her thesis all weekend.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 주말 내내 논문을 쓰느라 묵묵히 열심히 일하고(고군분투하고) 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something in a new or different way that is not based on old customs.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 전통을 깨다, 파격적으로 행동하다, 관습에서 벗어나다
meanings.example: The couple decided to break with tradition and have a small, private wedding.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 커플은 전통을 깨고(전통에서 벗어나) 작고 프라이빗한 결혼식을 하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop working as a group as a form of protest, especially to demand better pay or conditions.
meanings.example: The factory workers voted to go on strike next Monday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공장 노동자들은 다음 주 월요일에 파업하기로 가결했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To throw something away; to waste an opportunity, money, or talent.
meanings.example: Don't toss away such a great opportunity just because you're scared. / He tossed away his old shoes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 무섭다는 이유만으로 그렇게 좋은 기회를 허비하지(던져버리지) 마세요. / 그는 낡은 신발을 던져버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop making an effort and become complacent with one's current situation.
meanings.example: We cannot afford to sit back on our heels while our competitors are innovating.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경쟁자들이 혁신하고 있는 동안 우리가 안주하여 노력을 멈출(방심할) 수는 없습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce something slightly, especially a cost, amount, or time.
meanings.example: We need to shave down our expenses to stay within the budget.
meanings.exampleKorean: 예산 범위 내에 머물기 위해 우리는 지출을 깎아서 줄여야(삭감해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop working suddenly as a form of protest.
meanings.example: Hundreds of employees walked off the job this morning to protest the wage cuts.
meanings.exampleKorean: 임금 삭감에 항의하기 위해 수백 명의 직원이 오늘 아침 작업을 중단하고 나갔다 (파업했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize or bring down someone who is very successful or prominent.
meanings.example: In some cultures, there is a tendency to cut down the tall poppy whenever someone succeeds.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어떤 문화권에서는 누군가 성공할 때마다 그 우수한 사람을 깎아내리려는 경향이 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attack or impress someone with great energy and force (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 강하게 몰아붙이다, 전력을 다하다, 강한 인상을 주다
meanings.example: Go out there and sock it to them in the second half!
meanings.exampleKorean: 나가서 후반전에 그들을 강하게 몰아붙이세요!

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter a place in a very confident or grand way; to win an election by a large majority.
meanings.example: The candidate swept in with 70% of the vote. / She swept into the ballroom in a stunning red dress.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 후보는 70%의 득표율로 압승했다. / 그녀는 멋진 빨간 드레스를 입고 무도회장에 당당히 입장했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To revive someone who is unconscious or dying; to make something active or successful again after it has been forgotten or stopped.
meanings.example: Doctors managed to bring him back to life after his heart stopped. / The new director is trying to bring the classic play back to life.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의사들은 그의 심장이 멈춘 후 그를 다시 살려내는(되살리는) 데 성공했다. / 새로운 감독은 그 고전 연극을 다시 부활시키려(생명력을 불어넣으려) 노력하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To resolve a disagreement or argument by hugging someone (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 포옹으로 화해하다, 앙금을 털어내다, 안아서 풀다
meanings.example: You two need to stop fighting and just hug it out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너희 둘 싸움 좀 그만하고 그냥 포옹으로 화해해라(안아서 풀어라).

meanings.englishDefinition: To pull something out quickly; to choose someone or something from a large group.
meanings.example: She plucked out a grey hair. / The talent scout plucked him out from hundreds of young hopefuls.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 흰머리를 하나 뽑아냈다. / 그 인재 스카우트는 수백 명의 젊은 희망자들 중에서 그를 골라냈다(선발했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To cover something by winding or folding something around it; (of a car) to crash into a pole or tree and be badly damaged around it.
meanings.example: He wrapped a scarf around his neck. / The car was wrapped around a telephone pole after the accident.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 목에 스카프를 둘렀다(감쌌다). / 사고 후 자동차가 전신주를 휘감은 채 (심하게 찌그러져) 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be extremely surprised or amused by something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 깜짝 놀라다, 자빠질 정도로 웃다, 몹시 놀라다
meanings.example: I nearly fell off my chair when she told me how much the house cost.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 그 집값이 얼마인지 말해줬을 때 나는 깜짝 놀라 (의자에서) 자빠질 뻔했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To draw the outline of something; to discover the path or origin of something by investigation.
meanings.example: He traced out the route on the map with his finger. / The police are trying to trace out the movements of the suspect.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 손가락으로 지도 위에 경로를 그렸다 (표시했다). / 경찰은 용의자의 동선을 추적하려(밝혀내려) 노력하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue walking; to have a very small, often non-speaking, part in a play or film.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 계속 걷다, 단역으로 출연하다, 스쳐 지나가다
meanings.example: The soldiers walked on despite their exhaustion. / She had a walk-on part as a waitress in the movie.
meanings.exampleKorean: 병사들은 기진맥진했음에도 불구하고 계속 걸었다. / 그녀는 그 영화에서 웨이트리스 역으로 단역 출연했다(잠시 나왔다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To write something exactly as it is written somewhere else.
meanings.example: The students had to copy out the poem into their notebooks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 학생들은 그 시를 공책에 베껴 써야(옮겨 써야) 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To show off one's success, wealth, or power in order to impress or intimidate others (slang).
meanings.example: He's always trying to flex on everyone with his new designer clothes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 새로 산 명품 옷으로 모든 사람에게 자랑하려(허세를 부리려) 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To create a secret plan or plot (informal).
meanings.example: They hatched up a scheme to trick their competitors.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 경쟁자들을 속이기 위해 음모를 꾸몄다(계획을 몰래 세웠다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To drink or eat something noisily; to absorb information or resources greedily and quickly.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 후루룩 마시다, 빠르게 흡수하다, 게걸스럽게 먹다
meanings.example: He slurped up the last of his soup. / The public slurped up every bit of gossip about the celebrity.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 남은 수프를 후루룩 다 마셔 버렸다. / 대중은 그 연예인에 대한 온갖 가십을 매우 빠르게 흡수했다 (받아들였다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something by rubbing it.
meanings.example: She tried to rub away the mark on the table with a cloth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 천으로 테이블 위의 자국을 문질러 없애려(닦아내려) 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To result in something; to be a path to a place.
meanings.example: Eating too much sugar can lead to health problems. / This path leads to the main garden.
meanings.exampleKorean: 설탕을 너무 많이 먹는 것은 건강 문제를 초래할(야기할) 수 있다. / 이 길은 중앙 정원으로 이어진다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause a particular situation to happen (formal).
meanings.example: The accident resulted in severe damage to both cars.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 사고는 두 차 모두에 심각한 피해를 주는 결과를 낳았다(피해를 초래했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To have finished using or doing something; to no longer want to be involved with someone or something.
meanings.example: I'm glad to be done with that difficult project. / I'm done with him; I never want to see him again.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 어려운 프로젝트를 다 끝내서(마쳐서) 기쁩니다. / 나는 그와는 끝났어요(관계를 끊었어요). 다시는 그를 보고 싶지 않아요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move forward very slowly or in small stages.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 조금씩 나아가다, 엉금엉금 가다, 아주 느리게 움직이다
meanings.example: The traffic was inching along in the heavy snow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭설 속에서 차들이 조금씩 나아가고(엉금엉금 가고) 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To show total lack of respect for someone by laughing when they are being serious.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 면전에서 비웃다, 대놓고 무시하다, 코웃음 치다
meanings.example: When I asked for a raise, the boss just laughed in my face.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 임금 인상을 요구했을 때, 사장은 그저 내 면전에서 비웃었다 (나를 대놓고 무시했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce flowers; to develop or grow in a very successful and attractive way.
meanings.example: The cherry trees blossomed forth in early spring. / Her hidden talent for painting finally blossomed forth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 벚나무들이 이른 봄에 활짝 피어났다. / 그녀의 숨겨진 그림 재능이 마침내 활짝 피어났다 (발휘되었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To reach out with one's hand or arm (formal).
meanings.example: He stretched forth his hand to help the falling child.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 넘어지는 아이를 돕기 위해 손을 앞으로 뻗었다(내밀었다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To shout back in response; to contact someone later (slang).
meanings.example: Holler back if you're interested in going to the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 파티에 갈 관심이 있으면 나중에 다시 연락해(답장해) 줘.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wait for a long time at a particular place (informal).
meanings.example: Fans camped out on the sidewalk for days to get tickets.
meanings.exampleKorean: 팬들은 티켓을 구하기 위해 며칠 동안 보도에서 밤새며 기다렸다(머물렀다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To start a race or an activity very quickly and successfully.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 기세 좋게 출발하다, 전력 질주로 시작하다
meanings.example: The company flew out of the blocks with record sales in its first month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 첫 달에 기록적인 매출을 올리며 기세 좋게 출발했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To send a bill or report from a committee to a larger deliberative body for action.
meanings.example: The committee is expected to report out the new healthcare bill tomorrow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 위원회는 내일 새로운 건강보험 법안에 대한 심의를 마치고 보고할(제출할) 예정이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bend over suddenly, especially because of pain or laughter.
meanings.example: He buckled over in pain after the collision. / We all buckled over with laughter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 충돌 후 통증으로 몸을 굽혔다(고꾸라졌다). / 우리는 모두 웃느라 몸을 가누지 못했다 (자지러졌다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept an opportunity or offer eagerly and immediately.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 기회를 덥석 잡다, 즉시 받아들이다, 기뻐하며 응하다
meanings.example: I'm sure she'll jump at the opportunity to work abroad.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 해외에서 일할 기회를 덥석 잡을(기뻐하며 응할) 것이라고 확신합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take part in a particular activity or discussion (formal).
meanings.example: The two countries have agreed to engage in peace talks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 나라는 평화 회담에 참여하기로(임하기로) 합의했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join together to support a person or an idea.
meanings.example: The party members rallied behind their leader after the scandal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당원들은 스캔들 이후 그들의 지도자를 열렬히 지지하기 위해 결집했다 (힘을 보탰다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very eager to do something or to have more freedom.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 하고 싶어 안달하다, 속박에서 벗어나려 애쓰다, 몹시 갈망하다
meanings.example: The young players are straining at the leash to show what they can do.
meanings.exampleKorean: 젊은 선수들은 자신들이 무엇을 할 수 있는지 보여주고 싶어 안달하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do physical work or a craft rather than mental work in an office.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 육체노동을 하다, 직접 만들다, 손재주를 부리다
meanings.example: He decided to quit his office job and work with his hands as a carpenter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 사무직을 그만두고 목수로서 직접 물건을 만드는(육체노동을 하는) 일을 하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make or install something quickly, using whatever materials are available (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 임시로 만들다, 급히 설치하다, 뚝딱 만들다
meanings.example: We managed to rig up a temporary shelter using some old tarps.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 낡은 방수포 몇 장을 이용해 임시 대피소를 뚝딱 만드는(급히 설치하는) 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To repair or improve something in a casual or amateur way (informal).
meanings.example: He spent the whole afternoon tinkering up his old bicycle.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 오후 내내 낡은 자전거를 서투르게 수리하며(만지작거리며) 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To collect and store a large amount of something, especially in secret.
meanings.example: During the war, people began to hoard up supplies of fuel and grain.
meanings.exampleKorean: 전쟁 중에 사람들은 연료와 곡물 공급분을 비축하기(사재기하기) 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take someone to a private place to punish or scold them physically (informal).
meanings.example: My father threatened to take me out back if I didn't stop misbehaving.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아버지는 내가 계속 나쁘게 행동하면 (남몰래) 혼내주겠다고 위협하셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go out regularly with someone as a romantic partner (old-fashioned).
meanings.example: Is he still walking out with the baker's daughter?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 여전히 그 제과점 주인의 딸과 교제하고(사귀고) 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To polish or clean something to make it look new; to improve or refresh a skill or knowledge.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 닦아서 윤을 내다, 새롭게 하다, 복습하다
meanings.example: He spent the day furbishing up the old silverware. / I need to furbish up my French before my trip to Paris.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 하루 종일 낡은 은식기들을 닦아서 윤을 냈다. / 파리 여행을 가기 전에 프랑스어 실력을 좀 새롭게 다듬어야(복습해야) 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce something, such as costs or prices, to the lowest possible level.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 최대한 삭감하다, 뼛속까지 느끼다, 아주 깊이 베다
meanings.example: The company had to cut its marketing budget to the bone to survive. / Her harsh words cut him to the bone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 살아남기 위해 마케팅 예산을 최대한 삭감해야 했다. / 그녀의 모진 말은 그에게 뼛속 깊이 상처를 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return quickly to a former condition; to reply quickly and rudely to someone.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 즉각 반격하다, 원상태로 돌아오다, 빠르게 회복하다
meanings.example: He snapped back at the reporter's question. / The economy is starting to snap back after the recession.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 기자의 질문에 즉각 반격했다(날카롭게 대답했다). / 경기가 침체 이후 빠르게 회복되기(원상태로 돌아오기) 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a part in an activity, emotion, or reward with other people.
meanings.example: We all share in the joy of your success. / Every employee will share in the company's profits.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 모두 당신의 성공의 기쁨을 함께 나눕니다(공유합니다). / 모든 직원이 회사의 이익을 함께 나눌(공유할) 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to bite someone or something; to speak to someone in a sudden, angry way.
meanings.example: Don't snap at me; I'm just trying to help. / The dog snapped at the postman's leg.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나한테 쏘아붙이지(화내지) 마세요. 난 그냥 도우려는 것뿐이에요. / 그 개가 집배원의 다리를 덥석 물려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get into a car; to join an activity or conversation quickly (informal).
meanings.example: Hop in! I'll give you a ride home. / Can I hop in on this discussion for a second?
meanings.exampleKorean: 어서 타세요! 집까지 태워다 줄게요. / 이 토론에 잠시 합류해도(끼어들어도) 될까요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To fly into the air; to suddenly become very angry.
meanings.example: The birds flew up when they heard the noise. / He tends to fly up whenever anyone criticizes his work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새들이 소리를 듣고 날아올랐다. / 그는 누군가 자신의 일을 비판할 때마다 갑자기 화를 내는(폭발하는) 경향이 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stand up very quickly; to increase suddenly by a large amount.
meanings.example: He leaped up to answer the door. / Oil prices leaped up following the news of the conflict.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 문을 열어주기 위해 벌떡 일어났다. / 분쟁 소식 이후 유가가 급증했다(치솟았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To allow someone to use land, a building, or equipment in exchange for rent.
meanings.example: They decided to lease out their spare bedroom to a student.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 남는 침실을 학생에게 임대하기로(빌려주기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ask people to vote on a proposal or issue.
meanings.example: The chairman decided to put the proposal to the vote.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의장은 그 제안을 표결에 부치기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk in a friendly and informal way for a long time.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 즐겁게 수다 떨다, 한참 동안 이야기하다
meanings.example: The two friends chatted away for hours in the cafe.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 두 친구는 카페에서 몇 시간 동안 즐겁게 수다를 떨었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move about quietly and secretly (informal).
meanings.example: I saw him mousing around the back of the building last night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 어제 밤 그가 건물 뒤편을 살금살금 돌아다니는(염탐하는) 것을 보았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start drinking too much alcohol regularly, especially because of unhappiness or stress.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 술에 빠지다, 술을 마시기 시작하다, 알코올에 의존하다
meanings.example: He took to drink after losing his job and his house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 직장과 집을 잃은 후 술에 빠지기(술을 마시기) 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To save money for a time in the future when you might need it unexpectedly.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 만약을 위해 저축하다, 어려울 때를 대비하다
meanings.example: It's a good idea to save a portion of your salary for a rainy day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 월급의 일부를 만약을 위해(어려울 때를 대비해) 저축하는 것이 좋습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very interested in or enthusiastic about something (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: ~에 푹 빠져 있다, 아주 좋아하다, 관심이 많다
meanings.example: He's really into jazz music these days. / Are you into photography?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 요즘 재즈 음악에 정말 푹 빠져 있어요. / 사진 촬영에 관심이 많으신가요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To return something to its owner.
meanings.example: The teacher handed back the corrected essays. / Please hand back the keys before you leave.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님께서 채점된 에세이를 다시 돌려주셨다(반환하셨다). / 떠나기 전에 열쇠를 돌려주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something difficult or unpleasant quickly rather than dragging it out.
meanings.example: We need to rip off the bandage and tell the staff about the budget cuts now.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 고통스럽더라도 단번에 해결해야(미루지 말고 말해야) 합니다. 지금 직원들에게 예산 삭감에 대해 말합시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To appear suddenly and unexpectedly, especially when there is something to be gained (informal/disapproving).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 갑자기 나타나다, 잠자코 있다가 튀어나오다, 갑자기 출몰하다
meanings.example: As soon as he won the lottery, old friends started crawling out of the woodwork.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 복권에 당첨되자마자, 옛 친구들이 여기저기서 갑자기 나타나기(잠자코 있다가 튀어나오기) 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To feel so embarrassed that you want to disappear or hide from other people.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 쥐구멍에라도 들어가고 싶다, 꽁꽁 숨고 싶다, 몹시 창피해하다
meanings.example: After I tripped on stage, I just wanted to crawl into a hole and die.
meanings.exampleKorean: 무대에서 넘어진 후, 나는 그냥 쥐구멍에라도 들어가고 싶은(꽁꽁 숨고 싶은) 심정이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a way that does not attract attention; to disappear into the background.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 눈에 띄지 않게 행동하다, 배경처럼 묻혀 지내다, 존재감을 숨기다
meanings.example: He's very shy and always tries to blend into the woodwork at parties.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 매우 수줍음이 많아서 파티에서 항상 눈에 띄지 않게 행동하려(배경처럼 묻혀 지내려) 노력한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To betray someone while pretending to be their friend or supporter.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 겉으로만 위하는 척하며 배신하다, 비열하게 배반하다
meanings.example: He smiled and agreed to my face, but I knew he would betray me with a kiss.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 내 앞에서는 미소 지으며 동의했지만, 나는 그가 겉으로만 위하는 척하며 나를 배신할 것임을 알고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To include something in a piece of writing or film during the editing process.
meanings.example: We need to edit in a few more shots of the interview.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 인터뷰 장면을 몇 개 더 편집해 넣어야(삽입해야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To add up numbers or amounts (informal).
meanings.example: He toted up the bill and realized he didn't have enough money.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 청구서의 합계를 내보고(계산해보고) 돈이 부족하다는 것을 깨달았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To calculate the likely cost of a project or activity.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 비용을 산출하다, 예산을 짜다, 견적을 내다
meanings.example: We need to cost out the new marketing campaign before we approve it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 그 새로운 마케팅 캠페인을 승인하기 전에 비용을 산출해야(견적을 내야) 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause a lot of trouble or to complain loudly about something (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 소동을 피우다, 요란하게 불평하다, 난리를 치다
meanings.example: He really kicked up a stew when he found out his flight was cancelled.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 비행기가 취소되었다는 사실을 알았을 때 정말 요란하게 불평하며 소동을 피웠다(난리를 쳤다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To think about someone who is in a difficult or unfortunate situation.
meanings.example: Spare a thought for those who are homeless in this freezing weather.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 추운 날씨에 집이 없는 사람들을 잠시 생각해주세요(동정해주세요).

meanings.englishDefinition: To reunite with former colleagues or friends, especially to work on a specific project or goal.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 다시 뭉치다, 재결합하다, 다시 하나가 되다
meanings.example: After five years, the original team decided to get the band back together for one last mission.
meanings.exampleKorean: 5년 후, 원래 팀은 마지막 임무를 위해 다시 뭉치기로(재결합하기로) 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reveal the truth about something that was previously hidden or secret.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 진실을 밝히다, 내막을 공개하다, 실체를 드러내다
meanings.example: The documentary pulls back the curtain on the world of high-stakes gambling.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 다큐멘터리는 고액 도박의 세계에 대한 진실을 밝힌다(내막을 공개한다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To force an attacking enemy to retreat; to return to a place by driving a car.
meanings.example: The army managed to drive back the invaders. / We'll drive back to Seoul tomorrow morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아군은 침략군을 격퇴하는(밀어내는) 데 성공했다. / 우리는 내일 아침에 서울로 운전해서 돌아갈 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To think for a long time about something because it is difficult to understand.
meanings.example: He spent all night puzzling over the mysterious message.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그 신비로운 메시지에 대해 밤새도록 곰곰이 생각했다 (고심했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To solve or understand a difficult problem by thinking about it carefully and persistently.
meanings.example: She managed to puzzle through the complex legal documents.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그 복잡한 법적 문서들을 끈기 있게 읽어 결국 이해해냈다(풀어냈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To state something clearly and directly, without hesitation or apology (usually used in negative).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 주저하지 않다, 솔직하게 말하다, 불평하지 않다
meanings.example: He made no bones about his dislike for the new policy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 새로운 정책에 대한 자신의 반감을 주저 없이(솔직하게) 드러냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To close a gate to prevent entry; to block or restrict access to something.
meanings.example: The park entrance was gated off for maintenance. / They gated off certain features of the app for premium users.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공원 입구는 유지보수를 위해 게이트가 닫혀(차단되어) 있었다. / 그들은 앱의 특정 기능들을 프리미엄 사용자들을 위해 차단했다(접근을 막았다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To endure a difficult or painful situation through sheer determination (informal).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 끝까지 버티다, 끈기로 해내다, 악착같이 견디다
meanings.example: He had a fever, but he managed to gut through the final exam.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 열이 났지만, 끈기로 기말고사를 끝까지 버텨냈다(해냈다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To complain pointlessly about things that cannot be changed (slang).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 부질없이 화내다, 꼰대처럼 불평하다, 헛되이 외치다
meanings.example: Don't mind him; he's just yelling at clouds about how things used to be better.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 신경 쓰지 마세요. 그냥 옛날이 더 좋았느니 어쩌니 하며 부질없이 화내고(꼰대처럼 불평하고) 있을 뿐이에요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter a position in an investment by buying in smaller amounts over time.
meanings.example: I'm going to scale in on this stock over the next few weeks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 향후 몇 주에 걸쳐 이 주식을 분할 매수할(조금씩 진입할) 예정이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start behaving properly and following the rules after a period of bad behavior.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 정신 차리고 똑바로 살다, 품행을 고치다, 개과천선하다
meanings.example: He finally straightened up and flown right after getting out of prison.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 감옥에서 나온 후 마침내 정신 차리고 똑바로 살기(개과천선하기) 시작했다.
